The master was greatly surprised, thinking he had encountered a ghost; he suddenly realized and exclaimed, "The merchant is cunning, and he could imitate the story of Xiao Yi, tricking me into giving him my paintings and calligraphy!" Upon returning, he sent someone to secretly investigate, and it turned out that Mr. A's house was already filled with paintings hanging on the walls, with ink still wet and not yet dry. The Annoyed Man said: The dragon is a divine creature, with clouds and winds changing, and the world becomes dark; if one can understand its nature and tame it, it would submit to being cooked and slaughtered. Mr. A's trick was well arranged, touching every itch of Banqiao, making him hand everything over without any hesitation. Although A is a merchant, he is still a worthy person. Recently, he is superficial and hears with his ears, only buying copies, forcibly stealing and robbing, almost like a thief. If Banqiao were to be reborn, even with the plan of a soaring dragon, what can be done? I therefore say this in a turn of speech: people say that A tricked Banqiao, but I say that Banqiao tricked A.

    49

    东邻墓
    　　
    　　鸠江儒生解必昌，为解大绅耳孙。幼失怙恃，壮未婚娶，交鲜朋友，业拙营运。南山有祖遗瘠壤数亩，可勉供粥，就筑茅舍数椽，唯略避风雨。昕夕下帷读，志颇坚也。门之左，有古松一株，极夭娇，数百年矣。门之东，有古墓一坯，渐倾圮，墓中人，无姓氏，无子孙，佃恐碍出入，欲锄之使平。生不忍，曰：“我正苦无东邻，得此大好伴歌啸，残杯冷炙且祀之，忍去之乎？”呼工镌短碣，曰：“东邻墓。”左泐小篆，曰：“一个土馒头，在吾门之首，下有长眠人，名姓失传久。墓既为吾邻，鬼即为吾友。寒食自年年，歌哭奠杯酒。

    Explanation

    49

    Tomb of the Eastern Neighbor

    A scholar from Jiujiang named Xie Bichang, a descendant of Xie Daxian, lost his parents in early childhood and remained unmarried in adulthood, with few friends and inept at business. There were a few acres of barren land inherited from his ancestors in the south mountain, which could barely provide for a simple life. He built a few thatched huts and merely to shelter from wind and rain. He read diligently from dawn till dusk, with a very determined spirit. To the left of the gate, there was an ancient pine tree, extremely graceful and beautiful, for several hundred years. To the east of the gate, there was an ancient tomb, gradually collapsing, with the person buried there having no known name or descendants. The tenant farmer was afraid it would obstruct passage and wanted to level it. The scholar could not bear to, saying, "I am precisely troubled by having no eastern neighbor, to have such a great companion for singing and laughing, to offer cold wine and food to it, how could I bear to remove it?" He called a craftsman to engrave a short stone tablet, inscribed "Tomb of the Eastern Neighbor," on the left, carved in small seal script, "A mound of earth, at the head of my gate, below lies a long-sleeping person, whose name and surname have been lost for ages. Since the tomb is my neighbor, the ghost is my friend. Year after year, on Cold Food Festival, I sing and weep, offering wine and food."

    50

    吾子与吾孙，慎勿当敝帚。”工竣，即树墓门，且封植。人多诽之，而生则自若也。一夕，正把卷，灯摇摇若轻，自窗隙入。旋闻叩门声。问：“伊谁？”有女子声应之，曰：“东邻也。”曰：“昏夜叩门，其红佛文君一流欤？”曰：“然。”生欣然，拔键迎迓。翩然入，艳丽无双，装束亦富。问曰：“卿何人耶？”曰：“日在顾复，故冒幽冥之嫌，来报大德。郎非鲁男子，妾真薛校书也。”曰：“卿其墓中人耶？”曰：“然。”曰：“为卿表墓泐碣，自问尚有微劳，缘何见逼？”曰：“妾非祸人者。藏骨于此，三十余年矣。妾多姓，名络霞，本娼家，才色冠邯郸。

    Explanation

    50

    "Be cautious with my son and grandson, do not treat them as a humble broom." After the work was completed, he immediately erected a tomb gate and sealed and planted it. Many people criticized him, but the living remained unperturbed. One night, while he was reading, the lamp flickered lightly and seemed to drift through the window gap. Soon after, there was a knock at the door. He asked, "Who is it?" A woman's voice replied, "It's your neighbor from the east." He said, "Knocking at the door in the dead of night, are you a woman of the red-roofed temple or a courtesan?" "Yes," she answered. He was delighted and unlocked the door to greet her. She entered gracefully, her beauty unparalleled, and her attire was rich and elaborate. He asked, "Who are you?" She replied, "I have been taking care of the tomb, so I have ventured into the realm of the dead to repay your great kindness. You are not a man from Lu, I am truly Xue, the librarian." He said, "Are you the one buried in the tomb?" "Yes," she answered. "I have erected a tombstone and inscribed it for you, I believe I have done a small favor, why are you pressuring me?" she said. "I am not a bringer of misfortune. My remains have been buried here for over thirty years. I have many names, my name is Luo Xia, originally from a courtesan's house, my talent and beauty surpass those of Handan."

    51

    年十七，欲嫁有情郎，为鸨母所阻，郁郁而死。生前骨肉星散，仅剩荒邱，时为野魅所扰，樵人牧竖，更肆摧残。顷荷隆情，敢不呈身图报！”言已，荏苒拜灯下，洒泪唏嘘。生虽惊诧，然见其缟衣翠袖，娇小温柔，不觉其爱之深而畏之浅也。曰：“人偶鬼妻，得无促寿命乎？”曰：“非也。静好相依，鬼偶何碍；淫欲过度，人妻亦亡。”生曰：“卿既神灵，何难继丽娘再活？”曰：“是亦不难，然妍体不若丽魄，若定皮相订好逑，诚凿矣。”生喜极，揽入怀，觉体轻于叶，气馥于兰，遂登榻效于飞。鸡唱始去。明夕复来。

    Explanation

    51

    At seventeen, she wished to marry a beloved lover, but was obstructed by the madam, and thus died of sorrow. Before her death, her family members were scattered like stars, leaving her only with a desolate hill, often troubled by wild spirits. Woodcutters and herders even more cruelly destroyed her. Recently receiving your kindness, how dare I not present myself to repay your favor! Having said this, she slowly bowed before the lamp, shedding tears and sighing. Though the man was surprised, he saw her in her white robe and green sleeves, small and gentle, and did not feel that his love for her was deep while his fear of her was shallow. He asked, "Would it not shorten one's lifespan if a human and a ghost were to be a couple?" She replied, "Not so. To live peacefully and dependently, what harm would it do to be a ghost's partner? Excessive lust can be fatal even for a human wife." The man said, "Since you are already a spirit, how difficult would it be to revive Li Nian again?" She said, "This is also not difficult, but the beautiful body is not as good as the beautiful spirit. If you decide based on appearance, it would indeed be a mistake." The man was extremely happy, embraced her, felt her body lighter than a leaf, and her breath fragrant like orchids, then climbed onto the bed and acted like birds in flight. He left when the rooster crowed, and she came back the next night.

    52

    生偶以红丝系女髻，翌日果见丝飘冢首。女夕至，笑曰：“郎疑妾为狐媚耶？”生慰解之，亦不计较。一日，正为生录杜诗，佃蓦至，生急以袖遮盖。佃去，女笑曰：“郎所谓偷生鬼子，常畏人耶！郎见妾，他人不能见也。”既而验之，果然。由是日夜居生室，俨伉俪矣。瞬届秋闱，生略攻苦，女阻挠之曰：“青衿一袭，犹祖宗余荫，若丹桂红杏，实未坐郎命宫。”曰：“仆将以头巾终乎？”曰：“郎若听妾刍荛，准于闱后获一县令。”生问何术能先知，曰：“郎莫问妾，郎曷自问，半世之偃蹇何故？”曰：“不知也。”曰：“世之表表称英豪者，孰不和蔼迎人，春风满面。

    Explanation

    52

    The man accidentally tied a red thread to the girl's hair bun, and the next day he indeed saw the thread floating above the grave. The girl came in the evening and smiled, saying, "Do you doubt that I am bewitched by a fox spirit?" The man comforted and explained, and also did not care. One day, while he was copying Du Fu's poems, the tenant suddenly arrived, and the man quickly covered something with his sleeve. After the tenant left, the girl laughed and said, "What you call a ghost child who steals life, are you often afraid of people? You see me, but others cannot see me." When he verified it, it was indeed true. From then on, she lived in the man's room day and night, becoming like a married couple. Soon it was time for the autumn examination, and the man lightly endured hardship. The girl dissuaded him, saying, "A scholar's robe is still under the protection of our ancestors; if it comes to prestigious positions, it is not in your destiny." The man asked, "Will I end up as a scholar?" She replied, "If you listen to my humble advice, you will surely get a county magistrate after the examination." The man asked what method could predict in advance, and she said, "You need not ask me; ask yourself, why have you been so unlucky for half your life?" The man said, "I don't know." She said, "Those in the world who are called heroes, who are not friendly and welcoming, with a face full of spring breeze?"

    53

    郎满身皆傲骨，即侥幸，亦必遭妒嫉，覆身家，况蠖屈家园，有不动辄得咎者乎？妾善媚者也，一颦一笑，郎若师承之，而摹肖之，自有非常遇。”生颦蹙曰：“媚本天姿，岂东家施所能效乎？”曰：“痴哉！郎也。枕席狎匿，亲近极矣。彼举师保者，先有畏心；学宠爱者，纯秉天性，故知其易也。学而不成，由于爱妾之不深，妾去矣，实不愿郎之偃蹇也。”生曰：“善。”从此步趋，渐能神化，一切笑言，无不解颐。女曰：“可矣。”试期迫，女为束装。生曰：“如卿言，仆老不作茧，顷何劝驾之殷。”曰：“康了诚康了耳。

    Explanation

    53

    The young man is full of proud bones. Even if he is lucky, he will surely suffer jealousy, and ruin his family. How can one be coiled like a caterpillar in one's home and not be unscathed? I am good at flattering. Every frown and smile, if the young man imitates them as if he were learning from a master, he will have extraordinary luck. The young man frowned and said, "Flattery is a natural gift, how could it be something that the neighbor could teach?" She replied, "How foolish, the young man! Being intimate in bed, the closeness is extreme. Those who raise and protect, first have a sense of fear; those who learn to be loved, purely rely on their nature, therefore, it is easy. If learning does not succeed, it is because the love for me is not deep enough. I will leave, but I really do not want the young man to be in a difficult situation." The young man said, "Agreed." From now on, step by step, gradually achieving a divine transformation, all jokes and remarks, none fail to make people laugh. The woman said, "It is enough." The examination period is approaching, so the woman prepares her clothes. The young man said, "As you say, I am old and no longer spin cocoons, yet why are you so eager to urge me?" She said, "It is well, it is indeed well."

    54

    白下为冠，盖会萃之区，俾人人见郎，如再世人，或机缘即伏于是。”生乃挈女同行，寓东郭古废院之东室。其西庑，先有侠客金姓者，对门居。金须眉如戟，衣履极华，出无定踪，居亦鲜偶，唯一骡一马自随，刍秣之劳亦自任。生心异之，私询女，女曰：“此异人也。郎倾心与若定交，自有佳境。”翌即衣冠谒金，意气甚豪，交渐莫逆。金偶夜归，闻生室有妇人声，窗隙潜窥，则生正与女对奕。明晨仍茕独。生偶迟眠，闻金室正歌唱，潜窥之，则金方狎美人坐，珍错满前，雏奴夹侍；一堂灯火，举室通明，天明仍乌有。潜问女，女笑曰：“凡所谓异人者，必有异术。

    Explanation

    54

    White City is the crown, a place where gatherings converge, making everyone see the young master as if they were reborn, perhaps the opportunity lies hidden here. The protagonist then took the girl with him and found lodging in the east room of an old, abandoned courtyard in the eastern suburbs. In the west wing, there was already a wandering warrior named Jin, living opposite them. Jin had a beard and eyebrows like a halberd, wore extremely splendid clothing, went out without a fixed path, and lived rarely with others, accompanied only by one mule and one horse, taking on the labor of feeding and caring for them himself. The protagonist was struck by his uniqueness and privately asked the girl about him. The girl said, "This is an extraordinary person. If the young master forms a firm friendship with him, there will be a better future." The next day, the protagonist dressed formally and visited Jin, showing great enthusiasm, and their friendship grew closer. One night, Jin returned home and heard a woman's voice coming from the protagonist's room. Peering through a crack in the window, he saw the protagonist playing chess with the girl. The next morning, Jin was still alone. Once, the protagonist stayed up late and heard singing coming from Jin's room. Peering in, he saw Jin sitting with a beautiful woman, surrounded by delicacies and young servants; the entire hall was lit by lamps, illuminating the whole room, but by morning, it was as if nothing had happened. The protagonist secretly asked the girl about it, and she laughed and said, "All so-called extraordinary people must have special skills."

    55

    措大眼孔中，真未曾有耳。”翌晤金，略询昨宵事，金掀髯大笑，曰：“某之所乐，君亦有其乐，喋喋何为？虽然，当慎密，万勿为外人道也！”由是交益深。榜发出，果落孙山外，抑郁无聊赖，对女唏嘘。女曰：“郎勿戚戚，且市小肴馔，沽美酒，闭妾暗室，代烹调，延金啖，借以话别，或得将伯助。”生如言，折简招金。甫坐，果见肴核尊垒，均一一从壁间出，气蒸腾而味鲜清。异而问之，生笑曰：“落魄人，技只此耳。”已而以黜落兴叹。金问曰：“君既多才，又抱异术，何必拘拘矮屋，因此七尺躯？”生惨然曰：“是不过小游戏，若纳粟，需多金，则未可咄嗟办。

    Explanation

    55

    In the eyes of a scholar, truly something I have never seen before." The next day, meeting Jin, I briefly inquired about the events of the previous night. Jin laughed heartily, lifting his beard, and said, "What I find enjoyable, you also find enjoyable; why chatter incessantly? Nevertheless, we must be cautious and never reveal this to outsiders!" From then on, our friendship deepened. When the results were announced, I indeed failed the exam, and I was depressed and aimless, sighing in front of the woman. She said, "My lord, do not be sad. Let us buy some small dishes, procure some fine wine, close the dark room, prepare the food, invite Jin to eat, and use this as a way to say goodbye, perhaps we can get some help from him." I followed her advice and wrote a note to invite Jin. As soon as he sat down, indeed, dishes and wine were set out, all emerging one by one from the wall, their aroma rising and their flavors fresh and clear. I asked in surprise, and he smiled and said, "A down-and-out person, my skills are only this." Later, he lamented about being demoted. Jin asked, "You are so talented and possess extraordinary skills; why must you be confined to a small house, thus limiting your seven-foot body?" I looked sad and said, "It is only a small game. If I were to offer grain as a tribute, it would require a lot of money, and it cannot be accomplished in a hurry."

    56

    ”金醉，又见其惜别可怜，慨然与之订车笠盟。金复私语生曰：“仆更有术，为君谋财。但得志后，幸长勿相忘！”生以天日誓，且历历以络霞事告，金始恍然。夜深矣，金置大铜盆于几，储以水，中燃小灯一檠，闭户，更短衫，佩利刃，负空囊，环屋而走，愈走身愈小，长几寸许；跃登几，跳入盆水，不见，而灯火荧荧矣。炊许，忽盆中有声，灯光大明，有小人自水中跃出，飒然堕地，须臾复故貌，仍金也。背上橐，倾出白镪累累焉，得六七千金，曰：“是尚不足为进身阶乎？”生大喜，即援例，得真州令尹。迎金于署，视若同胞，仆从隶役，罔不知为令之真手足也。

    Explanation

    56

    Jin Zui, seeing his pitiful fondness for parting, was moved and made a pact with him under a carriage and a hat. Jin then privately told the young man, "I have another method to help you make money. But after achieving success, please remember me for a long time!" The young man swore by heaven and day, and recounted the details of the Luo Xia affair. Jin was then enlightened. It was deep into the night; Jin placed a large copper basin on the table, filled it with water, lit a small lamp inside, closed the door, changed into a short-sleeved shirt, wore a sharp blade, carried an empty bag, and walked around the house. The more he walked, the smaller he became, until he was only a few inches tall. He leaped onto the table and jumped into the water in the basin, disappearing, but the lamp glowed brightly. After a while, there was a sound from the basin, and the lamp light became very bright. A small person leaped out of the water, fell to the ground with a rustling sound, and in a moment regained his original appearance, still Jin. He had a bag on his back, which he opened to reveal a large amount of silver, totaling about six or seven thousand taels. He said, "Is this still not enough to advance your position?" The young man was greatly pleased and immediately followed the example, becoming the magistrate of Zhenzhou. He welcomed Jin to his office, treating him like a brother, and all servants and subordinates knew Jin to be the true brother of the magistrate.

    57

    生自得金始为官，自得女始善为官，柔顺解事，喜动上游。有张参军者，欲以妹子珠娘偶生，生不敢诺。张厚金，挽金先容，且允丰奁。金醉，遽应允。归以告生，稍拂，即拍案詈曰：“儇薄儿！倒插纱帽翅，即忘却接引佛耶？”生急引谢，始已。夕商于女，女怒曰：“郎固非金不得官，然非妾亦不克友金，遑得官耶？金固功臣，妾亦为功之首也。纨扇弃捐，郎何急急！郎心动，妾心死矣！虽然，妾必有以报金。东邻一坯土，尚在人间耳！妾请从此辞。”言已嗟叹不辍，握手悲啼。

    Explanation

    57

    The man began to serve as an official after acquiring wealth, and he began to serve as an official well after marrying a woman. She was gentle, obedient, and understood how to handle matters, and she was pleased to advance in social status. There was a certain Magistrate Zhang who wished to arrange a marriage between his sister, Pearl Girl, and the man, but the man dared not agree. Magistrate Zhang offered a large sum of money, used it to seek an introduction, and also agreed to provide a generous dowry. The man, overwhelmed, quickly agreed. Upon returning home, he told the woman, who was slightly displeased, and she slapped the table and cursed, saying, "You disrespectful young man! You've fallen from grace, and you've forgotten your spiritual guide?" The man hurriedly apologized, and she finally calmed down. In the evening, he discussed the matter with the woman, who became angry and said, "You could not have become an official without gold, but without me, you could not have befriended gold, how could you have become an official? Gold is a hero, and I am also the first among his achievements. You've cast me aside like a discarded fan, why are you so hasty? When you are moved, my heart dies! However, I will surely find a way to repay gold. The grave of my neighbor is still here in the world! I request to part ways from here." She sighed and wept, holding his hand.

    58

    生挽之，请须臾，而女已掀屏出户，悠忽无踪，觉佩声犹在耳也。数月，生娶珠娘，入门视之，貌果艳而性骄。幸生有心传，事事得夫人怜恕。时京口赛会，鱼龙曼衍，游人如云，为江左第一胜事。隔江伊迩，金欣然欲往，生不敢慢，急赁巨舫，锦旗灯伞，书真州令尹衔，更以干仆良庖伺应。时江天如画，水波粼粼，金欲溲便，抵岸尚迟，腹膨亨不可忍，乃蹲踞舵牙上私焉，而船行如故。时大江南北诸富绅，常被盗，各捕受敲朴，眷属困囹圄，颇为苦。白下老捕飞鸦儿，素有名，亦缉术穷。是日午睡，梦美女子姗姗来告曰：“扬子江心有巨贼，脚点舵牙正如厕。君速捕之，毋使逸。恶贯盈，将毙命。

    58

    The man tried to detain her, asking for just a moment, but the woman had already pushed the screen aside and stepped out of the door, disappearing suddenly and without a trace, and the sound of her ornaments still seemed to echo in his ears. After several months, the man married Zhu Niang. Upon entering the house, he saw that she was indeed beautiful in appearance and proud in nature. Fortunately, the man had a method of communication, and in all things, he received her sympathy and forgiveness. At that time, there was a festival in Jingkou featuring acrobatic performances involving fish and dragons, with a crowd of visitors like clouds, making it the greatest event in the east of the Yangtze River. Across the river, it was close by, and Jin happily wanted to go. The man dared not be slow, so he hurriedly rented a large boat, adorned with colorful flags, lanterns, and parasols, and wrote the title of the magistrate of Zhenzhou on it, and arranged for skilled servants and cooks to attend to them. At that time, the sky over the river was like a painting, and the water waves shimmered. Jin wanted to urinate, but it was still a bit late to reach the shore, and his stomach was distended and unbearable. He then crouched on the steering post and relieved himself, but the boat continued to sail as usual. At that time, wealthy and influential people in the Jiangnan region were often robbed, and each capture was met with severe punishment, with their families imprisoned, causing them quite a lot of suffering. The old detective Feiyaye from Nanjing, who was well-known, also had exhausted all possible methods. On that day during his nap, he dreamed of a beautiful woman who came gracefully and said, "There is a great bandit in the middle of the Yangtze River, and his feet are on the steering post, just like when he goes to the toilet. You must capture him quickly, do not let him escape. His evil deeds are complete, and he will be killed."

    59

    贼何人？金其姓。”捕惊醒，即挈伴当，怀利器，驾轻舫，破浪南下。适遇金，见其两足尖搭舵后，屹如山峙。详加物色，宛与梦符。尾之，见其一跃即入舱，便捷极矣。顾旗上字，又似官舫，心忐忑，姑唤之，辨真赝，大声曰：“金老公好身手耶！累吾辈死杖下者屡矣！”金回首色变，即挥弟子猬集，将飞黑索；仆皆呵叱曰：“此真州令伯氏也！”金急止之，顾众厉声曰：“尔辈求吾，为销官限，得赏金耳！就此缚老金，恐未能；即能，亦只得官金，恐不得吾之多金。”捕曰：“如君言若何？”曰：“且转帆，见真州令，自有说。”捕许可。

    Explanation

    59

    Who is the thief? His surname is Jin.

    The pursuer woke up, grabbed his companions, carried sharp weapons, and sailed south in a light boat, cutting through the waves.

    He happened to meet Jin, who had both feet resting on the stern of the boat, standing firm like a mountain.

    He carefully examined him, and it matched the dream.

    He followed him and saw that Jin jumped into the cabin in one leap, extremely agile.

    He looked at the flag on the boat, which seemed like an official boat, and felt uneasy. He called out tentatively to distinguish the real from the fake.

    He shouted loudly, "Old Jin, how good your skills are! You've made us die under the whip many times!"

    Jin turned around, his face changed, and he swung his disciples together, about to throw a black rope.

    The pursuers all shouted, "This is the elder brother of the magistrate of Zhenzhou!"

    Jin quickly stopped them, turned to the crowd, and shouted sternly, "You all seek me to clear official deadlines and receive a reward! To bind old Jin here, you probably can't; even if you could, you would only get official gold, not my generous reward."

    The pursuer said, "What do you say?" Jin replied, "Turn the sail and see the magistrate of Zhenzhou, there will be a discussion." The pursuer agreed.

    60

    生闻变大惊，几晕绝。会金至，款众捕于厅事，然后偕生密室商之。曰：“兄罪恶不可擢发数，事发宜也。然吾去，恐为弟累，故贿若辈来，与兄一计较。”生泣曰：“弟所以有今日者，兄也。兄死，弟未敢独生。”曰：“是已知吾弟之心矣！然骈死固无益，且为天下笑。曷诡云踪吾，惧其本领大，动辄逝，始假托结纳，以软禁之，且送观赛会以暗解之，适与捕遇，故辗转就缚。如是则弟免株连，且得勋绩，策之上也。”生意良不忍。曰：“男儿临事贵勇断，否则兄逸，弟又奈何？”生无已，商于捕，与重贿，俾如其说，而后涕泣以赭衣送登程。

    Explanation

    60

    The younger brother heard the news and was greatly alarmed, almost fainting. When the officials arrived, they interrogated the crowd in the hall, and then they went into a private room with the younger brother to discuss matters. The older brother said, "My crimes are too numerous to count, and it is fitting that they have come to light. However, if I leave, I fear it will burden you. Therefore, I bribed these people to come and discuss with you." The younger brother wept and said, "The reason I am in this position today is because of you. If you die, I dare not live alone." The older brother replied, "This shows your loyalty to me! However, dying together is of no benefit and will make us laughed at by the world. Why not pretend to follow me? I fear they have great skills and often disappear, so we can pretend to be friends, keep them under house arrest, and send them to watch the fair to secretly release them. If they happen to meet the officials, they will be gradually caught. If this is done, then you will be spared, and you will gain merit. It is the best strategy." The younger brother found this very hard to bear. The older brother said, "A man in the face of an event must be courageous and decisive. Otherwise, if you escape, what can I do?" The younger brother had no choice but to discuss with the officials, offering a large bribe, and they agreed to follow his plan. Then, tearfully, he sent the older brother off in prison clothes on his journey.

    61

    至秋曹，不假拷掠，一一具服，且曰：“赚我者，真州解令尹也！半生横行江湖间，一朝禄尽，为怯书生所算，奇哉！”上游本重生，至是益赏其能。决金市曹日，飞鸦儿为赏金巨，置酒肉送就死。金且啖且饮，问曰：“仆迟早有今日，不怨公也。但生平无半面缘，何一瞥即蓦知为金？祈告我，死无憾。”捕乃告以梦中语。金愕然唾曰：“淫娃朽魄，敢于樱金老公，死耳死耳，复何言！”生时在真州署，正惶惑，忽见金缨帽短衣，含笑掀帘入，笑曰：“吾弟安否？”生惊起，曰：“兄固无恙乎？”曰：“无恙。

    Explanation

    61

    In the autumn court, without the need for torture, he confessed in detail, and said, "The one who deceived me was the magistrate of Zhenzhou! For half my life, I brazenly roamed the world, but one day, my fortune ran out, and I was outwitted by a timid scholar—strange indeed!" The emperor, who had originally shown him mercy, now greatly admired his abilities. On the day of his execution at the Golden Market, the guard named Fei Yaer gave him a generous reward and provided him with food and drink to accompany him to death. As he ate and drank, he asked, "It was inevitable that this day would come, and I do not blame you. But we have never met before, how did you recognize me at a glance? Please tell me, so I have no regrets at death." The guard then revealed the words from a dream. The man was startled and spat out in reply, "A promiscuous woman's decaying spirit, dare to challenge the mighty man—death is all that matters, what more is there to say!" At the time, he was in the Zhenzhou office, feeling anxious, when suddenly he saw a man with a golden-embroidered hat and short clothes, smiling and pushing aside the curtain to enter. He laughed and said, "How are you, my brother?" The man was startled and asked, "Are you safe, brother?" The other replied, "I am safe."

    62

    ”旋呼进酒，取大爵斗饮者三，告生曰：“我游戏人间，不过破贪囊，取污财耳。顷亦蝉蜕羽化，如郭璞、谢灵运诸公，非真遭戮也。而告吾弟当日床头人敢于饶舌，诚不能恕。”言已，闻署外喧嚷声不已，金起，揖生曰：“兄将遐适矣，祈念手足情，收吾骨，免抛弃，感且不朽。兄留马一骡一，祈善视之，勿过加鞭笞。”言毕，匆匆遽杳。生趋出，则秋曹材官正送金之首级来。开椟视，面目犹是，然已子章骷髅，血模糊矣。遂贿易其首，联尸厚葬之。年余，生以才干保升浙之观察使，颇著政绩，珠娘亦有内助功。忽有故人自鸠江来，肮脏软红，意在乞助。

    Explanation

    62

    The official immediately summoned wine, took three large cups, and drank them down in one go. He then told the student, "I have been playing in the world, merely breaking open the bag of greed and taking tainted wealth. Recently, I have also transformed into a divine being, like the sages Guo Pu and Xie Lingyun, and I was not truly executed. But I must inform my younger brother that the person at the bedside dared to speak out, and I truly cannot forgive this." After speaking, he heard the continuous noise outside the office. The official stood up and bowed to the student, saying, "Brother, you will soon depart, so I ask you to remember our familial bond, gather my bones, and prevent them from being discarded. Your gratitude will be eternal. Leave me one horse and one donkey, and please take good care of them, do not overuse the whip and stick." After finishing his words, he quickly disappeared. The student hurried out and saw officials from the Ministry of Justice delivering the official's head. Upon opening the box, the face was still recognizable, but it was already a skull, with blood smeared all over. The student bribed the officials to exchange the head, reassembled the body, and gave a grand burial. A year later, the student was promoted to the position of Censor in Zhejiang due to his abilities, achieving notable political achievements, and Zhu Niang also contributed to his success. Suddenly, an old friend came from Jiujiang, looking disheveled and seeking assistance.

    63

    茶次，生问：“敝庐在南山之南，斗大一椽，想为秋风所破？”曰：“明公钓游之区，桑梓犹知敬礼。唯东邻墓，突于某日风雨疾雷，震裂成潭，碑亦断碎，白骨零星，抛洒堕溷，唯古松尚存，诚不可解耳。”生详询其日，即金弃市日也。由是竟惊悸成疾，床第缠绵，意在予告，上游不许，荐医来珍视，曰：“脑风也，服马脑即愈。”左右遂杀金马。数日，又委顿，医曰：“此肝气也，服骡肝即愈。”左右杀金骡进。由是精力强健，日劳案牍不为疲。生有玉枕金瓶，最宝重，千金值，归向珠娘，索以酬医，问何药得痊之速。

    Explanation

    63

    After the meal, the man asked, "My humble abode is south of Mount Nanshan, a tiny, insignificant house, perhaps broken by the autumn wind?" The other replied, "This is your familiar fishing and playing area, your hometown is still respected. Only the tomb of your eastern neighbor, on a certain day of violent wind and thunder, was shattered into a pool, the stone tablet also broke into pieces, the scattered bones fell into the toilet, only the ancient pine tree remained, truly incomprehensible." The man inquired in detail about the day, which was the day when Jin was executed in public. As a result, he developed a disease from shock and fear, became bedridden, intending to resign, but the higher authorities did not allow it. A doctor was recommended to come and treat him carefully, saying, "It is a disease of the brain, taking horse brain will cure it." The attendants then killed a horse to get its brain. After several days, he again became exhausted, the doctor said, "This is a disease of the liver, taking donkey liver will cure it." The attendants then killed a donkey to get its liver. As a result, he became strong and healthy, working hard on official documents without feeling tired. He had a precious jade pillow and golden vase, highly valued, worth a thousand gold coins, returning to the lady Zhu, asking for them as payment to the doctor, inquiring what medicine could cure it quickly.

    64

    珠娘以实告，生恸且哭，珠慰曰：“后厩不少骅骝，何惋惜之甚？”曰：“非重物也，为负故人托耳！”急延高僧法道，寻水陆，讽金经，超拔络霞与金，并骡马之灵。所撰祭文中有句云：“感恩报德，乃在红粉骷髅；舍己从人，何愧绿林豪杰。底事雷诛不免，法网难逃，数也天乎？搔首莫问，至若连钱珍重，蹄铁销磨，可怜供我加餐，肝脑涂地。想去从君射猎，飞走摩云，斯固生为人英，而死为鬼杰者也。噫！问寂寂黄泉，可再唱晓风残月？叹茫茫白骨，居然成一妹三郎！”懊侬氏曰：妓与盗，冤冤相报，可谓酷矣；而于解君，则报之殷殷，唯恐后焉者，何哉？无他，为其能有情耳。观其表墓泐碣，洒泪致祭，始终不外乎情。噫！人而有情者，佛心也；块然而无情，且悍然而恶人之多情者，吾不知其何心也。

    Explanation

    64

    Zhu娘 told the truth, and the man mourned and wept. Zhu consoled him, saying, "There are no shortage of fine horses in the stables. Why are you so sorrowful?" He replied, "It's not a heavy burden, but it's a promise to an old friend!" He quickly invited a high-ranking monk, Master Fa Dao, to seek out both water and land, recite the Golden Sutra, and elevate the spirits of Luo Xia and Jin, as well as the spirits of the mule and the horse. In the memorial text he wrote, there are lines that say: "Gratitude and repayment of kindness are found even in the red-skinned skull; to sacrifice oneself for others, what shame is there in being a hero of the Green Forest? Why was he not spared from the thunderbolt's punishment, unable to escape the legal net, is it fate? Scratching one's head and not asking, as for the valuable linked coins, the worn horseshoes, pitifully providing me with food, their brains and livers smeared on the ground. To go with you on hunts, flying and running under the clouds, this is indeed a person of great valor in life and a ghostly hero in death. Ah! Asking the silent realm of the underworld, can it sing again the dawn breeze and the waning moon? Lamenting the vast white bones, they have become a sister and three brothers!" The author, An Nong, said: Courtesans and bandits, avenging each other's wrongs, can be seen as cruel; but for Jie Jun, they sought to repay him earnestly, fearing to be left behind. Why? There is no other reason than that they were capable of feeling emotion. Seeing him erect a tombstone, weep, and offer sacrifices, his actions were always guided by emotion. Ah! A person who feels emotion has a Buddha's heart; one who is cold and heartless, and who viciously despises others' emotions, I do not know what their heart is like.

    65

    吴孝子
    　　
    　　孝子吴姓，忘其名，鲁之恩县东邻人。少孤，喑哑不能言。凡病哑者，耳必聋，盖有窍不能相通故也。其居距郭三四里，因贫，为邑之质库担水，得值即奉母，不敢浪以一钱自啖嚼。虽哑且聋，而性敏，能以己意揣母意；母亦习久，能以势与孝子言。每日，母思食何物，必呀呀请母命，而后诣郭购之回。如四指作圈，即知为饼；撮指覆腕，即知为馒首；又手成八字，即知为水角子；伸掌使平，知为鱼；垂手如提，知为肉之类。百无一爽。母老且病，每食若微且噎，孝子即于暗处洒眼泪。

    Explanation

    65

    Filial Son of Wu

    The Filial Son of Wu, whose name is forgotten, was a resident of the eastern neighbor of En County in the state of Lu. Orphaned at a young age, he was mute and unable to speak. All those who are mute are also deaf, because the passages within their bodies cannot communicate with each other. He lived about three to four miles from the city, and due to his poverty, he worked as a water carrier for the town's pawnshop. Whenever he earned money, he immediately gave it to his mother and dared not spend even a single coin on himself. Though mute and deaf, he was quick-witted and could infer his mother's wishes from his own thoughts; his mother, having grown accustomed to it, could communicate with him through gestures. Every day, when his mother thought of something to eat, he would make a sound to ask her, and then go to the market to buy it. If he made a circle with four fingers, it meant bread; if he covered his wrist with his fingers, it meant steamed buns; if he formed an eight with his hands, it meant dumplings; if he stretched out his palm flat, it meant fish; if he hung his hands as if holding something, it meant meat. Not once was there a mistake. When his mother was old and ill, and sometimes choked while eating, the filial son would shed tears in secret.

    66

    见人必指划，若言母食少，攒眉蹙额作忧虑状。食若甘且多，孝子即对母，呀呀若唱歌，张两手起舞，效演剧者关目，博母欢。见人又指划，若言母食多，拍掌狂笑作快活状。年五十，如一日，未尝稍变更。每严冬，必先以己体为母温布衾，既暖，又着起，为母解衣就枕，己则倦伏床足，听母鼻息酣，始逡巡趋就草榻眠。每暑，悬芦帘于门，寝母于中堂竹榻，己则解衣当门卧，意若代母饱蚊蚋然。居虽近野田，而白鸟竟绝迹。里人贤其行，争呼之曰吴孝子。然究赤贫，无肯以女妻之者。孝子亦绝不计及床头人。母每谓人曰：“吾宁有承顺反舌子，不愿有忏逆长舌妇也。

    Explanation

    66

    Whenever he met people, he would point and gesture, as if saying the mother had eaten little, frowning and making a worried expression. If the food was sweet and plentiful, the filial son would immediately face the mother, making sounds as if singing, stretching out his hands and dancing, imitating the gestures of actors, to please the mother. When meeting others, he would point and gesture again, as if saying the mother had eaten a lot, clapping his hands and laughing wildly, showing a happy expression. At fifty years old, he was like a day of his life, never changing even slightly. Every severe winter, he would always first warm the quilt with his own body, then get up when it was warm, undress the mother and help her lie down to rest, while he would be tired and lie at the foot of the bed, listening to the mother's deep breathing, only then cautiously move to sleep on the straw mat. Every summer, he would hang rush curtains at the door, have the mother sleep on a bamboo bed in the central hall, while he would take off his clothes and lie in front of the door, as if he were taking the place of the mother to be bitten by mosquitoes. Though living near a field, white birds were completely absent. The villagers admired his actions, and competed to call him Wu Xiaozhe. But he was ultimately extremely poor, and no one was willing to marry their daughter to him. The filial son did not even consider the person by the bed. The mother often said to people, "I would rather have a compliant, silent son than a rebellious, talkative daughter-in-law."

    67

    ”一日，正担水入肆门，适恩宰梅公子盛服至，误泼水溅公子服，怒且叱，肆中执事人，急趋至，拱谢曰：“公子莫怒，此吴家哑孝子也。”公子惊询其事迹，转怒为喜，假青蚨五竿赠之。孝子坚却不敢受，执事人举无名指示之。盖凡哑指均以拇者为天，食指为地，中指为父，无名指为母，小指为妻子耳。孝子见之，知公子怜伊有老母，始伏地稽首拜，呀呀指天地，感激非常。然当公子叱辱时，则固挺立不屈，毫无畏怖者也。担水讫，荷钱归，甫近村，见母正倚闾，遽欢喜呼啸，泥滑，几倾倒。母见钱，惊问何来，孝子口呀呀指划，唔不能道一字。母恐其因贫，误行窃，遍问邻右，亦不知其所由来。

    Explanation

    67

    One day, as he was carrying water into the shop, just then, the generous and kind梅公子 in his splendid attire arrived. Accidentally, he splashed water onto the公子's clothes, and he became angry and scolded. The attendant in the shop hurried over, bowed, and apologized: "公子, please do not be angry, this is the mute孝子 from the Wu family." The公子 was surprised and asked about his story, and then changed his anger to joy, giving five pieces of青蚨 as a gift. The孝子 firmly refused to accept it, pointing to the ring finger (symbolizing the mother). Generally, the thumb represents heaven, the index finger represents earth, the middle finger represents the father, the ring finger represents the mother, and the little finger represents the wife and children. The孝子 saw this, knowing that the公子 pitied him for having an elderly mother, and then knelt down and bowed, making sounds and pointing to heaven and earth, was extremely grateful. However, when the公子 scolded and insulted him, he remained upright and unyielding, showing no fear or terror. After finishing fetching water, he carried the money back home. As he approached the village, he saw his mother leaning on the gate. He immediately joyfully shouted, but the mud was slippery, and he almost fell. The mother saw the money, was surprised, and asked where it came from. The孝子 made sounds and gestures, but could not utter a single word. The mother worried that he might have stolen due to poverty, and asked all the neighbors, but no one knew where it came from.

    68

    叱使膝立，怒曰：“吾宁有残疾担水子，不愿有邪心穿窬子也！”自扶仗诣质库，遍问执事人，始知实是公子持赠。始诵佛而回。顾往返虽五里，然究因老病，步履迟滞，半日始归村。视孝子犹跪地，不敢动。笑慰之，孝子亦即收泪笑舞，牵母衣，视母床；其意盖欲为母易新制，但不知从何做起耳。噫！情态可想也。梅公知之，给匾旌其庐，孝子泣不受，里人悬于土圩门，以为荣。捻匪窜其乡，见匾，遽额手曰：“此孝子里也，莫惊他。”既而思识其风范，仰谓守者曰：“若肯以孝子一登陴，使我辈一瞻其面目，即退。”孝子卒抱母，摇手不敢出。贼亦旋去。时浙人孙君怡轩，为恩署钱幕，言于梅公，醵资得百金，存质库，嘱执事人代孝子略购田畴，兼觅配偶。因梅旋解任去，不知其究竟云。

    Explanation

    68

    The mother scolded him to kneel down, angrily saying, "I would rather have a disabled child who carries water than a child with evil intentions!" She then supported herself with a walking stick and went to the pawnshop, asking all the staff members. Finally, she learned that it was indeed the young master who had given it to her. She began reciting Buddha's name and returned home. Although the round trip was only five miles, due to her old age and illness, her steps were slow, and it took half a day to return to the village. She looked at the filial son as if he were still kneeling on the ground, not daring to move. She smiled and comforted him, and the filial son also wiped away tears and danced joyfully, holding onto his mother's clothes and looking at her bed. His intention was to buy something new for his mother, but he did not know where to start. Alas! The emotions and attitudes can be imagined. Mr. Mei heard about it and gave a plaque to honor the son's house, but the filial son wept and refused to accept it; the villagers hung it on the gate of the village, considering it an honor. The bandits who roamed the area saw the plaque and immediately clapped their hands, saying, "This is the village of the filial son; do not frighten him." They then thought to see the son's demeanor and called out to the guard, saying, "If you would allow the filial son to come up to the wall, so that we could see his face, we would withdraw." The filial son finally hugged his mother and shook his head, not daring to come out. The bandits also quickly left. At that time, Mr. Sun Yixuan from Zhejiang, who was serving as an assistant to En in the money office, spoke to Mr. Mei on behalf of the filial son, raising funds to obtain a hundred gold coins, which were deposited in the pawnshop, and he instructed the staff to buy some land and find a spouse for the filial son. Because Mr. Mei soon resigned from his post, it is unknown what happened in the end.

    69

    卷二
    　　
    　　龙梭三娘
    　　
    　　元季海陵江天石者，巨富也。其子名璧，字玉人，年甫冠，即夤缘魁乡捷。顾秉性鲁钝，文采非所长，然貌尚雅俊；书法二王，盖虚有其表耳。翁年古稀，亦老孝廉也。一日，对座客愀然曰：“仆中年失偶，虽满腹经史，苦无哲嗣能授以衣钵，奈何？”客勉谀璧曰：“公子折芹探桂，瞬又走马看长安十里红，岂尚有愧公冶欤？”翁唏嘘曰：“江天石儿子，豚犬耳。”时门下有慕荣者，揣翁意，以巨金自江北购得逃难女子，名曰龙梭三娘，貌既娟妍，齿亦稚弱，衣以绣襦，乘以油壁，媵以雏娃，踵门而献。翁一见大喜，即日筑金屋，开琼宴，迓珠履，赋催妆，谋合卺焉。午夜，两行画烛引翁入洞房，掀须微笑，却扇定情。

    Explanation

    69

    Chapter Two

    Dragon Silk Three-Named Maiden

    During the late Yuan dynasty, there was a wealthy man from Hailing named Jiang Tian Shi. His son was named Bi, courtesy name Yu Ren. At the age of twenty, he managed to advance through the provincial examinations through connections and favoritism. However, he was naturally dull and lacked literary talent, though he was still handsome and graceful; his calligraphy was modeled after the styles of Wang Xizhi and Wang Xianzhi, but it was merely superficial. His father, who was in his seventies, was also an elderly scholar of high moral standing. One day, he looked solemnly at his guests and said, "I lost my wife in middle age, and although I am well-versed in the classics and histories, I have no wise heir to pass on my legacy to, what shall I do?" A guest praised Bi, saying, "Your son has already excelled in the provincial examinations and will soon be galloping to the capital to witness the red banners of the metropolitan examinations. How could he still be inferior to Gongye?" The old man sighed and said, "Jiang Tian Shi's son is nothing but a useless offspring." At that time, there was someone in his household who admired glory and, sensing the old man's desire, spent a huge sum of money to purchase a young woman from the north who was fleeing from disaster. She was named Long Suo San Nian, and she was both beautiful and young. She was dressed in embroidered robes, rode in a carriage with red lacquer, and was accompanied by young maids. She was brought to the door and presented to the old man. The old man was greatly pleased upon seeing her and immediately built a luxurious mansion, held a lavish banquet, welcomed distinguished guests with fine shoes, composed poetry to celebrate the wedding, and planned the wedding ceremony. At midnight, two rows of painted candles led the old man into the bridal chamber, where he smiled with satisfaction, removed her fan, and established their relationship.

    70

    及见女，愁眉泪睫，粉黛浸淫，略与软语，则体态生硬，啼状惨然。翁怒曰：“小妮子！将毋嗔老夫衰迈耶？寒家不乏衣食，若能育一石麟，瞬及太母太夫人矣，何戚戚为？”女凄恻而前，拜伏膝下，直陈遭际，泪坠辞前。盖女为蒙古产，随侍尊人名鲁不花达赤达泥入中国，为淮西行省平章政事。其母梦织女投梭化龙而生，终鲜兄弟，常恨阙伦。顾父性峭鲠，与御史莽吉兔不相能。偶怒顽僮小张无礼，酒后鞭其背，遁入莽吉兔家中，以蜚语疏入，坐贪墨削职，诏收刑狱论斩，寻瘐死，上怒未已，籍没其家，女为恶叔诱出，盗卖于此。幼字父之同官子叶生，名子荷。叶父没，家赤贫，不得已，流入闽中，入某刺史幕，久无耗。罗敷本有夫女也，乞翁怜鉴之。

    Explanation

    70

    Upon seeing the girl, he noticed her furrowed brow, tears streaming down her cheeks, her makeup blurred by her weeping. When he tried to speak gently to her, she remained stiff and rigid in her posture, her face filled with sorrow. The old man became angry and said, "Little girl! Are you upset because I am old and weak? Our humble home is not lacking in food and clothing. If you can bear a son, you will soon become a grandmother. Why are you so worried?" The girl moved forward with a sad and pitiful expression, kneeling at his knees and speaking frankly about her circumstances, her tears falling before she spoke. She was of Mongolian origin, accompanying her father, named Lübu Hada Dachen, who had come to China and held the position of Pingzhangzhengshi in the Huai West Provincial Administration. Her mother had dreamed of a weaving goddess throwing a shuttle that transformed into a dragon, and she was born from this dream. She had no brothers and often regretted the lack of siblings. Her father, however, was sharp and stubborn, and he did not get along with the censor Mangjitui. Once, he was angry at the naughty servant Xiao Zhang for being disrespectful and, after drinking, beat him on the back. Xiao Zhang fled to Mangjitui's house and submitted a report with slanderous remarks, leading to her father being dismissed from office for corruption. He was arrested and sentenced to death, and soon died in prison. The emperor's anger was not yet satisfied, and his family's property was confiscated. The girl was lured out by a wicked uncle and sold here. She had been betrothed to the son of her father's colleague, Ye Sheng, whose name was Zihé. After Ye Sheng's father died, his family became extremely poor, and they had no choice but to move to Fujian. She entered the service of a certain prefectural governor and there has been no news of her for a long time. Luo Fu is actually a married woman, and she begs for your compassion and understanding.

    71

    翁闻之，豪气顿消，惊汗若雨，慨谓女曰：“老夫不才，有子已成乡贡士，敢恃阿堵物，而浪夺他人妇耶？女公子请安心小住，老夫自有法，为汝圆乐昌破镜。”言已即回旧卧孤榻。女牵衣哀泣，惊询之，女曰：“翁长者也，可否收儿为螟蛉女？不然，终有瓜李嫌。”翁曰：“善。”即如所请，遣妥婢伴女宿。翌晨，诡告人曰：“渠尊甫与仆有素，不忍污也。”函金遣急足入闽，物色匝月，招叶生归。视其人翩翩儒素，虽穷措大，而才则胜郎君万万，特荒芜耳。即日设筵宴，招赘于家，与之约曰：“婚后，仍旧就塾中宿，无论河魁，但得一课作合程式，许诣甥馆一次。”叶敬诺。一夕诣内，女曰：“郎知翁德如山岳乎？若不奋志，何以报鸿慈也！”嗣后虽命之入，亦不入，唯埋头下帷。

    Explanation

    71

    The old man heard this and his boldness suddenly vanished; he was drenched in sweat like rain. He said to the girl with emotion, "I am not worthy, but I have a son who has already become a local scholar. How could I rashly take another man's wife? Please rest here for a while, my dear, and I will find a way to reunite you with your husband, as in the story of the broken mirror of Lechang." After speaking, he returned to his solitary bed. The girl pulled at his clothes and wept bitterly, alarming him. She asked, "Sir, you are a respectable man; can you adopt me as your daughter? Otherwise, there will always be suspicion of Gu and Li." The old man said, "Very well." He then sent a reliable maid to accompany the girl for the night. The next morning, he deceitfully told others, "Her father and I have been acquainted for a long time, and I cannot defile her." He sent money by letter and dispatched a fast runner to search for the man for a month, eventually bringing him back. When he saw the man, he was a refined scholar, though poor, but his talent far surpassed his son-in-law, though it was somewhat neglected. That day, he held a banquet and took the man in as a son-in-law, setting conditions for him: "After marriage, you must still stay at the school, regardless of the first day of the month, but if you complete the required compositions, you may visit your in-laws' home once." The man respectfully agreed. One evening, when he visited the inner chambers, the girl said, "Do you know how great and benevolent my father is? If you do not strive with determination, how can you repay his kindness?" After that, even when ordered to join the household, he did not, but only buried himself in his studies.

    72

    经年余，旧业尽理，而功更倍，翁曰：“可矣！”馈赠，命入都。临行，女泣告叶某曰：“若蹉跎，可不必回见床头人矣。”比试南宫，一战而捷，出为会稽太守。先以轻车莅任，断狱称神明。翁闻之喜跃，为女束装，遣仆婢送之任。作书与生曰：“闻贤契贵，甚喜。舍眷属，莅官守，乃公而忘家者，甚善。但琼儿亦不栉进士也，为贤契内助，必多善政可观。矧贤伉俪，患难离合，婚媾尤非寻常，岂有稿砧已双旌五马，尚不谋璧圆剑会者乎？余询琼儿，自悉鄙况。林泉杳寂，车马音稀，唯濡笔为贤契纪循良善绩也。珍重珍重！不尽欲言。”叶得书盥诵，对使流涕再拜，而后以莲舆迓夫人。拟觅浙中土产及玩好以报翁，女曰：“止，受人大恩，岂报以琐琐者耶！空函裁答可也。”至是夫妇方得朝夕称静好，鼓琴瑟焉。然女恒郁郁不为乐，问之，亦不语，临风弹珠泪。

    72

    After several years, all previous matters were settled, and the achievements were even greater. The old man said, "It can be done!" He presented a gift and ordered Ye to go to the capital. At the time of departure, the daughter wept and told Ye, "If you delay, you need not return to see your wife." Ye competed in the palace and won in one battle, being appointed as the governor of Kuaiji. He arrived at his post in a light carriage and was renowned for his exceptional judicial ability. The old man heard of this and was overjoyed. He packed for his daughter and sent servants to accompany her to her post. He wrote a letter to Ye, saying, "I hear that my esteemed son-in-law has been honored, which I am very pleased about. Leaving one's family to take up one's post as an official, serving the public and forgetting the home, is very commendable. But Jiong'er is also a scholar without combing her hair, and as your wife's helper, she will certainly bring about many good policies to observe. Especially you and your wife, who have endured hardships and separations, your marriage is particularly extraordinary. How could it be that the husband already has a double flag and five horses, yet still not plan to reunite like the meeting of jade and sword? I asked Jiong'er, and she told me about her humble circumstances. The mountains and streams are quiet and secluded, and the sounds of carriages and horses are rare. Only to write with a brush to record your good deeds. Take care, take care! There are many things I want to say." Ye received the letter and read it with reverence, wept and bowed twice to the messenger, and then used a lotus carriage to welcome his wife. He planned to find local products and fine items from Zhejiang to thank the old man, but the daughter said, "Stop, accepting great favors, how can one repay them with trivial things! A simple reply will suffice." From then on, the couple could enjoy a peaceful and harmonious life together, playing the qin and se. However, the daughter was always sad and not happy. When asked, she did not speak, and she shed tears while facing the wind.

    73

    顾性喜种花，浙中异卉咸罗植，衙宅似画图。又喜购金线孔翠等物，奁筐几满。暇偕婢采花上露造酒，缄于瓮，亦不饮。更督婢织金翠，为女子软甲，雕绘刻画，穷极鬼工，工蒇亦不着。明年，翁子璧，艳叶贵显，私盗藏金数巨万，走辇毂下，遍贿当道，即嫔嫱中贵，咸得孔方。试日，倩名手捉刀，战捷，胪唱中状头。时莽吉公子哈哈木榷，以怀挟被黜，愤甚。适璧仆夜行，犯金吾禁，捉去研讯，得贿赂大概。莽吉公子罗织疏劾，奉旨江璧坐科场舞弊下狱，论弃市。翁闻之，悲曰：“吾不意垂暮年，见儿子遭断头之惨。”函告叶，女勃然起曰：“今而后可以一举而两得之矣。”夤夜更急装，偕两婢，携酒荷甲，策马遁去。叶恐外扬，遭物议，喻家人秘不宣。

    Explanation

    73

    The individual had a nature that favored planting flowers, and all exotic blooms from Zhejiang were collected and planted, making his official residence resemble a painting. He also enjoyed purchasing luxurious items such as gold-threaded fabrics and precious stones, filling his boxes and containers almost to the brim. In his leisure time, he would accompany his maids to collect dew from the flowers to make wine, sealing it in jars but not drinking it himself. He further supervised the maids in weaving gold-threaded items to create soft armor for women, carving and painting with utmost divine craftsmanship, yet the completed items were not worn. The following year, his son, Ji Bi, rose to prominence and power, secretly amassing vast wealth. He traveled to the capital, bribing officials in power, including concubines and high-ranking officials, all of whom received money. On the day of the examinations, he hired famous hands to cheat, winning the battle and becoming the top scorer when the results were announced. At that time, the powerful noble Mang Ji's son, Ha Ha Mu, harshly criticized him for cheating, leading to his dismissal and extreme anger. Coincidentally, one of Ji Bi's servants violated the night curfew and was arrested and interrogated, revealing the extent of the bribery. Mang Ji's son fabricated a report and submitted it to accuse Ji Bi, who was then sentenced to public execution for cheating in the exams by imperial decree. The father, upon hearing this, sadly said, "I did not expect in my old age to see my son suffer the cruel fate of decapitation." He wrote a letter to inform his daughter, who suddenly stood up and said, "From now on, I can achieve two goals with one action." In the dead of night, she hastily prepared, accompanied by two maids, carrying wine and wearing armor, and fled on horseback. The daughter feared public exposure and criticism, instructing the family to keep it secret.

    74

    女竭蹶出关，知上之长姑四公主名伊拉布者，下嫁于锦兰国王，嗜饮，喜田猎。女趋长城下，确得公主常到处，裹糇粮，伏丛莽以俟。婢睹沙碛穷荒，时兴怨怼。女唯翘首盼。闻行人相戒曰：“是日王妃将出猎，慎勿散牧惊驾。”顷许，果有健儿数十，荷马冲过。随有艳妆宫人，乘骏马，舞剑戟者数十；挟弓矢，持火器者又数十；锦旗如云，裹一黄衣美人，年约三十许，策紫骝马，按辔行缓缓。女知是公主，本兔伏，突鹘起，前趋。将卒遽攫主婢，掷马前，宝刀环粉头。主见其婉柔，不忍诛，唯含笑问：“何来？”女本善蒙古番绎语，至是神色不惊，裣衽启奏曰：“小女子日在庇覆，恨无报称，谨以葵忱，手酿千娇百媚酒，手织金翠如意通心甲，奉献娘娘，伏唯寿考千万。”言已呈上。

    Explanation

    74

    A woman struggled to leave the pass, knowing that the eldest granddaughter of the emperor, the fourth princess named Iralu, was married to the king of Jilin, who loved drinking and enjoyed hunting. The woman hurried to the foot of the Great Wall, finding the places where the princess often went, wrapped food, and hid in the bushes waiting. The maid saw the barren desert and sometimes felt resentful. The woman only looked up in anticipation. She heard travelers warn each other saying, "On this day, the queen will go hunting; be careful not to let the livestock scatter and frighten the carriage." After a while, indeed there were dozens of strong men, carrying horses rushing by. Followed by dozens of beautiful palace women, riding fast horses and wielding swords and spears, there were also dozens carrying bows and arrows, holding firearms. With banners like clouds, wrapped in a yellow robe, a beautiful woman around thirty years old, riding a purple steed, slowly controlling the reins. The woman knew it was the princess, originally crouching like a rabbit, suddenly standing up like a falcon, rushing forward. The soldiers suddenly grabbed the princess and her maid, throwing them in front of the horse, the precious sword circling the maid's head. The princess saw her gentle and soft, unable to kill her, only smiling and asking, "Where do you come from?" The woman originally spoke the Mongolian dialect, at this point, her expression was not startled, she bowed and said, "A humble woman under your protection, regretting not having a way to repay, respectfully presents with sincere heart, handcrafted wine of a thousand charming and beautiful flavors, handwoven golden and green如意 armor, to the noble lady, wishing for a long life." After speaking, she presented it.

    75

    主酌其酒，则香沁心脾，甘回齿颊，曰：“美哉酿也！”衣其甲，则身段符合，光彩烛云霄。马上女子齐声呼千岁，曰：“美哉织也！”携回宫闱，宫女教授，日渐稔熟，泣请遄回。主诧曰：“儿原是中国人耶？何故远跋涉，尽此孝心？小娇生曷明言，我老人当为女尽力。”女伏叩数十，称死罪者再，然后缕述所苦，诡云璧为其兄，遭莽侍御父子冤陷，求主援手，当能揭钵赦宾枷也。主曰：“吾道何大事，是区区者，尚值得如许耶？”即传懿旨，振师旅入中国，救江状元。命女自归。不日主驾将到。上悚惧，亲出迎迓。盖主在宫中时，上尚冲龄，左右谮青宫，赖主时调护。然貌美心慈而性刚烈，归宁求不遂，必诟谇遂始已。见上，遽问曰：“陛下亦知新状元江某，为老身螟蛉子乎？何绝无香火情，忍以鳌头佳士陷缧绁？”上默然。

    Explanation

    75

    The emperor tasted the wine, and its fragrance permeated his heart and lungs, its sweetness lingering on his teeth and cheeks. He said, "How fine the wine is!" He then wore the armor, and it fit his body perfectly, its radiance illuminating the clouds. The women on horseback shouted "Long live the emperor!" and said, "How fine the weaving is!" He brought the wine back to the inner palace, where the palace ladies taught him, and he gradually became familiar with it, weeping and pleading to return quickly. The emperor was surprised and said, "Are you originally a Chinese person? Why travel so far, showing such filial piety? My dear daughter, why not speak plainly? I, an old man, will do my best for you." The woman fell to her knees and bowed dozens of times, confessing her crimes twice, and then recounted her suffering in detail, cunningly claiming that Bi was her brother, who had suffered unjust persecution by the father and son of the tyrannical official. She sought the emperor's assistance, saying that she would be able to lift the pot and release the guest's fetters. The emperor said, "What great matter is this for me? Is such a trivial matter worth so much?" He immediately issued a decree, mobilizing the army and entering China to rescue the top scholar Jiang. He ordered the woman to return on her own. Soon, the emperor's carriage was approaching. The emperor was startled and personally went out to greet him. When the emperor was in the palace, the emperor was still a child, and those around him slandered the crown prince, relying on the emperor's timely protection. However, the emperor was beautiful in appearance, kind-hearted, but with a fierce temper. If she failed to obtain permission to return home, she would continue to scold until her request was granted. When she saw the emperor, she quickly asked, "Do you also know that the new top scholar Jiang is my foster son? Why have you completely severed ties, and why are you willing to imprison the best scholar in the imperial examination?" The emperor remained silent.

    76

    主立求诏出狱，与上面试真才。时左右均暗奉懿旨，为璧设倩无破绽。策成，条对精详，藻辞伟丽。上抚掌曰：“古今原无白腹状元，何物莽吉奴，敢妄饶舌！”降旨复璧官，封三代，出授两江监察道廉访使。左右更以莽吉子哈哈木榷怀挟入告，上震怒，籍其家，得数巨万。莽吉充云南军，死于道。斩哈哈木榷于市。主始归国。时叶生独居悬盼。一夕，掩灯坐，闻中庭履声细碎，女已偕婢入室，握手笑曰：“郎君别来无恙乎？妾回矣。”叶喜，揽入怀，问曰：“卿飞仙耶，剑侠耶？不然，何如是之神也？”女曰：“否，盖舍此，实无法可以抱恩怨耳。”江翁闻璧再生，不知所以开汤网之故。时正祝嘏，宾客满室，女忽锦衣跃马来，裣衽称觞，跪拜如礼，告翁曰：“人贵知足。”翁曰：“诺。”即日命璧上疏称病，解组归田园。

    Explanation

    76

    The emperor ordered the release from prison and personally tested true talent. At that time, the officials around him were secretly following the empress's instructions, setting up a flawless test for Bi. The essay was completed, the answers were precise and detailed, and the language was ornate and splendid. The emperor clapped his hands and said, "There has never been a true scholar who is ignorant. What kind of person is Mangjinu, to dare speak without knowledge!" He issued an edict to restore Bi's official position, bestowed titles on three generations, and appointed him as the Censorate Commissioner of the Two Jiangs. The officials also reported that Mangjizi and Hammuqiu were harboring ill intentions. The emperor was furious, seized their property, and obtained several million in wealth. Mangji was sent to the Yunnan army and died on the way. Hammuqiu was beheaded in the marketplace. The emperor finally returned to the country. At that time, Ye lived alone in anxious anticipation. One night, he sat in the dark, hearing the sound of footsteps in the courtyard. The woman had already entered the room with her maid and smiled, saying, "How have you been, my lord? I have returned." Ye was delighted, embracing her, and asked, "Are you a fairy, or a swordswoman? Otherwise, how could you be so miraculous?" She replied, "No, but without this, there would be no way to repay my gratitude and resentment." Old Master Jiang heard that Bi had been reborn but did not know why he was freed from the net. At that time, he was celebrating, with guests filling the room. The woman suddenly appeared in brocade, leaping onto a horse, and bowed to present a cup, kneeling and bowing according to etiquette, saying to Master Jiang, "It is valuable to be content." Master Jiang said, "Agreed." On the same day, Bi was ordered to submit a petition citing illness, resigning from office and returning to his fields.

    77

    迦陵配
    　　
    　　钟离笠乾寺，古刹也。老衲临风，时为游客说迦陵生故事。其先住持懋公，精梵律，尤喜擢人才。寿春孙主政，曾赠公有“说法鬼神环尘尾，怜才英俊集龙头”一联。法弟某髡，则唯唱佛曲，喜逢迎，品行远不逮懋矣。公偶扶杖水次，见中流浮一木板来，上卧小儿，白如瓠，啼呱呱。有比丘尼来此湔裙，以竹枝取抱入怀，意将作螟蛉。

    Explanation

    77

    Final Translation

    The Match of Kālīṅga

    The Temple of Zhōnglí Lì Qián, an ancient monastery. An old monk, standing in the breeze, would often tell visitors the story of Kālīṅga. The former abbot, Master Mào, was deeply versed in the Vinaya (Buddhist monastic rules) and particularly enjoyed nurturing talent. The high-ranking official from Shòuchūn, Master Sūn, once presented him with a couplet: "Speaking the Dharma, ghosts and spirits gather around the dust tail; pitying the talented and handsome, they congregate at the head of the group." His fellow monk, a certain bald monk, however, only sang Buddhist chants, was fond of flattery, and his moral character fell far short of Master Mào's. One day, Master Mào, leaning on his staff by the water, saw a piece of wood floating downstream, on which lay a small child, pale as a gourd, crying "waa waa." A bhikkhunī (female Buddhist monastic) came to the stream to wash her skirt, picked up the child with a bamboo branch, and held it in her arms, intending to adopt it as her foster child.

    78

    公合掌曰：“善哉善哉！然尺许襁褓物，非优婆夷所宜，曷布施老僧？”尼便略审视，付公曰：“此儿好骨气，读书可成名宿，入道可作飞仙，唯皈依佛则终一不了汉也。公善抚之！”言已，尼突不见。公携归，庸乳媪哺养，名曰小拾得，是则迦陵生之第一名也。稍长，即能辩之无，有宿慧。公以为辩才后身，预为他日衣钵计。年五岁，即为削发，授比邱戒，且筵师教之读，旋通六经，考内典，泛览诸家，莫不了了。生年十三，懋公病，将证涅，弥留时，以生托某髡曰：“是即我之藐孤也，祈阿叔爱怜之。”某髡诺，公目遂瞑。生踊号恸，如丧所生。

    Explanation

    78

    The monk clasped his hands and said, "Well done! Well done! Yet this small infant, though precious, is not suitable for a lay follower of the Buddha. Why not give it to an old monk?" The nun glanced at the child and handed it to the monk, saying, "This child has excellent potential. He can become famous through reading and enter the Dao to become a flying immortal. Only by relying on the Buddha will he avoid being an 'unenlightened person.' Please take good care of him!" After speaking, the nun suddenly disappeared. The monk took the child home, hired a wet nurse to feed him, and named him "Xiao Shide," which was the first name given to him. As the child grew older, he could distinguish right from wrong and possessed innate wisdom. The monk believed that the child was a reincarnation of someone with eloquent speech and planned for his future role as a spiritual successor. At the age of five, the child was tonsured and given the monastic precepts. He was then taught to read by a teacher and quickly mastered the Six Classics, studied Buddhist scriptures, and had a broad understanding of various schools of thought. When the child was thirteen, the abbot fell ill and was about to attain Nirvana. In his final moments, he entrusted the child to a senior monk, saying, "This is my young orphan. Please show him love and care." The senior monk agreed, and the abbot's eyes closed. The child jumped and wailed in grief, as if he had lost his own father.

    79

    某髡渐忘兄托，日肆摧残，颇有逐侄意，而未忍出诸口。生憨痴，不喜习梵呗，日以书画自娱。某益厌恶。一日，重新殿壁，始加圬垩，皎洁如银。某拟倩俗工画。生技痒，潜磨墨汁斗许，乘某出，登台握管，风飒飒，挥洒成荷花，环四壁。跃而大笑，曰：“此功德池中，清静菩萨身也！”某归，见其尚不俗，詈亦旋已。适有李太守，新任顺昌，挈眷自江南来，赁寺之西厢，勾当公事。闲步殿上，观西天像，突见墨荷，惊为八大再来人。

    Explanation

    79

    The monk gradually forgot his elder brother's trust and began to mistreat the nephew daily, harboring thoughts of expelling him, though he could not bring himself to say it. The boy was simple-minded, uninterested in learning Buddhist chants, and spent his days entertaining himself with painting and calligraphy. The monk grew increasingly disgusted. One day, he decided to repaint the temple walls, starting with a fresh layer of plaster, making them bright and clear like silver. He intended to hire ordinary craftsmen to paint them. The boy, eager to use his skills, secretly ground a bucket of ink. When the monk left, he climbed onto the platform, took up his brush, and with the wind rustling around him, freely painted lotus flowers around the four walls. He jumped up and laughed loudly, saying, "These are the lotus flowers in the pool of merit, the bodies of the Pure and Tranquil Bodhisattvas!" When the monk returned and saw the paintings, he found them not vulgar at all, and his cursing stopped immediately. Just then, Governor Li, newly appointed to Shunchang, arrived with his family from Jiangnan, renting the western wing of the temple to handle official business. He walked leisurely on the hall, observing the images of the Western Heaven, and suddenly saw the black lotus paintings, astonished to see them as the work of a reincarnated being from the Eight Great Bodhisattvas.

    80

    问谁之大手笔，髡某以生对。问法腊，以实告，且述其萍泛拾得因缘。太守急命呼至，则翩翩玉立，英致洒然，顶足童童，气则蔼蔼。问“菡萏是汝手笔乎？”揖而对曰：“然。”问：“能对乎？”曰：“能。”即出首联，曰：“壁上荷花和尚画，”生应声对曰：“月中桂子贵人攀。”守大惊服，因谓髡某曰：“汝勿须此子，曷以多金易于我？”某诺。即携生至顺昌，潜为蓄发，因抱邓攸戚，即蓄为儿，从李姓，名琛，字美玉，此则迦陵生之第二名也。

    Explanation

    80

    Who wrote the significant piece? I (the speaker) responded that it was me. When asked about my legal or official status, I answered truthfully and also described how I came to be involved in this matter, using a metaphor of floating on water and picking up connections. The magistrate urgently ordered someone to be called. The person stood gracefully and elegantly, with a youthful appearance and a warm, gentle demeanor. The magistrate asked, "Was the lotus flower (菡萏) written by you?" The person bowed and answered affirmatively. "Can you match it?" The person responded, "Yes." Immediately, the person recited the first couplet: "The lotus on the wall, painted by a monk." The person responded instantly with: "The osmanthus in the moon, plucked by a noble." The magistrate was greatly surprised and impressed. He then told me (the speaker): "You don't need this child, why not exchange him for a large sum of money?" I agreed. Immediately, I took the person to Shunchang and secretly grew their hair. Then, I adopted the person as a son, giving them the surname Li, the name Chen, and the courtesy name Meiyu. This is the second name of the person known as Jialing.

    81

    守妻本艳妾僭正位者，颇恶生，年余有妊，恐生他日碍真儿，益讽婢子小鹊谮生短，守笑置之，惟延师授生举子业，勖最殷而功亦大进。妻闻之，益愤，时于阃内施恶声，渐自操仗挞假子，逐之者屡矣。守度其终不相能，呼生于无人处，泣曰：“汝从来处来，仍从去处去，有千金相赠，了我父子缘。此去仍为僧，抑为儒，均自便，非阿翁所能计及也。好自为之，前途郑重！”生泣不敢受。坚与之，始稽首再拜，嗒焉出门，罔知去就。

    Explanation

    81

    The wife, who is jealous of the beautiful concubine who has usurped her position, strongly dislikes the son. After a year, she becomes pregnant and fears that the son will hinder the true son (the child she gave birth to). She further hints to the maid, Xiao Que, to slander the son's shortcomings. The husband, however, only invites a teacher to teach the son to prepare for the imperial examinations. The encouragement is very earnest, and the son's progress is also great. The wife, upon hearing this, becomes even more furious. She occasionally spreads bad words within the inner quarters and gradually takes the whip in hand to beat the adopted son, expelling him many times. The husband considers that they will finally not be able to get along and calls the son to a place where no one is around. Weeping, he says, "You came from where you came, return to where you will go. I have a thousand gold pieces to give to you, to end our father-son relationship. After this, you can still become a monk or a scholar, both are convenient. This is not something the father-in-law can plan. Take good care of yourself and take your future seriously." The son weeps and does not dare to accept the money. The husband persists in giving it to him, and only then does the son kowtow twice, bow deeply, and leave in a dazed state, not knowing what to do next.

    82

    途遇乡人某，同舟楫，瞰其多金，诱习贸易，辗转耗尽，仅余守囊钱，茫茫然，计唯仍返钟离。至则师叔物故，接住者为他族，遂亦赁西厢，安行李，布笔砚，仍事咿唔，不敢懈。时掩卷痛哭，曰：“逐我者，非太守也。遇我厚，望我深，世有如太守大德者乎？”本寺本孙主政家庙，主政致仕家居，斗山望重，偶自寿春来，见生玉貌，且阅课程，惊为远大才。询其姓氏乡贯，初不敢对，询再三，始和泪述由来。且恸曰：“身世不偶，勿释勿儒，独来独去，尚未遑定自家姓氏，何以生为？”主政筹思良久，庄色而言曰：“子自不知耳。

    Explanation

    82

    On his journey, the youth encountered a fellow villager named someone, and they shared a boat together. Observing that the fellow villager had much money, the youth was enticed to learn trade. After wandering and exhausting himself, he was left with only the money in his bag, feeling aimlessly lost. He calculated that he could only return to Zhongli. Upon arriving, he found that his uncle, the former head of the temple, had passed away, and the one who took over was of another clan. He then rented the western wing of the temple, arranged his belongings, set up his brush and inkstone, and continued to study quietly, never slacking off. At times, he would cover his book and weep bitterly, saying, "The one who drove me away was not the governor. He treated me kindly and expected much of me. Is there anyone in the world like the governor's great virtue?" The temple originally belonged to the family of the former governor Sun, who had retired and lived at home. He had a high reputation in the region and happened to come from Shouchun. Seeing the youth's beautiful appearance and reviewing his curriculum, he was amazed by the youth's great potential. When asked about his name, surname, and hometown, the youth initially dared not answer. After being questioned repeatedly, he finally told the story with tears, saying, "My fate has been unlucky. I have neither released myself nor become a scholar, coming alone and going alone, and I have not yet had the time to settle my own name and surname. What is the point of living?" The former governor thought for a long time and said solemnly, "You do not know yourself."

    83

    仆远族某，向居湖田，亦穷儒，暮年纳妾，生子甫周月，某逝，妾远醮，恐其子为累，即置子于木板上，听其浮沉，是即汝也。论支派，汝尚我之犹子。”生信为真，遽再拜，呼为叔，侍膝下，作燕子依人状。主政遂为之易姓曰孙，名曰，字曰风萍，是则迦陵生之第三名也。明日，衣冠诣谢。主政约略告以某某伯叔，某某兄弟行。益奖励，许为阅削诗文，俾应试。惟详询父母墓道，则漫应之，盖诡托以安其心，实无处供指南也。生年十有七，瞬又郡试，主政为生诣广文廪膳处乞印结。

    Explanation

    83

    My distant relative, Mr. So-and-so, once lived in Lake Field and was also a poor scholar. In his old age, he took a concubine who gave birth to a son just one month after their marriage. Mr. So-and-so then passed away, and the concubine remarried out of fear that the child would be a burden. She placed the child on a wooden board and let him float or sink, which is how you came to be. According to the lineage, you are still my younger brother. The main character then renamed you, changing your surname to Sun, your given name to Fengping, and your courtesy name to Fengping, making you the third name of the family of Jialing. The next day, dressed in formal attire, I visited to express my gratitude. The main character briefly told me about the various uncles and cousins, as well as the brothers. He encouraged and praised you, agreeing to review and edit your poetry and essays to help you prepare for the exams. However, when asked about the graves of your parents, you answered vaguely, as the main character had fabricated this story to comfort your heart, and there was no actual place to guide you. You were seventeen years old when the time for the prefectural examination arrived, and the main character went to the Guangwen Linshancu office to request an official seal and certification for you.

    84

    问伊谁，即大言曰：“谁不识孙为某之族侄耶？”众遂信。匆匆补县试后，暗嘱曰：“临场第草草，万勿过经营，恐致攻讦，反偾事。”生敬领诺。比属试题下，生小心就范，竟无一字。不得已，直抒所见，拼决裂。太守黄公，操衡文冰鉴，自诩老眼。见生文击节，拔置冠军。榜揭，则童子沸腾，疑从天外飞来者。翌日初复，太守坐堂皇，隶呼生名，无应者，再三呼，仍不应。太守勃然，目广文曰：“二千石非目无瞳子者，冠军人竟不至，究竟因何？是必有枪冒等弊，恐虚堂镜，不能遁形，故逸耳。然则非广文过欤？”广文推诿，廪膳以主政对。问主政，则顷正出游他郡矣。

    Explanation

    84

    Who was he? He immediately boasted, saying, "Who doesn’t recognize that Sun is a cousin of someone?" The crowd believed him. After hastily taking the county examination, he secretly instructed, "On the field, just hastily, do not overwork, for fear of causing accusations, which could instead ruin the matter." The student respectfully accepted and promised. When the exam questions were given, the student carefully conformed, but ended up writing not a single word. In desperation, he directly expressed what he saw, willing to break. Governor Huang, holding the balance of the text like an ice mirror, proudly claimed sharp eyes. Seeing the student’s work, he clapped hands in appreciation and placed it at the top. When the results were announced, the students were in a frenzy, wondering if it had come from the sky. The next day, during the initial review, the governor sat in the grand hall, and the attendant called out the student’s name, but there was no response. He called again and again, but still no response. The governor became furious, looking at the scholar and saying, "A high-ranking official is not one without eyes, yet the champion did not show up—what is the reason? There must be some cheating or other problems. Fear that the empty hall mirror cannot hide it, so he escaped. Then is it not the scholar’s fault?" The scholar tried to shift the blame, saying that the head of the examination office was responsible. When asked about the head of the examination office, it was revealed that he had just left for another prefecture.

    85

    太守怒益剧，咸屈膝曰：“请太尊且试孙以下者，准于日内获若献公庭。”守诺。广文即挈斗级多人，肩舆往侦诸寺，则书箧宛然，其人乌有。壁上墨渖淋漓，留一诗曰：一波才落一波生，旅馆频惊梦不成。白眼看他人世险，黄金散尽我身轻。浇愁惯借杯中物，惹祸翻嫌榜上名。屈指归期应不远，八公山下有疑兵。郡之西鄙，距十里，本有八公山，为淮南古迹。读其尾句，疑潜彼处，往视，果趺坐石洞中，痴如木鸡。扶之返，问之默然。

    Explanation

    85

    The magistrate's anger grew more intense, and they all knelt and said, "Please allow me to test the younger ones, and ensure that they will be captured and brought to the public court within the day." The magistrate agreed. The scholar immediately took a sedan chair with several attendants and went to inspect the temples. The bookcases were still there, but the person was nowhere to be found. The walls were covered with wet ink, and he left behind a poem saying:

    One wave has just subsided, another rises.
    In the inn, I am often startled awake, unable to sleep.
    I look at the world with contempt, seeing its dangers.
    Having spent all my gold, I feel lighter.
    I am used to drowning my sorrows in alcohol.
    I avoid trouble, but it finds me anyway.
    Counting the days, my return should not be far off.
    There are suspicious troops beneath Mount Baxia.

    In the western border of the prefecture, ten miles away, there is originally Mount Baxia, an ancient site of Huainan. Reading the last line, I suspected he was hiding there. When I went to look, indeed, he was sitting cross-legged in a stone cave, stupid like a wooden chicken. I helped him return, and when asked, he remained silent.

    86

    薄暮，棘门洞启，唤生入，太守已严具五刑以俟。及见生，温婉循循，知非儇薄子。略审诘，即伏地哀叩，历述生平。问何故潜逃，曰：“童子谨遵主政公谕，实不知无籍遭攻讦者是何等罪，故逸耳。”太守恍然，甚怜惜。既而飞寸楮下，曰：“非面试不足信。”生即就寸烛下，立成佳制，如宿构。再试他题，益工。守且阅且赏曰：“惜子他去，否则又冠军矣！国家取真才，何曾盖以资格限。当来应二复，容擢汝。”试事毕，欲取置第一人，格于例，遂列第二。

    Explanation

    86

    As dusk approached, the gate of the thorn barrier was fully open, and the governor summoned the young man to enter. The governor had already prepared the five punishments in anticipation. Upon seeing the young man, the governor was gentle and considerate, realizing he was not a frivolous youth. After a brief interrogation, the young man knelt and wept, recounting his life story. The governor asked why he had fled, and the young man replied, "I strictly followed the instructions of the chief administrator. I did not know what kind of crime someone without official records who was attacked was guilty of, so I fled." The governor suddenly understood and felt great pity for him. Soon after, the governor sent a quick message, saying, "Without an interview, I cannot believe your abilities." The young man immediately produced a fine work under the light of a small candle, as if it were pre-written. He was tested again with other questions, and his performance improved even more. The governor read and praised him, saying, "I regret that you are leaving. Otherwise, you would have been the champion! The country selects true talents, never limiting them by qualifications. When you come for the second level of the examination, I will surely recommend you." After the examination was finished, the governor wanted to place him first but was limited by regulations, so he was placed second.

    87

    主政归，携谒太守，又为守详述其曾作沙弥事。太守笑曰：“第能院试获隽，我当玉成一佳话。”请毕其说，曰：“仆莅任时，道出毗陵，内子诣惠泉尼庵酬香愿，爱一雏尼，貌艳而慧，且精律吕，解吟咏，亦老尼所遗，茫无来历者。观主因尚守雌，居奇货，内子不吝，购之归，名曰巧巧。顷已及笄，意欲配贤阮为妇，不几如佛经之迦陵共命鸟耶？”主政拜谢曰：“且看渠院试若何，倘如期望，求应金诺也。”院试揭晓日，太守傍徨中庭，蹀躞不已。

    Explanation

    87

    The governor returned and brought with him to pay respects to the prefect, detailing his past experiences as a novice monk. The prefect smiled and said, "If he can pass the provincial examination with distinction, I will make a beautiful story." Upon hearing this, the prefect asked for the full story and said, "When I took office, I passed through Pi Ling. My wife visited the Huiquan nunnery to fulfill her incense vow and fell in love with a young nun. She was beautiful and intelligent, and she was also versed in music theory and capable of reciting poetry. She was left by the old nun, of unknown origin. The abbess still adheres to her principles and treasures her rarity. My wife was not stingy and bought her back, naming her Qiaoqiao. She is now of marriageable age, and I wish to marry her to the virtuous Ruan as a wife. Wouldn't that be like the pair of birds in the Buddhist scripture?" The governor bowed in gratitude and said, "Let's see how he performs in the exam. If it meets expectations, I will fulfill my promise." On the day the results of the provincial examination were announced, the prefect wandered in the courtyard, walking back and forth incessantly.

    88

    夫人携尼婢出，蓦见，惊询之，应曰：“孙家小沙弥也。”夫人轩渠。婢不知前日事，亦掩口笑葫芦。突捷音至，守急问讯曰：“其孙家小沙弥耶？”左右对曰：“然。且冠全军。”守为之舞蹈大乐。明日，生雀顶青衿来谢。叩中庭。守喜曰：“汝来耶？”立命更章服，妆巧巧出，与交拜，成嘉礼。生辞曰：“身无立锥，家无四壁，安对芙蓉？”曰：“为汝筹之久矣。”呼鼓乐采舆二，送小夫妇归第，至则画室萦纡，几榻明洁，青庐中所需者，罔不措备，皆太守功德也。

    Explanation

    88

    The lady took her maid and a Buddhist nun out, and suddenly they saw someone. She was surprised and asked who it was, and they replied, "It's a young monk from the Sun family." The lady laughed heartily. The maid, unaware of the previous events, also laughed while covering her mouth. Suddenly, news of victory arrived, and the governor hurriedly inquired, "Is it the young monk from the Sun family?" Those around him answered, "Yes, and he has led the entire army." The governor was overjoyed and danced with great delight. The next day, the groom, dressed in the attire of a young scholar, came to express his gratitude. He knocked at the courtyard entrance. The governor, pleased, said, "You have come?" Immediately, he ordered a change of clothes, and the bride, beautifully dressed, came out. They bowed to each other, and the grand ceremony was completed. The groom modestly said, "I have no place to stand, and my home has no walls; how can I face such beauty?" The governor replied, "I have been planning for you for a long time." He called for two decorated carriages with music and sent the young couple back to their home. Upon arrival, they found the drawing room winding and elegant, the tables and chairs clean and bright, and all the necessities for the wedding ceremony were thoroughly prepared. All of this was due to the governor's virtue and merit.

    89

    越两日，哭告于懋公塔，泣谢于主政公门，函谢顺昌守，归写懋公李守黄守夫妇像，虔祀之如神。主政旋捐馆，生为之服心丧。黄太守迁豫之廉访使，走送三百里。迂顺昌，省假父，得厚赠归。归则闭双扉，对丽偶；生每作书画，署款必曰“风萍”，或曰“昔美玉”，或又曰“当年拾得子，”老不忘也。且深知孙亦非己真姓也。女笑曰：“郎名忒琐琐，黄公曾以我两人比佛家迦陵鸟，郎盍名曰迦陵生，妾即名曰迦陵女？”生大喜，如其说。闺中时以围棋猜谜赌酒角韵为乐。

    Explanation

    89

    After two days, he mourned and reported at the stupa of Master Mao, and cried and thanked at the residence of the high-ranking official. He sent a letter of thanks to the governor of Shunchang, and upon returning home, he wrote a portrait of the couple, Master Mao, Governor Li, and Governor Huang, and worshiped them devoutly as if they were gods. Shortly after, the high-ranking official passed away, and he observed a private mourning period for him. Governor Huang was promoted to the position of Inspector of Henan, and he traveled three hundred miles to send him off. He took a detour through Shunchang to visit his foster father and received generous gifts before returning home. Upon his return, he closed the double doors and faced the beautiful couple; whenever he created paintings or calligraphy, he would sign his name as "Wind and Reed," or "Formerly the Jade," or "The Child Found Long Ago," never forgetting these names. He also deeply understood that Sun was not his true surname. The woman laughed and said, "Your name is too trivial. Master Huang once compared us to the mythical birds of Buddhism. Why not call you 'Bird of Paradise' and me 'Bird of Paradise'?" The man was very pleased and followed her suggestion. At home, they enjoyed playing go, guessing riddles, betting on drinks, and competing in poetry.

    90

    生间习制艺，女即阻挠，曰：“俗极矣。”曰：“我亦知其俗，然读书人非借文章吐气，何以报知己？”曰：“是诚如茧之自缚，蛾之自投，幸俗障不深，急需解脱。妾非夸诩，郎以妾为妇，即胜于封万里候也。”女本工琴，因授以弹琴谱，使昕夕领会。一曰春水舣槎曲，觉清风泠泠，挟襟袖作凌波舞也。二曰清夜闻钟曲，觉梵王宫殿，如在枕畔也。三曰穷途自伤曲，觉风尘肮脏，落魄人歧路徘徊也。四曰水穷云起曲，觉别有天地，打桨桃花深处也。五曰采凤双飞曲，觉云路迢迢，将携手而上霄汉也。

    Explanation

    90

    In private, he studied the literary examination system, and the woman immediately objected, saying, "It's extremely vulgar." He replied, "I know it's vulgar too, but scholars must use their writings to express themselves, how else can they repay their benefactors?" She said, "It's truly like a silkworm binding itself, a moth flying into a flame, fortunately the veil of vulgarity is not too deep, and we need to urgently find a way to free ourselves. I'm not boasting, but if you marry me, it would be better than being granted a title of a marquis." The woman was skilled in playing the qin, so she taught him the score for playing the qin, instructing him to understand it thoroughly day and night. The first melody was "Spring Water and Docking Boat," which evoked a cool, refreshing breeze, as if dancing gracefully on the waves. The second was "Clear Night Bell Sound," which made one feel as if the palace of the Buddha was right beside the pillow. The third was "At a Dead End and Self-Pity," which evoked feelings of being at a dead end and self-pity, as if a person was wandering aimlessly at a crossroads. The fourth was "Water Dries Up and Clouds Rise," which evoked a sense of change and a new realm, as if paddling a boat deep into the peach blossoms. The fifth was "Two Phoenixes Flying Together," which evoked a sense of a harmonious and joyful relationship, as if ascending to the heavens together.

    91

    六曰仙心无恙曲，觉海涛洞，蓦证三生也。更请其余，曰：“只此六章，可示世上人耳。”生学之年余，竟能与女对鼓。绿窗静，雏婢焚香，逸韵若，真不知为人间天上。一夕，君弦断，女大惊曰：“殆矣！昔黄公以我两人为共命鸟，今作比翼禽飞去可乎？妾嫁尚未一归宁，郎送妾去，借逋红羊劫，何如？”曰：“卿前对黄夫人云无来历，今何又有家？”曰：“嘻！世岂有无来历者，特一履尘，即忘却真面目耳！”生不深信，意颇犹夷。女以丹药一粒，使吞服。

    Explanation

    91

    Six is the tune of the unharmed immortal heart, awakening to the waves of the sea of wisdom, suddenly certifying three lives. Further, she asked, "Only these six chapters can be shown to people of the world." After a year of learning, he was able to play the drum in harmony with her. The green window was quiet, and the young maid burned incense. The free and elegant rhythm was like a breeze, and one truly did not know whether it was on earth or in heaven. One night, the master's string broke, and the woman was greatly alarmed, saying, "It is dangerous! In the past, Master Huang considered us as two birds sharing one life; now, can we fly away as paired birds?" She continued, "I have not yet returned home even once after my marriage; if you send me away, will it be like borrowing from the red sheep calamity, what should I do?" He replied, "You said before to Madam Huang that there was no origin; why do you have a family now?" She responded, "Ha! How can there be someone without an origin? It is just that one steps into the dust and forgets their true face!" The man did not deeply believe it, and his thoughts were somewhat ambiguous. The woman gave a single pill of elixir to make him swallow.

    92

    生忽哑然笑曰：“咦！卿真欲归耶？某亦随之逝矣。”仆婢问娘子家在何处，曰：“远甚，不愿从行者请遣之。”翌即束妆携两婢，各跨一卫，冉冉向东去。近有钟离人自海上采药回者，云海上有桫椤岛，产药最夥，石径崎岖，颇不良于行。一日甫舣舟，忽见迦陵两婢子，蓬头赤足，走岛上如飞，追之莫能及。懊侬氏曰：迦陵生之遭遇，不为不苦；迦陵生之来去，不为不奇。独是浩劫将至，迦陵女能预知之，而预避之。卿其仙耶？吾尝谓佛子断人欲，真苦恼众生。若举世尽趋其教，恐佛种断矣。今观迦陵仙偶，益信曲躬之树不诬，吞针之戏不恕。

    Explanation

    92

    The woman suddenly laughed and said, "Huh! Are you really going to return? I will also follow and pass away." The servants and maids asked where the young lady's home was, and she replied, "It is very far away; those who do not wish to accompany us, please send them away." The next day, she quickly dressed and took two maids, each riding a guard, and slowly departed eastward.
