A person from Zhongli who returned from the sea after gathering herbs said that there is a Sāluó island in the sea, which produces the most herbs, but the stone path is rugged and not suitable for travel. One day, just as they moored their boat, they suddenly saw two maids of Kālíng, with disheveled hair and bare feet, running across the island like the wind, and could not catch up with them. The author, An Nong, said: The experiences of Kālíng Shēng are not without suffering; the coming and going of Kālíng Shēng is not without strangeness. What is strange is that a great calamity is imminent, and Kālíng Nǚ can foresee it and avoid it. Are you a fairy? I once said that the children of the Buddha, by cutting off human desires, truly cause suffering to the masses. If the whole world were to follow this teaching, I fear the lineage of the Buddha would be cut off. Now observing the fairy couple of Kālíng, I further believe that the story of the bent tree is not false, and the act of swallowing needles is not unjust.

    93

    刘子仪膏药
    　　
    　　国初，吾乡有刘公子仪，老学究也。授蒙为业，困顿不支。货所居大厦，得值，另卜城北临街一廛，内居妻孥，外作小贸易。公性旷达诚笃，不善较辎珠。年余，母金耗尽，依旧阮囊。顾新居仅数椽，蔽风雨而已。庭中环种野药材，名参三七，蔓延阶砌。公恶之，欲除去，植花草。一夜梦黄衣叟，指而告之，曰：“此良药也，和铅粉桐油熬成膏，能疗诸毒疮，芟刈何为？”公醒即谨志，检阅药性编，诚然。心喜，急购粉与油，苦无药灶。适有女丐在门，筐有小铜釜，短柄三足，以百钱购得之。又购得小铁炉，居然成就，碾锉虔修。

    Explanation

    93

    Liu Ziyi's Ointment

    In the early years of the state, my hometown had a man named Liu Ziyi, an old and somewhat pedantic scholar. He taught as a profession but was in dire straits, unable to sustain himself. He sold his large house and, with the proceeds, chose a new location near the street in the northern part of the city. Inside, he lived with his wife and children; outside, he engaged in small-scale trade. Liu Ziyi was open-minded, honest, and sincere, not concerned with minor financial matters. After a year, his savings were depleted, and he remained in poverty. His new home consisted of only a few rooms, just enough to shelter him from the wind and rain. The courtyard was surrounded by wild medicinal herbs, specifically Panax notoginseng, which spread over the steps and pathways. Liu Ziyi disliked this and wanted to remove them, planting flowers and grass instead. One night, he dreamed of an elderly man dressed in yellow clothing, who pointed to the herbs and told him, "These are good herbs. When mixed with lead powder and tung oil and cooked into a paste, they can treat various poisonous sores. Why remove them?" Upon waking, Liu Ziyi took the advice seriously and checked the pharmacological records, which confirmed its accuracy. Pleased, he urgently bought the powder and oil but was troubled by the lack of a medicine stove. Just then, a beggar woman came to the door, carrying a small copper pot with a short handle and three legs. He bought it for a hundred coins and also purchased a small iron stove. Surprisingly, he succeeded in making the ointment, grinding and preparing the ingredients with reverence.

    94

    先觅丐之有患者小试，良验。明年春，久雨，城中水尺余。夏则奇旱，毒日蒸腾，沙煎石铄，农贾无老稚，咸患潮湿，医家术穷，惟是膏能愈。由此得值甚丰，颇给朝夕。然公性最善，虽深夜，丐者以一文来市药，必起而与之。一夕，甫就枕，风雨满街，忽闻剥啄甚急。枕上询阿谁，曰“乞人市药者”。公披衣急起，启双扉，一丐者蹩而入。疮在左股上，大如钱。公详视巨细，然后折纸，就炉头摊与之，量必符所患。讵药成，而疮忽大如盆；更之，疮忽大如瓯；再更之，忽大如巨盎；如冰盘。凡十数易，皆不足盖完所患。听邻鸡乱鸣，孤檠闪壁。妻孥见久不返，甚催促，公如不闻。

    Explanation

    94

    A gentleman first tested his ointment on beggars with illnesses, and it proved effective. The following spring, with prolonged rain, the water in the city reached a foot deep. In the summer, there was an extraordinary drought, with scorching sun and sand and stones baking under the heat. Farmers and merchants, young and old, all suffered from dampness, and medical practitioners were at a loss; only this ointment could cure. Thus, he earned a very good income, sufficient to meet his daily needs. However, the gentleman was most kind-hearted. Even at midnight, if a beggar came with a single coin to buy medicine, he would get up to give it to them. One night, just as he lay down to sleep, the streets were filled with wind and rain. He suddenly heard a very urgent knocking. From his pillow, he asked who it was, and the beggar replied, "I am here to buy medicine." The gentleman quickly put on his clothes, opened the double doors, and a lame beggar entered. The wound was on the left thigh, as large as a coin. He carefully examined the wound, then folded the paper, heated it over the stove, and applied it, ensuring it matched the condition. When the ointment was applied, the wound suddenly grew as large as a basin; when changed, it suddenly grew as large as a bowl; when changed again, it suddenly grew as large as a large jar; like an ice plate. After changing it ten or more times, none were sufficient to cover the entire wound. He heard the neighbor's roosters crowing chaotically, and the lone lamp flickered on the wall. His wife and children, seeing he had not returned for a long time, urged him urgently, but he seemed not to hear them.

    95

    俯首呵冻，极力熨贴，绝不为一文琐屑生恚怒；而丐者反怒，大言之曰：“嘻！鄙哉伧也，药甚平淡，何子细乃尔！”公不语，仍更与之。视釜中药已竭，丐忽狂笑，声可震屋瓦，袖出一钱掷釜中，曰：“以此聊酬一夜劳！”踏雪竟去。视釜底，嵌一极古五铢钱，坚滞如铸，药气上腾，成五色香云，氤氲不散者终夜。由此药更灵，人疑丐者仙也。公寿八十，无疾一笑逝。子孙读书，多有游庠者，犹悬壶市药为生，世世保守釜炉如连城。余髫龄亲见之。釜者，古勺斗也；炉者，小折脚铛也。忠魂入梦兖济道署，在兖郡城西，本明季都阃府旧址。

    Explanation

    95

    Bending down and exhaling to warm himself, he applied great effort to make the medicine smooth and comfortable, never being angered by even the smallest trivial matter; but the beggar became angry and spoke loudly, saying, "Ha! What a vulgar peasant! The medicine is very plain, why so meticulous?" The gentleman remained silent and still gave it to him again. Seeing that the medicine in the cauldron was exhausted, the beggar suddenly laughed wildly, his sound shaking the roof tiles. He took out a coin from his sleeve and threw it into the cauldron, saying, "With this, I will pay for a night's labor!" He walked through the snow and left. Looking at the bottom of the cauldron, an extremely ancient five-zhu coin was embedded, as hard and solid as cast metal. The medicinal vapor rose up, forming a five-colored fragrant cloud that lingered and did not disperse all night. From this, the medicine became even more potent, and people suspected that the beggar was a fairy. The gentleman lived to be eighty years old, dying peacefully with a smile. His descendants studied, and many of them became scholars, still practicing medicine and selling herbs for a living. Generation after generation, they preserved the cauldron and stove as if they were treasures worth a thousand cities. I personally saw it when I was a child. The cauldron is an ancient ladle or measuring vessel, and the stove is a small tripod pot. The loyal spirit entered a dream at the Yanzhijiaodao office, located west of the city of Yanzhou, originally the site of the Ming dynasty's military governor's residence.

    96

    观察某公莅任后，爱署西隙地，可植花草，浚池灌泉，辇石堆山，筑小亭如盖。供吟啸，觞咏摩娑，收藏鼎彝为乐。余幕游滋阳时，公子某司马治樽招饮，偕登假山，顾西墙外有方土一坯，长可二丈余，高可五尺余，宽广平整，疑为台。若就势堆作嶙峋，绕以廊舍，则园势能曲，石径亦纡。心拟之而未言也。饮醉，篝灯回，朦胧就枕。梦一红袍纱帽贵人，面白多髭，长眉高颡，徘徊中庭。旋一秃发童子，投刺入，口称曹公奉拜。余方审刺上名氏，而贵人已入，昂昂抗手高坐，瞠目视余良久，曰：“子日间所见土阜，亦知其下为吾首邱乎？魂魄所栖，非可作游览所。当日仓卒捐躯，既无碑志，又无祠宇；老成凋谢，史册不书，殊寂寞耳。

    Explanation

    96

    After assuming office, a certain official took great pleasure in the vacant plot of land to the west of his residence, where he planted flowers and grasses, dredged ponds and irrigated them with springs, transported stones to build a hill, and constructed a small pavilion like a canopy. He enjoyed reciting poetry, drinking, handling objects, and collecting ancient bronze vessels as his pastimes. During my travels as a guest official in Ziyang, I was invited by a nobleman's son, a certain Marshal, to a drinking party. We ascended a man-made mountain together and noticed a rectangular piece of earth outside the west wall, approximately two zhang in length and five chi in height, broad and flat, which I suspected to be a platform. If one were to follow the natural slope and shape it into a rugged mountain, surrounding it with corridors and buildings, the garden's layout could become winding, and the stone path could also curve. I imagined this in my mind but did not express it. After drinking until intoxicated, I lit a lantern and returned, falling asleep in a drowsy state. In my dream, I saw a distinguished person wearing a red robe and a silk cap, with a white face, many whiskers, long eyebrows, and a high forehead, wandering in the central courtyard. Suddenly, a bald-headed child entered with an introduction, saying that the noble person was paying respects. As I was examining the introduction to see the name, the noble person had already entered, sitting in an elevated position, and raised his hand, staring at me for a long time. He said, "Do you know that the mound of earth you saw during the day is my ancestral tomb? My spirit dwells there, and it cannot be used as a place for touring or sightseeing. At that time, I sacrificed my body suddenly, without a stele or memorial, and without a temple or shrine; experienced people have passed away, and my name is not recorded in the historical records. It is very lonely indeed."

    97

    子既作《夜雨秋灯录》，何不纪其崖略，俾后之宦游者，知此中有人，不致削，岂非笔墨缘欤？”余心虽应诺，且欲咨询，而口噤不能言一字。贵人旋起，余唯拜送。贵人曰：“翌日，将遣人以名字相告，可以略见一斑。”言已，且行且吟，曰：寒泉百尺吐长虹，多少风云在瓮中。遗蜕纵教黄土压，精灵已逐鼎湖龙。回首燕台策马行，征途顺访绿杨营。惨闻帝抱虞渊痛，国破家亡敢再生。爱妾随身字娘，一般殉节共流芳。行人莫当胭脂井，玉虎偷窥水尚香。千古崇窿土一台，金蚕飞出总堪哀。年年风雨清明节，若个梨花麦饭来。忠义光能烛九渊，闲携桃叶岱云边。

    Explanation

    97

    Having already written Night Rain and Autumn Lantern Records, why did you not briefly record its outline to inform future officials about the presence of someone here, thereby not reducing the significance of the work? Is this not the fate of literature?" My heart, though agreeing to this, also wished to inquire further, but I could not utter a single word. The noble person stood up, and I bowed and sent them off. The noble person said, "Tomorrow, I will send someone to inform you with names, allowing you to get a brief glimpse." Having said this, they walked and recited, saying:

    A hundred-foot cold spring spews forth a long rainbow, how many events lie within the jar.
    Abandoned shells, even if pressed by yellow earth, their spirits have already followed the dragon of Dinghu.
    Looking back at the Yan Platform, riding a horse, the journey passes through the Green Willow Camp.
    Hearing with sorrow the emperor's embrace of the sorrowful place, daring to be reborn after the country is destroyed and the family is lost.
    The beloved concubine, by her side, named 娘, both sacrificed themselves for righteousness and shared in the fragrance.
    Travelers, do not mistake the well of rouge, the jade tiger secretly peeks, and the water still smells fragrant.
    Throughout the ages, a high and majestic platform of earth, the golden silkworm flies out, always worthy of pity.
    Year after year, wind and rain on the Qingming Festival, which one brings pear blossoms and wheat porridge?
    Loyalty and righteousness can illuminate the nine depths, leisurely carrying peach leaves by the clouds of Dai.

    98

    何须短碣题名字，杜甫南楼一散仙。吟已，回首顾余，挥手若示止步状。余正惶惑，若足底误踏苍莓，一滑倾扑而醒，枕上默忆所吟，一字不爽。听窗外风声飕然，若吟韵犹在耳边也。谨志于怀，殊不可测。次夕，适道署幕府某君来，试以土阜问。某愀然曰：“其下有井，为明忠臣昆山曹公廷桢死难处也。甲申年，公正行取入都，道出此邦，访友是署。忽侦隶报煤山之变，公抚膺大哭，曰：‘吾不忍事二主！’故纵身投井死。土人义之，遂闭塞井阑，上加黄土，因近官衙，不敢作殡宫墓道，然亦不忍再酌寒泉，遂筑如平阜。至道光某甲子，官此者某公，其妾素骄，亦河东怒狮也。

    Explanation

    98

    Why need a short stone tablet to inscribe one's name? Du Fu, in the Southern Pavilion, was a dispersed immortal. After reciting, he turned around and looked at me, waving as if to indicate that I should stop. I was in a state of confusion, as if my foot had accidentally stepped on wild strawberries, causing me to slip and fall, waking me up. Lying in bed, I silently recalled what I had recited, and not a single word was amiss. Hearing the wind outside, it sounded like the recited verses were still in my ears. I carefully recorded it in my heart, but it is deeply mysterious. The next day, a certain gentleman from the provincial office came, and I tested him with the question about the earth mound. He looked solemn and said, "Below it is a well, where the loyal minister Cao Tingzhen of Kunshan died a heroic death. In the year Jia Shen, he was on his way to the capital, passing through this region to visit friends in the provincial office. Suddenly, a spy reported the chaos on Coal Mountain, and he clutched his chest and wept, saying, ‘I cannot serve two masters!’ So he threw himself into the well and died. The locals honored him, so they sealed the well, covered it with yellow earth, and because it was near the government office, they did not dare to build a tomb, but they also could not bear to drink from the cold spring again, so they built it like a flat mound. In the year Jia Zi of Daoguang, a certain official who was in charge here had a wife who was usually proud, like a lioness from Hedong."

    99

    夏日怯暖，见此阜横绿荫下，四面凉习习，乃簪花傅粉，着短罗衫，坐土阜上纳凉；且双翘纤足，吸水烟，诸婢环侍，笑语喧哗。忽大叫倒地，若中癫，面青紫，目瞪视，口流沫，作昆山语音骂曰：‘何物淫娃，敢于无礼！此虽爽垲，然其下为吾窀穸。尔一妇人，坐吾屋顶上，亵孰甚焉。而且艳妆吸烟，是何体态！尔稿砧亦读书人，何绝无家教，想怯尔阃威耶？我实不能恕妖牝也！’言已，手自批颊，粉黛浸淫，花容揉碎矣。诸婢狂呼，仆媪咸至，不能救止。惊请某公来，听如夫人所语，知有干犯，急再拜认过，乞宽宥。旋闻冷笑曰：‘我家亦有妇人，设箕踞于妆宅鸱尾上，汝心安乎？’曰：‘是诚婢子之无礼，容痛饬之。但君既殡此，乞示姓名。

    Explanation

    99

    During the hot summer, seeking relief from the heat, she saw a small hill under the green shade, where the air was cool and refreshing. She adorned herself with flowers and makeup, wore a short silk robe, and sat on the hill to cool off. Her delicate and well-shaped feet were visible, and she smoked a water pipe, surrounded by maids who laughed and chattered. Suddenly, she let out a loud cry and fell to the ground, as if struck by madness. Her face turned blue and purple, her eyes wide with shock, and she foamed at the mouth, speaking in the Kunshan dialect. She cursed, saying, "What kind of lewd woman dares to be so disrespectful! Though this place is cool and dry, it is beneath my grave. You, a woman, sit on my roof, how much more disrespectful can it be? And wearing makeup and smoking, what kind of behavior is that! Your husband, a man of learning, has no family discipline, and he is afraid of your authority, isn't he? I cannot forgive this demoness!" After speaking, she struck herself on the cheek, causing her makeup and powder to run, and her beautiful face to bruise. The maids screamed in panic, and the servants and elderly women all arrived, but they could not stop or save her. Surprised, they called a certain gentleman, who listened to what the lady-in-waiting said and realized there had been an offense. He hurriedly bowed and admitted fault, begging for forgiveness. Then he heard a cold laugh and said, "I too have a woman who sits disrespectfully on the ridge of my house. Are you at ease with this?" He replied, "This is indeed the rudeness of a servant. I will severely discipline her. But since you have already been buried here, please tell me your name."

    100

    ’大声曰：‘吾明季曹廷桢也。’再问，而如君已苏，扶之上房，药饵始愈。然由此竟丧胆，淫威稍杀，不似从前之肆恣矣。今观察某公，旋以曹公问郡邑父老，咸云实有其人，与其事。至究何官属，同死者何人，死何月日，昆山有无耳孙，则不能得端的。观察函询昆宰，亦无还云，而府乘亦无纪载。”某君言至此，遽见烛跋，辞去，晨即前赴省垣。余蓦忆昨宵所梦忠臣曹公者，其即红袍纱帽，负手长吟者乎？玩其诗句，抑尚有朝云同死而兖人不知者乎？急呼墨搦管，敬谨录示同人。咸云附会，不深信。噫！此何事也，而敢以附会出之欤？夕照树荫之下，心有所思，灵即入我梦寐，曹公亦何其神欤！余命犯客星，萍踪靡定，倘到昆邑，当亲访曹氏云，或知其详也。姑记于此，庶不负忠魂垂诿之隆。

    Explanation

    100

    A loud voice cried out: "I am Cao Tingzhen of the late Ming dynasty." When asked again, the person being addressed had already recovered and was helped to the upper room, where they finally recovered after taking medicine. However, from that point on, their courage waned, and their tyrannical power was somewhat diminished, no longer acting as recklessly as before. Now, an official named Cao, upon hearing of the matter, immediately asked the elders of the county and town about Cao Gong. All said that the person and the events were real. As to his official position, who died with him, and the date of his death, there was no definitive answer. The official sent a letter to the magistrate of Kunshan, but there was no reply, and the local gazetteer also did not record it. The gentleman spoke up to this point, suddenly saw the candle wick burn out, and took leave. In the morning, he went to the provincial capital. I suddenly remembered the loyal minister Cao Gong from the dream last night—was he the same person wearing a red robe and a black hat, walking with hands behind his back and reciting poetry? Pondering his verses, perhaps there were others who died together with him but were unknown to the people of Yan. Quickly called for ink and a brush, respectfully recording and showing it to others. All said it was an embellishment, not to be deeply believed. Alas! What is this matter, and how dare I embellish it? Under the shade of trees at sunset, with thoughts in mind, the spirit entered my dream. How divine is Cao Gong! My fate is to be a guest star, my life is unsettled. If I go to Kunshan, I will personally visit the Cao family, perhaps they will know the details. Let me record this here, perhaps not to betray the loyalty of the spirit.

    101

    奚大瘤
    　　
    　　奚大瘤者，晋人也。肖神像，得刘元的传。幼失怙恃，因貌陋，痴肥多麻，且贫无一椽，人皆鄙之，无与论婚者。得神像值，辄饮啖，量兼人，以是囊中无一钱，夜多就古庙宿。夏日工倦，常卧烈炎中；秋夜饮酣，恒眠凉月下。由此得疾，腰下患瘤，始犹如盏，继如盎，旋大于瓮。蹒跚匍匐，不良于行。每有操作，痛不可忍。生计日蹙，医药罔效。自思不如速死。至夜，伏行入林下，将解带自缢。林后一道者出，羽衣翩翩，风神秀逸。遽喝曰：“止！堂堂男子，有何不了，学妇人女子，自寻短见耶？”奚痛哭，告以所苦。曰：“子与其觅死，曷譬若已死，去而学仙，必成大道。”曰：“无师传，奈何？”曰：“吾即为尔师。盍随我往深山，当授以法。”曰：“诺。但从行不易，登高涉险，能毋苦乎？”曰：“易耳。

    Explanation

    101

    Xī Dà Lóng was a man from the state of Jin. He resembled a deity and had received the teachings of Liu Yuan. Orphaned at a young age, he was ugly, foolishly fat, and covered in moles, and was extremely poor, having no roof over his head. People all despised him, and no one would marry him. Whenever he received money for making divine images, he would drink and eat, with an appetite double that of an average person, leaving him with no money in his bag. He often slept in ancient temples at night. In the summer, when he was tired from work, he would lie in the intense heat; in the autumn, after drinking to his heart's content, he would sleep under the cool moonlight. As a result, he fell ill, developing a tumor on his waist. It started as small as a cup, then grew like a jar, and soon became larger than a jar. He walked with difficulty, crawling along the ground, unable to move properly. Every time he tried to do something, the pain was unbearable. His means of livelihood dwindled daily, and medical treatments were ineffective. He thought it was better to die quickly. One night, he crept into the forest, ready to hang himself. A Taoist figure emerged from behind the trees, dressed in a feathered robe, with a graceful and elegant demeanor. He shouted abruptly, "Stop! A true man, what cannot you accomplish? Why imitate women and seek your own death?" Xī Dà Lóng wept bitterly, telling him of his suffering. The Taoist said, "Why not pretend to be dead and go to learn the ways of immortality? You will surely achieve great success." Xī Dà Lóng replied, "I have no teacher to guide me. What can I do?" The Taoist said, "I will be your teacher. Why not follow me to the deep mountains, where I will teach you the methods?" Xī Dà Lóng said, "Yes. But following you will not be easy. Climbing high and venturing into dangerous places, can it not be painful?" The Taoist said, "It will be easy."

    102

    ”袖出枣二枚，与之食。甫下咽，即遍体爽适，瘤痛亦止。再拜。随道者，行如飞。倏至一大山，叠岫层峦，隔绝尘境。进一石洞中，有药炉丹灶，位置井然。道者授一蒲团，曰：“子先学趺坐，坐四十九日，心不妄动，即得入道之门。身后石瓮内有干糇，石隙内有甘泉，足慰饥渴。成连迟余海上，吾去行即来。幸勿怠惰坏道，负吾期望之苦心！”奚一一允诺。道者长啸出门，即有云起足底，清飚远扬，其影上汉，杳矣。奚依法趺坐，甫三日，闻外间有妇女喧笑声，车马纷驰声，开目无所见。声渐近，忽头面七窍作痒，若声从窍入，渐达于瘤，亦无所苦。夜静，闻瘤内如有妇人语，悄呼“姊姊妹妹，新居颇不恶，早知有此处，不浪寻天台蓝桥等处，误作神仙窟矣。既得秘府，佳日不可辜负，宜小寻乐事，何如？”众曰：“诺。

    102

    The Taoist pulled out two dates from his sleeve and gave them to eat. As soon as he swallowed, he felt refreshed all over, and the pain in the tumor also stopped. He bowed twice in gratitude. Following the Taoist, he walked as if flying. Suddenly, they arrived at a great mountain with overlapping peaks and layers of mountains, isolated from the mundane world. They entered a stone cave, which had an alchemy furnace and an alchemy stove, arranged in neat order. The Taoist gave him a lotus seat and said, "First, learn to sit in the lotus position. Sit for forty-nine days, and if your mind does not wander, you will enter the gate of the Dao. Behind you, in a stone jar, there are dried provisions, and in the crevices of the stone, there is sweet spring water, enough to satisfy hunger and thirst. Cheng Lian is delayed at sea; I will leave but return soon. Do not be lazy and ruin the path, disappointing my earnest hopes!" Xī agreed to each point. The Taoist sang a long, clear note and went out, and immediately clouds arose from his feet, with a clear breeze blowing far away. His shadow rose to the sky and vanished. Xī followed the method of sitting in the lotus position, and after just three days, he heard the laughter of women outside and the sound of vehicles and horses rushing by. He opened his eyes but saw nothing. The sound grew closer, and suddenly, his head and face, including the seven orifices, itched, as if the sound entered through the orifices, gradually reaching the tumor, nor did he suffer. At night when it was quiet, he heard what sounded like a woman's voice inside the tumor, quietly calling out, "Sister, sister, the new residence is not bad. Had known about this place earlier, not to have searched aimlessly for places like TianTai and LanQiao, mistakenly taken as a fairyland. Having obtained a secret treasury, good days should not be wasted; it is appropriate to seek small pleasures, how about it?" The others said, "Yes."

    103

    ”四日，闻瘤内有敲棋声，纤手落子声，旋以悔着詈争声，两人劝解声，又有书声，吟哦声，排闼叩门声，众起让坐声，角酒赌枚声，枚输罚使歌以侑觞声，歌者呼拍板弹筝相和声，掷杯声，隔窗唾绒声，一女骂阿娇太懒，何匿不见客，拉之赴宴声，问绣务成否声，架上鹦鹉声，狸奴捕鼠声，众哄散话别声，一女殷殷叮后约声，声声不已。五日六日，瘤内声更烦而且奇。一女曰：“主人貌究若何，性情究若何？”一女曰：“闻亦从患难中来，想不大恶。

    Explanation

    103

    On the fourth day, I heard inside the tumor the sound of knocking chess pieces, the sound of delicate hands placing chess pieces, soon followed by the sound of regretful moves and scolding disputes, the sound of two people mediating, and the sound of reading, recitation, knocking on the door with a fist, people rising to make room for seating, drinking contests and betting with coins, the sound of someone being forced to sing as a penalty for losing the coin toss, the sound of singers calling out and accompanying with clappers and zithers, the sound of throwing cups, the sound of spitting cotton through the window, one woman scolding Ah-Jiao for being lazy and hiding from guests, dragging her to the feast, the sound of asking if the embroidery work is done, the sound of a parrot on the shelf, the sound of a cat catching mice, the sound of people dispersing and saying goodbye, one woman earnestly reminding about a future meeting, sounds continuing without end. On the fifth and sixth days, the sounds inside the tumor became even more complex and varied. One woman asked, "What does the owner look like? What is his character like?" Another woman replied, "I heard he came from hardship; I imagine he is not too bad."

    104

    ”一女曰：“休休休！彼近从道士学仙，误入迷途，必定夜郎自大，盛气向人。”一女曰：“妮子莫冤屈人，渠昨已觅死空林中，何有纨绔习气？”一女曰：“何妨趋出一窥探，便得匡庐真面目。”一女曰：“恐渠见吾辈，便颠狂煞，刻不能离，将令人琐屑死！”一女曰：“论居停分中，亦当为之效绵薄，但吾辈姊妹六人，谁肯先出头晤渠？”众曰：“叙齿，仍烦大姊姊去。”七日，闻瘤内一女曰：“三妹向后园摘素馨凤仙，大为姊姊助妆，好出见居停。”一女曰：“姊姊妆扮好，真不亚王母第三女玉九娘子风貌。

    Explanation

    104

    One of the women said, "Enough already! He has recently studied Taoism and sought immortality, but he has gone astray and is definitely overestimating himself, showing an arrogant attitude towards others." Another woman said, "Don't accuse him unfairly. He was already searching for death in the empty forest yesterday; how could he have the airs of an aristocrat?" Yet another woman said, "Why not go out and take a look, to see the true nature of the situation?" Another woman said, "I fear that if he sees us, he will go crazy, unable to leave, and we will be driven to distraction!" One of the women said, "Considering our duty to the host, we should still make a small effort. But among the six of us, who is willing to take the lead and meet him?" They all said, "Let's arrange by age, and still ask the eldest sister to go." Seven days later, one of the women inside the room said, "The third sister should go to the back garden to pick some Plumeria or another fragrant flower to help the eldest sister with her makeup, so she can go out and meet the host." One of the women said, "If the eldest sister dresses up well, she will truly be no less graceful and elegant than the third daughter of the Queen Mother of the West, Lady Yujiu."

    105

    ”一女曰：“论袅娜，犹胜渠。”一女曰：“吾为尔等开道路，反为狂婢奚落煞，真令人寒心也。”一女曰：“姊姊莫嗔怒，妹为姊姊预审瓜藕，俟姊姊归而解渴烦，何如？”众大笑。奚正俯首听，忽眼角作痒，泪涔涔，将用手揉擦，突一小美人，自两眦间跳出，落地宛转，少刻，即大如人，风致娟好，向奚裣衽曰：“妾秋水也，挈妹子等附托尊躯，感德非浅。闻主人独居孤寂，故首出参谒，乞垂怜悯。”奚爱其美，魄动神摇不能制止。

    Explanation

    105

    A woman said, "In terms of gracefulness, she is still better than him/her." Another woman said, "I was trying to pave the way for you all, but instead, I was ridiculed by a wild maid, which truly makes me feel cold-hearted." A woman said, "Sister, don't be angry. I will prepare melons and lotus roots for you, so when you return, you can quench your thirst and relieve your annoyance. What do you think?" Everyone laughed.

    Xī was listening with his head down, when suddenly his eyes became itchy, and tears welled up. He was about to rub his eyes, when suddenly a small beauty jumped out from between his eyes, fell to the ground, and wriggled around; in a short while, she grew to the size of a person. Her grace and beauty were charming. She bowed respectfully to Xī and said, "I am Autumn Water, and I brought my younger sisters to rely on your noble body; your kindness is deeply appreciated. I heard that the master lives alone and is lonely, so I came out first to pay my respects, begging for your mercy." Xī was captivated by her beauty, his soul moved and his spirit shaken, unable to control himself.

    106

    秋水善演剧，变幻小戏术，织锦裁云，雕琼镂月，事事皆令人喜。奚痴坐，唯视之笑。秋水探手于怀，意大动，遂与之狎。事讫整衣，仍小如指，跳入目中不见。即闻瘤内呼曰：“姊姊回也！”争起酌酒为贺。秋曰：“吾姐妹同事一主，当叙齿，勿争竞，致外人笑。”众曰：“善。”次日，奚又侧耳听，忽耳中习习痒，一美人跃出，如秋水行径，而貌不同，载拜曰：“妾双珠也，请献拙技，博主人欢。”品竹弹丝，无不极妙。奚闻之意迷，即拉与为乱。事已，亦跳入耳。明日，觉鼻内痒，猛嚏，一美人堕，名曰玉峰，遍体皆香，能夺兰麝。

    Explanation

    106

    Qiushui is skilled in performing small tricks and illusions, weaving clouds into tapestries, carving jade, and engraving the moon, everything of which delights everyone. The person sits idly, only watching and laughing. Qiushui reaches into his bosom, and his intention greatly moves him, so he engages in intimate behavior with her. After the deed is done, he dresses himself up, still small as a finger, jumps into the eye and disappears. Immediately, a call is heard from inside the tumor: "Sister has returned!" They all rise to pour wine in celebration. Qiushui says, "My sisters and I work for the same master; we should follow the order of seniority, do not compete, or make outsiders laugh." The others say, "Agreed." The next day, the person listens intently again, suddenly feels an itch in the ear, and a beautiful woman jumps out, like the way of Qiushui but with a different appearance. She bows and says, "I am Shuangzhu, please allow me to perform my humble skills to please the master." She plays bamboo flutes and stringed instruments, all of which are extremely wonderful. The person, enchanted by her performance, immediately pulls her into intimate behavior. After the deed is done, she also jumps into the ear. The next day, feeling an itch in the nose, the person sneezes violently, and a beautiful woman falls, named Yufeng, her entire body is fragrant, able to surpass the scent of orchids and musk.

    107

    四日，觉口内痒，一美人堕，名曰金海，善烹调，炮鳖烩鲤，莫不甘旨。五日，满身痒，一美女自毛孔出，名曰千金，自云“随孔可出，亦随孔可入。”善变。奚偶思游，即化为车，乘之恶掀簸；即又化为马。至断桥危堤，意思若乘秃尾，便入画意；即化为驴。倏至渡口，意度何处有楫，即化为小艇。甫渡，思稳坐看山，又化为肩舆。其神妙如是也。六日，觉心上痒，一美人自脑后跃出，名曰随娘，美尤冠诸姊，善缩地，九州不足论，九州而外，如东南神州，西南戎州，正西州，正中冀州，西南桂州，西北营州，东北咸州，正东扬州。五岳不足论，五岳而外，如东岳广乘，南岳长离，西岳丽农，北岳广野，中岳昆仑。偶注彼则奇山奇水，无不为之列几案，听游览焉。枕席之上，更有奇趣，令人销魂。奚日与六女戏，不复忆道士言。年余，六女均生子女，果闻瘤内婴姹啼笑声，索乳声，抓梨觅枣声。

    107

    On the fourth day, I felt an itch in my mouth, and a beautiful woman fell, named Jinhai, skilled in cooking, roasting turtles, and stewing carp, nothing was not delicious. On the fifth day, I felt an itch all over my body, and a beautiful woman emerged from my pores, named Qianjin, who said, "I can come out through the pores and enter through the pores." She was skilled in changing. If one thinks of traveling, it transforms into a carriage, but riding it, one dislikes the jolting; it then transforms into a horse. To a broken bridge and dangerous embankment, the intention is to ride a bald-tailed horse, it then enters the realm of painting; it then transforms into a donkey. Suddenly to a ferry crossing, the intention is to find a boat, it then transforms into a small boat. Just after crossing, thinking of sitting stably to watch the mountains, it then transforms into a sedan chair. Its supernatural nature is like this. On the sixth day, I felt an itch on my heart, and a beautiful woman leaped out from the back of my head, named Suiniang, whose beauty surpasses all her sisters, skilled in shrinking the earth, the nine provinces are not worth mentioning, beyond the nine provinces, such as the southeast Shenzhou, southwest Rongzhou, west Zhou, central Jizhou, southwest Guizhou, northwest Yingzhou, northeast Xianzhou, and east Yangzhou. The five mountains are not worth mentioning, beyond the five mountains, such as the east Guangcheng, south Changli, west Linnong, north Guangye, and central Kunlun. If one focuses on that, then strange mountains and waters are all set out on the table, to be listened to and toured. On the pillow and bed, there is even more strange interest, making one lose their soul. If one plays with the six women, they no longer remember the Taoist's words. After a year, the six women all gave birth to children, and indeed, one heard the crying and laughing sounds of infants, the sounds of seeking milk, and the sounds of grabbing pears and seeking jujubes.

    108

    稍长，即与论婚迎娶，鼓吹到门声，环佩跪拜声，开筵宴客声，人喧于室，马腾于槽，由是心大烦，日夜不能安枕。疾顿剧，瘤亦顿巨。忽闻六女喧于瘤内，曰：“好好房屋，奈何雨即漏，风即入，岂数椽将倾耶？明日，遣奴子赴江西，购良材来兴造。”一女曰：“以妹子愚见，可不必。日来主人大委顿，恐不久即捐舍，不如杀之，各挈子女逃远方，另寻屋宇住，何如？”众曰：“善。”奚闻之，大哭，旋闻瘤内霍霍磨刀声，哭更不辍。忽见道者冉冉降于庭，奚急跪迎。道者视之，诧曰：“尔奈何狼狈至此，曷明言无隐，庸可救。”奚具告之，道怒曰：“与其为贼杀，不如我自杀之！”壁上抽古剑，光晶莹，奚方哀求，寒锋已在颈上，用力一挥，头脆然落，亦无所苦。张目视道者，向腔内呼曰：“六贼六贼，可以速出，此中有丹，不容久宅！”随即有白气缕缕自腔内出，如釜上蒸腾。气尽瘤亦销。

    108

    As he grew older, discussions of marriage and the arrangements for it began, accompanied by the sounds of music and the clinking of women's jewelry as they knelt during the wedding ceremony, the sounds of setting up a banquet to entertain guests, the noise of people inside, and the movement of horses at the stable. From this, his heart became greatly troubled, and he could not sleep peacefully day or night. His illness suddenly worsened, and the tumor grew rapidly. He suddenly heard six women talking inside the tumor, saying, "Why does this good house leak when it rains and allow wind to enter, is the few beams about to collapse? Tomorrow, we will send a servant to Jiangxi to buy good materials to build." One of the women said, "In my humble opinion, it is unnecessary. The master has been very weak recently, and I fear he will not last much longer. It would be better to kill him, and we each take our children to a distant place to find another house to live in." The others agreed, saying, "Good." Xi heard this and cried loudly, and then heard the sound of grinding a knife inside the tumor, crying even more continuously. Suddenly, he saw a Taoist slowly descend into the courtyard, and Xi hurriedly knelt to welcome him. The Taoist looked at him and said in surprise, "How did you get into such a mess, why not speak openly without hiding anything, perhaps it can be saved." Xi told him everything, and the Taoist became angry, saying, "Rather than being killed by bandits, I would rather kill myself!" He drew an ancient sword from the wall, its light was brilliant. Xi was still pleading, and the cold blade was already at his neck. With a forceful swing, his head fell off easily, and he felt no pain. He opened his eyes and looked at the Taoist, calling out to the inside of his body, "Six bandits, six bandits, you can come out quickly, there is elixir here, it cannot be lived in for long!" Soon after, white vapor came out in threads from inside his body, like steam rising from a pot. When the vapor was exhausted, the tumor also disappeared.

    109

    道者拈其首，合腔上，端且正，然后敷以药屑，围以匹练，呼曰：“奚生醒醒！”奚果苏，觉所患顿失，心地光明，跪伏受教。道者曰：“吾教中度入，有顺缘，有逆缘，有孽缘。不图以逆缘度尔身，侥幸也。六贼已去，万念皆空，从此头颅可安可落，听其自便，何乐如之？再十年后，当寻我于六合以外，太微山顶也。”言已，道者又去。奚遂策杖出洞，再至人间。遇向日同侪，争讶问颈上何来此一围红线。奚扪之，亦无迹。此后不事肖像，能以奇术医病者。刀斧创伤，血肉狼藉，奚摸颈上垢，如玉红膏，敷之，血立止，而痛立定，肤立生。病家授以金，却之，惟仍好饮啖。一日，饮酒家，酒保答应稍迟，奚怒，自捋头落置案上，座客惊窜四散。酒保谢，自仍举置颈上如故。由是时以之恐怖人，酒家恶之。适醉后，又捋落，甫置案上，自仍倾杯酒灌腔中。酒保蓦地夺头，弃屋后溷中。奚摸头不得，两手怒搏，掷杯拍案，意甚惶迫，腔血将涌流。

    Explanation

    109

    The Taoist lifted his head, aligned his body, and stood upright. He then applied medicinal powder and wrapped it with a strip of silk, calling out, "Wake up, Xī!" Xī indeed awoke, feeling his illness suddenly gone, his mind clear. He knelt down and received instruction. The Taoist said, "In my teachings, there are karmic connections: favorable, adverse, and karmic. I did not expect to use an adverse connection to guide you, which is fortunate. The six thieves have departed, and all thoughts are empty. From now on, your head can be placed or removed at will, as you please, how joyful! In ten more years, you will find me beyond the six directions, at the peak of Taiwei Mountain." After speaking, the Taoist left. Xī then left the cave with a staff and returned to the human world. When he met his former comrades, they were amazed and asked about the red line around his neck. Xī touched it, and it was gone. Afterward, he did not seek portraits, but could use strange techniques to heal illnesses. For wounds caused by knives or axes, with blood and flesh torn, Xī would rub the dirt on his neck, which was like a jade-red ointment. The blood would stop, the pain would cease, and the skin would heal. Patients would give him gold, but he would refuse, only continuing to enjoy drinking and eating. One day, while drinking at an inn, the waiter responded slightly late, and Xī became angry. He grabbed his head and placed it on the table, causing the guests to scatter in fear. The waiter apologized, and Xī picked up his head and placed it back on his neck as if nothing had happened. From then on, he used this to scare people, and the innkeeper disliked him. Once, after getting drunk, he again grabbed his head and placed it on the table. Just as he was about to pour another cup of wine into his neck, the waiter suddenly snatched his head and threw it into the latrine behind the house. Xī could not find his head, and in anger, he clenched his fists, threw the cup, and hit the table, feeling extremely anxious. Blood was about to gush out from his neck.

    110

    突有道者自云中堕，手捧一美男子头，与奚按合，呼曰：“奚生奚生，尔头已污，不能再合。顷于富贵家寻得一颗，持来救汝。汝嗣后宜韬晦，勿再炫露！”言已，詈酒保曰：“狗彘奴！何遽杀我弟子，尔试回首看身后！”酒保蓦回首，即强项不能回止矣。再转身视，道者与奚已杳。至嘉靖某甲子，奚犹往来于晋，歌啸自如。忽中途遇一俊仆，乘怒马来，见之，遽跃马伏道左，悲曰：“公子乃在此耶？”奚闻之，径跳入水。炊许，乘龙入霄汉，不知所之。

    Explanation

    110

    Suddenly, a Taoist monk descended from the clouds, holding a beautiful man's head. He combined it with the body of a man named Xi An and called out, "Xi An, Xi An, your head is now tainted and cannot be reattached. I have just found a new one in a wealthy household and brought it to save you. From now on, you should remain hidden and not show off!" The monk then cursed the tavern keeper, saying, "Lowly scoundrel! How could you kill my disciple? Look behind you!" The tavern keeper suddenly turned around, but his head could not turn back. He then turned around again to see, but the monk and Xi An had already vanished. By the Jiajing era, Xi An continued to travel in the region of Jin, singing and laughing freely. Suddenly, he encountered a handsome servant riding an angry horse. The servant saw him and quickly dismounted, speaking in a sorrowful tone, "Is the young nobleman here?" Upon hearing this, Xi An jumped into the water. After a short while, he rode a dragon into the sky, and his final destination remained unknown.

    111

    懊侬氏曰：佛以眼耳鼻舌身意，为六贼。其贼也，即其性也。忍制之，则曰性；纵恣之，则曰贼。然天有阳即有阴，地有人即有鬼，人有形即有性。使尽如佛氏所云“灭性归寂”，则此形又何所寄乎？娲皇当时抟土为人时，又何必定与以眼耳鼻舌身意乎？彼世之不能辨性者，则有刑天氏之一教，当北面称弟子以事之可耳！昔有竖子，好集医方，偶之市，见决囚，囚患大气泡，侩子举刀一挥，头落地，而泡顿缩小。渠见之，即茫茫然归，举笔大书曰：“凡患大气泡者，将头割下即愈。”见者莫不捧腹。

    Explanation

    111

    The Sorrowful Woman said: Buddhism regards the eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, and mind as the six thieves. These thieves are, in fact, one's nature. If one restrains them, they are called nature; if one indulges them, they are called thieves. Yet, heaven has yang, so it must have yin; the earth has humans, so it must have ghosts; humans have form, so they must have nature. If everyone were to extinguish their nature and return to a state of stillness as Buddhism teaches, where would this form then find its place? When Nüwa was creating humans from clay, why did she necessarily give them the eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, and mind? For those in the world who cannot distinguish between nature and desire, there is the teaching of Xingtian. They should bow and call themselves disciples, serving him. In ancient times, there was a foolish youth who loved to collect medical prescriptions. One day, he happened upon the market and saw an execution. The prisoner suffered from a large swelling, and the executioner raised his blade, decapitating the prisoner, after which the swelling suddenly shrank. Seeing this, the youth returned bewildered, and with a brush in hand, he wrote: "For anyone suffering from a large swelling, cutting off their head will cure them." Those who saw this could not help but burst out laughing.

    112

    玉红册
    　　
    　　玉红册者，阴司纪善簿也。不必奇巧异行，凡有片长足录，即书名卷首，俾朔望上奏天曹，如人世官长之有月报。所最重者，则莫如拒色怜贫两事。于何证之？有杜君诗臣所述宝山朱君一案。朱君名鉴和，宝之罗溪世家子，美丰姿，性拓落。顾因贫，抱烟霞癖，居恒风雨一檠，怡然倚枕。一夕，正假寐，忽喃喃不辍。其妻灯下女红，听莫能辨，心疑为呓语耳。讵连夕作，醒，询之茫然。时有周大令夫人某氏，来辨所云。甫坐，而朱又对灯入黑甜。

    Explanation

    112

    The Jade Red Book
    　　
    The Jade Red Book is a register of good deeds kept in the underworld. It does not require extraordinary or unusual actions; any small virtue worthy of recording is noted, and the name is inscribed at the beginning of the book, to be submitted to the Heavenly Court on the first and last days of the lunar month, just as earthly officials have monthly reports. The most important matters recorded are, without a doubt, the two virtues of resisting temptation and showing compassion to the poor. How is this proven? There is the case of Zhu Jun from Baoshan, as described by Du Jun. Zhu Jun, whose name is Jianhe, is a member of a prominent family from Luoxi in Baoshan. He is handsome and has a reclusive nature. However, due to poverty, he developed a love for seclusion and nature, often spending his days in a simple, humble setting, contentedly leaning on his pillow. One evening, as he was dozing, he suddenly began to mutter incessantly. His wife, working on her needlework by the lamp, could not understand what he was saying and assumed it was just his rambling in his sleep. But the next night, he did the same thing, and when he woke up, he could not recall what he had said. At that time, there was a Mrs. Zhou, the wife of a magistrate, who came to inquire about what he had said. As soon as she sat down, Zhu Jun, facing the lamp, fell into a deep, dreamless sleep.

    113

    炊许，忽瞠目语曰“仆至此数日，汝等尚懵懵耶？”周夫人急问伊谁，曰：“仆赵姓，行三，生充院役，死作院城隍案下勾魂使，奉票来拘鉴和者。”朱妻惊而痛哭，周夫人急止之。遂详询神拘鉴和何事。曰：“噫！此前生因也。渠前生为殷凤鸣，孀嫂冯氏有姿色，盗之，私订偕老，忽毁盟另娶，冯郁郁遂自缢。今控于阴司，非拘渠就质不能了。”问：“事隔三十余年，何不报于前生，而报于再世？”曰：“亦自有说。凡自戕者堕枉死城，非三十年，不能出而报冤，此阴例也。

    Explanation

    113

    After cooking for a while, the messenger suddenly stared with wide eyes and said, "I have been here for several days, and you still seem confused?" Zhou's wife hurriedly asked who he was, and he replied, "I am Zhao, the third son, who served as a court official in life and now works as a soul-calling official under the city god's desk in the afterlife. I have come with an official document to summon Jianhe." Zhu's wife was shocked and wept bitterly, but Zhou's wife quickly stopped her. She then asked in detail why the god was summoning Jianhe. The messenger sighed and said, "Ah! This is due to his past life. In his previous life, he was named Yin Fengming. His widowed sister-in-law, Feng, was beautiful, and he seduced her, privately promising to grow old together. However, he suddenly broke the promise and married someone else. Feng, feeling deeply distressed, hanged herself. Now she is accusing him in the underworld, and unless he is brought to face her, the matter cannot be resolved." They asked, "Why is this accusation being brought in this life, after more than thirty years, rather than in his previous life?" The messenger replied, "There is a reason for this. Those who commit suicide fall into the City of Wrongful Deaths, and it takes thirty years before they can leave to seek retribution. This is a rule of the underworld."

    114

    ”周夫人与朱妻耳语，谋欲贿之。彼虽僵卧，已聆所语，急摇手冷笑曰：“莫妄饶舌，倘阴曹亦如人世徇私，尚有天道耶？”朱妻知不可挽回，哭失声，伏地哀叩。彼急禁止曰：“无妨碍，渠幸今生有两善事，救星也。”又问：“玉红册，汝等知之乎？”咸曰不知。既而自笑曰：“善，吾几昏瞀。此阴司纪善簿，无怪汝曹不知。冯氏始控于县，继控于府，均以渠名载玉红册，置不理。旋控于本院，亦不理。

    Explanation

    114

    Zhou's wife and Zhu's wife whispered to each other, planning to bribe someone. Although he/she lay motionless, he/she had already heard what was said and quickly shook his/her hand, coldly saying, "Do not rashly chatter. If the underworld is also biased like the human world, is there still a heavenly way?" Zhu's wife knew it was too late to change things and cried out in despair, falling to the ground to plead. He/She quickly stopped her, saying, "There is no harm. He/She is fortunate to have done two good deeds in this life, which serve as a savior." Then he/she asked, "Do you all know about the 'Jade Red Book'?" They all replied that they did not know. He/She then laughed to himself/herself, saying, "Good, I almost became confused. This is the record of good deeds kept by the underworld. No wonder you all do not know. Feng's family initially filed a complaint at the county level, then at the prefectural level, but both times the name was recorded in the 'Jade Red Book,' and the cases were ignored. Soon after, they filed a complaint at the higher court, and it was also ignored."

    115

    渠苦求昭雪，婉谕之，不听，大声呼曰：‘公左袒，不知有天外天耶？’都院恶其狡，故许以拘朱一对质，又恐他役滋扰，因仆素梗直，从不贪取他人一杯水，故有是遣。拘时令渠安眠三昼夜，头前灯，万勿灭。案结仍仆送之还也。”问：“朱何善，能于此中留名？”曰：“在苏却邻女夜奔，雪中救跛丐垂毙，两事琐琐，俟渠醒自述。”婢媪奉以烟，曰：“非所嗜，唯吸淡芭菰。”少许，言笑自若，既而作欠伸状，曰：“夜深矣，仆且去，俟汝等有成议，再来邀渠。”旋呼仆燃灯，声顿止，而朱亦倏然醒矣。询仍茫茫。

    Explanation

    115

    The person had suffered greatly to seek vindication, but gentle persuasion was ineffective. He shouted loudly, "You favor one side, do you not know there is a world beyond this one?" The high-ranking official, disapproving of his cunning, therefore allowed the detention of Zhu for a confrontation. Additionally, fearing that other officials might cause disturbance, because I have always been straightforward and never took even a drop of water from others, thus made this arrangement. During detention, he was allowed to sleep peacefully for three days and nights, with a lamp in front that must not be extinguished. When the case is concluded, I will still send him back. They asked, "What good deeds did Zhu do that can leave a name in this place?" He said, "In Su, a neighbor's daughter ran away at night, and in the snow, he rescued a crippled beggar on the verge of death. These two trivial matters, wait until he wakes up to tell it himself." The maidservants offered him a pipe, and he said, "It is not what I like, only to smoke mild tobacco." After a while, he spoke and laughed as usual, then yawned and stretched, saying, "It is late at night, I will leave. Wait until you have a decision, come again to invite him." Then he called the servant to light the lamp, the sound suddenly stopped, and Zhu also suddenly woke up. When asked, he remained confused.

    116

    告以故，且询两善事，朱详述颠末。且云：“此十年前事，从未向人一言，不徒冥中竟已登册，危哉！”晨集戚属议，中有少年好事者，不深信，夕约同人环守之。疾又作，少年遽呼曰：“鬼魅敢尔！”彼且笑且语曰：“妄人何足语，盍仍请周夫人打话！”众惭退，周告以：“虽感盛情，终难许允。若一去不返，究竟奈何？”曰：“诚哉！昨宵返县署，城隍张公，亦虑其迁延尸坏，难于返魂。盍共筹之。”众束手无一策。彼作思维再四状，曰：“有之矣。

    Explanation

    116

    The reason was explained to them, and they inquired about two good deeds. Zhu detailed the entire process. He also said, "This happened ten years ago, and I have never spoken of it to anyone. Not only was it recorded in the afterlife, but it is perilous!" In the morning, relatives gathered to discuss the matter. Among them was a young man who was skeptical. In the evening, he invited others to surround and guard the place. The illness flared up again, and the young man suddenly shouted, "How dare the ghosts and spirits do this!" Zhu laughed and said, "Foolish people are not worth talking to. Why not still ask Madam Zhou to speak?" The others felt ashamed and withdrew. Zhou told them, "Although I appreciate your kind intentions, it is difficult to agree. If I go and do not return, what then?" Zhu replied, "Indeed! Last night, I returned to the county office, and the city god Zhang Gong also feared that the body would decay from delay, making it difficult to return to life. Let's plan together." The others were at a loss with no plan. Zhu appeared to think deeply for a while and said, "I have an idea."

    117

    送渠买棹吴门，赁寓栖止，就质送还，均觉便易。”众曰：“善。”遂如议定期。临去，咸恳其照看，慨然自任，惟嘱其到苏，万勿诣庙进香焚楮帛，恐不足邀福，徒取咎戾也。鉴和遂有苏台之行。舍馆甫定，即摄去。家人悬悬，酣睡一榻，如痴如迷。其妻守之泣。婢媪恐灯灭，守益坚。明日忽苏，张目曰：“惫矣！”呼饮甚急。又起而跪拜，作答谢状，絮絮作坐谈状，旋即作送客状。既而卧，移时始起，则真清醒矣。自云：“拘时，有白发叟引之去，盖即赵君行三者也。

    Explanation

    117

    They sent him to buy a boat in Suzhou, rent a place to stay and rest, and handle the matter and send it back, all of which they found convenient and easy. The group said, "Good." They then set a date as agreed. On the verge of leaving, all earnestly requested him to take care of them. He took on the responsibility with a sense of duty, only instructing him to go to Suzhou, never to go to the temple to burn incense or paper money, fearing that it would not gain blessings, only bring misfortune. Jianhe then had a trip to Suzhou. Just settled in his lodging, he was immediately taken away. Family members were anxious, he was in a deep sleep on one bed, as if in a trance. His wife guarded him and cried. The maidservants feared the lamp would go out, so they guarded even more firmly. The next day he suddenly woke up, opened his eyes and said, "Exhausted!" He called for drink very urgently. Got up again and knelt and bowed, making a gesture of gratitude, talked incessantly as if sitting and talking, immediately acted as if sending off a guest. Afterwards lay down, for some time before finally getting up, then he was truly awake. He said himself, "During the time he was detained, an old man with white hair led him away, probably the third son of Mr. Zhao."

    118

    抵院署则巍峨壮丽，如王者居。殿上灯烛作绿色，两旁牛头排衙迄，神升坐，貌甚伟，不敢仰视。有黑衣妇，披发号叫甚厉，正遑骇间，闻呼己名，叟引之案下跪，神问曰：‘汝有罪，知之乎？’曰：‘不知。’神命视照胆镜，则前生盗嫂事，历历在目，悔怖无地，崩角叩数十。妇哓哓呼报冤，对质数十言。神谕妇曰：‘凤鸣应惩，但今生有两善事，名登玉红册，上帝所嘉，不便用刑，当令其亲讽佛经，祝汝投生，可乎？’妇不服，神怒曰：‘赏罚自有定谳，不能由汝纠缠也。

    Explanation

    118

    Upon entering the court, it was majestic and splendid, like the residence of a king. In the hall, the lights of the candles glowed green, and on either side, statues or figures were arranged in rows, resembling ox heads. The deity ascended to sit, and His appearance was very grand; I dared not look up directly. A woman in black, with disheveled hair, cried out fiercely. Just as I was in a state of panic, I heard my own name called. An old man led me to kneel before the table. The deity asked, "Do you know of your sins?" I replied, "I do not." The deity ordered me to look into the mirror of conscience, and there, I saw the deeds of my past life, particularly the act of stealing my elder brother's wife, vividly before my eyes. I was filled with remorse and fear, unable to find a place to hide, and I knelt and knocked my head to the ground many times. The woman continued to cry out, demanding retribution, and the deity questioned her for several sentences. The deity then addressed the woman, saying, "Fengming should be punished, but he has done two good deeds in this life, his name is recorded in the red jade register, and he is approved by the Heavenly Emperor. It is inappropriate to use punishment; instead, he should recite Buddhist scriptures for you to be reborn, is that acceptable?" The woman was not satisfied, and the deity angrily said, "Reward and punishment have their own verdicts, and they cannot be decided by your纠缠."

    119

    再多言，法即反坐！’妇哀呼不已，神曰：‘尔失节亦不能无过，尚不甘耶？’呼具供结，逐出。妇下阶，犹怒盼不已。叟挈余叩拜出，询神为谁，曰：‘向忠壮也。’问：‘玉红册何状？’曰：‘页页皆泥金字也。’”朱既返罗溪，日诣庙闲游，视东廊，果塑有赵君像，须眉酷肖。懊侬氏曰：放下屠刀，立地成佛，何速且易也。人当愧悔已极，发菩提心，立广大愿，功更倍焉。上帝嘉其现在，恕其既往，正上帝爱人以德处。否则身堕泥梨，尚有自新之路耶？三复此编，可当晨钟暮鼓，唤醒众生。

    Explanation

    119

    "If you speak more, the law will punish you in reverse!" The woman continued to wail in despair. The deity said, "You have lost your chastity and cannot be without fault, yet you still refuse to accept it?" The deity then summoned the woman to make a confession and expelled her. The woman descended the steps, still glaring with anger. The old man took me and knelt to bow out, asking who the deity was. The deity replied, "It is Xiang Zhongzhuang." He asked, "What does the Jade Red Book look like?" The deity replied, "Every page is inscribed with golden characters in mud." After Zhu returned to Luoxi, he visited the temple daily for leisurely walks. Looking at the east corridor, he indeed saw a statue of Zhao, whose eyebrows and beard were strikingly lifelike. Annoyed, he said, "Lay down the butcher's knife and become a Buddha instantly, how swift and easy it is! One should feel deep remorse and shame, cultivate the mind of enlightenment, and vow to great deeds, doubling the merit. Heaven rewards those who are present with virtue, forgiving their past. This is how Heaven loves people with virtue. Otherwise, if one falls into the realm of suffering, is there still a path to reform? Reading this text three times can serve as the morning bell and evening drum, awakening all beings."

    120

    一声雷
    　　
    　　明季，吾乡大雷雨，空中坠一异僧，蜷须广额，碧眼方瞳，耳戟双环，似是西域人。自云托钵朝五台，倦卧绝崖松树下，甫交睫，不知何故至此。语虽诞而貌慈，乡人多爱之，争延至家，进餐饮，问茹素否乎，曰：“素也食，荤也食，”更喜其诚笃，送之真胜寺，俾挂衲，自名铁罗汉，不事梵呗，唯日与所游者饮酒食肉而已。闻城北可帆园梅花甚佳，约居士二十余人往游。离城二十余里。时尚春寒，人皆重裘，僧则破衲。

    Explanation

    120

    A Sudden Thunderclap

    In the late Ming dynasty, my hometown experienced a violent thunderstorm, during which a strange monk fell from the sky. He had curly mustaches, a broad forehead, green eyes, square pupils, and ears adorned with double rings, suggesting he might be from the Western Regions. He claimed to have been begging for alms while traveling to Mount Wutai, had been tiredly resting under a pine tree on a cliff, and had just closed his eyes when he suddenly found himself here. Although his words were somewhat fantastical, his appearance was kind, and he was loved by the villagers. They competed to invite him to their homes, offering him food and drink. They asked if he ate only vegetarian food, and he replied, "I eat both vegetarian and non-vegetarian food." They were even more pleased by his sincerity and sent him to the True Victory Temple, where he was allowed to hang his robe and named himself Iron Arhat. He did not engage in chanting or recitation but spent his days drinking alcohol and eating meat with the people he traveled with. Hearing that the plum blossoms in the Can Garden to the north of the city were very beautiful, he invited about twenty laypeople to visit. The garden was about twenty miles from the city. It was still spring, and the weather was cold, so everyone wore heavy fur coats, while the monk wore a tattered robe.

    121

    行至半途，日卓午，各热甚，争解去，僧悉代衣之，亦绝无膨亨状，面亦无汗。花下亭子，趺坐倾谈。少顷，雷鸣，雨如霰，为花辟尘，霁则转凉，仍解付各人衣之。以是，人多乐与之游。游必饮，饮必醉，醉则随处倒卧，鼾息如雷。夜归寺，必索水濯。僧佣候门，多恶之。一夕以冰水进，僧抽袜伸足，故作蹙眉咬牙怯暖状，须臾，果然气蒸腾，且炙手矣。每闻钟鱼笳鼓，意颇厌恶；惟闻雷声必倾耳悚听，或悲或喜，或点头顿足，或合掌诵佛，曰：“善哉善哉，如是如是！”众笑之，曰：“若以我为妄乎？吾雷之知音也。

    Explanation

    121

    Walking halfway, the sun was directly overhead, and everyone was very hot, competing to take off their clothes. The monk took them all off and put them on himself, showing no signs of bloating and no sweat on his face. Under the pavilion among the flowers, they sat cross-legged and engaged in deep conversation. A short while later, thunder rumbled, and the rain fell like hail, protecting the flowers from dust. When the rain cleared, it became cooler, and they still gave the clothes back to everyone. Because of this, many people enjoyed traveling with him. Whenever they traveled, they must drink until they were drunk, and when drunk, they would lie down anywhere, snoring like thunder. At night, returning to the temple, they must ask for water to wash. The temple's servants waited at the door, often disliking them. One night, they brought in cold water. The monk pulled off his socks and extended his feet, pretending to frown and grit his teeth, showing discomfort with the warmth. In a moment, indeed, steam rose, and it felt hot to the touch. Every time he heard the temple bells, fish drums, and military music, he felt quite disgusted; but whenever he heard thunder, he listened intently and with awe, sometimes feeling sad or happy, sometimes nodding or stomping his feet, sometimes clasping his hands and reciting Buddhist scriptures, saying, "Well done, well done, just so, just so!" The others laughed at him, saying, "Do you think I'm delusional? I am the true appreciator of thunder."

    122

    天以云为容，沉霾如墨者愁，变幻如锦者喜；以风为气，长空怒号者戾，穿花飘忽者静；以雷为言语，罚恶则大声疾呼，以正其罪，赏善则郑重飞扬，以策其勋。”言次，又闻空中殷殷，众曰：“顷又作何语？”曰：“骨隆冬，骨隆冬，恼煞也碧翁，闷煞也化工；孝不孝，忠不忠，耳也波聋，目也波。骨隆冬，心冲冲，云消也，雨霁也，故态萌。”众轩渠以为妄。顷又睹雌电，走金蛇，霹雳震，屋瓦飞走。众掩耳，问曰：“此又作何语？”曰：“胡家媳，忤阿翁，翁茹素，饭断葱，击之击之稍从容，骨隆冬。

    Explanation

    122

    Heaven uses clouds as its appearance. Dark clouds like ink bring sorrow, while changing clouds like brocade bring joy. Heaven uses wind as its breath. The wind roaring in the vast sky brings anger, while the wind passing through flowers with a light touch brings calmness. Heaven uses thunder as its speech. To punish evil, it loudly calls out to correct the wrongs; to reward good, it solemnly and exuberantly bestows its honors.

    As they spoke, they heard a deep rumbling in the air. The crowd asked, "What is it saying now?" They replied, "Bone-loud, bone-loud, it annoys the Green Old Man, troubles the Artisan of Creation; those who are ungrateful and disloyal, their ears are deaf to the truth, and their eyes are blind to the right path. Bone-loud, their hearts are restless, the clouds disperse, the rain clears, and old habits reappear." The crowd laughed and dismissed it as nonsense. Soon, they saw a streak of lightning, like a golden snake, followed by a thunderclap that shook the house and sent tiles flying. The crowd covered their ears and asked, "What is it saying now?" They replied, "The daughter-in-law of House Hu, disobeying her father-in-law, who is a vegetarian and dislikes onions, is struck with a slight delay, and the thunder rumbles, 'bone-loud, bone-loud.'"

    123

    ”走询之，果有胡氏翁媳口角，闻雷怖，伏地尚未起。一日，雷声沉沉，若疾若徐，忽作奇响，云豁然开，蔚蓝如沐。走询之，曰：“宦十年，囊何空，灵辇至，城之东。吁嗟乎，王公！吁嗟乎，王公！荫及子孙富贵通。骨隆冬！”果闻门外鼓吹喧闹，人马杂沓。盖邑绅王公，卒于任，其子扶柩归，邑人士迎请入城耳。一日，雨倾盆，响震山谷，雷火光作青紫，炫人目。询之，曰：“有长虫，粗于瓮，灿于虹，久则助魃化毒龙，噬及生物神岂容，杂杂杂，骨隆冬。”走观之，果有大蛇震死于南岗之阳，以是人始惊其异。

    Explanation

    123

    I went to inquire, and indeed there was an argument between the old man and his wife-in-law of the Hu family. They were terrified by the thunder, fell to the ground, and had not yet risen. One day, the thunder was deep and rumbling, sometimes fast and sometimes slow, and suddenly made a strange sound. The sky suddenly cleared and became a bright, refreshing blue. I went to inquire, and they said, "He served for ten years, but the purse is empty. The spirit carriage arrived, heading to the east of the city. Alas, the nobleman! Alas, the nobleman! The blessings extend to the descendants, making them prosperous and powerful. Bone, bone, bone!" It turned out that there was indeed noisy music and the sound of people and horses outside. It was because the town's nobleman, Mr. Wang, died in office, and his son was bringing the coffin back, with the townspeople welcoming him into the city.

    One day, it rained heavily, and the sound shook the mountains and valleys. The lightning light was blue and purple, dazzling the eyes. There was a large snake that was as thick as a jar and as bright as a rainbow. If it stayed long, it would help the demon transform into a poisonous dragon, devouring living things, which the gods would not allow. Bone, bone, bone! There was indeed a large snake that was killed by the thunder on the sunny side of the southern hill. Thus, people began to be amazed by its strangeness.

    124

    居三年，乡之老幼妇孺，无不知有铁罗汉者。一朝濯足整衣讫，遍招所与游者，集于寺，笑曰：“和尚日日啖施主，绝不作东，何以为情？”乃折纸把笔，画酒樽匕鬯，鸡鱼虾蟹各种焚之，烟袅袅作彩云，众方凝视，忽奇震如爆竹，惊怖回顾，则室中已陈设完好，酒满樽，菜满盘，几案排列，就坐饮啖，品味无不绝佳。众乐之。有拇战者，有射覆唱歌者，僧亦欠伸而起，曰：“老僧愿招雷部阿香来同饮，何如？”众曰：“善则善，恐亵神耳。”曰：“无妨。

    Explanation

    124

    After three years, the elderly, young, women, and children in the village all knew of the Iron Arhat. One day, after washing his feet and dressing, he invited all those who associated with him to gather at the temple. He smiled and said, "The monk eats from the offerings of the patrons every day, but never hosts a meal. How can we be considerate?" He then took paper and a brush, drew wine cups, sacrificial vessels, and various dishes like chicken, fish, shrimp, and crab, and burned them. The smoke curled up like colorful clouds, and everyone was watching in amazement when suddenly a strange explosion like firecrackers startled them. They turned around in fright, and suddenly the room was well set up, with wine full in the cups, dishes full on the plates, tables and chairs arranged, and they sat down to eat. The taste of the food was all excellent. Everyone enjoyed it. Some played thumb wrestling, others played guessing games and sang. The monk also stretched and got up, saying, "The old monk wishes to invite the daughter of the Thunder God, Axiang, to drink with us, how about it?" The guests said, "It's good, but we fear offending the gods." The monk replied, "It's fine."

    125

    ”更折纸，画水天无际，远树迷蒙，一船挂帆，乘风破浪。焚之，烟团结如球。球破，成楼台，成山林，水澌澌流山足，顷刻流渐阔，若江若河，突一舟上流来，僧忽跃登舟，身仅盈尺，向众拱手，曰：“珍重！”船上帆影奔驰，鼓声大震，视之，已杳。回顾几案盘杯，亦复乌有。自是始服之如神。后十年，乡人某，因事至咸渎，偶游永宁寺，见铁罗汉趺坐廊下，左右两巨瓮，目若瞑。某惊喜欢跃，曰：“大师乃在此乎？”僧不答。拜之摇之，亦不动。询之寺僧，云：“来此久矣，瓮昨甫购来，不知作么生。

    Explanation

    125

    Again folding the paper, he drew a vast water-sky with no boundary, distant trees hazy, a boat with a sail hoisted, sailing against the wind and waves. He burned it, and the smoke gathered like a ball. The ball broke, forming pavilions and mountains, water flowing down the mountainside. In a moment, the flow became wider, like a river or a stream, suddenly a boat came upstream. The monk suddenly leaped onto the boat, his body only a foot tall, bowed to the crowd, and said, "Take care!" The sail's shadow raced, the drum beats loudly, looking at it, it had disappeared. Looking back at the table, plates, and cups, they were also nowhere to be found. From then on, he began to believe in it as if it were a god. Ten years later, a certain person from the village, because of an affair, went to Xiandu, happened to visit Yongning Temple, saw an iron Arhat sitting cross-legged under the eaves, two large jars on either side, his eyes seemed closed. The person was surprised and happy to jump, said, "Master, are you here?" The monk did not answer. He bowed to it and shook it, but it did not move. He asked the temple monk, who said, "It has been here for a long time, the jars were just bought yesterday, I don't know what it is."

    126

    ”乡人仍坐守之，夜三鼓，僧忽大呼“雷音王菩萨”不辍。某邀大众视之，已坐化矣。某遂缕述僧之灵迹，众始悔恨，遂以瓮作涅，合而葬之后圃。某待其竣事，拜而后去。阅二百六十余年，寺迭更主席，以无塔无碑碣，遂忘其事。忽一夕，大雷雨，土破瓮出，僧以为财，争启之，铁罗汉也。袈裟虽朽，面貌如生，惟四肢冷若冰雪，似非炼形者。舁之禅坐，环诵佛号，顶礼瞻拜。夜静大放光明，院宇如昼。远近冠钗，大善优婆，争来布施。遂装金建龛，供于西廊，约略生前趺坐处，颜曰“一声雷”。

    Explanation

    126

    The villagers continued to sit and guard the monk. At the third watch of the night, the monk suddenly began to call out "Bodhisattva of Thundering Sound" without stopping. The narrator invited the villagers to see the monk, who had already entered a meditative state and died. The narrator then recounted the monk's miraculous deeds, and the villagers began to feel regret. They then placed the monk in a jar and buried him together in the garden. The narrator waited until the burial was completed, bowed, and then left. After over 260 years, the temple changed its abbot multiple times, and because there was no stupa or stone tablet, the event was forgotten. One night, during a heavy thunderstorm, the jar was broken and the monk's remains were exposed. The villagers thought they had found treasure and opened the jar, only to find an iron arhat (a statue of a Buddhist saint). The monk's robe was decayed, but his face looked as if he were alive, while his limbs were cold like ice, suggesting he was not a physical body. They placed him in a meditative posture, surrounded him with chants of the Buddha's name, and paid homage. At night, he emitted great light, and the courtyard was as bright as day. People from far and wide, including virtuous lay Buddhists, came to offer donations. They then gilded and built an altar, placing it in the west corridor, roughly where the monk had sat in meditation during his lifetime, and named it "One Thunder."

    127

    余避乱，幕游咸城，乃同治龙飞二年也。偶谒金容，因忆吾乡邑乘，载有《铁罗汉传》，惟载着裘濯足两事。询诸寺僧，始得其详。且云一夜大雨，方丈知酱瓮未盖，急呼僧，声为雷声所隔，方丈谓酱必毁坏，清晨视之，瓮已盖好，询大众，无知者，及观僧像，口角指头，尚有余酱云。懊侬氏曰：东王公与玉女投壶，枭入不出，天且为之唏嘘；枭误不接，天且为之轩渠。蒿目下界，将痛哭而不制止者，能缄口而不言欤？霹雳大作，口舌且敝矣，唇且焦矣，而下界之人，恒梦梦而若聋焉。何来佛子，竟是知音！

    Explanation

    127

    During the second year of the Tongzhi era, I fled from turmoil and traveled as a guest in Xiancheng. One day, I paid homage to the Buddha's image and recalled that the local annals of my hometown recorded a story titled "The Iron Arhat," which only mentioned two specific events involving the Arhat: one where he washed his feet and another where he wore a fur cloak. I inquired of the monks in the temple and finally learned the full details. They recounted that on a night of heavy rain, the abbot noticed that a jar of sauce was left uncovered and urgently called out to the monks, but his voice was drowned out by the thunder. The abbot feared the sauce would spoil, but the next morning, he found the jar had already been covered. When he asked the monks, none knew who had done it. Upon examining the Arhat's statue, he noticed that the corners of its mouth and the tips of its fingers still had remnants of sauce. The commentator, Aonong, remarked: "When Dongwanggong and the jade maiden played touhu, the bird entered but did not emerge, and even Heaven was moved to tears; when the bird made a mistake and failed to catch it, Heaven itself laughed. Gazing down upon the world with concern, those who cry out in pain but do not stop, can they keep silent and not speak? When the thunderclap resounds, the mouth and tongue are exhausted, and the lips are parched, yet people on earth are often as if in a dream and deaf, oblivious to the truth. How come, then, that a son of the Buddha is truly a true appreciator?"

    128

    应声蓝面鬼
    　　
    　　济宁李琳卿茂才，与余订车笠交。尝为吾言其先曾王父侍御公，居京师，笺奏之暇，夕犹挑灯据案读，其刻苦如此。一夕，正把卷，忽闻梁间有声，异而睨之，则一巨鬼，肤靛色，口呀呀而角，由梁上堕地，旋堕旋长，长至丈余，立案侧，目闪烁有光。公立朝素骨鲠，非馁于中者，笑顾巨鬼。良久，仍俯首诵朗朗，公读鬼亦读，其句如应声然。公思《易》能驱邪魅，即为讲《易》之乾卦，鬼犹应声，唯至“地道光也”句，鬼忽不见，公亦掩卷就寝，尝举以告寮，莫测所谓。后竟以道光元年卒于官。侍御公垂绅有大度，遇朝廷大事，则铮铮敢言，无趋避，无阿附。骑箕之日，鬼神来告；或上帝怜公憨直，俾早知急流勇退欤？不然，何河岳日星，清夜亦遭鬼瞰，此中盖有天数欤？噫！异哉！

    Explanation

    128

    The Blue-Faced Ghost That Responds in Unison

    The scholar from Jinan, known as Mao Cai, and I were close friends. He once told me about his great-grandfather, the Shiyu, who lived in the capital. During his spare time from drafting memorials, he would still study late into the night with a lamp and a desk, showing his dedication and hard work. One evening, as he was reading, he suddenly heard a rustling sound from the beam above. Curious, he looked up and saw a giant ghost with blue skin, a gaping mouth, and horns. The ghost fell from the beam, growing taller as it descended, until it stood over ten feet tall beside his desk, its eyes flashing with light. The Shiyu was known for his upright and unyielding character, and he did not fear the ghost. He smiled and continued reading aloud, and the ghost responded in unison, as if echoing his words. The Shiyu thought that the Yijing (Book of Changes) could drive away evil spirits, so he began to recite the Qian hexagram. The ghost continued to respond, but when he reached the line "the earth's virtue is bright," the ghost suddenly disappeared. The Shiyu then closed his book and went to bed. He later shared this experience with his colleagues, but none could understand its meaning. Eventually, the Shiyu passed away in office in the first year of the Daoguang era. He was a dignified and broad-minded man, always speaking boldly and honestly on matters of state, without fear or favor. On the day of his death, the spirits came to inform him; perhaps the Heavenly Emperor pitied his honest and straightforward nature and allowed him to know early to retreat from the turbulent waters? Otherwise, why would even the great natural elements—mountains, rivers, sun, and stars—be subject to the gaze of ghosts in the stillness of the night? Is there not a fate or will of heaven at play here? Ah! How strange!

    129

    卷三
    　　
    　　桂林臬署三异
    　　
    　　慎斋又为余述臬署三异：当劳廉访任内，偶雇女佣蔡妪，年将不惑，尚风致，居恒亦解供操作，唯下榻不与人同舍。夜深有自窗外过者，闻一人屋内语，细听之，则有亵昵声，疑遭狐魅，不深异。久之，署中时有抛砖掷瓦等患，或匕鬯自飞于梁，或溲溺自集于盏，廉访疑为狐，虔祀之，而闹如故。一夕，内眷纳凉，忽见一黑衣人立蔡妪后，上下大哗，争逐之，行甚速，追莫及。而其恶闹，更无忌惮矣。

    Explanation

    129

    Chapter 3
    　　
    Three Strange Occurrences at the Guilin Judicial Office
    　　
    Mr. Shenzhai again told me about the three strange occurrences at the Guilin Judicial Office: during Mr. Lao's tenure as censors-in-chief, he once hired a female servant named Lady Cai, who was around forty years old but still had a graceful appearance. She usually understood how to perform domestic tasks, but she slept in a separate room from others. One night, someone passed by the window, and someone inside the room was speaking. Upon closer listening, there were intimate sounds. They suspected it was a fox spirit, but did not find it very strange. After some time, there were frequent incidents of throwing bricks and tiles at the office. Sometimes wine offerings flew onto the beams, and sometimes urine and feces collected in cups. Mr. Lao suspected it was a fox spirit and worshiped it devoutly, but the disturbances continued as before. One evening, the family members were cooling off when suddenly they saw a black-clothed person standing behind Lady Cai. Everyone made a great commotion and tried to chase it, but it moved very quickly and they could not catch up. The disturbances became even more brazen.

    130

    旋有女巫，私泄于廉访之执事人云：“闹者，山魈也，向与蔡妪私。初魅时，居然美少年，且时赠钱米博妪欢。久之，渐露真形状，则独足，怪丑莫名，然妪竟不能遣之去，且与之狼狈。凡佣主惮其闹，必托妪退送，以是得财贿。”执事人陈廉访，姑如其说，闹果已，不深究，立予多资，遣之去。是妪也，淫而贪，虺蜴其心，魑魅其偶。但不解煌煌戟，何能令若辈出入？其不遭显戮者，真漏网之鱼哉！此一异也。又署背倚城隍，且近山，常山率然之流，缘卑湿而滋长，弗触之，无害也。

    Explanation

    130

    Soon there was a female witch who privately disclosed to the official in charge of the censorate: "The one causing trouble is a shanxiao (mountain demon), who previously had an affair with Lady Cai. At first, when he was bewitching, he was surprisingly a handsome young man, and he often gave money and rice to win her favor. After a while, his true form gradually appeared, and he was one-legged, grotesquely ugly beyond description. But Lady Cai could not get rid of him, and she was entangled with him. Whenever the landlord was afraid of the disturbance, they would always entrust Lady Cai to send him away, and thus obtained wealth." The official in charge of the censorate, tentatively following her words, found that the disturbance ceased, and he did not conduct a thorough investigation, instead immediately providing her with substantial financial compensation and dismissing her. This woman, who was lustful and greedy, had a serpent's heart and a demon's companion. However, I do not understand how the splendid halberd could allow such creatures to come and go; those who did not suffer a public execution are truly escaped fish. This is one strange event. Moreover, the office is backed by the city god temple and is close to the mountains. The usual creatures of the Shanshuilie (a type of mountain creature) grow due to the low and damp conditions, but if not disturbed, they are harmless.

    131

    署之客厅窗后，植芭蕉数本，堆假山，极嶙峋；山足土洞，口圆如井，大仅如盏，瞰之似不甚深，填以土，旋启，中有巨蟒为之宅。蟒身作黄金色，顶有肉角寸许，色殷红，粗如桶，长十余丈。居恒不甚出。凡官有迁擢信，必预自榻后出，峨峨盘厅事中，目闪视人，然绝无噬人意。旋逡巡，仍自榻下没。出三日，官果迁，其征验如此。第榻后护壁，皆木板无隙，不知其从何出入也。顾身已蟒矣，犹知韬晦，恐致雷诛。迨官有莺迁，必预报鹊喜，似有卫主之心。

    Explanation

    131

    In the hall behind the window of the office, several banana trees were planted, and artificial hills were piled up, extremely rugged. At the base of the hill, there was a soil cave with a round opening like a well, as large as a cup, and when viewed, it seemed not very deep. When filled with soil and then opened, inside was a giant python that lived there. The python's body was golden-colored, with a fleshy horn about an inch long on its head, bright red in color, as thick as a barrel, and over ten zhang long. It rarely came out. Whenever there was news of an official's promotion, it always came out from behind the bed in advance, majestically coiled in the hall, its eyes flashing at people, but it had no intention of biting people. Soon it cautiously retreated and disappeared back under the bed. Three days later, the official indeed got promoted, confirming the snake's prediction. However, behind the bed, the wall panels were all wooden boards with no gaps, and it was unknown how it entered and exited. Even though it was a python, it still knew to keep a low profile, fearing punishment by thunder. When an official was promoted, it would definitely foretell the good news, seeming to have a heart to protect its master.

    132

    较之腆然人面，引鬼以挟主者，不大相径庭乎？此二异也。又厅之西，列小书舍三间，对面构六角亭。亭下土阜，高与墙齐，上植杂树十余株，花草遍覆。每夜深，击柝巡夜者，辄见无首人，双手捧金盆立坡畔，有时立亭侧，旋返旧处；不逐人，亦从不为人所逐。或云其下为明末忠臣墓，姓氏无考。然手捧金盆则何故欤？或云似黄叶道人栖魂于此。按道人系自刎于浙东，不死于粤西，而迢迢数千里，恋此一坯，又何故欤？噫！忠臣烈士，死不为猿鹤，即为神龙，岂花妖木客所可同语乎？此三异也。

    Explanation

    132

    Compared to a grotesque, face-like form that dominates with a ghostly figure, are they not quite similar? This is the first peculiarity. Further west of the hall, there are three small study rooms, facing a hexagonal pavilion. Below the pavilion, a hillock rises to the height of the wall, with over a dozen assorted trees planted on it, and flowers and grasses covering it. Each night, when the night watchmen beat the drum and patrol, they often see a headless person standing by the slope, holding a golden basin in its hands. Sometimes it stands beside the pavilion, then quickly returns to its original place; it does not chase people, nor is it ever chased by them. Some say that below it is the tomb of a loyal minister from the late Ming dynasty, but the surname is unknown. Yet why does it hold a golden basin? Some say that the soul of the Yellow Leaf Hermit has come to rest here. The hermit committed suicide in Zhejiang and did not die in Guangxi, yet traveled thousands of miles to remain attached to this small mound—why is that? Ah! Loyal ministers and heroes, even if they die, they do not become mere animals or birds; they become divine dragons. How can they be compared to mere flower spirits or tree ghosts? These are three strange phenomena.

    133

    烈殇尽孝
    　　
    　　河间西鄙，有烈孝女柳，传者忘其姓，仅得其小字也。恩县贾人某，以武庠习负贩，一骡一马，往来燕冀间；妻素悍，借以逃阃威。红羊之岁，北直大饥，道途枕饿殍，土人易子析骸，惨莫能喻。贾日至河间，从马背上见一贫媪走入篷颗中，对墓痛哭，良久，返之破屋。一髫龄女子出应门，虽菜色可掬，而容犹可怜也。贾下骑，趋索茗饮，女走避，媪迓客。问：“一坯土下，属媪伊谁？”曰：“亡夫巡检某公也。”问：“何哭之恸？”曰：“家赤贫，生者固饥，死者亦盼断麦饭，哪得不悲？”问：“女子千金耶？”曰：“然，名柳，年十六，至今未字。未亡人设旦夕殒霜露，诚不知一块肉将安归？”言已，更痛不可抑。贾亦代唏嘘，乃探囊出白镪十两授媪，坚却之。乘不意，蓦弃破几上，而飞骑兔脱。母子追莫及，遥遥叩拜，谓怜孤寡，有古侠士风，实不知其垂涎于二八娇娃也。

    Explanation

    133

    Final Translation

    Loyal Widow's Filial Piety

    In the western outskirts of Hejian, there was a filially devoted young woman named Liubai, whose surname was forgotten by the narrator, but only her nickname was remembered. A merchant from En County, who had learned the trade of transporting goods through military schools, traveled between Yan and Ji with one mule and one horse. His wife was typically domineering, and he used his business to escape her harshness. During the year of the red sheep (1644), a great famine struck the northern part of the direct-controlled territories, with the roads littered with the corpses of the starving. People were so desperate that they swapped their children for food and even resorted to cannibalism, a situation that was beyond comprehension.

    The merchant arrived in Hejian daily and, from his horseback, saw an old woman enter a dilapidated hut, where she wept bitterly over a grave for a long time before returning. A young girl, barely a child, answered the door, though her face was gaunt and her appearance pitiful. The merchant dismounted and hurried to offer tea, but the girl fled, and the old woman welcomed him. He asked, "Who is the man buried beneath this mound of earth?" She replied, "My late husband, a local official." He then asked, "Why do you weep so bitterly?" She answered, "Our family is extremely poor; the living are hungry, and the dead were deprived of even a simple funeral meal. How can I not be sad?" He asked, "Is this girl a precious treasure?" She replied, "Yes, her name is Liubai. She is sixteen years old and has not yet been married. If I were to die soon, I truly do not know what will become of her." As she spoke, she was overcome with grief. The merchant also felt moved to tears and took out ten taels of silver to give to the old woman, but she firmly refused. Taking advantage of her distraction, he left the money on the broken table and rode away quickly, like a rabbit escaping. The mother and daughter could not catch up to him and bowed from a distance, praising his compassion for the orphaned and widowed, saying he had the spirit of an ancient knight-errant. However, they did not know that he was actually lusting after the beautiful sixteen-year-old girl.

    134

    越半载，贾又履其地，归装更累累。入门，即大声询：“阿姥无恙？”母女奔出，泥首无算，曰：“微长者，当索我于北邙山枯鱼肆矣。”详询其乡贯姓氏，以俾供养酬大德。贾具以告，复笑云：“是戋戋者，何足挂齿颊！”媪具鸡黍供客。衔杯之次，贾询：“柳姑可得雀屏选否？”曰：“尚未。如长者作冰，真小女子有福。”贾乘间请曰：“某四十无一线，俗有招子说，愿奉白金五十两为阿姥寿，屈柳姑作螟蛉。但我老夫妇一索得男，他日遣嫁乘龙婿，当如某所生也。”媪似首肯，入与女絮絮多时，出云：“弱息凌夷，亦宦裔也，继膝下固佳；即下充妾媵，亦请明言于其先，免未亡人心旌常不定。”贾正色曰：“是何言欤？某之心，天日可鉴！”遂设信誓，媪遽诺。出银如数，曰：“粲粲者在是。但金允，即乞柳姑同归，莫耽延，俾早慰闺中望夫山耳。”媪抱女互哭，哀动林禽。贾云：“请姥暂割爱，若能如荆人意，当不日以犊车迓姥。

    Explanation

    134

    After half a year, the merchant returned to the same place, and his belongings were even more abundant. Upon entering, he loudly inquired, "Is the old woman well?" The mother and daughter rushed out, kneeling repeatedly, and said, "Without your kindness, we would have been lost in the desolate fish market on North Mountain." They asked in detail about his hometown, native place, and surname, so that they could provide support and repay his great kindness. The merchant replied, "These small things are not worth mentioning!" The old woman prepared a simple meal for the guest. As they were drinking, the merchant asked, "Can the Willow Girl be chosen as a wife?" She replied, "Not yet. If you act as an ice man, it would truly be a blessing for me." The merchant took the opportunity to ask, "I am forty and childless, and there is a saying about adoption. I wish to offer fifty taels of silver as a gift for the old woman, and adopt the Willow Girl as my son. But if we have a son in the future, we will send him to marry a noble wife, and he will be treated as my own." The old woman seemed to agree, and went inside to talk to her daughter for a long time. She came out and said, "My weak and declining daughter is also from an official family. It would be good for her to follow in your footsteps; even if she serves as a concubine, please clearly state this to her ancestors, so that my heart does not waver." The merchant said seriously, "What is this saying? My heart is witnessed by heaven and earth!" He then made a solemn promise, and the old woman immediately agreed. She took out the silver, saying, "The shining ones are here. But if you agree to the gold, please adopt the Willow Girl and bring her back, and do not delay, so that she can soon comfort the longing of the wife in her room." The old woman embraced her daughter and cried together, their sorrow moving the birds in the forest. The merchant said, "Please let the old woman temporarily part with her love. If she can follow the wishes of my wife, I will soon send a carriage to fetch her."

    135

    ”女恐过恸伤母心，即忍痛拜别。贾授以鞭，联辔就道。行里许，女耳中犹仿佛阿娘唤女声，遂大恸。贾告以不能两全，再三抚慰。一日，抵腰站，置女逆旅，距恩仅三十里，忽迟疑不发，询若何，曰：“此间有故人招饮，且有交易勾当耳。”地多妓，女不知也。日则静掩双柴，夕则一灯闪壁。鹃啼血洒，蝶梦魂飞。邻家马二娘，虽时来闲话，而忆母之心终不释。媪自送女出门，愈无聊赖，纵阿堵能疗眼前疮，而掌珠已失心头肉，昕夕泪雨，渐绝水浆。越七日夕，正呻吟病榻间，忽闻女子叩门声，媪颤声答云：“此穷窟也，谁家女娇生，来乞水火？”应曰：“儿声音，娘不识耶？柳归也？”媪惊喜，强起拔键，奔入，果不诬。

    Explanation

    135

    The daughter feared that excessive grief would hurt her mother's heart, so she endured the pain and bid farewell. Jia gave her a whip and they rode together on the road. After traveling a few miles, the daughter still faintly heard her mother's voice calling her, and she then wept bitterly. Jia told her that it was impossible to have both, and he comforted her repeatedly. One day, they arrived at a place called Yauzhan, where he left the daughter at an inn, only thirty miles from En. Suddenly, he hesitated and did not proceed. When asked what was the matter, he replied, "There is an old acquaintance who invited me to drink, and there are some business matters to attend to." The place was full of courtesans, but the daughter did not know. During the day, she quietly closed the double doors, and at night, a single lamp flickered on the wall. The cuckoo's cry was like blood being shed, and the butterfly's dream was like a soul flying away. The neighbor, Ma Ernang, would occasionally come to chat, but her longing for her mother never diminished. After sending the daughter out, the old woman felt increasingly aimless. Even if money could cure her current pain, her cherished daughter had become like a piece of her heart. She wept tears day and night, gradually losing her appetite for food and drink. After seven days, she was groaning in bed when suddenly she heard the sound of a woman knocking on the door. The old woman answered in a trembling voice, "This is a poor hovel, who is this pampered girl, coming to beg for water and fire?" The woman replied, "Is it my child's voice, mother doesn't recognize it? Is it Liu Pu returning?" The old woman was surprised and delighted, forcing herself to get up and unlock the door. She rushed in, and indeed it was not a lie.

    136

    冉冉拜灯下，曰：“儿真归矣，母心慰否？”媪转疑骇曰：“儿已随假父去，且深宵难独行，得毋鬼耶？抑梦寐耶？”女破涕为笑，对云：“渠携儿去，家中生菩萨，妒虎也，疑其纳小星，渠百辩，终不信，知万难相安，私以一骑送儿归。执鞭人已就前村宿耳。”媪庆珠还，沉疴顿已。女虽璧返，远山常颦。顾貌如故，音如故，而艺增且绝，人皆惘惘。自刺绣以至书画词赋，无一不精。每出手制，浼村人寄通都，售最速而价亦丰。唯款署“烈火夕阳子”，不书柳也。由是供饔餐，无事采薇矣；葺庐舍，无事牵萝矣。

    Explanation

    136

    冉冉在灯下拜谢，说：“我真回来了，母亲心里安慰了吗？”老妇人疑惑惊骇地说：“你已经跟着假父亲走了，况且深夜难以独自出行，难道是鬼吗？还是做梦呢？”女孩破涕为笑，回答说：“他带我走后，家里来了一个像菩萨一样的人，是个嫉妒心很强的人，怀疑他纳了小妾，他百般辩解，最终还是不信，知道我们很难相处，于是私下派一匹马送我回来。骑马的人已经在前面的村庄住下了。”老妇人高兴地看到女儿归来，严重的病痛突然痊愈了。女孩虽然像美玉一样归来，但远方的山峦常常皱眉。尽管她的容貌和声音与从前一样，但她的艺术技能却增加了且达到了极致，人们都感到困惑。从刺绣到书画词赋，她样样精通。每次出手制作的作品，都委托村民送到大城市，销售速度最快且价格也高。她只署名“烈火夕阳子”，不再写柳的名字。从此，她有了稳定的饮食，不再需要采摘野菜；修缮了茅舍，不再需要绑藤蔓。

    137
