    人有欲聘为妇者，曰：“俟吾母百年，终当求牡，岂竟守雌？”媪若怂恿之，则泪涔涔下，曰：“儿命薄，浩浩之水，育育之鱼，毕生无分矣。”又十年，媪病剧，弥留时，握女手哽咽，曰：“阿娘苦汝！”女拭涕云：“母往地下，自有安乐窝，儿在人间，亦非长命缕。儿不过身外身，影外影也，以生为死，以死为生。母行矣，儿当相从于地下。”媪一笑，恍然悟，目遽瞑。女号恸，集村之老成男妇，购具，制殓装，与亡父合葬，咸加封植。事毕，囊中资有余，散给茕独辈，且罄，曰：“烦代唪一声佛，祝我母早升天。

    Explanation

    137

    There is a man who wishes to marry a woman. He says, "I will wait until my mother passes away, and then I will eventually seek a husband. How can I remain unmarried forever?" If the mother encourages her, the daughter weeps continuously, saying, "Mother is suffering for me! The vast waters and abundant fish, I have no chance for life." Ten years later, the mother falls seriously ill. On her deathbed, she holds the daughter's hand and weeps, saying, "Mother is suffering for you!" The daughter wipes away her tears and says, "Mother, you will have your own place of peace and comfort in the afterlife. I am in this world, but I am not fated to live long. I am a body without a body, a shadow without a shadow. To treat life as death and death as life. Mother, you go ahead, I will follow you into the afterlife." The mother smiles and suddenly understands, and her eyes close. The daughter wails in grief, gathers the elders of the village, buys items, prepares the funeral attire, and buries her with her deceased father, ensuring the tomb is well-maintained. After the burial, there is still some money left in her purse. She distributes it to the lonely and destitute, and when it is all gone, she says, "Please recite a Buddhist prayer for me, pray for my mother to ascend to heaven soon."

    138

    ”临穴日，佛声震山谷，然后举火，焚所居成白地，惨与众别，曰：“将诣腰站，寻一故人，了吾事。”众疑其无依，将去寻假父，泣送之行。翩然抵旧处，仍宿当日之逆旅。主人目之，大骇，问何名，曰：“烈火夕阳子。”曰：“何酷似十年前之柳姑也？”曰：“虎贲貌似中郎耳。”观者咸啧啧称异。明日出资治觞，折简邀比邻十数人，至则觥筹交错，宾主极欢。讯招饮之由，则泫然以杯酌地，曰：“昔柳姑，吾姊也。闻化后，盛蒙矜怜，赐以帷盖，得正首邱。一杯浊酒，聊代长眠人答鸿慈耳。”众抚掌曰：“手足耶，无怪其貌之神似也。”因重与述柳姑当日惨死事。女亦不悲，唯乞引之埋骨处，亦不瞻拜，趺坐黄土上，合十作偈云：“贞洁女儿花，安能污狭斜。殒身遭毒鸨，炼魂慰慈鸦。不遇奸人赚，何邀上帝夸。可怜残月底，《折柳》唱无家。

    Explanation

    138

    On the day of the grave, the sound of the Buddha's voice shook the mountains and valleys. Then, fire was raised, and the residence was burned to turn it into a white land. A poignant farewell was made, saying, "I will go to the waist station to meet an old friend and complete my task." The crowd doubted that he had no support and was about to go to find a father figure, crying as they sent him off. He arrived gracefully at the old place and stayed at the same inn as before. The innkeeper looked at him and was greatly startled, asking what his name was. He replied, "Sunset of the Burning Flame." "How similar you are to Liu Bo Gu from ten years ago!" "A military officer looks like a middle-ranking official." The crowd marveled at the resemblance. The next day, he contributed money to prepare a feast, inviting several neighbors, and when they arrived, they drank heartily, the host and guests were extremely happy. When asked the reason for the invitation, he wept and poured wine on the ground, saying, "Liu Bo Gu was my sister. After her transformation, she was greatly favored and granted a canopy, allowing her to return to her ancestral tomb. A cup of cloudy wine, just to offer to the long-sleeping person in return for the great kindness." The crowd clapped and said, "Siblings, it's no wonder you resemble each other so closely." He then recounted the tragic story of Liu Bo Gu's death. The woman did not grieve, only asking to be led to the place where her bones would be laid to rest. She did not pay respects, but sat cross-legged on the yellow earth, joining her hands and reciting a verse: "A chaste daughter flower, how could she be tainted by the narrow and crooked paths. She met her death at the hands of a wicked woman, her soul purified to comfort a kind bird. She did not encounter a deceitful person, why would she invite praise from God. Alas, under the waning moon, the song of farewell 'Breaking Willow' echoes, and she feels homeless."

    139

    咦！假即真，真即假，我与我兮，是耶非耶？”言已，顿灭幻躯，衣衫如蜕。众大哗，不能穷其变。盖先与女闲话之马二娘，鸨而猾者也。瞰女美，欲攘为钱树子，以贾妻毒焰告女，不信；又蹈隙恐贾曰：“秀才祸不远矣！君家胭脂虎，砸碎醋坛子，尚懵懵耶？”贾色骇变。鸨冷笑云：“日昨有东邻卖货者来，云阃人知汝买艳妇，骂不绝声云：‘俟红妆到门，当以白刃毕命！’”贾嗒然求计于鸨，曰：“是何难哉！姑以女与我，汝只身归，是非犹可狡辩，譬诸盗无赃物也。

    Explanation

    139

    Ah! False becomes true, true becomes false, am I myself or am I not myself?" Having spoken, she suddenly extinguished her illusory form, her clothes shedding like a snake's skin. The crowd erupted in commotion, unable to fathom the transformation. It was the woman named Ma Erniang, who had previously been chatting idly with the lady, a prostitute and cunning one. Seeing the lady's beauty, she wanted to seize her as a money tree, warning the lady about the jealous rage of her husband, but the lady did not believe her. Then, taking advantage of an opportunity, she frightened the scholar, saying, "Your misfortune is not far off! Your household's lipstick tiger, the vinegar jar is about to be broken, yet you remain clueless?" The scholar's face turned pale with fear. The prostitute coldly remarked, "Yesterday, a neighbor selling goods came, saying that the woman in the inner quarters knew you had bought a beautiful woman, and she scolded continuously, saying, 'Awaiting the arrival of the bride, she will use a white blade to end her life!'" The scholar helplessly sought advice from the prostitute, saying, "What is difficult about this? Let me have the woman, and you return alone. Right and wrong can still be argued cleverly, it's like a thief with no stolen goods."

    140

    ”旋出百金授贾，曰：“以此买瘦马，尚不丰耶？”贾惑之，乃囊金弃女，如飞去。鸨以女归，始爱若明珠，旋使应客，不允，詈且挞；仍倔强，即倒悬，出利刃割其臂肉饲猫犬。女痛极，佯允之，是日裹创盛妆饰。适一贵公子来，欣然愿荐枕席。漏三下，公子醉而假寐，女闭户卸妆，久无声。灯小如豆，公子犹扯女衣作昵声，讵僵立不动；烛之，舌出三寸，已雉经榻前短柱上矣。大号，鸨来救，莫及，是即女归叩门之夜也。

    Explanation

    140

    The merchant immediately handed over a hundred gold coins to the dealer, saying, "With this, buy a thin horse; is it not enough?" Deceived by this, the dealer put the money in a bag, abandoned the girl, and fled away like a bird. The madam, upon the girl's return, began to love her as if she were a precious pearl. She then made her entertain guests, but the girl refused, and the madam scolded and beat her. Still stubborn, the girl was hung upside down, and the madam took out a sharp knife to cut her arm flesh and feed it to the cats and dogs. The girl, in extreme pain, pretended to agree. That day, she bandaged her wounds and put on her best clothes. Just as a wealthy young man arrived, he gladly offered his bed. At the third chime of the night clock, the young man, drunk and pretending to sleep, closed the door and took off her makeup, remaining silent for a long time. The lamp was as small as a soybean, and the young man still pulled her clothes and made intimate sounds. How could she stand motionless; looking at her, her tongue protruded three inches, and she had already hanged herself on a short pillar in front of the bed. She shouted loudly, and the madam came to rescue her, but it was too late. This was the night when the girl returned and knocked on the door.

    141

    鸨痛人财两空，裸女尸弃荒野，雀衔树叶蔽女体，村环守之，野物不敢近。土人怜其烈，敛资以薄桐棺，瘗之干净土，即重来所酬之十数人也。至是显幻迹。旋与河间人互述其事，咸以手加额上，曰：“柳姑不死，柳姑不死。”邑宰阮公，闻而义之，拟请旌，格于例，私表其阡，曰：“贞烈惨孝柳姑之墓。”幕府磊落子，擅扶鸾之术，书符召之，乩云：“柳至。贤使君为薄命人表墓，光及九泉，特来奉谢。”即自述之遭遇甚悉。问：“何能幻形，复归事母？”曰：“儿自戕后，魂遇梁孝王，怜我烈，授以炼形诀，吞以益智珠，俾骨立形成，毕十年孝养之志。”问：“何必易香名？”曰：“返魂之香，固恐骇俗。且上帝悯儿贞，赐封烈殇贞女，盖拆字谜也。”问：“何不报贾之计赚，与鸨之毒凌？”曰：“贾究以资买我身，救我母，季常之惧，亦所诚然，故不忍报。鸨则恶豺之性，淫雉之心，毒其本真也，又不屑报。

    Explanation

    141

    The mother, Liu Bugu, was heartbroken over the loss of both her daughter and her wealth. The naked body of the deceased girl was left in the wilderness, and birds carried leaves to cover her body. The villagers surrounded the area to protect it from wild animals. The locals, moved by her virtue, gathered funds to bury her in a thin wooden coffin, and they buried her in clean soil, the same dozen or so people who had previously paid for her. At this point, her spirit became visible. She soon recounted her story to people from Hejian, and they all touched their foreheads in relief, saying, "Liu Gu is not dead, Liu Gu is not dead." The magistrate, Yuan Gong, was moved by her story and planned to request an imperial edict, but was stopped by regulations. He privately marked her grave with a tablet inscribed with "The Tomb of Liu Bugu, the Chaste, Loyal, and Filial." A man from the magistrate's office, skilled in spiritual practices, wrote a talisman to summon her spirit. The spirit responded through the divination board, thanking the magistrate for honoring her memory. She then recounted her experiences in great detail. The man asked, "How could you transform your form and return to care for your mother?" She replied, "After my suicide, my spirit met King Liang Xiao, who admired my virtue and taught me a method to refine my form. He gave me an intelligence-enhancing pearl, allowing me to complete my vow of filial piety." The man asked, "Why did you change your name to something fragrant?" She replied, "A fragrant name was to avoid shocking the world. Moreover, God, moved by my virtue, bestowed upon me the title 'Loyal and Chaste Maiden,' which is a riddle." The man asked, "Why did you not seek revenge on the man who tricked you and the brothel owner who mistreated you?" She replied, "The man who tricked me saved my mother, and I feared the consequences, so I did not seek revenge. The brothel owner, however, was evil and cruel, and I had no desire to seek revenge against her."

    142

    然渠等早干冥罚，罪岂能逃，更不必报而自报。”问：“父母尚团聚乎？”曰：“已度父母成鬼仙，颇觉逍遥，永除烦恼，较生时判云泥也。”后附短诗五章，掷笔而去，诗曰：抗手巫咸走碧天，非人非鬼亦非仙。自从觅得淮南诀，那许精灵化杜鹃。死死生生死亦生，死生俱是女儿身。才投孽海抽帆早，如此风波愁煞人。紫诰新颁号烈殇，揶揄弄玉魅寒簧。九京那有埋忧窟，碧汉横骑白凤凰。醴有真源玉有芽，无端化作断肠花。

    Explanation

    142

    But they had already faced divine retribution, how could their crimes escape punishment? There was no need for retribution to be reported, as it was already self-evident." The questioner asked, "Are the parents still reunited?" The response was, "The parents have transcended to become ghost immortals, feeling quite carefree, forever free from troubles, vastly different from life." Afterward, five short poems were appended, and the pen was cast aside. The poems read:

    Raising hands, Wu Xian walks in the blue sky,
    Neither human, nor ghost, nor immortal.
    Since finding the secret of Huainan,
    How could spirits transform into cuckoos?

    Death and life, life and death, also live,
    Both in death and life, they are female.
    Just thrown into the sea of sin, pull the sail early,
    Such waves make people extremely sad.

    Newly issued purple edict titled heroic martyr,
    Mock playing with jade and bewitching cold reed.
    How could there be a burial place of worries in the nine tombs,
    Ride across the blue sky on a white phoenix.

    Sweet wine has a true source, jade has a sprout,
    Without reason, transform into a heartbreaking flower.

    143

    火中烧出青莲蕊，愿借罡风卷狭斜。似此烟云亦太奇，姗姗月下报乌私。可怜阿母龙钟甚，十载何曾悟鬼儿。座客莫不顶礼传诵。时某贾已死，悍妻亦别抱琵琶。鸨则被盗扳，瘐死黑狱。嘻！姑即恕汝，彼苍苍者，能恕汝乎？快哉快哉！懊侬氏曰：烈孝固能格天，当其惨痛呼号，宜有真灵下来，授炼形衣钵，亦由其身有仙骨，迥异寻常也。不然，何鸾飞数言，竟若是之雍容大度耶？噫！如柳姑者，虽格于例而不旌，亦可不必旌，盖已荣于钟鼎，而寿同金石矣。

    Explanation

    143

    From the flames emerged the lotus bud, and I wish to be carried by the divine wind to the heavens. Such smoke and clouds are indeed extraordinary, gracefully reporting under the moon to the crows. Pitifully, the old mother is quite feeble, and for ten years, she never understood her son's ghostly nature. The guests all paid homage and passed it on. At that time, the merchant had died, and his fierce wife had taken up the lute. The madam was framed by thieves and died in prison. Ah! I will forgive you, but can the vast heavens forgive you? How delightful! How delightful! The Grieved Woman said: Filial piety can indeed move heaven, and when he was in agony and crying out, it is fitting that a true spirit would descend, bestowing the robe and bowl of transformation. It is because his body had the bone of a sage, quite different from the ordinary. Otherwise, how could the words of the immortal be so graceful and dignified? Alas! As for Liu Gu, although she was barred by the rules, she did not need to be honored, for she was already honored by the bells and tripods, and her life was as enduring as metal and stone.

    144

    父子神枪
    　　
    　　枪炮者，火器也，弁士行伍者习之，轰击凫雁者亦习之，往往行伍多不及猎凫雁者。何故？盖一则敷衍耳目，绳亮焰发，便能饱食国家饷；一则弋获飞走为生，枵腹而出，伏于湖滨，凝神息虑，专注如承丈人蜩，如射大夫雉，如兔起鹘落，鲜有不技精而近于神者。此中有人，正未可忽。泗州大圣庙前，戈叟名辽，其子名继辽。叟鳏，子未娶，贫无生计，均善火枪，逐日于湖畔击水鸟，易柴米糊口。时明季正德朝，人见其技良，呼曰大小戈，讽之入行伍，不愿也。一日乘夕阳，各荷一枪，上禽兔累累，将归。经宝积山下，见营卒数十人，擒一贩私盐者，殴几死。贩滚哭求饶，不许。贩者妻与女，蓬头献银簪珥，赎其罪，不许；获簪珥，仍牵之行。

    Explanation

    144

    Father and Son with Divine Spear Skills

    Firearms are weapons of fire, which military officers and soldiers practice, and those who hunt waterfowl also practice them. Often, the soldiers are not as skilled as the hunters. Why is that? On one hand, the soldiers merely go through the motions, firing their guns to earn a living from the state; on the other hand, the hunters, going out with empty stomachs, crouch by the lakeside, concentrate their minds and calm their thoughts, focusing as intently as a child catching cicadas, as precisely as shooting a pheasant, as swiftly as a rabbit rising and a hawk falling—rarely do they fail to achieve skillful and almost divine proficiency. There are people among them who should not be overlooked. In front of the temple dedicated to a saint in Sizhou, there was a man named Gao Lao and his son named Gao Jilao. Gao Lao was a widower, and his son was unmarried, both poor and without means of livelihood. Both were skilled with fire guns, and every day they would shoot waterfowl by the lakeside to exchange for firewood and rice to make a living. During the late Ming Dynasty in the Zhengde era, people saw their skills as good and called them the Big and Little Gao, advising them to join the military, but they did not wish to. One day, as the sun was setting, each carrying a gun, they had shot down many birds and rabbits, and were about to return. Passing by Baoji Mountain, they saw several dozen soldiers who had captured a smuggler of salt, beating him nearly to death. The smuggler rolled on the ground and begged for mercy, but it was not allowed. The smuggler's wife and daughter, with disheveled hair, offered silver hairpins and earrings to redeem his crime, but it was not allowed; they took the hairpins and earrings and still dragged him away.

    145

    妻女随之哭，两婴孩见父母被获，更哭，几滚入水。叟目睹惨甚，遽呼曰：“来！若牵渠将何之？”曰：“捉将官里去。”问何罪，以私枭对。曰：“嘻！小人肩挑步担，借此获蝇头利，得谓之枭乎？彼大商巨贾，公然夹私，漏税虐民，是枭也，汝何不牵之？”众怒其饶舌，曰：“是何预汝事而左袒，得毋党乎？”曰：“党便若何？”曰：“依样捉去敲胫股！”言已，探怀飞黑索套叟。其子婉言父愚憨，求恕，不听，更击子，且攒殴之。叟大吼，翻身触机，发背上枪，青烟一缕而两卒倒。子知业贾祸，亦即燃枪，左右击，众披靡逸去。叟顾贩曰：“尔夫妻刺船，曷急遁！”遂同诣州牧自首。牧与营弁，均商家走狗也，械梏系狱，照袒匪戕捕论斩，父子延颈待秋决。

    Explanation

    145

    The wife and daughter followed suit, weeping. The two infants, seeing their parents being captured, cried even more, nearly rolling into the water. The old man, witnessing the scene, was deeply distressed and suddenly shouted, "Come! What will you do with him?" They replied, "We will take him to the authorities." He asked what crime they were accused of, and they answered that it was private smuggling. He exclaimed, "Ah! These common folk who carry goods on their shoulders and earn meager profits, can they be called smugglers? Those wealthy merchants and large traders who openly engage in private business, evade taxes, and oppress the people, those are the real smugglers. Why don't you go after them instead?" The crowd was angered by his interference and said, "What business is it of yours to side with them? Are you not part of their faction?" He retorted, "If I am part of their faction, what then?" They replied, "Treat you the same way and beat your legs and thighs!" Saying this, they reached into their chests, threw out black ropes, and tied the old man. His son pleaded with the crowd, saying his father was foolish and innocent, asking for mercy, but they did not listen and continued to beat him, even attacking him with multiple blows. The old man roared, turned around, and hit a machine, causing a gun to fire from his back. A thin trail of smoke emerged, and two of the men fell down. The son, realizing the danger of engaging in illegal trade, also ignited a gun, striking left and right, and the crowd scattered in panic. The old man looked at the vendor and said, "You two, row the boat quickly and escape!" They then went together to the governor to surrender. The governor and the military officers were all enforcers of the merchants, so they were shackled and imprisoned, sentenced to death for supporting bandits and harming officials, and the father and son waited for their execution in autumn.

    146

    其子上书，立辩两捕之毙者，乃己之枪，非父之枪，老惫何能为？愿以一身抵。不许。贩者姓邬名义，逸而侦其死有日，夫妻号哭，祷于山神，愿代死。遣其女螺娘，伪为叟之女者，携羹饭馈狱食，遥致声曰：“若乔梓死，愚夫妇何敢独生！”夜深，夫妻倚棹苇中，待女归。夜色昏黑，突有月光出水际，漾晶莹。正痴望，光忽分散，如万颗琉璃球激波欲舞。飕一声，光忽飞至船之篷下，走不定。趋视之，一蚌珠也，大如弹子。知非凡宝物，急藏诸箧。少时女归，述叟状，语呜咽，夫妻浩叹而已。明日，闻都御史奉旨巡按皖郡，将至钟离，谋买珠，以媚如夫人。女喜曰：“得之矣！”青衣怀珠别父母，曰：“儿去，或得酬戈翁德，倘羁留有时日，幸勿以儿为念！”遂泣与别。女托卖珠婆携之，登御史舫，以珠献。

    Explanation

    146

    His son submitted a petition, immediately arguing that the two deceased were killed by his own gun, not his father's gun. An old and feeble man, how could he have done it? He was willing to take responsibility with his own life. This was not allowed. The vendor named Wu Yi escaped and investigated when the man died. The couple wept and prayed to the mountain god, willing to die in his place. They sent their daughter Luoniang, pretending to be the old man's daughter, to carry soup and rice to feed the prisoner. She called out from a distance, saying, "If both father and son die, how dare we live alone!" At night, the couple leaned on the boat in the reeds, waiting for their daughter to return. The night was dark, but suddenly moonlight emerged from the water, glinting and shimmering. While gazing fixedly, the light suddenly dispersed, like thousands of glass balls dancing on the waves. With a sudden sound, the light flew to the boat's canopy, moving restlessly. They ran to see it, and it was a pearl, as large as a bullet. Knowing it was a valuable treasure, they quickly hid it in a chest. After a while, the daughter returned, described the old man's condition, spoke with tears, and the couple sighed deeply. The next day, they heard that the Censor-General was on a tour of Anhui Province, approaching Zhongli, planning to buy the pearl to please his concubine. The daughter said happily, "We have it now!" In plain clothes, she carried the pearl and left her parents, saying, "I am leaving, perhaps to repay Old Knife's kindness. If I am detained for a while, please do not worry about me!" And they cried as they parted. The daughter entrusted the pearl to a pearl seller, who took it to the Censor-General's boat and presented it.

    147

    御史大喜，嘱送玉人，自详审。妾更喜，问从何处来，曰家藏耳。问珠值，曰：“芹献耳。自伤父兄出不返，贫无依，愿为夫人婢，以此为进身阶耳。”妾视其宛丽明艳，遽收录，且酬卖珠婆去。女善伺人意，眉语目听，不数日，为诸婢冠。一夕侍宴，妾正褒述女于御史，女忽伏地悲啼，叩有声。惊询之，唏嘘曰：“妾父戈辽，妾兄戈继辽也。”遂缕述戕捕之由，泣求揭钵。御史愕然久之，曰：“尔父兄事，吾已阅其牍，案如山，不易反。姑念尔缇萦再生，明即诣泗，当提讯而平反之。”女顿首谢，妾揽于怀，曰：“尔若背我去，即歼尔父兄。”女曰：“奴愿终生侍夫人。”明日，按临泗州，牧出迎，肃入巡署，坐堂皇。首提戈之父子，穷诘之，览继辽状，故惊曰：“孝子也！”牧极言其横，非骈斩不足明刑谳。御史曰：“罪尚可原。

    Explanation

    147

    The censor was greatly pleased and instructed that the beautiful woman be brought to him for personal examination. The concubine was even more delighted and asked where the girl had come from. The girl replied that she had been kept in the family. When asked about the value of the pearls, she said, "It is a modest offering. I grieve that my father and brothers have not returned, leaving me poor and without support. I wish to serve as the lady's maid, using this as a means to advance in social status." The concubine saw that the girl was exceptionally beautiful and graceful, and quickly took her in, paying the pearl seller to leave. The girl was adept at reading people's intentions, communicating through subtle gestures and expressions. Within a few days, she became the best among the maids. One evening, during a banquet, the concubine was praising the girl to the censor when the girl suddenly fell to the ground, weeping and knocking loudly. The censor was startled and asked what was wrong. The girl sighed and said, "My father is Ge Liao, and my brother is Ge Jili." She then detailed the reasons for their capture and killing, crying and asking for the truth to be revealed. The censor was astonished for a long time and said, "Your father and brother's case, I have already reviewed the documents, and the case is as heavy as a mountain, not easy to reverse. Temporarily considering you as a reborn Tiying, the next day, I will go to Sui, where I will interrogate and overturn the verdict." The girl bowed and thanked him, and the concubine embraced her, saying, "If you betray me, I will destroy your father and brother." The girl replied, "I am willing to serve the lady for life." The next day, the censor arrived in Sui Prefecture. The governor came out to greet him, and they solemnly entered the governor's office, sitting in the main hall. The censor first summoned Ge Liao and his son, interrogating them thoroughly. After reading Ge Jili's statement, the censor exclaimed, "A filial son!" The governor strongly stated their brutality, saying it was not enough to execute them both to clarify the verdict. The censor said, "The crime can still be pardoned."

    148

    ”即勒供疏奏，以戈父子正弋凫，两卒巡湖，隔芦苇发枪，适中，照误死，拟将戈辽父子充极边不赦。疏入，诏许。遂立遣戈父子配云南军，徒步抵配所，隶边将熊公麾下。熊览其符，知为轰毙命案，问：“枪能命中乎？”憨对之，颇矜夸，曰：“盍自武库中，自检良者来，吾有遣尔处。”戈果检得巨而准者，面试之，均先中。熊边将大喜，曰：“西南有大山，万岩耸翠中，产珍禽异兽甚伙。尔父子日荷枪往猎，得则献军门，当陆续纪尔功，满贯自有珠还日。唯内山皆毒蟒所栖，误入恐陨命，牢记不可往。”应曰：“诺。”明日裹粮领药入山，果得虎豹之属，归献，恒犒赏。逾一载，腰橐甚丰，颇觉此间乐。父子偶私计，内山究作何状，盍往观之？遂入视。

    Explanation

    148

    "Upon receiving the report, the authorities immediately ordered the submission of a confession. The father and son, Go, were hunting ducks when two soldiers patrolled the lake and fired a gun through the reeds, accidentally hitting and causing a mistaken death. It was proposed to exile the father and son to the farthest frontier without pardon. The report was submitted, and the emperor approved it. They were immediately sent to serve in the Yunnan military, on foot to their assigned place, under the command of General Xiong. General Xiong reviewed the evidence and knew it was a case of accidental death. He asked, "Can the gun hit the target?" The father, Go, replied with great pride, saying, "Why don't you go to the armory and bring back a good one? I will send you there." Go indeed found a large and accurate gun, and after testing it, it hit the target every time. General Xiong was very pleased and said, "There is a large mountain in the southwest, with many rocks and greenery, producing many rare birds and animals. You and your father should carry guns and go hunting every day, and bring back the catch to the military headquarters. Your achievements will be recorded gradually, and you will be rewarded when you achieve enough. Only the inner mountains are inhabited by venomous pythons, and entering them could lead to death. Remember not to go there." They replied, "Yes." The next day, they packed food and medicine and went into the mountains. They indeed caught tigers and leopards, brought them back to present to the military, and were always rewarded. After more than a year, their bags were very full, and they found this place quite enjoyable. The father and son privately discussed, wondering what the inner mountains were like, and decided to go and see. They then entered to see."

    149

    峰峦嶷恶，树石怪丑，沙碛断涧，人迹全无。倏腥风至，木叶为脱，一象狂奔，后随一巴蛇，目闪，行如飞。象见戈伏地，若稽首状。父子急登象背，发连珠枪，中蛇之双目。蛇怒，行更速，象急负之狂奔，旁入大谷。蛇如箭急直驶，堕大崖下，如雷霆，毙矣。遂舍象觅路归。日暮，视谷口有古庙，权止宿。伏神龛中。夜静，闻庭树顶时有舐咂声与堕物声。向晨视之，树杪一大鸟，人首五色，羽大如车盖，巨爪搏死蛇食，堕者骨也。大惧，潜灌药发火，伺其飞起，击之，中胸际，声烈烈，啼如鬼车，展翅生大风，飞空际多时，堕则毙。父子大喜，负之归献，边将惊且诧，慰劳甚殷，嘱勿往。再数日，技痒，又入山。视前象立谷口，若拱候，试登之，果负而趋。倏又腥风至，私计曰：“又一蛇耶？”至绝岭下，象掀堕地，以鼻示之，伏草际。

    Explanation

    149

    The mountain peaks tower ominously, the trees and rocks appear strange and ugly, the sandy deserts and broken ravines are devoid of human traces. Suddenly, a foul wind arrives, stripping the leaves from the trees. An elephant runs wildly, followed by a large snake, its eyes flashing, moving as fast as flying. Seeing a weapon, the elephant lies down on the ground, as if bowing in respect. The father and son quickly climb onto the elephant's back, firing rapid-fire weapons that hit the snake's eyes. The snake, enraged, moves even faster. The elephant quickly carries it and runs wildly into a large valley. The snake moves straight and fast like an arrow, falling off a large cliff, making a sound like thunder, and dies. They then leave the elephant and find their way back. At dusk, they see an ancient temple at the entrance of the valley and decide to stay there for the night. They hide in the shrine. At night, they hear licking and sucking sounds and falling object sounds from the tops of the trees in the courtyard. In the morning, they see a large bird at the top of the tree, with a human-like head of five colors, feathers as large as a carriage canopy, large claws grasping a dead snake to eat, and the fallen objects are bones. They are greatly afraid, secretly pour medicine and light a fire, waiting for it to fly up, strike it, hit its chest, making a loud noise, crying like a ghostly carriage, spreading its wings and creating a strong wind, flying high for a long time, and falls and dies. The father and son are greatly happy, carry it back to present, and the border general is surprised and amazed, giving generous comfort and rewards, advising them not to go there again. A few more days later, they itch to show off their skills and go into the mountains again. They see the previous elephant standing at the entrance of the valley, like bowing in greeting, try to climb onto it, and it indeed carries and moves forward. Suddenly, another foul wind arrives, and they privately think, "Is it another snake?" Reaching the top of a cliff, the elephant throws it to the ground, showing it with its trunk, and they hide among the grass.

    150

    象去，引一兽来，首如驴，人足，白毛黑章，攫虎豹食，追象欲并食之。戈俟其过，急双枪发，中两乳。物大吼，人立，挝碎沙石塞乳际，拔大树离地，痛极长号，震山岳。戈视象已逸，再发枪，物惊向西去，堕壑毙。遂又舁之归献，边将更惊诧，曰：“尔真神勇也，从此可无须猎矣！”叩请其说，曰：“今上好奇，此鸟两翼天然有龙凤纹，夏日蝇不集，可作宫扇。此兽毛极暖，以瓯雪插一豪入，雪立化，可为御裘。行将献天子，我既膺上赏，尔亦得赐还也。”疏入，上喜甚，诏赐边将极优，赦戈父子，转送回籍，均赐官游击，仕邻封。遂叩别边将。甫登程十里外，突众象奔至，伏叩若感恩状。一象负戈父子行，一象舁大牙一支随。行十日，达山陕，父子下而拜祝，曰：“君其归乎，不敢劳远送，恐惊行人。

    Explanation

    150

    The elephant departed, leading another creature, whose head resembled a donkey, but with human feet, white fur marked with black spots. This creature pounced on tigers and leopards, devouring them, and pursued the elephant, intent on eating it too. The hunter, named Ge, waited for it to pass and quickly fired his two spears, striking both its breasts. The creature roared loudly, standing upright, and crushed sand and stones with its fists, filling the spaces between its breasts. It uprooted a large tree from the ground, howling in agony, its cries shaking the mountains. Ge saw that the elephant had fled and fired his spears again. The creature, startled, turned westward and fell into a ravine, dying. Ge then carried the creature back to present it to the local military commander, who was even more astonished. He exclaimed, "You truly are a god of bravery! From now on, you won't need to hunt anymore!" Ge asked for an explanation, and replied, "The current emperor is curious. This bird's wings naturally have dragon and phoenix patterns, and in summer, flies do not land on them, making them suitable for palace fans. This creature's fur is extremely warm; if you insert a single hair into a bowl of snow, the snow will immediately melt, making it ideal for a royal fur coat. We are about to present it to the emperor. I will receive a generous reward, and you will also be granted a return trip." The report was submitted, and the emperor was very pleased, issuing a decree to grant the local military commander exceptional rewards, pardoning Ge and his son, and sending them back to their home province, where they were both appointed as commanders and allowed to serve in neighboring regions. Ge then bid farewell to the local military commander. Just ten miles into his journey, a group of elephants suddenly rushed towards him, bowing as if grateful. One elephant carried Ge and his son, while another carried one of the creature's tusks. They traveled for ten days, reaching the Shanxi and Shaanxi provinces. Ge and his son dismounted and bowed, saying, "May you return, for we dare not trouble you with a long farewell, as we fear it might startle the travelers."

    151

    ”象亦悲鸣，置牙于戈前，相将返。戈赍牙，遇勾丽国使，惊曰：“此万年象齿也。”剖之，中有山水人物若墨画者，以万金购之去。由是富且贵。时御史出为皖抚，隶麾下，更感恩，恭献异域宝物甚多。抚曰：“女公子亦记忆否？”戈愕然。命之出拜，螺娘也。相与缕述，始共悲泣。抚喜，急为招女之父母，亲主婚，即命以螺娘字继辽，诹吉成礼。戈叟辞不仕。其子官寿春参将，升六合镇军，迎父与妻父母就养于署。叟暇，犹以枪法授帐下健儿，至今寿春背枪，为天下冠。懊侬氏曰：吾尝怪天之生物也，犬守夜，鸡司晨，羊触邪，马致远，豚适口，亦已足矣。而深山穷谷之中，又有锯牙钩爪，怪怪奇奇者在，味既不甘，性犹好杀，毋乃过欤？顷因戈家父子生还一事，然后知虞翳送勒之派，既为圣主报祯祥，且为孝慈赎罪过。碧翁生且育之，亦良有故耳。

    Explanation

    151

    The elephant also mourned, placing its tusk before Gao, and they returned together. Gao carried the tusk and encountered an envoy from Goryeo, who exclaimed, "This is a tusk from a thousand-year-old elephant!" They cut it open, and inside were landscapes, figures, and scenes like ink paintings, which they purchased for ten thousand gold pieces. Thus, Gao became rich and noble. At that time, the censor was appointed as the governor of Anhui and had Gao under his command. Gao was even more grateful and offered many exotic treasures. The governor asked, "Do you remember the young lady?" Gao was startled. He ordered her to come out and bow, and it was Luoniang. They recounted the details together and began to cry. The governor was pleased and quickly arranged for Luoniang's parents to be summoned, personally presiding over the wedding and ordering that Luoniang be married to Jixiao, choosing a favorable day to complete the ceremony. Gao's father declined to take office. His son held the rank of General in Shouchun and was later promoted to General of Liuhe, welcoming his father and Luoniang's parents to live in the official residence. In his spare time, Gao's father still taught gun techniques to the soldiers under his command, and to this day, the backslab gun in Shouchun is the best in the world. The author says, "I often wonder why heaven creates things. Dogs guard at night, chickens crow at dawn, sheep ward off evil, horses travel far, and pigs provide food—these are sufficient. But in the deep mountains and remote valleys, there are also creatures with sharp teeth and claws, strange and bizarre, whose taste is not pleasant, yet they are still fond of killing. Is this not excessive? Recently, due to the story of Gao's father and son being alive, I then understood the lineage of Yu Yi sending the tusk, which reported auspicious signs to the holy emperor and redeemed the sins of filial piety and kindness. The Green Old Man raised and nurtured it, and there must be a good reason for it."

    152

    珊珊
    　　
    　　楚之凤凰厅，万山中有石亭，颜曰“苗姑救夫处”。间疑为跳月人绣帕涅面，吹芦笙呜呜声，自觅藁砧故事，而不知其非也。明季，焦生鼐，字梅仲，中州人，任侠放生，读书学剑，偕友游汴之上河。时值清明，士女如织。有健儿弄虎演剧者，围观若堵墙。虎眇一目，爪牙钩刺，文质斑斓。弄者故以头触其吻，手捋其须，背承其腹，而虎且宛转如人意。众掷青蚨，胜撒白雨。市散，驱入大木函，荷之去。

    Explanation

    152

    In the midst of ten thousand mountains in the state of Chu, there stands a stone pavilion known as the Phoenix Hall. The pavilion is named "The Place Where Miao Girl Saved Her Husband." Some have suspected it to be the site of the "Jumping Moon" festival, where participants wear embroidered handkerchiefs and face paint, and play the lute, producing a mournful sound, while searching for stories about a husband, but they did not know that it was not so. In the late Ming dynasty, there was a scholar-warrior named Jiao Nai, courtesy name Meizhong, from the central plains region of China. He was known for his chivalry and compassion, studying books and learning swordsmanship. He traveled with friends along the Bian River in Bianzhou (modern-day Kaifeng). It was during the Qingming Festival, when men and women gathered in large numbers. There was a performance involving strong men and a tiger, surrounded by a dense crowd. The tiger had one blind eye, sharp claws and teeth, and a beautiful, variegated fur. The performer intentionally touched the tiger's mouth with his head, stroked its whiskers with his hand, and supported its belly with his back, and the tiger moved gracefully as if to please the audience. The crowd threw coins, and the winners scattered them like white rain. When the market dispersed, the tiger was driven into a large wooden box and carried away.

    153

    生归而冥想，太息曰：“丈夫不能自全，误落陷阱，亦犹是夫！”友戏曰：“然则封使君亦将买而放之乎？”曰：“有何不可？”夜寝，梦老父闯然入，白衣绛冠，向生拱揖，曰：“封使君谪限已满，郎君若仗义侠，放归山林，则得美妇，解奇厄，证仙果，功德无量也。”生曰：“弄虎者以之攫阿堵，为衣食券，恐靳而不售也。”曰：“有机可乘。”生一诺而醒，朝暾满窗，起呼盥栉，挈友再往。至则鸣钲开场，虎摇尾瞑目，意甚颓败。倏一老叟，科头袒背而前，骑虎背，虎颔，更以髡颅抵唇侧。虎忽大吼，利喙一合，则头脆如瓠落矣。观者尽奔，两健儿哭曰：“杀者吾父也！虎向驯，不知何故突变性！将杀毙抵吾父！”子操刀欲砍，生忽止之曰：“子迂矣！虎噬人，性也。即毙之，岂即能抵尔父？人财两空，殊失算计。

    Explanation

    153

    The young man returned home and pondered deeply, sighing and saying, "A man cannot save himself, and I have fallen into a trap by mistake, just as I have!" His friend joked, "Then will Governor Feng also buy and release it?" He replied, "Why not?" That night, as he slept, he dreamed of an old father suddenly entering, wearing white clothes and a red hat, facing him with a bow and saying, "Governor Feng's term of exile is over. If you, young man, act with righteousness and bravery, and release him back to the mountains and forests, you will obtain a beautiful wife, resolve extraordinary difficulties, achieve immortality, and gain infinite merit." The young man said, "Those who play with tigers might seize something as a means of livelihood, but they probably won't sell it." The old father said, "There is an opportunity." With a promise, the young man awoke. The morning sunlight filled the window, and he got up, called for washing and grooming, and took his friend to go again. Upon arriving, the bell was rung to open the market, and the tiger wagged its tail and closed its eyes, seeming very dejected. Suddenly, an old man appeared, with his head uncovered and his back bare, riding on the tiger's back, biting the tiger's jaw, and using his shaven head to press against the side of the tiger's mouth. The tiger suddenly roared loudly, and the sharp beak closed, making the head as fragile as a gourd. The spectators all ran away, and two strong boys cried and said, "The one who was killed was my father! The tiger was usually tame, but for some unknown reason, it suddenly changed its nature! To kill it and use it to pay for my father!" The son took a knife and wanted to chop, but the young man suddenly stopped him, saying, "You are being foolish! Tigers eat people, it is their nature. Even if you kill it, how could it immediately pay for your father? Both people and money would be wasted, it would be a great mistake."

    154

    ”曰：“将奈何？”曰：“曷卖于我，以资殓尔父，余则另作生涯，此计之善者。”健儿私议久之，以为然。问其值，曰十万钱，如数交兑。生命仆人放之去，咸不敢，曰：“索在颈，尚噬人，若解去，不将继叟伥耶？”生怒，自策马，送虎至深山中，曰：“荒野穷岩，不少生物，幸勿扰行路，罪株小生。”虎颔之，独目若流涕。生亲解铛锒，急上马返辔，挥之曰：“去！”遂分道行。甫转官衢，忽狂飚骤起，砂石横飞，虎至，急夺路，则已伏马前，叩数十下始去。归告友，咸不甚信。是秋礼闱获解，往应南宫试。行至燕赵间，仆马奔驰，日色已堕，疏林叠，倏迷路歧。忽林中矗出欹石，高丈余，瘦削可爱，炊烟缕缕。

    Explanation

    154

    The man replied, "What shall we do?" The tiger answered, "Why not sell me to you? You can use the money to bury your father, and I can start a new life. This is a good plan." The young man discussed this with his companions for a long time and they agreed. They asked about its value, and the tiger said ten thousand coins. They exchanged the full amount. The man then ordered his servant to let the tiger go, but the servant was afraid, saying, "The rope is around its neck, and it still bites people. If you remove it, won't you become the next follower of the tiger?" The man became angry and rode the horse himself, taking the tiger to a deep mountain. He said, "In this desolate wilderness and rugged cliffs, there are few living things. Please do not trouble the travelers, do not blame me." The tiger nodded, and its eyes looked like they were shedding tears. The man personally removed the rope, quickly mounted the horse, and turned back, waving and saying, "Go away!" They then went their separate ways. As soon as he turned onto the official street, suddenly a fierce wind arose, and sand and stones flew around. The tiger arrived, and the man hurriedly took the road, but the tiger was already in front of the horse. It knocked several times before leaving. Upon returning, he told his friends, but they did not believe it very much. That autumn, he passed the imperial examination and went to the palace to take the exam. Traveling through the regions of Yan and Zhao, the servant and horse were galloping, and the sun had already set. The sparse forest layers made it easy to get lost in the fork of the road. Suddenly, a tilted stone appeared in the forest, more than ten feet high, slender and lovely, with smoke from cooking fires in thin wisps.

    155

    知有野人家，趋求止宿。则老屋数椽，门临曲涧，一眇目老叟龙钟迓客，曰：“何处贵人，下顾草野？”生自陈名氏，且告所求。邀入坐草堂中，仆马亦有安置。叟衣冠整洁，言语粗豪，自言苗姓，向客中州，遄归未久。倏一红妆屏角窥客，又一老媪上堂篝灯，蹀躞颇苦。叟曰：“寒家无仆御，此山荆也。”生局促不安，意在呼仆，曰：“累夫人不当。”叟止之曰：“纲纪劳乏，已安栖止。”向屏内呼曰：“大姑姗姗儿，出拜郎君，一代母劳。”女果盈盈趋前裣衽。生见其媚态万方，神魂飞越，几致失礼。揖而问叟，曰：“女公子耶？”曰：“然。以郎君贵人，敢以女儿相见。”须臾，肴陈于案，酒沸于铛，叟以巨瓯自饮，以常樽劝客。

    Explanation

    155

    Knowing of a wild man's home, he sought shelter for the night. The house was a simple thatched hut, with a door facing a winding ravine. An elderly man with one blind eye, bent and leaning on a staff, greeted the guest, saying, "Who is the esteemed person, and why have you come to visit this humble place?" The guest stated his name and surname, and informed of his request. He was invited to sit in a thatched hut, and the servant and horse were also taken care of. The old man was neatly dressed, with rough and bold speech. He introduced himself as surnamed Miao, saying he had just returned from the Central Plains. Suddenly, a woman in red makeup peered at the guest from behind the screen, and an elderly woman came up to light the lamp, moving about with some difficulty. The old man said, "My humble home has no servants. This is my wife." The guest felt uneasy and constrained, intending to call for a servant, saying, "It's not right to trouble the lady." The old man stopped him, saying, "The servants are tired. They have been settled." He called out to the screen, "Eldest daughter, come out to bow to the young gentleman. So that I don't have to trouble the mother." The girl indeed came out gracefully to bow. The guest saw her charming and alluring demeanor, his soul transported, almost losing himself. He bowed and asked the old man, "Is she your daughter?" "Yes," the old man replied. "As the young gentleman is an esteemed person, I dare to present my daughter to you." In a short while, food was laid out on the table, and wine was boiling in the pot. The old man drank from a large bowl, offering regular cups to the guests.

    156

    酒阑，女出，为生解装设榻，布枕拂衾，殷勤臻至。生逊谢，女一笑去。餐已，叟诣内与媪絮语，遂不出。生醉而隐几，女摇生醒，曰：“郎可寝矣。”曰：“卿尚未去耶？”曰：“父母遣视安枕，防呼茗饮耳。”问：“芳龄几何？”曰：“十六。”问：“有婿家否？”女酡然久之，微嗔曰：“夜深可寝，絮絮何为？恐老亲闻知，叱辱将及。”生倚醉，遽揽红袖，女挣脱移立，不得近，遂寝。醒则吻燥，试呼茗，则女已捧磁盎立榻下。生饮已，牵玉臂求欢，女呼曰：“鲁莽儿！何动欲丧人廉耻？”叟媪内呼问，手释，女急遁。意将诟谇，转寂然。天明，晨光透，女起，出洒扫。生惴惴不敢语。

    Explanation

    156

    The wine had run out, and the woman left, helping the guest remove his clothes and set up a bed. She laid out a pillow and smoothed the quilt, attending to him with great care. The guest modestly declined, and the woman smiled and left. After the meal, the old man went inside to chat with the old woman, and did not come out. The guest, drunk, leaned against the table, and the woman gently woke him, saying, "You may sleep now." He asked, "Have you not yet left?" She replied, "My parents sent me to see if you were comfortable, in case you needed tea." He asked, "How old are you?" She said, "Sixteen." He asked, "Do you have a husband?" The woman blushed for a long time, then slightly scolded, "It is late, and you should sleep. Why are you talking so much? I fear my parents will hear and scold me." The guest, half-drunk, suddenly grabbed her sleeve, but she挣脱 and moved away, unable to get close, so he slept. When he woke, his mouth was dry, and he called for tea. The woman had already brought a ceramic jar and stood at the foot of the bed. After he drank, he grabbed her jade arm to seek pleasure. She shouted, "Rash boy! Why do you act so as to lose one's sense of shame?" The old man and old woman called out from inside, and she freed herself by hand, fleeing in haste. She intended to scold him, but suddenly became silent. The guest was nervous and did not dare to speak. At dawn, the morning light came through, and the woman got up, went out to clean. The guest was anxious and dared not speak.

    157

    女呼曰：“郎起耶？满天风雪，真天留客也。”生披衣视庭外，果花飞六出，片如掌大。旋进盥具，更沏苦茶。女笑曰：“痴郎子，昨宵几惊破胆！”曰：“忍哉，卿也！”曰：“柔情媚骨，何必尔尔！”生益惑，语渐狎亵。女秋波微怒，似又欲呼，生哀之，始已。临去，忽红涨于面，欲言又止者再。曰：“郎娶否？”曰：“未。”曰：“真耶？”曰：“天日可誓。”曰：“郎求婚于吾父母，无不谐。万勿望非礼苟合也。”生曰：“诺。”时仆亦起，问生行否，生痴立犹豫。叟出，挥仆夫曰：“茫茫风雪，向何处去？霁即行，岂碍程途耶？”少顷，女子又陈餐膳，饼饵带松子香，雉羹鹿脯味尤美。生且啖且问女郎年齿，与婿家姓氏。

    Explanation

    157

    The woman called out, "Young man, have you risen yet? The snowflakes fill the sky, truly as if heaven is keeping us as guests." He put on his clothes and looked outside, seeing snowflakes as large as a palm. She quickly brought the bathing supplies and brewed a bitter tea. She teased, "Foolish young man, you nearly scared me out of my wits last night!" He retorted, "Why be so harsh, my dear?" She replied, "Why must you be so gentle and charming?" Their conversation grew increasingly intimate. Her gaze, like autumn waves, showed a slight anger, and she seemed about to call out, but he pleaded with her, and she relented. As she was leaving, she blushed and hesitated twice before speaking. She asked, "Are you married?" He answered, "Not yet." She pressed, "Is it really true?" He swore by heaven and earth, "It is true." She said, "If you ask for my hand in marriage from my parents, they will agree. But do not hope for improper or immoral behavior." He agreed. At the time, the servant also rose and asked if the young man was ready to leave. He stood there dazed and indecisive. The old man came out and told the servant, "The vast wind and snow, where are you going? Once the weather clears, we can continue our journey without hindrance." Soon, the woman brought out a meal, the baked goods had the aroma of pine nuts, and the pheasant stew and dried venison were especially delicious. He ate while asking about her age and the name of her family.

    158

    叟答以择配甚难，红鸾犹缺。生曰：“仆不才，尚属清门，忝登桂籍，未知可列雀屏选否？”叟曰：“姗姗甚倔强，容归与山荆询明白，免他日怨老朽孟浪。”顷出，告生曰：“大喜大喜，小妮子竟首肯。但夫妇老矣，风烛草霜，一朝殒谢，反累弱息茕独。山野无鼓乐傧相，意屈东床即于今夕草草花烛，明即携去。愧无妆奁，能相谅否？”生喜极再拜，一一承命。媪扶女出，韦布新更，云环微掠，愈觉妩媚动人。交拜讫，重设尊，一家团聚。仆在斗室，亦小犒赏，痛饮极欢。夜深，二老去，生移灯掩门，即就客榻成婚礼，缱绻恩爱，盟誓万言。明晨雪霁，叟媪并出，曰：“姗姗儿娇惯，乞郎君百事看老朽，勿加罪责。

    Explanation

    158

    The old man replied that finding a suitable partner was very difficult, and the red phoenix (symbol of marriage) was still missing. The young man said, "I am not talented, but I come from a respectable family and have passed the imperial examinations, entering the ranks of scholars. May I be considered for the Screen of Magpies (a metaphor for being chosen as a potential bridegroom)?" The old man said, "My daughter is somewhat stubborn. Please allow me to consult with my wife (山荆) before making a decision, so as not to blame me for acting rashly in the future." After a short while, he came out and told the young man, "Great news, my daughter has agreed. But we are old, and our lives are as fragile as wind and candle, like grass in the frost. One day, we may pass away, leaving our daughter alone. We have no drumming, music, or ceremonial attendants. I suggest that you be the East Bed (a term for a prospective son-in-law) and we can perform a simple wedding ceremony tonight. We have no dowry, but I hope you can understand and forgive us." The young man was extremely happy and bowed deeply, accepting all the arrangements. The old woman helped the daughter out, who was dressed in new clothes, with her hair slightly swept up, looking even more charming and lovely. After the exchange of bows, they set up a new seat, and the family gathered together. The servant in the small room was also given a small reward, and they drank to their hearts' content. At night, the old couple left, and the young man moved the lamp, locked the door, and performed the wedding ceremony on the guest bed, filled with deep affection and love, making countless vows. The next morning, after the snow melted, the old man and woman came out and said, "My daughter is spoiled, so please be considerate and patient with us, old man, and do not blame us for anything."

    159

    双双登程，不敢以私爱误功名事也。”母流涕悲恋曰：“勉事郎君，锦旋时可一归宁，何须戚戚！”生以马授女，自则与仆徒步，拜别出门。叟于欹石下，掀卧石起，内皆朱提，曰：“仓卒不及备奁，以此为倩。”生曰：“客途无玉台下聘，尚敢领厚贶乎？”曰：“聊壮行色耳。”生勉取三锭，叟以为太少，尽代捡入囊，挥之曰：“去！”出山数十里，入一大城市，为女购簪珥裙服，崭然一新。再觅车马，入都赁宅居，倡随乐甚。榜发，成进士，授浙之会稽令。挈夫人同之官，多政声，皆内助也。然生性好客，旧雨新云，争来趋附。明年，升钱塘太守，而客益多。女请却之，不听。客闻之惧，醵千金购妖姬名窈娘者，奉生为妾。

    Explanation

    159

    Both ascended the journey, daring not to let personal love interfere with their pursuit of fame and success. The mother wept and grieved with affection, saying, "Please serve your husband diligently, and when you return victorious, you may come back once to visit your parents. Why be sorrowful?" The husband gave his wife a horse, while he himself and his servant traveled on foot, bowing and bidding farewell as they left the house. The old man, beneath a leaning stone, lifted up a sleeping stone, revealing that it contained pure gold, and said, "In haste, I couldn't prepare a proper dowry, so I offer this as a substitute." The husband replied, "On a journey, there is no need for formal engagement rituals. How dare I accept such generous gifts?" The old man said, "Just to boost your morale." The husband reluctantly took three ingots, but the old man thought it too little and helped him put them all into his bag, waving him away and saying, "Go!" After leaving the mountain for dozens of miles, they entered a large city where the husband bought new hairpins, earrings, skirts, and clothes for his wife. They then sought out carriages and horses, rented a house in the capital, and lived happily together. When the imperial examination results were announced, the husband passed and was appointed as the magistrate of Kuaiji in Zhejiang. He brought his wife with him to take up the post, and she was praised for her many contributions to his success. However, the husband had a fondness for guests, and both old and new friends vied to attach themselves to him. The following year, he was promoted to the position of governor of Qiantang, and the number of guests increased even more. His wife asked him to refuse them, but he did not listen. Hearing this, the guests were afraid and pooled a thousand gold to buy a beautiful woman named Yao Niang, intending to serve the husband as a concubine.

    160

    窈娘色既艳冶，弦索歌唱无一不工，床第之间，尤多内媚。生惑之，嬖昵忘政事，而客皆隐攘其权矣。女独宿，决不争夕。然生偶抱恙，女辄鸡鸣起，侍汤药，不啻孝子。窈娘见女肢体发肤无处不美，即乱头粗服亦饶姿致；退而揽镜，愈自惭汗。由爱生惭，惭生妒，妒生恨，遂广结婢媵，环布腹心，思倾女，不得入。暗以鸩毒置酥酪中，布女室。生偶入呼饥，女以酥酪进，窈急夺而弃之，啖猫犬，立毙。乃娇啼求去，曰：“夫人妒忌，意毒良人。妾若不去，恐难免也。”又女每夜焚香于庭，礼拜北斗，潜告生，曰：“夫人毒未成，又用诅禳法，妾时心痛，恐中魔巫。

    Explanation

    160

    Yao Niang was both alluring and refined in appearance, and she was proficient in all forms of music and singing. In private moments, she was especially charming. The protagonist became so enamored that he neglected his duties, and the guests all secretly seized power. The other woman would never share the bed, even if offered. However, when the protagonist occasionally fell ill, she would rise at dawn to serve him with tea and medicine, as if she were a filial son. Yao Niang saw that the other woman was beautiful in every aspect, even with disheveled hair and coarse clothing, and she still had a graceful presence; when she looked in the mirror, she felt even more ashamed. From love came shame, from shame came jealousy, from jealousy came hatred. She then widely connected with maids and concubines, surrounding herself with trusted allies, and tried to undermine the other woman but failed. She secretly placed poison in the butter and cheese, spreading it in the other woman's room. When the protagonist entered and called for food, the other woman offered him the butter and cheese. Yao Niang quickly took it and threw it away, and the poison killed the cat and dog instantly. The other woman then cried pitifully to leave, saying, "The mistress is jealous and intends to harm the husband. If I do not leave, I fear I will not escape." She also burned incense in the courtyard every night, worshipping the Big Dipper, and secretly informed the protagonist, saying, "The mistress's poison failed, so she resorted to curses and spells. I often feel heartache, fearing I have been bewitched."

    161

    ”由是生怒女，动辄得咎，曰：“终非好相识！”立逼大归。女泣曰：“自为君妇，有何失德？”曰：“吾与尔缘尽，眼中钉，喉中骨鲠，不能顷刻留！”女大恸。曰：“若留，须跪受鞭笞始已。”女即膝立受辱，婢媪争伏女旁，愿代受杖。邑之仕官眷属闻之，咸不平，声名益狼藉。当道者罗织生之荒怠酒色，侵蚀库币十余款，欲劾之。生惧，谋于客，出千金，购玉鼎，将献中丞。又出千金，购冬貂，献侍御。同列中堂，鼎无故碎，裘无故焚，至问谁毁，窈坚以夫人对。生大怒，狂呼不可忍，操杖立逐女出。女曰：“是真不可留矣！”自脱簪珥裙服掷地下，着嫁衣，匆匆出门，飘瞥不见。当道待生贿不至，疏劾之，奉旨降官东鲁滕阳丞。

    Explanation

    161

    The husband became angry with his wife, often blaming her, and said, "We were never meant to be good friends!" He immediately forced her to return home. The wife wept, saying, "Since becoming your wife, what wrong have I done?" He replied, "Our karmic connection has ended. You are a thorn in my eye, a bone stuck in my throat. I cannot bear to keep you for even a second!" The wife was greatly distressed. He said, "If you want to stay, you must kneel and accept beating until it stops." The wife immediately knelt and endured the humiliation, with maids and servants rushing to kneel beside her, willing to take the beating in her place. The officials and their families in the county heard of this and all felt unjust, and his reputation became even more tarnished. Those in power fabricated charges against him for his negligence and indulgence in alcohol and women, accusing him of embezzling more than ten items of treasury funds, and intended to impeach him. The husband was afraid and sought advice from his guests. He spent a thousand gold pieces to buy a jade tripod to present to the Governor, and another thousand gold pieces to buy a winter fur coat to offer to the Imperial Censor. While they were in the same hall, the tripod shattered inexplicably, and the fur coat burned without reason. When asked who destroyed them, the wife firmly replied it was her. The husband screamed in anger, unable to tolerate it, and promptly drove his wife out with a stick. The wife said, "This truly cannot be tolerated anymore!" She removed her hairpins, earrings, skirt, and robe and threw them on the ground, put on her wedding clothes, and hurried out the door, vanishing in an instant. Those in power, expecting a bribe from the husband, did not receive it and submitted a report to impeach him. The emperor issued an edict demoting him to the position of County Magistrate of Tengyang in Eastern Lu.

    162

    婢仆与客，一时星散。生典质玩物，得千金，携窈就丞任。策马悠悠，误入山谷。见疏林烟里，欹石犹存，忽至当年止宿处，大惊，恐翁媪出，无颜相见，勒马不前，遣仆觇视，则空林无屋宇，仅曲涧流泉，荒苔虎迹，急趋而过。丞任清苦异常，窈不能堪，终日悲啼，生唯隐忍。旋抱病，呼窈不至，盖早已随仆遁去。至是始悔，恸曰：“其负吾结发苗姑报乎？”而已无及矣。更以行赇革职，充云南军。赭衣登程，监者呵詈，资斧一空，货马徒走，两足肿溃，踯躅不前。

    Explanation

    162

    Servants and guests scattered like stars in an instant. The protagonist pawned valuable items to raise a thousand gold coins and took Yao to his new post. Riding leisurely, he mistakenly entered a mountain valley. Seeing sparse trees and smoke in the distance, and leaning stones still standing, he suddenly arrived at the place where he had once stayed, and was greatly shocked. Fearing that the elderly couple might appear, he felt ashamed to face them, so he pulled the reins to stop his horse, and sent a servant to discreetly inspect. The servant returned to report that there was only a winding stream and flowing water in the empty forest, with patches of moss and tiger tracks. The protagonist hurriedly passed by. The post was exceptionally poor and hardworking, and Yao could not endure it, weeping all day. The protagonist endured in silence. Soon, he fell ill and called for Yao, but she did not come, having already fled with the servants. At this point, he began to regret, and lamented, "Has Yao betrayed our lifelong bond?" But it was too late. He was further dismissed from office for bribery and sent to serve in the Yunnan military. Wearing the brownish-red clothing of a convict, he set off on his journey, only to be scolded and berated by the supervisor. His funds were completely depleted, and he had to sell his horse and walk. His feet were swollen and ulcerated, and he hesitated to move forward.

    163

    比至凤凰厅，万山中人迹断绝处，有亭翼然，监者引入，嗔目叱曰：“尔罪应受，我辈何辜？请速自戕，免污吾刃！”生哀涕不已，监者操刀而至。正惶急间，忽腥风怒号，一白额猛虎自绝岭下，爪搏监者，三人死路侧。生亦迷闷。微苏，觉耳畔有妇人哀唤声，启眸视之，非他，姗姗苗大姑也。生反痛哭，曰：“夫妻邂逅，得毋梦中乎，抑冥中乎？”曰：“窈娘何在，客又何在？”生以头触地，泣言知悔。问：“虎究何往，卿究何来？”曰：“郎至此，量言亦无惧。妾非人，虎也。郎在中州所放者，妾生身父也。父母感大德，遣侍巾栉，又以无状被逐。若非大难当前，实无颜见夫子。然真面目已露，郎能无以非类见疑乎！”曰：“岂但无疑！”言已，抽刀断拇指，血涔涔焉。女惊救之，已断，急出药末糁而接之，裹以残帛，竟不痛。

    Explanation

    163

    When the protagonist arrived at the Phoenix Hall, in a remote part of the mountains where human tracks were cut off, there stood a pavilion with wings spread out. A guard led him in and, glaring angrily, shouted, "Your punishment is deserved; what guilt do we bear? Please quickly take your own life to avoid staining my blade!" The protagonist continued to weep in sorrow, and the guard approached with a knife. Just as the protagonist was in a state of panic, suddenly a fierce wind roared, and a white-faced tiger leaped down from a distant peak, clawing at the guard. All three died by the roadside. The protagonist was also confused and dazed. As he barely regained consciousness, he heard a woman's sorrowful cry beside him. Opening his eyes, he saw that it was none other than Miss Miaodagugu. The protagonist turned to weep and said, "Is this not a dream, or is it in the afterlife?" She asked, "Where is Yoyaoniang? Where is the guest?" The protagonist knocked his head to the ground and wept, saying he knew he was in the wrong. He asked, "Where has the tiger gone? Where did you come from?" She replied, "If you have come here, I have no fear. I am not a human; I am a tiger. The one you released in Zhongzhou is my biological father. My parents were deeply grateful for your great kindness and sent me to serve you, but I was later expelled for my misbehavior. If it were not for this great calamity, I would not have the face to see you. But my true form has been revealed; will you not doubt me as a monster?" The protagonist said, "Not only am I not doubtful!" After saying that, he drew a knife and cut off his thumb, blood flowing continuously. The woman was shocked and tried to save him. It was already cut off, so she quickly applied some medicinal powder and reattached it, wrapping it with some tattered cloth. It didn't hurt at all.

    164

    曰：“郎君既悔，又何必尔！”曰：“非此无以对我贤卿也。”问：“翁媪何往？”曰：“天谪已满，重证仙班，不在人间矣。南山之南尚有敝庐，能惠临否？”曰：“逃军杀监，出则领断，茫茫海内，托足无区，愿随卿隐。”曰：“以郎资质，堪破泡幻，大丹且成。”言已，携手同行。穿云越涧，约十余里，怪石数转，忽见洞府，门前长须赤脚者三四辈，翘首拱候，呼曰：“大姑救得郎君归矣。”问：“此数辈何来？”曰：“老父遣留婢仆，侍郎君耳。”入见釜铛鼎臼，几案床第，无一非石。曲折数层，若分内外。西偏一洞，为女卧房，房内陈设古雅，帷帐悉具。床上坐一婴儿，呀呀索乳。

    Explanation

    164

    The young master said, "Since you have already repented, why do you need to do this?" The other replied, "Without this, I cannot show my respect to my esteemed lady." The first asked, "Where are the old man and woman going?" The other answered, "The heavenly punishment has been fulfilled, and they have been reborn into the ranks of immortals, no longer in the mortal world. There is still a humble cottage to the south of Mount Nanshan. Can you grace us with your presence?" The first responded, "Fleeing soldiers and killing guards, if one leaves, they face execution; in the vast land, there is no place to put one's feet. I wish to follow you into seclusion." The other said, "Given your noble qualities, you can transcend illusion, and the great elixir is nearly complete." After speaking, they held hands and walked together. They passed through clouds and crossed ravines, traveling about ten miles. After several turns among strange stones, they suddenly saw a cave temple. There were three or four people with long beards and bare feet at the entrance, bowing and waiting respectfully. They called out, "Aunt has saved the young master and brought him back." The first asked, "Where did these people come from?" The other replied, "My father sent these servants to attend to the young master." Upon entering, they saw pots, cauldrons, mortars, tables, beds, and everything was made of stone. The structure was winding and had several levels, as if divided into inner and outer parts. To the west was a cave, which served as the lady's bedroom. The room was elegantly furnished, with all the curtains and bedding in place. On the bed sat an infant, cooing and crying for milk.

    165

    问是谁氏子，曰：“此君种也。渠外大父命名曰寅生。”生亲与摩顶，见其丰丽魁梧，知是国器。夜夕燃石灯，出石瓮中花酿饮生，烹茯苓松花饼啖生。晨起，督婢仆各出采药，自以野蚕织布，无一废驰。寅生五岁，颇慧。生拾树叶为笺，燃松枝为笔，抄书教儿读。十岁即通六经，能韵语。生悲曰：“吾负罪窜匿，累娇儿何时出头？”女问：“中州有手足否？”曰：“有”。问：“曾受君惠否？”曰：“有从堂弟，名者，从未贷一钱。”女以纤指卜再四，曰：“是真可托。”翌日早起，呼秃发僮驾牛车抱儿端坐，以生手书置儿怀，自脱金钏束儿腕，并与玉瓶，曰：“需果饵，此中索即得。”安置讫，遽挥曰：“去！”车如电掣风驰，突入云际。

    Explanation

    165

    Who is this child? It was said, "This is the son of the man." His maternal grandfather named him Yinsheng. The man personally touched his head, seeing that he was tall, handsome, and dignified, knowing him to be a person of great talent. At night, he lit a stone lamp, poured out wine from a stone jar for the child to drink, and cooked porridge made from茯苓 (a type of fungus) and松花 (pine flower) for the child to eat. In the morning, he supervised the maids and servants to go out and gather herbs, and he himself wove cloth from wild silkworms, not allowing any of them to slack off. Yinsheng was five years old and quite intelligent. The man picked up leaves to make paper, used a burning pine branch as a pen, and copied books to teach the child to read. By the age of ten, Yinsheng had mastered the Six Classics and could write poetry. The man lamented, "I am guilty and have gone into hiding, burdening my beloved child. When will he be able to face the world?" The woman asked, "Do you have any close relatives in the Central Plains?" He replied, "Yes." She asked, "Have they ever received your kindness?" He replied, "I have a cousin named [name], who has never borrowed even a single coin from me." The woman used her fine finger to divinate several times and said, "He is truly trustworthy." The next day, early in the morning, she called a bald servant to drive a cow cart, had the child sit upright in it, placed the man's handwritten letter in the child's arms, took off a gold bracelet and tied it around the child's wrist, and gave him a jade bottle, saying, "If you need fruits or snacks, you can ask for them here and they will be given to you immediately." After everything was set up, she quickly waved and said, "Go!" The cart moved as fast as lightning and the wind, suddenly entering the clouds.

    166

    生失声哭，女笑曰：“君别儿即苦，妾父母嫁女时亦苦耳。何一入宦途，顿加白眼？”生大愧，以指示女，曰：“卿忘却耶？”相与大笑。焦，中州名士也，四十无子以为忧；族人子蓬头历齿，不欲继。夫人为置妾，又恐分恩爱，不肯受。是日，忽牛车到门，僮抱儿入，投书案上。拆阅，见的真为兄鼐笔迹，大喜，阅至托儿为嗣一节，更觉欢欣。一瞥眼，僮与牛车不见，唯儿束钏捧瓶依膝下。市人哄闹，曰：“焦家门内，豹负猩猩，奔出城去。”夫妻爱儿逾所生，寝必搂于怀，食必加诸膝。冬日病，思樱桃不得，儿忽捧金丸至，问何来，笑指瓶以母语告。戏呼他物，无不应，由是大富。寅生冠而就试，贵为大中丞，征云南寇。

    Explanation

    166

    When the man cried out in grief, the woman laughed and said, "When you part from your child, you are sad; when my parents were marrying off a daughter, it was also hard. Why, once you enter the bureaucratic career, do you suddenly give a cold shoulder?" The man felt deeply ashamed and showed her the letter, saying, "Have you forgotten?" They laughed together. Jiao was a famous scholar from the Central Plains, and at forty, he had no son, which troubled him. His cousin, with a messy appearance and gaps in his teeth, was not a desirable match. His wife arranged for a concubine, but he was afraid of dividing his affection and refused. On that day, a cart suddenly arrived at the door, and a servant brought a child inside, placing a letter on the table. When he opened and read it, he saw that it was indeed written by his brother Nai, and he was greatly pleased. As he read the part about entrusting the child to be his heir, he felt even more joy. In a blink, the servant and the cart disappeared, leaving only the child holding a bracelet and a vase, leaning on his knees. The townspeople were in a commotion, saying, "Inside Jiao's house, a leopard is carrying a chimpanzee, rushing out of the city." The couple loved the child more than their natural-born child, holding him in their arms at night and placing him on their knees during meals. In winter, when the child was sick and unable to find cherries, he suddenly brought a golden ball, and when asked where it came from, he smiled and pointed to the vase, speaking in his mother's language. Whenever they called for something else, he always responded, and thus they became greatly wealthy. Yin, after coming of age, took the imperial exams and became a high-ranking official, campaigning against the rebels in Yunnan.

    167

    时夫妇寿八十，犹健饭，领军拜别，谕功成速回，顺路访亲生父母。寅泣受教。凯旋时，果访至旧处，则洞口云迷，树叶零落而已。痛哭榛莽，视石壁镌草书一行，曰：“中州焦鼐，遇虎得生，洞居卅载，吐纳通神。天降丹颗，服之身轻。水火调御，夫妇道成。某年月日，白昼飞升。儿读能贵，勉事圣君。石啮流水，岭横白云。人间天上，一样看承。”懊侬氏曰：人虽至愚，当其受恩，则未尝不疾首抚心，以为苟渝此盟，有如江水。及至嬖昵既久，责报太苛，反面若仇，有终身切齿者。姗姑姗姑，既报德于未遇之时，又救之极危既穷之后，其亦愧夫人而须眉者乎！至于女子小人，谗谮惑主，虎且惮之矣。噫嘻！姗姑，慎勿孟浪唱《想夫怜》也！

    Explanation

    167

    At the time, the couple was eighty years old and still healthy and eating well. The leader bid them farewell, instructing them to return quickly after their success and to visit their biological parents on the way. Yin wept and accepted the instruction. Upon their triumphant return, they indeed visited the old place, but the cave entrance was shrouded in mist, and the leaves had fallen. Crying bitterly among the thorny bushes, they saw a line of grass script engraved on a stone wall, which read: "From Central China, Jiao Nai survived a tiger encounter, lived in a cave for thirty years, and practiced breathing techniques to achieve divine abilities. A pill descended from heaven, and when consumed, it made the body light. Mastering the control of water and fire, the couple completed their Taoist practices. On a certain date, they ascended during the day. Their child should read and serve a virtuous ruler. The rocks gnaw at the flowing water, and the mountains are covered in white clouds. Both heaven and earth are viewed equally." Aongnong said: "Even the most foolish person, when receiving kindness, cannot help but feel deeply moved, thinking that breaking such an oath is as impossible as stopping the flow of a river. But after long familiarity, if the demands become too harsh, the relationship can turn into enmity, with lifelong resentment. Shangu Shangu, you both rewarded the person when they were unknown and saved them in their most desperate state. Should you not be ashamed of those who are women and men? As for women and small people, who spread slander and deceive the ruler, even tigers fear them. Ah! Shangu, be careful not to rashly sing 'Thinking of My Husband and Loving Him'!"

    168

    麻疯女邱丽玉
    　　
    　　淮南禹迹山，林壑深幽，神龙窟宅也。至明季，始有居人，渐成聚落。陈生名绮，字绿琴，亦卜居山麓。父，母黄氏，耕种习贾，能小康。生年十五，善读。母仅有弱弟，名海客，游粤之某郡，货殖得资，遂落籍。至是母病革，私执绮腕，泣曰：“为母死后，汝父必继娶。庐花衣今古如一辙；汝穷促，可遁粤，寻依舅氏。”并私以所蓄数十金，与作旅费，生泣受。母殁，父继弦乌氏，果悍恶如母言，朝夕不能容，遂诣母墓痛哭，留书父枕侧而去。跋涉几半载，至则资耗而舅杳，遍询无其人。茕茕走村郭，渐以乞食度命，深悔孟浪，时思遄回。

    Explanation

    168

    Translation

    The Leprous Girl, Qiu Lihua
    　　
    In Huainan, Yuji Mountain, the forest and ravines are deep and secluded, a dwelling place of divine dragons. By the late Ming Dynasty, there were finally settlers, gradually forming a village. Chen Qi, known as Lüqin, also chose to settle at the foot of the mountain. His father and mother, Huang, engaged in farming and commerce, achieving a modest living. Chen was fifteen years old and skilled in reading. His mother had only one younger brother, named Haiguo, who traveled to a certain prefecture in Guangdong, amassed wealth through trade, and settled down. When his mother fell seriously ill, she privately took Chen's wrist, weeping and said, "After I die, your father will surely remarry. Wearing mourning clothes has always been the same throughout history; you are poor and pressed, so flee to Guangdong and seek help from your uncle." She also secretly gave him several dozen taels of silver as travel expenses, which he accepted with tears. After his mother's death, his father remarried a woman surnamed Wu, who was indeed as harsh and evil as his mother had predicted. Unable to tolerate him day or night, Chen went to his mother's grave and wept bitterly, leaving a letter by his father's pillow before departing. After traveling for nearly half a year, he found his resources depleted and his uncle nowhere to be found. He searched everywhere in vain. Alone and destitute, he wandered through villages and towns, gradually surviving by begging, deeply regretting his impulsive actions and often thinking of returning home.

    169

    一日至郭之东，有槟榔树覆柴门，方引吭唱莲花落，内有短髯赤面一颁白叟出，睨生诧曰：“小乞儿，子何貌之文，而音之悲也？”生曰：“腹有诗书，焉得不文；落魄穷途，焉得不悲？”曰：“何得至此？”生遂自陈乡贯，述寻舅状。叟默视生曰：“子舅其黄姓海客，面白多麻者耳？”曰：“然。”曰：“客死于此久矣。渠生为某巨室司会计，善营运，娶青楼女。病殁，女窃资随仆遁。老夫与渠有杯酒之交，代市具，葬东郭尼庵侧大柳树下，墓树短碑者是也。”陈伏谢，径至所指处，果得舅墓。问庵尼，亦如叟言。遂呼舅哀哭，祝曰：“舅若有灵，佑甥生还，当负舅骨返祖域。

    Explanation

    169

    On a certain day, the protagonist arrived at the east of the city, where a betel nut tree covered a thatched door. He raised his voice to sing a folk song called Lianhua Luo. Suddenly, a gray-haired old man with a short beard and a red face stepped out, looking at the protagonist with surprise and asking, "Little beggar, how can your appearance be so refined, and your voice so sad?" The protagonist replied, "With many books in my belly, how can I not be refined? In a state of poverty and despair, how can I not be sad?" The old man asked, "How did you get here?" The protagonist then explained his hometown and origin, describing his search for his uncle. The old man looked at the protagonist silently and said, "Your uncle is a man named Huang, a sea merchant with a white face and many freckles, isn't he?" "Yes," the protagonist confirmed. "He has been dead here for a long time," the old man continued. "He was born into a large household and served as an accountant, skilled in business, and married a courtesan. He died of illness, and his wife stole the money and fled with the servant. I had a drinking friendship with him, bought the coffin, and buried him near a nunnery on the east side of the city under a large willow tree. The tomb with a short stone marker is that one." The protagonist bowed in thanks, went directly to the place indicated, and indeed found his uncle's tomb. He asked the nun, and she confirmed the old man's words. Then, he called out to his uncle and cried, praying, "Uncle, if you have a spirit, bless me to return alive. I will carry your bones back to our ancestral land."

    170

    ”尼怜之，餐以豆粥，语云：“子所遇叟，姓司空，名浑，与汝舅有素，第往祈援手，切勿道方外饶舌。”明日，生见叟遽呼司空伯，惊讶曰：“小子何得知吾姓？”曰：“且知我伯名。”即诡云：“夜宿墓下，梦舅氏详告，且谕乞援。”叟愕然曰：“仆与渠，原无车笠盟，不过曾觌面。虽然，当为子徐图，尽寸心。”三日后，以绨袍一袭赠生，慨然有德色，且说生云：“仆清贫，无丰赠子，谅可原。幸邻郡某山中，有富室邱丈，本仆之葭莩也。老夫妇生有娇女，名元媚，字丽玉，年与子等，貌则鲜丽，择婿眼高，雀屏无选。子虽贫，而清才雅范，此间无与比俦。

    Explanation

    170

    The nun pitied him and fed him millet porridge, saying, "The old man you encountered is surnamed Sikuang and named Hun. He has a long-standing relationship with your uncle. Just go and ask for assistance, but do not mention anything about the outside world." The next day, the protagonist saw the old man and hurriedly called out, "Master Sikuang!" The old man was surprised and asked, "How did you know my surname?" The protagonist replied, "At least I know your elder brother's name." He then lied, "I stayed overnight at the tomb last night and dreamed that my uncle gave a detailed account, instructing me to seek help." The old man was astonished and said, "I and he originally had no sworn friendship; we have only met once. However, I will help you gradually and do my best." Three days later, he gave the protagonist a silk robe, looking with a sense of virtue, and said, "I am poor and cannot give you a generous gift; I hope you can understand. Fortunately, in a certain mountain in a neighboring prefecture, there is a wealthy man named Qiu, who is related to me by a distant connection. The elderly couple has a beautiful daughter named Yuan Mei, whose courtesy name is Li Yu. She is the same age as you, and her appearance is fresh and beautiful. She has high standards for choosing a husband and has not chosen any of the candidates. Although you are poor, you have clear talent and elegant demeanor. There is no one in this place who can match you."

    171

    仆作函代子执柯，往就甥馆，邱丈必有厚贶，尚不足运舅柩返珂乡欤？”陈生闻之，请思其次。问何故，曰“侄家山野，荆布藿藜，恐富室千金，未能习惯。矧彰彰入赘，能任坦腹人乘龙自便者乎？”叟抚掌，曰：“迂哉，书痴也！是不过攫伊财耳。茫茫天壤，渠于何处逋逃亡婿？”生计窘，姑受函往。至则渠第峨峨，春深兽锁，司阍人见其落拓，叱远立。及函入，两少年出，揖客云：“奉严命，恭迓玉趾。”知为翁子，随入。见栋宇庭院，俱类世家。一伟丈夫，修髯过腹，立阶上，生趋与展谒。坐间询司空氏起居。旋白夫人来，两婢扶一四十余美人出，翁曰：“此山荆也。公子既司空世好，与寒门谊即通家，敢以妻子相见。”生又展拜。

    171

    I wrote a letter to act as an intermediary for you, going to the son-in-law's residence. Uncle Qiu must have given a generous gift, but is it not enough to transport the uncle's coffin back to his hometown? Mr. Chen heard this and asked to think of another option. When asked why, he said, "My home is in the countryside, with coarse clothing and simple food. I fear that a wealthy family with a thousand gold pieces would not be accustomed to it. How much more so for someone who openly enters as a son-in-law, who could allow a man with a broad belly to ride a dragon at his convenience?" The old man clapped his hands and said, "How naive, a bookworm! It is nothing more than seizing his wealth. In the vast world, where would he escape to be a fugitive son-in-law?" Mr. Chen was in a difficult situation and reluctantly accepted the letter to go. Upon arriving, his house was grand, deep into spring, the gates were locked like those of a beast. The gatekeeper saw his disheveled appearance and ordered him to stand far away. When the letter was delivered, two young men came out and bowed to the guest, saying, "We respectfully received orders to welcome your presence." Knowing he was the old man's son, he followed him in. Seeing the grand buildings and courtyards, they all resembled a wealthy family. A tall and handsome man with a long beard that reached his belly stood on the steps. Mr. Chen hurriedly bowed to him. During the sitting, he inquired about the well-being of the family of Sir Kong. Soon, the lady came, two maidservants helping a beautiful woman in her forties come out. The old man said, "This is my humble wife. Since you are related by friendship to the family of Sir Kong, we have a close relationship with your family. We dare to present our wife and children to you." Mr. Chen bowed again.

    172

    妇凝睇笑谓翁曰：“司空妹倩眼力不差，公子真可人也。”倏具筵宴，劝爵甚殷。席间略询乡贯，即语生云：“舍亲与郎君言否？仆小女丽玉，素所钟爱，不欲嫁远方。然觅婿欲得如仙乡人物裙屐翩翩者，杳不可得。今得红丝牵引，文星惠临，是真石证三生，愿即日奉为箕帚。”生离席唯唯肃谢，婉陈曰：“自惭樗栎，仰托茑萝，良所深愿。然小生实为寻舅至此，婚后三四日，即拟暂返蓬门，事蒇再回瀛第，是不得不预陈长者。”妇微笑曰：“公子何匆促若此耶？”翁急止之，曰：“公子孝心，何可过拂，容即代筹朱提五百金，作为旅费。”生心喜，敬诺。

    Explanation

    172

    The young woman gazed fixedly and smiled at the old man, saying, "Miss Cai of the Sima family has good judgment; this young gentleman is truly admirable." Suddenly, a banquet was prepared, and the toasts were offered with great sincerity. During the meal, she inquired about his hometown and then told the young man, "My relative informed you, didn't they? My daughter, Li Yu, whom I have always cherished deeply, does not wish to marry someone far away. However, when seeking a husband, I desire someone like a figure from a fairyland with a graceful and elegant appearance, but such a person is nowhere to be found. Now that we have the red thread of fate, and the star of literature has graced us, this is truly a proof of three lifetimes. I hope you will marry her immediately and serve as her husband." The young man left his seat and repeatedly bowed respectfully, gently explaining, "I feel ashamed, as I am unworthy, but I rely on your kindness like a vine clinging to a tree, which I deeply desire. However, I actually came here to find my maternal uncle. After our marriage, in three or four days, I plan to temporarily return to my humble home. Once the matter is completed, I will return to your grand residence. I must mention this to you now." The young woman smiled and said, "Why are you so hurried, young gentleman?" The old man quickly stopped her, saying, "Your filial heart is commendable; how could I go against it? I will immediately arrange five hundred units of currency as travel expenses for you." The young man was pleased in his heart and respectfully agreed.

    173

    旋即笙管呕哑，灯火匝地，干仆引生之曲室，更簇新冠带，出就氍毹；雏姬三四，引一二八好女子，珠翠绮罗，盈盈自内出，与生交拜，送之洞房。却扇视女，则荷露桃霞，无比艳冶。生心意飞驰，反恨顷言新婚暂别，未免孟浪，容有意迁延，图静好耳。酒阑灯，听莲漏三催，婢妾亡去。生正隐几枨触，而女亦时牵绣窥良人，粉黛间隐有惨悴色。生不知就里，趋近软语，代为卸妆，女则拒以纤腕，再近则潜然流珠泪，徐起弹烛，视近闼无一人，始闭门小语曰：“郎亦知死期将近乎？”曰：“不知。”曰：“郎从何处来，何处去，曷明告妾也？”生具告之，女唏嘘，欲言又止。生知有变，伏地乞怜。

    Explanation

    173

    Immediately, the flutes and pipes played softly, and the lights spread all over the ground. The servant led the guest to a private room, where he adjusted and arranged his new cap and belt, then stepped onto the carpet. A few young maids led a group of eight beautiful women, dressed in jeweled and embroidered silk robes, who gracefully emerged from inside and bowed to the guest, leading him to the bridal chamber. Removing the fan to see the woman, the guest found her as beautiful as a lotus flower and peach blossom, unparalleled in her charm. The guest's heart flew away, and he regretted saying goodbye briefly after their new marriage, feeling it was rash. He thought it might be for peace and happiness. As the wine and lights dimmed, and the lotus-shaped water clock ticked three times, the maids and concubines left. The guest leaned against the desk, feeling something, and the woman also peeked through the embroidered curtain to see her husband. There was a hint of sorrow in her makeup. The guest did not understand the situation and approached her gently, trying to comfort her by helping her remove her makeup. She resisted with her slender wrist, and when he came closer, she silently shed tears. She slowly stood up, extinguished the candle, and looked around to see no one nearby. Then she closed the door and whispered, "Do you know that your death is near?" "I don't know," he replied. "Where did you come from, where are you going? Please tell me clearly." The guest told her everything, and she sighed, wanting to speak but stopping herself. The guest sensed something was wrong and knelt on the ground to plead for mercy.

    174

    女曰：“妾观郎君风采，意良不忍，故以机密告。妾麻疯女也。此间居粤西边境，代产美娃，悉根奇疾，女子年十五，富家即以千金诱远方人来，过毒尽，始与人家论婚，觅真配。若过期不御，则疾根顿发，肤燥发卷，永无问鼎者。远方人若贪资误接，三四日即顷有红斑，七八日即遍体骚痒，年余拘挛拳曲，虽和缓，亦不能生。”生闻之，始恍然悟，泣曰：“小生万里孤身，担荷甚重，乞娘子垂怜，容我潜逃可乎？”曰：“休矣，此间觅男子甚难，郎入门时，外间已环伏壮汉，持刀杖防逸。”生泣曰：“身死不足惜，所悲者，家有老亲耳。”曰：“妾虽女子，颇知名节，常恨是邦以地限，无贞妇，愿死不愿生。

    Explanation

    174

    The woman said, "I observe your appearance, feel very reluctant, and therefore reveal this secret to you. I am a leper. This place is on the western border of Guangdong, where beautiful girls are produced, all of whom suffer from a strange disease. When a girl reaches fifteen, wealthy families offer a thousand gold pieces to lure distant men, and after the disease has been treated, they can be married off, finding a true match. If not used within the period, the disease suddenly breaks out, the skin becomes dry and hair curls, and they will never have anyone who will marry them. If a distant man is greedy for the money and mistakenly accepts, within three or four days, red spots appear, within seven or eight days, the entire body becomes itchy, and after a year, they become stiff and twisted, even with the best medical care, they cannot survive." The young man heard this and suddenly realized, weeping, "I am alone on a journey of ten thousand miles, bearing a heavy burden. I beg you to have mercy, allow me to escape secretly, can I?" She said, "It is too late. Finding a man here is very difficult. When you entered, there were already strong men surrounding you outside, holding weapons to prevent your escape." He wept, "My death is not worth mourning, but what I am sad about is that I have elderly parents at home." She said, "I, although a woman, am very aware of my reputation. I often regret that this place, due to its isolation, has no chaste women. I would rather die than live."

    175

    郎且与妾和衣眠三日，得资即返。妾病发，亦不久人世，乞归署木主，曰：‘结发元配邱氏丽玉之位。’则瞑目泉台下矣！”言已，抱持隐泣，生愤然悲曰：“噫！婚则仆死，否则卿死，曷饮鸩同死，结来生缘乎？”曰：“不可，请书居址门巷，与妾纫衣缝中。俾他日柔魂，度关山，省舅姑，受郎君一盂麦饭耳！”生虽书与之，而涕不可仰。入衾共枕，生屡屡不能自持，女悉劝慰禁止。对食不餐，几与石女天阉，同一恨事。翌日，翁媪果顿同陌路。是夕，女以香舌吮生颈，作胭脂色者三四处，曰：“可矣。”私赠黄金白玉缠臂各二。生订后约，女悲曰：“恐君再来，妾墓门之木拱矣！”明日，翁媪果践言，即挥手令去。

    Explanation

    175

    The young master and I will sleep together for three days in the same clothes, and once we have the money, I will return. My illness has flared up, and I will not live much longer. I ask to be buried with a wooden tablet inscribed with my name, saying, "The position of my original wife, Lady Li Yu." Then I will close my eyes and rest in the underworld!" After speaking, she held me and cried softly. I became angry and sad, saying, "Ah! If we marry, I will die, and if we do not, you will die. Why not drink poison together and die, thus tying our fate for the next life?" She replied, "No, please write down my address and street, and place it in the seam of my clothes. So that one day my gentle spirit can cross mountains and pass through gates, visit my in-laws, and receive a bowl of millet porridge from my husband!" Although I wrote it down for her, I could not look up due to my tears. We got into bed together, and I could not control myself repeatedly. She comforted and restrained me. We ate but did not consume anything, almost like being a stone maiden or a eunuch, the same kind of regret. The next day, my parents-in-law indeed became strangers to me. That night, the woman used her sweet tongue to lick the back of my neck, leaving red marks in three or four places, and said, "It is enough." She secretly gave me two bracelets, one of gold and one of jade. I made a future appointment with her, and she said sadly, "I fear that by the time you come again, the trees at my tomb will be huge!" The next day, my parents-in-law indeed kept their word and waved me away.

    176

    重到尼庵，尼见项上痕，闭门不纳。急以资赁巨舫，启舅榇，载之南下。夜在舟中泣，舟子疑渭阳情重，奇之，敬礼益恭。抵家见父，则继母已殁，纳婢为小星。见子甚慰，见腰缠，疑妻弟所遗，不深诘。葬旅榇，买山田。陈翁善酿，遂种秫开酒肆，得利甚丰。生乃下帷读，入胶庠。邱翁见生去，谓其女毒尽无疑，正说媒妁觅东床，女忽疾发，视之，麻疯也。翁穷追，惟含涕；媪扪之，仍是处子，交詈曰：“淫婢太不长进，宁定不欲生耶？”月余益惫，遂遣之麻疯局。是局，乃长官好善者所设也。因是病，向能传染，家有一，则全家皆病，虽掌上珠，亦恩断义绝，无复顾情。

    Explanation

    176

    Returning to the nunnery, the nun saw a mark on his neck and closed the door, refusing to admit him. Hastily renting a large boat with money, he opened his uncle's coffin and carried it southward. At night, he wept in the boat, and the boatman suspected that his feelings for Wei Yang were deep, finding it strange. He treated him with even greater courtesy. Upon arriving home and seeing his father, he found that his stepmother had already passed away, and a maid had been taken as a concubine. Seeing his son, he felt very comforting, and upon seeing the money on his waist, he suspected it was left by his wife's brother, but did not inquire deeply. He buried the coffin of the traveler and bought mountain fields. Mr. Chen was good at brewing, so he planted sorghum and opened a wine shop, gaining great profit. The son then studied diligently and entered the school. Mr. Qiu saw the son leave and thought that his daughter's poison was completely gone without doubt.
