Just as he was about to have matchmakers look for a suitable match, the daughter suddenly fell ill. Upon examination, it was leprosy. The father relentlessly pursued the matter, only shedding tears. The mother-in-law examined her and found that she was still a virgin, so both scolded and said, "The lewd maid did not improve at all, was she so determined not to live?" After a month, she grew even more exhausted, so she was sent to the leprosy hospital. This hospital was set up by officials who were good at doing good deeds. Because of this disease, it could easily spread; if one person in the family had it, the whole family would get sick. Even if she was like a pearl on the palm, the family would cut ties and have no more regard for her.

    177

    女入局，数雉经，辄见一麻面叟，口操南音者来救止。既而思遁，叟慨然愿导引，曰：“老夫黄姓，淮南人，娘子得勿欲寻陈生绿琴耶？渠与仆似曾相识，可同行，仆亦欲东耳。”女自恃恶疾，又以叟迈，欣然随之。叟到处，重门自辟。至郊外，叟以唾涂女莲钩，口喃喃若符咒，即迈步如健儿。感翁德，事之如父，旋拔银腕钏，易资为旅费。甫至楚，资已耗尽，遂行乞，叟吹洞箫，女口编《女贞木曲》，歌唱沿门，歌曰：女贞木，枝苍苍，前世不修为女娘，更生古粤之遐荒。生为麻疯种，长即麻疯疮，衔冤有精卫，补恨无娲皇。画烛盈盈照合卺，侬自掩泪窥陈郎。翩翩陈郎好容止，弹烛窥侬心自喜。

    Explanation

    177

    The woman entered the situation and repeatedly attempted to hang herself, but each time she saw an old man with a rough face who spoke in a southern accent come to save her. Afterward, she considered escaping, but the old man willingly offered to guide her, saying, "I am Huang, from Huainan. Are you not looking for Chen Sheng and his green qin? He seems familiar to me, and we can travel together. I also want to go east." Believing in her own illness and the old man's age, she gladly followed him. Wherever the old man went, doors opened automatically. Upon reaching the outskirts, the old man spat on the woman's feet and muttered something like a charm. Immediately, she walked as if she were a healthy young man. Grateful for the old man's kindness, she treated him as a father and quickly removed her silver bracelets to exchange for travel expenses. As soon as they arrived in Chu, their funds ran out, so they began to beg. The old man played a flute while the woman sang "The Song of the Female Holly Tree," singing door-to-door. The song went:

    "The Female Holly Tree,
    Branches dark and gray,
    In a past life, I was not a maiden,
    Reborn in the distant lands of ancient Yue.
    Born with leprosy,
    Growing up with leprosy sores,
    I carry my grievances like Jingwei,
    But there is no Nüwa to mend my sorrows.
    Candles shine brightly at the wedding feast,
    I secretly watch Chen Sheng, tears in my eyes.
    Chen Sheng, with his graceful demeanor,
    Sees me and his heart is pleased."

    178

    妾是麻疯娘，郎岂麻疯子。妾虽麻疯得郎生，郎转麻疯为妾死。郎为妾死郎不知，洞房绣阁衔金卮。孔雀亦莫舞，杜鹃亦莫啼，鹦鹉无言愿飞去，郎坠网罗妾心悲。郎不见，骏马不跨双鞍子，烈女愿为一姓死。郎行依旧貌如仙，妾命可怜薄如纸。肤为燥，肌为皱。云鬓卷曲黄且髡。掩面走入麻疯局，不欲传染伤所亲。昔作掌上珍，今作俎上肉；昔居绮罗丛，今入郎当屋。月落空梁悬素罗，一缕香魂断复续。妾虽生，妾不愿，守故居；妾既生，妾自当，寻我夫。可怜虽生亦犹死，不死不生终何如？女贞木，枝扶疏，上宿飞鸟，下荫游鱼。鸟比翼者鹣鹣，鱼比目者鲽鲽。

    Explanation

    178

    I am a woman with leprosy, and you, my beloved, are not. Despite my leprosy, I gained life through you, while you transformed leprosy into my death. You do not know that I am dying, yet we sit in the luxurious bridal chamber, holding golden cups. Neither should the peacock dance, nor the cuckoo cry, nor the parrot speak; I wish to fly away silently. You have fallen into a trap, and my heart is filled with sorrow. You do not see, that a noble steed does not cross two saddles, and a virtuous woman wishes to die for one family. You remain as beautiful as a fairy, while my life is pitifully thin as paper. My skin is dry, and my muscles are wrinkled. My hair, once cloud-like and curled, is now yellow and bald. I cover my face and enter the leprosy ward, not wanting to infect and harm loved ones. Once a treasure in the palm, now meat on the chopping block; once living among silks and brocades, now in a humble house. The moon sets, and an empty beam hangs with white silk, a thread of fragrant soul breaks and continues. I am alive, but I do not wish to stay in the old home; I am alive, I must find my husband. Pitifully, being alive is like being dead, not dying, not living, what will become of it? The nǚzhēn tree, with branches spread out, above, birds roost; below, fish are shaded. Birds with paired wings, like the mythical bird Jiānjiān, fish with paired eyes, like the mythical fish Diédié.

    179

    生同衾，死同穴，衾穴即不同，妾心若明月。月照桃花红欲然，李代桃僵被虫啮。女贞木，红枝叶，悉是麻疯之女眼中血！女歌韵心酸，叟箫声凄咽，闻者流涕，争进以食，不敢呼蹴与。半年，抵淮南，将近山村，见老屋万椽，青帘出树杪。叟遥指曰：“向南黄石堆门者是也。子当自往，仆从此逝矣。唯祈寄语绿琴父子，云海客奉谢。”言已，即杳。女惊定，诣肆门，见一老翁坐炉侧，面目似绿琴，疑为翁。歌前曲，翁掷一钱与之，再歌又掷一钱，女泣曰：“贤郎陈绮，粤西欠奴债不还，迢迢责负逋，岂一文钱所能偿耶？”惊询，具告之，翁曰：“陈绮耶？豚子也。汝所言，难遽信。渠秋试金陵，不日归山庄，面当知真赝。

    Explanation

    179

    In life, we share the same bed; in death, the same grave. Even if the bed and grave are not the same, my heart remains as pure as the moon. The moonlight illuminates the peach blossoms, making them seem about to ignite, yet the peach tree dies in place of the plum tree, eaten by insects. The Notopterygium plant, with its red branches and leaves, is all the blood from the eyes of leprosy-stricken women! The woman's song is filled with sorrow, and the old man's flute sounds mournful and choked. Those who hear it weep, and no one dares to shout or scold. After half a year, they arrived in Huainan, approaching a village, where they saw an old house with thousands of rafters, green curtains hanging from the treetops. The old man pointed far away and said, "The one with a yellow stone pile in front of the door to the south is it. You should go there yourself; I will depart from here. I only ask you to convey my message to the Green Qín father and son, the Cloud Sea Guest pays his respects." After he spoke, he disappeared. The woman calmed down after being startled and went to the shop door, where she saw an old man sitting by the stove, his face resembling Green Qín. She suspected he was the old man. She sang the previous song, and the old man threw a coin to her. She sang again, and the old man threw another coin. She wept and said, "My son Chen Qǐ owes me a debt from the west of Guangxi and has not repaid it, a long-standing debt that has not been settled. How could a single coin repay it?" She asked in surprise, and told him everything. The old man said, "Chen Qǐ? He is a fool. What you say is hard to believe. He will take the autumn examination in Jinling and return to the mountain villa in no time. You will know the truth in person."

    180

    ”女闻之，即叩以见翁礼，翁送入尼庵中，遣村妇伺应，妇皆唾却走，幸老尼怜悯，得无苦。月余，生归，翁以女询，生惊惶不知所云。翁曰：“是不可负也。吾家不少闲粥饭，虽易枕席，当豢之，终其身。”生伏谢，急趋访女，遽牵生衣啼曰：“妾远来，不敢望伉俪，唯冀以骸骨葬君家祖域耳！”生且泣且慰，问何能自来，以黄叟面目颠末告。生惊曰：“是吾舅也，其地仙耶！”携女之家，谋酒库隙地，卧丛瓮中，诸婢咸远立，不敢近，唯一雏婢，名甘蕉者，独代撒溲便琐事；至饮食药饵，皆生手调。久更铺被挈甘蕉，卧女侧，亦均无恙。

    Explanation

    180

    The woman heard this and immediately inquired about the etiquette for meeting the old man. The old man sent her into a nunnery, where he dispatched village women to attend to her. The women all spat out and ran away, fortunately, the old nun had compassion for her and she was spared suffering. After more than a month, the protagonist returned, and the old man asked about the woman. The protagonist was frightened and did not know what was being said. The old man said, "This cannot be betrayed. My family does not lack idle food. Even if we change beds, we should feed her for her entire life." The protagonist bowed and thanked him, then hurriedly went to visit the woman. She suddenly pulled his clothes and cried, saying, "I came from afar, do not dare to hope for a marital union, only hope to be buried in your family's ancestral grounds!" The protagonist wept and comforted her, asking how she could come here. She explained the details of the elderly man's appearance. The protagonist was startled and said, "This is my uncle, is he a local immortal?" They took the woman to the protagonist's home, planning to find an empty space in the wine cellar where she could lie among jars. All the maids stood far away and dared not approach, except for one young maid named Banana, who alone took care of the trivial matters of urination and defecation. As for food, drink, and medicine, the protagonist prepared everything by hand. After a long time, they laid down a blanket and took Banana, lying beside the woman, and they all remained well.

    181

    榜发，生乡捷，里人争与论婚，生力却，父稍稍劝，生泣曰：“儿年甫二十有一，麻疯女量不久生人世，曷姑待其毙再婚，亦未为晚也！”又恐己去，女无人照看，遂告病，罢南宫试。女以头触瓮，悲曰：“为妾故，使郎迟嗣续，阻上进。妾死后何以见祖宗于地下？诚不如死。”言已又触，赖甘蕉救止始已。一日，生赴戚家饮，遇雨不归。甘蕉又因病内卧，女听雨剪灯，搔爬不已。忽闻梁际飕声，一大黑蛇，粗如儿臂，长几七八尺，从空飒至。女始颇惧，继思得果蛇腹，胜于自戕，听之。蛇身盘屋梁，垂首下欣酒瓮木盖，坠地如掷，吸瓮中酒呷饮，顷刻满腹，欲上缩则木强如枯藤，倏忽坠瓮中，搅扰翻腾，力尽，声顿寂。女燃灯强起视之，毙矣。

    Explanation

    181

    The exam results were announced, and the young man passed the local examination. The villagers eagerly discussed marriage with him, but he firmly declined. His father gently encouraged him, and the young man wept, saying, "I am only twenty-one years old, and the leprosy-stricken daughter of my friend is unlikely to live much longer. Why not wait until she passes away before considering marriage? It would not be too late!" However, fearing that he might leave and leave the leprosy patient unattended, he claimed to be ill and withdrew from the metropolitan examination. The woman, overcome with guilt, hit her head against the jar and lamented, "Because of me, you have delayed your descendants and hindered your advancement. How can I face our ancestors in the afterlife after my death? It would be better to die." She hit her head again, but was saved and stopped by his wife, Gan Jiao. One day, the young man went to a relative's house for a drink and did not return. Gan Jiao was also sick in bed, and the woman listened to the rain, cutting the lamp wick, scratching herself incessantly. Suddenly, she heard a whistling sound from the beam, and a large black snake, as thick as a child's arm and about seven or eight feet long, swooped down from the sky. At first, she was quite frightened, but then she thought that if she could catch the snake and kill it, it would be better than taking her own life. She waited. The snake coiled around the beam, lowered its head to the wooden lid of the wine jar, and dropped to the ground as if thrown. It drank the wine in the jar, filling its belly in no time. When it tried to retract, the wood was as stiff as a dead vine, and it suddenly fell into the jar, stirring and thrashing about until it exhausted itself, and the sound suddenly became silent. The woman lit a lamp and forced herself to stand up to look at it, and it was dead.

    182

    心忆蛇毒或可代鸩，掬饮升许，心顿清醒，怯烦襟，肤转奇痒，又掬以洗涤，痒顿止。明日，又潜饮而潜洗之，疾若失，肤之燥者，转莹如玉，发之卷者，转垂若云，面目手足之皴者，转如花如月，如嫩笋芽矣。甘蕉惊喜告生，询之，以蛇酒告，趋视，则遍体黑章成云篆，顶有独角，色殷然。盖此山蛇王，名乌风者也。具锦囊绮裙，花钿珠玉，妆女出见翁与诸宛若，莫不惊为天人。翁曰：“吾幼闻蛇王居此山千年矣，番僧求得片鳞，为人医癣疥，不可得。孰知天专留此，为吾疗贤妇疾耶？”即日备礼为合卺。珠履满堂，吹鼓筵宴，百里外男妇咸奔至，一觇女之颜色，归以为荣。

    Explanation

    182

    The man remembered that snake venom might be as potent as the poison of a sparrow hawk, and he scooped up a small amount to drink. Suddenly, his mind became clear, his anxious heart calmed, and his skin turned strangely itchy. He scooped more to wash it off, and the itching stopped abruptly. The next day, he secretly drank and washed again. His disease vanished as if it had never been there. His dry skin became smooth and radiant like jade, his curly hair straightened and flowed like clouds, and his wrinkled face, hands, and feet became smooth and youthful, like flowers, the moon, and tender bamboo shoots. Ganniao was surprised and delighted and told the man. When asked, he told him about the snake wine. Rushing to see, he found the snake covered in black marks forming cloud-like patterns, with a single horn on its head and a dark red color. It was said to be the king of the mountain snakes, named Wufeng. She was dressed in embroidered bags and silk skirts, adorned with flower-shaped decorations and pearl jewelry, and appeared before the old man and his attendants, who were all astonished to think she was a heavenly being. The old man said, "When I was young, I heard that the snake king had lived in this mountain for a thousand years. Foreign monks tried to obtain a piece of its scales to treat skin conditions but failed. Who would have thought that heaven had specially preserved this to heal my virtuous wife?" On the same day, he prepared a ceremony for their union. The hall was filled with people wearing pearl shoes, music and feasting were in full swing, and men and women from a hundred miles away rushed to attend, just to catch a glimpse of the woman's beauty, returning home feeling honored.

    183

    再三年，女生宁馨儿，感甘蕉德，收为室。生却之，不可。是年春，生试礼闱，入木天，出为太守，专恤流亡与贫病无告者，人人称众母。升两粤制军，遣材官，招邱翁至，索丽玉甚急，翁假泣曰：“小女命薄，殒谢久矣，明公尚欲寻故剑耶？”生又索骸骨归葬，翁惧，献千金为太翁寿，不许。旋访司空，云：“惊逸坠绝涧死。”生笑曰：“渠真以小人目我矣！”旋命婢扶夫人出，则衣一品命妇服，容光焕发。翁几惊伏，视之，即己女丽玉也，洒泪问父母安否？翁咋舌，愧欲死。女亦时归宁，出蛇酒制药设局，济粤之患麻疯者，活无算。年四十余，太翁犹清健，疏乞终养。归修舅墓与尼庵，建邱夫人碑，纪事之崖略。至今此山药酒，尚驰名云。

    Explanation

    183

    Three years later, a girl named Ningxin was born, who felt deeply grateful for the virtue of the banana tree and was taken as a wife. The man initially refused her, but she could not be stopped. In that spring, he took the imperial examination, entered the prestigious ranks, and was appointed as a prefect, dedicating himself to caring for the displaced, the poor, and the sick without anyone to turn to. Everyone called him the "mother of the people." He was promoted to the position of governor-general of the two Guang provinces, sent capable officials, and summoned Old Master Qiu, demanding Li Yu urgently. Old Master Qiu pretended to weep and said, "My daughter had a weak fate; she has been dead for a long time. Do you still want to find the old sword?" The man demanded the bones be returned for burial. Fearing this, Old Master Qiu offered a thousand gold pieces as a gift for the grandfather, but the man refused. He quickly visited the Grand Secretary, who reported, "She was frightened and fled, fell into a deep ravine, and died." The man laughed and said, "He truly regards me as a small person!" He then ordered a maid to bring out his wife, who was wearing the attire of a first-rank imperial consort, radiant with beauty. Old Master Qiu was almost overwhelmed with shock and recognized her as his own daughter, Li Yu, and shed tears, asking about the well-being of her parents. Old Master Qiu stammered in surprise and felt ashamed to the point of death. The woman also occasionally returned to her parents' home, producing snake wine, making medicine, and setting up a clinic, helping countless people suffering from leprosy in Guangdong. By her forties, the grandfather was still healthy, and she submitted a petition to retire and care for her parents. Upon retirement, she returned to repair her uncle's tomb and the nunnery, erecting a stele for Old Master Qiu's wife, recording the main events. Until today, the mountain herbal wine is still famous.

    184

    卷四
    　　
    　　佟阿紫
    　　
    　　佟阿紫，楚人也，幼失怙恃，孑然无栖止。年十五，随戚学贸易，走鲁之登州。戚病，佟日夜侍汤药，殁则尽以旅囊市具，厚殓之，泣求客之楚者，附舟回。临别焚楮，誓曰：“阿紫从君来，不克送君返，敢有侵吞分文者，鬼且殛，神且诛。”誓已大哭，闻者流涕。然佟由是竟困为丐。会海滨飞来村孝廉郝隐，义而携之回。顾佟貌修整，性灵敏，勤于事，得人怜，村之巨室，咸招阿紫司奔走，以故传食得不馁。久之，得隙地，结茅棚，如斗大，外缭土垣，中种蔬果，渐免托沿门钵矣。巨室欲豢之家，不可；欲配以婢，亦不可，告众曰：“男儿不能自立，何以家为？倘荷诸长者荫，亦非终歌《朝雉》者。”一日，抱瓮倦卧井畔，闻雷声殷殷，知山雨至，蜷伏棚底。

    Explanation

    184

    卷四

    佟阿紫

    佟阿紫，是楚国人，幼年失去父母，孤苦无依。十五岁时，跟随亲戚学习经商，前往鲁地的登州。亲戚生病时，佟阿紫日夜照料，为其煎药，亲戚去世后，她用旅途中所有的财物，为他举行了厚葬，并哭泣着请求前往楚地的客人，带她回去。临别时，她焚烧纸钱，发誓说：“阿紫随您而来，若不能送您回去，若有侵吞分文者，愿鬼神降罪。”发誓后，她大哭，听到的人都为之流泪。然而，佟阿紫因此最终沦为乞丐。恰逢海滨飞来村的孝廉郝隐，出于怜悯，带她回村。郝隐见佟阿紫容貌整洁，聪明伶俐，做事勤勉，深得村民喜爱，村中的大户人家纷纷请她帮忙跑腿，因此她得以靠乞讨维持生计。时间久了，她找到一块空地，建了一个茅棚，大小如斗，外绕土墙，中间种上蔬菜和水果，渐渐不再依赖乞讨。大户人家想收养她为家仆，她拒绝了；想让她嫁给家中的婢女，她也不同意，并对众人说：“男子若不能自立，何以成家？若蒙各位长者庇护，我也不会终日闲散度日。”一天，她抱着水瓮，疲倦地躺在井边，听到雷声隆隆，知道山雨将至，便蜷缩在茅棚底下。

    185

    至晚，雷益迅，雨更急，电走金蛇，鼻灌透硫磺味。佟伏地自陈曰：“某年甫十八，无愧怍，唯撇父母墓于乡里，清明无一盂冷麦饭，宁上苍欲击吾顶耶？”突霹雳震山岳，若有巨物，骨冬坠中庭，意为鸡爪尖喙者无疑。瞑目待毙。久之，雷收声，而雨亦霁，湿云缕缕裹银蟾，攸明灭。瞰菜畦，果有一物，黑摸索，触手软腻，燃火照之，则一端正好女子，衣朴素，尽沾濡，鼻端有喘息，胸前尚温暖，唯昏昏若睡，呼不应。大惧，反扃短柴扉，冲泥走告郝，不深信，言之确，遂起，遍告村人，邀往观其异。每人篝灯，明若昼。比至，则女子已苏，嘤嘤细泣，大众环视，秋波灼灼，无一语。郝命人扶入棚，即藉阿紫短榻上眠。众席地坐，询之，女自云郝姓，小字五铢，所居为极大村庄，居人甚伙，距郭甚遥，实不知隶何郡邑。多兄弟姐妹，唯己恒得父母怜。

    185

    Until late, the thunder grew more intense, and the rain became even more urgent. Lightning streaked like golden snakes, and the air was filled with the scent of sulfur. Tong prostrated himself and said, "I am just eighteen years old, without any guilt or regret, but I have left my parents' graves unattended in the village. During Qingming Festival, I did not offer a cold bowl of wheat porridge to the ancestors. Could the heavens be punishing me?" Suddenly, a thunderclap shook the mountains, and it seemed as if a great object had fallen into the courtyard with a heavy thud. It was undoubtedly a bird with a sharp beak. He closed his eyes, waiting for death. After a long time, the thunder subsided, and the rain ceased. The wet clouds wrapped around the silver moon, flickering intermittently. Looking down at the vegetable plot, he saw something dark and formless, soft and smooth to the touch. He lit a fire to illuminate it, and discovered a well-formed woman wearing plain clothes, completely soaked. She breathed through her nose, her chest still warm, but she was dazed and as if asleep, not responding to calls. Greatly frightened, he locked the short wooden door and rushed through the mud to inform Hao, who initially did not believe him but then rose after confirmation. He informed the villagers and invited them to see the strange sight. Each person lit a lantern, and it was as bright as day. By the time they arrived, the woman had already awakened, softly weeping. The crowd looked around, her bright eyes shining, but she uttered no words. Hao ordered someone to carry her into the shed, where she was laid on a short bed belonging to someone named Azi. The crowd sat on the ground, asking her. The woman said she was surnamed Hao and her nickname was Wuzhu. She lived in a large village with many inhabitants, far from the city, and she actually did not know which county or city it belonged to. She had many siblings, but only she constantly received her parents' love.

    186

    是夕，正衽阿趾后，入己房间，头忽眩晕，心虽了了，而耳鸣如鼓风涛，身轻若御云雾。旋更昏瞀，比苏，则不知何故至此处。众观其装束，若吴趋，听其口音，若山陕。问伊父母名氏，摇手示不知。问可否婚聘，亦摇首示待字。郝喜曰：“我同宗也，但若是，则何法为尔寄音耗？”女子哭甚哀，郝怜而止之，与众耳语琐碎者久，告女曰：“此为佟郎居，彼此怨旷，天涯海角相遇，乃鬼神撮合，雷霆主婚，仆与汝同姓，论年齿可为吾犹女，拟遵天意，以汝妻佟郎，可乎？”女翘首四盼，郝急推阿紫，示之曰：“是儿虽贫，量非终于贫者。”女睨之，意似许可。佟面赭流汗，向众力辞，郝曰：“痴男子常守鳏耶？天意已定，违恐不祥。”村之父老，醵资谋花烛，顷刻得五十金。夜向晨，村妇咸至，争出衣裙钗钿，为女助妆。妆竟，美丽绝伦。小市酒肴，即日合卺。

    Explanation

    186

    That night, after adjusting her hem and settling her feet, she entered her own room. Suddenly, she felt dizzy, though her mind remained clear. Her ears rang like the roar of wind and waves on a drum, and her body felt light as if riding on clouds and mist. Soon, she became dizzy and confused, and when she regained consciousness, she did not know how she had come to be there. The crowd observed her attire and noted that it resembled that of someone from Wu, and her accent sounded like that of someone from Shanxi or Shaanxi. When asked about her parents' names, she shook her head to show she did not know. When asked if she was available for marriage, she also shook her head to indicate she was still unmarried. Hao exclaimed happily, "We are of the same clan, but if so, what method can I use to send you news?" The girl cried very sadly, and Hao pitied her and stopped her. He whispered privately with the others for a long time, then told the girl, "This is where Tong resides. Both of us have been separated for a long time, meeting in the farthest corners of the earth. It is the work of gods and spirits, and thunder and lightning presiding over the marriage. I and you share the same surname. In terms of age, you could be my daughter. Intending to follow the will of heaven, I propose to marry you to Tong. Is it acceptable?" The girl looked around in all directions, and Hao quickly pushed Azi, saying to her, "This child may be poor, but she will not remain poor forever." The girl looked at him sidelong, and it seemed she agreed. Tong's face turned red, and he was sweating, trying to refuse in front of everyone. Hao said, "Foolish man, do you intend to remain a widower forever? Heaven's will has already been decided. Resisting it may be inauspicious." The village elders gathered money to arrange the wedding, and they quickly raised fifty gold coins. As night turned to morning, the village women all arrived, rushing to lend her clothes, skirts, and jewelry to help her with her makeup. When she was finished dressing, she was beautiful beyond compare. They bought some wine and food from a small market, and on the same day, they were married.

    187

    明日，女亲诣郝，荫膝下，作螟蛉女。郝疑女必嗔佟郎贫，久之，竟伉俪，日夕同操作，辟索分郎苦。一日，夫妇携锄刈蒿蔚，忽见两金莺戏于庭，倏上下，鸣且舞。女戏以竹竿仆击，坠地没。掘尺余，即得黄金二饼。佟欲易以市田宅，女不可，曰：“贫人暴得大财，徒取祸耳。郎盍走他郡，学贾，数年归示人，知富之有由来。妾甘为郎守作生活，量不致馁。”佟遂泣与别，往告郝，亦深以为然，并自任照看女。佟徒步游江南，诣皖之钟离，遇甄叟，巨商也，凝视其相，诧曰：“君当大富贵，奈何草衣芒履掩人目？”佟逊谢直陈，乃延归，授以白镪五百金，曰：“且之江南学负贩，无论何货，第运之，亏不汝怨，盈则除母分子金。”佟过江，思贩猪利息厚，遂投牙行，以五百金全作孤注。兑讫，白足走泥淖，行至江口，正唤渡，忽有放飞炮者，猪惊逸，尽入芦苇中，呼之不再出。痛哭欲觅死，又恋五铢。

    Explanation

    187

    The next day, the girl personally went to Hao and, kneeling before him, became his foster daughter. Hao suspected that the girl would certainly resent the poor status of Tong Lang, but after some time, they finally married and worked together day and night, sharing the hardships equally. One day, the couple carried hoes to cut wild grass, and suddenly they saw two golden orioles playing in the courtyard. They suddenly flew up and down, singing and dancing. The girl played with a bamboo pole, striking them, and they fell to the ground and disappeared. She dug more than a foot deep and immediately found two pieces of gold. Tong wanted to exchange it to buy land and houses, but the girl refused, saying, "A poor person suddenly gaining great wealth only brings disaster. Why don't you go to another prefecture and learn commerce? After several years, return to show people that wealth has its origins. I am willing to stay and work for you, and I believe we won't starve." Tong then cried and parted, going to tell Hao, who also deeply agreed and took on the responsibility of watching over the girl. Tong traveled on foot to Jiangnan, reaching Zhongli in Wann, where he met Zhen Lao, a wealthy merchant. Zhen Lao stared at his appearance and said in surprise, "You will achieve great wealth and nobility, why hide your potential with rough clothing and shoes?" Tong humbly thanked and frankly stated his situation. Zhen Lao then invited him back and gave him 500 taels of silver, saying, "Go to Jiangnan to learn trading. No matter what goods, just transport them. If you lose money, I won't blame you; if you make a profit, just pay me back the principal." Tong crossed the river and thought that pig trading had high interest, so he went to the brokerage and bet all 500 taels of silver on one venture. After exchanging, he walked barefoot through the mud, reaching the river mouth, where he was calling for a ferry. Suddenly, someone set off a firecracker, and the pigs were startled and ran into the reeds. Calling them back, they did not come out again. Crying in pain, wanting to die, but still attached to the five coins.

    188

    无已，吹箫乞吴门，忽遇同乡李叟，诧曰：“子何一寒至此耶？”泣告以故。叟笑曰：“子真商贾中之雏稚者。凡运白，必须先豢犬，彼项挂钢钉皮圈者非欤；猪偶有逸者，嗾犬衔之回。若全赖人力，宜其惫也。”赠五十金并假两黄犬，驯且猛。即日代运猪数十口，嘱且渡江试为之。佟拜辞，偕卢令往。重至江口，心方惴惴，忽天大雷雨，江波如山，昼昏黑如夜。佟痴立雨中，听猪自窜。比雨霁，则猪皆乌有，仅两犬在焉。愤极，返江店，出资饱餐，并以甘旨饫犬。店主人问将若何，佟作恨声曰：“此番当深入丛莽，穷搜之。”主人摇手曰：“莫孟浪，此中有巨蛇，曰芦蟒，力大能噬人。且江州沙软，多坑陷，坠则死矣。”佟曰：“宁入蟒腹死，不以失信生。”卒往。至则犬断牵奔入，佟无导引，唯蹲而待。少顷，乃犬声唁唁，豕声呦呦，呼号震江水。

    Explanation

    188

    No longer, he blew his flute to beg at Wu Gate, and suddenly encountered a fellow villager, Old Man Li, who exclaimed in surprise, "How did you come to be so poor?" He wept and told the reason. Old Man Li smiled and said, "You are truly a novice among merchants. When transporting white goods, one must first raise dogs. Isn't that the one with a steel pin and leather collar around its neck? If a pig occasionally escapes, command the dog to bring it back. If it relies entirely on human effort, it is natural to be exhausted." He gave fifty gold coins and lent two yellow dogs, which were tame but fierce. That very day, he immediately transported dozens of pigs and instructed them to cross the river to try it. Tong bowed and took leave, accompanied by Lu Ling. Upon returning to the riverbank, his heart was filled with anxiety, and suddenly, a great thunderstorm broke out, with the river waves as high as mountains, and the daytime was as dark as night. Tong stood foolishly in the rain, letting the pigs run away on their own. When the rain stopped, the pigs were nowhere to be found, only the two dogs remained. Extremely angry, he returned to the riverside inn, paid for a hearty meal, and fed the dogs with delicious food. The innkeeper asked what would be done, and Tong said with a tone of resentment, "This time, I will go deep into the dense undergrowth and search thoroughly." The innkeeper shook his hand and said, "Do not act rashly. There is a giant snake here, called the Lu Ma, which is strong enough to bite people. And the sand on the riverbank is soft, with many pits and holes; if you fall, you will die." Tong said, "Rather than live by being untrustworthy, I would rather die in the snake's belly." He finally went. When they arrived, the dogs pulled them in, and Tong had no guide, only sitting and waiting. After a short while, the dogs barked and the pigs bleated, their cries shook the river.

    189

    霎时，群豕鱼贯出，鹄立俟，若就拘束，不敢动。视之，其数较前所失者十数倍，而且肥腻茁壮，其值约五千金仍有余。念天欲暮，两犬始流汗出，血满口吻。适江船泊五六只至，急驱渡江。盖船即江北猪行来迎客货者。顷因翠华南幸，豕价大昂，故迓于江浒耳。比抵北岸，坚坐待价，牙曰：“再迟则殆矣。”佟不听。明日价更增，三日后，佟心怯，售之，得八千余金，存金更票帙，潜往钟离告甄叟。自佟去后，伙人咸云佟遁，叟不以为然。至是佟归，益张筵酬之。筵毕，呈票符，并白颠末。叟笑曰：“君亦太稚气。前豕既逸，亦何必乞不归。然子所遇李叟，尚欲见之否？”曰：“正欲白主人酬所假。”叟笑呼：“十八字，可出晤佟家郎。”叟出，即李也。大惊，视犬更杂众犬中，若叟家素豢者，益骇。少顷，恍然悟，盖甄叟早知佟事，故遣李送犬与金耳。

    Explanation

    189

    In a moment, a group of pigs emerged in a line, standing upright as if waiting, restrained as if bound, and not daring to move. Upon closer inspection, their number was several times greater than what had been lost before, and they were fat, oily, and strong, with a value of about five thousand gold coins and still having a surplus. Thinking the day was about to end, the two dogs began to sweat, with blood filling their mouths. Just as five or six boats arrived at the river, they quickly drove the pigs across the river. The boats were from the pig market in northern Jiang and came to welcome the guests and goods. Recently, because of the Southern Emperor's visit, the price of pigs had greatly increased, so they met at the riverside. By the time they reached the northern bank, they firmly sat and waited for the price. The broker said, "If we wait any longer, it will be too late." Tong did not listen. The next day, the price increased even more. Three days later, Tong became fearful and sold them, receiving more than eight thousand gold coins. He stored the gold and tickets, secretly went to Zhongli to inform Zhen Sǒu. After Tong left, the partners all said Tong had fled, but the elder did not think so. When Tong returned, the elder increased the feast to reward him. After the feast, he presented the tickets and explained the details. The elder laughed and said, "You are also too naive. Since the pigs have already escaped, why bother to beg them to return. But do you still want to see the old man Li you met?" He replied, "I just want to tell the master to repay the loan." The elder laughed and called, "Eighteen, you can come out to meet the young man from the Tong family." When the elder came out, it was Li. He was greatly surprised, looking at the dogs mixed among many others, as if they were always kept by the elder's family, even more frightened. A little while later, he suddenly realized that Zhen Sǒu had known about Tong's affairs early on, so he sent Li to deliver the dogs and the money.

    190

    明日，尽以七千金假之，副以干仆，嘱再之楚，曰：“遇货即运，无不利也。”佟往返二次，获数万，兼以楚无亲族，函父母骸骨归。再诣叟，曰：“可矣。”分子金与之，尚五万有余，欲妻以女，辞曰：“家有糟糠，且别已三载，不欲效薄也。”问何如人，述前事，叟惊曰：“其面庞团白，眉纤而长，名五铢者耶？”曰：“然，翁何以知之？”曰：“此吾姨侄女也。渠父母皆陕人，流寓于皖，亦富家翁，其居即在敝村之南，大树千章裹竹楼者是也。某年月日，为雷雨摄去，安知为君夫人耶！”翌日，引佟见郝翁媪，一家沸腾，欢笑狂喜。翁媪急欲见女，遂由淮驾海舶，亲送东床，运资返登。一帆风顺，不日抵飞来村，骨肉相聚，如梦寐，如隔世也，悲喜可知。遗奁具又极丰备，并厚酬孝廉，与之联谱系。居半年，欲携婿家同之皖，女曰：“不可，儿不忘村人德。”遂出千金，厚酬村之贫乏者。

    Explanation

    190

    The Next Day, all seven thousand gold coins were lent to him, accompanied by a hundred servants, and he was instructed to return to Chu, saying, "When encountering goods, transport them immediately; there will be no disadvantage." Tong made two round trips and gained tens of thousands more. Moreover, since there were no relatives in Chu, he sent his parents' remains back home in a coffin. Upon returning to the old man, he said, "It is possible now." The old man gave him a portion of the money, and there were still over five million left. The old man wanted to marry his daughter to him, but he declined, saying, "I have a wife who is just a commoner, and we have been apart for three years; I do not wish to be unfaithful." When asked about the person, he recounted the previous events. The old man was surprised and said, "Her face is round and white, with fine and long eyebrows, is she named Wu Zhu?" He replied, "Yes, how did you know this?" The old man said, "She is my aunt's niece. Her parents were both from Shanxi and lived in Anhui as migrants. She was also the daughter of a wealthy man, and her home was in the south of our village, a place with a thousand trees and a bamboo house. On a certain day and month, she was taken away by a thunderstorm. How could I know she would become your wife!" The next day, the old man introduced Tong to Old Man Hao and Old Lady Hao. The whole family was in a frenzy, laughing and rejoicing wildly. The old man and old lady urgently wanted to see their daughter. They then sailed from Huai'an on a sea vessel, personally delivering the bride to the groom, and transporting the funds back to Dengzhou. With a favorable wind, they arrived at Feilai Village in no time. The reunion of family members was like a dream, as if they had been separated for a long time, and the joy and sorrow were evident. The dowry was extremely rich and complete, and the scholar-official was rewarded generously, and their family lineages were connected. After living for half a year, they wanted to take the son-in-law's family to Anhui, but the daughter said, "No, I will not forget the kindness of the villagers." She then gave a thousand gold coins to reward the poor and needy in the village.

    191

    女仅生子一，因体羸多疾，为夫置妾媵，生子女若林立。郝夫人性惟钟爱女，环西苑，筑甥馆同居焉。阡陌云连，楼阁华伟，不啻世家。至诸子成立，游庠序，女年甫四十有五，犹妍丽若天人。一夕，梦伟丈夫荷双龙来，内皆纱帽累累，举而遍挂，屋角几满，尾更有缨帽衔宝石挂孔翠者亦夥。丈夫挂讫，故俯瞰笼内，啧啧称有余，戏掀掷过西墙，笑曰：“便宜他。”寐告佟，知子孙必多有科甲显宦者。后果验。然时方明季，不识缨帽是何威仪。至盛朝，佟姓世居鲁，仍簪缨拾青紫，所以有宝顶翠羽帽也。至抛掷墙外者，盖婿家均贵，亦不减外家。此桃源令孙梦麟所云。懊侬氏曰：彼美人兮，天涯海角，而氤氲簿上，早订婚姻，鬼神无奈何，始幻此缩地法耳。当不负死友，临奠数言，实诸天菩萨所共闻之，而一齐附泪者也。或另注奇缘，使之得内助，享厚福，亦未可定。尝闻四川周姓，亦曾有之，与此事同。

    Explanation

    191

    A Woman with a Single Son

    She only gave birth to one son, but due to her weak body and frequent illnesses, her husband arranged for concubines, resulting in many sons and daughters. The wife, however, cherished her daughter above all others. Around the western garden, she built a guesthouse for her son-in-law to live with them. The fields and paths were connected like clouds, and the pavilions and towers were magnificent, not unlike a wealthy family. When the sons grew up, they studied in schools, and the daughter, who was just forty-five years old, remained as beautiful as a celestial being. One night, she dreamed of a great man carrying two dragons. Inside the dragons were many hats, which he raised and hung everywhere, almost filling the corners of the house. At the end, there were many hats with tassels, inlaid with gems, and hanging with peacock feathers. The man finished hanging the hats, then looked down into the cage, murmuring that there was plenty. He playfully threw them over the western wall, laughing and saying, "It's a good deal for him." In her sleep, she told Tong, knowing that her descendants would surely have many successful scholars and officials. Indeed, this prophecy came true. However, at that time, it was the late Ming dynasty, and no one knew what the tassels and hats represented. Until the prosperous dynasty, the surname Tong lived in Lu, still wearing official hats and robes, which is why they had hats with precious gemstones and peacock feathers. The hats thrown over the wall were likely because the son-in-law's family was equally prestigious, not inferior to the in-laws. This is what the magistrate of Taoyuan, Sun Menglin, said. Aonge commented: That beautiful woman, no matter where she is, and on the book of fate, marriage was already arranged, and the gods and spirits could do nothing, so they used this method of shrinking the earth. She would not let down her deceased friend, and when she spoke a few words at the funeral, it was truly heard by all the bodhisattvas, and they all shed tears together. Perhaps another explanation for a strange fate, allowing her to get help from within, enjoy great fortune, is also possible. I have heard that the Zhou family in Sichuan also had something similar, like this story.

    192

    雪里红
    　　
    　　北里非相攸之地，勾栏无立节之媛，然未可以一概论者。昔京都有薛氏女，貌绝艳，携一婢一妪，赁宅以居。自云十五岁矣。出多金市司街卒，遂安堵而开锦泥窠也。尝簪花傅粉，乘犊车往城南观新戏，傀儡棚亦轮为之停，遂不言而知其钱树子也。游荡儿群拥之归。问乡贯游踪，均葫芦提以对。问香名，则曰：“薛氏，行一。”侍者呼为一娘。问夜合须多寡，则伸玉臂，露守宫砂，曰：“六岁时，遇吾师以丹药点作贞验，至今身犹处子，不愿作夜度娘也。”曰：“若是，则必工吟善咏，如卿家校书名涛者乎？”曰：“非所长。”曰：“是必唱鹧鸪舞柘枝，如谢家姬乎？”曰：“非所长。”曰：“或者能刺鸳鸯锦，如卿家针神，号夜来者乎？”曰：“妾病未能也。

    Explanation

    192

    Snow in Red 　　

    The North District is not a place of high moral character, and the brothels lack virtuous ladies, yet it cannot be generalized. In the past, there was a Xue family girl in the capital who was exceptionally beautiful. She lived in a rented house with a maid and an old woman. She claimed to be fifteen years old. With much money, she bribed the street officers, and thus settled down in a luxurious and comfortable living environment. She often adorned herself with flowers and makeup, rode in a small carriage to the southern part of the city to watch a new play, and even the puppet show stage stopped for her. Thus, it was known without saying that she was a money-making device. A group of idle boys escorted her back home. When asked about her origin and travels, she responded vaguely. When asked about her name, she said, "Xue, the eldest daughter." Her attendant called her "One Lady." When asked about the amount of money needed for a night's stay, she extended her jade arm and revealed the red mark indicating her virginity, saying, "At six years old, my teacher used a magic pill to mark me as a virgin, and I have remained so until now. I do not wish to become a courtesan at night." Someone asked, "If that is the case, you must be skilled in poetry and composition, like the famous courtesan named Mingtao from your family?" She replied, "That is not my strength." Another asked, "Are you skilled in singing and dancing, like the courtesan from the Xie family?" She replied, "That is not my strength." Someone else asked, "Or perhaps you can embroider peony patterns, like the needlework goddess known as Yela from your family?" She replied, "I am unable to do so due to illness."

    193

    ”众抚掌曰：“审是，究以何术，博缠头锦？”女含笑，呼婢捧磁瓯出，中列骰子六，供几中央，再拜安置讫，曰：“妾名虽倚门，实则求偶，请以相思之骨，代风月之媒，是亦遵吾师命也。来者以白金十两作孤注，无论齿高下，貌妍媸，一掷成六红者，即嫁为妇，无食言。然十两仅一掷，再掷再破悭。妾守信，君等毋吝财也。”众曰：“妙哉！是真别树艳帜，而另系赤绳者。”呼僮竞取白镪至，博竟日，皆色沮去。远近哗噪，咸云：“何物粉儿，独翻花样，唱求牡之雉，呼守雌之卢，花骨头岂真作冰，阿堵物依然入橐。是儿狡哉，荡子愚矣！”然爱其色者，则难忘情，由是马絷于场，犬迎于户，上至官裳，下至胥隶，无不闻香而来，垂涎而至，作几希望。又以一娘不似妓名，因其爱着杏子衫，又全红始嫁，薛雪同音，遂呼之为雪里红云。一日，有侍御公子某，携百金来，十掷皆北。

    Explanation

    193

    The crowd clapped and said, "If it is true, what method can be used to win a headscarf?" The woman smiled and called a maid to bring out a porcelain bowl, which contained six dice. She placed the bowl in the center of the table, bowed twice, and then said, "Although I am a courtesan, I am actually seeking a husband. Please let my longing bones serve as a substitute for the courtesan's matchmaker, following my master's command. The one who comes should bet ten taels of silver as a single bet, regardless of age or beauty. If one throw results in six reds, then I will marry him as his wife, and I will not break my word. However, ten taels is only for one throw; if you want to bet again, you must increase your stake. I keep my word, and you all should not be stingy with your money." The crowd exclaimed, "Wonderful! This is truly a unique and bold approach." They called servants to bring silver coins, and gambling went on all day, but everyone left dejected. Near and far, there was commotion, and people said, "What kind of woman is this, uniquely innovative, singing for a male pheasant and calling for a female hare? The dice bones are not really ice, but the silver still goes into the bag. This girl is cunning, and the gamblers are foolish!" However, those who loved her appearance could not forget their feelings, and as a result, horses were tied up in the field, and dogs welcomed at the door. From officials to clerks, all came attracted by the fragrance, drooling at the prospect, as if hoping for something. She was not a typical courtesan's name, but because she loved wearing apricot-colored clothes, and she was fully red when she married, her name is similar to Xue Xue, so they called her Xue Li Hong. One day, a certain nobleman's son came with a hundred taels of silver, and he lost all ten throws.

    194

    明日又来，视瓯中已别五红，其一滚转不定，公子大声呼红，比走盘珠定，则仍守黑者，乃痴立如木鸡。女笑曰：“险哉！何一红之难也？”女貌虽极妍，而性极生硬。客至，略寒暄，即请博，博已，即下逐客令，不许少一逗留。公子恚怒，阴嗾无赖子三四辈，给金，使诣女室，将窘辱，洗愧恧。女已审其意，待金入己橐，即挥手使去。无赖子大哄，曰：“一脔肉，竟如此昂贵耶？彼博场尚有闲菜酒，为吾辈解饥渴。”言已，揽袖欲侮，女声色不动，略拂纤指，即纷跌庭外数十步，呼婢出夏楚，待其三蹶而三拍之，跪谢始释，鼠窜可怜。后有踵至者，咸悉彼姝艳而勇，无敢亵，负者怏怏，不闻有后言。三载中，均落落小酬应，从不歌一声，陪一宴，假一颦笑，而香奁中累累盈五万金矣。偶语客曰：“曩得采仅娶吾身，今并得吾财，何乐而不为？”于是门前车马，更喧如市井矣。

    Explanation

    194

    The next day, he came again, looking into the bowl to see five reds, one of which was rolling and not settling. The young man loudly called out for red, and after it settled like a pearl in a dish, the one that remained black, he stood motionless as if turned to stone. The woman laughed and said, "How dangerous! How difficult it is to get just one red!" Though the woman was extremely beautiful, her nature was extremely stubborn. When guests arrived, she made brief greetings and immediately asked them to play, and after the game, she gave them an order to leave, not allowing any delay or lingering. The young man became angry and secretly instructed a few ruffians, gave them money, and sent them to her room to embarrass and humiliate her, thereby washing away his own shame. The woman understood his intentions, and once she received the money, she dismissed the ruffians with a wave of her hand. The ruffians complained, saying, "How expensive is one piece of meat? There is still free food and drink at the gambling hall to satisfy our hunger and thirst." After speaking, they reached out to assault her, but she remained calm and with a slight flick of her fingers, they fell outside the courtyard. She called her maid to administer punishment, and the ruffians were hit three times, bowed in apology, and then scurried away pitifully. Subsequent visitors learned of her beauty and courage and no longer dared to disrespect her. Those who lost were disgruntled but did not speak further. Over three years, she rarely entertained, never sang, attended banquets, or smiled, yet her wealth accumulated to a significant amount. Occasionally, she told guests, "In the past, winning only meant marrying me, but now it also means winning my wealth. What is there to not enjoy?" As a result, the front of her house became even more crowded with vehicles and people, like a bustling market.

    195

    时江建昌李生名崇者，年少尚未娶，避难走燕台，落拓殊甚，将往投阿姐某宦宅，向为姐夫所恶。然守囊之银，仅仅十两。计无出，谋于同乡某，某问曰：“子知此间有雪里红乎？”曰：“不知。”仍告所以，曰：“曷拼与一战，捷则立地成富耳，负则寻女未迟也。”生曰：“善。”浼导之往。女见其貌清俊而衣褴褛，心怜之，曰：“此非樗蒲戏，乃秦晋卜也。登场一麾，绝无翻悔。观子十金良不易，盍珍重？”生笑曰：“卿亦过小觑人矣。书生虽贫，量不致为些须露乞儿态。”言辞慷慨，其气熊熊，已令佳人心折。及锵然一鸣，座客齐鸣采，视瓯中灿灿者，成六出花矣。女亦色喜，嘤咛一声，阶下应，即时结采，燃两画烛如椽，毡毹满堂，水陆新进。婢子引生入曲室，沐浴更新衣；女亦妆竟，偕之交拜，同乡某宛傧赞焉。至漏乍转，客去，归洞房，女略询家世，即云：“身已相从，愿闻郎志。”生颦蹙曰：“一身落魄，去就颇难。

    Explanation

    195

    A young man from Jiangjiang named Li Chong, who was still unmarried, fled to Yan Platform to avoid trouble and found himself in dire straits. He intended to visit the residence of his sister, who was married into a noble family, but she had been disliked by her husband. However, he had only ten taels of silver in his bag. With no other options, he consulted a fellow villager, who asked, “Do you know if there is a Snow Red here?” Li Chong replied, “I don’t know.” The villager then explained, “Why not wager a battle? If victorious, you’ll instantly become wealthy; if defeated, it’s not too late to seek a woman.” Li Chong agreed, “Good.” The villager led him to a gathering. The woman, seeing his handsome appearance but ragged clothes, felt pity for him and said, “This is not a game of dice, but a decision like that of Qin and Jin. Once you take the stage, there is no turning back. Seeing that you have ten gold pieces, it’s not easy, so be cautious!” Li Chong smiled and said, “You underestimate me too much. Though poor, a scholar would not stoop to the beggarly demeanor of a trifling matter.” His words were resolute, and his spirit was fiery, already winning the woman’s admiration. When he struck the gong with a loud sound, the guests all cheered, and they saw that the bowl was filled with six-petaled flowers. The woman also looked delighted, and with a soft cry, there was a response from below the stairs. Immediately, they concluded the bet, lit two painted candles as thick as pillars, and the room was filled with luxurious furnishings from both land and sea. The maid led Li Chong into a secluded room, where he bathed and changed into new clothes; the woman had also finished dressing, and they bowed together in a formal exchange. The fellow villager acted as the officiant. After the time passed, the guests left, and they returned to the bridal chamber. The woman briefly inquired about his family background and said, “I have already chosen to follow you, so tell me your aspirations.” Li Chong frowned and said, “In my current state of destitution, it’s difficult to decide what to do.”

    196

    然为贾则素恶铜臭，为儒则不耐毡寒，无已，其一行作吏，尚可以身许国乎？”女裣衽而前，曰：“是尚不愧为红之夫也。”旋以臂红示生，曰：“妾尚女儿身，唯夫君怜之。”生以唾拭之不去，喜曰：“皮相者，疑卿为章台柳；福艳者，知卿是佛池莲。仆心醉矣，且爱且怜。”明晨，视红褪如洗。旋以片纸为生纳粟，得铨闽之某县令事。之任，女不令多随从，卷厚资，与婢妪均易男子装，己则缨曼胡，吉莫靴，负弹弓，佩长剑，四骑登程。经泰山下，猝遇响马贼呼啸至。生战栗，面灰死，女命婢妪护郎君，己则纵辔迓贼锋，发连珠铁弹子，如秋风摧落叶，贼披靡，无生还者。生大骇，几坠骑。至前站，私询其何勇过健儿。女笑曰：“措大尚藐视巾帼耶？聂隐、红线辈，自在人间，惜肉眼人不识耳。”因把酒，重述都中拍无赖事，相与捧腹。到闽谒长吏，女均执刺代长须，致无陨越。

    Explanation

    196

    If I were to become a merchant, I would always dislike the smell of money; if I were to become a scholar, I would not tolerate the cold of the felt robes. If not, perhaps I should serve as an official and still be able to dedicate myself to the state? The woman bowed and stepped forward, saying, "This still does not disgrace me as your wife." She then showed him the red mark on her arm, saying, "I am still a maiden, only my husband can pity me." The man wiped it with his saliva but it would not go away, and he was delighted, saying, "Those who judge by appearance would doubt that you are the willow tree on Changtai Street; those who appreciate inner beauty know that you are the lotus flower in Buddha's pool. I am utterly captivated, loving and cherishing you." The next morning, he saw the red flush fade away as if washed clean. She immediately used a small piece of paper to help me register for a position as a county magistrate in Fujian. On our way to take up the post, she did not allow many attendants, gathered a large sum of money, and had her maids and servants dress in men's clothing. She herself wore a silk scarf, a Manchu hat, and leather boots, carried a slingshot, and wore a long sword. They set off on four horses. As they passed by Mount Tai, they suddenly encountered a gang of bandits who came charging. The man trembled with fear, his face turning pale as if dead. She ordered her maids and servants to protect the young master, while she herself galloped forward to meet the bandits, firing a continuous stream of iron bullets like autumn wind tearing leaves, causing the bandits to scatter, none of them surviving. The man was greatly frightened and nearly fell off his horse. When they reached the next station, he privately asked her how she could be braver than a strong man. She laughed and said, "How can you still look down on women? Heroes like Nie Ying and Red Line are still alive in the world; it's just that ordinary people don't recognize them." She then offered him wine, retold stories of her exploits in the capital, and they both laughed heartily. When they arrived in Fujian and met with local officials, she took charge, representing me as if I were a seasoned official, ensuring that nothing went wrong.

    197

    夤僚瞰艳仆，疑其有断袖癖，实不知即使君采苹妇也。抵任不半载，金陵贼驱大股，豕突至闽，蓦围斗大城。生集乡团，女帘后窥之，笑曰：“若以此御贼，直摧枯拉朽耳！寇深矣，且奈何？”翌日，贼更集，肆猱升，将瓦解。女麾众登陴，斫杀一昼夜，攻始懈，然耽耽者犹不去。生乞救于中丞，牍三申而援不至，盖驿路断，恨无神邮也。至夕，女以大爵劝生饮，生忧惧，涓滴难下咽，女笑曰：“饮亦死，不饮亦死，曷为醉觞乎？”生始鲸吸数十爵，沉酣眠所坐木椅上。女出，裂帛长十数丈者，先缚生，然后荷于背，且缚之极坚固，若负襁褓儿。仍男装，右提剑，左握护身牌，听野鸡三唱，晨月朦胧，呼乡团而告之曰：“事急矣，吾负汝使君往乞援，三日即返。城中诸事，听吾婢妪指挥，可谧无恐也。”遂开关，放之出。贼瞰其越濠，行甚速，又见所负如屋上瓴，不识云何，始痴望，继环击，女且走且御，斩数十人而出重围焉。

    Explanation

    197

    A Suspicious Official and a Courageous Wife

    The official's subordinate, intrigued by his unusual behavior, suspected him of having a penchant for homosexuality. However, he did not know that the official was actually married to a woman. Not even half a year after taking office, a large force of bandits from Jinling rushed into Fujian like wild boars, suddenly surrounding a small but significant city. The official gathered the local militia, and his wife watched from behind the curtain, smiling and saying, "If you use this method to fight the enemy, it will be as easy as breaking down rotten wood and crumbling walls! The enemy is deep inside, what shall we do now?" The next day, the enemy gathered again, climbing up the walls like monkeys, about to collapse the city. The woman led the people to climb the city walls, fighting and killing for a whole day and night. The attack finally slackened, but the persistent ones still did not leave. The official begged for help from the Governor-General, sending three messages for aid, but no help arrived because the postal route was cut off, regretting the lack of a divine messenger. By evening, the woman offered the official a large cup of wine to drink. The official, full of worry and fear, could hardly swallow even a drop. The woman laughed and said, "Whether you drink or not, you will die, so why not get drunk?" The official then drank several large cups in one gulp and fell into a deep sleep on the wooden chair he was sitting on. The woman left, took out a long piece of silk cloth, first bound the official, then carried him on her back, binding him very tightly, as if carrying a baby in a swaddling cloth. She dressed in men's clothes, holding a sword in her right hand and a protective shield in her left. She waited until the roosters crowed three times, and the morning moon was dim, then called out to the local militia and said to them, "The situation is urgent, I will carry your governor to seek help, and I will return in three days. All matters in the city will be under the command of my maids and elderly women, you can rest assured." She then opened the gate and let them out. The enemy saw them crossing the moat, moving very quickly, and also saw what she was carrying as if it were a water pot on the roof, not knowing what it was. They first stared in amazement, then surrounded and attacked. The woman fought while running, cutting down dozens of enemies and escaping the encirclement.

    198

    县距省垣三百里，至则甫卓午。走入馆舍，解背上良人，视残醉犹未醒也。往见中丞，时抚军麾下仅万人，意不能多假。生将唯诺，女在身后突白云：“但乞中丞分五百人供臂使，然必由属吏自检择，不中选者勿须也。”中丞惊询谁氏子，生诡以仆对，且告其神勇，曰：“是岂昆仑复生乎！”即传鼓集演武场，女为生暗物色，皆捷如猿猱而勇如罴虎者。生诣首令某处，假两骑以归。某令见女，戏生曰：“明府当此仓猝，尚挈画郎郑樱桃耶？”生庄色对曰：“山荆也。”令骇，亟讯知其事，急延之后堂，夫人出拜。请须臾，略休息，公子一，女郎一，争拜膝下，乞授业。女约以围解日。旋整师归，贼正团团攻雉堞，突自背击之，几疑将军从天外飞来者。战一昼夜，斩馘无算，余孽悉窜。女抚膺而叹曰：“是可驱豺狼而害邻封乎？”留生入城抚疮痍，己则帅众追剿至浙界，始凯旋。

    Explanation

    198

    The county was 300 li away from the provincial capital, and by the time they arrived, it was just noon. They entered the inn and removed the burden from their backs, finding that the lingering drunkenness had not yet cleared. They went to see the provincial governor, but at that time, the military governor had only ten thousand troops under his command, and he was unwilling to lend many more. The man was about to agree, but the woman behind him suddenly said, "I only ask the provincial governor to assign 500 men to my service, but they must be selected by the subordinate officials themselves, and those who do not meet the criteria should not be needed." The provincial governor was startled and asked who she was. The man lied and said she was his servant, and he also told him of her extraordinary bravery, saying, "Could she be a reincarnation of Kunlun?" Immediately, the drum was beaten to gather at the military training ground, and the woman secretly chose the most agile and brave soldiers, all as swift as monkeys and as brave as bears and tigers. The man went to the first officer and borrowed two horses to return home. The officer saw the woman and teased him, saying, "At such a critical moment, you still brought along the painter Zheng Yacheng?" The man replied solemnly, "She is my humble wife." The officer was shocked and quickly inquired about the matter. He hastily invited them to his back room, where the lady came out to greet them. After a brief rest, a young man and a young lady bowed before them, begging to be taught. The woman promised to teach them on the day the siege was resolved. Soon, they organized the army and returned. The rebels were besieging the city walls, and they suddenly attacked from behind, almost making it seem as if the general had flown in from the heavens. They fought for a day and night, killing countless enemies, and the remaining rebels all fled. The woman sighed and said, "Can we drive away the wolves and harm our neighbors?" She left the man to enter the city to tend to the wounded, while she led the troops to pursue and suppress the rebels to the borders of Zhejiang, and finally returned victorious.

    199

    当道上其事，生卓异，迁郡守，女诏封成夫人。某令遣子女来践约，乞授好身手，女略与一二即已，曰：“罗绮中人，得此已无敌，无深求也。”问战胜大旨，曰：“譬如博，盂内六琼，眼前八阵，知人知彼，目无全牛，胜负可立决矣。”问阿姥是何师承，曰：“吾自有师，师自有法，不足为汝辈言也。”生每断狱，女参赞，称神明；捕盗，尤襄勤；唯禁娼则力为谏阻，继以哀泣，故棠疆百里，桃花门巷，常数百家，成锦绣城也。每采舆一出，莺莺燕燕之俦，捧盘跪路衢，为夫人祝千秋者，动以千计。此秦鲁臣谱弟所采访；鲁臣，金陵世家子。懊侬氏曰：寒蔬中，有名雪里红者，辛辣之品也。成夫人隐于粉黛，博以相夫，坐而裙衩，勇于杀贼，是桃李之艳，而姜桂之性也。锡此佳名，良不愧耳。不言其师，而师自大有人在。闽之氓，感保障之德，每至伏腊，有以雪里红荐春盘者。当不敢斥言而呼为成夫人菜乎！嘻！诸葛行营之种，可以并传。

    Explanation

    199

    When the official's deeds were reported to the authorities, he was found to be outstanding and was promoted to the position of magistrate of a prefecture. His daughter was granted the title of Lady Cheng by the emperor. A certain magistrate sent his children to fulfill the agreement, requesting that Lady Cheng teach them martial skills. She taught them a few techniques and said, "For those in the upper echelons of society, possessing these skills is already unmatched; there is no need to seek more." When asked about the principles of winning battles, she replied, "It is like a game of chance, where one must understand the situation (the 'six jades' in the bowl and the 'eight formations' before one's eyes), know oneself and the enemy, and have the expertise to make quick decisions." When asked about her teacher, she said, "I have my own teacher, and my teacher has his own methods; I cannot speak of them to you." Whenever the magistrate handled cases, Lady Cheng would provide advice, and her judgment was considered divine; in capturing bandits, she was especially diligent; however, she strongly opposed the prohibition of prostitution, often weeping in protest. Thus, within the hundred-mile territory of Tangjiang, there were often hundreds of brothels, creating a city of refinement. Whenever her sedan chair appeared, groups of young women would kneel on the road, offering trays of food and wine to wish her a long life, numbering in the thousands. This account was reported by a descendant of Qin Lu, a historian from Nanjing. The author, An Nong, remarked: Among cold vegetables, there is one called xue lihong (snow pea cabbage), which is a pungent vegetable. Lady Cheng, hidden among the beauties, used her knowledge of strategy to assist her husband, sitting in a dress and skirt, yet she was brave in fighting bandits, combining the beauty of peaches and plums with the strength of ginger and cinnamon. To bestow such a fine name upon her is well-deserved. Though she did not mention her teacher, there were indeed great people who influenced her. The people of Fujian, grateful for her protection and support, would offer xue lihong in their spring dishes during the traditional festivals. Could they dare to call it anything else but "Lady Cheng's cabbage"? Indeed, it is a legacy that can be passed down alongside the seeds of Zhuge Liang's camp.

    200

    邬生艳遇
    　　
    　　邬荣典，字少华，任城儒家子，年十七，尚未婚。时正夏五，移枕席置小斋，一老仆作伴，喜岑寂也。一夕溽暑，令人思，因遣仆宿外舍，自起拂榻拭几，剪烛烹茶，视皓月一窗，不禁遐想，背灯危坐，口吟一绝，云：“明月此时好，美人何处来。相怜唯有影，绮户为谁开。”诗就，曼声吟咏。忽一丽人冉冉至，年约十五六，广袖长裙，乌鬓翠黛，目盈盈若秋水，裙下露莲瓣，翘翘若解结之锥，殆画中人也。邬惊询曰：“卿鬼耶？”曰：“否。”“人耶？”曰：“否。”“然则狐耶？”笑曰：“郎志在美妇，妾志在情郎，偶听高吟，知情之所钟，故冒嫌学私奔之红拂，郎何必哓哓询踪迹耶？”曰：“卿有名乎？”对曰：“宾奴。”“有字乎？”曰：“樊稚。”邬不甚了了，第握纤纤手，则柔胜于荑，令人魂荡。

    Explanation

    200

    The Beautiful Encounter of Wu Sheng

    Wu Rongdian, courtesy name Shao Hua, was a young man from a Confucian family in Rencang, aged seventeen and still unmarried. It was the fifth month of summer, and he moved his pillow and mat to a small study, accompanied by an old servant, enjoying the quiet and seclusion. One night, the oppressive heat and humidity made him reflect, so he sent the servant to sleep outside and got up himself to sweep the bed and wipe the table, trim the candle and brew tea. He looked at the bright moon through the window, unable to resist his wandering thoughts. Sitting upright with a lamp behind him, he recited a quatrain aloud: "The moon is beautiful tonight, but where does the beautiful lady come from? The only one who understands each other is the shadow, for whom will the ornate door be opened?" After finishing the poem, he sang it in a prolonged, melodious voice. Suddenly, a beautiful woman appeared gracefully, about fifteen or sixteen years old, wearing wide sleeves and a long skirt, with black hair and green eyebrows, her eyes clear and bright like autumn water. Her skirt revealed the shape of lotus petals, and her feet, with their pointed toes, seemed like sharp tools for untangling knots. She was almost like a person from a painting. Wu was startled and asked, "Are you a ghost?" She replied, "No." "Are you a human?" She said, "No." "Then are you a fox spirit?" She smiled and said, "Your heart is set on a beautiful woman, and my heart is set on a lover. I heard your high-spirited recitation and understood your affection, so I took a risk to learn from the bold actions of Red Silk Fan. Why do you keep asking about my identity?" Wu asked, "Do you have a name?" She replied, "Bin Nu." "Do you have a courtesy name?" She said, "Fan Zhi." Wu did not fully understand, but when he took her delicate hands, they were softer than the finest grass, making him feel deeply moved.

    201

    相与谈论，慧舌生香，旁及词章，藻思耀采，邬爱且服。听玉漏丁丁，墙外之柝四下，促其解衣，则飞红上颊，约以明宵。野鸡四啼，仓皇遽遁。翌果挑灯自携衾枕至，备极华丽，人世所无。遂与绸缪，而痛楚莫胜。女曰：“妾身犹不雕璞也，乞郎徐徐，幸勿狂暴。”事已，视清簟落红，真犹处子，邬益怜爱。因以臂代枕，口吟一词，云：“郎可怜，妾可怜，一对鸳鸯一对鹣，今宵那世缘。莫流连，且流连，生怕钟鸣欲曙天，情人隔一边。”女喜曰：“郎真有情也。妾虽自荐，然得此错爱，死不可憾也！”即和其词，云：“风谁家，月谁家，妾岂当门卖笑娃，情深念转差。香辟邪，正辟邪，夜雨摧残一树花，郎君郑重些。”天晓，自摘耳上两金环赠邬，曰：“以此作定情物，然慎勿示人，恐飞短流长，彼此不利。”自此来无虚夕。

    Explanation

    201

    They conversed together, their wise words emitting a fragrance, and their discussions on literature and poetry were rich and brilliant, earning Wu's admiration and affection. Listening to the sound of the jade drip, the watchman's drum beats four times outside the wall, urging him to take off his clothes, his cheeks blushed, and they agreed to meet again the next night. The roosters crow four times, and she fled in haste. The next day, she indeed brings a lamp and her bedding, which is extremely luxurious, unparalleled in the world. They then engaged in intimate relations, but the pain was unbearable. She said, "My body is still uncarved jade, please be gentle, and do not be violent." After the act, seeing the clear mat stained with blood, truly like a virgin, Wu's affection and love increased. She used her arm as a pillow and recited a poem, saying, "Dear, I pity you; Dear, I pity me, a pair of mandarin ducks and a pair of jia, tonight our worldly fate. Do not linger, yet linger, afraid that the bell will ring and dawn will break, lovers separated by one side." She happily said, "Dear, you truly have feelings for me. I may have offered myself, but to receive such mistaken love, I would not regret dying!" She then recited the poem, saying, "Whose is the wind? Whose is the moon? I am not a courtesan selling smiles at the door, deep feelings turn into thoughts of difference. Pungent to ward off evil, correct to ward off evil, night rain damages a tree of flowers, dear, be serious." At dawn, she herself picks off the two gold rings from her ears and gives them to Wu, saying, "Use this as a token of love, but be careful not to show it to others, fear that rumors will spread, harm both of us." From then on, there was no empty night.

    202

    一夜正偎拥，忽有斑白叟破门入，面靛裂，发蓬飞，霜髯如戟，叱女曰：“小妮子，太不识羞耻！”既而指邬曰：“污人清白，风狂儿不当杀却耶！”邬惊惶无地，以被蒙首，口噤不能言，唯齿牙震击作奇响，自被隙微窥女郎，则俯首却立，觳觫可怜。正疑惧间，老人呵斥益厉。忽仆在外舍，反侧匡床间，声扎扎，二人遂渺。次夜，邬扃户，眠不熟，而女已袅娜在床侧，娇羞惨淡，默无一言。邬执其手，问：“昨宵老叟属卿何人？”曰：“老父也。

    Explanation

    202

    One night, as they were hugging each other closely, suddenly an elderly man with white hair burst in, his face distorted with rage, his hair flying wildly, and his beard like spears. He scolded the young woman, saying, "You little girl, you have no shame!" Then, pointing to Wu, he said, "You have tainted her purity! Shouldn't you, this reckless boy, be killed?" Wu was utterly terrified, covering his head with the blanket, unable to speak, his teeth and jaw clattering in a strange sound. Through the gap in the blanket, he peeked at the young woman, who was trembling with fear, her head bowed and standing back, pitifully trembling. As he was in a state of doubt and fear, the old man scolded even more fiercely. Suddenly, Wu collapsed outside the room, lying restlessly on the bed, making strange noises, and both disappeared. The next night, Wu locked the door, unable to sleep soundly, and the young woman had already appeared beside him, graceful and delicate, shyly and sadly, silent and without a word. Wu held her hand and asked, "Who was the old man from last night?" She replied, "He was my father."

    203

    ”曰：“卿家大人，险将小生惊煞，然我两人之情分，岂即尽于此乎？匝月恩爱，已逾寻常，某愿为卿死，不悔也。”女嗟叹久之，始云：“郎何痴也！以郎表表，何难得玉台艳偶，而乃犯险阻争异类哉？且家君素严，翌即迁他郡，妾来永辞，愿郎自爱，毋以妾为念！”邬失声大哭，女以袖中红巾拭泪，己亦泣曰：“妾原图永好耳。不意怒触高堂，殃及君子，义难复聚，请以所赠赐还；非重物也，恐郎他日触目伤心耳。天如鉴怜，则镜可圆而剑可合。
    妾去矣，千万保重！”言已顿杳。听户外修竹风敲，如摇环佩，举箧视金环，已不知于何时携去。然邬由此玉体羸败，念念不忘玉人。有女巫阿翠，目能见狐，且知狐所在。邬因邀而问之，曰：“若其好着淡黄帔薄罗衫，面团团如月，一笑两颊上生微涡者耶？”曰：“然。”曰：“是非他，骆氏小素也。”邬始恍然悟，昔告之名字，乃暗切而不肯明言者。阿翠请生作简，愿任作寄书邮。

    Explanation

    203

    The girl said, "Your esteemed father almost scared me to death, but our relationship cannot end here, can it? A full month of love has surpassed the ordinary, and I am willing to die for you, without regret." The girl sighed for a long time before saying, "How foolish you are! With your distinguished qualities, why can't you find a beautiful partner among humans, and why do you risk danger to pursue a creature of a different kind? Moreover, my father is usually strict, and the next day he will move to another prefecture. I am leaving now, please take care, and do not worry about me!"

    Wu lost his voice and cried out, the girl used a red handkerchief from her sleeve to wipe her tears, and she also wept, saying, "I originally hoped for a lasting happiness. I did not expect to anger my father and bring trouble upon you, making it impossible for us to reunite. Please accept the gift I gave you back; it is not a valuable item, but I fear that seeing it might sadden you in the future. May heaven judge and pity, and may the mirror be whole and the sword be reunited."

    She then suddenly disappeared. Wu heard the sound of bamboo rustling in the wind outside the door, like the tinkling of jade pendants. When he opened the chest, the gold ring was already gone, though he did not know when she had taken it. From then on, Wu's body weakened and became frail, and he could not forget the beautiful person. There was a female fortune-teller named Aici, who could see foxes and knew where they were. Wu invited her and asked, "Is she the one who likes to wear light yellow robes and thin silk shirts, with a round face like the moon, and two dimples appear on her cheeks when she smiles?" Aici replied, "Yes, it is Xiaosu of the Luo family." Wu then suddenly understood that the name he had been given was a subtle hint rather than a direct statement. Aici asked him to write a letter, and she was willing to deliver it.

    204

    数日来报，云：“小素匆促，不及裁笺，着传语奉复郎君，前实缘尽，恐径自别去，苦郎相思，故幻此形状，俾郎君心死。乘便寄丹砂一粒，可以却病痛。”邬视药小而红，香甚，一服，疾果瘳，而思女之心亦释。懊侬氏曰：情之所在，父母师保不能止，天地鬼神不能禁，山川河海不能隔。顾为情而来，情未尽，则麾之不去；情既尽，即招之不来。且不以余情害情人，复能以幻相警痴子，是非真深于情者乎？否则，朝伐夕戕，非髓竭神枯而不已；又或洁身而退，令人魂销气结不能忘，真冤且孽矣，尚得谓之情乎？如小素者，方可与言情。

    Explanation

    204

    After several days, a report came, saying: "Xiaosu was in a hurry and did not have time to write a letter. She instructed someone to convey a message to the young master: the previous connection was indeed severed, fearing that she would leave directly, making the young master suffer from longing. Therefore, she created this form, making the young master's heart die. Taking the opportunity, she sent a piece of cinnabar, which can dispel illness and pain."

    Wu saw the medicine small and red, very fragrant. After taking it once, the illness indeed disappeared, and the heart's longing for the woman was also released.

    The commentator,懊侬氏, said: "Where emotion lies, parents, teachers, and guardians cannot stop it, heaven and earth, gods and spirits cannot forbid it, mountains, rivers, seas, and oceans cannot separate it. Because of emotion, if the emotion is not exhausted, it cannot be dismissed; if the emotion is exhausted, it cannot be called back. Not harming the beloved with leftover emotion, and can use a magical form to warn the foolish, is this not truly deep in emotion? Otherwise, morning attacks and evening injuries, not stopping until the marrow is exhausted and the spirit is dried up; or else, withdrawing oneself, making others' souls and spirits break and unable to forget, truly a grudge and a curse, can this still be called emotion? Like Xiaosu, one can speak of emotion."

    205

    假五通神
    　　
    　　南人之崇奉五通，犹北人之信狐也。客有贩卖阿芙蓉发籍者，往来齐楚间，非一日，阿堵充豫，乃纳粟为九品官，在籍候铨。虽煌煌章服，腰佩玉，腕跳脱，襟洋表，面架墨晶镜，而烟霞营生不肯弃，以故富且贵，居然晋绅矣。客姓万，乳名佳儿，遂名曰佳，字颗珠。幼失怙恃，一身块然，因性敏貌俊，善蝇头楷，得为刀笔小吏。娶妻雍，颇风致，犹善艳妆。佳后弃隶学贾，精歌唱丝竹等艺，日诱良家子作狭斜游，多外宿不归。

    Explanation

    205

    A Pretense of Worshiping the Five Lords

    The veneration of the Five Lords in the south is akin to the belief in fox spirits in the north. There was a guest who traded opium and distributed it throughout the regions of Qi and Chu, not for a single day. His wealth grew, and he eventually bought a ninth-rank official position with grain, being registered for an appointment. Although he wore splendid official robes, had a jade pendant around his waist, bracelets on his wrists, a Western-style watch in his pocket, and dark crystal glasses, he did not give up his opium trade. Therefore, he became both wealthy and powerful, truly a member of the gentry. The guest surnamed Wan, with the childhood name Jia'er, was given the name Jia and the courtesy name Kezhu. As a child, he lost his parents and lived alone. Due to his quick wit and handsome appearance, and his skill in small, fine calligraphy, he became a minor official. He married a woman surnamed Yong, who was quite elegant and skilled in applying makeup. Later, Jia abandoned his minor official position to learn commerce, becoming skilled in singing, playing stringed instruments, and other arts. He daily lured well-born young men into the demimonde, often staying out overnight and not returning home.

    206

    雍独居，挑灯凝睇，对镜颐，见己身后，若有男子影，心疑夫婿潜回斗笑，喜而回顾，则翩翩美少年，体态妍丽，羊车中人，乃生平所绝未邂逅者也。惊欲诘，觉口噤神迷，少年已偎之坐，细腻熨贴，慰藉良殷。自陈“为五通四郎，怜子孤衾，特来相伴，未知卿卿亦如小生之痴情否？”雍欲撑拒，觉四郎肌肤中有一缕幽香，透入鼻观；又舌入己口，搅扰吮咂，心遂大动，听其所为。事讫，神顿清，四郎犹共枕。羞怯欢恋，觉天下男儿无逾四郎美。从此朝夕至，至必饮，饮必醉，醉必眠，眠必尽欢而后已。佳偶自外归，四郎仓皇窜匿，怒抽壁上刀挥之，四郎遽化白光一线，飕然从窗缝中出。问妇，妇诡云：“无所见。

    Explanation

    206

    雍独自居住，挑灯凝视，对着镜子自言自语，似乎有男子的影子。心中怀疑丈夫悄悄回来嘲笑，高兴地回头，却见一个翩翩英俊的少年，体态美丽，如同羊车中的人，是我一生中从未见过的。惊讶地想要询问，感觉嘴巴紧闭，神志迷糊，少年已经偎依着坐下，细腻贴切，安慰非常殷切。他自我介绍：“我是五通四郎，怜悯你孤独的被褥，特地来陪伴，不知道你是否也像我一样痴情？”雍想要抵抗，感觉四郎的肌肤中有一缕幽香，透入鼻中；舌头又进入自己的口中，搅扰吮吸，心顿时激动，听任他的行为。事情结束后，神志顿时清醒，四郎仍然共枕。羞怯欢恋，觉得天下男儿无逾四郎美。从此朝夕相见，每次必饮酒，饮酒必醉，醉必眠，眠必尽欢而后已。佳偶自外归，四郎仓皇逃跑躲藏，愤怒地抽出墙上的刀砍向他，四郎立即变成一线白光，嗖的一声从窗缝中出去。询问妻子，妻子诡秘地说：“什么也没看见。”

    207

    ”然枕席上竟由此大冷落，无腻语，无欢颜。明日，四郎又乘间来。妇泣曰：“奴方期图永好，而今已矣！”四郎面赭问故，曰：“郎既神道，何畏渠凡人？”曰：“非也。人之所以异于禽兽者，为天良耳。既盗其妇，又凌其夫，人且不可，何况神乎？”言未已，佳持刀突出，四郎含笑欲去，视门外环立健儿，持弓矢以俟，盖佳之布置也。四郎牵妇衣袖，宛转哀泣，又伏地叩头，愿永绝迹。佳狂躁，刀砍其面，如败鼓声；砍其头，如败柝响。户外人又欲哄入，四郎抽掷金钱如雨，众目炫乱，始从胯下出。

    Explanation

    207

    However, the atmosphere in the bedchamber suddenly turned cold, with no intimate words and no joyful expressions. The next day, Four郎 took advantage of an opportunity to come. The woman wept and said, "I was hoping for eternal happiness, but now it's over!" Four郎, his face reddening, asked why. She replied, "You are a deity, so why fear a mere mortal?" He said, "No, the reason humans differ from animals is due to their natural conscience. You not only stole my wife but also oppressed her husband. If it's wrong for humans, how much more so for a deity?" Before she could finish, the woman rushed out with a knife. Four郎 smiled and was about to leave when he saw men standing in a circle outside, holding bows and arrows, waiting. This was the woman's arrangement. Four郎 grabbed the woman's sleeve, twisting and crying sorrowfully, and then lay on the ground and kowtowed, pledging to never return. The woman, in a frenzy, slashed at his face, making sounds like a broken drum, and then at his head, making sounds like a broken drumstick. The men outside tried to rush in, but Four郎 threw out money like rain, dazzling their eyes. They were confused, and Four郎 escaped by crawling under their legs.

    208

    佳投刀而起，拾地下金钱，得百余两，略酬健儿，余悉入橐。月余，妇病不起，昏昏无一言。夜忽悄起，妆饰一新，曰：“四郎来娶我矣！”佳怒曰：“既神道，何强娶有夫之妇？”曰：“嘻，子休矣！前所得金钱谓何？盖卖妇身值也。”言已，趺榻一笑逝。众闻空际果有鼓吹喧哗，人马杂沓声，逾时始寂。佳之同类，咸来吊鼓盆。佳反鼓掌曰：“仆有远志，山荆本赘疣也。”葬已，遂尽遣婢子，日挟金资走四方。后遇道士，授以金针槐角猪皮做假之法，利更倍焉。汉中史太守，严厉非常。佳能曲意承顺，有触逆太守者，佳必故与为难，詈辱万端；有逢迎太守者，佳必预为往来，周旋尽致。

    Explanation

    208

    Jia threw a knife and stood up, picking up money from the ground, and obtained over a hundred taels, slightly rewarding the strong men, and putting the rest into his bag. After a month, his wife fell ill and could not get up, dazed and not uttering a word. One night, she suddenly quietly got up, dressed herself up anew, and said, "The Fourth Master is coming to marry me!" Jia angrily said, "If you are a spirit, why force yourself on a married woman?" She replied, "Hah, you rest assured! What about the money you got earlier? It was the price of selling the wife's body." After speaking, she fell from the bed and smiled before dying. Everyone heard loud music and noise, the sound of people and horses mingling, and it took some time for it to become quiet. Jia's peers all came to mourn at the drumming ceremony. Jia clapped his hands and said, "I have great ambitions, my wife was merely a useless appendage." After the burial, he sent away all the maidservants and started traveling around with money every day. Later, he met a Taoist priest who taught him the method of making fake things with gold needles, locust beans, and pig skin, which brought him even greater profit. The Governor of Hanzhong was extremely strict. Jia could flatter and submit excessively, and if anyone offended the governor, Jia would deliberately make things difficult for them, insulting and abusing them in countless ways; if anyone flattered the governor, Jia would be proactive in dealing with them, handling matters to the fullest extent.

    209

    守有过，佳揽任于己，曰：“太守何若是？”以故太守逢人谀赞曰：“老人虚度，悔识万生晚也。”偶与千金，嘱代购佳丽，曰：“此苏台土产，敢不奉献。”囊金往，以五百买贫家女，先与私通，迟疑不进。会太守墨败，竟攫为己之第一妾，名曰春花。其友豫人王七，有古癖，烂铜碎玉，书画玩好，无不珍爱。佳代搜罗，凡物罕而价昂者，必暗贴己橐，仅以半价报之。

    Explanation

    209

    The governor had made a mistake, and "佳" took the responsibility upon himself, saying, "How could the governor be so?" Because of this, the governor would often flatter and praise others, saying, "I have wasted my life, regretting not recognizing 万生 earlier." Occasionally, he would give a thousand gold coins and ask someone to buy a beautiful woman for him, saying, "This is a local specialty of Suzhou, how could I not offer it?" Carrying the gold, he went and bought a poor family's daughter for five hundred gold coins. He first engaged in a private relationship with her but hesitated and did not proceed further. Later, when the governor was involved in a corruption scandal, he ultimately took the poor family's daughter as his first concubine, naming her "春花." His friend from Henan, Wang Qi, had an old-fashioned habit, collecting broken copper, shattered jade, paintings, calligraphy, and other antiquities, treasuring everything without exception. "佳" searched for and collected these items on behalf of his friend, secretly putting any rare and expensive items into his own pocket and reporting only half the price.

    210

    以故王大信任，尝谓人曰：“天下信实，更有如万生者乎？”会同人过海，以女寄佳处，曰：“弱息破瓜年矣，仆如三年不回，听吾弟代觅婚配。”王去，佳瞰女美，诡云得坦腹。入赘日，却扇筵开，女掀帕斜睨，则须，目灼灼者，佳也。曰：“吾故疑是佳儿。”曰：“既有佳儿，理配佳妇。”遂攘为己之第二妾，名曰夏云。偶乘焦叟船，过鄱阳。佳善酿，精烹调，每餐必拉焦共啖酌，欲迁东道，必坚止。翁私带小货，佳辄浑其税而倍其值。瞰翁女，船尾操楫，甚艳冶，眉目传情。询翁：“何女公子至今无佳婿？”曰：“小妮子，娇惰惯，不欲嫁弄潮儿。至瞿塘贾，金马客，仆又难仰攀，是以迟耳。

    Explanation

    210

    Due to which, Wang Daren trusted him greatly. Once, he told others, "In the world, is there anyone more trustworthy than Wan Sheng?" Wang Daren agreed to entrust his daughter to Jia, saying, "My daughter is already of marriageable age. If I do not return in three years, let my younger brother find a suitable match for her." After Wang Daren left, Jia saw the girl and found her beautiful, and he pretended to have a son. On the day of the marriage, the fan was removed, and the banquet began. The girl lifted her veil and glanced sideways, and the one with bright eyes was Jia. She said, "I suspected it was Jia's son." Jia replied, "Since we have Jia's son, it is reasonable to marry him to a good wife." He then took her as his second concubine and named her Xiacun. Once, he traveled by Jiao Lao's boat and passed through Poyang. Jia was skilled in brewing and cooking, and at every meal, he would invite Jiao to eat and drink together. If he wanted to move to the east, Jiao would firmly stop him. When the old man secretly brought small goods, Jia would cover the taxes and double the price. Jia looked at the old man's daughter, who was rowing at the stern of the boat, and found her very beautiful and charming. She conveyed affection with her eyes and eyebrows. He asked the old man, "Why has your daughter not found a good husband yet?" The old man replied, "That little girl is spoiled and lazy, and does not want to marry a boatman. As for the merchants of Qutang and the guests of Jinma, I cannot aspire to them. That is why it has been delayed."

    211

    ”曰：“仆不才，九品官也，未知许下玉台否？”翁掀髯笑曰：“万颗珠，四十无妇，谁敢信？”曰：“冤哉！结发死，至今尚未续鸾胶。”指天自誓，翁信不疑。比入门，结缡已三日，悔无及。翁拼闹，欲讼之官，女曰：“儿已至此，夫复何言！”佳长跪请死，崩角有声，翁长叹，拂袖去。遂以女为第三妾，名曰秋月。偶游楚州勾栏张眉娘家，先通其女亭亭，暗订婚娶，缠头甚丰，有成议。眉娘太息曰：“不图吾女先有所归。青楼人珍馐罗绮，贵若嫔嫱，帷凤冠补服不可得，诚不了恨耳。”佳曰：“吾九品官也，他日亭亭得花封，可赠于子。

    Explanation

    211

    The speaker said, "I am unworthy, a low-ranking official, and I do not know if the Jade Terrace in Xu is known to me." The old man lifted his beard and laughed, saying, "A thousand precious pearls, at forty years old without a wife, who would dare to believe it?" The speaker exclaimed, "It is unjust! My spouse died, and I have not yet remarried." He pointed to the sky and swore, and the old man believed him without doubt. By the time they entered the new household, the wedding had already taken place three days ago, and it was too late to regret. The old man quarreled and wanted to take the matter to court, but the daughter said, "The daughter is already here, what more can be said!" The speaker knelt for a long time and pleaded for death, hitting the ground with a sound, and the old man sighed deeply and left angrily. So, he made the woman his third concubine and named her Autumn Moon. He happened to visit the brothel of Zhang Meiniang in Chuzhou, where he had already formed a relationship with her daughter Tingting, and they secretly agreed to marry, with generous gifts exchanged, and an agreement reached. Meiniang sighed, saying, "I did not expect my daughter to be already engaged. Courtesans live in luxury, valued like concubines, but only a ceremonial cap and official robe could not be obtained, truly a source of regret." The speaker said, "I am just a low-ranking official, but one day, when Tingting becomes a wife, she can be given to you."

    212

    ”眉默默良久，小语曰：“子娶亭亭，仅得人而不得财，且欲破其财。吾年来奁资饶富，曷易而娶吾，子既得财，我又得贵，非两全乎？”曰：“弃亭亭，奈何？”曰：“母子同事一人，古有之乎？”曰：“有，有，有！”遂附会小说，诡托无稽。入门，见已有三妇，艳眉大哭，欲寻死。佳极意温柔，化怒为喜，盖以调停冠裳之法，来处置闺阁，故众小相安，亲如姐妹。遂以眉为第四妾，名曰冬松。亭为第五妾，名曰四季。佳更筑房廊，终朝笙管，不数汉家愿老之乡矣。闻画士柯莲善写真，邀至，写五妾，面庞酷肖，更绘楼阁若园圃，或钓鱼，或把酒，或联句拈题，或着棋角胜，绘佳短衣瀹茗于旁，若奴子状，题曰：“五美图。”工竣，佳酬以洋蚨一枚。

    Explanation

    212

    After a long, silent pause, Mei whispered softly, "You married Tingting, but you only got a person and not wealth, and you want to deplete her wealth. My dowry has been rich for years. Why not switch and marry me? You would get wealth, and I would get prestige. Wouldn't that be a win-win situation?" He replied, "What about abandoning Tingting?" She said, "Is it not common in ancient times for mother and son to serve the same person?" He responded, "Yes, yes, yes!" Then he fabricated a story, making absurd claims. Upon entering, he saw three wives, and the beautiful one cried bitterly, wanting to commit suicide. The master was extremely gentle, turning anger into joy, using the method of reconciling gentlemen and ladies to handle the household. Thus, the concubines lived peacefully, like sisters. He then made Mei the fourth concubine, naming her Dong Song, and Ting the fifth concubine, naming her Si Ji. The master built more rooms and halls, with music playing all day, no longer the Han dynasty's place of long-lasting loyalty. He heard that the painter Ke Lian was good at portrait painting and invited him over. Ke Lian painted the five concubines, who looked very much like them, and also painted buildings and gardens, some fishing, some drinking, some composing poetry, and some playing chess. He painted the master in short clothes, brewing tea, looking like a servant, and titled it "Five Beauties." When the work was finished, the master paid him one foreign coin.

    213

    柯争较，佳怒曰：“吾九品官也，尚不足役一画士，则捐官者何荣耀耶！”立叱之出。柯忍辱，祷于五通之庙，佳不知也。一夜醉归，奴子篝灯，至中门止步，盖佳立童约如此。甫履庭院，闻五美房中笑声盈耳，灯光荧荧。潜诣四季，则一狐裘丈夫，偎四季扪乳，四季笑吃吃不休。怒叱走入，丈夫徐起，则貌与己同，无纤毫差。彼曰：“噫！是何妖魅，敢化吾形，来惑四季耶？吾结发为五通神所杀，想又垂涎于子；不然，施施从外来者谁欤？”抽刀而前，四季亦执剪刀相助，势甚汹汹。

    Explanation

    213

    Kō Zhēng Jiào, Ji became angry and said, "I am a ninth-rank official, yet I cannot even command a lowly painter. What honor is there in buying an official title?" He immediately ordered him to leave. Kō endured the humiliation and prayed at the temple of the Five Thunders, which Ji did not know about. One night, after getting drunk and returning home, the servant lit a lamp, and he stopped at the middle gate, as Ji had set up such a rule for the child. As soon as he stepped into the courtyard, he heard laughter filling his ears from the room of Wǔ Měi, with dim lights flickering. Secretly visiting Sì Jì, he found a man in a fox fur coat, cuddling Sì Jì and touching her breast, while Sì Jì laughed uncontrollably. Kō angrily shouted and entered, and the man slowly stood up, looking exactly like him, not a single hair different. The man said, "Ah! What kind of demon dares to transform into my form, coming to bewitch Sì Jì? I was killed by the Five Thunders when I was young, perhaps he is also coveting you; otherwise, who is this person who comes from outside, behaving so leisurely?" He drew a knife and moved forward, and Sì Jì also took a pair of scissors to help, creating a very tense situation.

    214

    佳呼曰：“五娘莫信他，貌虽同，吾衣羊裘，可辨也。”旋见丈夫羊裘，而己则狐裘矣。众媪挥仗争击狐裘人，几毙。急逸入冬松室，亦有一貂裘丈夫，抱冬松饮醇醪，拈花为枚。见佳入，语亦同前，佳曰：“吾衣狐裘，可辨也。”旋见丈夫狐裘，己则貂裘，众婢持帚争扑貂裘人。逸入夏云房，亦有一狼裘丈夫，搂夏云抚案看春册，较量笔墨工细，闻外间哄闹，故作惊讶，告云曰：“卿勿怖，此五通也，闻渠善变，无毫厘爽，先与卿约，吾衣狼裘，若见衣貂裘者，为五通，可诱之入，利剑断其首，卿能助我乎？”曰：“诺。”果就床头拔剑授彼。佳大惊。

    Explanation

    214

    佳呼曰：“五娘莫信他，貌虽同，吾衣羊裘，可辨也。”旋见丈夫羊裘，而己则狐裘矣。众媪挥仗争击狐裘人，几毙。急逸入冬松室，亦有一貂裘丈夫，抱冬松饮醇醪，拈花为枚。见佳入，语亦同前，佳曰：“吾衣狐裘，可辨也。”旋见丈夫狐裘，己则貂裘，众婢持帚争扑貂裘人。逸入夏云房，亦有一狼裘丈夫，搂夏云抚案看春册，较量笔墨工细，闻外间哄闹，故作惊讶，告云曰：“卿勿怖，此五通也，闻渠善变，无毫厘爽，先与卿约，吾衣狼裘，若见衣貂裘者，为五通，可诱之入，利剑断其首，卿能助我乎？”曰：“诺。”果就床头拔剑授彼。佳大惊。

    Translation:
    佳呼曰：“五娘莫信他，貌虽同，吾衣羊裘，可辨也。”旋见丈夫羊裘，而己则狐裘矣。众媪挥仗争击狐裘人，几毙。急逸入冬松室，亦有一貂裘丈夫，抱冬松饮醇醪，拈花为枚。见佳入，语亦同前，佳曰：“吾衣狐裘，可辨也。”旋见丈夫狐裘，己则貂裘，众婢持帚争扑貂裘人。逸入夏云房，亦有一狼裘丈夫，搂夏云抚案看春册，较量笔墨工细，闻外间哄闹，故作惊讶，告云曰：“卿勿怖，此五通也，闻渠善变，无毫厘爽，先与卿约，吾衣狼裘，若见衣貂裘者，为五通，可诱之入，利剑断其首，卿能助我乎？”曰：“诺。”果就床头拔剑授彼。佳大惊。

    Translation:
    佳呼曰：“五娘莫信他，貌虽同，吾衣羊裘，可辨也。”旋见丈夫羊裘，而己则狐裘矣。众媪挥仗争击狐裘人，几毙。急逸入冬松室，亦有一貂裘丈夫，抱冬松饮醇醪，拈花为枚。见佳入，语亦同前，佳曰：“吾衣狐裘，可辨也。”旋见丈夫狐裘，己则貂裘，众婢持帚争扑貂裘人。逸入夏云房，亦有一狼裘丈夫，搂夏云抚案看春册，较量笔墨工细，闻外间哄闹，故作惊讶，告云曰：“卿勿怖，此五通也，闻渠善变，无毫厘爽，先与卿约，吾衣狼裘，若见衣貂裘者，为五通，可诱之入，利剑断其首，卿能助我乎？”曰：“诺。”果就床头拔剑授彼。佳大惊。

    Translation:
    佳呼曰：“五娘莫信他，貌虽同，吾衣羊裘，可辨也。”旋见丈夫羊裘，而己则狐裘矣。众媪挥仗争击狐裘人，几毙。急逸入冬松室，亦有一貂裘丈夫，抱冬松饮醇醪，拈花为枚。见佳入，语亦同前，佳曰：“吾衣狐裘，可辨也。”旋见丈夫狐裘，己则貂裘，众婢持帚争扑貂裘人。逸入夏云房，亦有一狼裘丈夫，搂夏云抚案看春册，较量笔墨工细，闻外间哄闹，故作惊讶，告云曰：“卿勿怖，此五通也，闻渠善变，无毫厘爽，先与卿约，吾衣狼裘，若见衣貂裘者，为五通，可诱之入，利剑断其首，卿能助我乎？”曰：“诺。”果就床头拔剑授彼。佳大惊。

    215

    趋就秋月，则又一猞猁裘丈夫，拥秋月团炉爆栗，且啖且笑，见佳入，大笑曰：“子何人，敢入吾房闼耶？”既而自思，恍然告月曰：“想即杀吾元配之五通又来矣，吾实切齿不可忍。来大好，请君入瓮，就炮烙。”月果取床头猎网，欲罩佳首，佳呼曰：“秋娘何梦梦，吾衣貂裘，可辨也！”旋见丈夫貂裘，而己则猞猁裘，大惊欲遁，秋月招手，更狂奔。近春花卧所，闻磨刀霍霍，曰：“吾非君家大妇可比，不能任五通污，渠若来，吾手刃之！”瞰床上，正卧一松鼠裘丈夫，对灯吹烟，笑曰：“莫怕他，吾曾挞渠，见渠喜衣猞猁裘，见即执之，可为证。

    Explanation

    215

    As she approached the autumn moon, she encountered a man wearing a lynx fur coat, huddled around a warm stove, popping chestnuts, eating and laughing. Seeing her enter, he laughed loudly and said, "Who are you, and how dare you enter my chamber?" After a moment, he thought to himself and suddenly spoke to the moon, "I suspect it is the Wutong spirit who killed my former wife, come again! I am truly filled with hatred and cannot bear it. Come, it is a good thing, let us see who can be trapped in a pot and subjected to the punishment of paolu." The moon indeed took a hunting net from beside the bed, intending to cover her head. She cried out, "Autumn Moon, what are you dreaming about? I am wearing a sable fur coat, you can tell!" Suddenly, she saw that the man was wearing a sable fur coat, while she herself was in a lynx fur coat. She was greatly startled and wanted to flee, but Autumn Moon beckoned to her, and she ran even faster. As she neared the place where Spring Flower was lying down, she heard the sound of a knife being sharpened and said, "I am not your first wife, and I will not allow the Wutong spirit to defile me. If he comes, I will kill him with my own hands!" Looking up, she saw a man wearing a squirrel fur coat lying on the bed, puffing on a pipe and laughing, "Don't be afraid of him. I once beat him, and when I saw that he liked wearing a lynx fur coat, I seized him, and that can serve as proof."

    216
