    ”佳愤极大呼曰：“春娘，卿床上妖也，吾万佳在此！”春花申申詈，曰：“万佳乃九品官，岂死魅所能冒假？”鼠裘丈夫亦笑曰：“冒假万佳，理不可解，汝不过贪淫，吾小星甚多，听自检择，何必混乱庐山真面目乎？”言已，春花掷刀出，几中肩。奔出，被卧厅事中，自顾己衣，仍为羊裘。明日，诣内窃听，均有私议，或曰：“莫出门，彼四家均为五通占，吾夫妇落得逍遥，免争夕。”或云：“自家儿夫，宁不能辨？而乃为五通占，彼四家何其愚！”或互詈曰：“妮子不知耻，何不视吾房中有真郎耶？”或互诫婢媪曰：“莫乱道，恐触犯彼四家五通神也。

    Explanation

    216

    Jia, in great anger, shouted loudly, "Chunniang, you are a demon on my bed; I, Wanjia, am here!" Chunhua, in a fit of rage, retorted, "Wanjia is a ninth-rank official; how could a dead ghost impersonate him?" The man in the rat fur coat also laughed and said, "Impersonating Wanjia is logically impossible. You are nothing but a greedy and lustful person. I have many small stars; you can choose one for yourself. Why confuse the true face of Mount Lu?" After he spoke, Chunhua threw the knife out, almost hitting his shoulder. She ran out and fell in the hall. Looking at her own clothes, she found she was still wearing a sheepskin coat. The next day, she went inside to secretly listen. Everyone had private discussions. Some said, "Don't go out; all four families are occupied by the Five Gods. My husband and I can live carefree, avoiding the night's disputes." Others said, "Can't you tell your own husband? Yet you are occupied by the Five Gods; how foolish are those four families!" Some cursed each other, saying, "This girl doesn't know shame; why don't you look at my room to see if there's a real husband?" Others warned the maids and servants, "Don't speak rashly, or you might offend the Five Gods of the four families."

    217

    ”冬松更伏丈夫身上笑骂曰：“何如？连亭亭都为神踞，还是老娘待汝情份不薄耶？”旋闻断云零雨之声，意态亵极。潜出仰卧，见一髫发垂肩小女子，艳绝，徘徊床下，手弄烟筒，笑曰：“与郎有缘，腆颜毛遂。”佳大呼，以烟筒击之，女顿杳。闻屏后有男子声骂曰：“万佳无礼！我辈昆仲皆五通，与尔妾嬉，未免尔鳏，故遣小妹来伴汝寂，反恶声向人，岂真倒插沙帽翅，便妄自尊大耶？”佳悚怕，叩首连称不敢，问：“君既为神，亦知明理乎？”曰：“唯禽兽不知理。

    Explanation

    217

    Winter Song lay on top of the man, teasing him with laughter and insults: "How about it? Even the elegant lady has been possessed by a deity, or is it that I have been kind to you?" Suddenly, they heard the sound of broken clouds and scattered rain, indicating an intimate encounter. Winter Song slipped out and lay on her back, seeing a young girl with braids hanging down to her shoulders, breathtakingly beautiful, walking back and forth near the bed, playing with a pipe, and smiling, saying, "Fate has brought us together, so I boldly approach you." The man shouted and hit the girl with the pipe, and she suddenly disappeared. Behind the screen, a man's voice shouted, "Wan Jia is impolite! We five brothers are all Five Tongs, and we have been intimate with your wife, leaving you alone. So we sent our younger sister to keep you company, but instead of speaking kindly to others, are you really acting arrogantly despite your lowly position?" The man was frightened, knelt down, and repeatedly said he would not be impolite. He asked, "Since you are a deity, do you also know right from wrong?" The man replied, "Only beasts do not know right from wrong."

    218

    ”曰：“是也，神既占娶吾妇雍氏矣，何又占吾诸妾？”神曰：“是何言欤？”既而大笑，曰：“子稳戴绿头巾，尚不知奸夫伊谁耶？前娶尊阃，乃假托吾辈者也，如不信，子试言，渠当日来作何状。”佳缕述挥刀掷金等语，神曰：“若何？渠真五通，能如是之畏子乎？我辈俱在，子刀亦在，姑试为之，较真赝也。”佳哀泣，自称死罪，诸祈成全。神曰：“嘻！万佳儿，若能供养我，既不汝仇，亦使子富。”佳敬诺，从此遂事神。

    Explanation

    218

    The spirit said, "Yes, I have already claimed your wife, Lady Yong. Why is it now claiming your other wives?" The spirit replied, "What are you saying?" It then laughed loudly and said, "You are steadily wearing the green headgear, and still don't know who the adulterer is? The previous marriage to my esteemed wife was actually someone pretending to be us. If you don't believe, tell me, what did he look like when he came?" Jia described the words about swinging the sword and throwing gold. The spirit said, "How so? Is he really the Wutong Spirit, and would he be so afraid of you? We are all here, your sword is also here. Let's try it, to see if it's real or fake." Jia wept in sorrow, claimed to be guilty of a capital offense, and pleaded for mercy. The spirit said, "Ah! My dear Jia, if you can nourish me, I will not hold a grudge against you, and I will make you wealthy." Jia solemnly agreed, and from then on, he began to serve the spirit.

    219

    一夕，梦妻雍氏珠翠盈头，锦袍玉带，门外多护从，掩泪入帏，执佳手唏嘘，曰：“万郎，何可怜人也！妾所适之四郎，乃真五通，渠之兄若弟，早为郭孝子所斩，剩渠一身，不复为厉，然威权亦炽，管领东南一角，亿万游魂都监使，士卒如云。妾每怀故剑，渠亦惨然。今闻诸妾所占得，乃假五通。言于四郎，愿效绵薄，明日领兵至，代郎驱除，乞暂避其锋，毋干犯也。”万欲拉与叙旧，雍羞拒起辞，黄梁遽醒。谨志所言。次夕，潜伏东邻，果闻宅内戈戟交鸣声，四郎叱咤声，五妾环泣声，媪婢奔窜声。又闻四郎誓师曰：“如此假五通，横行若是，若能尽缚以献者，膺上赏！”众卒邀应战斗声。

    Explanation

    219

    One night, Wan dreamed that his wife, Yong, had jewels and jade ornaments adorning her head, wore a brocade robe and jade belt, and was accompanied by many attendants outside the door. She covered her face with tears as she entered the bedchamber, holding his hand and sighing, saying, "Wan, what a pitiful person you are! My husband, the fourth lord, is a true Wu Tong. His brothers were early on slain by Guo Xiaosi, leaving him alone, no longer causing harm, but his power and authority are still strong. He rules over a corner of the southeast, a supervisor of countless wandering spirits, with soldiers like clouds. I often miss my old sword, and he too looks sad. Now I hear that you have obtained a false Wu Tong. I told the fourth lord, willing to contribute my humble efforts. He will lead troops here tomorrow to drive away on your behalf. Please temporarily avoid his sharp edge and do not provoke him." Wan wanted to pull her and talk about old times, but Yong shyly refused and left, and he suddenly woke up from the dream. He carefully recorded what was said. The next night, he hid in the eastern neighbor's house and indeed heard the sound of swords and spears clashing inside the house, the fourth lord's shouts of command, the cries of the five wives surrounding, and the sounds of old women and maids running around. He also heard the fourth lord proclaim, "Such a false Wu Tong, acting so arrogantly, if anyone can capture and present them, they will receive a great reward!" The soldiers eagerly responded with battle cries.

    220

    移时，四郎大呼曰：“遁其一矣，奈何？且归报夫人，再图侦捕。”旋闻空中吹螺击鼓，为四郎凯歌声。声顿寂。晨携仆入视，则妾婢皆昏迷，救苏，犹各哭其夫而不悟。视箧内黄白，则外虽扃，而内皆空空。阶下有黑白小花犬四，皆断头，血涔涔，想即假五通也。整顿门庭，医治五妾，渐次就痊。收集逋负，仅剩千金。深惧食齿浩繁，若何生活。既而自喜，曰：“我九品官也，当以官为乞食左券。”尽以千金贿赂长者，授实任某县典史。甫莅任，红黑帽鸣锣呵殿，四轿游长街。忽有黑气一团，呜呜自空中奔至，内有毛手如葵扇，遽搏其颈，詈曰：“吾兄弟辈，待汝不薄，为何引恶人来肆杀戮？吾诚假五通，然不汝恕矣！”言已，手批其颊，其声甚脆，佳大呼，死舆中，五美图遂四散。

    Explanation

    220

    After some time, Si Lang shouted, "One has escaped! What shall we do? I will go back and report to the lady, and we will plan another search and capture." Suddenly, they heard horns blowing and drums beating from the air, as if celebrating Si Lang's victory. The sound then abruptly fell silent. In the morning, Si Lang brought his servants to inspect the house, and they found that the concubines and maidservants were all unconscious. They revived them, but each was still crying for her husband, unaware of what had happened. When they looked inside the chests, they found that though they were locked from the outside, the contents were all empty. On the steps, there were four black and white spotted dogs, all with their heads cut off and blood dripping, which they suspected to be the false Five Ways God. Si Lang tidied up the household, treated the five concubines, and they gradually recovered. He collected the overdue debts and was left with only a thousand gold pieces. He was deeply worried about supporting so many mouths to feed, wondering how they would live. Then he consoled himself, saying, "I am a ninth-rank official, and I will use my position as a means of support." He bribed the elders with all the thousand gold pieces and was appointed as the actual magistrate of a certain county. As soon as he took office, he paraded down the long street in four palanquins, with red and black hats, drums, and banners. Suddenly, a black cloud of smoke rushed from the sky with a whistling sound, and inside it was a hand with fur like a sunflower fan, which immediately seized his neck and cursed, "My brothers and I have not been unkind to you, why did you bring evil people to commit slaughter? I am indeed a false Five Ways God, but I will not forgive you!" Saying this, he hit his cheek with his hand, the sound was very crisp, and Si Lang cried out, dying in the palanquin. The five beauties then scattered.

    221

    郝腾蛟
    　　
    　　床头夜叉啼，河东狮子吼，能令铁铮铮汉子丧胆寒心，恨无杜兰香重到人间耳。然每闻吾乡父老谈郝总兵事，未尝不须眉欲动。总兵登州武世家，姓郝名腾蛟，字春霆，少以武庠获解，事父母至孝。两臂能开铁胎弓，矢无虚发。拳勇得僧耳的绍。貌虽如处女，然路遇不平，辄拔刀睚眦之。家赤贫，不妄干人，或樵采山林，或猎捕飞走，供菽水；然里有窘急来告，则资助之。顾东砍西砍，如杨阿若。人虽德之，而恶其横，故年已廿五，无敢以女妻者。会礼闱较武有期，整装欲往，因贫，无仆御，无坐骑。自以雨盖被，弓矢刀戟负于背，以米麦菽粟蒸馍馍揣于裹，登堂拜别。父母诫之曰：“儿功名无庸计，所忧者性不纯，好预不干己事，辇毂下，重则弃首领，轻则窜穷边。”言已泣下。郝亦泣，言知悔。命解衣，亲为刺字于臂上，文曰：“能忠则名扬，能忍则气降。

    Explanation

    221

    Stirring at the mention of the valiant General Hao Tengjiao, who, despite his gentle appearance, was a force to be reckoned with. His family, hailing from the martial Dengzhou, was renowned for its military prowess. Named Hao Tengjiao, with the courtesy name Chunting, he was a young prodigy who excelled in the military academy and was filial to his parents. His strength was legendary; he could draw a heavy bow with ease, and every arrow he shot found its mark. He learned martial arts from a monk named Shou and, though he looked like a maiden, he was quick to draw his sword and right any wrongs he encountered. Despite his family's poverty, he lived a simple life, gathering firewood in the mountains or hunting small animals to provide for his family. Yet, when neighbors faced dire straits, he would lend a helping hand. His strength and skill were compared to the legendary Yang Aoruo, but his boldness often earned him both respect and resentment. By the age of twenty-five, no one dared to marry their daughters to him. When the time for the martial examination arrived, he prepared to go, despite his lack of resources. He used a rain cover as a blanket, carried his bow, arrows, and weapons on his back, and packed steamed buns with millet, wheat, beans, and rice in his bag. He went upstairs to bid farewell to his parents, who warned him, "Son, there is no need to worry about your career. What we are worried about is that your nature is not pure, and you like to meddle in things that do not concern you. In the capital, if you are caught, you might lose your head; if you are lucky, you might be exiled to the remote border." They wept, and Hao Tengjiao, moved to tears, promised to heed their advice. They ordered him to take off his clothes, and they personally tattooed a message on his arm: "If you are loyal, your name will be famous; if you can endure, your anger will subside."

    222

    好勤职，报君王。毋贾祸，累高堂。”刺已，以朱涅之，深入肌里。然后徒步行。逾武定，近德州界，山雨暴至，装负淋漓，瞰岭后有古精蓝，即奔入暂避，意待霁而后行。久之日暮，雨淅沥如故，遂决意止宿。视庙中断井颓垣，神鬼露处，惟中殿尚能避风雨。香案尘渍，杂蜡泪，抽刀铲削，以案作榻。遂对神再拜，祝而高坐，咽囊中干糇，倚装假寐。夜阑顿醒，视月色晶莹，两廊鬼判欲攫人，心亦不惧。甫再交睫，闻声，睨之，一二八好女子，自龛后徐步出，妆虽不整，貌惨淡，衣朴素，而五官位置极丽端。宛转下庭阶，迎月长叹，掬破瓮中雨水饮，有声。饮已，跪伏神座下，喃喃不能辨。郝毛发森竖，意为鬼魅无疑。抽刀跃起，大呼曰：“死魔敢尔！”女子应声倒，近身详视，有形质，以手抚，肌尚温，大诧曰：“人耶鬼耶？幸未孟浪！”遂殷殷唤苏醒，缓缓询踪迹。

    222

    Loyal to one's duties and serving the king, one should avoid seeking disaster and burdening one's ancestors. After the tattooing, the design was dyed with red ink, deeply penetrating the flesh. Then, he walked on foot. He crossed Wu Ding and approached the border of Dezhou. Suddenly, a mountain rainstorm arrived, soaking his clothes and belongings. Looking behind the mountain, he saw an ancient temple and rushed in for temporary shelter, intending to wait for the rain to stop before continuing. After a long time, as the sun set, the rain continued to fall, so he decided to stop for the night. He saw broken wells and collapsed walls in the temple, with gods and ghosts exposed, but the main hall could still shelter from wind and rain. The incense table was dusty and mixed with wax tears. He drew his knife to scrape it clean and used the table as a bed. He bowed twice to the gods, prayed, and sat high. He ate the dry food in his bag and leaned on his luggage, pretending to sleep. As the night deepened, he suddenly woke up and saw the clear and bright moonlight. The ghosts in the two corridors seemed to grab people, but he was not afraid. Just as his eyelids blinked again, he heard a soft sound. He glanced and saw one or two beautiful women slowly stepping out from behind the altar. Their makeup was not neat, their appearance was sad, their clothes were simple, but their facial features were very beautiful. Gracefully descending the steps, they faced the moon and sighed deeply. They drank rainwater from a broken jar, making a sound. After drinking, they knelt and bowed before the god's seat, murmuring incoherently. Hao's hair stood on end, and he thought they were definitely ghosts. He drew his knife and leaped up, shouting loudly, "Dare you, demon of death!" The women fell over in response. He approached and examined them closely, finding they had a form, touched them, and found their skin was still warm. He was greatly surprised and said, "Are you human or ghost? Fortunately, I did not act rashly!" Then, he earnestly called them awake and slowly asked about their origins.

    223

    女子泣诉曰：“妾海州人，小字红红。亲卒，为恶叔诱卖勾栏中，誓死不接客，鸨母痛楚之。蒙东村李秀才名郝字伯调者，怜而买为妾，希育子，甚相爱。讵家室悍妒，动即詈挞。昨侍早妆，失手碎玉搔头，遽呼烙铁，妾怖而急遁，潜伏于此，已三日不食。顷口燥，就瓮饮，实不意惊大王，乞赐剑下死，妾目瞑，胜于朝秦暮楚，亦绝不居地下，唱《比红儿》诗也。”郝大笑曰：“我以子为妖，子以我为盗，两误矣。我非绿林豪，乃武孝廉，子勿怖。”分干糇与之食。霎时天向晨，详问夫家远迩，曰：“二十里。”曰：“送子归去来。”女泣，似不愿行。曰：“痴矣，无论寒饿煞，即有匪徒至，能保贞以生乎？吾送子回，当认我为姨父，我能代子区处。”女始随之行。

    Explanation

    223

    A woman wept and said, "I am from Haizhou, and my childhood nickname is Hong Hong. After my parents passed away, a wicked uncle lured me into a brothel and sold me there. I vowed never to entertain customers, and the madam was deeply grieved by this. A scholar from East Village named Li Hao, courtesy name Bo Diao, pitied me and bought me as his concubine, hoping to have children with me. We loved each other greatly. However, his wife was jealous and fierce, scolding and beating me at the slightest provocation. Yesterday, while assisting her with her morning makeup, I accidentally broke her jade hairpin, and she immediately called for a branding iron. I was terrified and fled hastily, hiding here for three days without food. Recently, I became thirsty and drank from a jar, not realizing that I had startled the king. I beg for a sword to end my life, for I would rather close my eyes than live a fickle life, nor do I wish to remain in the underworld to sing the 'Bi Hong'er' poem." Hao laughed and said, "I thought you were a demon, and you thought I was a thief. Both of us were mistaken. I am not a bandit, but a scholar of martial virtue named Li Hao. Do not be afraid." He shared some dry food with her to eat. As dawn approached, he asked in detail about the distance to her husband's family, and she replied, "Twenty miles." He said, "I will send you back home." The woman wept, seeming reluctant to go. He said, "How foolish you are! No matter how cold and hungry you are, if bandits were to come, could you preserve your chastity and live? I will send you back, and you should recognize me as your uncle. I can help you arrange things." The woman then followed him.

    224

    纤足步泥淖，颇滑挞，郝曰：“子速行，若欲步步生莲花，不误乃公事耶？”女泣言其惫，郝自思男女有别，挽之负之均不可，乃铺巨袱于地，令女蜷卧于上，裹之如襁褓，左负装而右挈女，提携如幼孩，令女口授路径，行如飞。顷刻至村中，犹家家闭户寝。女出指门，叩之三四声，一村佣出，睨女立郝后，遽反奔。

    Explanation

    224

    A woman with delicate feet walked through the muddy, slippery ground. Hao said, "You should hurry, if you want to walk gracefully like lotus flowers blooming with each step, don't neglect your important duties?" The woman cried and said she was exhausted. Hao thought about the propriety between men and women and realized that neither pulling nor carrying her was appropriate. So, he spread a large cloth on the ground, had the woman curl up and lie down on it, and wrapped her as if she were a baby. He carried her on his right side while holding his luggage on his left, lifting her as if she were a child, and had her tell him the path orally. They moved like the wind. In a moment, they arrived at the village, where everyone was still asleep and the houses were closed. The woman pointed to a door, and after knocking a few times, a villager came out, looked at the woman standing behind Hao, and ran away quickly.

    225

    闻宅内妇人声，音破裂如鸱啼，云：“吾道小妮子不正经，果偕破野头来，反图赖一顶绿头巾，诰赠汝矣，尚以老娘为盲耶？来即执之，烤且烙，方泄吾恨！若左袒，宁先见杀！”旋闻喧嚷索杖声，开户索履声。一男子披衣奔出，瞠目视，低告曰：“此即藁砧也。”郝颔之，略拱手，即放步入厅事高坐，命女与生旁坐，凝视再四，遽问曰：“男子，汝即李秀才乎？”曰：“然。”曰：“此即汝之小妻乎？”曰：“然。”曰：“后宅汹汹者，汝之大妇乎？”乃战兢羞愧不敢答。郝即捧腹大笑，声琅琅震屋瓦。村人无老少男妇，或立或坐，济济满堂。忽一蓬头妪走出唤女，郝止之；旋有赤脚婢唤且挽，郝更止之。

    Explanation

    225

    I heard a woman's voice inside the house, sharp and piercing like the cry of a bird. She said, "I say this young woman is not behaving properly. If she really came with a vulgar man, she is trying to make you wear a green hat, a symbol of infidelity. I will have you officially disgraced. Do you still think I am blind? Come, I will capture her and punish her severely, only then will I vent my anger! If you side with her, I would rather die first." Soon after, I heard the sounds of people searching for tools and opening doors. A man rushed out in a state of shock, staring in disbelief. He whispered, "This is your husband." The woman nodded, made a slight bow, and then sat down in a position of authority, ordering the woman and the man to sit beside her. She stared at them repeatedly and asked, "Are you Mr. Li, the scholar?" "Yes," he replied. "Is this your concubine?" "Yes," he said again. "Is the one causing a commotion in the back of the house your main wife?" The man was too frightened and ashamed to answer. The woman then burst into hearty laughter, her voice echoing through the house. The village people, regardless of age or gender, filled the room, standing or sitting. Suddenly, an old woman rushed out, calling for the woman, but the woman stopped her. Soon after, a barefoot maid called out and tried to pull her, but the woman stopped her as well.

    226

    突一杵飞出，中女臂；又一砖来，中郝肩，如不觉。妇怒吼而奔出，面靛色，目裂，发蓬，出即挝女，又其夫，语且侵客，郝仍如不觉。妇吼怒詈骂声，女涕泣求饶声，生咨嗟太息声，郝捧腹大笑声，众纷乱排解声，声大震，如鼎沸，以致邻村父老，与妇之兄弟，咸奔至，劝妇诣内，无贻外人笑。郝大声喝止之，如晴空霹雳骤下，众声遂寂。少顷，与众问询，自道姓氏来历，述邂逅遇女之由。言毕飕然刀出，晶莹插几上，厉声曰：“吾试期近，不能久留。

    Explanation

    226

    A stick suddenly flew out and hit the girl's arm; then another brick came flying and struck 郝's shoulder, but he seemed unaware. The woman, furious, shouted and rushed out, her face blue with rage, her eyes wide open, her hair disheveled. She immediately slapped the girl and then her husband, also insulting the guest. Yet 郝 remained as if he had not noticed. The woman's shouting, cursing, and scolding filled the air, along with the girl's weeping and pleading for mercy, the sound of someone sighing deeply, and 郝's hearty laughter. The crowd was in chaos, trying to intervene, and the noise was so loud, it was like a pot boiling over. This caused the elders from neighboring villages and the woman's brothers to rush over, advising the woman to go inside to avoid embarrassing others. 郝 loudly stopped them, like a sudden clap of thunder on a clear day, and the crowd fell silent. After a short while, he asked around, introduced himself, and explained how he met the girl. After finishing his explanation, he swiftly drew a knife, which gleamed as it was placed on the table. He said sternly, "My exam is coming up soon, so I can't stay long."

    227

    且为天地间除害去！”遽挈妇跪厅事，伸掌上下拍击，每一击则数责曰：“秀才家不过爱面孔，不与汝较，汝竟猖狂耶？红红亦好人家女，何罪过，挞且烙耶？人我一样皮肤，挞烙汝，痛否？汝不能生子，秀才始娶妾；妾有子，即汝子，定欲置之死，视香火断，宗祧斩耶？四德无，七出备，尚装憨耶？红红背后语不足信，当面乔行，迳能抵赖耶？红为吾姨侄女，今始寻觅着，当送官笞汝，岂一拍了事耶！尔夫尔邻尔兄弟均在此，敢以我为如何？若左袒，当尽杀却，况汝耶？”始拍，妇怒詈，再则呼救，再则号哭求恕，再则如豕就斩，长号而已。

    Explanation

    227

    "Rid the world of harm!" He suddenly took the woman and made her kneel in the hall, stretching out his hands and striking her up and down. Every time he struck, he would scold several times, saying, "Scholars' families only care about appearances. I won't argue with you, but you're being outrageous. Honghong is also a good family's daughter, what crime has she committed, why beat and brand her? Our skin is the same, would it not hurt you if I beat and brand you? Since you can't have children, the scholar started taking a concubine. If the concubine has a child, it is your child, but you want to kill it, do you want to break the ancestral rites and cut off the family line? You lack the four virtues, you meet all the seven grounds for divorce, yet you still pretend to be innocent. Honghong's words behind your back are not to be trusted, but her behavior in front of you is pretentious, can she deny it? Hong is my aunt's niece, and I have just found her. I will send you to the authorities for punishment, how can one slap settle it? Your husband, neighbors, and brothers are all here, dare you treat me like this? If you side with them, I will kill them all, let alone you?" At the first strike, the woman angrily cursed; at the second, she cried for help; at the third, she wept and begged for mercy; at the fourth, she acted like a pig being slaughtered, and longed only to wail.

    228

    郝正拍且数，忽风吹衣露臂，一童子呼曰：“个位官人，臂有朱文字！”郝蓦忆父母诫，遂止。众见其凛凛如天神，不敢劝阻，至是始请入座，求曲恕。先是女逋后，访无耗。李虽心怜，无如何。顷闻郝言，竟信而惮之深，遂长跪请罪，众亦罗拜代缓颊，郝曰：“尔曹欲罢休甚易，曷书一保状，署押与我。我会试回，验红红，若顶少一茎发，身有一伤痕，即与汝曹决死战！”众曰：“诺。”遂折券书与之。留午餐，赠赆金，不受，唯立饮一巨觥，收刀负装怀券去。入都成进士，官侍卫，司豹尾神枪，随跸木兰出征，歼寇有功，膺心简，迎养父母入都。计五载，恩授寿春镇，先回籍，始莅任。

    Explanation

    228

    As Hao Zheng was counting, suddenly the wind blew his clothes, exposing his arm. A child called out, "Your Excellency, there are red words on your arm!" Hao Zheng suddenly remembered his parents' warnings and stopped. The crowd saw him as if he were a heavenly deity, too afraid to dissuade him, and only then did they invite him to sit and asked for leniency. Before this, a woman had fled, and her whereabouts were unknown. Li, though sympathetic, could do nothing. Upon hearing Hao Zheng's words, they believed him deeply and were greatly afraid, so they knelt long and asked for forgiveness. The crowd also knelt in a circle to plead on his behalf. Hao Zheng said, "It is very easy for you to stop, why not write a guarantee, sign it, and give it to me. When I return from the imperial examination, I will verify the condition of Red Red. If her head has even one strand of hair missing or if her body has any wound, I will fight to the death with you!" The crowd said, "Yes." So they wrote it down and gave it to him. They invited him to stay for lunch, offered him a gift, but he refused, only standing and drinking a large cup, then collected his knife, packed his belongings, and left with the bond. He entered the capital, passed the imperial examination, became an imperial guard, was in charge of the ceremonial spear, accompanied the emperor on a military campaign, killed the enemy with merit, received imperial favor, welcomed his parents to the capital. After five years, he was granted the position of governor of Shouchun, returned to his hometown first, and then assumed his post.

    229

    重过此境，路旁店，父母偶语曰：“儿气平，果贵显，当年刺臂上字，不以为痛耶？”郝蓦忆前事，悚惧直陈，且言知悔。父母骇诧。即命材官往探。须臾，鼓乐壶觞，李生吉服负女，红红绣裳负儿，均顶香跪门外。询之，盖郝去后，大妇羞愤病卒，红红扶正，一胎生子女各一，已呀呀学语矣。坚请恩公重到山庄，笑遣之，挽益坚，村之父老，亦环叩曰：“相公锦衣回里，况又南极双辉，当为山野祓除不祥。”至则居以燠室，享以珍馐，优伶演剧，为二老寿。侍从皆厚劳。村人争宴请。生与女，晨夕问起居，如见父母礼。因述红红，并无葭莩戚，当日诡言以恐之耳。然夫妇事之，礼益恭，堂上绘郝小像，神毕肖，虔祀之，已五载矣。村中有罗氏女，貌妍丽，生而识字通文，唯两手握固不开，衣食须婢，乡人聚妇同作苦，以致年三十，无与议婚者。素与红红善，来谒太夫人，叩膝下，经红代述其异。

    Explanation

    229

    Revisiting this place, at a roadside inn, Hao's parents happened to speak: "Your temper has calmed, and indeed you have achieved a high position. When you had the character tattooed on your arm back then, did you not feel the pain?" Hao suddenly recalled his past deeds, spoke out in fear and trembling, and confessed that he knew regret. His parents were astonished. They immediately ordered officials to go and investigate. Soon after, there was music, drums, and cups of wine. Li, dressed in festive attire, carried his daughter, while Hong Hong, in embroidered garments, carried their son, both kneeling with incense outside the door. Upon inquiry, it was revealed that after Hao left, his first wife had died of shame and anger, and Hong Hong had taken over, giving birth to a son and a daughter, who were already babbling. They firmly requested the benevolent sir to return to the mountain villa, gently dismissed them with a smile, and pulled even more strongly, the village elders also encircled and pleaded: "The young master returns home in splendid attire, and moreover, with the double brilliance of the South Pole, it should be to cleanse the countryside of misfortune." Upon his arrival, they provided him with a warm room, served him with delicacies, and had actors perform plays to celebrate the longevity of the two elders. The attendants were all well rewarded. The villagers vied to invite him for a feast. Li and his daughter inquired about his health every morning and evening, treating him with the respect due to parents. They then recounted Hong Hong's story, noting that she had no close kinship and had spoken falsely to frighten them. However, the couple treated her with even greater respect, painting a small portrait of Hao on the hall, which resembled him perfectly, and worshipped it with sincerity, having done so for five years. In the village, there was a Luo's daughter, beautiful and graceful, who was born with the ability to read and write, but her hands were clenched and would not open, requiring a servant for her daily needs. The villagers gathered women to work together, leading to her being thirty years old without a suitor. She had always been close to Hong Hong, came to pay respects to the great lady, and knelt at her knees, her story recounted by Hong Hong.

    230

    太夫人不信，视之，戏擘其右腕，遽开，擘左亦然，中有两玉如棋子，一文云：“罗氏女名娇娆，”一云：“年三十嫁腾蛟。”太夫人惊喜曰：“此天数也。”即聘为儿妇，借李宅成嘉礼而后去。郝武人，字仅辨之无。镇寿春时，案牍扎符，皆夫人标判。尝于红烛下草露布，公侍侧，无惰容。夫人虽为公置姬妾，不妒忌。然每因公事争论，辄捋公须，使戎服跪谢而后已。官十年，父母卒，回籍；服阕再起，多政声，皆出自内助。每叹谓僚佐曰：“我今而后，方知阃威之可畏也。”立命材官携金，诣李生家，为前妇建醮求忏悔。夫人生子二，妾生子三女一，子皆贵，多与红红所生子同科，世世联婚姻，若朱陈焉。懊侬氏曰：佛家有伏狮罗汉，将军岂其化身欤？不然，何毙之速也！迨至虎帐谈兵，蛾眉秉笔，一言不合，辄捋其须而屈其膝，将军身蹈李生覆辙，得毋哑然而大笑乎？所以孔雀择林，必先顾其尾。

    Explanation

    230

    The Grandmother did not believe, but when she looked, she playfully split open her right wrist, and it opened suddenly; when she split open her left wrist, it was the same. Inside were two jade pieces like chess pieces, one inscribed with "Lady Jiao Ráo of the Lu Family," and the other with "To be married to Teng Jiáo at age thirty." The Grandmother was surprised and said, "This is fated by Heaven." She immediately arranged for her to be married as her daughter-in-law, using Li's house to complete the ceremony before departing.

    Hao, a martial man, whose name is unclear, was governing Shouchun at the time. All official documents and seals were signed by his wife. She once wrote a document under the red candlelight while the general stood beside her, and she showed no signs of laziness. Although she arranged concubines for the general, she did not show jealousy. However, whenever they argued over official matters, she would pull on the general's beard, making him kneel in military attire and apologize until he submitted. After serving for ten years, his parents passed away, and he returned to his hometown; after mourning, he was reinstated and gained a good reputation, all due to his wife's support. He often said to his officials, "From now on, I understand the fear of a woman's power in the inner quarters." He immediately ordered an official to carry gold to Li's house to perform a ritual for the former wife, seeking atonement. His wife had two sons, and the concubine had three sons and one daughter; all the sons were honored, many of them being classmates with those born to Red Red, and they formed a lifelong alliance of marriage, like the families of Zhu and Chen. Annoyed, the author said: In Buddhism, there is a Vajra Bodhisattva who subdues lions; could the general be an incarnation of him? Otherwise, why did he die so quickly? When it came to discussing military affairs in the tent, with a woman holding the pen, if there was even a slight disagreement, she would pull on his beard and make him kneel, causing the general to fall into the same trap as Li Sheng, would he not laugh silently? Therefore, a peacock chooses a forest, but must first look at its tail.

    231

    夜雨秋灯录　二集
    　　
    　　清　宣鼎
    　　
    　　卷一
    　　
    　　卓二娘
    　　
    　　彭泽孝廉宋景玉，字东墙，好狭斜游。家富有，日掳金钱为锦缠头。少娶吴氏，貌美，结缡二三月，颇静好；旋就荡妇宿，稍讽之，即拂袖起，誓以黄泉始相见。吴郁郁忧愤死。虽归为营斋奠，而心终不怿。契友某，疑且不平，曰：“夫人貌端好，较章台柳色高百倍，是何心性，舍珍珠而嗜疮痂？宁割席，求明示。”生曰：“仆亦不自省，无论鸠盘荼，一入勾栏，即西子南威也。近即作文，必于彼处，始得成杰构，否则枯肠而已。食非娼不甘，寝非娼不熟，虽刀锯鼎镬，不能易其性也。”友闻之，叹曰：“今而后，敬闻命矣！”出而宜于众，戒桑梓，无与婚。生内顾乏中馈，急谋胶续，媒妁去不回。大怒，以千金买艳妾，初亦静好，旋亦宠衰，月余，故态复萌。劝之，生怒曰：“贱人敢尔！”始詈继挞，妾亦死。

    Explanation

    231

    Yèyǔ Qiūdēng Lù: Second Collection

    Qīng Dynasty, Xuān Dǐng

    Volume One

    Zhuo Erniang

    Song Jingyu, a xiào lián from Pengze, known as Dōngqiáng, enjoyed the company of courtesans. His family was wealthy, and he spent money daily on luxurious gifts for them. He married Wu, a beautiful woman, but after two or three months of marriage, she began sleeping with courtesans. When he gently admonished her, she would angrily leave, vowing not to see him again until they met in the afterlife. Wu died from deep sorrow and anger. Although he held a memorial service for her, he remained unhappy. A close friend, who was suspicious and dissatisfied, said, "Your wife was beautiful, far surpassing the courtesans of Chang'an by a hundredfold. What kind of heart does she have, to discard pearls and prefer scabs? Rather than sit on the same mat, I seek a clear explanation." Song replied, "I do not understand myself either. Regardless of whether it is a courtesan or a prostitute, once I enter the brothel, she becomes like Xi Zi or Nan Wei (famous beauties). Recently, when I write, I must go there to produce something excellent; otherwise, it is just empty writing. I cannot enjoy food unless it is served by a courtesan, nor can I sleep well unless I sleep with one. Even if faced with the most severe punishments, I cannot change my nature." The friend sighed and said, "From now on, I will follow your lead!" He then advised others, warning his hometown people not to marry him. Song worried about lacking a wife to manage the household and hurriedly sought a new marriage. The matchmakers left without returning. He became furious and bought a beautiful concubine for a thousand gold pieces. Initially, she was also peaceful and good, but soon her favor waned. After a month, she returned to her old ways. When he tried to advise her, he became angry and said, "How dare you!" He began to scold and beat her, and she also died.

    232

    里中相诫，曰：“生女宁作娼，不嫁宋东墙。”生闻亦愧悔，然顾空闱，睹遗，更觉岑寂，益宿青楼。不两载，业已去其十之三。里有谢氏卓二娘，新寡，貌仅中人，体复羸弱，愿嫁生，遣媒示意。生不耐鳏，急允诺，聘娶。人恒为二娘危，而二娘顾自若也。入门操作，如贫家妇，绝口不问前番事，夫即归晏，惟问安否。即枕上情浓时，不问意中人若何。生反愧恧曰：“仆有奇癖，是天下女子所最恶者，卿审之乎？”二娘故诧曰：“男女体殊而性一，床第琐屑，均同乐，未知何事而好恶之悬殊？请明告妾也。”生太息曰：“风月膏盲，烟花痼疾耳。”二娘抚掌曰：“幸哉，醮也。妾前夫日坐愁叹，见粉头，面即，妾时劝驾，不许，反得痨疾死。今得后夫若是，妾愿足矣。”言已，袖与金帛，逼令往。生由是益放纵。偶晤马媪，问曰：“姥终日如穿花蝶，如另有奇卉，乞导引一豁眼界，当酬以巨金。

    Explanation

    232

    In the village, people warned each other, saying, “It’s better to be a prostitute than to marry someone like Song Dongqiang.” Upon hearing this, the man felt ashamed and regretful, but looking at the empty room and seeing the remnants, he felt even more desolate and increasingly frequented brothels. Within two years, he had already spent three-tenths of his wealth. In the village, there was a woman named Zuo Erniang from the Xie family, who was newly widowed. Her appearance was only average, and her body was weak. She was willing to marry the man, so she sent a matchmaker to express her willingness. The man could not bear being alone and quickly agreed, marrying her. People always worried about Erniang, but she remained calm. Upon entering the household, she worked diligently like a poor woman, never mentioning the past. When her husband returned late, she only asked if he was well. Even when in bed with him, she did not ask about his previous lover. The man felt ashamed and said, “I have a strange habit, which is the most hated by women in the world. Do you know about it?” Erniang was surprised and said, “Men and women may be different in body, but their nature is the same. In the bedroom, everything is the same pleasure. I don’t know why there is such a difference in likes and dislikes. Please tell me plainly.” The man sighed and said, “I am addicted to courtesans and prostitutes.” Erniang clapped her hands and said, “How fortunate, to be remarried. My former husband sat and sighed every day. When he saw a prostitute, his face would light up. I tried to persuade him, but he refused, and he died of tuberculosis. Now that I have a husband like this, my wish is fulfilled.” After saying this, she took out gold and silk and urged him to go. The man thus became even more unrestrained. He happened to meet an old woman named Ma and asked, “Auntie, you spend your whole day like a butterfly among flowers. If you have any other rare flowers, please guide me to see them, and I will reward you with a large sum of money.”

    233

    ”媪曰：“郎又娶得床头生菩萨第三尊，不怕醋瓶倒耶？”生以二娘贤淑告媪，媪曰：“陶公祠畔，枣花门内，新来江南白妪，携小娇生四名，均钱树子。第一为西贾攫去，第四美尤冠，老身请为郎执鞭。”生喜，偕入门，见庭宇雅洁，笔床茶灶皆备，架上鹦鹉呼曰：“郎君来，姐姐烧好茶也！”牵珠帘，拉生入，诸婢含笑迓客。鸦髻绿裤摺，已觉可人。问媪曰：“姆即前云大钱神耶？”曰：“然。”曰：“恐吾家四官见之，当为相思死。”询四官，曰：“已为王天官公子携去看花，夕方回。”引入小阁，云即四官香巢。壁上诗笺，极奈莺燕，床头舄，奁畔粉，镜边钗，已见一斑。少顷，阿二阿三来，貌俱纤宛，亦极奉承。生神夺，呼曰：“仆当老于是乡。”媪急摄以足，耳语生曰：“阿四尤巨擘，毋遽示馋眸，为若辈小觑！”旋进珍馐，穷治水陆，笙歌鼎沸，各献所长。

    Explanation

    233

    The old woman said, "You have married another virtuous and beautiful wife, the third one by your side, and you're not afraid of jealousy, are you?" The young man told the old woman about the second wife's virtue and beauty. The old woman replied, "At the side of the Taoist temple, inside the gate with red flowers, a new old woman from Jiangnan has arrived, carrying four young and beautiful girls, all of whom are like money trees. The first one was taken away by a Western merchant, and the fourth one is the most beautiful. I will serve the young man as a servant." The young man was delighted and entered with her. He saw the courtyard and buildings were elegant and clean, with writing desks and tea stoves all prepared. A parrot on the shelf called out, "Young master has come, sister has prepared good tea!" She pulled the pearl curtain, led the young man in, and the maids greeted him with smiles. The girl had a crow-like bun and green pleated pants, which already made her endearing. The young man asked the old woman, "Is she the big money goddess mentioned before?" The old woman said, "Yes." The young man said, "If my four officials see her, they will die of love." When asked about the four officials, it was said that they were taken to see flowers by the son of the Wang Tian Guan and would return in the evening. She led him into a small pavilion, saying it was the nest of the four officials. Poems on the wall, very much like swallows, shoes by the bed, powder by the chest, and hairpins by the mirror, showed a glimpse of her elegance. After a while, A'er and A'san came, both were delicate and graceful, and also very attentive. The young man was so captivated, he called out, "I will grow old here." The old woman quickly grabbed his foot and whispered, "A'si is even more exceptional, do not show your greedy eyes, or you will be looked down upon by them." Soon, fine dishes were served, a variety of seafood and freshwater delicacies, music and singing filled the air, and everyone showed their best.

    234

    生乘间问芳名，二曰巧云，三曰倩云，曰四停云。二稳重，三风骚，时于席上传眉语。生虽迷惑，而意在得龙头，仅含笑小酬应。听更柝转，忽见灯火一丛，肩舆飞至，婢挽美人出，四也。醉眼腻涩，意态阑珊，扶入香帏，即拥被寝。生微睨之，果如媪言。席终不去，三曰：“郎若不畏河东吼，何妨屈玉趾。”媪笑曰：“大奇，他家娘子，能任郎君跳入云霄去。”生亦夸耀。三曰：“阿四沉醉，恐失礼，妾又陋，不足攀龙凤，奈何？”媪曰：“莫错过，郎与三官，真是一对鸳鸯偶。”三曰：“不若妾为四官权印务，俟解醒，郎兴问罪师，何如？”生恐拂其意，即携之所居室，偎之坐，见清雅缭曲，案列文房，曰：“卿女学士耶？能书否？”曰：“间颇涂鸦，实不成字。”问：“工吟否？”曰：“鼓儿词，未尝不成句也。”解衣入帷，意仅借红娘馋，讵一张旗鼓，觉另有一种奇趣，令人销魂，真生平所未经者。

    Explanation

    234

    The protagonist took advantage of a moment to ask for their names. The second woman replied, "Qiaoyun," the third, "Qianyun," and the fourth, "Tingyun." The second was steady and composed, the third flirtatious, and they often exchanged glances and whispered during the party. Though the protagonist was confused, his ambition to win the top position prevailed, and he merely smiled and made small talk. As the night watchman's gavel sounded, signaling the passage of time, he suddenly saw a cluster of lights. A sedan chair arrived swiftly, and a maid helped a beautiful woman out, who was the fourth. She had a drowsy, bleary-eyed appearance and showed signs of weariness. She was helped into a fragrant bedchamber and immediately covered with a quilt and went to sleep. The protagonist glanced at her, and she was exactly as the old woman had described. The party did not disperse, and the third woman said, "If you're not afraid of your wife's scolding, why not come over here?" The old woman laughed and said, "Very strange, another lady, able to let you soar to the heavens." The protagonist also boasted. The third woman said, "Afourth is drunk, and I'm afraid she might lose her composure. I'm also plain, not worthy of your company, what should we do?" The old woman said, "Don't miss this opportunity, you and the third official are truly a perfect match." The third woman said, "Why not let me take over for the fourth official temporarily? When she wakes up, you can play the role of a matchmaker, how about that?" The protagonist, fearing to offend her, immediately took her to her room and sat her down. He saw that the room was neat and elegant, with writing materials on the desk. He asked, "Are you a learned lady? Can you write?" She replied, "Occasionally scribble, but it's not really legible." He asked, "Are you good at composing poetry?" She said, "Never fails to make a verse with a folk song or ballad." She took off her clothes and entered the bedchamber, intending only to play the role of a matchmaker. But suddenly, a different flag was raised, and he felt a different kind of strange charm, making one's soul melt, truly something he'd never experienced before.

    235

    大惑，为之盟山海，三笑曰：“论郎表表，唯四官方称，碗许米汁，请留以灌四官。妾不过代庖人也。”生愈迷，极力缱绻。日三竿，犹未起，婢入唤醒，进以茶果。询四，则又为李侍御公子招去，幸意全注三，不复问鼎，亦不复言归。鸨母遣婢索夜合资，惟以片纸画押，谕仆归索；二娘如数与之，甚捷。一日得句粘壁曰：“魂被香笼魄粉薰，此中温暖更谁分。从今莫忆秦淮月，笑倚花前看白云。”偶携三出游归，见阿四坐案头，观其诗，大赞誉。搦笔擘笺，立和曰：“温台荀席异香薰，饱满恩情已十分。无怪阿三狂欲死，宋郎词藻艳于云。”生自窗隙潜窥，四觉，急团其稿。坚索，始与阅之。意更夺，目荧荧，似碍三。三窥其意，笑曰：“代庖人瓜期届也。”是夕，即送就四官寝，昵爱殊甚。然四美固冠，其骄慢贪得尤冠。生嬖昵既深，不复计阿堵，日遣仆索资甚急。

    235

    Great confusion, he swore an oath by the mountains and seas, and the third laughed and said, "The gentleman is outstanding, only the four officials can be called, a bowl of rice porridge, please leave it to pour into the officials. I am merely a substitute cook." The man became even more entranced, striving with great affection. The sun was high in the sky, yet he had not yet risen, the maid entered to wake him up, offering tea and fruit. Inquiring about the fourth, he was again invited away by the son of Li the Attendant. All the affection was focused on the third, no longer seeking the tripod, nor speaking of returning. The madam sent a maid to demand the night's fee, only signing a piece of paper, ordering the servant to return and collect; the second mistress gave him the full amount, doing so very quickly. One day, he wrote a poem and stuck it on the wall: "The soul is enveloped by incense, the spirit perfumed by powder, this warmth, who else can share? From now on, do not recall the moon over Qinhuai, laugh and lean against the flowers, watching the white clouds." Accidentally taking the third out for a walk and returning, he saw the fourth sitting at the desk, reading his poem, greatly praised. Grasping the pen and tearing open the paper, he immediately replied: "The warm seat of Xun is perfumed by a strange fragrance, the fullness of love and affection is already tenfold. No wonder the third is mad with desire, Song's words are more splendid than the clouds." The man peeped through the window secretly, the fourth noticed, hastily balled up the draft. Insisting on demanding, he finally allowed to read it. The intention was even more taken, eyes shining brightly, seeming to hinder the third. The third, seeing his intention, laughed and said, "The substitute cook's melon period has arrived." That night, he immediately sent to the fourth official's bed, affection was particularly intense. However, the fourth's beauty is certainly supreme, his arrogance and greed are even more outstanding. The man's affection was already deeply entrenched, no longer counting the money, daily sending the servant to demand funds urgently.

    236

    年余，三生一子一女，四无出，二潜与生私，亦生二子。计迷于此者，三年余，偶归，不常见卓，均云归宁。计良得。年余，索资渐以钗钏，又以衣履且以书画玩具来变质。又年余，索忽靳，因詈仆，仆蹀躞至再，携一册来，曰：“娘子传语，家中产已罄，孑然一身，实不能作娼饱郎欲。”问田宅，曰：“货去久矣。”生大惊，阅其册，细流支取年月，田宅售价，甚详，并云：“宝山已空，日乞食于尼寺。”急趋归，寻卓，则门户犹是，而主者已非。询之，以妻卓卖券示。问妻无耗，寻仆，忽不见。无计，再返白家，则搬运一空，玉人早散。居停遣仆洒扫，下逐客令。茕茕顾影，托足无区，赧而求依于亲族，不许。无已，寄古寺宿。久之，为乞于村郭，悬鹑百结，呼号两年。西风骤来，鸡皮皴裂。欲觅死无法，意不若为梁上君子，得则苟且生，犯则杖下毙，犹胜于自戕。

    Explanation

    236

    After more than a year, three children were born: one son and two daughters, but four others did not have offspring. Two of them secretly had a private affair, and they also gave birth to two sons. I had been entangled in this situation for more than three years. Occasionally returning home, I rarely saw my wife, Zuo, and everyone said she had gone back to her parents' house. I considered this a good decision. After another year, she gradually demanded jewelry, clothing, shoes, and even art and toys, which led to a deterioration in our relationship. After yet another year, she suddenly became stingy and cursed the servant. The servant returned twice with a book, saying, "Madam has sent word that the family's wealth is exhausted, and she is alone, unable to satisfy your desires." When asked about the family's property, the servant replied, "It has been sold long ago." I was shocked and read the book, which detailed the exact dates and prices of the property sales, and also mentioned, "The family's wealth is empty, and she now lives in a temple and begs for food." I rushed back home to find that the house was still the same, but the owner (Zuo) was no longer there. The new owner showed me a deed indicating that Zuo had sold the house. I asked about her whereabouts and the servant, but neither could be found. With no other options, I returned to the white family (likely a reference to a previous benefactor or place of residence), only to find everything moved out and the beautiful people (玉人) dispersed. The host sent the servant to clean and gave me an eviction notice. Feeling lonely and having nowhere to go, I sought help from relatives, but they refused. With no other choice, I stayed in an old temple. After a long time, I began to beg in the village, looking destitute and calling out for help for two years. The cold wind suddenly arrived, causing my skin to crack. I considered suicide but found no way, and instead decided that being a thief might be better, as it offered a chance to live or die by the law rather than taking my own life.

    237

    窘且决，夜潜越富家墙，惊仆起，蜂聚而攒殴之。主人出，即前之契友某也。谕勿殴，送公庭。生呼曰：“即速殴毙为快！”某曰：“曷书券自任贼，即释汝去。”不得已，书与之。某执券，仍加以缚，送至一处，扃斗室中，不加缧绁，而坚守甚严。日给两餐冷粥饭，夜藉湿草眠。久之，闻官长坐堂皇，呼己名。即有一役引伏阶下，堂上人呼生仰视，则为自家厅事，东西坐者皆亲族。卓二娘鲜衣艳服立庑下，白家三姬左右侍。大骇，首复俯，妻卓曰：“嘻！郎不肖，一致于此乎？诸长者均在，更有何言？妾当时若规谏，是直驱郎死，否则妾蹈前辙死，妾愚不至此。赁宅购三艳妇，引郎入八阵图，若真为销金窝，试问郎居四年，何绝不一睹鸨母与他客面？郎承祖父资已竭，且为丐与贼，笔迹在此，非可以口舌争。妾忍守孤枕，忍设丑局，始保脂膏；且督课耕织，更有盈余，与郎无涉。

    Explanation

    237

    In a desperate situation, he decided to act, and at night, he secretly crossed over the wall of a wealthy household. Startled, he fell and was immediately surrounded, pounced upon, and beaten. The master came out, and it was his former close friend. The master ordered them not to beat him and sent him to the public court. The man shouted, "Beat me to death quickly!" His friend said, "Why not write a bond acknowledging yourself as a thief, and I will release you." Reluctantly, he wrote the bond and gave it to the friend. The friend took the bond, still tied him up, and sent him to a place, locking him in a small room, not adding chains, but guarding him strictly. He was given two cold meals a day and slept on wet grass at night. After a long time, he heard the officials sitting in the hall, calling his name. A servant led him to kneel at the bottom of the stairs, and the people in the hall called him to look up, and it turned out to be his own hall, with relatives sitting on both sides. Madam Zhuo was standing under the eaves in brightly colored and finely dressed, with three of Bai's maids serving her on both sides. He was greatly startled, bowed his head again, and his wife Zhuo said, "Ah! My husband is unworthy, to be in such a state! All the elders are here, what more can be said? If I had advised him at the time, it would have driven him to death, or if I had followed the same path, I would have died. I am not so foolish. Renting a house and buying three beautiful women, leading my husband into the Eight Diagrams formation, if it were truly a place of gold consumption, why didn't he see the madam or other guests in four years? My husband has exhausted the resources inherited from his ancestors, and he has become a beggar and a thief, the handwriting is here, it cannot be argued with words. I endured loneliness, endured setting up a shameful scheme, to protect the wealth; and supervised farming and weaving, with more surplus, which has nothing to do with my husband."

    238

    倘改悔，请仍归主人翁；姬俱在，妾亦不争夕。但手不许攫一文钱，足不容逾一重阈，坐守安享，以尽余年。若不遵，请郎自便。妾有子，亦可守门楣，不须藁砧也。长者均在，郎曷早为计？”生涕泣誓天日，愿如二娘言，众赞叹，玉成始去。卓为生盥沐，更新衣，羁内室，日周旋于妻妾间。视屋宇更华，阡陌更广，三子就傅，已将能文，皆二娘经营也。始审媪之勾，姬之吟，富家之获，皆二娘安排也。由是改过迁善，目睹子成名，毕婚嫁，寿八十，犹抱孙，不敢出门户。懊侬氏曰：孟光举青玉案，仅守现成之局；文君作《白头吟》，徒兴怨怼之辞。卓哉二娘，以不谏为谏，以不防为防，使易巾帼而须眉，必能委曲求全，善处家庭骨肉间也。然而甘居再醮，甘随荡子，岂以良人早殁，而不能展其所长欤？抑另有宿缘，而莫能守其故辙欤？士君子生有奇才，而急于自荐者，皆二娘之流也。噫嘻，悲哉！

    Explanation

    238

    If one repents, please return to the original master; the concubines are all present, and I will not vie for the night. But the hands are not allowed to seize even a single coin, and the feet are not allowed to cross even a single threshold. Sit and enjoy quietly, to live out the remaining years. If one does not obey, please let the master do as he pleases. I have a son who can also guard the door, and there is no need for a husband. The elders are all present; why not make plans early, master?" The man wept and swore by the heavens and the sun, vowing to follow the Second Lady's words. All praised her, and the matter was completed before she departed. The Second Lady had the man wash his hands and bathe, change into new clothes, and confine him to the inner chamber, where he spent each day among the wives and concubines. He saw the buildings more splendid and the fields and paths wider. The three sons were attending school and were already able to write, all arranged by the Second Lady. Only then did one realize the schemes of the old woman, the songs of the concubines, and the gains of the wealthy family, all arranged by the Second Lady. Therefore, one changed for the better and improved, witnessing the sons become famous and complete their marriages, living to eighty and still having grandchildren, dare not leaving the home. Ao Nong said: "Ban Jieyu raised the jade case, merely adhering to the existing situation; Zhuo Wenjun wrote the 'White Hair Song,' only arousing resentment. Outstanding indeed is the Second Lady, using not advising as a form of advice, using not guarding as a form of guarding. If she were to change from a woman to a man, she would certainly be able to compromise and handle family matters well. Yet she willingly remarried and willingly followed a wandering husband. Is it because her husband died early, and she could not demonstrate her abilities? Or is it because there was another predestined fate, and she could not follow the old path? Scholars and gentlemen who are born with extraordinary talents and are eager to recommend themselves are all like the Second Lady. Ah, how sad!"

    239

    丹青奇术
    　　
    　　皖人鲍打滚，画师也，能召亡写真。虽逝者逾十余载，鲍往墓上伏地一滚，瞑目久之，起则把笔勾勒，敷色渲染，举示其子孙戚属，无不惊为酷肖。人子思慕之极，不能重见容范，多乐与鲍游，以故倒屣争迎，声价颇重。一领皂色大布衫，敝犹衣之，盖滚久无从收拾也。然得资多供缠头费，尤嗜酒不羁，为君子所嫉。皖北有谢君，父早殁，其兄以微员仕都中。偶晤鲍，邀至家，嘱为其父传神。鲍如法写就，神颇似，惟颔下有八字红血迹，不可解。

    Explanation

    239

    The Extraordinary Art of Painting

    A man from Anhui named Bo Da Gun, a painter by profession, possessed the remarkable ability to summon the dead for portraits. Even if the deceased had passed away more than ten years ago, Bo would go to their grave, lie on the ground, and roll around once. After closing his eyes for a long time, he would rise, pick up his brush, and sketch the portrait, then color and shade it. He would show the completed work to the descendants and relatives of the deceased, and they were all amazed at how lifelike it was. People who missed their loved ones greatly and could not see them again often enjoyed spending time with Bo, and thus he was eagerly welcomed, his reputation and fees highly valued. He wore a dark-colored, coarse garment that was well-maintained despite its age, as he had long since stopped caring for his appearance. However, he often spent much of his earnings on drinking, living a wild and unrestrained life, which made him despised by the virtuous. In northern Anhui, there was a man named Xie Jun, whose father had died early. His brother, holding a minor official position in the capital, once met Bo and invited him home, asking him to paint a portrait of his father. Bo followed his usual method and completed the painting, which bore a striking resemblance to the deceased. However, there was an unusual red bloodstain on the chin, which remained unexplained.

    240

    询于母，盖其父曾因公收刑部狱，惧法，自以黑索扼吭死，故有此迹。睹其颠冠蓝顶，其父生固八品者，不可解。次日其兄书来，已为太翁覃恩加级，请三品封矣。兴化有陈孝子，名嘉谟，国初时，增广生员。其父某，与鹾商争海地，兴讼；商负，衔之。会其父往海滨，商嗾灶丁殴之，自踢其子死，告于官。往验，迳诬为其父踢杀。讼两年，商遍贿当道，遂以生父为把持盐务，殴杀人命，拟斩。生号诉诸大府，不直，欲叩阍，知秋决近，恐不及，祷于神，不应。

    Explanation

    240

    I inquired of my mother, and it is said that my father was once detained in the Ministry of Justice on official business. Fearing the law, he strangled himself with a black rope around his neck, hence this trace remains. To see him with a disheveled hat and a blue topknot, his father was originally an eighth-rank official, which is incomprehensible. The next day, my brother's letter arrived, stating that my grandfather had already been granted a promotion by imperial grace, and a third-rank official title was requested. In Xinghua, there is a filial son named Jiamo, who was an additional student at the beginning of the state. His father, whose name is not given, disputed with a salt merchant over coastal land and initiated a lawsuit. The merchant lost and harbored resentment. When his father went to the seaside, the merchant incited the salt workers to assault him, and he kicked his own son to death. He reported this to the authorities. They went to verify the case and falsely accused his father of kicking him to death. The lawsuit lasted two years, and the merchant bribed influential officials everywhere. They then accused his father of monopolizing salt affairs and killing someone, sentencing him to be beheaded. The son appealed to the higher authorities, but his case was not upheld. He wanted to appeal to the imperial court, knowing that the autumn execution was approaching and fearing it would be too late. He prayed to the gods, but they did not respond.

    241

    日夜仰天泣，目尽肿。闻巡按御史将至扬，急于神前，刺血写冤状二通，一藏于怀，一捧于手，油纸封固而标题之。文甚长，皆历诉商横吏贪，父抱黑盆等语。末有：“与其父死而儿亦死曷若儿先死，而父可或生”两语，尤为酸鼻。书成，公服立河畔。俟巡按官舫鸣钲鼓乐从上流下，两岸有司跪接，生乘其不备，突于人丛中跃出，大声呼冤。摊手中状于官舫，自投长河死。巡按悬赏募捞救，大索三日，不能得；捞出瓜州口，亦无耗。翌晨，巡按素服亲祭于浮桥口。

    Explanation

    241

    He wept to the sky day and night, his eyes swollen from crying. Hearing that the provincial inspector was about to arrive in Yangzhou, he urgently wrote two petitions of injustice in his own blood before a deity, one he hid in his bosom and the other he held in his hand, sealing them in oil paper and labeling them. The text was very long, detailing the corruption of officials and the incident of his father holding a black pot. At the end, there were two poignant lines: "Why not let the son die first, so that the father may perhaps survive?" These words were particularly heart-wrenching. After completing the petition, he stood on the riverbank in official attire. He waited for the official boat of the provincial inspector, with gongs and music, to descend from upstream, and officials on both banks kneeling to greet. Seizing the moment when they were not expecting it, he suddenly leaped out from the crowd, loudly calling out his冤 (injustice). He presented the petition in his hand to the official boat and then threw himself into the Yangtze River, ending his life. The provincial inspector offered a reward to search for and rescue him, and a thorough search was conducted for three days without success; even searching at Guazhou Mouth yielded no news. The next morning, the provincial inspector, dressed in mourning, personally offered a sacrifice at the Floating Bridge Mouth.

    242

    风大作，日色惨淡，众见水面竖一指出，盖尸犹直立逆流中。负出水，面如生，握拳透爪，切齿穿龈。置于岸，僵立不仆。巡按亲许代昭雪，始仆。阅怀中状词，琐而更哀。立刻坐堂皇，提人证，审讯刑求。商服，遂斩商，而出生父于狱。谕本籍邑宰，善视生父，厚殓生尸。然后奏劾上下承审官，请旌孝子，祀入乡贤，刊事迹入邑乘。诏许，邑人建祠于学宫西隅，春秋官祭私祭礼不衰。至道光某甲子，适鲍君来，邑人痛孝子无遗像，求写真。鲍以为事隔百年，难之。

    Explanation

    242

    A strong wind arose, the sun's color became dim, and the crowd saw a finger-like protrusion on the water. It was the corpse, still standing upright against the current. They carried it out of the water, and its face looked as if it were alive, with clenched fists, nails piercing the skin, and teeth biting through the gums. When placed on the shore, it stood rigidly without falling. Only when the provincial inspector personally promised to clear its name did it finally fall. Reading the petition in his hand, it was detailed and even more sorrowful. He immediately sat in the main hall, summoned witnesses, and interrogated under torture. The merchant confessed, so he was executed, and the victim's father was released from prison. He ordered the local magistrate to treat the victim's father well and to give the victim a proper burial. Then he reported to the imperial court, accusing the officials who had handled the case, and requested that the filial son be honored, included in the local list of virtuous people, and his story recorded in the local annals. The imperial decree approved, and the locals built a shrine in the west corner of the school, where official and private sacrifices continued without decline. By the year of a certain Jiazi in the Daoguang era, when Master Bao arrived, the locals lamented that there was no portrait of the filial son and requested a likeness. Master Bao thought that the matter was too distant in time and was difficult to achieve.

    243

    邑人请益坚，乃试往殡宫，滚五次，不可得。恐损己名，自剪爪发，刺血书疏文，杂符，焚于城隍神祠，跪拜禹步，久之，怀纸笔就神座下宿，嘱庙祝无窥探。漏三下，万籁寂，见龛灯顿缩，阶下若人影，往来甚伙。两廊各出一卒，一长如山魈，一短如僬侥，互揖出门去。少时，闻柝声鼓声，请钥开门声，四褐衣人来，伏阶下，白有词。即见案上设符剑印信，阶下多执戟横刀，若大府体。乐三奏，神金冠蟒服，呵殿出，升堂坐。貌古髯浓，鬓已斑白。

    Explanation

    243

    The townspeople asked even more insistently, so he tried to go to the mortuary, rolling five times but failing to achieve success. Fearing damage to his reputation, he cut his own nails and hair, wrote a memorial with blood, mixed in talismans, and burned it at the temple of the city god. He knelt and performed the ritual dance of Yu, and after a long time, he carried paper and pen to sleep at the foot of the deity's throne, instructing the temple attendant not to peek. At the third stroke of the hourglass, all sounds were silent, and he saw the lamp in the niche suddenly shrink. On the steps below, there were human shadows, many coming and going. From each corridor, one soldier emerged—a tall one like a mountain demon and a short one like a dwarf. They bowed to each other and left. Soon, the sound of the watchman's staff and drums were heard, followed by the sound of keys being asked for and doors being opened. Four people in dark clothes came, knelt at the steps, and reported words. Immediately, he saw the table set with talismans, swords, and seals, and below the steps, many held spears and swords, as if in the presence of a high official. Music was played three times, and the deity emerged with a golden crown and a robe of snakes, with attendants shouting. The deity ascended the hall and sat, with an ancient appearance, a thick beard, and gray hair.

    244

    判事毕，问褐衣人，曰：“孝子来何迟。”曰：“孝子现为崆峒山都总管，云程尚二千里耳。”须臾，鼓乐大震，列炬如火，城吏白：“孝子到！”神供偻出迎，礼甚恭，肃入，分东西坐。孝子冠服甚都，貌亦丰润。寒暄茗已，神敬白乡人意与画士疏。孝子颦蹙曰：“何必尔？”神曰：“乡梓情深，欲求音容，为后学榘，俾瞻仰耳。”一朱衣吏，请入西厢更衣，少时，复就坐，则衣公服，乌靴露顶，貌极清癯。少定，即更来时服，再拜兴辞。神皇皇送之登舆去，三揖而返。

    Explanation

    244

    After finishing the case, he asked the person dressed in coarse clothing, "Why did the filial son arrive so late?" The person replied, "He is currently the governor of Mount Kongtong, and the journey is still two thousand miles away." Soon, the drums and music sounded loudly, and the torches were arranged like a blazing fire. The city official reported, "The filial son has arrived!" The deity bowed slightly to greet, showing great respect, and then entered solemnly, sitting facing each other. The filial son's attire was very elegant, and his appearance was also plump and healthy. After exchanging pleasantries and serving tea, the deity respectfully explained that the local people's intentions were distant from the painter. The filial son frowned and said, "Why must it be so?" The deity replied, "Due to deep affection for his hometown, he wants to obtain the image and appearance to serve as a model for future generations, allowing them to look up to and admire." A red-clothed official asked the filial son to enter the west pavilion to change clothes. After a short while, he returned to his seat, wearing official robes, black boots, and an exposed top, with an extremely thin and gaunt appearance. After a short while, he calmed down, changed back to his original attire, bowed twice, and rose to take leave. The deity respectfully sent him off in a carriage, and after bowing three times, he returned.

    245

    灯光大放，满堂寂然。鲍蓦如梦醒，即抽毫就灯写就。天明举示人，与孝子曾孙骨气同，鲍未面也。惟首无帽，颈无领。盖国初时，公服上以尺布围颈，投波时领与帽飘去，故冥冥现形，犹貌当年精卫，由是人益神其术。后与扬州营李游击善。李有雏婢小玉，与娈童施姓私通，李见而怒叱之。两小私遁，觅无迹，急延鲍，恐之曰：“君有术，必能知渠所在。如言之确，当酬以重金，若不语，当以妖法收汝狱。

    Explanation

    245

    The lights shone brightly, and the entire hall was silent. Bo suddenly woke up from his dream, immediately took out his brush, and wrote by the light. When dawn broke, he showed his writing to others, and it matched the spirit of the filial grandson, though Bo had not met him. But it lacked a hat on the head and a collar around the neck. It was said that at the beginning of the dynasty, public officials wore a strip of cloth around their necks, which flew off when they were thrown into the waves. Thus, the figure appeared in the darkness, still resembling the spirit of Jingwei from that time. As a result, people became even more amazed by his skills.

    Later, he became friends with Li, the military officer in Yangzhou. Li had a young maid named Xiao Yu, who had an affair with a boy named Shi. Li saw them and angrily scolded them. The two fled secretly, and no trace of them could be found. Li urgently invited Bo, threatening him, saying, "You have skills; you must be able to find out where they are. If you speak the truth, I will reward you with a large sum of money; if you do not speak, I will use magic to imprison you."

    246

    ”鲍不得已，勉为一滚，即伸纸遣墨，画长堤浅水，疏柳数行，一渔家，门首晒罾，屋后有覆艇，微露男女足。李遣役照图捕，果获。略审诘，双双活瘗之。鲍持金急遁，走仪征，将渡江，夜从荡妇宿。甫登榻，见婢与仆，相搂自帷后出，笑而招以手，鲍大呼，阳脱，死于妇腹上。懊侬氏曰：身有绝技，不知敛藏，犹贾祸患。弄怒潮者必溺毙，戏竿杪者终坠亡。况明明邪教，且攫金而卖命者乎？死妇腹上，犹便易渠。至孝子风采，如旭日之当空，如朝霞之映海，英风凛凛，千载如生，固无日不在人心目间；乞渠写真，本为多事。

    Explanation

    246

    Reluctantly, Bo performed one action, then spread out paper and wrote, drawing a long dike with shallow water and sparse willows, a fishing family with a net drying at the door and a boat covering the back of the house, slightly revealing the feet of men and women. Li sent out officials to capture according to the diagram, and they succeeded. They were briefly interrogated and then buried alive together. Bo fled quickly with money, went to Yizheng and prepared to cross the river, spending the night with a promiscuous woman. As soon as he sat on the bed, he saw the maid and servant embrace and come out from behind the curtain, smiling and beckoning with their hands. Bo shouted loudly, pretending to escape, and died on the woman's abdomen. The commentator said: Having exceptional skills, yet not knowing how to keep them hidden, still brings disaster. Those who play with angry tides will surely drown, those who play with the tips of poles will eventually fall. How much more so for those who openly practice heretical teachings and sell their lives for money? Dying on the woman's abdomen is still easier for him. The noble character of filial sons is like the rising sun in the sky, like the morning glow reflecting on the sea. Their heroic spirit is awe-inspiring, as if they were alive a thousand years later, always in people's hearts and minds. To ask him to paint a portrait is indeed a troublesome matter.

    247

    范小仙
    　　
    　　范小仙，不知何许人，与吾乡城隍庙住持白道士友善。白面貌清秀，性情恬适，少慕勒敕之学，不得其传。徒步走江西之龙虎山谒真人，栖玉真观，随法官叶某朝夕炼，将功颇进。三年后，白忽思归，叶曰：“功成只一篑耳！”白终不欲须臾留。叶赠一硕腹大牝驴，绾以草绳，云：“两日即到珂里，但半途万不可饮以水，至则喂养，亦可代步。”白拜别，果两日到。由庙之后门入，系驴斗姥阁下。入而解橐更衣，参师长，并拜见同侣，咸问：“何时离真人府？”具告之。众讶曰：“跋涉何其速也！”以驴对。

    Explanation

    247

    Fan Xiaoxian, of unknown origin, was on good terms with Bai Daoist, the abbot of our city隍庙. Bai had a clear and handsome appearance, with a calm and contented disposition. In his youth, he admired the teachings of Lü Chong (or Lü Chi) and sought to learn Daoist alchemy, but he failed to receive the full transmission. He walked to Longhu Mountain in Jiangxi Province to pay homage to a real person (a Daoist master) and resided in Yuzhen Temple, where he followed Judge Ye to practice daily and made considerable progress. After three years, Bai suddenly missed home. Ye said, "Success is just one basket away!" but Bai still did not want to stay for even a moment. Ye gave him a large-bellied female donkey tied with a grass rope and instructed him, "It will arrive in Keli in two days, but do not give it water halfway, feed it upon arrival, and it can also serve as transportation." Bai bade farewell and indeed arrived in two days. He entered through the back door of the temple and tied the donkey under the Doutu Pavilion. He unpacked his belongings, changed clothes, paid respects to the senior monks, and greeted his fellow monks. They all asked, "When did you leave the abode of the True Person?" He told them in detail. The others were surprised and said, "How quickly you traveled!" He replied with the donkey.

    248

    众走觇之，驴乌有，惟青草擒一大虾蟆，解之，跃入水际不见。白由是能召亡求雨诸法事，至飞升吐纳之妙，则依旧茫然也。一日，范自他郡出，指名访白，宾主问讯，欢若平生。终夜清谈，语甚玄妙。白即欣然留范下榻。白长素，而范则茹荤，且嗜酒。白非笑之，范饮啖自若。暇亦与邑之士大夫游，人咸爱其豪迈俊爽，不以羽流目之。工书，善堪舆，然不轻言，言必有中。一日，同白饮庭中，月色清洁，水镜高悬，满地树影，若即若离。白乐甚，告范曰：“如此良宵，若有灯戏看，庶不负此一轮。”范曰：“有灯戏，所在并不远，顷正开场，曷往观乎？”白问何处，曰：“去便审其地。

    Explanation

    248

    The crowd went to investigate, but the donkey was nowhere to be found; only green grass had caught a large toad. When released, it leaped into the water and disappeared. From then on, Bai was able to summon the dead and perform rain-summoning rituals, but he remained clueless about the wonders of flying and breathing. One day, Fan came from another county, specifically visiting Bai by name. The host and guest greeted each other warmly, as if they had known each other for a long time. They engaged in philosophical discussions all night, and the conversation was very profound and mysterious. Bai immediately and gladly invited Fan to stay overnight. Bai practiced vegetarianism, while Fan ate meat and loved to drink alcohol. Bai laughed at him, but Fan continued to drink and eat as usual. In his leisure time, Bai also socialized with the local scholars and officials, and everyone admired his bold and refreshing personality, not regarding him as a hermit or recluse. He was skilled in calligraphy and excellent in geomancy, but he did not speak lightly; his words were always on target. One day, they drank together in the courtyard, where the moonlight was clear and bright, a high mirror-like surface reflected the sky, and the ground was covered with tree shadows, appearing both close and distant. Bai was very happy and told Fan, "On such a beautiful night, if there is a light show to watch, it would not be wasted." Fan replied, "There is a light show, and it is not far away. It has just started, why don't we go and watch it?" Bai asked where it was, and Fan said, "Let's go and check the location."

    249

    ”言已，以庭中长木凳，自骑一半，以半骑白，嘱闭目，以两手抱其腰，曰：“慎勿遽开目，违则坠地死矣。”白应之，范咒曰：“起！”凳已腾空，两耳风飕飕，闻江涛澎湃声，又人语喧哗声，旋钲鼓齐鸣声。曰：“至矣。”凳已落于地。白启眸纵观，则一极大戏园，士女如蚁，莫不仰视。台上正演新剧，满场灯火，开不夜天。范与白同立凳上，观良久，忽一秃发短童，一垂髫美女，一鹤发老叟，一跛足乞丐，联臂踏歌，嬉笑踏月来。见范，睇之笑，似欲有语。范急取袖中钱囊与白：“君若饥渴，中有孔方，可随意用，吾与故人略走走即来。”白曰：“诺。”范跳下凳，即走入四人丛中，且语且笑且信步，略转瞬即不见。

    Explanation

    249

    After finishing the speech, Fan took a long wooden bench from the courtyard and had White ride on half of it while he rode the other half. He instructed White to close his eyes and hold onto his waist with both hands, saying, "Be careful not to open your eyes hastily, for if you disobey, you will fall to the ground and die." White agreed, and Fan chanted, "Rise!" The bench ascended into the air, and the wind whistled in their ears. They heard the sound of surging river waves, followed by the noise of people talking loudly and the ringing of gongs and drums. Fan said, "We have arrived." The bench had landed on the ground. White opened his eyes and looked around. They were in a very large theater, filled with men and women like ants, all looking up. On the stage, a new play was being performed, and the entire venue was lit up, illuminating the night sky. Fan and White stood together on the bench and watched for a long time. Suddenly, a bald-headed short boy, a beautiful girl with braids, an old man with white hair, and a lame beggar joined hands and stepped to the music, laughing and coming under the moonlight. When they saw Fan, they smiled at him as if wanting to say something. Fan quickly took a money pouch from his sleeve and gave it to White, saying, "If you are hungry or thirsty, there is money inside; you can use it freely. I will go for a walk with an old friend and come back soon." White replied, "Yes." Fan jumped off the bench and immediately entered the group of four, talking, laughing, and strolling leisurely. After a brief moment, they disappeared.

    250

    白痴候，听村鸡四唱，台上撤钲鼓，收灯火，观者四散，而范久不至。白露坐以待。至天明，且卓午，仍不至，肩凳往询，行人云：“此为毗陵城，距故乡已五百余里矣。”大窘，痛骂范道士，无良失信，致受奔波苦。意将乞食，突忆钱囊，扪之，内有碎银二三两，易之足敷川资。时因纳凉，仅着短葛衫，遂徒步肩凳过江，由竹西直至故里。至则问庙佣，云：“范公连日均在庙内，并未出门一步。”往观之，则范犹酣卧未起，呼而怨之。范惟含笑问曰：“木凳弃之否？”曰：“自家长物，能不携归耶？”笑曰：“吾固知君必不忍弃己物。”时白所主者，庙之东房也。多楼阁，缠延三四进。西房素贫，无力建筑，内外皆平房。范以为乾方太塌，若无楼，庙必败，于邑亦大不便。

    Explanation

    250

    At the break of dawn, listening to the village chickens crow four times, the stage drums and gongs were removed, the lights extinguished, and the audience dispersed, yet Master Fan did not arrive. White sat waiting. By dawn, and even by noon, he still did not come. White shouldered his stool and went to inquire, and the passersby said, "This is Pi Ling City, more than five hundred miles from your hometown." He was greatly distressed and scolded Master Fan fiercely for being untrustworthy and causing him much hardship. He intended to beg for food but suddenly remembered his money pouch, feeling it, found a small amount of silver worth two or three taels, which was enough for travel expenses. Because it was cool, he wore only a short hemp shirt and walked over the river with his stool, passing through Zhuxi and finally reaching his hometown. Upon arrival, he asked the temple attendant, who said, "Master Fan has been inside the temple every day and has not stepped out once." Going to see him, White found that Master Fan was still sound asleep and called out, blaming him. Master Fan merely smiled and asked, "Did you abandon the wooden stool?" White replied, "It is my own property, how could I not take it back?" Master Fan laughed and said, "I knew you would not abandon your own things." At that time, White was in charge of the eastern wing of the temple, which had many pavilions and courtyards, winding around three or four times. The western wing was usually poor and lacked the means to build, so it was all flat-roofed buildings. Master Fan thought the eastern side was too low, and if there were no upper floors, the temple would surely fail, and it would also be very inconvenient for the town.

    251

    瞰庙西王氏宅，尚有堂楼五间，串楼五间，年久欲倾颓。王姓欲拆毁，有所成议。范急往告王姓，求勿毁，曰：“神为一邑冥官，庙则关阖姓风水，西偏处全赖瀛第楼为靠厢，若毁之，恐均不利。”王姓曰：“楼将倒塌，奈何？”曰：“吾有术在。”即以竿木自上面拄之。王姓曰：“楼往西倾，子在东拄，是速其倒也。”曰“拄之可延数百年，岂速其倒乎？”王姓亦姑妄听之，而竿木竟坚不可摇。楼素危，每风雨辄动摇，至是竟无恙。范于无人时便告白曰：“子知我远来之意乎？”曰：“不知。

    Explanation

    251

    Looking down upon the western residence of the Wang family, there still stands five halls and five row houses, which have been leaning and collapsing after many years. The Wang family wants to tear it down and has a plan to rebuild. Fan hurriedly went to inform the Wang family, asking them not to destroy it, saying, "The deity is a local underworld official, and the temple is related to the family's feng shui. The western side entirely relies on the Yingdi Building as a support. If it is destroyed, it will be detrimental to all." The Wang family said, "The building is about to collapse, what should be done?" Fan replied, "I have a method." He used poles and wood from above to prop it up. The Wang family said, "The building is tilting westward, you are propping it up from the east, this will hasten its collapse." Fan said, "Prop it up and it can last for several hundred years, how can it hasten its collapse?" The Wang family also listened to it half-heartedly, but the poles and wood were so firmly in place that they could not be moved. The building has always been dangerous, and every time there is wind or rain, it shakes. Until now, it has remained undamaged. Fan explained in private, "Do you know the reason for my long journey?" The Wang family said, "We do not know."

    252

    ”曰：“前生与子有缘，特来邀子同往，栖深谷，习至道，功成登金阙，朝玉真。子奈何恋尘，毫无去志乎？”曰：“吾原不耐岑寂，始由江西归耳。不然，至今尚未离龙虎山。更能舍家园，随子浪漂泊耶？”范每与白观火瞰井，登绝，涉危桥，辄拉白同下。白俱以为幻术，不深信。荏苒三年，范忽谓白曰：“子既不去，吾亦欲归矣。”明日，遍别所与游者，问何之，曰：“远甚远甚。”时陈君习医，亦与范善，闻将去，即杯酌饯行，求范曰：“君有奇术，必多良方，今将别矣，曷举一二赠我，亦可济世行方便也。”范笑顾厅事西畔土墙一围，曰：“即此便是催生药。”时积雨数十日，南山蛟水大发，巨浪围女墙。

    Explanation

    252

    "Fan said, 'From a past life, we have been bound by fate, and I have specially come to invite you to retreat to a deep valley together, to study the highest truths. Upon achieving success, we shall ascend to the golden palace and pay homage to the Jade True One. Why do you still cling to the world, showing no desire to leave?' White replied, 'I originally could not tolerate the loneliness of seclusion, which is why I returned from Jiangxi. Otherwise, I would still not have left Longhu Mountain. How could I abandon my home and follow you on a life of wandering?' Fan often accompanied White in observing dangerous situations, climbing to perilous heights, and crossing treacherous bridges, always pulling White down with him. White believed these were illusions and did not fully trust them. Three years passed, and Fan suddenly told White, 'Since you are not leaving, I also wish to return.' The next day, he bid farewell to all his companions, asked where he was going, and replied, 'Extremely far, extremely far.' At the time, Chen, who practiced medicine, was also friends with Fan. Upon hearing of his departure, Chen held a farewell banquet and requested Fan, 'You possess extraordinary skills and must have many effective remedies. Now that we are parting, please share a few with me, so that I may help the world and do good deeds.' Fan smiled and pointed to a circular wall of earth on the west side of the hall, saying, 'This is the medicine to induce childbirth.' At that time, it had rained for dozens of days, and the waters of the southern mountains surged, with huge waves surrounding the parapet."

    253

    范归，拉白登墙视大水，久之，俯曰：“此中有佳境，吾与子投入如何？”白不肯，范太息曰：“子所谓有仙缘，无仙骨者。虽然，吾先以术坚子信，亦可登。”袖出匹布，长十余丈，向空一掷，即成危桥，若接霄汉，曰：“吾试与子游月宫，好否？”白坚立不肯登。范遂揖而后登，耸身跃入空际，人影依稀，突布坠，范亦坠，投水中，风挟洪涛两三卷，则人布俱杳。白大声呼救，已无及。归而涕泣，以为范小仙左道自杀，饱鱼腹矣。明日，客有自邗江回者，携扇访白，云：“昨在东门浮桥遇范，渠云行时误携君扇，嘱乘便返璧。”白视之，果己扇，至是始悟范真仙去也。

    Explanation

    253

    Fan Gui pulled Bai to climb the wall to look at the flood. After a while, Fan bent down and said, "There is a fine place here. How about we enter it together?" Bai refused, and Fan sighed, "As you say, you have a connection to the divine but lack the divine essence. Nevertheless, I will first use my skills to strengthen your belief, and you can also climb." Fan drew out a piece of cloth from his sleeve, longer than ten zhang, and threw it into the air, where it immediately formed a dangerous bridge, as if connecting to the heavens. He said, "I will try to take you to the moon palace. Is that good?" Bai firmly stood his ground and refused to climb. Fan then bowed and climbed, leaping into the air. His figure was faint, and suddenly the cloth fell, and Fan also fell, plunging into the water. The wind carried the huge waves several times, and both person and cloth disappeared. Bai shouted for help, but it was too late. Fan returned and wept, believing that Fan, the little immortal, had committed suicide by heretical means and been eaten by fish. The next day, a guest returned from Hangjiang, carrying a fan to visit Bai, saying, "Yesterday, I met Fan at the floating bridge in the east gate. He said he accidentally carried your fan and asked me to return it to you." Bai looked at it and saw it was his own fan. At this point, he realized that Fan, the true immortal, had left.

    254

    又十余年，王姓不能守范约，径拆楼，工人以石杵凿去竿木，有金光一道，大声若雷，楼主在串楼上，突颠下，几毙。陈姓遇有难产者，姑以墙土试之，颇验，然施送则验，索谢则不验。远近闻之，争来索土，不两载，土墙无一撮之多矣。余童时，犹在庙之东房，见正中悬丰干禅师骑虎像，又楼上悬白鹿衔芝图，笔墨秀逸，然出尘。道士云为小仙遗笔，未知真赝耳。懊侬氏曰：苦口婆心，百计莫悟。古人处君父师友间，药石为仇者，均不外是。然犹得曰：“吾君父也，吾师友也，吾非得已也，吾尽其在己之道也。”若范之与白，生不同井，道不同门，秦越殊途，一朝邂逅；顾白已桎缚其体，胶糊其心，清真之风，万无从入；犹复于洪涛汨没之后，道出广陵，寄归别面，其将以此自炫耶？抑使彼悟而深悔耶？噫！白固痴，而范亦痴。

    Explanation

    254

    More than ten years later, the Wang family could not uphold the agreement with the Fan family and proceeded to dismantle the tower. The workers used a stone pestle to chisel away the poles, and a beam of golden light appeared, accompanied by a sound like thunder. The owner of the tower suddenly fell from the top floor, nearly dying. The Chen family encountered someone with a difficult childbirth and tried using the wall soil, which proved somewhat effective, but it did not work when they asked for payment. People from far and wide heard about it and came to ask for the soil. Within two years, there was not even a handful of soil left on the wall. When I was a child, I was still in the eastern room of the temple. I saw a statue of Master Fenggan riding a tiger hanging in the center, and on the upper floor, there was a painting of a white deer holding a lingzhi mushroom. The brushwork was elegant and refined, as if transcending the mundane. The Taoist said it was a legacy of a minor immortal, but I don't know if it's genuine or fake. The commentator, Annoyed Man, said: "Speaking earnestly and patiently, yet no matter how many ways, they could not understand. In ancient times, those who treated medicine as an enemy, whether in the roles of sovereign, father, teacher, or friend, were all the same. Yet one could still say, 'I am your sovereign and father, I am your teacher and friend, I had no choice, I have done my best.' As for Fan and Bai, born from different wells, following different paths, like Qin and Yue, taking different routes, they met by chance one day. But Bai was already bound in body and mind, glued together in his thoughts, and the pure and true spirit could never enter. Yet, after passing through Guangling, he sent back a different face. Was it to show off? Or to make him realize and deeply regret? Ah! Bai was foolish, and Fan was also foolish."

    255

    郁绿云
    　　
    　　来安山中，富家郁道生，良田千顷，华屋百椽，牛羊千蹄，而胸无点墨。娶歙人鲍姓十姑，貌美，通书史，惟隐嗔乃夫忒俚鄙。孕十五月，产一女，名曰绿云。因设时有绿云一线，自北斗边降中庭云。女生而识字，目下十行，再得慈母训，五六岁即卓荦观书史，摇笔为文章。母喜曰：“此吾家女学士也，曹大家管仲姬有替人矣！”翁因十姑生女后，久不作茧，娶绳妓金关为妾，娶昵殊深。鲍略争夕，金关娇啼进谗，谮造黑白。翁惑之，然尚念结发情。金关又私埋木人于后圃，乘翁种花时，故出，上有翁名氏八字，符篆若蚓。

    Explanation

    255

    In the mountains of Lai'an, there lived a wealthy man named Yu Daosheng. He owned vast tracts of fertile land, splendid houses, and countless cattle and sheep, yet he was illiterate. He married a woman from She County named Bao Shigu, who was beautiful, well-versed in books and history, and secretly resented her husband for his crudeness and vulgarity. She gave birth to a daughter after a pregnancy of fifteen months, whom she named Lüyun. It was said that at the time of her birth, a single line of green clouds descended from the edge of the Big Dipper to the courtyard. The girl was born knowing how to read, able to scan ten lines at a glance, and under the guidance of her loving mother, she excelled in studying history and writing articles by the age of five or six. Her mother was delighted and said, "This is our family's female scholar, a worthy successor to Cao Cao and Guan Zhong!" After his daughter was born, Yu Daosheng had not been able to produce more children for a long time, so he took a courtesan named Jin Guan as a concubine, whom he favored deeply. Bao Shigu occasionally complained about this, and Jin Guan, pouting and crying, spread false accusations, twisting the facts black and white. Yu Daosheng was deceived by her, but he still remembered his long-standing affection for Bao Shigu. Jin Guan secretly buried a wooden figure in the garden, taking advantage of the time when Yu Daosheng was busy planting flowers, and deliberately revealed it, with the old man's name and eight characters inscribed on it, the symbols resembling a worm.

    256

    翁见之，大怒，以为暗算巫蛊，必十姑妒嫉所为，乃扃之幽室，将设马厩之谋。女时甫十二龄，侦知之，夜穴墙壁，扶母出，藏东邻家，仍回房闼卧。翁始犹叫噪，继知之，亦怜其孝，置不问，仍招十姑归。然由此反目，恩断义绝，觌面无一言。十姑幽郁冤愤久，得沉疴，誓不服药，遂逝。女心痛不敢哭，惟枕上多泪痕。翁宠妾为嫡，胁女母事之。女貌极温柔，而行动皆不合继母意，始毒詈，继痛楚。翁左袒殊难；久益昏瞀，房欲过度，鸡骨支离。念得病将毙，思族中惟一从弟玉生，邀至与诀。伏枕哀泣云：“兄无后，只此弱息，乞吾弟照看之！田产饶沃，渠皆巾帼，何能为？乞为觅一佳婿，赘作吾嗣。析产为二，弟得半，与半可也。”玉生一一敬诺，翁目旋瞑。

    Explanation

    256

    The old man saw it and became very angry, thinking it was a secret plot of witchcraft, and must be the work of the tenth aunt's jealousy. He then locked her in a secluded room, intending to set a cruel plan of a horse stable. The girl was just twelve years old. She discovered it and tunneled through the walls at night, helped her mother out, and hid her in the east neighbor's house, still returning to her room to lie down. The old man initially still shouted, but then realized it and also pitied her filial piety, leaving it uninvestigated and still calling the tenth aunt back. However, from this, they turned against each other, severing all ties of kindness and righteousness, and when they met, there was not a single word. The tenth aunt had long been in a state of hidden depression and unjust anger, got a serious illness, swore not to take medicine, and thus died. The girl was heartbroken but dared not cry, only leaving many tear marks on the pillow. The old man favored his concubine as the principal wife, forcing the girl's mother to serve her. The girl's appearance was extremely gentle, but her actions did not meet the stepmother's expectations, initially being cursed, then being inflicted pain. The old man's favoritism was particularly difficult; for a long time, he became increasingly confused, with excessive sexual desire and bones like those of a chicken. Thinking that the disease would lead to death, he thought of his only cousin, Yu Sheng, and invited him to bid farewell. Lying on the pillow, he wept and said, "My brother has no descendants, only this weak child, please take care of my younger brother!
