The land is rich and fertile, but they are all women, what can they do? Please find a good husband for her, to marry into our family. Divide the property into two, the younger brother gets half, and the other half can be given." Yu Sheng respectfully agreed to each point, and the old man's eyes soon closed.

    257

    女哀哭尽礼，而金关御脂粉，餍粱肉，如平时。女对主哭失声，金关怒曰：“呱呱者，殊可厌也！”立毁其容，使杂家人妇中供操作。女潜诉于叔，玉生怫然曰：“渠汝母也，母之教女，有何不是处！”久之，詈辱益惨，女再诉于叔，玉生曰：“是由尔之不能承顺也。母纵虐，即质诉官庭，又将奈何？”金关潜知之，益凌女而德玉生，从此家事，咸听玉生之命是行。洁厨扫榻馆玉生，饮食丰甚。夕与玉生絮语，恒中夜始散。一日，授女以鞭，命牧豕，女掩泪去。晨出夕归，食仅与脱粟一瓯。花容月貌，顿作鸡皮皴。途遇叔，伏叩泣诉，玉生耳如不闻，将掩面走。

    Explanation

    257

    The daughter wept and mourned according to proper etiquette, while Jin Guan adorned herself with makeup and indulged in rich food, as she had always done. When the daughter cried out to her, Jin Guan flew into a rage and said, "That incessant whining is extremely annoying!" She immediately marred the daughter's appearance and forced her to mingle with the other women of the household to perform menial tasks. The daughter secretly complained to her uncle, Yusheng, who angrily retorted, "She is your mother! What wrong has she done in teaching you?" After a while, Jin Guan's abuse grew even more severe, and the daughter complained to her uncle again. Yusheng replied, "This is because you cannot be submissive. If your mother continues to mistreat you, you can take the matter to court, but what then?" Jin Guan secretly learned of this and began to bully the daughter even more while showing gratitude to Yusheng. From then on, all household matters were under Yusheng's command. She prepared a clean kitchen and a tidy room for Yusheng, providing him with abundant food and drink. At night, she engaged in lengthy, detailed conversations with Yusheng, often continuing until late at night. One day, she gave the daughter a whip and ordered her to tend the pigs, and the daughter left with tears in her eyes. She went out in the morning and returned at night, eating only a bowl of coarse rice. Her once beautiful face suddenly became wrinkled like chicken skin. On the way, she met her uncle and knelt before him, weeping and pleading for help. Yusheng, however, seemed not to hear her and turned away, walking off.

    258

    女牵衣使止，曰：“叔乎，奈何忘我死父赠产托孤之遗嘱乎？”玉生怒曰：“小妮子宜其遭鞭笞！尔父赠我产，非尔产也。托孤一言，虽有凭证，其如汝之倔强何？祖父数世，均未分析，何得言赠！且虐汝者，汝母也，非叔也。再饶舌，即直陈于汝母！”言已拂袖去。时金关又有中表弟王禽来，与玉生结酒肉交。禽瞰女貌，谋欲污之。东邻白七姑，觉而私泄于女，大惧，曰：“是真不可留矣！”弃豕，逸入东山岩深处，垢面蓬头，栖止山穴，饥食野蔬，渴饮涧泉，得不死。年余，见涧底有草，亭亭如笔管，根团结如姜芽，食之味甘，即蓄为御冬计。洞穴大可容膝，石为门，藉草作榻，立门前眺望，其景更幽。久则身轻，大壑能越。

    Explanation

    258

    The girl tugs at his clothes to make him stop, saying, "Uncle, how can you forget my dead father's will to gift property and entrust me to your care?" Yusheng angrily says, "The little girl deserves to be beaten! Your father gifted me the property, not you. Even if there is evidence for the entrustment, what can you do against your stubbornness? For several generations, the family has not divided the property, so how can you talk about gifting? And the one who mistreats you is your mother, not your uncle. If you keep talking, I will tell your mother directly!" He then sweeps his sleeves and leaves. At that time, there was also a cousin named Wang Qin from Jin Pass, who formed a friendship with Yusheng over wine and meat. Wang Qin, seeing the girl's beauty, plots to defile her. The neighbor White Seven Elder, realizing this, privately tells the girl. She is greatly afraid and says, "This is truly something that cannot be left!" She abandons the pig and escapes into the deep rocks of East Mountain. With a dirty face and disheveled hair, she lives in a cave, eating wild vegetables when hungry and drinking from the stream when thirsty, surviving. After a year, she sees grass at the bottom of the stream, tall and slender like a pen tube, with roots clustered like ginger sprouts, which tastes sweet when eaten. She stores it as a plan for winter. The cave is large enough to fit one's knees, with stone as the door and grass as the bed. Standing at the door to gaze, the scenery is even more secluded. After a long time, the body becomes light, able to leap over great ravines.

    259

    山中无历日，瞬又好春。女思既无拘束，大可出游东南诸山，穷极登览。偶至都梁之牧羊山，俗云龙女牧羊处。遂仰天大哭，曰：“龙女获罪舅姑而牧羊，奴获罪继母而牧豕。然龙女得柳毅传书，尚有还宫之日；奴所遭若是，欲寄书于地下父母，庸可得乎？”言已大恸。烟林宿鸟，嘈杂惊飞。忽对崖一垂髫女子，持鸦锄，携筠篮，若画上采药仙童状，向女招手。女知其非人，然亦不怖，拭泪趋就，对坐岩石上。垂髫人曰：“适闻子言，似是闺秀，然尚非真通者。泾河距此甚遥，何预龙女事？山之所以名牧羊者，乃楚怀王孙心牧羊处耳。

    Explanation

    259

    In the mountains, there is no calendar, and soon it is spring again. The woman’s thoughts were unrestrained, and she could freely travel to the mountains in the southeast, exploring and climbing to the fullest extent. She happened to arrive at the Pasture Sheep Mountain in Duliang, which is commonly believed to be the place where the Dragon Maiden tended sheep. She then wept to the heavens, saying, “The Dragon Maiden was punished by her in-laws and had to tend sheep, while I was punished by my stepmother and had to tend pigs. But the Dragon Maiden received a letter from Liu Yi and still had a chance to return to her palace. But to send a letter to my deceased parents, how could that be possible?” After saying this, she wept bitterly, and the birds in the misty woods, startled by the noise, flew away in confusion. Suddenly, a girl with braids, holding a hoe and carrying a bamboo basket, appeared on the opposite cliff, resembling a child in a painting who gathers herbs, and she waved to the woman. The woman knew she was not human, but she was not afraid, wiped her tears, and approached, sitting on a rock face to face with her. The girl said, “I just heard what you said, it seems you are a lady, but you are not truly enlightened. The Jing River is far from here, how does it relate to the Dragon Maiden? The reason this mountain is called Pasture Sheep Mountain is because it was where the grandson of King Huai of Chu tended sheep.”

    260

    项梁求得，立为义帝，都盱眙，旋为黔布所杀，难及长公主，积薪自焚。奴毕姓，名岫芙，字女须，三阿亭长女，白昼杀父仇于市，帝怜之，赦而没为奴。时得公主怜，故以身殉，同焚死。上帝怜鉴，敕主为此山之神，都梁石梁两邑之薄命女子，皆归主管，各有所司，奴则专司采药。”女惨然曰：“然则姊为鬼乎？”曰：“何得仍为鬼也？”曰：“仙乎？”曰：“尚不得谓之仙，然羽化有时耳。顷闻子恸，想亦伤心人，曷告我颠末？”女洒泪缕陈，且哀哀乞拯。曰：“将若何？”曰：“姊姊不弃，收作泥中人，或亦可以分劳乎？”岫审度久之，曰：“且同归，哀于主，或有主仆分。若作子夏之门人小子，则吾岂敢！”女喜，尾之行。落照坠崦嵫。

    Explanation

    260

    Xiang Liang sought and obtained the title of Yi Di, made his capital at Xuyi, and was soon killed by Qian Bu. Her difficulties reached the Long Princess, and she piled up firewood and burned herself. The slave's surname was Bi, her given name was Xiu Fu, and her courtesy name was Nu Xu. She was the daughter of a village headman in San A, and in broad daylight, she killed her father's killer in the market. The emperor pitied her, pardoned her, and made her a slave. At that time, she gained the Long Princess's pity, so she sacrificed herself and died in the fire with her. The Heavenly Emperor pitied and recognized her, decreeing her to be the spirit of this mountain. All the unfortunate women of the towns of Duliang and Shilang are under her jurisdiction, each with their own duties, and the slave is specifically in charge of collecting herbs. The girl sadly said, "Then is my sister a ghost?" She replied, "How can she still be a ghost?" The girl asked, "Is she a fairy?" She said, "She cannot yet be called a fairy, but she will undergo spiritual transformation at some point." Just now I heard your grief, I imagine you are also a heartbroken person, why don't you tell me the whole story? The girl wept and recounted her story in detail, and pleaded for help. She asked, "What should we do?" She said, "If my sister doesn't reject me, she can take me in as a servant, perhaps I can share in the work." Xiu pondered for a long time and said, "Let's go together, weep before the master, and perhaps there will be a master-servant relationship. If I were to become a student of Zixia, I dare not!" The girl was pleased and followed her. The setting sun fell on Yanzhi.

    261

    林木幽深处忽现一宫殿，伟丽非常，中有美人数十辈，风裳月帔，皆非时世装。或倚树听泉，或秉拂趺坐，或调鹦鹉，或鼓凤凰，或小聚清谈，或独立遐瞩。岫曰：“此皆执事人也。”众见岫，争迎曰：“岫姊归何晏也？”岫笑曰：“偶然拾得一下界人，是有慧根而遭奇厄者。”遂代为陈述，且令女与众问讯，然后引谒公主，言所由来。女伏叩阶下，偷睨殿上，坐一黄裳美女子，侍立者即入门所见诸人。主启齿微粲曰：“来大好，且随阿岫小住，晨起采药，勿惰。”女叩谢已，即至岫室，寝同榻，食同案，亲爱若同胞。岫又私授健步丹药一粒，吞之，山行若飞。凡山川古迹，女问之，岫无不确凿以对。

    Explanation

    261

    Deep within the woods, a palace suddenly appeared, extraordinarily magnificent. Inside, there were many beautiful women, dressed in flowing garments and veils, all not of this world. Some leaned against trees listening to the stream, others held a whisk and sat cross-legged in meditation, some were training parrots, others were playing the flute, some had small gatherings for philosophical discussions, and others stood alone gazing far off.

    Xiu said, "These are all attendants." The women saw Xiu and rushed to greet her, saying, "Sister Xiu, why have you returned so suddenly?" Xiu smiled and said, "I accidentally found a person from the lower world, someone with a wise root but suffering a strange misfortune." She then explained the situation and had the women greet her, and then led her to the princess, explaining her arrival.

    The woman knelt and bowed at the steps, secretly glancing up at the hall, where a beautiful woman in a yellow robe sat, and the attendants standing by were the same people she had seen upon entering. The princess smiled slightly and said, "You've arrived well, and stay with me for a little while. Rise early to gather herbs, do not be lazy."

    The woman bowed in thanks and went to Xiu's room, sleeping in the same bed and eating from the same table, loving each other like siblings. Xiu then privately gave her a pill for quickening steps, which she swallowed, and she walked in the mountains as if flying. Whenever the woman asked about mountains, rivers, and ancient sites, Xiu answered with certainty.

    262

    女由是更事之如傅，不敢目为姊妹行。居二年，另遣他婢来采药，命岫掌小琅环曲籍，女副之。鸿文宝笈，人世所无，朝夕研磨，岫更为之讲解，遂通内典，且习遁禽。其中有《剑诀》一册，最合己意，潜自演习。岫知之，笑曰：“妹将学妙手空空儿乎？”曰：“然。”曰：“妹有幽恨，学此恐非所宜也。然无神人授以秘宝，学亦不成，徒搅清课耳。”一日，庭前木苓花开，灿烂如火。公主开樽赏宴，侍者各奏技，或管弦，或歌舞，或献书画；岫则以手采都梁香草为寿。女时立阶下，主顾之，笑曰：“女学士何绝无所长耶？”女应命而起，聚壁上长剑之雌雄者，揎袖舞翩翩，飘风疾雨，寒光逼人。

    Explanation

    262

    The girl thus treated her as a tutor, no longer regarding her as a sister. After two years, another maid was sent to gather herbs, and Xiù was ordered to manage the records of the small Lánguán. The girl assisted her. Magnificent texts and precious scrolls, unparalleled in the world, she studied day and night, and Xiù explained them further, thus she came to understand the inner scriptures and practiced falconry. Among them, there was a volume of Jiànjué (Sword Manual), which best suited her own inclinations, and she secretly practiced it herself. Xiù knew about it and laughed, saying, "Are you going to learn the skills of the Empty-Handed Master?" She replied, "Yes." Xiù said, "You have deep-seated grievances, and learning this might not be appropriate. However, without a divine being to bestow secret treasures, learning this would be futile, only disrupting your studies." One day, the mùlíng flowers in the courtyard bloomed, shining brightly like fire. The princess opened a wine jar for a feast, and the attendants performed various skills, some playing music and strings, others dancing and singing, and some presenting paintings and calligraphy; Xiù picked dūliáng fragrant grass with her hands as a birthday gift. The girl stood at the bottom of the stairs, and the princess noticed her, smiling and saying, "What are you, a scholar, without any special talents?" The girl rose in response, gathered the male and female long swords on the wall, rolled up her sleeves, and danced gracefully, with the wind and rain swirling around, and the cold light shining brightly.

    263

    公主正击节，忽一美人奔白：“程太夫人赍天符至！”公主起，更朝服，执笏焚香祗迓。岫私告女曰：“此东阳程婴母也。”旋见夫人跨鹤降于庭，公主匍匐，夫人宣云：“九霄灵宝天尊玉清帝王诏曰：下界前义帝女楚姑，生而婉淑静贞，殁更英烈惨孝，风云咤叱，青史留香；火焰销熔，红颜有泪。彼姬随骓逝，后号雉淫者，视姑之闺范，悬鸡凤乎！前敕姑为牧羊山神，又复感化其氓，精勤厥职，实无愧天潢之裔，忍久羁地祗之流。兹敕程安人亲来迓汝，升金阙，朝玉妃。谪满归真，勋高锡爵。忆投山木石，可怜精卫衔冤；看云程旌旗，聊代娲皇补恨。

    Explanation

    263

    The princess was clapping in rhythm when suddenly a beautiful woman rushed in to announce, "Lady Cheng has arrived with a heavenly symbol!" The princess rose, changed into her court attire, held a tablet of office, burned incense, and respectfully welcomed her. The mountain spirit privately told the woman, "This is Lady Cheng of Dongyang." Soon, the lady descended from a crane to the courtyard, and the princess prostrated herself. The lady proclaimed, "The decree of the Supreme Lord of the Nine Heavens, Jade Purity Emperor, says: The former daughter of the Righteous Emperor of Chu, born gentle and virtuous, died with even greater heroism and sorrow, her deeds echoing through history like thunder and lightning; her beauty, consumed by flames, shed tears of blood. That woman, who followed the king of Wu to his death, was later labeled a 'pheasant of lust'; how does her behavior compare to the chaste and virtuous example of the princess? Previously, the princess was appointed as the mountain deity of shepherding, and she transformed and enlightened the common people, diligently performing her duties, truly without愧to her noble lineage, yet enduring long as a local deity. Now, Lady Cheng has been specially sent to welcome you, to ascend to the Golden Hall and pay homage to the Jade Empress. After completing your period of punishment, you will return to your true nature and be granted a noble title. Remember the story of Jingwei, who threw stones and wood into the sea, bearing a grudge; see the flags flying through the clouds, symbolizing the goddess Nüwa mending the sky and making amends for grievances.

    264

    钦哉！”主舞蹈谢恩讫，与夫人相见，叙寒暄。龙肝麟脯，洁膳款留。岫泫然告女曰：“吾辈侍主跸上升，妹尚凡体，奈何？然良晤亦正自不远，有数在耳。”女忽奉主唤入，谕曰：“子有仙骨，而性气未平，未敢授以大道。今且别矣，聊以一物相赠。”言已，袖出两白丸，令吞服，曰：“此剑丸也。顷因舞剑，故投其所好耳。然宜谨志，莫妄杀人。他日山阴道上，有跛足少年，负葫芦者，子婿也。功成，当重见我。”女方恋恋，主已策凤，程仍跨鹤，余皆乘锦禽；力士前驱，香风远引，霎时飞入空际。女仰瞩不见其影，回视宫殿全无，唯乱石流泉而已，大哭失声。

    Explanation

    264

    "Admire!" The sovereign performed a dance of gratitude, then met with his consort, exchanging pleasantries. They offered the finest delicacies, a feast of dragon liver and unicorn meat, to warmly welcome and entertain. Xiu, with tears in her eyes, told her daughter, "We will serve the sovereign on his ascent to the heavens, but you still have a mortal body. What shall we do? However, a good meeting is not far off; it is fated." Suddenly, the daughter was summoned by the sovereign and brought into his presence. He said, "You have the bones of a celestial being, but your temperament is not yet calm, so I dare not impart the great Dao to you. For now, let us part. I will give you something." Having said this, he drew out two white pills from his sleeve and instructed her to swallow them. "These are sword pills," he explained. "I recently danced with a sword, so I gave you something that aligns with your interests. However, you must be cautious and not kill recklessly. One day, on the mountain road, you will encounter a lame young man carrying a gourd; he will be your husband. When your mission is accomplished, we will meet again." As the daughter clung to him, the sovereign mounted a phoenix, Cheng still rode a crane, and the rest rode colorful birds. Warriors led the way, and a fragrant breeze guided them, and in an instant, they flew into the sky. The daughter looked up but could not see their shadows, turned around and saw no palace, only scattered stones and flowing streams, and she wept loudly, losing her voice.

    265

    时滁阳牧高公，遣迎太夫人就养，道出于此，见女作道家装，哭于歧路。太夫人素仁慈，招致车前，殷殷详询。女跪以孑然无依，投亲失路为对。太夫人携之归，作赤脚小婢，周旋合度，处处得人怜，太夫人尤宠爱。其东邻白七姑者，亦寻夫至此，闻人云：“近同友人去塞外，遂流落此邑，乃佣于署。”女晤之，大惊喜，私询家事，白云：“姑去后，金关先乱于玉，继乱于禽，所有尽偿枭雉积逋，先尊人遗产荡然矣。不知近作何状。犹记出门时，见其所往来者，非人也。”女惨然流涕，坚乞勿漏言。会州牧以娇女下嫁于邻封某明府之子，彩舆鼓吹，甫逾清流关，忽驺从奔回，喘息曰：“中途遇盗，攫女公子入山巢矣。

    Explanation

    265

    At the time, Governor Gao of Chuzhou sent to welcome the Grand Lady for support, and they passed through this place. There, they saw a girl dressed in Daoist attire, weeping at a fork in the road. The Grand Lady, known for her kindness, invited her to the carriage and inquired about her situation in great detail. The girl knelt and explained that she was alone and without support, having lost her way while seeking relatives. The Grand Lady took her back with her, and she became a barefoot maidservant, acting in a proper and measured way, gaining the affection of everyone, especially the Grand Lady’s special love. Her neighbor, an older woman respectfully called White Seventh Aunt, had also come to search for her husband and heard people say, “Recently, she went to the frontier with a friend and ended up wandering and settling in this town, where she works as a servant in the office.” The girl met her and was greatly surprised and joyful, privately asking about her family matters. White Seventh Aunt replied, “After I left, the gold lock was first taken by jade, then by birds. All her possessions were used to pay off the accumulated debts of the crows and hawks, and the inheritance of her late father is completely gone. I don’t know what it’s like now. I still remember when I left, I saw that the people she associated with were not human.” The girl wept bitterly and firmly begged her not to reveal what she had heard. Coincidentally, the governor was arranging the marriage of his beloved daughter to the son of a prominent official in a neighboring county. The wedding procession, with a decorated carriage and music, had just passed through Qingliu Pass when suddenly the attendants returned in haste, panting, and said, “On the way, we encountered bandits who seized the young lady and took her into a mountain lair.”

    266

    ”一门窘急无计，忽曰饬役捕，忽曰回营剿。女闻之，抗声曰：“是皆非良策也。夫女子所重者，唯贞洁耳。若辗转逾一宵，虽湔以西江之水，已无及矣。”众曰：“然则奈何？”曰：“婢子不才，愿为一往。”言已，即如鸟之纵翼，忽不见。众愈惊骇，不知所云。夜三更，新月挂树杪，金柝交鸣，众痴对若木偶。忽闻檐际有人云：“幸不辱命，迎得掌上珍珠还也。”旋即坠地，视之果女，且负女公子至。斯时，鸡犬不惊，漏甫三转。众围询之，女公子云：“贼之甫攫去也，闭置斗室，旋有男子至，意在逼奸，挡拒呼号，苦无死所。忽有白光一线，自窗隙入。凉飒然，视盗首已堕地，婢亦至，遽负于背，御风行，始生还耳。

    Explanation

    266

    One day, the family was in dire straits and had no plan. Suddenly, the orders changed: first to arrest, then to return to camp and attack. The girl heard this and spoke out boldly, "These are not good strategies. For a woman, the most important thing is chastity. If she is delayed for even one night, even washing with the waters of the Xi River would be too late." The crowd asked, "Then what should we do?" She replied, "I am unworthy, but I am willing to go." With that, she spread her wings like a bird and suddenly disappeared. The crowd was even more shocked and bewildered, unsure of what to do. At the third watch of the night, a new moon hung at the tip of the tree. The sound of the watchman's bell and the drumming mingled, and the crowd stood motionless like wooden figures. Suddenly, a thin white light entered through the window crack. A chill breeze blew, and the bandit leader was seen lying on the ground. She quickly picked her up, flew through the air, and returned safely.

    267

    ”众方知其为非常人，向殊昧于物色，大加敬礼。而女无骄矜态。太夫人问所操何术，女不言，询再三，白七姑始述甚继母恶叔，虐逼入山之由，然究不省其术之神也。太夫人闻之，大不平，云：“儿之术，红线与隐娘也。蹈虎穴，攫凤雏，且易如反掌，更何难潜回故居，手刃此贼？”女曰：“如太夫人言，诚易事耳。然儿非若辈之凌逼，则不过以田舍妇终，何能有此薄技？且酬报之，何必定污吾刃。”太夫人益钦其容物。谋为作伐，意即妻其孙公子也。女知之，夜告白曰：“吾踪迹已露，难久居。

    Explanation

    267

    People now realized that she was no ordinary person; previously, they were completely unaware of her, and they greatly respected and honored her. However, the woman showed no arrogant or conceited attitude. The grandmother asked what kind of skills she possessed, but the woman did not speak, and was asked repeatedly. After explaining to the seventh aunt, she began to describe how her stepmother and uncle were cruel and forced her into the mountains, but she still did not fully understand the miraculous nature of her skills. Upon hearing this, the grandmother was greatly dissatisfied and said, "Her skills are like those of Hongxian and Yin Niang. Stepping into the tiger's den, capturing the young phoenix, and it is as easy as turning one's hand. How much more difficult is it to secretly return to her former home and kill this villain with her own hands?" The woman replied, "If the grandmother says so, it is indeed an easy task. However, if it were not for their bullying, I would have ended up as a simple village woman, how could I have acquired such a minor skill? Moreover, to repay them, why must I stain my blade?" The grandmother was even more impressed by her composure. She planned to arrange a marriage for her, intending to make her the wife of her grandson. The woman knew this and, at night, told her, "My presence has been revealed, and I cannot stay here for long."

    268

    然此处不日有盗警，危矣哉！”白问何以抵御，女自脱指上铁箭环授白，曰：“他日难及，姐以环遥投之，盗即获。”问何之，曰：“天涯海角耳。”问更有何嘱，曰：“姐姐遄回，烦照看先人之殡宫，感且不朽。”言已，抵足眠，清晨视之，杳矣。遍觅无迹，太夫人更悼叹，然无如何，久亦遂已。白之夫，近亦从塞外回，道出于滁，夫妇会合，正欲辞旋。数夕，果有盗众，逾垣入内宅，短衣锦帕，持利刃，带假面具，四十余人。牧奉母潜仆媪宅，余皆藏匿。盗谋登楼肆搜括，白之夫，抽刃欲起，白急止之，曰：“吾有郁姑至宝，今夕可用矣。”如女言掷之，环之放光，如火焰下扑，又如猎网四面，尽覆诸盗，使不得出。白之夫辟门大呼，众役哄进，咸以黑索套颈如犬羊，而铁环亦竟杳然。

    Explanation

    268

    Soon there will be a bandit alert here, it is dangerous!" White asked how to defend against it, and the girl removed the iron arrow ring from her finger and gave it to White, saying, "In the future, when it is difficult to reach, sister, you can throw the ring at them from afar, and the bandits will be captured." When asked where she would go, she replied, "To the ends of the earth." When asked for any further instructions, she said, "Sister, please return quickly and take care of the ancestral tomb. My gratitude will last forever." After saying this, they slept side by side, and in the morning, she had vanished. They searched everywhere but found no trace, and the lady mother grieved and lamented, but there was nothing they could do, and eventually, they came to terms with it. White's husband had recently returned from the frontier and met her in Chu, and the couple reunited, just about to depart. Several nights later, indeed, there was a group of bandits, more than forty, who climbed over the wall and entered the inner courtyard, dressed in short clothes and wearing silk headbands, carrying sharp knives and wearing false masks. The lady mother, taking her mother-in-law, secretly fled to the old lady's house, while the rest hid. The bandits planned to climb up to the second floor to search and loot. White's husband drew his sword and wanted to stand up, but White quickly stopped him, saying, "I have the magical iron ring, which can be used tonight." Following the girl's advice, she threw the ring, and it emitted light like flames, spreading out like a hunting net, covering all the bandits and preventing them from escaping. White's husband opened the door and shouted loudly, and the guards rushed in, tying all the bandits up with black ropes like dogs and sheep, while the iron ring also vanished completely.

    269

    牧高坐堂皇，研讯云：“尔辈汹汹而来，何遽自缚？”曰：“小人等正拟拾阶以进，忽有火光自顶上落，即如身陷狱中，不容动一步。”再询，则知前夺女公子者，亦即若辈所为。问盗魁何人，众囚前伏，指三人泣曰：“吾辈皆赌博场中无赖子耳。为受伊等蛊惑，遂至此，复何言欤！”问三人何名，则一名郁玉生，一名王禽，尾一人名金关，盖女易男装者也。绳妓近绿林，近因窘故，再习故业耳。牧闻之，大骇。使白之夫妇睨之，云不诬。遂取供申详，然后斩于东市。白告归，牧奉母命，厚酬之。

    Explanation

    269

    The magistrate sat high on the main hall, interrogating them: "You all came here in a rush, why did you bind yourselves?" They replied, "We humble ones were just about to ascend the steps to enter, when suddenly there was a flash of fire falling from above, as if we were trapped in a prison, unable to move a single step." Upon further questioning, they learned that those who had abducted the daughter were also the same group. When asked who the leader of the thieves was, the prisoners fell forward, pointing to three individuals and weeping, saying, "We are all scoundrels from the gambling dens. Because we were deceived by them, we have come to this, what more can we say!" When asked for their names, one was named Yu Yusheng, another Wang Qin, and the last one named Jin Guan, who was a woman disguised as a man. The prostitutes had turned to a life of crime due to poverty, resuming their old profession. The magistrate was greatly startled. He sent the couple to verify the information, and they confirmed it was true. The magistrate then took their confessions and detailed accounts, and they were beheaded in the eastern market. The informant reported back home, and the magistrate, following his mother's command, rewarded him generously.

    270

    后数年，牧辞官归闽，仆夫车马，行乱山中，方有戒心，忽见女作剑装，偕一跛足葫芦之男子，拜伏车前曰：“奴已嫁得婿，知夫人至，特来问太夫人安否。前途虽多伏莽，然儿已剪除之矣，请安轮前进，无怖也。”牧尚欲豁白，女遽致声珍重，化作彩云一线，冉冉向西去，跛足葫芦之男子亦杳。适太夫人后车继至，宰趋白所见，太夫人更惋惜浩叹不辍。白夫妇归来安，以牧之所酬者，略营运，遂市田产，称小康。一夜，方闺中对酌，赏秋月，忽空际坠一物，声铿然，旋闻云际有女子声，曰：“吾毕岫芙也，奉楚姑差遣，省义帝陵，遇妹绿云，盛称贤伉俪，不负所托，烦顺路以不腆奉寄耳。”旋觉彩云东去，玉人无声。俯拾之，则彩袱包黄金二锭。遂诣郁翁媪之墓，盛加封植，清明寒食，夫妇具浆饭茶酒冥钱往墓，呼女名，哭而奠之，数十年无间断。

    Explanation

    270

    After several years, the woman resigned from her office and returned to Fujian. Accompanied by her servants, carriages, and horses, she traveled through the rugged mountains. Just then, she felt a sense of caution. Suddenly, she saw a woman dressed in armor, accompanied by a lame man with a gourd-like appearance, who prostrated themselves in front of her carriage and said, "I have already married and found a husband. I know that the lady has arrived, so I have come especially to inquire about the well-being of the lady's mother. Although there are many hidden dangers ahead, I have already eliminated them. Please proceed safely without fear." The woman still wanted to speak frankly, but the woman hastily said to take care, then transformed into a streak of colored clouds that slowly moved westward. The lame man with a gourd-like appearance also disappeared. Just then, the lady's mother's carriage arrived subsequently. The servant hastened to report what was seen. The lady's mother even more regretted and sighed deeply without stopping. The woman and her spouse returned home peacefully. Using what the woman received as compensation, they slightly managed their affairs, then bought land and property and became moderately well-off. One night, just then, in the inner chamber, they drank together, enjoying the autumn moon. Suddenly, something fell from the air with a clanging sound. Soon, they heard a woman's voice in the clouds, saying, "I am Bi Xiufu. I received Lady Chu's dispatch to visit the tomb of the Righteous Emperor. I met my sister, Lü Yun, who highly praised the virtuous couple. I am entrusted to deliver a small amount of gold on the way." Soon, the colored clouds moved eastward, and the woman was silent. They picked up the object, which was a colorful cloth bundle containing two ingots of gold. They then went to the tomb of the elderly man and woman, adding more offerings and tending to it. On Qingming and Hanshi festivals, the couple brought offerings of rice, tea, wine, and paper money to the tomb, calling out the woman's name, crying, and offering prayers. They did this for several decades without interruption.

    271

    樟柳神
    　　
    　　张大眼者，催租隶也。一日五更起，贸贸入城完秋赋。时正酷暑，晨风清凉，行至秋稼湾，日渐上，热甚。路旁有人家，茅舍闭门，主犹酣寝。门外搭豆花棚，蔓延接髡柳，下有两石凳，颇洁净，露水犹湿，遂拭以布巾，就坐小歇，钻火吸烟。忽闻棚上有歌者，声啾啾如秋后知了吟，倾听之，歌曰：“郎在东来妾在西，少小两个不相离；自从接了媒红订，朝朝相遇把头低。低头莫碰豆花架，一碰露水湿郎衣。”大眼闻之，骇诧欲绝。周细讯，则一小木雕婴孩，粉面朱唇，目清眉秀，长二寸许，跃豆花上，笑容犹可掬也。然却为一缕头发系颈，扣棚隙苇叶上，不能逸。大眼心知其为樟柳神，必茅屋中有术人止宿，夕系于此吃露水耳。素审其灵妙，能报未来事，即断发擎腕中，戴笠西行。将见城垣，腕中跃跃若不安，急珍藏于笠内，果安。旋小语曰：“张大眼，好大胆，来捉咱，一千铜钱三十板。”言之不辍。

    271

    Translation:

    The God of Camphor and Willow Trees

    Zhang Dayan, a tax collector, rose at the fifth watch of the night and stumbled into the city to complete his autumn tax. It was the height of summer, but the morning breeze was cool. As he walked to the bend of the autumn crops, the sun rose higher and the heat became intense. Along the road, there was a thatched cottage with closed doors, and the owner was still deeply asleep. Outside the door, a trellis covered with bean vines spread and connected to a willow tree. Below the trellis were two stone benches, quite clean, with dew still wet. He wiped them with a cloth and sat down for a short rest, making a fire to smoke. Suddenly, he heard a song from the trellis, a sound like a cicada singing after autumn. He listened carefully, and the song went: "The young man is east, the young woman is west, two of us have never been apart; since the matchmaker set the date, we meet every day and bow our heads. Don't bow your head and touch the bean vine, or the dew will wet the young man's clothes." Zhang Dayan was extremely surprised and amazed. He carefully inquired and found a small wooden carving of a baby, with a white-powdered face and red lips, clear eyes, and elegant eyebrows, about two inches long, jumping on the bean vines, with a smile still visible. However, it was tied around the neck with a strand of hair, fastened to a gap in the trellis with a reed leaf, unable to escape. Zhang Dayan knew it was the God of Camphor and Willow Trees, and that there must be a sorcerer staying in the thatched cottage, tying it there to drink dew at night. He had always known of its mysterious and wondrous powers, able to predict future events. He immediately cut off his hair and held it in his hand, wearing a straw hat and walking west. As he approached the city walls, his hand was restless and uneasy, so he quickly hid it under the hat, and it was indeed safe. Then he spoke softly: "Zhang Dayan, how bold you are, to come and catch me, one thousand copper coins for thirty lashes." He kept speaking without stopping.

    272

    大眼心计完纳不亏，何至于笞责？听言如不闻。甫进城，邑宰王公，适呵导出行香，见大眼心急足忙，疑为匪，呼从者执之伏地。问伊谁，大眼语钝滞，喘息流汗，不能达。宰怒曰：“非良善也，盍笞三十！”大眼伏街上大笑，宰问：“笞必痛，何反轩渠耶？”曰：“小人预知有三十板之厄，今果然，始笑耳。”宰婉讯之，大眼具述己为租隶，路得樟柳神，预告受杖等语，宰命以神献，大眼即于笠中，取出呈上。

    Explanation

    272

    Da Yan, with his meticulous planning and flawless execution, should not have been subject to flogging. He seemed to ignore what was said. Just as he entered the city, Mr. Wang, the local magistrate, was bellowing and leading out for incense offerings. Seeing Da Yan’s hurried and agitated movements, the magistrate suspected him of being a bandit and called his attendants to seize him and make him kneel. When asked who he was, Da Yan spoke slowly and hesitantly, breathing heavily and sweating profusely, unable to answer clearly. The magistrate, angered, said, “You are not a good person, why not flog you thirty strokes!” Da Yan, kneeling on the street, laughed loudly. The magistrate asked, “Flogging must be painful, why are you laughing instead?” Da Yan replied, “I had predicted a calamity of thirty strokes, and now it has come true, so I am laughing.” The magistrate then gently questioned him, and Da Yan fully described himself as a tenant farmer who had met the god of Camphor and Willow on the road, who had predicted that he would receive a beating. The magistrate ordered him to offer the god, and Da Yan immediately took it out from his hat and presented it.

    273

    宰舆中详审，知有灵，立命赏给青蚨一竿，以慰其冤责。宰由是听狱，必以神置帽中，坐堂皇，为两造预言曲直，如目睹。人争诵神明，比诸虚堂悬镜，无微不烛，而不知公帽中有樟柳神也。公卒后，为乡里城隍，甚灵。懊侬氏曰：近有人亲往姑苏，从巫蛊家买一樟柳神而回，意可以未卜先知矣。讵神殊缄默，所报者无非鼠动鸡啼鸦噪等事，且夜伏枕畔，哓哓烦琐，搅梦不酣。及问以他事，稍有关系者，皆对以不知。私问何故，曰：“惧祸耳！”噫！鬼且惧祸，人可知矣！

    Explanation

    273

    The Magistrate carefully examined the situation and knew there was a spirit present. He immediately ordered a generous reward of a large sum of money to console the spirit for its unjust accusation. From then on, when hearing cases, he would place the spirit in his hat, sit in the grand hall, and predict the right and wrong for both parties as if seeing with his own eyes. People争相颂扬 his clear and divine judgment, comparing it to a hanging mirror in an empty hall that reveals nothing hidden. However, they did not know that the spirit was in the magistrate's hat. After the magistrate died, he became the city god of the village, and his power was very effective. The commentator懊侬氏 said: Recently, someone personally went to Suzhou and bought a spirit of camphor and willow from a witch and returned, intending to predict the future. But the spirit remained unusually silent, reporting nothing but the movements of mice, the crowing of chickens, and the cawing of crows. At night, it lay beside the pillow, chattered incessantly, and disturbed dreams. When asked about other matters, even slightly related, it all answered with "I don't know." Privately asked why, it said, "It's afraid of disaster!" How much more so for humans, if even spirits are afraid of disaster!

    274

    古铁剑
    　　
    　　篆刻家，牙石竹根，无不捉刀，唯玉章坚硬，辄见之生畏。或曰：“蟾蜍肪涂之，软如蜡。金刚石钻之，烂如泥。”试之，均不验。歙有方生雪蓬，刀法得家传，然刻玉，亦深恨不能得利器。偶游扬州，市上见骨董家有古铁一方，苔花斑剥，隐有篆文，较以秦尺，长五寸许，宽一寸许，知为古物，悄以青蚨二百，购之归。试削磷玉，纷纷落，然笨拙无锋芒规角，不能刻。悬针趸尾，就石磨砺，石损而铁无恙。送诸冶师，改铁笔，熔炉中三日，如红莲花瓣，取出火，即坚，不能剖，不能卷。遍询冶师，皆不知其原与诀。

    Explanation

    274

    Ancient Iron Sword
    　　
    A seal carver, skilled in carving ivory, stone, and bamboo, found that jade seals were particularly hard and daunting to carve. Some said, "Apply toad fat to it, and it will be soft like wax. Drill it with diamond, and it will be as soft as mud." He tried both methods, but neither worked. In She County, there was a man named Fang Shengxuepang, whose knife skills were passed down through the family. However, when carving jade, he deeply regretted not having a sharp tool. Once he traveled to Yangzhou, where he saw an antique dealer with an ancient iron block, covered in moss and lichen spots, with hidden seal script characters. Measuring it with an ancient Qin ruler, it was about five inches long and one inch wide, and he knew it was an ancient artifact. He secretly bought it for 200 copper coins and took it back home. He tried to cut phosphorus jade with it, and the jade fell in pieces, but the iron was clumsy and lacked sharp edges, making it unsuitable for carving. He hung it like a needle and ground it against a stone, damaging the stone but leaving the iron unharmed. He sent it to a metallurgist to transform it into a writing tool, melting it in a furnace for three days. It looked like red lotus petals, and when removed from the fire, it became hard, unable to be cut or bent. He consulted many metallurgists, but none of them knew its origin or method.

    275

    旋游苏台，访之久，闻人云：“余杭西湖边有仲叟，世传铸刀剑，或有秘密法。”生访之，果有叟，须眉如霜雪，温雅若儒生。以铁示之，并告以故，求玉成。叟曰：“不意垂暮年，尚能寓眼，眼福不浅哉！”曰：“是究何物？”曰：“此昆吾古剑头也。仆之祖若父曾言之，而未能见。仆马齿加长，见仅第一次。成就自有法，然工值须朱提二百金方可。”生以值太昂，出三十金，不可，再加一倍，仍不可，笑曰：“此宝物也，五金之精，凝结于此。又入土久，得山川秀，已通灵；炼得法，可得铁笔三枝，不假雕琢，一镌石，较之泛常钢器已无敌，二镌铜铁之属，三镌晶玉之属，如刀斫木，其指挥如意，真无上品；售一即千金，区区工值，尚以为昂乎？”生怒携之去。

    Explanation

    275

    The protagonist toured Suzhou, inquiring for a long time, and heard someone say, "There is an old man named Zhong near the West Lake in Yuhang, traditionally known for forging swords and knives, perhaps possessing a secret method." The protagonist visited him, and indeed found an old man with beard and eyebrows as white as frost and snow, gentle and refined like a scholar. He showed the old man the iron and explained the reason, requesting his help. The old man said, "Who would have thought that in my old age, I could still see, what a great eye fortune!" He asked, "What exactly is this?" The old man replied, "This is the head of an ancient sword from Kunwu. My ancestors and father spoke of it, but I never saw it. My years have increased, and I have only seen it once. There is a method to achieve it, but the labor cost requires two hundred gold pieces of Zhu." The protagonist thought the price was too high and offered thirty gold pieces, which was not acceptable. He then doubled the amount, but it was still not acceptable. The old man smiled and said, "This is a treasure, the essence of five metals, condensed here. Having been buried for a long time, it has absorbed the beauty of the mountains and rivers, and has become spiritual; by refining it, one can obtain three iron pens, without any carving or polishing, one can carve stone, surpassing ordinary steel tools, two can carve copper and iron, three can carve crystal and jade, like a knife cutting wood, it is perfectly controllable, truly a supreme quality; selling one would fetch a thousand gold pieces, a small labor cost, is it still considered too high?" The protagonist, angry, took it away.

    276

    曰：“郎君莫错过，仆衰老，将就木，倘过此，则茫茫天壤，永无能辨者。其负此宝物乎！”生不听，竟去，奔驰三年，迄无一遇，不得已，仍往觅叟，愿如数为酬。叟曰：“郎君既去，又何必来？论倔强，当见绝。然仆欲为人间多一宝，盍先以金来，明晨坐视，炊许时即成。”生晨即携金往，先兑与叟，叟邀生坐肆门品茶，童子开炉，炎炎甚炽，叟亦坐，与生闲话。倏一女丐来，污秽非常，面垢盈掬。叟呼丐坐矮几上，酬青蚨二百，愿代为栉发。丐得钱，亦不问云何，听之。叟高坐，袖出小梳，为丐栉之。发垢已两捧，遣丐去。旋又呼别丐，如法为之，垢更多。

    Explanation

    276

    The old man said, "Young master, do not miss this opportunity. I am old and about to die. If you pass this chance, then in the vast universe, there will be no one who can ever discern the value of this treasure. Should you take it with you?" The young man did not listen and left, traveling for three years without encountering anyone or anything. Eventually, he had no choice but to return to seek out the old man, willing to pay whatever was required. The old man said, "Since you have already left, why come back? Your stubbornness should have been enough to discourage you. However, I wish to add one more treasure to the world. Why don't you bring the gold first? Sit and watch tomorrow morning, and it will be done in a short while." The next morning, the young man brought the gold and exchanged it with the old man. The old man invited the young man to sit by the shop door and enjoy tea. A servant boy lit the stove, which burned brightly. The old man sat down and chatted with the young man. Suddenly, a beggar woman came, extremely filthy with dirt covering her face. The old man called for the beggar to sit on a low stool, gave her two hundred coins, and asked her to comb her hair. The beggar received the money and did not ask any questions, simply accepting it. The old man sat high and took out a small comb from his sleeve, combing the beggar's hair. The dirt from her hair filled two handfuls, and he sent her away. Soon, he called another beggar and did the same, and the dirt was even more abundant.

    277

    已乃对炉端坐，口喃喃若勒勒者多时，涂垢于铁，就火灼，灼复涂，涂复灼，如是十余次，垢尽矣，铁忽放五色光，蓦作奇响。视之，已判为三，端正条直，取出，略锻炼，即为镌文；一曰“切玉”，二曰“断金”，三曰“镌云”。又出红石如玛瑙，磨须臾，即湛湛如碧芙蓉，利刃也。叟拂拭付生，曰：“愿郎君世世珍藏，勿堕勿弃。”生携归，急试玉之至老者，无坚不入。遂仍返竹西，日卖篆玉章，一字银一两，索刻者户外履常满，由此大富。惜咸丰三年，匪至城陷，生且不知何所归矣。懊侬氏曰：炊米之杪，断戟同沉；绕指之柔，敝帚谁惜。天下不完之物，岂尽废材耶？要在无正法眼藏耳。噫！薛烛不逢，张华已渺；茫茫天壤，知己何人！

    Explanation

    277

    He then sat facing the furnace, muttering to himself for a long time. He coated the iron with a substance, heated it, and then coated it again, repeating this process over ten times. When the dirt was completely removed, the iron suddenly emitted a colorful light and made a strange sound. Upon inspection, it had been divided into three parts, each straight and orderly. He took it out, slightly refined it, and then engraved text on it; the first inscription read "Cutting Jade," the second "Breaking Gold," and the third "Engraving Clouds." He then took out a red stone resembling agate, ground it briefly, and it shone like a green lotus, becoming a sharp blade. The old man wiped it clean and handed it to the young man, saying, "May you treasure this for generations, do not lose or discard it." The young man took it home and immediately tested it on the hardest jade, finding that nothing could withstand it. He then returned to Zhuxi, selling engraved jade seals daily, earning one silver piece per character, and became wealthy due to high demand. Unfortunately, during the third year of the Xianfeng era, bandits attacked and captured the city, and the young man's fate remained unknown. The commentator lamented: "In the midst of cooking rice, even a broken spear sinks; a sword that is as soft as silk, who would value a broken broom? Is everything in the world that is incomplete truly useless? It all depends on having the right eye to see its value. Alas! If only薛烛 had not been encountered, and张华 had not disappeared; in this vast world, who is a true understanding friend?"

    278

    丧事演剧
    　　
    　　某邑某宦，以进士宰于粤，迎养尊甫某翁。翁殁，宦扶榇回乡里。时伊伯叔均科甲，作显官，势甚炽。故灵之回也，盛夸耀，驺从卤薄排列长三里。里之绅衿无老稚，咸公服出城迎。次日领拜，夜夕行招魂礼。衔牌伞盖，魂轿香亭，悉假城隍出巡灯会上五色琉璃者易字为之。而各家又出两明角灯，挑诸竿，遣仆持之前驱；龙虎狮象灯，又杂出于卤薄中；火树银花，远望若火城。名为招魂，实则张灯。鼓乐喧阗，冠钗云集，自宵达旦，缓缓绕街行。各家又设祭筵，摆供献，金玉花草，异宝奇珍，香风扑鼻。

    Explanation

    278

    Performing Plays at a Funeral

    In a certain county, an official, who had passed the imperial examinations and served as an administrator in Guangdong, returned his elderly father, Mr. So-and-so, to their hometown for care. After Mr. So-and-so's passing, the official carried the coffin back to his hometown. At that time, his uncles and aunts were all successful scholars and held prominent positions, and their influence was very powerful. Therefore, when the spirit was returning, there was great show and display, with a retinue and ceremonial array stretching for three miles. All the local gentry, regardless of age, dressed in official robes and came out of the city to greet. The next day, they led the way in paying respects, and at night, they performed the soul-summoning ceremony. Banners, umbrellas, and canopies, as well as the sedan chair for the spirit and the pavilion for incense, were all adorned with inscribed characters on the five-colored glass lamps used during the city god's procession. Each household also brought out two bright horn-shaped lamps, carried on poles, with servants holding them in front. Lanterns shaped like dragons, tigers, lions, and elephants were also mixed into the retinue. From afar, the scene looked like a city of fire, with "flame trees and silver flowers." Although named as a soul-summoning ceremony, it was actually a lantern festival. Drums and music resounded, and crowds of people with hats and hairpins gathered, from night until dawn, slowly circling the streets. Each household also set up sacrificial feasts, arranged offerings, and presented precious metals, jade, flowers, and plants, rare treasures and exotic jewels, all of which were fragrant.

    279

    魂轿到门，主人拜，宦答拜，拜已，优伶唱戏文，以媚亡者，名曰献曲。正哗乱间，突一某茂才盛服挤堕路旁厕，几没顶上，大呼救，人援之出，臭味差池矣。一市大哗，友方掩鼻慰藉，而茂才顾仆干急，曰：“愚才！速取冠带来，这一跪三叩礼，是万不能少者。”友有旁观冷眼者。唯闻鼓吹声，人马杂沓声，孝子假哭声，僧道梵呗声，步履声，茂才着急声，友人慰劳声，路人相戒声，声有不同。迎归，悬太翁像于后堂，客与主人下及执事奴仆，均掷去白衣冠，更吉服，抚掌欢跃，曰：“太翁归也！”开筵款客，堂下演剧，须臾五色脸登场，金鼓大震，客有题其额云：“吊者大悦，”联云：“吊者在门，贺者在室；哀不可过，乐不可支。”

    Explanation

    279

    The spirit carriage arrived at the door, and the host bowed, to which the officiant returned the bow. The performers then sang theatrical texts to please the deceased, a ritual known as "献曲." In the midst of the commotion, a certain scholar in formal attire accidentally fell into a roadside toilet, nearly drowning, and cried for help. People pulled him out, and he was covered in foul odors. The friend covered his nose to avoid the smell and comforted the scholar. The scholar's servant was in a hurry, and the scholar exclaimed, "Foolish scholar! Quickly fetch the hat and sash. This ritual of kneeling and bowing three times is absolutely essential." Some onlookers watched with a cold eye. The only sounds heard were the music, the noise of people and horses, the fake crying of the mourners, the Buddhist chants, the footsteps, the scholar's anxiety, the friend's consolation, and the warnings from passersby. The spirit was welcomed back, and the portrait of the deceased was hung in the back hall. The guests, the host, and the servants all threw away their mourning clothes and changed into festive attire, clapping and cheering, saying, "The great old man has returned!" A banquet was held to entertain the guests, and a performance was staged. Soon, actors with colorful makeup appeared, and the drums and gongs sounded loudly. One guest inscribed on the wall, "Those who mourn are delighted," and wrote a couplet: "Those who mourn are at the door, those who celebrate are in the room; sorrow should not be excessive, joy should not be overwhelming."

    280

    谷於菟
    　　
    　　明季青鲁山中，尝有虎患。有山家小女子，年十二，携斧入山，采樵以助炊。偶失足，坠山谷中，下皆叶，得不死。然上视壁立百余仞，无阶梯，高声呼救，继以哀泣，终无应者。女视东壁有洞，内空阔，若夏屋，伏两乳虎，驯若猫犬。女至虎窟，愈怖，知必死，乐与乳虎嬉。夕照坠崦嵫，腥风突起，虎母归，见女始大惊，继见女抱乳虎于怀，嘻嘻了无怖。又瞠目良久，即坐引乳虎哺。哺已，将眠，女叩拜曰：“儿蒙大王怜我，不杀我，尚能分乳救我饥乎？”虎凝思，又良久，颔首若肯。女即逡巡就虎食，倦即眠虎颔下。明晨，虎母舐乳虎，兼以舌轻舐女面，然后跃出。至晚归，衔果饵置女侧。女笑舞，虎母意亦甚乐。月余，乳虎渐长成，虎母遽负之出洞。女大嚎，虎俯瞰，又良久，重复跃下，负女于背，一跃而升高处，女于斯时庆再生也。

    Explanation

    280

    The Tiger Girl

    During the late Ming Dynasty, in the green mountains of Lu, there was once a period of tiger attacks. A young girl from a mountain village, aged twelve, carried an axe into the mountains to cut firewood to help with cooking. Accidentally losing her footing, she fell into a valley, where the bottom was all leaves, and she survived. However, looking up, she saw a cliff standing over a hundred ren high with no stairs, and she shouted for help loudly, followed by crying in sorrow, but no one answered. The girl noticed a cave on the east wall, which was spacious inside, like a summer house, with two suckling tigers lying down, tame like cats and dogs. Upon arriving at the tiger den, the girl became even more terrified, knowing she would surely die, but she enjoyed playing with the suckling tigers. As the evening sun set behind the mountain, a foul wind suddenly arose, and the tiger mother returned. Seeing the girl, she was greatly surprised at first, then saw the girl holding the suckling tigers in her arms, smiling and showing no fear. The tiger stared for a long time, then immediately sat down and let the suckling tigers nurse. After nursing, the girl was about to sleep, and she knelt and said, "I have been favored by the great king, not killed, and can you still share your milk to save my hunger?" The tiger pondered for a long time again, then nodded as if agreeing. The girl then hesitantly approached the tiger to eat, and when tired, she slept under the tiger's chin. The next morning, the tiger mother licked the suckling tigers and lightly licked the girl's face with her tongue, then jumped out. Returning in the evening, she carried fruit and placed it beside the girl. The girl laughed and danced, and the tiger mother also seemed very happy. After a month, the suckling tigers gradually grew up, and the tiger mother suddenly carried them out of the cave. The girl cried out loudly, and the tiger looked down, then jumped down again, carried the girl on her back, and jumped up to a high place. At this time, the girl celebrated her rebirth.

    281

    虎引女至通衢，女拜辞，虎犹回顾频频而复去。女抵家，见翁媪方手之舞之，足之蹈之，历历述遇虎得生状，翁媪曰：“嘻！安有遇虎反生者耶？是必为虎食，死为伥，归惑人，将引全家葬虎腹。此伥为厉也，岂得为吾女！”女号哭再三，辩莫能白，因闭之室，不与以餐，女转饿将毙，号救亦无应者，力竭声嘶，待毙而已。翁媪夜同梦一黄衣婆子来，怒目视曰：“汝女即吾女也，若饿毙，当杀汝一家！”惊醒，觉怒吼声犹震林木间也。至是始释女囚。女自服虎乳，长而貌益艳，有勇力，少年将军某，闻而聘之，屡屡助战功，封夫人。懊侬氏曰：女不死于虎，几死于骨肉，可为奇矣。嗟乎！古之孤臣孽子，血凝赐剑，祸伏寝门。山鬼朝游，岸有行吟之客；妖狐夜泣，庭有待罪之人。沉郁悲凉，有至死而不能见谅于君父；恐山鬼闻之，亦当为之不平。噫！安得千万亿白额，遍为梦中人饶舌耶？

    Explanation

    281

    The tiger led the girl to a crossroad, where she bowed and bid farewell. The tiger looked back repeatedly before leaving. Upon arriving home, the girl saw her grandparents clapping their hands and dancing with joy, recounting her miraculous survival from the tiger. The grandparents exclaimed, "How can anyone survive an encounter with a tiger? She must have been eaten by it and turned into a伥 (a supernatural being), returning to deceive us and leading our entire family to be devoured by the tiger. This伥 is a malevolent spirit; how could she be our daughter!" The girl cried repeatedly, but her pleas went unheeded. They locked her in a room, withholding food. She grew weak from hunger and was on the verge of death, crying for help to no avail. Exhausted and hoarse, she awaited her fate. That night, both grandparents had the same dream of an old woman in yellow clothes, who glared at them and said, "Your daughter is my daughter. If she dies of hunger, I will kill your whole family!" They awoke to the sound of an angry roar still shaking the trees. Only then did they release the girl. She drank tiger milk, grew up, and became even more beautiful, possessing great strength. A young general heard of her and married her, frequently aiding him in battles, earning her enfeoffment as a lady. The commentator懊侬 said, "The girl did not die at the hands of the tiger, but nearly died at the hands of her own family. What a strange fate!" Alas! In ancient times, loyal ministers and their families faced the congealed blood of their swords, with disaster lurking at their bedroom doors. Mountain spirits roamed in the morning, and there were wandering poets by the shore; supernatural foxes wept at night, and there were people awaiting punishment in the courtyard. Deeply melancholic and sad, there were those who could not be forgiven by their sovereigns and fathers even until death. Even the mountain spirits, upon hearing this, would be unjustly treated. Ah! How could there be millions of white-tigers, all speaking for the people in their dreams?

    282

    卷二
    　　
    　　木孩童
    　　
    　　诸妹子，不知何处人，少无赖，嗜饮好博，日渐困窘，乃以后庭诱市井儿与之游。年二十有五，色衰，人皆唾弃，而饮博如故。无已，唯稍稍学穿窬。夏日戴草笠，行陇亩间，瞰禾苗深处，若有男女足微动，潜诣其侧，则果有野合者在焉，弃紫白罗纱衫裤于左，裸抱正浓。诸攫而胁之，男女咸起，欲遁，索衣不与，伏地哀泣。诸视其女颇有色，曰：“若肯与我依样云云，即与尔。”女不肯，男劝之，曰：“无奈何，姑从之，免播扬耳。”女含羞与合，男伺坐于侧，局促难堪。事讫，又胁其男曰：“尔太便宜，当罄腰橐与我作赌本，始与尔衣。”男云：“私约到此，实未携得分文，奈何？”既而凝思久之，曰：“仆虽无持赠，然有一处指君去，可得大财。

    Explanation

    282

    Book Two
    Wooden Child
    There was a woman named Zhu Meizi, whose origins are unknown. In her youth, she was unruly and without shame, addicted to drinking and gambling, which gradually led to her impoverishment. To survive, she lured street children into her company using her back garden. At the age of twenty-five, her beauty faded, and people began to reject her, yet she continued to indulge in drinking and gambling. Unable to continue this lifestyle, she resorted to petty theft. During the summer, she wore a straw hat and walked through the fields, observing the deep parts of the rice paddies. If she saw slight movements of male and female feet, she would stealthily approach them. Indeed, there were people having sex in the wild, and they left behind purple and white silk robes and trousers on the left side. The man and woman were naked and embracing intensely. Zhu seized them and threatened them. Both rose, wanting to escape, but she refused to give them their clothes, causing them to crouch on the ground and weep. Seeing that the woman was still quite attractive, Zhu said, "If you agree to do as I say, I will give you your clothes." The woman refused, but the man persuaded her, saying, "There is no choice; you must comply to avoid scandal." The woman, feeling ashamed, agreed to join her. The man sat beside them, feeling awkward and uncomfortable. Afterward, Zhu threatened the man again, saying, "You got off too easily; you must empty your pockets and give me the money for gambling before I return your clothes." The man replied, "We came to an agreement, but I did not bring any money with me. What should I do?" After pondering for a long time, he said, "Although I have nothing to give you, I can show you a place where you can find great wealth."

    283

    ”问何所，曰：“去此而南，林中有古精蓝，破废无住持。第入，瞰中殿，地有卵石数十枚，堆作一团，君即拾庙外累累者之一添其上，龛后僵卧，待少顷有十余男子来瓜分阿堵，君出数石，自有分。”诸曰：“诺。”掷衣与之，男女着而急窜，绕过古墓道，突不见。趋入林，果有庙，果无僧众，而地果有石子。遂如其所说，卧而俟。及昏暗，有男子二人进庙，数石子得十二枚，讶曰：“何故多一石子？岂有新来不速之客乎？”旋又陆续数男子至，至皆豹头环眼，腰下横刀。先来者与客寒暄，且告以异，众曰：“吾辈已有十一，若添一佳宾，成阑干数矣，曷搜取？”诸自龛后倔然起，出与为礼。众曰：“子既来，缘也，曷同去打黑风？得财当分赠，然须订盟共生死。”诸曰：“诺，固所愿也。”乃于神前沥血为誓，祷拜讫，众有携来酒肴，团坐饱啖。

    Explanation

    283

    He asked where, and was told, "Go south from here, there is an ancient temple in the woods, it is in ruins with no one in charge. Enter, look into the central hall, the ground has dozens of pebbles, piled in a heap, you should pick up one of the many pebbles outside the temple and add it to the pile, lie stiffly behind the shrine, wait a moment, and then more than ten men will come to divide this (the pebbles), you will get a share." They said, "Yes." They threw their clothes to them, men and women put them on and rushed away quickly, circling around the ancient tomb path, suddenly disappearing. Rush into the woods, there is indeed a temple, there are indeed no monks, and the ground indeed has pebbles. Then as he said, lie down and wait. When it gets dark, two men enter the temple, count the pebbles and get twelve, they are surprised, "Why is there one extra pebble? Is there a new, unexpected guest?" Soon more men arrive one after another, they all have fierce features and swords. The first arrivals greet the guest, and tell of the unusual, the group says, "We already have eleven, if we add one good guest, it will make a perfect number, why not search for it?" He suddenly rises from behind the shrine, comes out and greets them. The group says, "You have come, it is fate, why not go together to fight the black wind? If we get money, we will share it, but we must swear an oath to share life and death." He says, "Yes, it is indeed what I wish." Then they take an oath by spilling blood before the gods, after praying and bowing, the group brings wine and food, sit together and eat heartily.

    284

    众问诸曰：“此等生涯，子见惯乎？抑初学乎？”曰：“不敢欺，若逾墙钻穴，弟所优为，余非所知也。”曰：“如是亦佳。子到彼第，先入探虚实，启门户，随出伏暗处，防外来。”曰：“善。”夜静，听村柝转三更，众曰：“是其时矣。”各执坚利，与诸以械，蜂拥鱼贯行，逾数叠岗阜，至一极大孤村，重扉严，灯火全无，而左右皆山岩水沼，绝少比邻，然舍宇鳞接，知为大家。众曰：“新来同盟，可先入瞰之。”诸乃逾数重垣，直达内寝，各室皆黑，唯西厢窗牖时露灯光，鹭伏蛇行，以唾湿窗上纸，微破，睨之。一四十岁半老佳人，高坐床第，摇招凉；一二十余岁佳人，着轻碧纱裤裆，对镜匀铅粉，卸晚妆；一十七岁小女子，发垂垂，尤艳绝。三人皆弱不胜衣，临风欲化者。小女子抱小孩童尺许，白如雪，莹如玉，呱呱啼不辍。女子且弄且怨曰：“连曰阿官甚不乖，夜深犹呱呱不肯睡。

    Explanation

    284

    The crowd asked Zhu, saying, "Are you familiar with this kind of life or profession? Or are you just learning it?" Zhu replied, "I dare not deceive you. If it comes to climbing over walls and digging through holes, what I excel at; the rest I do not know." The crowd said, "Like this is also good. When you arrive at their house, first go in to check the situation, open the doors and windows, follow and hide in the dark places, and guard against outsiders." Zhu said, "Okay." When the night was quiet and the sound of the village watchmen's drums indicated the third watch, the crowd said, "It is the right time now." Each holding a strong and sharp weapon, and Zhu with his tools, they moved like bees swarming and fish swimming in a line, crossing several layers of hills and mounds, arriving at a very large isolated village with heavy doors and strict guards, no lights at all, surrounded by mountains, rocks, and marshes, with very few neighbors, yet houses connected like fish scales, knowing it belonged to a big family. The crowd said, "New allies, can go in first to observe." Zhu then climbed over several walls, reaching the inner bedroom, where all rooms were dark, only the west wing occasionally showing light through the windows. Moving like a heron crouching and a snake creeping, he used saliva to moisten the paper on the window, slightly breaking it, and looked at it from the side. There was a forty-year-old semi-aged beautiful woman sitting high on the bed, waving to cool herself; a woman in her twenties wearing light green silk trousers, applying makeup in front of the mirror, taking off her evening makeup; a seventeen-year-old girl with hair hanging down, especially beautiful. All three were so thin they could not bear the weight of their clothes, as if they would dissolve in the wind. The young girl held a child about a foot long, white as snow, bright as jade, continuously crying. The woman played with it while complaining, "The child is very naughty, even at night, it still cries and won't sleep."

    285

    ”对镜人笑曰：“渠是姑姑娇惯，埋怨谁耶？”床上人曰：“将阿官来与我。”果送至床上，老幼共调笑。对镜人忽唏嘘，曰：“莲姑莫怨阿官，令伯仲至今不归，险不教你两嫂嫂孤寂煞。幸有襁褓物斗笑耍也。”床上人曰：“莲姑，两哥哥不在家，莫贪耍，忘却门户。”小女子笑曰：“妹子痴耶？深野孤村，妹时时怕有贼觑。夕照衔山时，已重重门闭矣。”对镜人曰：“倘有恶人来，我妯娌落得一死，未免苦妹及阿官耳。”女子急摇手，曰：“莫道莫道，嫂何必故作险语，令人怕。约再迟二三日，大哥纵耽延，二哥亦断无不遄回也。灯花结如红豆子，有征验矣。”床上人曰：“小妮子，只记得二哥，不想念大哥耶？今夕，盍仍是三人同榻，俾胆子稍壮。”女子笑曰：“二嫂嫂睡态不雅相，动辄翘纤足压人肩头。”对镜人妆亦竟，笑骂移时，掩关移灯，遂下帷同寝，长者鼾声渐酣，孩童啼声亦止。

    Explanation

    285

    The person looking into the mirror smiled and said, "He is spoiled by his aunt, who should we blame?" The person on the bed said, "Bring Aiguan to me." Indeed, he was brought to the bed, and the old and young laughed together. The person looking into the mirror suddenly sighed and said, "Lotus Sister, don't blame Aiguan; your uncles have not returned home, nearly making your two sisters-in-law lonely to the point of despair. Fortunately, there is something in the cradle to laugh and play with." The person on the bed said, "Lotus Sister, since your two brothers are not at home, don't play too much and forget the doors and windows." The little girl laughed and said, "Am I being foolish? In this remote and isolated village, I am always afraid of thieves. When the sun sets behind the mountains, the doors will be closed tightly." The person looking into the mirror said, "If a bad person comes, my sisters-in-law would rather die, sparing my sister and Aiguan." The girl quickly shook her hand and said, "Don't say that, don't say that; why do you deliberately speak such dangerous words, making people afraid? It will be about two or three more days; even if Big Brother is delayed, Second Brother will surely return soon. The lamp flower has formed like red beans, which is a sign." The person on the bed said, "Little girl, you only remember Second Brother, don't miss Big Brother? Tonight, why don't we still sleep together as three, so that our courage might be a bit stronger?" The girl laughed and said, "Second Sister-in-law's sleeping posture is not elegant, she often lifts her slender foot and presses on my shoulder." The person looking into the mirror finished dressing and laughed and scolded for a while, then closed the door and moved the lamp, and they all went to bed together. The elder began to snore deeply, and the child's crying also stopped.

    286

    诸窥瞰多时，心喜，急循厅事出，欲拔关，则门皆暗闩，机键牢固，无已，仍逾垣出，缕述所以。众亦喜，遂嘱诸在外，各皆登屋诣内，飞行鸳瓦上，曲折数道，视灯光，耸身跃落，伏诸窥处窥之，果闻床上有妇孺酣眠声。满屋箱箧，扃甚丰。雌物无能，藐忽放胆，拔刀破窗入，灯光大明。掀帐视之，则空无一人；举箱箧启之，皆空无一物。遍屋穷搜，绝无人迹。知有变，仍跃登屋，甫过楼角，则先行者无故堕地死；陆续进者二人亦然。其余见楼角有刀光，急转而之北，视下有小圃，拟自彼处遁。突屋后飞出一人，乃对镜人也，手起刀落，连歼者又五人，仅剩其四；茫茫无所之，大呼跃落庭院，则年长者，已自后刀落，断其首矣。

    286

    Many of them peered for a long time, and they were pleased in their hearts. They quickly followed the hall out, wanting to pull the bolt, but the doors were all secretly bolted, with mechanisms firmly locked. Having no other choice, they still climbed over the wall to escape, narrating the reasons in detail. The others were also pleased, and they then instructed those outside, each climbing onto the roof to enter, flying across the twin tiles, taking several winding paths, seeing the lights, leaping down with a crouched body, crouching at the peeping places to observe. They indeed heard the sounds of women and children sleeping soundly. The entire room was filled with chests and boxes, with locks very abundant. The women were incapable, carelessly and boldly, pulling out their swords and breaking through the window to enter, the lights becoming very bright. They lifted the curtain to look, then there was no one inside; they lifted the chests and boxes to open them, all were empty with nothing inside. They thoroughly searched the entire room, with no traces of people. Knowing something had changed, they still leaped back onto the roof, just as they passed the corner of the building, then the ones who went ahead fell to the ground and died; the two who followed also fell to the ground and died. The others saw the glint of a knife at the corner of the building, quickly turning north, seeing a small garden below, intending to escape from there. Suddenly, a person flew out from behind the house, it was the person who was facing the mirror, they raised their hand and the knife fell, killing five more people in succession, only four remained; they were lost and had nowhere to go, shouting and leaping into the courtyard, then the eldest one, had already had the knife fall from behind, cutting off their head.

    287

    诸在外痴守，至东方将白，而众仍不出，听之亦无声息，意众俱得采，各拥妇眠，满腹妒念，登墙外高树潜窥，则众皆身首异处，无一完躯。大惊而下，欲进不敢，欲退又以同盟之谊不忍，乃潜伏草堆中，姑窥其变。少顷日出，闻小女子启庄门，两妇人亦均出外，四望笑曰：“恶贼无故来送死，又欲累老娘亲手葬，渠好侥幸也！”小女子问：“葬何处？”或曰水边，或曰柳林下，商量多时，遂定为南岗头。

    Explanation

    287

    Those outside foolishly waited until dawn was approaching, yet the crowd still did not emerge. Listening, there was no sound. They thought everyone had gotten what they wanted, each holding their wives in sleep. Filled with jealousy, they climbed onto a high tree outside the wall to secretly look, and found that the crowd was all decapitated, not one complete body. Greatly shocked, they descended, wanting to advance but not daring to, and not wanting to retreat because of the bond of the alliance. They hid in the grass pile, temporarily observing the changes. A short while later, the sun rose, and they heard a young woman opening the gate of the mansion. The two women also came out. Looking around and laughing, they said, "The wicked thief came to deliver his own death without reason, and also wanted to burden the old lady to bury him by hand. He is so lucky!" The young woman asked, "Where to bury?" Some said by the water, others said under the willow forest. After much discussion, they finally decided on the south hilltop.

    288

    小女子果携锄先去，两妇人陆续抬尸出，血涔涔，染畦町。诸心惨然。及见其抬尽，心计娘子军全在野田，室内尚有婴儿，何不潜入杀却，为同盟报仇。意决，乃逾垣入，拾地上刀，奔进绣闼，视儿尚仰卧，劓息咻咻，遽挥以刀，竟两段矣；讵声如破柝，视之，盖木头雕成也。大惊，欲反遁，突一白发老妪，龙钟非常，以杖支门，问：“子何来？敢入人家闺阁，且杀人家孩童乎？”藐其老，甫拟以刃，妪笑曰：“子欺我衰迈耶？若以杖击汝，终非好手。”骈二指击诸肩，痛如中斧质，不觉伏地哀号。

    Explanation

    288

    The young woman indeed left first, carrying her hoe. Two women followed, carrying the corpse out one after another; blood dripped and stained the fields. Everyone's heart was filled with sorrow. When they saw the corpse carried away completely, the protagonist thought that all the female warriors were in the fields, and there was still a baby inside the house. Why not creep in and kill the baby to avenge the alliance? Determined, she climbed over the wall and entered, picked up a knife from the ground, rushed into the ornate entrance, and saw the child still lying on his back, breathing heavily. She immediately waved the knife, and the child ended up in two pieces. But the sound was like a broken drum. Looking at it, it turned out to be a wooden carving. Greatly surprised, she wanted to retreat, but suddenly encountered an old woman with white hair, who was extremely unsteady. The old woman used a staff to prop the door and asked, "Where do you come from? Dare you enter someone's inner chamber and kill their child?" Disregarding her old age, the protagonist just intended to use the knife. The old woman laughed and said, "Do you mock my old and feeble state? If I hit you with the staff, I am not a good hand." She joined two fingers and hit the protagonist's shoulder, causing pain like being struck by an axe on a block. Unconsciously, the protagonist fell to the ground and cried out in pain.

    289

    视妪后，又立一婢，抱孩童来，方是昨宵英物。两妇携姑旋归，妪婢争告之，妇曰：“噫！如此物事，亦学作贼，真辱抹煞人！汝在草堆中，贼瞳灼灼，以我未曾寓目耶？姑念汝为手足报仇，尚有义气，免污吾刃，且就缧绁，俟吾夫回，再发落汝！”言已，婢付孩于小女子，出黑索，绾诸颈，若牵犬羊。扃之斗室，日与两餐，颇甘旨，尚无苦。至三日，忽闻女子喜曰：“大哥二哥回矣！”又闻妇笑曰：“郎君又携得妹夫回矣！”旋闻男子劳妇与妹曰：“吾兄弟远游，子等杀得好贼！”言已，均轩渠，一家宴燕，笙管嗷嘈，中夜方止。次日，始闻大郎坐中堂，命婢引诸出，伏阶下，若囚之就决者，觳觫非常。潜视堂上三人，皆冠玉美男子，唯大郎有须，詈曰：“吾道谁，原是市上无赖诸妹子耶？前宵之举，罪不在子，但不应图灭人后，此等便非丈夫，宜其雄而雌也。

    Explanation

    289

    After seeing the woman, a maid appeared carrying a child, who was the same remarkable creature seen the previous night. The two women took their mother-in-law back home, and the maid and woman vied to tell her about the incident. The woman said, "Ah! Such a thing, even learning to steal, truly disgraces and diminishes one! You were in the haystack, with the thief's eyes glaring at you, did you not notice me? I will spare your life since you avenged your siblings and still have a sense of righteousness, but you will be bound in chains. Wait for my husband's return, and then we will decide your fate!" After saying this, the maid handed the child to a young girl, took out a black rope, and tied it around the neck, as if leading a dog or sheep. They locked them in a small room and provided two meals a day, which were quite tasty and not too harsh. On the third day, a girl suddenly said happily, "The eldest brother and the second brother have returned!" Then they heard the woman laugh and say, "The young master has also brought back his brother-in-law!" Soon after, a man praised the woman and her sister-in-law, saying, "Our brothers have been away, but you have killed the thief well!" After saying this, they all laughed and enjoyed a feast, with music and singing until the middle of the night. The next day, they heard the eldest son sit in the main hall, ordering the maid to bring them out. They knelt at the steps, like prisoners awaiting execution, trembling with fear. Peeking at the three men on the dais, they were all handsome and noble, except the eldest son, who had a beard. He scolded, "Who did I think I was dealing with, the scoundrels from the market? The action of last night, the blame does not lie with you, but you should not plan to destroy someone's family, such behavior is not that of a man. It is appropriate that they are male but act like females."

    290

    且吾友盗邻妇野合，干尔何事？尔胁淫其偶，又诈取其金。此等恶顽童，宇宙间真一刻容不得！”言已，掷刀下，令自死，诸哀叩不已，满堂皆失笑，二郎曰：“姑贷尔命。”喝左右健儿，且成就渠，作留半释迦，众曰：“然。”两健儿果缚诸于柱，出薄刃如纸，脱布裤，宫之；血淋漓满地，昏而复苏，健儿笑敷以药，痛顿止，大郎笑曰：“这般方成个妹子也。”掷数金与之，立命人送出村。踯躅田间，旋走旋歇，两日始回旧处。见日前所遇男妇，遥遥视之笑。诸愤极，拾地下卵石投之，两人皆化作白狐窜去。年余，里之人家被盗窃，闻于官，诸潜告捕役，会同营弁百余人，重至旧处，则村舍全无，荒烟零落，众于草中拾一木雕孩童，诸见木上有伊旧斫刀痕仍如故云。懊侬氏曰：以盗杀贼，所谓以豹狼而戮枭獍者也。使诸妹子，若非同盟一念之义，又安得获保首领，仅为天疴已乎？龙阳小儿，身有淫具，本无用之物；二郎刑罚，可谓当矣。

    Explanation

    290

    My friend and his lover engaged in an adulterous affair with the neighbor's wife. What business is it of yours? You coerced your lover into adultery and deceivedly took her money. Such wicked and foolish children truly cannot be tolerated in the universe for even a moment!" Having said this, he threw down his knife and ordered them to die, but they continued to plead and weep. The entire hall burst into laughter. The second prince said, "I will spare your life temporarily." He ordered the strong men to complete the punishment on them, leaving them half-naked like the Buddha. The crowd agreed, "Yes." The two strong men indeed tied them to the pillars, took out thin blades like paper, removed their cloth pants, and castrated them. Blood flowed everywhere, and they fainted and then regained consciousness. The strong men laughed and applied medicine to them, and the pain immediately subsided. The first prince laughed and said, "Only then can she become a proper sister." He threw some money to them and immediately ordered someone to escort them out of the village. They stumbled through the fields, stopping and walking again, and after two days, they returned to their old place. They saw the man and woman they had encountered before, looked at them from afar, and laughed. The crowd was furious and picked up stones from the ground, throwing them at the couple. Both transformed into white foxes and fled. A year later, a family in the village was robbed, and the incident was reported to the authorities. The crowd secretly informed the capture officers, who, along with over a hundred soldiers, returned to the old place. The village houses were gone, and the scene was desolate. The crowd found a wooden carved child in the grass, and the crowd saw that the old knife marks on the wood were still there. Agnos said, "To kill a thief with a thief, as it is said, to punish a wolf and a leopard with a crow and a fox. If my sister, if not for the bond of loyalty and righteousness, how could she have escaped with her head intact, only because of divine intervention, perhaps? A boy from Longyang, who has a sexual organ, is a useless thing; the second prince's punishment can be said to be appropriate."

    291

    痴兰院主
    　　
    　　诗臣既述朱君还魂事，又述其妹朱贞女生死本末，尤足艳称。朱本巨族，其尊人负郭觅幽境，筑别墅，供吟啸。花草甚夥，牡丹一株，姚黄也，称巨擘，而兰尤富焉。园丁谷姓，其妇盆婆子，同任灌溉劳。花时游屐往来，藉卖苦茗，博蝇头利。兰之种，素心者为上，紫心者次之，并蒂者尤不数数见。墅中素心者仅蓄一本，夙珍爱。是年风光乍转，奇馨胜常。忽一夕，紫者咸化为素，又本本皆作并蒂，若夫妻蕙。三四日，旋复本质，而封姨肆虐，落英满阶。

    Explanation

    291

    The Master of Orchids
    　　
    　　The poet-official has narrated the story of Zhu Jun's return from the dead, and also the life and death of his sister, Zhu Zhen, which is particularly praiseworthy. The Zhu family was a prominent clan, and their patriarch sought a secluded place outside the city walls to build a villa for leisure and poetry. There were many flowers and plants, including a single peony, the Yao Huang variety, which was considered the best, and the orchids were especially abundant. The gardener, named Gu, and his wife, who was called Pot Mother, shared the task of watering the plants. During the flowering season, visitors came and went, buying bitter tea to make a small profit. The orchids were categorized by their hearts: pure-hearted (white) were the best, purple-hearted were second, and twin-flowered (double-headed) were especially rare. The villa had only one pure-hearted orchid, which was highly valued. That year, the scenery suddenly changed, and the fragrance was unusually strong. One night, the purple orchids all turned white, and each plant produced twin flowers, like a pair of hui (a type of orchid). After a few days, the orchids returned to their original state, but a strong wind (symbolizing misfortune) caused the petals to fall, covering the steps.

    292

    盆婆子亦有爱花癖，往告主人，意似惋惜。一夕，自邻村社饮回，扶醉踏月，经短墙外，闻墅中有女子言笑声，偷瞰之，则倚石坐者，为黄帔美妇人；席苔坐者，为缟衣美女子；左右侍者娇鬟三四辈，语细不可辨。忽闻妇曰：“小妮子弄狡狯，无怪封家婢妒欲死。”女笑曰：“儿本太憨生，偶为诸姐妹袒衷曲，不意遭奇虐，近亦自悔恨。”妇曰：“儿过矣，颠倒生物，上帝所忌，况自家形骸乎？然则封家婢饶舌，奈何？”女子闻之，掩袖汛澜，不欢而散。

    Explanation

    292

    The old woman also had a fondness for flowers. She went to inform the master, and it seemed she was expressing regret. One night, returning from a village social drinking, she staggered home under the moonlight, passing by a short wall. She heard the sound of women's laughter from a villa and peered at them secretly. There was a beautiful woman in a yellow robe sitting on a stone, and a beautiful girl in white sitting on moss. Around them were three or four young maids, their words too soft to understand. Suddenly, the woman said, "The little girl is being mischievous, no wonder the servant from the Fang family is envious to the point of death." The girl laughed and said, "I was too naive, occasionally revealing my inner feelings to my sisters, not expecting to suffer a strange torment. Recently, I have also regretted and felt ashamed." The woman replied, "You were wrong, to reverse the natural order is forbidden by the heavens,何况自己的身体呢？既然如此，那封家的婢女多嘴，怎么办？”女子听了，掩袖而泣，不欢而散。

    293

    婆子归告其夫，皆不解，由是时伺于墙外，冀穷底蕴。然仙踪杳矣。一夕正夜绩，忽闻墅中有丝竹声，泣别声，走觇之，则遥见黄帔妇，手白玉斝，斟清泉，灌素心，祝曰：“汝不自，过炫露，果为封氏劾，往堕闺秀情痴劫。当于十六年后，早证果，勿迷本性也。”祝已，洒涕不已，诸婢劝慰，始含泪入牡丹花丛，不见。婆子惊，翌告于朱之夫人，则正于昨日生一女公子，梦兰而诞，彼此互述，始审生女之为素心后身，遂名曰纫兰。

    Explanation

    293

    The old woman returned and told her husband, who did not understand. Thus, she began to watch from outside the wall, hoping to uncover the truth. However, the fairy's trace vanished. One night, as she was weaving, she suddenly heard music in the village, along with sounds of crying and bidding farewell. She ran to spy on it and saw a woman in a yellow robe from afar, holding a white jade cup, pouring clear spring water into the pure heart. The woman wished, saying, "You did not know yourself, exposing yourself too much, and indeed, you were accused by the Feng family, falling into the劫of a young lady's痴. In sixteen years, you will soon achieve enlightenment, do not lose your true nature." After the wish, she cried continuously. The maids comforted her, and she began to cry, entering the peony garden, and disappeared. The old woman was shocked and the next day told Zhu's wife, who informed her that on the previous day, she had given birth to a daughter, dreaming of orchids and giving birth. They told each other, and finally understood that the daughter was the reincarnation of Su Xin. So they named her Rui Lan.

    294

    夫人生子四女二，纫兰行五，人呼五姑，为父母所钟爱。明年，尊人茂才公捐馆，母夫人自坐丝幔，即教子女。纫兰幼颖敏，貌丽性贞，学绣之余好吟咏。夫人每语人曰：“此吾家女学士也，潇湘入梦，巾帼谪仙，惜尔翁不克见其咏絮才，为可悲耳。”女偶咏秋海棠，自云：“秋雨又秋风，苔阴瘦一丛。如何人不寐，泪洒可怜红。”母闻之，不怿曰：“是儿何语出不祥？”遂藏其卷册翰墨，专督女红，不容习咕哔。女虽遵慈训，但往往倚窗调鹦鹉，卷帘放归燕，临水数游鱼，口常喃喃，偷聆之，皆绝妙好词也。

    Explanation

    294

    The woman had four daughters and two sons, and Rinlan was the fifth child. People called her the Fifth Daughter, and she was deeply cherished by her parents. The following year, her esteemed father, the scholar-official, passed away. The mother, sitting under a silk canopy, immediately began educating the children. Rinlan was young, sharp-witted, beautiful, and virtuous. In her spare time from embroidery, she loved to recite poetry. The mother often told others, "This is my daughter, a scholar. Like a dream of the Xiang River, she is a female immortal. It's a pity that your father couldn't see her talent for poetry; it's truly a pity." Once, Rinlan recited a poem about the autumn hibiscus, saying, "Autumn rain and autumn wind, a sparse cluster in the shade of moss. How can anyone sleep, tears falling on the pitiful red." The mother, upon hearing this, was displeased and said, "What unlucky words are these?" She then hid her writings, focused on teaching embroidery, and forbade her from reciting poetry. Though Rinlan followed her mother's instructions, she often leaned against the window and played with a parrot, rolled up the curtain and let the swallows return, stood by the water and counted the fish, and often murmured to herself. Listening in secretly, all her words were exquisite poems.

    295

    顾性最好兰，见兰又娇啼不辍；墅之素心者，自女设后，即顿萎。盆婆子护以竹栏，仍勤浇灌，冀再萌耳。七岁时，早许字成都守张公子，本与女为中表兄妹行，两小无猜，曾共梨枣。至十岁时，即走匿不面。公子素羸弱，随任之蜀，鲤鱼音断。又六年，女忽持斋，朝夕稽首拜大士像，讽《心经》。母询何意，则泪涔涔焉。时正早春，半窗旭日，女拥绣被不起，泪雨湿枕簟几满。婢白于母，询之呜咽，曰：“儿不孝，将长辞阿母，负教育恩矣。

    Explanation

    295

    The girl had a fondness for orchids. When she saw them, she would cry tenderly and continuously. The orchids that had always been pure in heart suddenly withered after she set them up. The gardener protected them with bamboo enclosures and tended to them frequently, hoping they would sprout again.

    When she was seven, she was betrothed to Zhang's son, the governor of Chengdu, who was originally her cousin. They had been innocent and without suspicion of each other, having once shared sweet fruits. By the time she was ten, she began to hide and avoid meeting him. The young man was always frail and followed his official duties to Shu (Sichuan), and communication ceased like the breaking of a fish's sound.

    Another six years later, the girl suddenly began to fast, bowing her head and worshipping the Bodhisattva image morning and evening, and reciting the Heart Sutra. Her mother asked her why, and she sobbed, tears streaming down her face. It was early spring, and the morning sun shone through half the window. The girl lay in her embroidered quilt without getting up, her tears wetting the pillow and mat almost entirely. The maid reported to the mother, and when asked, she sobbed, saying, "I am ungrateful, I will soon leave my mother, betraying the kindness of my education."

    296

    幸有兄妹在，愿母勉加餐，勿以薄命女为念。”母悲曰：“儿何出此言？”泣曰：“儿昨梦张郎来，掷枯兰一枝于地，再拜辞出。儿知其必赴修文矣。”母素审公子病，闻女言，心若割，勉慰之，而女由是竟毁妆，食顿减。月余，蜀中噩耗至，公子果死。母背女哭，秘不使闻。女忽晨起，对镜理妆，问婢曰：“盆婆子来乎？”曰：“未。”又问：“墅中素心兰再生乎？”曰：“不知。”问：“亭畔黄牡丹无恙乎？”曰：“无恙。”既而解颐，曰：“来处来，去处去，自家苦问讯，诚恐封姨笑人。

    Explanation

    296

    Fortunately, there are brothers and sisters to support me; I hope Mother will eat more and not worry about me, a weakly girl.” The mother mourned and said, “Why would you say such things?” She wept and said, “I dreamed last night that Zhang Lang came, threw a withered orchid branch onto the ground, bowed twice, and then left. I know that he must have gone to serve in the imperial court.” The mother, who had always understood the illness of the young master, heard her daughter’s words and felt her heart as if it were torn apart. She comforted her daughter, but the daughter, from then on, destroyed her makeup and ate less. After more than a month, news of the young master’s death came from Shu, and indeed he had died. The mother cried behind her daughter’s back, keeping it secret so that she would not hear. Suddenly, the daughter woke up in the morning, stood before the mirror to arrange her makeup, and asked the maid, “Has the old woman come?” The maid replied, “No.” She then asked, “Has the pure-hearted orchid in the villa been reborn?” The maid said, “I don’t know.” She asked again, “Is the yellow peony by the pavilion unharmed?” The maid replied, “It is unharmed.” Afterward, she smiled and said, “Come from where you come, go to where you go; striving to inquire about oneself, truly fearing that Feng Yi would laugh at us.”

    297

    ”旋又悲涕，曰：“悔不听姚夫人语，一至于此。”婢见其或哭或啼，状类颠，急白夫人，偕兄妹至，女笑曰：“偶与婢子嬉耳，何遽惊高堂？”言已，举止如故，防遂渐疏。然每夕则扶病挑灯，缮诗稿，成一帙，题曰《别鹄吟》。稿成，泣拜其母，曰：“乞以此卷寄巴蜀，焚亡者灵前，以当同穴。”时盆婆子不知也，忽见已萎之素心，顿发旧丛，含苞怒放，香更袭人。是夜，又闻弦管呕哑，人马杂。

    Explanation

    297

    She suddenly burst into tears again, saying, "I regret not listening to Mrs. Yao's words, to such a point." The maid saw that she was either crying or whimpering, her state resembling madness, and quickly reported this to Mrs. Yao, who came with her siblings. The girl smiled and said, "I was just playing with the maid, why so suddenly alarm the elders?" After speaking, she resumed her usual behavior, and Mrs. Yao gradually became less wary. However, every evening she would support herself while ill and light a lamp, writing poetry drafts, eventually compiling them into a collection titled The Farewell to the Swans. Upon completing the collection, she wept and bowed before her mother, saying, "Please send this scroll to Bashu, to be burned before the spirit of the deceased, as if we were buried together." At that time, the old lady in the pot was unaware of this. Suddenly, she saw the withered white heart, which had bloomed again from the old cluster, with buds bursting into full bloom, the fragrance even more overpowering. That night, she heard the cacophony of pipes and strings, with people and horses mixed together.

    298

    启双柴，则见灯火若城，旌旗如云，侍从策花骢，皆古装，拥一采舆，冉冉门外过，舆中丽人，五姑纫兰也。婆子遽挽舆，呼曰：“五姑将焉往？”卤薄怒，欲加鞭笞，女曰：“莫惊他，此吾守园人。然汝来大好，烦寄语高堂，云我赴诏为天上痴兰院主，脱去三尘，毫无苦趣。姚夫人尚不及我贵。可请我母减哀思。”言已，车马如飞，倏忽不见。香风习习，彩云纷纷。明日婆子来，见张夫人，始知五姑已于昨宵化去。至今《别鹄吟》一稿，士女传诵，价重鸡林。人第赏其词章之工，初不知其节操之贞，与生死之颠末。花耶人耶？仙乎仙乎！

    Explanation

    298

    Opening the double gate, one sees lanterns as numerous as a city, flags like clouds, servants riding flower-patterned horses, all in ancient attire, escorting a splendid carriage that slowly passes outside the gate. Inside the carriage is a beautiful woman, none other than Aunt Five, Rening. An old woman suddenly stops the carriage and calls out, "Where is Aunt Five going?" The attendants become angry and want to whip her. The woman says, "Do not startle him, this is my gardener. However, your coming is a good thing. Please convey to my mother that I have been summoned to become the lady of the Heavenly Chilin Garden, having shed the three dusts, with no suffering or hardship. Lady Yao is not as noble as I am. Please ask my mother to reduce her grief." After she finishes speaking, the chariots and horses fly away, disappearing suddenly. A fragrant breeze gently blows, and colored clouds scatter. The next day, the old woman comes to see Lady Zhang, and only then does she learn that Aunt Five had already transformed away last night. Today, "The Poem of Parting from the Crane" is widely recited among scholars and women, valued as highly as in Chilin. People only appreciate the skill of her poetry, initially unaware of her noble character and the details of her life and death. Is she a flower, a person, or a fairy? Truly, she is a fairy!

    299

    沉香街
    　　
    　　江南通衢，有街名曰沉香。询土人以命名之义，云富家子，金姓名不换，蜀人，美丰姿，财雄于乡。年二十，偶阅画图，慕江南风土之美，金粉之丽，山川之秀，遂挟重资学贾。偶游于此街，经桃花门巷，忽一荔枝壳堕肩头，仰视之，珠帘绣阁，一美女子凭拦，顾之微笑，访而知为娼家，乃第十三女素娇也。钗光鬓影，魄荡神迷，遂入院。女笑迓之，肃入己之绣闼，别有洞天，茶铛沸鼎，香髜棋枰，弦索互弄，铮铮然。生惑之，几不知身之所在。旋即开筵，酒肴精绝，雏姬五六辈来，互相侑殇，或素之姐，或素之妹，叠媚之。生微吟“除却巫山不是云”句，众遂哄散。酒阑人静，解衣入帷，素出十洲春册，照谱为之，魂更销矣。由是遂假馆素娇家，不复事贸易。资耗尽，素忧之，生曰：“卿无忧，吾家虽不及邓尉石崇，然西蜀亦著名富室也。我以贸易为游戏耳。盍暂归，取重资，当访天台。

    Explanation

    299

    Sinking Fragrance Street

    In the southern region, there is a street named Sinking Fragrance.
