Inquiring about the meaning of its name from the locals, they said it was the son of a wealthy family, named Jin, whose name is unchangeable, a native of Shu, with a beautiful appearance and wealth surpassing the village. At twenty years old, he happened to read a picture, admiring the beauty of the Jiangnan landscape, the splendor of gold powder, and the beauty of the mountains and rivers, and thus carried heavy capital to learn trade. He happened to visit this street, passing through the Peach Blossom Lane, when suddenly a lychee shell fell on his shoulder. Looking up, he saw a beautiful woman leaning on the railing of a jeweled curtain and embroidered pavilion, smiling at him. He visited and found out she was a courtesan, the thirteenth daughter named Su Jiao. The light of her hairpins and the shadows of her hair, causing a disturbance in his spirit and mind, he then entered the courtyard. The woman smiled and welcomed him, leading him into her embroidered room, which was another world altogether. The tea pot was boiling, incense was burning, chess was being played, and musical strings were being plucked, making a ringing sound. He was confused, almost not knowing where he was. Soon a feast was set up, with exquisite food and wine, and several young maids came, serving drinks to each other. Some were Su's sisters, some were Su's younger sisters, all showing their charms. He softly recited the line 'Except for the clouds of Mount Wushan, all others are not clouds,' and the crowd dispersed. When the wine was finished and the people had quieted down, he undressed and entered the curtain, and Su took out the Ten Isles Spring Album, following the score, and his soul was even more captivated. From then on, he stayed at Su Jiao's place, no longer engaging in trade. When his funds were exhausted, Su was worried. He said, 'Do not worry, my family may not be as rich as Deng Wei or Shi Chong, but we are also well-known wealthy families in Western Shu. I treat trade as a game. Why don't you return temporarily to get more capital, and we will visit Tian Tai.'

    300

    ”素涕泣曰：“妾已委身事郎，誓不再作章台柳，但恐瓜期届而郎断尾生信，奈何？”生誓以天日，曰：“剑欲会，镜欲圆，请留一信物，以当左券。”生笑曰：“小生囊橐，尽入卿家，更有何长物可赠？”素曰：“请凿一齿，以昭践齿之约。”生怯痛，素即娇啼，曰：“郎非真爱妾者。”生恐拂其意，即忍痛与之；素喜，藏诸妆台。治酒饯行，谆谆订婚嫁，临别啼哭，生衣袖尽为素泪湿透，生亦泣。榜人催再四，始判袂去。归则出资，盛备青庐洞房所需者，费二万金，将买舟往迎桃叶。友人劝诫曰：“钱树子，多不义，所以恋恋者，为有刘濞钱，非为潘安貌也。”生不信，卒治任往。巴峡下流，匝月即到，甫登岸，猛忆友人言，未卜素娇之意诚否。遂敝衣囚面，携筐持竿，诈为乞丐，诣素家。素方坐大贾怀中，持爵劝饮，蓦见生，若不识，詈妪曰：“姆姆，病盲耶？若不驱出，便嗾狗龅胫股，无悔也！”妪果持杖逐之。

    Explanation

    300

    Su wept and said, "I have entrusted myself to your service, and I swear I will never be a willow tree at Changtai. But I fear that when the time comes, you will break your promise like the man who cut off his own tail. What shall I do?" The man swore by the heavens, saying, "When the sword meets, and the mirror is whole, please leave a token of trust, as a bond." He smiled and said, "My meager possessions have all been given to you; what else do I have to give?" Su said, "Please carve one of my teeth, to seal our promise." The man was afraid of the pain, and Su immediately began to cry, saying, "You are not truly in love with me." Afraid of offending her, the man endured the pain and let her carve his tooth. Su was delighted and hid the tooth in her dressing table. They prepared wine to see him off, earnestly discussing their marriage. They cried at the moment of parting, and his sleeves were completely soaked with Su's tears. He also wept. The boatman urged them repeatedly, and they finally parted ways. Upon returning, he spent money to prepare everything needed for the bridal chamber, spending two thousand gold pieces, and planned to buy a boat to go and fetch Tao Ye. A friend advised him, saying, "Money tree, you are very unrighteous. The reason you are so attached is because you have Liu Bi's money, not because of Pan An's appearance." The man did not believe him and finally prepared and went. After a month, he arrived at the lower reaches of Ba River. As soon as he stepped ashore, he suddenly remembered his friend's words and could not predict whether Su's feelings were genuine. So he put on ragged clothes, covered his face, carried a basket and a fishing rod, and pretended to be a beggar, going to Su's house. Su was sitting in the arms of a wealthy merchant, holding a cup and urging him to drink. Suddenly seeing him, she pretended not to recognize him and scolded the old woman, saying, "Old woman, are you blind? If you don't drive him out, I'll make the dog bite his shins, and I won't regret it!" The old woman indeed took a stick and chased him away.

    301

    生哀呼曰：“姐姐莫打，我金不换也！”妪与素审之确，问何一寒至此。以途中遇盗对，素问：“尔至此，尚欲何为？”曰：“来践卿约耳。”素笑曰：“我金玉锦绣中，尚三日两日病，能为丐人妇耶？请君归休，勿妄想！”生遂哀哭曰：“明知婚媾难，但不久填沟壑，乞卿垂怜，赐一殓具费耳。”素冷笑曰：“几见勾栏门，化作施材局，恐市上木空矣。”生又蹙额曰：“小生两日无一餐，腹鸣若雷，乞卿赐一饭，俾作饱鬼，瞑目耳。”素不应，妪意若怜，以剩羹杂饭，贮破窑器，折秭与之食。生且吃且请，曰：“约既毁，小生一齿，须见还也。”素命妪捧巨箧出，其中人齿列贝，若货假药者。生大怒，掷器拂袖去，将履阈，闻素在内大粲曰：“花郎好性气！”明日，尽携所带，命人陆续扛至素家门首，绣裳珠履不计外，中有沉香床，穷极雕镂，费数千金，纵火焚之，烈烟亘天，香闻十里。素闻之，遂自缢。妪哀生，生转厚瘗之。

    Explanation

    301

    The man cried out in sorrow, "Sister, don't hit me, I'm worth my weight in gold!" The old lady and Su confirmed his story, asking how he had ended up in such a pitiful state. He replied that he had been robbed on the way, and Su asked, "Why did you come here then?" He said, "To fulfill your promise." Su laughed and said, "Even in my life of luxury and comfort, I still get sick for a few days. Could I become the wife of a beggar? Please return home, don't have any wild hopes!" The man then wept bitterly, "I know marriage is difficult, but I won't be around for long, I beg you to have pity and give me a burial fee." Su sneered, "How often do you see a brothel turn into a coffin shop? Fear that the city's wood will be exhausted." The man frowned and said, "I haven't had a meal in two days, my stomach is rumbling like thunder, I beg you to give me a meal so I can die as a full ghost, with closed eyes." Su did not respond, but the old lady seemed to feel sorry for him and gave him some leftover soup and mixed rice, stored in a broken pottery vessel, and gave him some money to eat. The man ate and said, "The promise is already broken, I must have my tooth back." Su ordered the old lady to bring out a large chest, inside which were human teeth arranged like pearls, as if they were fake medicine. The man became very angry, threw the vessel, and left in a huff, almost stepping over the threshold when he heard Su laughing inside, "Flower郎 has a good temper!" The next day, he carried all his belongings, and had people carry them one by one to the front of Su's house. Among them were luxurious clothes and shoes, and a sandalwood bed, intricately carved and costing thousands of gold pieces. He set it on fire, and the smoke reached the sky, the fragrance was smelled for miles. Su heard about it and hanged herself. The old lady mourned for the man, and he buried her deeply.

    302

    噫！荥阳生岂少也哉？世无李娃，幸勿浪作坠鞭人耳！又闻姑苏曾有一娼，名香侬，居通衢，朱门洞辟，墙悬虎牌，门首粘示条，屏缝缄丹封，蛇鞭雉帽，赫赫炫人。司阍者庞然坐视，耽耽如罗刹。香侬小有才，凡佳客到门，必坐与司阍者语良久，询明来历，然后具禀，禀云：“敬禀姑娘妆次：敬禀者，顷有贵客，貌比潘安，富同陆贾；身有衣而楚楚，家有粟而陈陈。面同李貌之柔，货定张驴之大。请添树上钱，勿韫椟中玉。

    Explanation

    302

    Ah! Are there not many people from Xingyang? In a world without a Liwa, please do not casually become a man who drops his whip! I also heard that in Suzhou there once was a courtesan named Xiangnong, who lived on a busy street, with a grand entrance and red doors, a tiger-shaped sign hanging on the wall, notices affixed at the door, a sealed red envelope inserted into a screen, a snake-shaped whip and a hat adorned with peacock feathers, shining brightly and dazzling people. The gatekeeper sat in a large, imposing manner, watching over the entrance, his presence as intimidating as a demon. Xiangnong had some talent; whenever a distinguished guest arrived, she would sit and converse with the gatekeeper for a long time, inquiring about the guest's background, and then report to her, saying: "Respectfully, I report to Miss Xiangnong: There is now a distinguished guest, as handsome as Pan An and as wealthy as Lu Jia; dressed in fine clothing and appearing elegant, with abundant grain stored in his home. His face is as soft and gentle as Li's, and his wealth is as large as Zhang's donkey. Please add more money to the tree, do not hide the jade in the box."

    303

    乞伏照验施行，准赏云雨，曷胜恩戴。”云云。须臾掷出批云：“据禀已悉，细勘来人，貌既不扬，衣又不华，钱必不多，不准。云云。该毛勿得妄渎，此檄。”懊侬氏曰：娼家钱树子，认钱不认人，此故态也，何足奇。所奇者，金家郎，迢迢巴蜀，垂到姑苏，忽然醒悟。饥来驱我，得卿卿数言，能令千古有情人，怜香惜玉之心，一齐灰死。当其拉杂摧烧时，想诸天菩萨，闻香而来，必合掌而作诵曰：“苦海无边，回头是岸，登徒之流，大家来看。噫！火中烧出青莲花，败子回头金不换。”

    Explanation

    303

    A formal request was made: "Please approve and carry out this matter, grant rewards and recognition, and how could one bear the kindness and favor." After a moment, a reply was tossed out: "The matter has been reviewed, and the person has been carefully examined. Their appearance is not striking, and their attire is not splendid. They likely have little money, so the request is denied. Do not make further appeals, this is the decree." The narrator commented: "A prostitute is a money tree, recognizing money but not the person, this is her usual state, what is there to be surprised about? What is surprising is the man from the Jin family, traveling from far-off Bashu to Suzhou, suddenly coming to a realization. Driven by hunger, he received a few words from someone dear, which could extinguish the hearts of all lovers throughout the ages, their cherishing of beauty. When everything was torn apart and burned, one might imagine the heavenly deities, drawn by fragrance, would clasp their hands and chant: 'The sea of suffering is boundless, but turning back is the shore. The licentious and immoral, come and see. Ah! An emerald lotus blooms from the fire, a prodigal son who repents is more valuable than gold.'"

    304

    小癞子
    　　
    　　今有人焉，于光天化日之中，九陌通衢之地，公然攫财物，使行道者耳如不闻，目如不见者，谁欤？即北之剪绺，南之扒儿手也。扬州东关，有小癞子，尤称巨擘。其先癞母，本习猴婆技者。癞之父操此术游江湖间，遇猴婆莫逆，遂成夫妇，而生小癞子。年十五六，其父母已饱食暖衣，称小康焉，出资送小癞子就村傅读，俾改业。而癞性最敏，咿唔之暇，辄习弄好身手，恐坠箕裘。一日，二老寿花甲，座客方称觞祝嘏，癞子遽跪请仍习父业。

    304
    305

    父唏嘘曰：“儿能象贤，原大好事；然习则易，精则难。所谓精者，如承丈人之蜩，如运郢人之斧，如射甘蝇之箭；胸有成竹，目无全牛；游戏出之，俯拾即是；所谓如与可画竹，兔起鹘落，少纵即逝者也。否则带水拖泥，动遭诟辱，捉将官里去，一般断送头皮，危如何乎！业此者，炼目炼心炼手，以我之目，视彼之目；以我之心，印彼之心；以我之手，扒彼之手。彼钝而我灵，彼劳而我逸，彼垂而我起，犹下乘也。俟其灵而我故钝，俟其逸而我故劳，俟其起而我故垂，犹中乘也。灵即应之以灵，逸即应之以逸，起即应之以起，始为无乘。

    Explanation

    305

    Father sighed and said, "If you can emulate the virtuous, it would be a great good deed; however, learning is easy, but mastering is difficult. What is meant by mastery is like carrying a cicada by its tail, like wielding the axe of the man from Ying, like shooting arrows like those of Ganfeng; to have a clear plan in mind, to see only the joints, not the whole ox; to produce it as if playing a game, and to find it easily; as in the case of Wuyu painting bamboo, where the brush moves like a hare and a hawk, and if you miss the moment, it’s gone. Otherwise, you’ll drag along with water and mud, and constantly face criticism, to be taken to the authorities, to lose your head in the same way, how dangerous it is! Those who practice this, train the eyes, heart, and hands, with my eyes, I see their eyes; with my heart, I align with their heart; with my hands, I grasp their hands. They are dull, but I am sharp, they are tired, but I am at ease, they are exhausted, but I am ready, this is still the lowest level. Wait until they are sharp, but I remain dull, wait until they are at ease, but I remain tired, wait until they are ready, but I remain exhausted, this is still the middle level. When they are sharp, I respond with sharpness, when they are at ease, I respond with ease, when they are ready, I respond with readiness, only then is there no level."

    306

    汝未习传灯，妄思跨灶，轻举妄动，岂不贻老父忧乎？”曰：“儿已习之矣，请大人面试可否，然后出门攘行人。”父尚犹夷，众极怂恿，癞更自夸，父曰：“姑试之。”二老登厅侧小楼，凭栏下顾，呼癞子，使赚之下，癞子故摇短发，作徘徊状，告众曰：“由高自卑，诚非易事。若二老下堂阶，吾赚之登绝顶，尚易如反掌，况区区一楼乎？”父闻之，即拾级下，曰：“是依样也，盍赚老人上楼？”癞子再拜曰：“即赚下矣，更何上焉？”父母与众均大笑，赏其灵妙。叠试，均有巧思，遂任伊所为，日获甚夥。

    Explanation

    306

    Your son has not yet mastered the art of [insert specific skill or tradition here], yet he rashly thinks he can surpass his teachers, acting without thought, how can this not cause your old father worry?" The son replied, "I have already mastered it. Please let me demonstrate to you, then I can go out and push people aside." The father was still hesitant, but the crowd strongly encouraged him, and the son boasted even more. The father said, "Let's try it." The two elders climbed up to a small pavilion on the side of the hall, leaned on the railing and looked down, called out to the son, and instructed him to lure them down. The son shook his short hair, made a hesitant gesture, and told the crowd, "Moving from high to low is truly not an easy thing. If the two elders descend the steps, I can easily lure them to the top, it's as easy as turning one's hand, how much more so for a mere small building?" The father heard this, immediately descended step by step, and said, "It's the same manner, why not lure the old man up the stairs?" The son bowed twice and said, "I have already lured them down, what more is there to go up?" The parents and the crowd all laughed, admiring his cleverness. They tested him repeatedly, and he had clever ideas each time, so they let him do as he pleased, and his daily gains were plentiful.

    307

    时有盐商江某，老而淫，婢妾外，更广集大脚仙，荐枕席。一日，鲜衣便服，小坐肆门，眺瞩往来，瀹茗为乐。小癞子嬉戏肆前，见江某，遽鞠躬问安否？江笑骂曰：“秃贼两目如椒，垂涎吾手中物耶？”癞子连称不敢，然亦不去。适肆中执事人看元宝，江笑擎一宝，语癞子曰：“吾夙谂汝神通，大元宝置案头，吾坐守之，众目瞰之，尔能炊许时，公然攫宝去，使吾与众皆不知，即以宝赏汝。否则再致吾门，当以大杖敲折狗胫股！”癞子笑称不敢，江又言之，癞子略凝视，遽屈膝仰首，曰：“公真慷慨，惠赐小人耶？”“诚然。”曰：“如是当预谢赏。”言已，即去无迹。江端坐，目时顾宝。

    Explanation

    307

    Occasionally, there was a salt merchant named Jiang, who was old and promiscuous. Beyond his concubines and maidservants, he further collected large-footed courtesans and engaged in sexual intercourse with them. One day, he wore fresh clothes in casual attire and sat briefly at the shop door, gazing at passersby and brewing tea for pleasure. A young boy, known as Xiao Lai Zi, was playing in front of the shop and saw Jiang. He hastily bowed and asked for his well-being. Jiang laughed and scolded, "Bald thief, your eyes are like pepper, lusting after what I have in my hand?" The boy repeatedly said he dares not, but he still did not leave. Just as someone in charge of the shop was looking at a treasure box, Jiang smiled and held up a large treasure box, saying to the boy, "I have long heard of your magical abilities. A large treasure box is placed on the table. I will sit and guard it, while everyone watches. If you can take the treasure away openly within a certain time, so that I and everyone else do not know, I will reward you with the treasure. Otherwise, come back to my door again, and I will beat you with a big stick and break your dog's leg!" The boy smiled and said he dares not. Jiang repeated the challenge, and the boy glanced briefly, then knelt and raised his head, saying, "Sir, you are truly generous, are you bestowing a gift on me?" "Indeed," Jiang replied. "If so, I should thank you in advance." After speaking, the boy disappeared without a trace. Jiang sat upright, occasionally glancing at the treasure.

    308

    忽一妖艳大脚仙，年甫十六七，内着绮罗，外罩布素；满头花朵，云鬓堆鸦；裙下莲船，崭新花履；上身衣雪青比甲，扎月白绣花汗巾；挟柳筐，内承麦面斗许，飘飘从东来，粉汗浸淫，樱口喃喃，若深怨柳斗之重。至门首，见江翁抚案坐，故止步笑曰：“且小憩片时，量午餐汤饼，尚不过迟。”旋有数妇人，陆续过门，问女曰：“巧姐竟亲执其劳耶？不怕闪坏嫩腰肢，累主人心痛？”女怨曰：“无奈何，阿六官忽思啖汤饼，所幸大脚能走，那怕踏破多子街。

    Explanation

    308

    Suddenly, a beautiful and large-footed fairy appeared, just sixteen or seventeen years old. She wore fine silk inside and plain cloth outside; her head was adorned with flowers, and her hair was piled up like a crow's nest. Her skirt revealed new flowered shoes, and she wore a light blue jacket and a white embroidered sweatband. She carried a willow basket containing about a dou of wheat flour, coming from the east, her face drenched in sweat, and she murmured softly, as if deeply resenting the weight of the willow basket. Upon reaching the door, she saw Jiang翁 sitting and writing, so she stopped and smiled, saying, "Let's take a short rest, and we can have some noodle soup for lunch, which isn't too late." Soon, several women passed by the door, asking the girl, "Is it really Qiao Jie who is doing this work herself? Isn't she afraid of hurting her delicate waist, causing the master to worry?" The girl replied with a sigh, "I have no choice; Ah Liuguan suddenly wants to eat noodle soup. Fortunately, I have large feet and can walk, even if it means wearing out the street of many children."

    309

    ”江瞰其冶容，已神迷；又闻其娇语，更心动。情不自禁，遽问曰：“小大姐，尔主人谁耶？”女敛笑正色曰：“兜兜巷东首罗大官也。”江曰：“此吾之至戚，何绝未曾一见子？”女笑曰：“妇记曾识翁面，但不知姓名。翁贵人，能有下眼觑贱婢？”翁谦逊渐入谑浪，女不怒，唯含笑小酬应。翁令其辞罗就彼，女似许可，旋问曰：“翁痴耶？坐守元宝，将以炫路人耶？”曰：“非也。”遂曲折道与小癞子相赌之由，女嗤然一笑，曰：“翁莫斗人耍，是必假宝。若真矣，翁即富，何肯以之作孤注？”翁极言其真，举宝使女自鉴。

    Explanation

    309

    "Looking upon her beautiful appearance, Jiang was already enchanted; hearing her flirtatious words, he was even more moved. Unable to control his emotions, he suddenly asked, 'Little Sister, who is your master?' The woman smiled and said seriously, 'The wealthy man Luo at the eastern end of Doudou Lane.' Jiang replied, 'He is my close relative, how could I have never seen you before?' The woman laughed and said, 'I remember recognizing your face, but I don't know your name. You are a noble person, could you lower your eyes to look at a lowly servant?' Jiang gradually became more playful and flirtatious, but the woman did not get angry, only smiling and responding lightly. Jiang asked her to leave Luo and come to him, and the woman seemed to agree. He then asked, 'Are you crazy? Sitting and guarding the treasure, to show off to passersby?' Jiang replied, 'No, it is not so.' He then told the complicated story of gambling with the little beggar. The woman laughed derisively and said, 'You are not fooling me, it must be fake treasure. If it were true, you would be rich, would you dare to risk it all?' Jiang insisted that it was real and showed the treasure to the woman for her to examine."

    310

    女果倚案捧宝审视，翁笑曰：“此吾家所最夥者，汝若肯来，何愁无十数枚？”女大笑，失手误坠柳斗面中，失色曰：“殆矣！”急从面中捧出，取袖中罗帕拂拭，而后置翁前，曰：“幸不跌伤，然奴几惊破胆矣。”翁曰：“痴妮子，几见有元宝跌损者乎？”女曰：“贪看元宝，憩此多时，恐六官又着急，奴去休！”言已，匆匆挟筐向西去。翁方与众月旦女貌若何，言辞若何，衣饰又若何。小癞子忽含笑来，径诣翁前，伏地拜谢厚贶。曰：“我元宝具在也。”曰：“翁宝已化为铅矣，真宝已蒙赐，呈小人父母储箧之固。

    Explanation

    310

    The woman indeed leaned against the table, holding the treasure to examine it closely. The old man laughed and said, "This is the most abundant treasure in our family. If you were willing to come, why worry about not having dozens more?" The woman laughed heartily, but then accidentally dropped the treasure into her hair, and she exclaimed in alarm, "Oh no!" She quickly retrieved it from her hair, wiped it clean with a handkerchief, and then placed it before the old man, saying, "Thank goodness it wasn't damaged, though I nearly had a heart attack." The old man teased her, "Foolish girl, have you ever seen a treasure fall and get damaged?" The woman replied, "I was so engrossed in looking at the treasure that I stayed here for a long time. I'm afraid the Six Officials are getting anxious, so I must go now!" With that, she hurriedly took her basket and headed west. The old man was just discussing with others about the woman's appearance, speech, and attire. Suddenly, the young man arrived with a smile, approached the old man directly, and bowed to thank him for his generous gift. He said, "My treasure is all here." The old man responded, "Your treasure has turned to lead, but the real treasure has been given to me, and it will be stored securely in my parents' chests."

    311

    ”翁细审案头灿灿者，果为铅铸，惊询何术，曰：“顷来尤物，乃小人之妻，与翁喋喋时，已由面中更换去矣。”翁始恍然，干笑曰：“便宜煞秃贼！”至咸丰四年，粤匪陷扬时，小癞子之父母已逝，妻与妹均送至乡间。癞一身，时至城中侦虚实，每拟乘隙刺杨秀清，不果。富家眷属，多藉癞子奇计赚出。审杨贼左右有大半三江人，遍结纳，坚订里应外合之计。出设大营，详告营主，当事者不深信，叱出；再欲有言，即将传令斩秃颅。癞惊逸，仰天大哭曰：“吾幼不肖，学做扒儿手，今老矣，思捐躯粉骨以报国家，奈其说不行，尚有颜面与若辈共生活乎？”遂挈家远窜，不知所终。

    Explanation

    311

    The elder carefully examined the shiny object on his desk, which turned out to be made of lead. Surprised, he asked how this had happened, and the bald man replied, "Just now, a beautiful woman came, and she was my wife. While we were talking, she replaced the object with her face." The elder suddenly realized and forced a laugh, saying, "What a lucky bald thief!"

    By the fourth year of the Xianfeng era (1854), when the Taiping rebels captured Yangzhou, the bald man's parents had already passed away, and his wife and sister had been sent to the countryside. Alone, the bald man often went to the city to investigate the situation, always intending to take advantage of an opportunity to assassinate Yang Xiuqing, but he never succeeded. He often helped wealthy families by using his ingenious plans to rescue their members. After investigating, he found that most of those close to Yang Xiuqing were from the Three Rivers region, so he widely made connections and firmly agreed to a plan of inside and outside cooperation. He set up a large camp and detailed the plan to the camp commander, but those in charge did not deeply believe him and scolded him out. When he tried to speak again, they were about to issue an order to behead the bald one. The bald man was shocked and fled, crying out to the sky, "When I was young, I was unworthy and learned to be a pickpocket. Now that I am old, I intend to sacrifice my body and soul to repay the country, but my plan did not work. How can I still have the face to live with such people?" Then, he took his family and fled far away, and his fate remains unknown.

    312

    稽耸殁为文信国公冥幕
    　　
    　　按《宋鉴》，恭宗时，陈宜中因柳岳还，复奏遣宗正少卿陆秀夫、吕师孟等同囊加歹使元军，求称侄纳币，不从，则称侄孙。秀夫等见伯颜于平江，伯颜不许。宜中乃白太后，奉表求封为小国，太后从之。直学士院高应松不肯草表，改命京局官刘裒然为之。岳等至高邮稽家庄，为义民稽耸所杀。夫耸，一民耳，其忠愤果敢如此。吾邑北乡临大湖，有城门乡，土人云：即宋之长天关，至今名为小关。城垣廨署，基址犹存。隔湖三四里，宛在水中央，有生聚百家，即稽家庄。众稽所居，耸之子孙也。足征宋时此处尚有陆路可通，是必柳岳过江，由真至长，由长往邮，道出稽庄，始为耸杀耳。尤奇者，前明枢密院罗万象，清鲠不阿，正笏垂绅有大度，曾为天长宰，政绩甚夥，乡人爱之如慈母。鼎革后，隐于城门乡，自号湖滨旅舍。

    Explanation

    312

    Ji Song died and became the ghost attendant of Wen Xingguo Gong.

    According to the Song Mirror, during the reign of Emperor Gong, Chen Yizhong, because Liu Yue returned, repeatedly petitioned to send the Grand Councillor Lu Xiufu, Lü Shimeng, and others along with Nang Jia Dai to the Yuan army, requesting to be called nephew and to pay a dowry. If not agreed upon, then to be called great-nephew. Lu Xiufu and others met Bayan at Pingjiang, but Bayan did not agree. Chen Yizhong then informed the Empress Dowager, presenting a petition to request to be enfeoffed as a small country, which the Empress Dowager agreed to. The Director of the Imperial Academy Gao Yongsong refused to draft the petition, and the order was changed to have the official Liu Pou Ran from the capital bureau draft it. Liu Yue and others arrived at Gao You Ji Family Village and were killed by the righteous man Ji Song. Ji Song, a commoner, displayed such loyalty, anger, and courage. Our county's northern village borders a large lake, and there is a village called Chengmen. The locals say it was the Song Dynasty's Changtian Pass, which is still called Xiaoguan today. The city walls and government offices still have their foundations. Separated by a lake, about three or four miles away, it seems to be in the middle of the water, with over a hundred households living there. That is Ji Family Village, the residence of the Ji family, descendants of Ji Song. It is sufficient evidence that during the Song Dynasty, there was still a land route accessible here. It must be that Liu Yue crossed the river, went from Zhen to Chang, and then to Gao You, passing through Ji Village, where he was killed by Ji Song. Especially strange is that Luo Wanxiang, a high-ranking official in the Ming Dynasty, was clear and upright, not fawning. Holding his笏 (a ceremonial tablet) upright and wearing a绅 (a sash), he had great dignity. He once served as the governor of Tianchang, having many political achievements. The villagers loved him like a mother. After the change of dynasty, he retired to Chengmen Village and called himself Hu Bin Lushi (Lake Shore Traveler's Inn).

    313

    黄冠草履，陇亩经行，田衣泥屐辈见而忘其为旧令尹也。公犹劝植农桑，教义学子弟。忽一夕，梦一青衣来招，曰：“主人传语，奉迓玉趾。”随之行，入一朱门，殿宇伟丽。主人出，揖而就坐。视其貌，如王者，绿袍珠履，须髯飘飘，谓公曰：“吾血食于此，庙中签诗俚俗鄙恶，真牧竖子语。敬知先生一代才人，衙官屈宋，敢乞赐以珠玉，为冥冥增辉。”公诺，载拜退。逾阈惊寤，以为梦幻无恁，不甚信。明日，又入梦，王者诿尤谆切。醒异之。偶游关之城隍庙，庭阶曲折，依稀梦游，视壁上签诗果俚，然公究素慕王阳明理学，不以此为事。是夕假寐，梦两侍来，状如古之虞候，招公曰：“速去速去！主人候久矣！”公若疾趋随之行，视殿上仍前王者，色甚怒，曰：“何吝才！是区区者，尚欲效《东》《西》赋，研之十年耶？”责让严厉。公再拜曰：“鄙人鲁拙机滞，忧患膺心，乱离亡命，风雅事久已抛却。

    Explanation

    313

    Wearing a yellow hat and straw sandals, walking through the fields, those dressed in simple farmer's clothes and wooden clogs saw him and forgot he was the former magistrate. He still encouraged the planting of crops and raising of silkworms, and taught the principles to young students. Suddenly one night, he dreamed of a young man in green robes coming to invite him, saying, "The master sends his regards and welcomes your noble presence." Following him, he entered a red gate, where the halls and buildings were magnificent. The master came out, bowed, and they took their seats. Seeing his appearance, he looked like a king, dressed in a green robe with pearl shoes, his beard and mustache fluttering. He said to the man, "I receive offerings here, but the temple's divination poems are vulgar and tasteless, truly the words of a rustic. Respecting your talent as a man of your generation, an official inferior to Qu Yuan and Song Yu, I dare to ask for your precious words to add luster to the afterlife." The man agreed, performed a double bow, and then left. Waking up in a state of shock, he thought it was a dream and did not believe it much. The next day, he entered another dream, where the king blamed and admonished earnestly. He found it strange upon waking. By chance, he visited the city god temple in the city of Guan, where the courtyard and steps were winding, vaguely like a dream. Seeing the signs on the walls were indeed vulgar, he had always admired Wang Yangming's philosophy but did not consider it important. That evening, he fell asleep and dreamed of two attendants coming, looking like ancient yuhou, calling him, "Hurry, hurry! The master has been waiting for a long time!" He hurriedly followed them, and seeing the king on the hall as before, the king's expression was very angry, saying, "Why be stingy with your talent! This simple matter, still wanting to imitate the East and West essays, study them for ten years?" He scolded severely. The man bowed twice and said, "I am a humble person who is slow and clumsy, suffering from worries and troubles, in chaos and fleeing for my life, the affairs of elegance and literature have long been abandoned.

    314

    然既承隆委，敢自讳其拙耶？容即拟稿求教。”王者色犹怒。旁有冠带客，修髯面白晰，若幕宾状，代公缓颊甚殷。色始霁，即命送公出。拱而别，遂问客乡贯姓氏，曰：“宋之杀降表者，即某是也。对湖大村落，习耕读者，皆某之子孙。”问王者何人，曰：“噤声！”以指划公腕，书曰：“文山相国。”公惊醒，盥沐作诗三十首，清丽缠绵，温柔敦厚，缮稿焚之神前，而后书木悬之壁，自叙甚详。并云：“文山先生与其客杜浒等亡入真州，苗再成绐之出，抵扬，四鼓矣。问候门者，曰：‘制司下令，捕文丞相。’先生穷促无策，素闻稽名，乃反驭访稽。稽迎之于家，事之甚谨，因而慷慨泣下。散家资，招健丁，复遣其子与客伴送文山先生至泰州，航海达闽。”生前道经于此，殁即为此乡之神，亦理之不可解者。

    Explanation

    314

    Having already accepted such a significant responsibility, how dare I hide my own inadequacies? I will draft a response and seek your guidance." The ruler's expression remained angry. Beside him stood a bearded man with a white, clear complexion, dressed like a retainer or guest, who spoke on your behalf very earnestly. The ruler's expression softened, and he ordered to escort you out. Bowed and parted, the speaker then asked the guest about his hometown, origin, and surname. The guest replied, "I am the one who killed the envoy of surrender in Song. The large village across the lake, where those who practice farming and reading reside, are all my descendants." When asked who the ruler was, the guest said, "Be silent!" He wrote on your wrist with a finger, saying, "The Prime Minister Wen Shan." You woke up in shock, washed, and composed thirty poems, which were clear and beautiful, lingering and tender, gentle and sincere. Prepared the draft, burned it before the deity, and then wrote it on wood and hung it on the wall, speaking in great detail about himself. He also mentioned, "Mr. Wen Shan and his guest Du Hu escaped to Zhenzhou. Miao Zai Cheng deceived them into going out, and they arrived in Yangzhou at the fourth drum. They asked the gatekeeper, 'The provincial command issued an order to capture Chancellor Wen.' Mr. Wen was at a loss, with no strategies left. Having heard of Ji's reputation, he went to visit Ji. Ji welcomed him at home and treated him with great respect, causing him to cry emotionally. He dispersed his family wealth and recruited strong men, then sent his son and guests to accompany Mr. Wen Shan to Taizhou, where they sailed to Fujian." Before his death, he passed through here, and after his death, he became the deity of this village, a mystery that cannot be explained.

    315

    至稽公殁为先生幕中客，岂非订生死交欤？前曾游庙堂，悚然瞻仰，中殿肖信国公像，少偏肖儒衣冠者，正襟危坐，英风侠骨，凛凛如生，即稽公像也。闻之土人云：“罗公卒于是乡，葬长亭，四围多孤冢，泛泛若水上凫。”余时拟出资为公加封植，立崇碑，叙其事，且肖像于信国右，宛与稽公对峙，血食千秋。惜阮囊依旧，奈何？懊侬氏曰：由宋末至明末，相隔垂四百余年。

    Explanation

    315

    Upon Jigong's death, he became a guest in the master's entourage, which surely sealed a life-and-death friendship. Previously, I had served in the imperial court, and with awe, I looked up to the image of Xin Guo Gong in the main hall. To the side, there was a figure dressed in the attire of a scholar, sitting upright and solemnly, exuding a heroic and chivalrous spirit that seemed as if he were alive—this was the image of Jigong. I heard from the locals that Lu Gong died in this village, was buried at the long pavilion, and his grave was surrounded by many isolated graves, scattered like ducks on water. At the time, I had planned to contribute money to improve the tomb, erect a grand stele, narrate the story, and place a portrait to the right of Xin Guo Gong, so that the two figures would face each other, to be worshipped for generations. Alas, my small purse remained the same, what can be done? The speaker lamented: from the end of the Song dynasty to the end of the Ming dynasty, there was a time span of nearly four hundred years.

    316

    文公生为河岳，殁为日星，何尚拳拳于弹丸小邑哉？读其诗集，《过稽庄》句云：“小泊稽庄月正弦，庄官惊问是何船？今朝哨马湾头出，又在青山大路边。”当其亡命流离，犹自吟咏自如，良由襟怀朗然，故能定静若此。罗公以枢密之伟望，隐稽公之故居，作野老之哀音，荷忠魂之隆委，所谓声气相通者非欤？罗公宰吾乡时，曾诣汊涧大觉寺拈香，甫履寺阈，愕然以惊，恍然若悟，预问寺中廊舍联额，语无不符。盖公之前身，为寺之住持僧也。因题其额曰：“相看在此。”由是观之，如三君子者，可鼎足而不愧乎神。

    Explanation

    316

    Translation:

    The Master was born as a river or mountain, and after his death, he became the sun or stars. Why should he still be so deeply attached to a small town? Reading his poetry collection, there is a verse in "Passing by Ji Village" that says: "A small boat docks at Ji Village, the moon in its crescent phase. The village official is surprised and asks, 'What kind of boat is this?' Today, it emerges from the outpost at the bay, and again, it is by the roadside of Green Mountain." When he was fleeing in exile, he still recited poetry freely, truly because his mind was broad and clear, thus he could remain so calm and composed. The Master, with his great reputation as a high-ranking official, lived in the former residence of Ji, composed the mournful sounds of a hermit, and carried the noble spirit of a loyal soul. Is this not what is meant by "spirits and breaths are connected"? When the Master governed my hometown, he once visited the Great Awakening Temple at Cha Jian to offer incense. As soon as he stepped through the temple threshold, he was startled and surprised, and suddenly realized something. He then asked about the inscriptions on the walls and the names of the halls, and everything matched. It is said that his former life was as the abbot of this temple. He then inscribed the signboard with the words: "We look upon each other here." From this perspective, the three gentlemen can stand on equal footing without shame to the gods.

    317

    丐癖
    　　
    　　花根云，年二十，天台儒家子。貌翩翩，性嗜读，幼失怙恃。年十四，即补博士弟子员，戚里推为国器。欲妻以女，笑却之。询何故，曰：“我命中虽无十二钗，然绝非田舍翁抱着黄脸婆子眠一世者。”家赤贫，日把卷，不顾灶突尘。同人偶拉往东廓观戏，一二阙，即称赏。又演荥阳公子教歌，觉鳏寡孤独之流，疲癃残疾之辈，弄蛇牵犬，刻划尽情，大乐曰：“妙哉！此天人菩萨，宰官身而说法像也。

    Explanation

    317

    Hua Genyun, at the age of twenty, was a scholar's son from Tian Tai. He was handsome and had a passion for reading, having lost his parents at a young age. At fourteen, he was admitted as a student of the Imperial Academy and was highly regarded by his relatives and friends as a national talent. When someone proposed to marry their daughter to him, he laughed and rejected the offer. When asked why, he replied, "Although I am not destined to have twelve beauties in my life, I am definitely not someone who will spend his life with a plain wife." Despite being extremely poor, he devoted himself to his studies, often neglecting the smoke from the stove. Occasionally, his fellow scholars would take him to watch plays in Dongguo. After a few acts, he would praise them. Once, they performed a play about a nobleman from Xingyang teaching songs, which vividly depicted the lives of widowers, widows, orphans, the elderly, and the disabled, as well as those with disabilities, performing with snakes and dogs. He was greatly delighted and said, "Wonderful! This is like a heavenly being or a bodhisattva, a high official speaking like a sage."

    318

    ”人以为颠，生曰：“丐也者，不识不知，无拘无管，以天地为蘧庐，以日月为灯烛，以江河为襟带，以木石为友朋，歌哭无常，叫号随己，是真薄诸侯而不为，比散仙而无愧者。何乐如之？”时生有远族伯叔行胞兄弟者四，业半儒贾，产极膏腴。有德耀妻，抱邓攸戚。偶中秋邪，相继殒谢。铜山万丈，悉归四嫠妇撑持。议择嗣未决，族之无赖者乃蝇集，谋瓜分，厅事几榻，不羽亦飞；田亩佃人，被胁咸逸。流言四布，不止穿窬，嫠辈稍理，即横目争曰：“寺人产耳，孰不有分哉？”嫠愤极鸣宰。宰至，谕速立嗣，承宗祧。

    Explanation

    318

    People thought he was crazy, but Sheng said, "A beggar is someone who knows nothing, is not bound by anything, regards the world as a temporary dwelling, the sun and moon as lamps, rivers as belts, trees and stones as friends, sings and cries without restraint, and calls out as he pleases. This is truly someone who despises the nobility and does not seek their status, and who compares himself to a wandering immortal without shame. What greater joy could there be?" At the time, Sheng had four distant relatives who were brothers or cousins, whose professions were partly scholarly and partly commercial, and who owned extremely fertile land. He had a wife of high virtue and a son like the virtuous son of Deng You. Coincidentally, during the Mid-Autumn Festival, they died one after another. The vast wealth, like mountains of copper, was now entirely supported by the four widows. The discussion about choosing an heir had not been resolved, and the worthless members of the clan had gathered like flies, plotting to divide the estate. The furniture in the hall, without wings, flew away; the tenants of the fields, under coercion, all fled. Rumors spread everywhere, not just thieves, and the widows, when they tried to manage things, were met with hostile glares and arguments. "The estate is just the property of the eunuch, who has no right to keep it all," they said. The widows, in their frustration, appealed to the magistrate. The magistrate arrived and ordered that an heir be chosen quickly to continue the family line.

    319

    嫠叩首，曰：“族子皆豚犬耳，与其遗风木忧，盍若视亡者成伯道。今乞贤宰官出示于族人约：凡子侄行，无论遐迩，但秋试一获解，即嗣之；否则未亡人宁乞不怨也。”宰曰：“善。”即示悬里门并花氏宗塾，族咸惶惶作希冀想，然临时掘井，又自悔其迟。茂才花根石，富不仁，亦夙涎嫠产者，浼生作捉刀人，隽即酬千金。生正苦囊涩，即乘便之钱塘，同舍馆。石时出宿勾栏，生独居。忽一褐衣人贸贸然来，问生曰：“君茂才花根石耶？”姑应之，曰：“然。

    Explanation

    319

    The widow bowed her head and said, "My family members are like pigs and dogs; it is better not to leave behind an empty sorrow, but rather to treat the deceased as if they had achieved the status of a noble. I now request the wise and virtuous official to show the clan the agreement: all descendants, regardless of distance, if they pass the imperial examination once, will inherit; otherwise, the widow would rather beg than complain." The official replied, "Good." He immediately displayed it at the village gate and the Hua family temple school. The clan members all anxiously hoped for something, but when they dug a well at the last moment, they regretted their delay. Hua Genshi, a talented person who was wealthy but lacking in benevolence, and had long coveted the widow's property, asked the student to act as a proxy. If successful, he would reward a thousand gold pieces. The student was in dire financial straits and took the opportunity to go to Qiantang, staying in the same lodging. Hua Genshi often stayed at a brothel, while the student lived alone. Suddenly, a man dressed in a brown robe came uninvited and asked the student, "Are you the talented person Hua Genshi?" The student temporarily agreed and said, "Yes."

    320

    ”曰：“曷试言长上名字事迹。”石父本生叔，即诡告且确。袖出一函与之曰：“此家主所持赠者，秘莫示人，揭晓来讨喜觞饮耳。”言已即去。生潜阅之，闱中关节也。盖石父亦明经，有富名，帘官某与有素，媚之希丰酬，适来者，价也。生怀入闱，草草与石文，自则略经营，嵌关节。及榜发，石落而生魁矣。荣归在途，方自惭蓬荜湫隘。比入里门，而四嫠已拥之至家。满堂设红氍毹，璧上泥光可耀目。会宰亦呵道来贺，生错愕不知所云。嫠出叩谢玉成，生则再三辞。

    Explanation

    320

    He said, "Why not try to mention the names and deeds of superiors?" Shi Fu, who was originally his uncle, immediately lied and confirmed it. He discreetly pulled out a letter and said, "This is a gift from the head of the household, keep it secret and do not show it to others, just to invite you to a celebratory drink." After speaking, he left. The young man secretly read it, and it contained secrets from the examination hall. It turns out that Shi Fu is also a scholar who has passed the imperial examinations, known for his wealth. He has a long-standing relationship with a certain censor, flatters him in hopes of receiving a generous reward, and the reward is likely a high position. The young man carried the letter into the examination hall, hastily wrote an article for Shi Fu, did a bit of planning himself, and inserted the secrets. When the results were announced, Shi Fu failed, but the young man became the top scorer. On his way home, he felt ashamed of his humble and poor home. Upon entering the village gate, the four widows had already escorted him to his home. The entire hall was set up with red carpets, and the walls were covered with mud plaster that shone brightly. The local magistrate also came to congratulate him with a shout, and the young man was startled and did not know what to say. The widows came out to thank him for his success, and the young man repeatedly declined.

    321

    宰曰：“是有议约，何必辞？”立命冠服，入叩四母与亡者木主，礼成始去。嫠呼生曰：“得汝为子，足为孀孤吐气。家中不少阿堵物，听挥霍，不吝也。”生拜曰：“诸母有子，若挥霍，是何异外人之鲸吞。但儿尚未娶，意欲娶两妇，一为本生洁苹蘩，一为诸慈奉榛栗。”嫠笑曰：“吾辈本意为儿娶四妇，顷如儿言，即娶五妇何如？”生拜谢，昕夕定省如所生，嫠亦钟爱如己出。凡一裘一葛，制必四，恐其判低昂也。然生则以来时敝衣私扃一箧，不肯弃。

    Explanation

    321

    The magistrate said, "There is an agreement; why bother to refuse?" He immediately ordered ceremonial attire to be put on, entered to kneel before the four mothers and the spirit tablet of the deceased, and only after the ceremony was completed did he leave. The widowed woman called out to the adopted son, saying, "To have you as a son is enough to give the widowed and orphaned a sense of relief. The home is not lacking in wealth; feel free to spend, without any stinginess." The adopted son bowed and said, "The mothers already have a son; if I were to spend lavishly, it would be no different from the excessive consumption of outsiders. But I have not yet married. I wish to marry two wives, one to serve my biological parents with purity and diligence, and one to serve the adoptive parents with respect and diligence." The widowed woman smiled and said, "We originally intended to marry four wives for you. If you wish, we could marry five wives. What do you think?" The adopted son bowed in gratitude and visited the parents in the morning and evening as if they were his biological parents, and the widowed woman treated him with love as if he were her own child. For every fur coat and cotton robe, four were made, to ensure they were not of different quality. However, the adopted son secretly locked away the old clothes he had brought, unwilling to discard them.

    322

    一朝，有戚属诸人坐厅事，大声呼生名。嫠争出屏后，大骂曰：“狗彘奴！尚敢尔耶？我辈有孝廉儿，寺人产俱在，曷再攫乎？”言已，呼仆缚之公庭，敲断狗胫股，诸仆应，声若雷，怒目，臂露筋，执索将系维。戚惶急摇手曰：“何敢尔，何敢尔！我辈来与贤郎作冰耳。”曰：“姑毕其说。”曰：“里有富室刘叟，老无子，唯五女，皆诸妾所生，年相若，极友爱，尝闺中设誓，愿同事一良人。顷闻贤郎，力能享五美，故倩某等来执柯。”嫠始唯唯，改容逊谢。

    Explanation

    322

    One day, several relatives were sitting in the hall, loudly calling out the name of the person. The widow stepped out from behind the screen, shouting, "Dog and pig scum! How dare you still act this way? We have a filial and upright son, and our eunuchs and offspring are all present, so why seize again?" After speaking, she called the servants to bind him in the public court, breaking the dog's leg. The servants responded, their voices like thunder, glaring with anger and veins bulging on their arms, holding ropes to tie him up. The relative panicked and waved his hand, saying, "How dare you, how dare you! We came to make an ice with your noble son." She said, "Let me finish my explanation." She continued, "There is a wealthy man named Liu in the village, who is old and has no sons, only five daughters. They are all from concubines and are of similar age, extremely loving, and once made a vow in their chambers, wishing to share one good man. Recently, we heard that your noble son is capable of enjoying five beauties, so we asked some to come to act as matchmakers." The widow then nodded and humbly thanked them.

    323

    然婚议成，婉倩里之素有德望者代月老，众唯酬以酒肉而已。亲迎之日，陪奁极丰；彩舆一队，如凤集，如鸦衔，如鱼贯，固已奇矣；而嫠更招优演剧，生且请于是日设厂赈流人。鼓乐丝竹，与乞食诵佛声相错杂，则尤奇也。却扇后，视五女，皆美绝伦，燕瘦环肥，各极态度；香温玉暖，互斗尹邢。孟曰璧月，仲曰香月，叔曰好月，季曰琼月，五曰今月。姐妹善事姑与良人，年余毫无争夕事。生自嗣四嫠也，手不拈斑管，目不睹制艺，足不踏塾门，唯豪饮鼓哀弦，挈同好串繁响。

    Explanation

    323

    The marriage agreement was made, and a respected and virtuous person from Wanqian Village acted as matchmaker, rewarding the matchmaker only with wine and meat. On the day of the wedding, the dowry was extremely rich; a line of decorated carriages, like phoenixes gathering, like crows carrying, like fish swimming in a line, was indeed a strange sight; and the widow also hired actors to perform plays, while the groom requested on that day to set up a relief station for displaced people. The sounds of drum music, string instruments, and the recitation of Buddhist scriptures by beggars were mixed together, making the scene even more unusual. After removing the fan, the groom saw the five daughters, all of whom were exceptionally beautiful, each showing their unique charm, like Yan's slender figure and Huán's plump figure. They were warm and fragrant, like jade, and competed with each other like Yin and Yang. The eldest was named Bixue, the second Xiangyue, the third Hao Yue, the fourth Qiongyue, and the fifth Jinyue. The sisters were good at serving their mother-in-law and husband, and for over a year, there was no dispute over night visits. The groom had inherited four widows, and he did not pick up a brush, did not see the examination essays, and did not step into the school. He only drank heavily, played sad music, and gathered friends to make loud noises.

    324

    初犹奉母斑衣，学老莱子；后渐闺中乞食，学韩熙载；久即村人赛会，生亦粉墨登场，歌喉一声，诸伶拜下风，观者呼绝调矣。嫠稍讽劝，勖以南宫，生笑曰：“儿命宫虽有些顽福，今为闺中艳福折算尽矣。若再痴望，恐促生年，母亦何乐有此短折子哉？故不为也。”年余，璧香好琼四妇皆生子，试啼声，知英物，而五娘今月尚未妊，生诧曰：“岂老天尚不欲我逸哉？”由是日与今月嬉曰：“而今而后，卿当视吾目若巧流盼，即二五妙合时也。”今月戏曰：“行当豢乞人子为儿，免郎笑我不作茧。

    Explanation

    324

    Initially, he dressed in colorful robes to please his mother, following the example of Lao Lai Zi; later, he gradually began begging for food within the home, imitating the unconventional behavior of Han Xizai; eventually, during village festivals, he appeared on stage in makeup, and with a single song, all the performers acknowledged his superiority, and the audience hailed him as a masterpiece. The widow gently advised him, encouraging him to seek education at the South Palace. He smiled and said, "My destiny may have some stubborn fortune, but now it has been fully consumed by the beauty of my surroundings. If I continue to foolishly hope, I fear it might shorten my life, and my mother would not be happy with such a short-lived person. So, I will not do it." After a year, four women, including Bi Xiang, Hao Qiong, and Wu Niang, all gave birth to children. They tested the cries of the newborns and found them to be exceptional. However, Wu Niang, who was called Jin Yue, had not yet conceived. He was surprised and said, "Could it be that heaven still does not want me to enjoy ease?" From then on, he played with Jin Yue every day, saying, "From now on, you should look at me with a clever gaze, and that will be the perfect match." Jin Yue teased him and said, "I will raise a beggar's child as my own to avoid being laughed at for not being industrious."

    325

    ”越两年，四儿皆呀呀学语，而今月亦孕然尚未知雏之雌雄，生卜于五相祠，笺曰：“巧巧巧，心事了，回头宜早，俗尘宜扫。”生玩之，哑然笑曰：“是其时乎！”翌庆次母诞，丝竹之声遏云，杯斝之流泛月。生突入，跪白四嫠曰：“儿所以能生子者，父之荫，母之福，非儿所有也。诸母他日各抱孙，各教妇，胜于日对不才子。儿事毕，儿去矣！”旋揖五妇曰：“卿等有儿，好努力作人家，无以我为念。

    Explanation

    325

    After two years, all four children are babbling. And now this month is pregnant, but it is still unknown whether the offspring will be male or female. He sought divination at the Five-Phase Shrine, and the note read: "Clever, clever, clever, your heart's matter is settled, turn back early, sweep away the mundane dust." He played with it, and laughed silently, saying, "Is this the time?"

    The next day, celebrating the second mother's birthday, the sound of silk and bamboo instruments blocked the clouds, and the flow of cups and bowls reflected the moon. He suddenly entered, kneeling to tell the four widows: "The reason I can have children is due to my father's protection and my mother's blessing, not because of me. One day, each of you will hold your own grandchild, teach your own daughter-in-law, which is better than daily dealing with an unworthy son. My duties are complete, I am leaving now." He then bowed to the five women, saying: "You all have children, work hard to build your families, do not worry about me."

    326

    ”众诧其妄，生仰天大笑曰：“索逋而来，逋偿而去，何妄之有？”是夕，犹问诸母安否，晨则潜更来时敝衣，飘然远引矣。仆四出寻之，无踪迹，一家哭失声。是年夏五，今月亦生子，悲喜交集，然无如何。旋有乡人自豫来者，云生入优人班，敝衣囚面，坐场后点腰鼓，神色恬然。又有自鲁来者云，近更落拓，日困卑田院，拍板摇铃，唱《蒿里曲》以度日。然循之，则均于先夕遁去。想即当日所谓乐者，今乐其乐矣。生之五子也，孟曰环，仲曰琅，叔曰琥，季曰珊，五曰玖，皆颖敏，早入泮。
    又数年，琅补邑明经，环珊均乡魁，琥中明通榜进士，玖食廪饩。太母四，均长斋绣佛，委家政于妇。大太母寿八十五，孙环视于庭，嫠泫然曰：“汝皆成名，亦知尚有父乎？”乃涕不可抑，跪求祖母训，曰：“昨梦菩萨指示，云汝父乞食于潇湘岸上，当往晤一面，了父子缘。”环等遽束装，蹶往。至湘水，凡有城廓村堡，无不暗物色。

    Explanation

    326

    The crowd was surprised by his absurdity, but he laughed at the sky and said, "I came seeking debt and left after paying it off. How can there be any absurdity?" That evening, he still asked about the well-being of the mothers, but in the morning, he secretly changed into the tattered clothes he had worn before and departed lightly and far away. The servant searched everywhere for him, but found no trace, and the whole family cried out in despair. In that year, during the fifth month of the summer, the family also gave birth to a son, and they were overwhelmed with a mix of sorrow and joy, but there was nothing they could do. Soon, a villager from the region of Yu came and said that he had joined the ranks of entertainers, dressed in tattered clothes with a face like a prisoner, sitting behind the stage and beating a waist drum, with a calm demeanor. Another villager from the region of Lu said that he had become even more destitute, spending his days in a lowly place, beating a clapper and ringing a bell, singing the Hao Li tune to pass the time. However, when they tried to find him, he had already vanished by the previous night. It is likely that this was the same person they had spoken of as someone who found joy in his own way, now finding joy in his own joy. He had five sons: the eldest was named Ring, the second was named Ling, the third was named Amber, the fourth was named Coral, and the fifth was named Jade. All of them were sharp-witted and entered school early.

    Several years later, Ling passed the local examination for Confucian scholars, Ring and Coral both won the top honors in their district, Amber passed the imperial examination and became a jinshi, and Jade received a stipend from the government. The four grandmothers all maintained a vegetarian diet and embroidered Buddha statues, entrusting household affairs to their wives. The eldest grandmother was eighty-five years old, and Grandson Ring looked at her in the courtyard, and the widow wept, saying, "You have all become famous, but do you know there is still a father?" She then could not stop crying and knelt to ask the grandmother for guidance, saying, "Last night in a dream, the Bodhisattva gave instructions, saying that your father is begging on the banks of the Xiang River, and you should go to meet him once to resolve the father-son bond." Ring and the others immediately packed their belongings and set off hastily. Upon reaching the Xiang River, they searched every city, village, and fort, trying to find him.

    327

    偶至飞琼村，有张团头者，凡流民皆为所辖，衣冠往咨询，张恍然曰：“公子来耶？大奇大奇！渠三年前由豫鲁至此，遇乞丐有恩德，昨正盗鸡斩狗，一同饮废寺中，忽东望良久，掷杯大哭，众惊询，渠曰：‘我天台花孝廉也。明日我儿来捕我归，奈何？’众额手庆，渠大不乐，众曰：‘我辈知君之心矣，诚恐一朝返初服，必遭吾辈扰。今愿凭神誓，君曷归休。’渠曰：‘善！’痛饮达旦，众鼾，而渠已无迹矣。众丐顷正各处寻，而公子竟真来耶。”兄弟泣浼张，引至饮处，则破瓢挂壁，短竹倚门，迹如故也。寻守几月余，始厚酬诸丐，痛哭而归。明年，四嫠先后逝。

    Explanation

    327

    Chancing upon Feiqiong Village, there was a man named Zhang Tuantou, who oversaw all the displaced people in the area. Scholars and officials went to consult him, and Zhang was startled and said, "Have you come, sir? How extraordinary! He came here from Henan and Shandong three years ago. He had shown kindness to beggars. Yesterday, he was stealing chickens and killing dogs, drinking together in an abandoned temple. Suddenly, he looked east for a long time, threw down his cup, and wept bitterly. The crowd was startled and asked him. He said, 'I am Huaxiaolian from TianTai. Tomorrow, my son will come to capture me and take me back. What shall I do?' The crowd clapped their hands in celebration, but he was greatly unhappy. The crowd said, 'We understand your heart, sir. We truly fear that one day when you return to your original state, you will surely suffer from our disturbances. Now, we swear by the gods, why not return home and rest?' He said, 'Good!' They drank heartily until dawn. The crowd snored, but he had already disappeared. The beggars searched everywhere, but the young master actually came after all." The brothers wept and pleaded with Zhang, who led them to the place where they had drunk. There, a broken gourd hung on the wall, and short bamboo leaned against the door, just as before. After guarding the place for several months, they finally generously rewarded the beggars, cried bitterly, and returned home. The following year, the four widows passed away one after another.

    328

    服阕，琅出官邻邑广文；环珊皆大令：一宰豫，一宰鲁；琥官皖大方伯；玖家居守墓田。环珊生母均多病，遂各奉其母以居。琥在皖，一日车骑出，忽有少年丐者犯卤薄，左右正呵叱，丐含笑掷纸裹于地，声锵然，且曰：“乞人宝此无用，请归遗太夫人。”言已，行甚速，追莫及，拾而睇之，则一金钗嵌巨珠。归奉母，母泣曰：“乞人即汝父也。钗吾旧饰，亡已久，今特送珠还哉！”又环捧檄之东鲁，藉以看弟。

    Explanation

    328

    Upon the end of his mourning period, Lang left public service and moved to a neighboring county to engage in scholarly pursuits; Huan and Shan were both high-ranking officials: one governed Yu, and the other governed Lu; Hu served as a high-ranking provincial governor in Anhui; Jiu stayed at home to manage the family tomb grounds. Huan and Shan's biological mothers were both often ill, so they each took care of their mothers and lived with them. Hu was in Anhui when, one day, he set out in a carriage. Suddenly, a young beggar intruded into his entourage. The attendants were shouting to stop him, but the beggar smiled and threw a paper package on the ground, making a clear and resonant sound. He said, "The beggar considers this item worthless. Please return it to my mother." After speaking, he ran very quickly, and no one could catch him. When they picked up the package and looked at it, they found a golden hairpin with a large pearl. They returned the hairpin to his mother, who wept and said, "The beggar is your father. This hairpin was my old ornament, and I lost it a long time ago. He has come to return the pearl to me!" Later, Huan took a document to the eastern part of Lu, using the opportunity to visit his younger brother.

    329

    珊出迎东廓门，班荆甫坐，忽见一少年丐，倚墙扪虱，且高歌曰：“雪中人是将军种，口角莲生极乐花。”兄弟心异之，趋与拱揖，唾不顾；再询之，则搓肤垢成二巨丸，分与之曰：“勿多言，宝此，可疗妇人病。”言已跛足去，瞥即杳然。归各与母服，病果瘳。又玖因家事往询兄琅，道遇一穷男子，插草卖画幅，展阅之，则云树数重，一少年仙风道骨，飘飘走山巅，作采药状。问何来，曰：“我母卒，贫无以殡，痛将殉，忽一道流假村塾，楮墨自写小照与我，曰：‘持往可售二十千，尚不敷戢身具耶？’方叩谢，其人顿渺。”玖爱其笔尚不俗，如数购回。琅阅亦莫解。

    Explanation

    329

    Shan welcomed him at the eastern gate of Dongguo and spread out mats to sit down. Suddenly, they saw a young beggar leaning against the wall and picking lice, singing loudly: "In the snow, a person is a descendant of a general, and at the corners of his mouth, lotus blossoms of bliss bloom." The brothers were surprised by this and hurried forward to bow, but the beggar spat without looking; they asked him again, and he rubbed dirt from his skin to form two large pills, giving them to him and saying, "Do not speak much, treasure these, they can cure women's illnesses." After speaking, he left limping and vanished instantly. They returned and each gave their mother the pills, and the illness indeed healed. Later, Jiu went to consult his brother Lang about family matters and met a poor man on the road who inserted grass to sell scrolls. They unfolded and read it, which depicted clouds and trees in multiple layers, a young man with the air of a Taoist sage walking lightly on the mountain peak, acting as if gathering herbs. They asked where he came from, and he said, "My mother died, I am poor and unable to bury her, in pain, ready to die with her. Suddenly, a Taoist monk appeared at a village school, wrote a small self-portrait with paper and ink and gave it to me, saying, 'Take it and it can be sold for twenty thousand, but it is not enough to cover the burial expenses.' Just as I was about to thank him, the person suddenly disappeared." Jiu loved his brushwork, which was still elegant, and bought it for the full price. Lang read it but did not understand.

    330

    唯琅生母，知为阿翁小像，旋以携归呈母，且述所以。母泣曰：“汝去后，汝父亦于是日负包裹回，儒衣冠，极华瞻，初不类丐。我方惊喜，渠云：‘二十余年不归矣，且先省殡宫，然后道契阔。’我因藏其包裹，同之墓。奠毕，忽大风扬沙，日色昏黑，我坚抱之，恐其逸，闻云际大声呼曰：‘仆非丐根，然有丐癖，烦卿寄语儿曹，宁有丐父，不可有丐子丐孙也！’天霁，视所抱乃一枯松树。归阅包裹，则去时所着之敝衣。

    Explanation

    330

    Only the mother of Lang Sheng knew it was a small portrait of Grandfather. She quickly brought it back to show the mother and explained the circumstances. The mother wept and said, "After you left, your father also returned on that day with a bundle. He was wearing scholarly robes and hats, extremely elegant, and at first did not look like a beggar. I was just surprised, and he said, 'I have not returned for over twenty years. I will first visit the tomb, then talk about the long separation.' I then hid his bundle and went to the tomb with him. After the offering was completed, suddenly, a strong wind blew sand, and the sun turned dark. I tightly held it, afraid it would escape, and heard a loud voice from the clouds say, 'I am not a beggar by nature, but I have a beggar's habit. Please tell my children: it is better to have a beggar father than beggar children and grandchildren!' The sky cleared, and what I held was a dead pine tree. When I returned to check the bundle, it was the old clothes he wore when he left."

    331

    ”明年兄弟偶团聚，互述前事，盖见于皖，见于鲁，见于乡里者，皆此一日一时耳。悬像中堂，哭拜作礼，纸上神情，与所见皆酷肖。乃索题于当道诸父执，莫不饮佩，曰：“此由儒而丐而仙，天台之花孝廉也。”而孝廉远矣！懊侬氏曰：花孝廉一瓶一钵，云水往来，当其撒手悬崖，视妻子如敝屐，亦何其忍哉！或别有慧根，知满场袍笏，终有散场时耳。尝谓富且贵者，人视之，诚神仙不若；己视之，亦南面不易；而仙人视之，则桎梏其身，毒其性，不过成一可怜虫，乃彼不自怜，犹津津骂花孝廉，曰：“是不肖薄福儿也，是无知怪诞人也。”噫，愚哉！

    Explanation

    331

    In the following year, the brothers coincidentally reunited and recounted their past experiences. They had met in Anhui, Shandong, and their hometown, all on the same day and at the same time. They hung a portrait in the main hall and performed a ritual of weeping and bowing. The expression in the portrait closely resembled what they had seen. They then requested inscriptions from local officials and respected elders, and everyone was impressed, saying, "This person transformed from a scholar to a beggar and then to a sage, the scholar-official Hu Hualian from TianTai." Yet, the scholar-official is far away! A person named Aonong said: Hu Hualian, with only a bottle and a bowl, traveled like clouds and water. When he let go of the cliff, he viewed his wife and children as old shoes—how cruel it is! Perhaps he had a different kind of wisdom, knowing that the stage of officials and scholars is full, and there will come a time when the show ends. Once said that the rich and powerful, when viewed by others, are truly not as good as immortals; when viewed by themselves, it is also not easy to sit in the south (a metaphor for being in power); but when viewed by immortals, they are bound by their bodies, poisoned by their nature, and become nothing more than pitiful creatures. Yet, they do not pity themselves, still talking about it eagerly, criticizing Hu Hualian, saying, "He is an unworthy and unfortunate child, an ignorant and eccentric person." Alas, how foolish!

    332

    发绣佛
    　　
    　　东海掠网寺，藏有绣佛一帧，绫本，长二丈四尺，横八尺。佛像科头披发，面如满月。胸前缨络，垂如蛛网。左手当胸，宛抚字；右手秉羽扇，下垂水纹袈裟。袒右臂，赤双趺，危立龙头黾背上。鼍龙半身在海涛中，四足摆簸，举头张吻，吐白毫，升空成楼阁台榭，日月山河。其下则飞蛇飞鱼，水母，争来朝拜，怪怪奇奇。佛目微睨，慈悲苦恼，意甚垂怜。其上则《金经》全卷，蝇头小楷，粲如列眉，末注嘉靖某甲子，优婆夷女弟子叶苹香盥沐发绣。帧左空隙，为伊戚丁尚书草隶，序绣佛颠末。文甚长，不复记忆，仅缀其略云：浙人叶公大钟，以翰林官侍御，秉性鲠峙，时以白简劾当道权贵。

    Explanation

    332

    A Buddhist Figure Embroidered with Hair

    The East Sea Lüewang Temple houses a piece of embroidered Buddhist figure, made on a silk base, measuring two zhang and four chi in length and eight chi in width. The Buddha's image is depicted with loose hair flowing down, his face round and full like a full moon. Adornments hang from his chest, resembling a spider's web. His left hand rests on his chest, as if gently touching a character, while his right hand holds a feather fan, with a water-patterned robe hanging below. His right arm is exposed, and his feet are red and bare, standing precariously on the back of a mythical creature with a dragon's head. Half of the creature is submerged in the sea waves, its four feet thrashing, its head raised, mouth open, emitting white rays, and ascending into the sky to form towers, pavilions, the sun, the moon, and mountains and rivers. Below it, flying snakes, fish, and jellyfish come to pay homage, creating a scene of strange and wondrous beauty. The Buddha's eyes gaze slightly downward, filled with compassion for suffering beings, showing deep pity. Above the figure is the entire scroll of the Golden Sutra, written in minute regular script, as clear as the lines of eyebrows. At the end, it is noted that it was created in the Jiajing era by a female lay follower named Ye Pingxiang, who washed and prepared the hair for the embroidery. On the left side of the frame, there is an inscription by the official Ding Shangfu, detailing the process of creating the embroidered Buddhist figure. The text is quite long, and I cannot remember all of it, but I will summarize the main points: Ye Gong Dazhong, a man from Zhejiang, served as a censor in the imperial bureaucracy, known for his upright and resolute character, and often used a white bamboo tablet to impeach powerful officials.

    333

    严分宜遗以书画鼎彝悦公心，公叱却之，劾益力。未几难作，海盗冤扳公曾行赇，革职，廷杖几死，诏收刑狱，论斩。公子伯仲，皆知名士，束手无策。公女号苹香，貌温婉，至孝。闻公陷死地，日夜祷神，得感应。年方十四龄，善刺绣，爰市巨绫，自摘头上发，以金刃薄如稻芒者，擘作四缕，绣佛与《金经》，二载始成。功德满日，而公亦邀天恩，以金鸡诏出狱矣。由是挂惠文冠，退老林下，不复预朝政。女自绣佛，目力已竭，双瞳遽盲，里中无与论婚者。公年六十，病卒，女年仅二十有五。弥留时涕泣，握长次两公子手，嘱善视盲妹，无令穷失所。两公子泣受教。讵兄虽谨奉遗命，而两嫂妖牝司晨，时谮小姑懒惰，渐加诟詈。

    Explanation

    333

    Yan Song gave paintings, calligraphy, and bronze vessels as gifts to please the official, but the official sternly rejected them, and the impeachment became more vigorous. Soon after, trouble arose, and pirates falsely accused the official of bribery. He was dismissed from office, nearly beaten to death with the rod, and imprisoned by imperial decree, sentenced to death. The official's sons, both elder and younger, were well-known scholars, but they were helpless and at a loss. The official's daughter was named Pingxiang, gentle in appearance and extremely filial. Hearing that her father was in mortal danger, she prayed to the gods day and night, and received divine response. She was only fourteen years old, skilled in embroidery, and bought large pieces of silk. She took her own hair from her head, with a golden blade as thin as rice straw, divided into four strands, and embroidered a Buddha and the Golden Sutra, which took two years to complete. On the day of her completed merit, the official also received divine favor and was released from prison by the imperial decree of the Golden Chicken. Thereafter, he hung up his official cap and retired to the woods, no longer involved in court affairs. After embroidering the Buddha, the daughter had exhausted her eyesight, and both eyes suddenly went blind. There was no one in the village to discuss marriage with her. The official, aged sixty, died of illness, and the daughter was only twenty-five years old. At the moment of death, she was tearing and weeping, took the hands of her two brothers, and entrusted them to take good care of her blind sister, not to let her fall into poverty and lose her place. The two brothers wept and accepted her instruction. But despite the elder brother's careful adherence to her last wishes, the two elder sisters-in-law, like hens, took charge, often slandered the younger sister-in-law for being lazy, and gradually increased their insults and abuse.

    334

    女日夜哭，两兄亦深恶而痛绝之矣。忽有昆仑女，丫髻，绿裤褶，举止娴雅，翩翩到门，问女曰：“姑姑病目，坐耗神损肝肺，非真盲也。妹能盗得天河升斗水，为姑姑银海生光，但瘥后何以酬妹德？”曰：“妹神灵，能使地狱人重见天日，无不如命。”曰：“姑姑善绣，乞绣双凤，一红一白，为天孙下嫁助妆，但勿遽点睛，恐其飞出也。”女曰：“诺。”昆仑女袖出金篦，稍稍刮大小眦，泪涔涔如铅泻。然后袖出小玉瓶，中储甘露，如人乳，倾滴眶中，嘱瞑坐炊许，启则双瞳中更有双瞳，光更清洁。又与神膏服之，胸怀恬适，郁愤捐除。

    Explanation

    334

    The girl wept day and night, and her two brothers also deeply hated and utterly despised her. Suddenly, a Kunlun girl arrived, with her hair tied in a bun at the top of her head, wearing a green pleated skirt, and moving with graceful and refined manner. She gracefully entered the door and asked the girl, "Your aunt has an eye illness, sitting and wasting her spirit, damaging her liver and lungs. She is not truly blind. Can you steal a bucket of water from the Milky Way to make her silver sea shine? But what can be done to repay your virtue after her recovery?" The girl replied, "I am a divine being, able to make people in hell see the light of day again, and nothing is beyond my command." The Kunlun girl said, "Your aunt is good at embroidery. She asks you to embroider two phoenixes, one red and one white, to help with the dowry for the celestial princess's marriage. But do not hastily add the eyes, for fear they will fly out." The girl said, "Yes." The Kunlun girl pulled out a golden comb from her sleeve and gently scraped the inner and outer corners of the eyes, causing tears to flow like lead. Then she pulled out a small jade bottle from her sleeve, which contained sweet dew, like human milk. She poured it into the eye sockets and instructed the girl to sit in meditation for a while. When she opened her eyes, the pupils contained even more pupils, and the light was even clearer. The Kunlun girl then gave her the divine ointment to take, and her heart and mind became peaceful and comfortable, discarding all resentment and anger.

    335

    明日果市巨绫来，视女手安于帧，每日来指点，彩丝综理，花样翻新，告成之日，即来替女点凤目，双凤忽栩栩活，飞落中庭，鼓翼如待。乃携女各跨一凤，乘云飞走。家人咸仰首，呼苹姑不应。昆仑女拨云下视，曰：“下界人不须惊讶，苹姑至孝，感动上苍天孙，天孙遣使奉迓，补天上针神缺，从此化去，量不再累嫂嫂看顾矣。”通城士女，无不见女冉冉空际若画图。焚香祈祷，呼苹姑，声如雷，两兄愧悔无地，嫂不为怪也。懊侬氏曰：吾乡山中有鸟，当春夏之交，且飞且鸣，曰：“嫂亏姑！”鸣至新秋始已。闻古有姑受嫂嫂凌虐死，精魂所化者。苹姑双瞳已盲，听此得无堕泪。幸至孝感动天仙，盲目复明，偕登云路。兄固知悔，嫂独恬然。彼英皇之爱首，独何人乎？唐人书“三日入厨，未谙姑性”之句，何其亲切而有味也！

    Explanation

    335

    The Next Day, a large piece of fine silk was indeed brought to the market. The girl placed her hands on the frame, and every day she came to give instructions, intricately weaving colorful threads, constantly renewing the patterns. On the day of completion, she immediately came to paint the phoenix eyes. The two phoenixes suddenly came to life, flying down to the courtyard and flapping their wings as if waiting. She then took the girl, each riding one phoenix, and they rode the clouds away. The family all looked up, calling out to Pinggu but receiving no response. A woman from the Kunlun Mountains parted the clouds to look down and said, "People of the lower realm do not need to be surprised. Pinggu was extremely filial, moving the celestial granddaughter. The celestial granddaughter sent an envoy to welcome her, to fill the vacancy of the celestial needle goddess. From now on, she will transform and will no longer burden your care." The women of the city all saw the girl slowly ascending into the sky like a painting. They burned incense and prayed, calling out to Pinggu, the sound like thunder. The two brothers felt ashamed and regretful, while the sister-in-law did not blame them. Annoyed, she said: In my hometown, there is a bird in the mountains that, at the transition between spring and summer, flies and calls, saying, "Sister-in-law mistreated Sister!" It calls until the beginning of autumn. Heard that in ancient times, a sister was mistreated and killed by her sister-in-law, transformed into a spirit. Pinggu's eyes were already blind; listening to this, would she not shed tears? Fortunately, her filial piety moved the celestial beings, restoring her sight, and together they ascended the path of the clouds. The brothers certainly knew they were in the wrong, while the sister-in-law remained calm and indifferent. Who is the beloved head of the British royal family? A Tang Dynasty poem says, "Three days into the kitchen, not yet familiar with the sister-in-law's character," how intimate and meaningful it is!

    336

    卷三
    　　
    　　大脚仙杀贼三快
    　　
    　　半截美人宋氏，甘泉人，归某甲。甲粗蠢，贫不能养母，赖美人为商家保母，得资奉甘旨。生有殊色，不施脂粉，不作时样妆，以裙下双趺，不作弓月样，故人皆呼为半截美人，其实即近今所谓黄鱼，所谓门槛里，又所谓大脚仙也。盐商某，慕其容，厚值致之。所乳子多肥白，又善伺主人意，惑之深。主妇偶审之，逐美人，子辄呱呱啼，美人转，子又咭咭然喜也，故得值恒倍于常。甲善博，资耗则索美人值，无怨也。咸丰三年，粤匪踞金陵，扬震恐，议降议御，纷纷不能定。

    Explanation

    336

    Volume 3
    　　
    The Three Speeds of the Bigfoot Fairy in Killing Bandits
    　　
    Cao Shi, known as the Half-beautiful woman, was from Ganquan and married to a certain Jia. Jia was coarse and stupid, poor and unable to support his mother, relying on Cao Shi as a merchant's wet nurse to provide delicious food for his mother. She had exceptional beauty, did not use cosmetics, did not wear fashionable makeup, and her feet were not arched, so people called her Half-beautiful woman. In fact, she was the same as what is now called a bigfoot, or a largefoot, and also known as the Bigfoot Fairy. A certain salt merchant admired her appearance and paid a high price to acquire her. The children she nursed were often fat and white, and she was good at reading the master's mind, deeply fascinating him. The mistress occasionally discovered this and expelled Cao Shi, but the children would cry, and when she returned, they would laugh and be happy. Therefore, the price she received was always twice the usual. Jia was good at gambling, and when his funds ran out, he would demand payment for Cao Shi, without complaint. In the third year of Xianfeng, the Taiping rebels occupied Nanjing, causing Yangzhou to be in terror, with discussions of surrender and defense, leading to confusion and indecision.

    337

    美人私说于主人曰：“降御皆非善策。扬俗奢，必灾，盍早营兔窟乎？”已而城陷，美人先夕出，将奉姑远徙。一黄衣贼目，突至其家，杀姑及夫，拥美人上马，键巨室中，将污之。美人含笑甘语以媚之曰：“郎在天朝何官？”贼屈拇指示之曰：“占天侯。”曰：“位已列爵，尚未经人道耶？长夜漫漫，杯酒相乐，若白昼活秘戏，得毋为将士笑乎？”贼大喜，开筵张乐。须臾月上，美人艳妆出，歌吴侑觞，韵可销魂荡魄。忽睹甲仗，手颤而股栗。贼醉睨曰：“卿何怖？”曰：“妾小家女也，见兵革能勿惊耶？”贼立命撤却。

    Explanation

    337

    The beauty privately told her master, "Submitting to rule is not a good strategy. Promoting luxury will surely bring disaster; why not build a rabbit's burrow early?" Soon after, the city fell. The beauty left the night before, intending to take her mother-in-law far away. A yellow-robed bandit leader suddenly arrived at her home, killed her mother-in-law and husband, carried the beauty onto a horse, and locked her in a large room, intending to defile her. The beauty smiled sweetly and spoke gently to flatter him, saying, "What position do you hold in the imperial court?" The bandit bent his fingers to indicate, "Heavenly Marquis." She replied, "You have already been granted a title, but have you not yet experienced human relations? The long night is endless; let us drink and be merry. If we engage in secret play during the day, will we not be laughed at by the soldiers?" The bandit was greatly pleased and set up a feast with music. Soon the moon rose, and the beauty appeared in her gorgeous makeup, singing Wu songs to accompany the wine, her voice melting one's soul and shaking one's spirit. Suddenly, she saw weapons, and her hands trembled while her legs shook. The bandit, drunk, looked at her and said, "Why are you afraid?" She replied, "I am just a girl from a small family; how could I not be frightened by weapons and warfare?" The bandit immediately ordered them to be removed.

    338

    顷又抱贼耳语，曰：“麾下将士，耽耽虎视，霎时我两人赴阳台，渠等穴壁看，得毋大扫兴！”贼即传令，各归伍退三舍，不唤汝，不入也。贼醉，乃代弛亵衣，裸而仰卧，昵声促美人寝，曰：“少缓。”乃自注水于浴器，一丝不挂，徐徐濯下体，渍渍有声。听贼鼾息，已十数转，虞其诈，故试以亵语，不应。遂柳眉倒竖，粉黛生杀气，视窗前月朗，刁斗远鸣，急索剪刀，就鞋底磨再四，跳登榻，跨贼身上，觑定咽喉，猛之。贼瞠目视美女，奋欲起，压之不得动，血喷出，满被褥，霎时毙矣。复拔剑刺其腹，肠出，乃止，展衾覆之。

    338

    He then hugged the thief and whispered in his ear, saying, "The soldiers under my command are hungrily watching, and in a moment, the two of us will go to the boudoir. If they dig holes in the wall to watch, won't it be very disappointing!" The thief immediately issued an order, instructing everyone to return to their posts and retreat three li. If not called, do not enter. The thief was drunk and undressed himself, lying naked on his back. He coaxed the beautiful woman to sleep, saying, "Wait a moment." He then poured water into the bathing container, completely naked, and slowly washed his lower body, with a dripping sound. Listening to the thief's snoring, which had already turned several times, he suspected a trick and tested with vulgar language, but received no response. Angered, he raised his eyebrows, a murderous aura appeared on his face, looking at the bright moon outside the window, and the watchman's drum sounded from afar. He hurriedly fetched a pair of scissors, sharpening them on the bottom of his shoe several times, jumped onto the bed, straddled the thief's body, stared at his throat, and stabbed him. The thief stared at the beautiful woman, trying to get up, but was pressed down so he could not move. Blood spurted out, covering the bedding, and in an instant, he died. Again, he drew the sword and stabbed his abdomen, causing his intestines to come out. He then stopped, spread the quilt over him, and covered his body.

    339

    听漏已四鼓，潜浣手，整衣出，户宵遁，望门投宿，不敢言，第诡云逃难者。贼中绘图索之，不可得。尝读《元史》，至正年濮州薛花娘杀贼一事，如窥谗鼎，如玩秘戏，半截美人，何其不侔而合耶？因思扬州女仆果艳冶，佣于商家，凭官媒写靠身纸，必须书刻己身怀六甲，防后患也。近日宴客，多招以侑觞，否则座客不欢，缠头之锦，竟多于缠足者。又一女陈姓阿脆，真州人，浪甚。冠陷时，女逸出，踽踽走西山，昼伏夜行，将奔大仪，寻伊姐妹行讨生活。至秦栏镇，以为距贼远，放胆行。偶思遗，遂循大溪，意入芦苇中私且歇。突一黄巾贼目，负洋枪佩刀，贸贸然从溪右来，两面皆水，不及避，反坐以待之。贼拉与乱，女正苦无川资，瞰贼腰缠累累，欣然就之。

    Explanation

    339

    After the fourth drumbeat, she secretly washed her hands, adjusted her clothes, and quietly left through the door, fleeing at night. She sought shelter at a door, but dared not speak, simply claiming to be a refugee. The bandits drew a picture to search for her, but could not find her. I once read the History of Yuan about the incident in Puzhou during the reign of Emperor Taiding where a woman named Xue Huan娘 killed bandits. It was like peering into a cauldron of slander, like playing a secret game, a half-completed beauty—how unalike yet how similar? Thinking about the beautiful maidservants in Yangzhou, who worked for merchants and relied on official matchmakers to write marriage certificates, they must be recorded as being pregnant to avoid remarriage. Recently, when entertaining guests, they were often invited to serve wine; otherwise, the guests would not be happy. The silk used to wrap the head was more than that used to wrap the feet. Another woman, Chen Aci, from Zhenzhou, was very promiscuous. When the city was captured, she escaped, wandering alone to the West Mountain, hiding during the day and traveling at night, intending to go to Dashi to find her sisters and make a living. She reached Qinlan Town, thinking it was far from the bandits, and dared to travel. Suddenly, she felt the need to relieve herself, so she followed the large stream, intending to enter the reeds to relieve herself and rest. Suddenly, she encountered a bandit leader with a foreign gun and a sword, stumbling along from the right bank of the stream. Both sides were water, unable to escape, so she sat and waited for him. The bandit pulled her into a fight. The woman was distressed by lack of funds, saw the bandit's money, and happily accepted.

    340

    贼脱女衣，一丝不挂，仰卧溪岸，而己则仅捋穷。女佯笑曰：“急色儿可笑！男女欢合，全赖裸抱，肌肤磨掺得趣。若此，则终是隔靴搔痒耳。”贼笑从之，甫近身，尚未解铃，女故作浪态，乘不意，遽搂之，滚入溪水中。女本江边产，向习流而善泅者，贼入水，四肢浮泛，女力捺下沉，三冒而三捺之，已作尾生桥下死矣。女抽刀断其头，取臂上金跳脱，席卷囊中黄白，着衣打包，从容负之去。临行，复回顾水际詈曰：“狗贼快乐耶？”后入安宜，嫁一少年郎，颇伉俪，称小康，移家秦邮。近已为子纳粟，称太母矣。

    Explanation

    340

    The bandit stripped the woman's clothes, leaving her completely naked, and lay face up on the riverbank, while he himself was only partially undressed. The woman pretended to laugh and said, "The eager one is laughable! The union of man and woman depends entirely on naked embrace, the rubbing of skin brings pleasure. If like this, it will always be like scratching through a boot." The bandit laughed and followed her, as soon as he got close, before he could undo the bell, the woman acted wantonly, seizing the opportunity, she suddenly embraced him, and they rolled into the stream. The woman, born by the river, was accustomed to the water and skilled at swimming. The bandit entered the water, his limbs floated, but she pressed him down, three times she surfaced and three times she pressed him down, and he died like a drowned man under the bridge. The woman drew her knife and cut off his head, took the gold bracelet from his arm, rolled up the yellow and white (gold and silver) into her bag, dressed herself, packed up, and calmly carried the items away. As she was leaving, she turned back and scolded from the water's edge, "Is the dog-bandit happy?" Later, she went to Anyi and married a young man, they were quite harmonious and lived a modestly comfortable life, and they moved their home to Qin You. Recently, she had her son admitted to the imperial service, and she is now called "Grandmother."

    341

    懊侬氏曰：人间最惨，莫如女子缠足声。母之于娇女也，虽爱若掌上珠；独缠得双趺，如酷吏之施毒刑，曾不能少加顾惜。主之督婢，鸨之饰雏，惨尤甚焉。每闻此声，辄痛东昏侯寡耻鲜廉，宜乎覆国。缠已纤纤，阿母意犹未足。及步步生莲花矣，而豺虎猝来，挪移倾仆，直恨无彩翼两飞耳。爱女之家亦曾记此乱离时乎？之三子者，谈笑不惊，或手刃之，或计赚之，而且尺二金莲，其行便捷，出入虎穴，极纡极闲。倘遇斯人，当破产以购之，一捧砚，一添香，一负剑，粉黛中饶有英气。又闻一周姓妇，吾乡挈东鄙人，自恃足大善走，难将及，先嘱良人挈子女潜遁，己则摒挡长物。

    Explanation

    341

    懊侬氏曰：人间最惨，莫如女子缠足声。母之于娇女也，虽爱若掌上珠；独缠得双趺，如酷吏之施毒刑，曾不能少加顾惜。主之督婢，鸨之饰雏，惨尤甚焉。每闻此声，辄痛东昏侯寡耻鲜廉，宜乎覆国。缠已纤纤，阿母意犹未足。及步步生莲花矣，而豺虎猝来，挪移倾仆，直恨无彩翼两飞耳。爱女之家亦曾记此乱离时乎？之三子者，谈笑不惊，或手刃之，或计赚之，而且尺二金莲，其行便捷，出入虎穴，极纡极闲。倘遇斯人，当破产以购之，一捧砚，一添香，一负剑，粉黛中饶有英气。又闻一周姓妇，吾乡挈东鄙人，自恃足大善走，难将及，先嘱良人挈子女潜遁，己则摒挡长物。

    342

    甫就绪，郊外边马已四出。无已，怀一利剪，出门，将觅小道，寻亲戚家暂避其锋。忽一贼目自远瞰瞰妇，似有风致，扬鞭追及，喝之止。妇亦不惧，含笑相迎，宛如旧识。下马推妇于地，将淫之，妇佯解裤带，而笑露其齿，嗤形于鼻，贼问云何，曰：“我惜子愚耳，子等跳梁，全赖骥足，设与我苟合时，马遽逸，奈何？”贼思其言颇近理，又能慰己，然四顾荒郊，无一树一石，可以揽辔，颇筹度。女云：“献一策，然后为所欲为。”贼求计甚急，女大声曰：“急煞儿，盍以缰系于两足乎！”贼抚掌称善，乃弯腰俯首，牢缚不少松。

    Explanation

    342

    The task is to translate a piece of Classical Chinese text into English. The text describes a woman who, after completing her preparations, encounters a bandit leader in a desolate countryside. The bandit leader, finding her charming, attempts to rape her, but she cleverly suggests tying the reins of his horse to his feet, allowing him to control the situation.

    Translation:

    Just as she had finished, the border horses outside the suburbs had already scattered in all directions. With no other choice, she carried a sharp knife and went out, intending to find a small path and visit a relative's house to temporarily avoid the danger. Suddenly, a bandit leader noticed her from afar, finding her charming. He whipped his reins, caught up with her, and ordered her to stop. The woman was not afraid, greeted him with a smile, as if they were old acquaintances. The bandit leader dismounted and pushed her to the ground, intending to rape her. The woman pretended to undo her belt, smiled, and showed her teeth, snorting through her nose. The bandit leader asked what she meant, and she replied, "I pity you for being foolish. You lot are causing trouble, completely relying on your strong legs. If we were to have sex, the horse would suddenly run away, what then?" The bandit leader thought her words were quite reasonable and also comforted himself, but looking around the desolate countryside, he saw not a single tree or rock where he could hold the reins. He pondered deeply. The woman said, "Offer a strategy, then do as you please." The bandit leader urgently sought a plan, and the woman shouted loudly, "You're in a hurry, why not tie the reins to your feet?" The bandit leader clapped and praised the idea, then bent down and tied the reins tightly without loosening them.

    343

    时妇之剪已在手，乘不意，蓦以剪刺马腹，马负痛，遽咆哮拖贼绝尘奔。剪在腹肉中，愈走愈摇，愈摇愈痛，痛则狂奔，如蹑电，如追风，十里外犹不辍，而贼已肤裂额烂，骨折气竭，不似人形矣。妇徐徐整衣裙，拾贼遗之包裹，遥望马拖贼去，觅路始行。及寻得良人，相与剪灯话终夜，吃吃笑不休。懊侬氏曰：缙绅家闺秀，原难尺二飞凫，使外观不雅，然亦何必过小。彼媸者，背曲肩驼，虽裙下解结极纤，亦非真丽；妍者体柔腰细，即裙下玉笋稍巨，何碍轻盈？总之以五六寸为准，庶合中庸。安得贤有司出示严禁，凡五六寸以外置不论；若五六寸以内，定求纤纤而翘翘者，即照妖冶诲淫论。

    Explanation

    343

    At the moment the woman had her scissors in hand, she seized the opportunity, suddenly thrusting the scissors into the horse's belly. The horse, in pain, roared and dragged the thief away at an incredible speed. The scissors were embedded in the horse's flesh, and as it ran faster, they shook more, causing more pain, which made the horse run even more wildly, like lightning and the wind. It did not stop even after ten miles, while the thief's skin split, his forehead rotted, his bones broke, and he was exhausted, no longer resembling a human. The woman slowly straightened her clothes, picked up the thief's abandoned package, and watched the horse drag the thief away before finding her way to continue her journey. When she finally found her husband, they spent the entire night talking by the light of a lamp, laughing uncontrollably.

    The author,懊侬氏, said: "For women from noble or scholarly families, it is difficult to have feet as long as a flying duck, as it makes them look unattractive. However, why must they be too small? Those who are deformed, with hunched backs and bent shoulders, even if their skirt laces are extremely thin, are not truly beautiful; those who are beautiful, with soft bodies and slender waists, even if their laces are slightly larger, do not hinder their grace. In summary, the ideal length should be five or six inches, which is in harmony with the middle way. How can competent officials issue a strict prohibition? Any length over five or six inches should be disregarded; if it is within five or six inches, then demand that the laces be both fine and raised, and punish according to the law on debauchery and seduction."

    344

    南郭秀才
    　　
    　　东鲁婚姻俗例，凡彩舆到门，女家必预缮一简，名启书，随新嫁娘送去。其辞无非吉利语，即如苏才、郭福、姬子、彭年之类也。有南郭秀才，本不羁士，因贫，馆田舍翁某甲家。甲有女，字某乙子，婚有日矣。甲告秀才曰：“某粗鄙，不解文字，将以启书浼先生。某固村，而亲家亦非雅，请先生务去陈言，别翻花样，说庄家本来面目，写农人老实因缘，庶免雷同，敬求椽笔。”秀才曰：“善。子当以黄鸡白酒，享我烂醉饱餐，看我挥毫洒翰，何如？”甲果如愿以偿。秀才作文曰：“伏以咬文嚼字，秀才当行；拙口笨腮，农人本色。冠既带乎平顶，礼休重乎尖酸。

    Explanation

    344

    The Scholar of South De
    　　
    In the eastern region of Lu, marriage customs dictate that when the colorful sedan chair arrives at the door, the bride's family must prepare a document called an "opening letter" (启书), which is sent along with the new bride. The contents of this letter are invariably auspicious, such as words like Sucai, Guofu, Jizi, and Pengnian. There was a scholar from South De who was not bound by conventional norms due to poverty, and he stayed at the home of a farmer named Mr. Jia. Mr. Jia had a daughter who was betrothed to the son of Mr. Yi, and the wedding was soon to take place.
