Mr. Jia asked the scholar, "I am crude and rustic, and I do not understand the intricacies of writing. I will entrust the task of writing the opening letter to you. Although I am from the countryside, my future father-in-law is also not refined. Please, sir, avoid clichés and find a unique way to express the true nature of our humble families and the honest character of farmers, so as to avoid repetition. I respectfully request your skilled pen." The scholar replied, "Very well. You should prepare a simple feast of yellow chicken and white wine, treat me to a hearty meal, and watch me wield my brush and write, how does that sound?" Mr. Jia indeed had his wish fulfilled. The scholar then wrote, "I humbly submit that nitpicking words is a scholar's duty; being clumsy and dull is the true nature of a farmer. The groom's hat has a flat top, and the etiquette does not demand sharpness or acidity."

    345

    恭维亲家老哥，耕耨事业，朴实人家，筑蜗牛之庐，黄坯当甓；铺牡蛎之路，绿柳成行。陈谷烂芝麻，真是小囤尖而大囤满；肥葱嫩韭菜，不减南园枣而北园桑。槽头喂板角之青，力能耕地；门前拴粉嘴之白，喊可惊天。而弟则徒守清贫，难期浊富。身穿四块瓦，露后遮前；头顶一盏灯，没棱少纬。伸出去两只赤手，缩回来一对空拳。闻你家令郎，才读诗书，即识一丁之字；愧我家大姐，甫知针黹，难堆满面之花。幸逢月下老人，得配人间佳偶。伏愿女知静好，男解爱怜。孝顺公婆，和睦妯娌。养儿做极大官员，改其门而换其户；生女织许多布匹，长其财而肥其家。

    Explanation

    345

    Praising my father-in-law, a man of simple and honest farming, he builds a humble abode like a snail's shell, using yellow clay instead of bricks; he paves a path with oyster shells, with rows of green willows. His old grain and spoiled sesame truly fill both small and large granaries; his fresh and tender vegetables are no less abundant than jujubes in the south and mulberry trees in the north. He feeds the oxen with green fodder at the stable, strong enough to plow fields; a white cow with a pink muzzle tied in front of the door, its moo can be heard far away. But I, on the other hand, merely live a simple and poor life, hard to expect wealth and prosperity. I wear a simple robe made of four pieces of cloth, covering the back but leaving the front exposed; I wear a simple hat, lacking edges and threads. I extend two bare hands, bringing back empty fists. I hear that your son-in-law, just beginning to read poetry and books, knows only a few characters; I feel ashamed of my eldest daughter, who just learns to sew and embroidery, hard to make her face full of flowers. Fortunately, we met the matchmaker, and found an ideal couple in the world. I hope the daughter knows peace and goodness, and the son understands love and care. They should be filial and respectful to in-laws, and get along well with sisters-in-law. May they raise a son to become a great official, changing the door and replacing the gate; may they raise a daughter to weave many pieces of cloth, increasing wealth and enriching the family.

    346

    趁此良辰，图其好事。行见三村五舍，牵来告朔饩羊；会看黄酒白烧，醉倒奔泉渴骥。五百年冤业，棒打不开；一肚皮牢骚，写来好笑。临启雀跃，忭颂莫名。”秀才书就，颇自负，甲听其雒诵，亦为之首肯。讵乙与贺客传视，莫不以为讥诮，且以“渴骥”句，比客为畜；以“冤业”句，视乙非人。乙大怒，朱陈会晤时，挥以老拳，遂致雀鼠。适邑宰亦援例出身者，之无莫辨。见两家争讼甚急，穷执笔人，以秀才对。遂飞签械至，与以夏楚。秀才不服，语侵长官，乃付广文箝禁，据实申详，以为兴大狱矣。

    Explanation

    346

    Take advantage of this auspicious occasion to aim for a favorable outcome. Soon you will see three villages and five farms, bringing offerings of sheep for the ritual sacrifice; you will enjoy good wine and become intoxicated, like a thirsty horse galloping to a spring. A long-standing grudge, spanning five hundred years, cannot be easily resolved; a belly full of complaints, when written down, is amusing. At the time of the event, there was great excitement and joy, joyfully praising without reason. The scholar, having written it, was quite proud of himself, and A listened to his recitation aloud, nodding in agreement. But B and the guests passed the writing around to look at, all finding it amusing, and even using the phrase "thirsty horse" to compare the guest to an animal, and the phrase "long-standing grudge" to see B as not human. B became very angry, and during a meeting with A, he hit him with his fists, leading to a chaotic situation. The county magistrate, who had risen through the ranks by following precedents, was unable to distinguish clearly. Seeing the two parties in a heated dispute, he questioned the person who wrote it, identifying him as the scholar. He then sent a summons to bring the scholar in by force and punished him with corporal punishment. The scholar disagreed and insulted the superior official, so he was handed over to the school for confinement, and the facts were reported in detail, thinking it would lead to a major legal case.

    347

    而上游瞩之，狂笑不已，判牍尾云：“缀俗成文，不过秀才游戏；小题大作，足征县主糊涂。夏楚枉及无辜，冬烘是其本色。而两亲家兴讼，只为不通；百里侯申详，何其多事。但启书别样，机趣横生；当付彼广文，为诸生逞才之炯戒。且罚汝薄俸，酬文人遭拍之冤刑。两造逐回，一批绝倒”。宰奉拟，甚惶恐，而秀才亦摇摆出牢笼矣。懊侬氏曰：语云：“对不识字人，莫作才语。”读书人不可不知。余向好弄笔，频遭不韪之名，良由鄙夫俗子，知识虽鲜，而忌讳颇多也。昔有村学究，为东人书联，有“老熊如鹤健”之句，东人大骂云：“仆即陋，何至比为老熊？”学究百辩莫信，遂致解馆。噫，鼎彝珠玉，原不能执担粪人辨其真赝耳。

    Explanation

    347

    The magistrate, upon reading the scholar's writing, laughed uncontrollably and wrote at the end of his judgment: "Stringing together mundane words into text, it's nothing more than a game for a scholar; making a big deal out of a small matter, it sufficiently proves the magistrate's foolishness. Punishment falls on the innocent, and his dull character is evident. The two in-laws sue each other, only because they are uneducated; the hundred-mile lord submits a detailed report, how many unnecessary things. Opening the book reveals something different, abundant wit and humor; give it to the schoolmaster, as a warning for the students to show off their talents. Punish you with a slight reduction in salary, as compensation for the injustice suffered by scholars. Both parties are sent back, one batch is utterly defeated." The magistrate, following the verdict, was very frightened, and the scholar also swayed out of his difficult situation. Ah Nong says: It is said, "Do not speak eloquently to those who cannot read." Literate people should not forget this. I have always enjoyed writing, and often suffered unjust criticism, largely because of vulgar and ignorant people who, though lacking knowledge, have many taboos. Once there was a village schoolmaster who wrote a couplet for a local person, which included the line "An old bear as strong as a crane." The local person angrily said, "I am already humble, why compare me to an old bear?" The schoolmaster defended himself with many arguments but was not believed, and thus he was dismissed from his position. Ah, ritual vessels and precious jewels, originally cannot be held by someone carrying manure to distinguish their authenticity.

    348

    驴化为履
    　　
    　　东台某镇，有富翁朱叟，拥厚资，而悭吝殊甚。体患疥，与人较锱铢，恒狡赖，故里人呼之曰“癞皮狗”。为子延师，馆于家，多以冷字问师，师略嗫嚅，即云不通，摄揄之。人多裹足不敢就邑。邑佟生，滑稽士也，贫无已，俯就其馆。甫莅皋比，即大书一“”字于壁下，注云：“人能识得，方许以冷字问我。”翁遍搜六书八法，广询名儒硕彦，不可得其音。婉询于生，笑不答。翁于市上购物，必精择其价之廉者，买鱼为膳，非腐败不入门，人问之，曰：“吾恐伤生耳。

    Explanation

    348

    A Donkey Transforms into Shoes

    In a certain town in Dongtai, there was a wealthy old man named Zhu, who possessed great wealth but was extremely stingy. He suffered from scabies and always argued over the smallest details, often deceiving and denying others, so the villagers called him "Scabby Dog." He hired a tutor for his son, who taught at home, and often asked the tutor difficult or obscure questions. The tutor would hesitate and say he didn't understand, so he would ridicule him. Many people hesitated to teach in his town. A townsman named Tong, a witty and humorous scholar, was poor and unable to refuse, so he accepted the tutoring position. As soon as he arrived at the teaching position, he immediately wrote a large blank space on the wall, noting: "Only if someone can identify it will I allow them to ask me difficult questions." The old man searched through all six scripts and eight methods, and widely consulted famous scholars, but could not find its meaning. He gently asked the scholar, who smiled and did not reply. The old man always carefully selected the cheapest items when shopping, and he bought fish for meals, only accepting rotten ones. When asked, he said, "I'm afraid of harming the fish's life."

    349

    ”一日，邻家豕瘟死，人以为有毒，不敢食。独翁以半价买归，剖而腌作脯，每夕登盘。生误食，欲呕，因拈“瘟猪肉”三字嘱徒对，徒蹙额，苦无对，转求教，生笑曰：“蠢才俯拾即是，何不径对‘癞皮狗’？尚不工巧耶？”适催租隶来，翁畏而勉留饭，即邀与生同案餐。盘有咸猪首，生吟曰：“盘中尚有猪头肉，座上何来狗腿差。”租隶闻之，愧逸去。一日，有陕客牵驴来镇，乞于市，云断资斧不能归，求众援，不应。客叹曰：“吾馁甚，实力穷，本拟乘驴返，今欲货之，急切无售主。盍杀之，货驴肉较易也，且肉值廉，仅取价常之半。

    Explanation

    349

    One day, a neighbor's pig died of an epidemic, and people believed it was poisonous and did not dare to eat it. Only the old man bought the pig at half price, cut it open, salted it to make jerky, and placed it on the table every night. The student accidentally ate it and felt nauseous. He picked up the phrase "infected pork" and asked the disciple to compose a couplet. The disciple frowned, struggling to come up with a response, and turned to ask for help. The student laughed and said, "There are plenty of stupid people, why not just answer with 'scabby dog'? Isn't that still clever?" Just as the tax collector arrived, the old man, fearing him, reluctantly invited him to stay for a meal and invited the student to share the meal. The dish had salted pork head, and the student said, "There is still pork head on the plate, but what kind of tax collector do we have who has a dog's leg?" The tax collector left in shame. One day, a guest from Shaanxi brought a donkey to the town and begged in the market, saying he had no money to return home and asked for help, but no one responded. The guest sighed, saying, "I am very hungry and have no strength left. I originally planned to ride the donkey back, but now I want to sell it, but there is no buyer in a hurry. Why not kill it? Selling donkey meat is easier, and the meat is cheap, only half the usual price."

    350

    ”因假屠刀挥之，驴首断，血缕缕湿街市尘，再加脔切成块，系以草缕挂壁上，人争售之，顷刻去其半。翁闻之，急携钱，尽购其剩者归。客醵货肉钱，得十竿，太息徒步去。翁归，以驴肉渗盐，储于翁，剖小小一脔，炊于釜，欢忭庆喜，不可名言。厨婢燃薪煮，移时，偶揭釜盖，睨其生熟，大惊，盖内突化为烂草履一双。告翁，大骇诧，视瓮中，则满满皆双不借。问邻家有货肉者，亦如是。然邻货肉少，不似翁之多，盖游方术士，用障眼法，破悭囊者，翁不知也。愧悔叫骂，又不可名言。

    Explanation

    350

    Using a butcher's knife, he struck the donkey's head, severing it; blood dripped in streaks, wetting the dust of the street market. He then sliced the meat into chunks, tied them with grass threads, and hung them on the wall. People competed to buy it, and in a moment, half of it was gone. The old man heard about it, hurriedly took money, and bought all the remaining pieces, returning home. The guests pooled money to buy the meat, received ten units of money, and went away on foot. The old man returned home, salted the donkey meat, stored it, cut it into small pieces, cooked it in a pot, and rejoiced and celebrated, inexpressibly. The kitchen maid lit the fire to cook, after some time, accidentally lifted the lid of the pot, looked at its doneness, and was greatly surprised: inside the pot suddenly turned into a pair of rotten straw shoes. She told the old man, who was greatly shocked, and he looked into the jar, finding it full of pairs of straw shoes. He asked the neighbors who had sold meat, and they also had the same experience. But the neighbors sold less meat, not as much as the old man, likely because it was a wandering magician using a trick of misdirection to break into the miser's purse, which the old man did not know. He felt ashamed and regretful, and shouted in anger, again inexpressible.

    351

    生闻之，大笑捧腹，戏仿《月令》句，粘于壁云：“是月也，骗子至，悭囊破，铜钱去，驴肉入釜化为履，癞狗无声。”翁见之，益怒生无礼，年终解馆，嘱人示意，请另就。生曰：“诺。”即刻解馆。翁盛治觞，送生行，甫执匕，翁盛服跪地叩有声，询所求，曰：“师所书‘’字，老汉几闷成瘿，乞明示，救残喘。”曰：“此‘牛’字，翁不识耶？”曰：“何无一悬针？”生笑曰：“渠倔强，好以冷字炫人；又贪婪，吝于资。强在筋，故抽去脊筋耳。”闻者莫不大笑。懊侬氏曰：海滨之鱼，有名“草鞋底”者；釜中之脔，竟亦化为双不借耶？夫履，适足之物也，术人岂勖翁以知足之意乎？翁不知之，犹然怒骂。师之戏也，徒取怨尤。

    Explanation

    351

    The student heard it and burst into laughter, holding his belly, playfully imitating a phrase from the Yueling (Monthly Ordinances) and posting it on the wall: "In this month, the con man arrives, the stingy bag breaks, copper coins go, mule meat enters the pot and transforms into shoes, the leprous dog is silent." The old man saw it and became even more angry at the student's disrespect, and at the end of the year, he dissolved the school, instructing someone to hint that the student should leave. The student said, "Yes," and immediately left the school. The old man prepared a grand feast, saw the student off, and just as he was about to serve the food, the old man, dressed formally, knelt on the ground, knocking with sound, and asked what he sought. He said, "The character written by the teacher, ' ' has nearly made the old man suffocate with a goiter; please explain clearly to save my remaining breath." The student replied, "This is the character for 'cow,' does the old man not recognize it?" The old man asked, "Why is there no vertical stroke?" The student laughed and said, "He is stubborn, likes to impress people with obscure characters; he is also greedy, stingy with money. His strength lies in the sinews, so the spine has been removed." Those who heard it could not help but laugh. The Aonong said: Fish from the sea, named "grass shoe sole"; chunks in the pot, have also transformed into double shoes? Shoes are suitable for the feet, but could the magician be encouraging the old man to know contentment? The old man did not understand, and still angrily cursed. The teacher's jest only invited resentment.

    352

    树孔中小人
    　　
    　　广省澳门岛，有居人，姓仇名端，时随海舰出外洋，贸易各国。一日，遇飓风，船中老大，面无人色。洪涛巨浪中，隐隐现古岛，因急就而舣其舟，得无恙。少顷风息，老大等持篙弄楫，力已惫。仇登岛散步，见岛中枯树甚多，大可十围。树多孔，孔中有小人居之，人长仅七八寸，有老幼男妇、妍媸尊卑之别，肤色如栗子皮。每人身上系小腰刀弓矢等物，大小与人称。见仇窥之，齐声曰：“蜡渠三尹利。”仇适思遗，即解裤蹲地上，并就石钻火吸烟。

    Explanation

    352

    In the tree holes of an ancient island in Macau, Guangdong Province, there lived little people. A resident named Cao Duan was traveling with a naval ship overseas when he encountered a typhoon. The captain of the ship looked pale. In the midst of the raging waves, a distant ancient island appeared. They hastily docked their boat and escaped unharmed. After the wind ceased, the crew, exhausted, rested. Cao climbed the island to take a walk and saw many dead trees, some as thick as ten people could encircle. The trees had many holes, and in each hole lived a little person, only about 7-8 inches tall, with distinctions of age, gender, beauty, and social status. Their skin color was like chestnut skin. Each little person wore small knives, bows, and arrows, appropriately sized for their stature. When Cao looked at them, they all said together, "Wax channel three officials and benefits." Cao just thought of relieving himself and undressed, squatting on the ground, and used a stone to drill fire and smoke.

    353

    忽闻人声嘈嘈，如秋塘凫雏，结队而至。惊视之，见枯树最高处，有小城郭，高可及膝，皆黑石砌就。城门大启，小人约千余，联臂而出。摇旗一呼，各树孔中皆有小人出迎，拱听号令。其中有年轻者，面目端正，束发紫金冠，双雉尾，银锁甲，骑拳大鸡雏，指挥如意，口喃喃不知作何语，旋闻众应曰：“希利！”执坚拥至。仇大骇，知为驱己，然藐其小，不甚恐怖，蹲如故。年轻者，又喃喃多时，仇不应，即挥众与战。小箭小枪，小刀小戈矛，钻刺两股，颇痛。恶之，戏以手中烟筒击年轻者，一击，遽翻落鸡背上，毙矣。

    Explanation

    353

    Suddenly, there was a noisy commotion, like the sound of young ducks in a pond, and a group of people arrived. Startled, I looked up and saw a small city at the top of a withered tree, about knee-high, built entirely of black stones. The city gate was wide open, and about a thousand small people emerged, holding arms together. They waved flags and shouted, and from each hole in the trees, small people came out to greet them, standing respectfully to listen to commands. Among them was a young man, with a proper and handsome appearance, wearing a purple-gold crown with double pheasant tails, silver armor, and riding a small chicken. He directed things as he pleased, murmuring in a language that was not understood, and soon heard the crowd respond with "Hili!" They held firmly and came close. Chou was greatly frightened, knowing they were driving him, but looked down on their smallness, not very frightened, and sat as before. The young man murmured for a long time, and Chou did not respond, so he ordered the crowd to fight. Small arrows, spears, knives, halberds, and other weapons pierced both thighs, causing quite painful injuries. Chou hated it and playfully struck the young man with his pipe. One strike, and he immediately fell off the chicken's back and died.

    354

    众攫尸回，城坚闭，其余皆窜入树孔中。仇亦回船。夜静，闻岸上小人大至，掷砂泥而呼曰：“梨二师四尹利！”鸡鸣始寂。仇枕上自思，若攫得一二头回故里，转可炫攫孔方。翌晨，托言采薪，携斧与布袋之故处。甫破一树，其中小人甚伙，尚有酣眠未醒者，仇一一拾而装布袋中。约略一门眷属，无一逸者。归舟，潜以饭哺之，亦食，而尤好食松子果品。正拟复往，而岸上小人如蚁集蜂屯，如恒河沙数，口喃喃若詈骂，且小箭如雨，船人怨恐，解缆去。月余回广，以之问名宿，咸以为僬侥国人。

    Explanation

    354

    The crowd returned with the corpses, the city was strongly closed, and the rest all fled into the tree holes. The enemy also returned to their boat. At night, they heard a large number of small people on the shore, throwing sand and mud and shouting, "Lí, two teachers, four masters, and four officials are profitable!" It became quiet again at the crow of the rooster. The enemy lay in bed and thought to himself, if he could grab one or two and return to his hometown, he could show off the grabbed coins. The next morning, he claimed to gather firewood and carried an axe and a cloth bag to the former place. Just breaking open one tree, there were many small people inside, still those who were soundly asleep and had not woken up. The enemy picked them up one by one and put them in the cloth bag. Approximately one family was captured, none escaped. Returning to the boat, he secretly fed them with food, they also ate, but especially liked eating pine nuts and fruit. Just planning to go back again, but on the shore, the small people gathered like ants and bees, like the number of grains of sand in the Ganges River, muttering as if cursing, and small arrows like rain. The boatmen were resentful and fearful, and untied the ropes to leave. After more than a month, he returned to Guangzhou, asked the famous scholars about it, all thought they were people from the land of the Jiao Ráo.

    355

    问洋人，云是物能腌以腊，其味甚甘。一人不敢独行，恐为海鹄衔去耳。仇喜，于市上设布障，置小人于盒，周围嵌水晶片，观者如看洋画，得资甚富。时都转某公爱之，授意于盐贾，贾出千金购去，雕紫檀作小屋宇，前后三进，两旁游廊，其中更设几案床幔，衣箱奁具等器，即日献都转，宝若连城。小人以幼者为尊，时见年老者折腰揖孩提；又以妇人为重，时见须眉者屈首向巾帼。一昼夜宿三次，盖以一日为三日也。男妇各就一处宿，每宿必媾，然不容人偷瞰，瞰则羞愤，拔刀自刎死。又最畏雷，闻雷声须装于瓮中，藏地窖内。

    Explanation

    355

    Inquiring of foreigners, they said this thing can be preserved with salt and dried, and its taste is very sweet. One person dares not walk alone, for fear of being taken away by a sea goose. The merchant was delighted and set up a cloth screen in the market, placing little people in boxes, with crystal pieces embedded around them. Viewers looked at it as if it were a foreign painting, and he earned a lot of money. At that time, a certain official in the capital loved it and instructed the salt merchant. The merchant paid a thousand gold pieces to buy it, carving purple sandalwood into small houses with three courtyards in front and back, and corridors on both sides. Inside, he set up tables, beds, and curtains, as well as clothing boxes, makeup boxes, and other items. He immediately presented it to the official, who valued it as if it were worth a city. The little people regarded the younger ones as superior, often seeing older ones bowing to young children; and they also regarded women as important, often seeing men bowing to women. They stayed overnight three times in one day and night, because they considered one day as three days. Men and women each stayed in one place, and they must engage in intimate relations every night. But they did not allow others to peep; if peeped at, they felt ashamed and angry, and killed themselves with a knife. They were also most afraid of thunder; when they heard thunder, they must be placed in jars and hidden in underground cellars.

    356

    豢久之，渐通人意旨，每见都转所蓄艳妾顽童，必叩首；若见道学龙钟老辈者，匿不出。爱人着鲜衣阔服，见必舞刀弄棍献诸技；若见破帽残衫者，必争出指之怒詈曰：“蒜平尹利！”性最妒，见人有技能者，必效其所为，不成，又詈曰：“苛二乌三尹利！”性又最疑，防人窃窃耳语；然又畏大声疾呼，高谈阔论者。每闻声必詈曰：“饭平饭平，师二尹利！”都转爱之深，遂禁人对小人语，语必代小人詈，以媚小人，而小人益横矣。

    Explanation

    356

    After being kept for a long time, the small person gradually understood human intentions. Whenever they saw the beautiful concubines and mischievous children kept by the high-ranking official, they would bow; but if they saw elderly scholars, they would hide and not come out. They loved to wear bright clothes and broad garments, and whenever they saw someone in such attire, they would dance with swords, play with sticks, and display various skills; but if they saw someone wearing a tattered hat and ragged clothes, they would strive to come out and angrily scold, saying, “Garlic flat, Sir Yi Li!” They had the most jealousy, and whenever they saw someone with skills, they would imitate what others did, and if unsuccessful, they would scold, saying, “Strict two, bird three, Sir Yi Li!” They also had the most suspicion, guarding against people whispering secretly; yet they feared loud shouting and those who spoke loudly and argued. Whenever they heard a sound, they would scold, saying, “Food flat, food flat, Sir Er Yi Li!” The high-ranking official loved the small person deeply, so he forbade people from speaking to the small person, and anyone who spoke must speak on behalf of the small person, to flatter the small person, and thus the small person became even more arrogant.

    357

    都转谕像生店，制小纱帽，玉带袍笏，小兜牟，铠甲兵器与小人，争着之，摇足作学究状，跪拜如官人状。渐引导学串戏，性又最灵，不数日，已能演五六折，但舞虽中肯，而歌则不辨为何腔也。有时与以铜钱，则爱不释手，口咬脚踏，而钱不能碎，则又嘤嘤啼。仆人偶有相扑骂为戏者，小人见之，手足舞蹈，乐不可支，由是知小人喜人扑与骂。小人有时戚戚思岛屿，都转必命仆人扑骂，博小人欢。又为小人制小匾额，悬木盒上，曰：“犹傲螟。”又曰：“罔谈彼短。”自制小合头房，题联曰：“槐郡能游，芥舟可渡；壶天不远，橘隐非诬。”小人见之，知向都转鞠跽谢。

    Explanation

    357

    The official instructed the lifelike figure shop to create a small silk cap, a jade belt, ceremonial robes, a small helmet, armor, and weapons for the small person. The small person competed for them, shaking their feet and assuming the posture of a learned scholar, kneeling and bowing like an official. Gradually, they were guided to learn and perform plays. The small person was exceptionally intelligent and, within a few days, could already perform five or six acts. However, while their dance was appropriate, their singing was indistinguishable in terms of style. Sometimes, when given copper coins, the small person loved them so much that they wouldn't let go, biting and stepping on them. When the coins didn't break, they would cry softly. Servants sometimes mocked and scolded each other as a game, and the small person, seeing this, danced with joy, unable to contain their happiness. Thus, it was known that the small person enjoyed being mocked and scolded. At times, the small person felt sad and thought of islands, and the official would command the servants to mock and scold them to make the small person happy. The official also made a small plaque for the small person, hanging it on a wooden box, inscribed with the words, "Still proud like a gnat," and "Do not speak ill of others." The official then made a small room and inscribed a couplet on it: "The槐郡 can be traveled, the芥舟 can be crossed; the壶天 is not far, the橘隐 is not false." The small person saw this and knew to bow and kneel in gratitude to the official.

    358

    年余，审其安之，开木盒面玻璃门，小人间出，而游于文房左右。一日，都转他去，遗红顶花翎帽于几，小人瞰其状，手扳红顶，两人对踢之如球；摘下孔雀毛，两人互扛之，扫案上灰如帚。正嬉笑间，都转至，小人争弃顶翎逸。顶坠地，碎矣；翎落火盆中，焚矣。而都转不怒，反顾之笑。明日，有年家子衣冠谒都转，偶游书院，正值小人跃几上，年家子不知为何物，大惊失声，狂呼曰：“怪哉！”视小人，已惊毙其二，余者皆潜盒中，詈曰：“娇三尼二，师二尹利！”都转由是深恶年家子，置不理。

    Explanation

    358

    After a year, the supervisor examined if it was safe, opened the wooden box with a glass door, and the small person emerged, moving around the study. One day, the supervisor went elsewhere, leaving a red-topped feathered hat on the table. The small person observed the hat, held and bounced the red top, and two people kicked it back and forth like a ball. They took off the peacock feathers, carried them between them, and swept the ashes on the table like a broom. In the midst of laughter and play, the supervisor arrived, and the small persons rushed to discard the hat and feathers and escape. The hat fell to the ground and broke; the feathers fell into the fire basin and were burned. The supervisor was not angry but rather looked at them and laughed.

    The next day, a young man from the same household, dressed formally, paid respects to the supervisor, happening to visit the academy. Just as the small person jumped onto the table, the young man screamed in shock, yelling, "Strange!" Seeing the small persons, two of them were startled to death, and the rest hid in the box, muttering, "Three sweet nuns, two teachers, and two officials." The supervisor deeply disliked the young man from the same household and ignored him.

    359

    懊侬氏曰：《神异经》云：“西海之外有鹄国，其人长七寸，有礼好经，能跪拜，行如飞，日行千里，百物不敢犯。唯畏海鹄，遇辄吞之，在鹄腹中不死。然皆多寿，有寿至千年者，”不闻其一惊即死也。又西北荒中有小人，其君朱衣黄冠，辂车乘马而出，人遇其出，抓而食之，其味辛。能识万物名字，杀腹中三尸虫。然皆小一分，不似其长七寸也。又《辍耕录》中载海外有名靖人者，亦长七寸，然皆露处，不闻其有城郭也。《熙朝新语》载僬侥国曾遣使入贡，其使为纱绛袍，后拥曲柄小伞，然尚长如五岁儿，不闻其如是之小也。藐矣幺麽，其所谓小人之尤者欤？

    Explanation

    359

    Translation

    懊侬氏曰：《神异经》云：“西海之外有鹄国，其人长七寸，有礼好经，能跪拜，行如飞，日行千里，百物不敢犯。唯畏海鹄，遇辄吞之，在鹄腹中不死。然皆多寿，有寿至千年者，”不闻其一惊即死也。又西北荒中有小人，其君朱衣黄冠，辂车乘马而出，人遇其出，抓而食之，其味辛。能识万物名字，杀腹中三尸虫。然皆小一分，不似其长七寸也。又《辍耕录》中载海外有名靖人者，亦长七寸，然皆露处，不闻其有城郭也。《熙朝新语》载僬侥国曾遣使入贡，其使为纱绛袍，后拥曲柄小伞，然尚长如五岁儿，不闻其如是之小也。藐矣幺麽，其所谓小人之尤者欤？

    懊侬氏曰：According to the Shen Yi Jing (Classic of the Marvelous): "Beyond the Western Sea there is a country of swans called the Swancountry. The people of this country are seven inches tall, well-mannered, and skilled in etiquette. They can kneel and bow, move like flying, travel a thousand miles in a day, and nothing dares to harm them. They fear only the sea swan, which, when encountered, will swallow them whole. Yet they do not die inside the swan’s belly. They all live long lives, some living up to a thousand years." It is not heard that they die from a single shock. In the northwest wilderness, there are also small people. Their ruler wears a red robe and a yellow crown, rides out in a chariot and on horseback. When people encounter them, they are seized and eaten; their taste is pungent. They can recognize the names of all things and expel the three worms from their bellies. However, they are only about one inch tall, not seven inches as described. The Zhuangzi mentions a people called the Jing people who also live beyond the seas, and they are seven inches tall. They live in the open and are not heard to have cities or walls. According to the Xichao Xin Yu (New Sayings of the Western Court), the country of the Jiao Ryo once sent an envoy to pay tribute. The envoy wore a red-silk robe and carried a small umbrella with a curved handle, but he was still as tall as a five-year-old child. It is not heard that they are as small as this. How insignificant and tiny they are, indeed the most insignificant of small people!

    360

    楠将军
    　　
    　　吾乡石梁镇，当元季，有古刹，为梁武帝所建。殿宇甚宏，院产尤沃，秃奴橐富，不知焚修而好淫，藏美妓于地窖中，外人不审也。时正修缮，工匠满室。有漆工某，正操垩丹漆，忽睹梁上有光，倏一砖坠地，上有守宫二，睛赤髯苍，鳞爪沃雪，一瞥眼，已长尺有咫。正痴睨，忽闻人语曰：“此龙也。”守宫腾起，众争逸。工不及奔，伏几下，适蒙师供圣孔子木主，即戴于首，悚怖不敢动。闻雷声大震，雌电飞驰，云雾中，犹睹门外一青龙至，首横尺木，丹书若符，角杈，裹两黄绢。门小，龙首碍，因侧角蜿蜒入。门内守宫突化为小龙，若迎迓戴尺木者。

    Explanation

    360

    The General Nan
    　　
    During the late Yuan Dynasty, in my hometown of Shilang Town, there was an ancient temple built by Emperor Wu of Liang. The temple was grand and spacious, with fertile land and abundant resources. The monks were wealthy but neglected their religious duties and engaged in immoral behavior, keeping beautiful women in underground chambers, which outsiders were unaware of. At the time, the temple was undergoing renovation, and the room was full of workers. One of the craftsmen, a painter, was working with lime, red, and black pigments. Suddenly, he saw a light on the beam, and a brick fell from the ceiling, revealing two lizards on it. The lizards had red eyes and gray whiskers, and their scales and claws were as white as snow. In an instant, they grew to a significant size. The painter was mesmerized, and suddenly heard someone say, "These are dragons." The lizards jumped up, and the crowd fled in panic. The painter did not have time to run and instead hid under a table. He had just placed a wooden tablet of Confucius on the table and wore it on his head. He was filled with fear and immobility. He heard the sound of thunder and the flash of lightning. In the clouds and fog, he saw a green dragon approaching the temple, its head carrying a wooden tablet with red writing, resembling a talisman. Its horns were adorned with yellow silk. The door was too small for the dragon's head, so it entered by turning its head sideways. Inside the temple, the lizards suddenly transformed into small dragons, welcoming the larger dragon carrying the wooden tablet.

    361

    略一转身，瓦砾飞舞，视青龙攫宫脊宝瓶中珠，大如碗；两小龙各挟楠木梁，拍打若斗，屋宇楼阁一齐成齑粉。少顷，雨霁，烟雾散，工晕而复苏，所戴木主，犹耸立不动，而庙则乌有，僧则更不可问。遍地积水浸瓦砾。走告市人，奔视之，唯正殿基陈设女子净桶莲舄数事而已。其雨之倾盆时也，远村见黑云如山，垂垂扑庙下，旋即腾上，犹约略见龙伸巨爪，持殿梁舞。旋闻甓湖边渔人云：“是日龙以双梁斗空际，移时，蓦然抛坠湖心，泛泛忽不见。”已而每逢阴雨，湖入则闻两木相撞声登登，止则天霁，验之不爽。由明季至昭代，梁在湖中，受日星精气，渐为厉虐。

    Explanation

    361

    A swift turn, and debris flew in all directions. Looking up, one saw the Azure Dragon seizing a pearl from a vase on the palace ridge, as large as a bowl; two smaller dragons each holding camphor wood beams, striking each other as if in combat, causing houses and buildings to crumble into dust. Shortly after, the rain ceased, the smoke and mist dissipated, and the workers, who had fainted, regained consciousness. The wooden idol they wore still stood tall and unmoved, but the temple was nowhere to be found, and the monks were also untraceable. The ground was covered with water soaking the debris. People ran to the scene to see what had happened, and only a few items remained in the base of the main hall: a woman's toilet and lotus shoes. During the torrential rain, distant villagers saw dark clouds like mountains approaching the temple, then quickly rising, and faintly seeing a dragon with huge claws holding the roof beams and dancing. Soon after, fishermen by the lake reported that on that day, the dragon had fought with the two beams in the air, and after some time, suddenly threw them into the center of the lake, where they floated away and disappeared. From then on, whenever it rained, people near the lake heard the sound of two pieces of wood colliding, and when the rain stopped, the sky cleared, and this was always true without exception. From the late Ming dynasty to the Qing dynasty, the beams in the lake absorbed the essence of the sun and stars, gradually becoming more dangerous and malevolent.

    362

    行人船遇一木如箭激赶，至则船碎。以至放船时，必预呼大楠将军，二楠将军，香帛礼祭之，始获免。时湖心更有巨瓮，不知何年沦入水，每夜，闻瓮中吸水吐水声，即雨。人以为湖中二怪。至道光某年，有渔人父子拉丝网。夜向晨，网重，轻易不能达岸，以为得大鱼甚夥。久之愈重，心急，欲弃网割缆则不舍，欲曳则船将覆。惶急间，忽上流来一官舫，男子数十人，皆箭衣窄袖，貌甚都。因大声呼救。官舫靠渔舟，互结缆，助曳其网，渔人两舟跳踯施力，误坠一履官舫中，不及捡，而网果轻。须臾曳起，掷岸上，官舫欲去，解所结缆。渔人将烹茗炊饼以酬，不受，匆匆去。

    Explanation

    362

    A ship encountered a log that behaved like an arrow, striking it with such force that the vessel shattered. As a result, whenever ships set sail, they must first call upon the Great Nan General and the Second Nan General, offering incense and silk as sacrifices, only then can they be spared from disaster. In the middle of the lake, there was a large jar, whose origins were unknown. Every night, the sound of water being sucked and expelled from the jar could be heard, and it would rain. People believed these were two strange creatures in the lake. During a certain year in the Daoguang period, a fisherman and his son were pulling a net. As night approached morning, the net became heavy, and they could not easily bring it to shore, thinking they had caught many large fish. After a while, the net became even heavier, and they became anxious. They wanted to abandon the net and cut the rope, but they hesitated. If they tried to pull it, the boat would capsize. In their panic, suddenly a government boat came upstream, with dozens of men on board, all dressed in tight sleeves and arrow-like clothing, and they were very handsome. They loudly called for help. The government boat tied up to the fishing boat, and they tied the ropes together, helping to pull the net. The fishermen on both boats jumped and strained, and one of them accidentally fell onto the government boat. He didn't have time to pick up anything, and the net became light. Soon, the net was pulled up and thrown onto the shore. The government boat wanted to leave and untied the ropes. The fishermen tried to offer tea and baked cakes as a thank you, but the men refused and left quickly.

    363

    渔父子餐已，天大明，私衷庆幸，以为获必胜常。及启，则并无寸鳞，唯一极大楠木，满身生绿苔如毛，隐隐有鳞甲纹，一头双孔若目，且有睛，知将化龙，亦不知何故罹于网。始审网之所以重也。因忆曩危急，诣岸上金龙四大王庙，焚顶酬神，视壁上所悬神船，淤泥水尽湿，草履亦在，益恍然悟神之冥助也。因送梁于庙庭。远迩闻之，莫不骇诧。一日，示梦于寺僧曰：“吾兄弟成材于隋之开皇，落水于元季，行将化龙，上帝怒我虐行人，谴谪于此。吾弟逃，不敢再恣肆，然吾躯尚受三百年香火，幸无亵渎。”僧告于众，为之雕大王像，纹甚细。从此湖中无撞木，而瓮声如故。

    Explanation

    363

    After the father and son had eaten, it was dawn, and they secretly rejoiced in their hearts, thinking that the catch would surely surpass the usual. When they opened the net, there was not even a single scale, only a very large piece of nanmu (a type of wood), covered with green moss like hair, with faint scales and armor patterns, one end with two holes like eyes, and they had pupils. Knowing it was about to transform into a dragon, they did not know why it had been caught in the net. Only then did they realize why the net was so heavy. They remembered the previous danger and went to the Gold Dragon Four Kings Temple on the shore, burning incense to thank the gods. They saw the god's ship hanging on the wall, completely wet with mud and water, and the straw sandals were also there. They suddenly understood the divine assistance more clearly. They sent the beam to the temple courtyard. People far and near heard about it and were all amazed and surprised. One day, they showed a dream to the temple monk saying: "My brothers and I grew up during the Kaihuang period of the Sui dynasty, fell into the water during the Yuan dynasty, and were about to transform into dragons. The Heavenly Emperor was angry with us for mistreating travelers, and banished us here. My younger brother fled and dared not act recklessly again, but my body still receives three hundred years of incense offerings, fortunately, it has not been desecrated." The monk told the people, and they carved an image of the Great King, with very fine patterns. From then on, there were no more collisions in the lake, and the sound of the gourd remained as before.

    364

    昙花记
    　　
    　　昙花本佛国产，放大光明，生自在香，每闻梵呗声，则婆娑而舞。奈朝开夕落，赋命不长。佛祖慈悲，见之泪下。昔太史戴公督学西秦时，辟门唱名，有七龄章童子名节，丫髻缠红丝，面如冠玉，提笔囊登阶接卷。太史藐其稚，曰：“咄！节院乃文战之地，非婴孩跳荡之区，汝来此何为？”揖而对曰：“童子无知，观光有志。”曰：“汝能作文乎？若块然没字碑，当以夏楚惩汝。”曰：“虽未敢径夺锦标，亦未必遽撄扑教。”太史颇以为夸。

    Explanation

    364

    Translation:

    The Record of the昙花

    The昙花, originally from the land of Buddha, emits great light, produces a natural fragrance, and dances gracefully when it hears the sacred chanting. Unfortunately, it opens in the morning and falls in the evening, with a short lifespan. The Buddha, out of compassion, weeps upon seeing it. In ancient times, when Scholar Dai supervised education in the western region of Qin, he opened the door and called out names. Among the candidates was a seven-year-old child named Jie, with a topknot wrapped in red threads, a face like a jade crown, and carrying his writing bag, he ascended the steps to receive the exam paper. Scholar Dai looked down on his youth and said, "Hmph! This is a place for scholarly combat, not a playground for infants. What are you doing here?" The child bowed and replied, "I am just a child, but I have the ambition to observe and learn." Scholar Dai then asked, "Can you write an essay? If you are a blank stone without any writing, I will punish you with a stick." The child responded, "Although I may not dare to win the championship outright, I also do not immediately expect to be punished." Scholar Dai found this quite boastful.

    365

    询广文，对云：“此儿素有神童之誉，渠父名九如，亦久困童子军者。”太史疑九如携来，将贾余勇，为儿捉刀，乃杜其弊，呼从者送交幕府诸君。及再点，则鱼贯中果有九如其人者，年逾不惑，野朴颓唐，一村学究耳。问：“章节汝子耶？”曰：“然。”曰：“如此髫龄，强来作麽生？若露庐山真面目，法不汝容！”九如唯唯以退。院门扃，太史危坐堂皇。过卓午，返内省，甫履阈，即闻童子喧笑声，与诸老辈辩难声，且脱帽露顶，榻上翻筋斗为乐。
    太史蓦入，略呵叱，节悚惕，徐起整衣冠，侍而听教。太史笑云：“吾固知汝不能文也，日移八砖矣，不构思而喧闹，此岂三家村塾耶？”对曰：“不奉题纸，从何作文？”太史恍然，亦自捧腹，询诸幕云：“是儿伎俩如何？”佥曰：“敏甚，唯狡狯不受羁勒，然读书甚熟，百举而不一遗。”乃授题，与以小几，使坐，并与果饵使餐。节略一颦蹙，即奋管直书，不啻宿构。洋洋洒洒，出色当行。

    Explanation

    365

    The Grand Historian inquired of the scholar, who replied, "This child has always been known as a prodigy. His father is named Jiur, and he has long been stuck in the ranks of young scholars." Suspecting that Jiur had come to exert himself on behalf of his son, the Grand Historian decided to eliminate this corruption and ordered his attendants to bring Jiur to the officials in the inner office. Upon checking again, they found Jiur among the crowd, over forty years old, rustic, simple, and somewhat disheveled—a mere village scholar. The Grand Historian asked, "Is this your son, Chapters?" Jiur nodded, "Yes." The Grand Historian then said, "At such a young age, why are you here? If you reveal your true self, the law will not tolerate you!" Jiur nodded and backed away. The courtyard gates were locked, and the Grand Historian sat upright in the main hall. After noon, he returned to the inner office and as soon as he stepped through the threshold, he heard the laughter of the young scholars, their debates with the elders, and even the sound of hats being removed and heads being exposed, as they performed acrobatics on the bed for amusement.

    The Grand Historian suddenly entered, gave a slight rebuke, and Chapters became apprehensive. He slowly rose, adjusted his clothing, and stood ready to listen. The Grand Historian smiled and said, "I knew you could not write. It is already past noon, and you are making noise without any preparation. Is this a small rural school?" Chapters replied, "Without receiving the exam paper, how could I write?" The Grand Historian realized his mistake and laughed, asking the officials, "How is this child's ability?" They all said, "He is very quick, but he is cunning and difficult to control. However, he has read extensively and never misses once in a hundred attempts." The Grand Historian then gave him a topic, provided him with a small table, and allowed him to sit, along with some fruit and snacks. Chapters, after a slight frown, quickly picked up his brush and wrote fluently, as if he had prepared it in advance. His writing was extensive, smooth, and outstanding in his field.

    366

    缮就，跪呈其卷云：“童子节，愧少如椽之笔，且为刺促之文，良由时近昏黄，不过免于曳白耳。”太史阅之，击节者再。适壁上粘《兰亭》本，拈“此地有崇山峻领，茂林修竹”句，命对；对云：“怕你不雕虫篆刻，断简残篇。”诸幕叫绝。太史佯怒云：“童子亦读《西厢》耶？”即以为句曰：“童子读西厢，”对云：“大人应东井。”太史色为之霁，指庭树曰：“老树千年，”对云：“香昙一现。”太史恐其不祥，然心赏灵慧。适左右举烛，节将出，因抚其背曰：“好为之。一领青衿，便易子矣。

    Explanation

    366

    The student completed his work and knelt, presenting his scroll, saying, "Child, I am ashamed to have a pen as weak as a beam, and to have written in haste. Truly, it is because the time is near dusk, and I have only managed to avoid leaving the scroll blank." The historian read it and clapped twice in admiration. Happening to see a copy of The Preface to the Poems Composed at the Orchid Pavilion hanging on the wall, he picked out the line, "Here are lofty mountains and dense forests, with tall bamboos," and asked the student to compose a couplet. The student replied, "I fear you are not concerned with intricate carving and minor details, but with broken and incomplete pages." The other scholars exclaimed in delight. The historian feigned anger and said, "Does the child also read The Western Chamber?" He then used the line, "The child reads The Western Chamber," to which the student responded, "The adult should face the well." The historian's expression softened, and he pointed to a tree in the courtyard, saying, "An old tree for a thousand years," to which the student replied, "A fragrant lotus appears briefly." The historian feared it was an inauspicious omen, but he admired the student's wit. Just as the attendants lit the candles, the student was about to leave, and the historian patted his back and said, "Do well. With a scholar's gown, you will soon become a scholar."

    367

    ”节忽颜色惨戚，伏地泪雨，崩角有声，力辞盛意。太史大诧，问：“汝既高尚，何劳此行？”泣曰：“童子有苦衷，不敢言，言必获罪戾。”曰：“第言之，无恐。”曰：“父困此有年矣，顷此之来，原冀为椿庭作倩，不意隔绝且先获售，则父于今科固已无望，即下科亦何能为哉？乞录父而黜节，转移之德，没齿不忘！”太史呼九如卷至，则荒率较雏凤判若天渊矣。因以之示节曰：“汝父文似此，奈何？”节叩不已，太史矜其志，怜其孝，嘉其慧，遂许其请，曰：“冰鉴之明，暂为汝屈。然汝下科必捷，鸿飞不远矣。”节欢跃再拜而出。翌晨揭晓，榜首为章节还，亦隽才也。九如则勉附榜末。星轺启行，诸生走送。九如亦携节拜车下。太史谓九如曰：“汝之售，汝子所赠也。鸦巢之凤，岂有种哉！”又询节曰：“冠军人汝同宗乎？”曰：“同。”曰：“我行矣，盍以一对送我：章节章节还。”节应声曰：“吕蒙吕蒙正。

    Explanation

    367

    Jié Hū appeared with a somber and sorrowful expression, kneeling on the ground, shedding tears profusely, and weeping so intensely that it sounded like a crack. He strenuously declined the generous offer. The historian was greatly surprised and asked, "Since you are noble, why bother with this journey?" Jié Hū wept and said, "I have a difficult situation, and I dare not speak, for speaking will surely bring blame and punishment." The historian reassured him, "Just speak, do not fear." Jié Hū replied, "My father has been in this predicament for many years. I originally hoped to find a substitute for my father, but unfortunately, he was cut off and sold first. Then my father has no hope in this current examination, and even in the next examination, what can be done? I request that my father be recorded and I be excluded. The virtue of shifting or transferring will never be forgotten!" The historian called for Jiǔ Rú's paper, and it was found to be sloppy and careless, as different as heaven and earth compared to the standard. He showed it to Jié Hū and said, "Your father's writing is like this, what can be done?" Jié Hū continued to plead, and the historian admired his resolve, pitied his filial piety, and praised his intelligence, so he agreed to his request, saying, "The clarity of the ice mirror, I will temporarily yield to your request. However, you will certainly succeed in the next examination, for your great potential is not far off." Jié Hū joyfully leapt, bowed twice, and then left. The next morning, the results were announced, and the top position was awarded to Jié Zhāng Huán, also a talented person. Jiǔ Rú勉强附榜末。星轺启行，诸生走送。九如亦携节拜车下。太史谓九如曰：“汝之售，汝子所赠也。鸦巢之凤，岂有种哉！”又询节曰：“冠军人汝同宗乎？”曰：“同。”曰：“我行矣，盍以一对送我：章节章节还。”节应声曰：“吕蒙吕蒙正。”
    勉强附榜末。星轺启行，诸生走送。九如亦携节拜车下。太史谓九如曰：“汝之售，汝子所赠也。鸦巢之凤，岂有种哉！”又询节曰：“冠军人汝同宗乎？”曰：“同。”曰：“我行矣，盍以一对送我：章节章节还。”节应声曰：“吕蒙吕蒙正。”
    勉强附榜末。星轺启行，诸生走送。九如亦携节拜车下。太史谓九如曰：“汝之售，汝子所赠也。鸦巢之凤，岂有种哉！”又询节曰：“冠军人汝同宗乎？”曰：“同。”曰：“我行矣，盍以一对送我：章节章节还。”节应声曰：“吕蒙吕蒙正。”
    勉强附榜末。星轺启行，诸生走送。九如亦携节拜车下。太史谓九如曰：“汝之售，汝子所赠也。鸦巢之凤，岂有种哉！”又询节曰：“冠军人汝同宗乎？”曰：“同。”曰：“我行矣，盍以一对送我：章节章节还。”节应声曰：“吕蒙吕蒙正。”
    勉强附榜末。星轺启行，诸生走送。九如亦携节拜车下。太史谓九如曰：“汝之售，汝子所赠也。鸦巢之凤，岂有种哉！”又询节曰：“冠军人汝同宗乎？”曰：“同。”曰：“我行矣，盍以一对送我：章节章节还。”节应声曰：“吕蒙吕蒙正。”
    勉强附榜末。星轺启行，诸生走送。九如亦携节拜车下。太史谓九如曰：“汝之售，汝子所赠也。鸦巢之凤，岂有种哉！”又询节曰：“冠军人汝同宗乎？”曰：“同。”曰：“我行矣，盍以一对送我：章节章节还。”节应声曰：“吕蒙吕蒙正。”
    勉强附榜末。星轺启行，诸生走送。九如亦携节拜车下。太史谓九如曰：“汝之售，汝子所赠也。鸦巢之凤，岂有种哉！”又询节曰：“冠军人汝同宗乎？”曰：“同。”曰：“我行矣，盍以一对送我：章节章节还。”节应

    368

    ”太史顾广文诸人曰：“能不以此子为无价之宝耶？”旋解襟下佩玉以赠节，曰：“汝第自珍爱，明年今日，当以茂才还汝。此玉即他日券也。”节感激涕零，呜咽惆怅，视星轺影远，始随父而归。阅半载，太史忽梦节持昙花冉冉来谢，口吟一绝云：“身本优钵罗，托身植瑶岛。入世偿宿逋，昙花依旧好。”迨重莅是郡，急欲见孺子，而踪迹杳如。惊询广文，广文命九如自陈。双泪盈睫，抽泣而对曰：“节儿自承明训，归后惨以痘殇。弥留时，坚抱所赐玉佩，遂以为殉。”太史惊惋无既。

    Explanation

    368

    The Grand Historian, addressing Con Guangwen and others, said, "Can this child not be considered a priceless treasure?" He then unfastened his collar and took off his jade pendant to give to Jie, saying, "You just keep it precious and cherish it. Next year on this day, I will return you as a talented person. This jade pendant is a bond for the future." Jie was deeply moved to tears, sobbing and sighing with sadness. He saw the shadow of the star carriage far away and began to follow his father back. After half a year, the Grand Historian suddenly dreamed of Jie holding a lotus flower and coming to thank him. Jie recited a short poem: "Originally a lotus, planted on a jade island. Entering the world to repay past debts, the lotus remains beautiful." When the Grand Historian revisited the prefecture, he was eager to see the child, but found no trace of him. He was astonished and asked Con Guangwen, who then instructed Jiu Ru to state his thoughts. With tears welling up in his eyes, Jiu Ru wept and spoke, saying, "Jie has followed your wise teachings. After returning home, he tragically died of smallpox. At the moment of death, he firmly held the jade pendant given to him and thus died with it." The Grand Historian was continuously shocked and grieved.

    369

    九如又云：“渠降生时，本梦一老枯禅，手赠昙花而诞，宜其不永也。”太史爽然，始悟昔之联句可为谶，后之梦返其真耳。乃振腕作《昙花记》，以志其事。懊侬氏曰：负逋而来，偿逋而去，人间佳子弟，莫不云然。独章氏子，可叹可怜，令人有回也短命之感。慧既非凡，孝尤卓著，昙花之喻，虽想当然语，亦作如是观也。又棠邑有古梅书院，邻果老庵，乃唐人附会神仙古迹。邑宰长公名在，试书院日，少长咸集，中有八岁童子来观场，长公命对，曰：“梅花果老矣”，即应声曰：“棠阴长在哉。”长公大喜，呼为千里驹，奖赐极隆，旋亦夭亡，孺林伤悼。

    Explanation

    369

    Jiu Ru said again: "At the time of his/her birth, he/she dreamed of an old, withered Zen master who gave a lotus flower by hand and was born. It is appropriate that his/her life would not be long." The historian was suddenly enlightened and began to realize that the previous couplets could be seen as an omen, and the dream returned to its true meaning. He then wrote the "Lotus Flower Record" with great effort to document the event. An Nong said: "Coming with a debt and leaving with it paid, the good children of the world, none are not like this. Only Zhang's son, pitiable and pitiful, evokes a feeling of short-lived life. His wisdom was exceptional, and his filial piety was outstanding. The lotus flower metaphor, though seemingly obvious, should be seen in this way."

    In Tang County, there was an ancient academy called the Plum Blossom Academy, which was adjacent to a temple dedicated to the God of Fruits. This place was where people believed in and associated with ancient legends of immortals. The local official, Zhang Zai, was testing the academy on a day when people of all ages gathered. Among them was an eight-year-old child who came to watch the event. Zhang Zai asked him to respond, and he said, "The plum blossoms are old." The child immediately replied, "The shade of Tang will last forever." Zhang Zai was very pleased and called him a prodigy, giving him great rewards. However, the child soon died young, causing great sorrow among the scholars.

    370

    博山两贤妇
    　　
    　　博山钟十六，其父以负贩起家，十六亦废读绍弓冶。年十六，聘里人李氏女耐姑为妇。未娶之先，偶诣村市勾当，遇风鉴士叟，鹤发龙钟，碧眸炯炯，求相者门如市，为其言多应，无丝毫爽也。十六羡其神，出腰际铜钱二百求摸索。叟云：“来者父母俱存，弃儒习贾，”余亦多验，惟云：“应得两妻，相伴偕老。”十六温噱云：“某田舍郎，仅一床头人，愿斯足矣，何福消受英皇耶？”时父执陈老，亦欣然延至家，遍相眷属，至陈女让姑，问：“字人否？”曰：“尚未。”曰：“一语孟浪，莫见责。女公子当是人家小星也。”陈大怫意，曰：“陈某不才，忝为此乡之望，何至以弱息为人妾！”叟云：“但求相法不验耳。”言已拂袖径出，人俱以为颠。

    Explanation

    370

    Two Virtuous Women of Boshan

    In Boshan, there was a man named Zhong Shiliu. His father had risen to prominence through trade, and Zhong Shiliu, following in his footsteps, abandoned his studies to take up the family trade in bow-making and metalworking. At the age of sixteen, he married a local woman named Nai Gu from the Li family. Before his marriage, Zhong Shiliu happened to visit the village market to conduct business. There, he encountered an old fortune-teller with white hair and a stooped appearance, but with bright and alert green eyes. Many people flocked to him for divination, as his predictions were often accurate and without error. Zhong Shiliu admired the old man's skill and offered him two hundred copper coins to divine for him. The old man said, "The person who comes here has both parents alive and has abandoned Confucian studies to become a merchant." He also made several other accurate predictions, but then said, "You should have two wives and live together to old age." Zhong Shiliu laughed warmly and replied, "I am just a farmer's son, with only one person in my bed. That is enough for me. How could I have the fortune to enjoy an empress?" At the time, Zhong Shiliu's father's friend, Mr. Chen, also invited the old man to his home, where he divined for all of Mr. Chen's relatives. When it came to Mr. Chen's daughter, Nang Gu, the old man asked, "Have you been married?" She replied, "Not yet." The old man then said, "This prediction is rash, but please do not blame me. Your young lady should be a minor star in someone's home." Mr. Chen was greatly displeased and said, "I am unworthy, but I am respected in this village. How could I let my young daughter be someone's concubine?" The old man replied, "It's just that my divination method is not accurate." He then waved his sleeve and left directly, and everyone thought he was crazy.

    371

    年余，十六行亲迎礼，耐姑艳而不浮，慧而不肆，温清无缺，伉俪亦浓。讵结缡甫六月，竟生一子。十六怒，疑必不贞于室，朝夕诟詈，翁姑亦时时诮让。耐姑无以自明，唯向隅痛哭。十六欲杀其子以灭迹，姑不忍，弥月即迫之归宁。甫入门，而离婚一纸，已接踵至矣。其父李翁见而气结，入咎其母李媪。媪思己女素守闺范，动以礼闲，然婴儿固在抱也。下嫁日至今，始六阅月也。嗒焉气丧，唯母女相对哭。娣姒嗤嗤笑于后，臧获落落慢于前。族人更说翁曰：“若耐姑者，诚门楣之丑，不殪之，亦当醮之，否则逐之，然则豢而终其身，作有夫寡耶？襁褓物，他日究谁氏子耶？”翁曰：“我亦筹之久矣，若倔强，当以斧加其颈。”明日，果有媒妁集于门，或云某尹二郎贤，或云某薄四郎美。耐姑知不能容，然亦无所归。

    Explanation

    371

    After more than a year, in the sixteenth year, the groom performed the formal wedding ceremony. The bride, known as "耐姑," was beautiful but not frivolous, intelligent but not arrogant, always attentive to her husband's needs, and the couple's affection was also strong. Just six months after the wedding, she gave birth to a son. The husband became angry, suspecting that she must have been unfaithful, and he scolded her day and night. The in-laws also frequently reprimanded her. The bride had no way to prove her innocence and could only cry in a corner. The husband wanted to kill the child to cover up the scandal, but the mother-in-law could not bear it and forced her to return to her parents' home one month after the birth. As soon as she entered the door, a divorce document arrived. Her father, Li翁, saw it and was deeply upset, blaming his mother-in-law, Li媪. Li媪 thought that her daughter had always adhered to the proper conduct of a woman, always acting with propriety, but the baby was still in her arms. From the day of the wedding to now, it had only been six months. Li翁 was despondent, and only the mother and daughter wept together. The sisters-in-law laughed behind their backs, and the servants were indifferent in front of them. The clan members said to Li翁, "If your daughter-in-law is indeed a disgrace to the family, you should either kill her, remarry her, or expel her. Otherwise, you are keeping her alive, making her a widow with a child. What will happen to the child in the future?" Li翁 replied, "I have thought about it for a long time. If she is stubborn, I will strike her neck with an axe." The next day, matchmakers gathered at the door, saying that the second son of a certain magistrate was virtuous or the fourth son of a certain clerk was handsome. The bride knew she could not be tolerated, but she had nowhere to go.

    372

    晨起抱儿，走投雨香庵，鬻簪珥，僦尼别舍以居。禅榻砖灯，纺织自活。庵主人大悟，优婆中善知识也，颇怜之，时加调护。一夕，儿呜呜不寐，耐姑孤枕伤心，亦自哀哭。大悟自蒲团惊寤，呼曰：“耐姑，何其不耐耶？暂时盆覆，冤亦前因；有日珠还，圆成后果。寡妇且耐夜哭，况有夫之妇哉？”耐闻之，抽哀而已，不敢信口头禅。十六自出妻也，颇惧泰山涉讼。既而寂然，知无他患，遂另议姻于陈女让姑。让姑虽艳慧，终觉逊耐，弦续良辰，十六思叟两妻之言，虽微验，然出一娶一，终不足云箭贯双雕也。陈翁更以娇娃已得所天，虽属补房，然非室，行当抉叟双瞳子，责其狂瞽云。是日，大悟偶自他村行，见钟家鼓吹喧阗，贺客杂，知是再娶，急归告耐姑，耐姑洒涕默不语。

    Explanation

    372

    In the morning, she held her child and walked to the Ruyang Temple, sold her hairpins and earrings, and rented a separate room from a nun to live in. She lived on a meditation mat and a brick lamp, weaving to make a living. The abbess, Great Enlightenment, was an excellent teacher among the lay devotees and showed great compassion, providing timely care and support. One night, the child cried incessantly, and Nai Gu slept alone, feeling heartbroken and wept herself. Great Enlightenment suddenly woke from her meditation cushion and called out, "Nai Gu, why are you so impatient? For a moment, the pot is overturned, but the cause of the suffering is also from the past; there will be a day when the pearl returns, and the result will be complete. Even a widow can endure the night of tears, let alone a married woman?" Nai Gu heard this and could only weep in grief, not daring to believe in empty words. At sixteen, she was divorced and was very afraid of being sued by her mother-in-law. Later, everything became quiet, and she knew there were no other troubles, so she arranged a marriage for herself with Chen Nü Rang Gu. Rang Gu was beautiful and wise, but ultimately, she was inferior to Nai Gu. The good time for marriage was set, and at sixteen, she thought about the words of the old man with two wives, although they were slightly verified, but it was still insufficient to say that he married two women, ultimately not enough to say that he shot two eagles with one arrow. Chen翁 (Chen翁) thought that his pretty daughter had found her rightful place, although it was a secondary wife, it was not a primary wife, he should have poked out the old man's eyes and blamed him for his madness. On that day, Great Enlightenment happened to be walking in another village and saw the Zhong family with music and guests, knowing it was a remarriage, she rushed back to tell Nai Gu, who wept silently and remained silent.

    373

    问：“娘子于意云何？”曰：“死耳。”大悟狂笑云：“前日奇冤莫白，若反生；今日大屈将伸，若反死，何其痴乎？”耐姑知其中含妙谛，即跪求开示。曰：“娘子当乘此机会，往登其门，抵死不去。神佛菩萨，一切有情，自来玉汝。请以二十字禅言相送，偈云：‘但得灶下养，重燃狱底灰。香闺联二美，此去莫低徊。’”耐姑祗领，乃襁负其子，登钟氏堂。姑觌面，批其颊以逐之，不去；亲诣厨中，霍霍磨刀以恐之，不去；呼媪持短棒交挞之，遍体青伤，仍不去。惟伏地哀号，自云“死罪。”见姑怒稍解，始叩首请代女仆，供传呼，不计佣值，日唯求两餐，夕唯求一席地，惰再逐，无怨言。翁与十六已有怜惜意，邻里又缓颊云：“不端妇亦可怜生也，阿姥何惜一碗闲粥饭，俾渠亦可代新妇劳。

    Explanation

    373

    Madam, what do you think? she replied, "Nothing but death." With sudden enlightenment and uncontrollable laughter, she said, "The great injustice of yesterday remains unexplained, as if I were to be reborn; today, the great wrong will be righted, as if I were to die, how foolish it is!" Nai Gu realized that there was a profound truth hidden within these words and immediately knelt to seek enlightenment. He said, "Madam, you should take this opportunity to go to their door and refuse to leave no matter what. The gods, Buddhas, bodhisattvas, and all sentient beings will naturally help you. Please accept these twenty words of Zen wisdom as a parting gift: 'Just to tend the stove, relight the ashes of the prison. In the fragrant boudoir, two beauties are united. Do not linger on your way.' Nai Gu respectfully accepted the advice and carried her child, entering the hall of the Zhong family. When she faced the old lady, she was slapped across the cheek to drive her away, but she did not leave; she went personally to the kitchen, sharpening a knife to intimidate her, but she did not leave; the old lady called for the maid to beat her with a short stick, leaving her body bruised and battered, yet she still did not leave. She only fell to the ground and wailed, saying, "I am guilty of death." Seeing that the old lady's anger had somewhat subsided, she finally knelt and requested to serve as a maid, offering to act as a messenger and attendant, not counting or caring about wages, only asking for two meals a day and a place to sleep at night, and being lazy again and driven away without complaint. The old man and the sixteen-year-old girl had already shown compassion for her, and the neighbors interceded on her behalf, saying, "Even an unvirtuous woman deserves pity, grandma, why not spare a bowl of spare porridge, so she can also help the new wife with her work."

    374

    ”姑不得已，颔之，惟命宿东厨隙地藉稿眠，不容其擅入中堂，不容其妄与新妇抗礼，耐连连应诺。由此洁庭除，操井臼，虽新妇不洁，亦代湔除。姑于初至，颇吹毛求疵，后见其服劳不少怠，渐亦相安。耐不呼姑，而曰太母；不呼舅，而曰太翁；新妇则曰娘子；见故夫则走避恐不及。戚属乡邻，罕识其面。幸让能怜耐，且怜其儿，避人则呼曰姊，时周恤之，不忍目之为佣。计重来瞬息年余矣。会舅姑寿辰，十六效莱舞，如期称觞，戚属咸集。忽雨香庵尼遣雏送仪至，开箧视，非祝具，乃汤饼也。莫不鼓掌笑老尼荒廖，几如叟妄言。

    Explanation

    374

    The grandmother, having no choice but to accept the situation, nodded in agreement and ordered that he only sleep in the spare space of the kitchen on straw. She did not allow him to enter the main hall without permission, nor to treat the new wife as an equal without proper respect. He repeatedly agreed. From then on, he cleaned the courtyard and handled the kitchen and grinding tasks. Even if the new wife was dirty, he would clean her as well. Initially, the grandmother was quite nitpicky, but after seeing that he did not slack off in serving, she gradually became accustomed to him. He did not call her grandmother, but called her "Grandmother." He did not call her uncle, but called him "Grandfather." He called the new wife "Lady" and fled in fear when seeing the former husband. Relatives and neighbors rarely saw his face. Fortunately, the grandmother showed mercy and also pitied his child. When avoiding people, he called her "sister" and often helped her, and did not look at her as a servant. It has been more than a year since she came back. On the occasion of the grandmother and grandfather's birthday, the sixteenth performed the "Lai Dance." As scheduled, the relatives gathered to drink and celebrate. Suddenly, a nun from the Rain Fragrance Temple sent a young girl to deliver gifts. Opening the box, it was not for the celebration, but for noodles. Everyone laughed at the old nun's absurdity, almost like the old man's foolish talk.

    375

    旋开宴，鼓乐大作，觥筹互飞，忽闻灶下有呱呱声聒耳，灶婢奔白云：“李氏又分娩矣！”宾主愕然，其姑大怒，趋而责之曰：“淫婢定不欲生耶？前已玷汝家，今又玷我家耶？”耐含笑云：“阿姑勿怒，儿今日鸣吾冤矣。速邀良人来，岂有两子而不识其父乎？”十六犹未审何事，贸贸自外至，耐蓦起执其手，涕泣曰：“我自入汝家，服役之苦，妇人本分，何足云？然未尝出一瞻眺，与人一语言。汝于某日挑吾，吾不理，夜间乘醉来逼吾，草榻上勉就之，今几月耶？抑仍六月耶？二老如不信，有渠头上柳花为证。”先是清明日，其俗男女均簪柳花少许。新妇媚稿砧，以五色丝缠作彩缕，有文理，非代人所能。盖由清明至今，又将中秋也。正错愕间，忽报李家老夫妇闻信至。

    Explanation

    375

    The banquet was just about to begin, and the music and drums sounded loudly, with cups and tokens of drinking flying around. Suddenly, a loud baby cry came from under the stove, and a kitchen maid rushed in, saying, "The Li family has another birth!" The guests and hosts were startled, and the elderly woman became furious, rushing forward to scold her: "Is this immoral servant determined not to have children? She already disgraced your family, and now she disgraces ours too?" The servant smiled and said, "Aunt, please don't be angry. I am declaring my innocence today. Quickly invite my husband here. How can there be two children and not know their father?" The man, still unsure of what was happening, stumbled in from outside. The servant suddenly stood up, grabbed his hand, and wept, saying, "Since I entered your household, I have endured the hardships of service, which are a woman's duty, but what is worth mentioning? However, I have never had the chance to look outside or speak to anyone. On a certain day, you flirted with me, but I did not respond. Later, while drunk, you forced me, and I reluctantly agreed on the straw mat. How many months have passed? Or is it still six months? If you do not believe me, there is the willow flower on his head as proof." It was the Qingming Festival, and the custom was for both men and women to wear a few willow flowers. The new wife flirts with her husband, weaving the willow flowers into colorful threads, a skill not easily learned. Since the Qingming Festival, it is almost the Mid-Autumn Festival. Just as everyone was in a state of confusion, suddenly it was reported that the elderly couple from the Li family had heard the news and arrived.

    376

    十六父子道左迎入门，即揖众宾曰：“不肖女亦有今日，不然天网漏矣！”李翁犹刺刺理论，李媪则发指，面色靛，叫骂万端，毁器皿无算，遽揪十六之母，饱以老拳，衣裤撕碎，几露其私，无敢阻者。十六崩角有声，亦为腾足而颠，捉发而掷，夫妇扬言曰：“今日事，非鸣官荡其产毁其家不可！”众宾稍劝慰，则反唇讥曰：“当日吾女被逐时，诸君何不援手一启齿？”宾语塞。时让姑父母亦在座，知事必决裂，急号于众曰：“耐姑之贞，其含垢忍耻，固已上格苍穹，始送石麟为姑解秽。顷欲解铃而息争，合璧而免讼，似非吾女一言不可。”让姑果自屏后冉冉出，拜诸长上云：“耐姐沉冤，白于一旦，天之灵，家之福也。请姐复正位，儿副之。若是贞操，即为之执巾栉亦所甘心。长者俱在，求勉如儿言，勿再鼓舌。”众曰：“善。”陈翁亦乐为之。

    Explanation

    376

    The younger sister's family welcomed the guests from the side of the road into the house, and her father immediately bowed to the guests and said, "My unworthy daughter has reached this day, otherwise she would have escaped divine retribution!" The older sister's father continued to talk incessantly, while her mother's hair stood on end, her face turned blue, and she shouted and cursed in countless ways, destroying countless utensils. She suddenly grabbed the mother of the younger sister, beating her with fists, tearing her clothes to pieces, almost exposing her private parts, with no one daring to intervene. The younger sister's head cracked and made a sound, and she also jumped up and down, grabbing her hair and throwing her. The couple proclaimed loudly, "Today's matter cannot be resolved without reporting to the authorities to ruin their property and family!" The guests tried to comfort the situation, but the older sister's parents retorted sarcastically, "When our daughter was expelled, why didn't you offer help or speak up?" The guests were speechless. At that time, the parents of the younger sister were also present, knowing that the matter would surely lead to a rupture, and they urgently called out to the crowd, "The chastity of the younger sister, having endured shame and humiliation, has already reached the heavens, starting with the stone unicorn to cleanse the older sister. Now, we want to untie the bell and stop the quarrel, unite and avoid litigation, it seems that my daughter's words are necessary." The younger sister indeed emerged from behind the screen, respectfully bowing to the elders and saying, "The older sister's long-standing injustice has been cleared in a day, it is the will of heaven and the blessing of the family. Please let the older sister return to her rightful place, I will serve her. If it is about chastity, I am willing to hold the towel and comb. The elders are all here, please support my words, and do not speak further." The guests said, "Good." The older sister's father was also willing to comply.

    377

    忽叟来观热闹场，从翁后拍其肩曰：“陈君陈君，仆之双瞳，能赐保全否？”陈大骇，继而大噱，遂与众互述其事，始知数之所在，有莫知其然而然者也。惟大悟能知必于是日临蓐，又不知操何术耳。乃张灯结彩，奉两家父母高座，钟携儿叩拜伏罪，然后互荫其女作螟蛉，宾客极欢而散。翌置绰楔于门，表两妇之贤。耐姑再生女，仍六月。让姑生子二，即如寻常。其后四子皆贵，惟先封谪母，而后封庶母，诚如叟言。耐姑年六十，出资为雨香庵建佛塔，报大悟也。懊侬氏曰：中牟谣云：“少所见，多所怪；见橐驼，言马肿背。”钟十六以妇六月产即云不贞，可谓少见多怪矣。孕月深者主寿，月浅者主贵。老随园堂皇一语，能为婴孩造福，是真佛子心肠，慈母荫庇也。吾愿不识字人，其胸无点墨而腹有疑团者，当三复此编，免唱东南孔雀。我佛闻之，必合十而作颂曰：“善哉善哉，如是如是。”

    Explanation

    377

    An Old Sage Came to Watch the Bustling Scene

    An old sage suddenly came to watch the bustling scene, and from behind the old man, he patted his shoulder and said, "Mr. Chen, Mr. Chen, can you preserve my eyes?" Chen was greatly startled, and then burst out laughing. He then shared the story with the crowd, and only then did he realize the place of fate, where things happen without anyone knowing why they happen that way. Only the great sage knew that she would definitely give birth on that day, but he did not know what method he used. They then lit lamps and decorated with colorful streamers, placing the parents of both families on high thrones. Zhong took his child and knelt to confess his fault, and then they mutually adopted their daughters as their own. The guests left very happy. The next day, they placed a banner at the door to commend the virtue of the two women. Nai's wife gave birth to another daughter, still at six months. Rang's wife gave birth to two sons, just as usual. Afterward, all four sons became prominent, but only the eldest son was posthumously ennobled first, and then the younger son, just as the old man had said. Nai's wife, at sixty years old, funded the construction of a Buddha stupa at the Yu Xiang Temple to repay the great sage.

    Ah Nong said: A saying from Zhongmou county says, "Seeing little, one finds much to be strange; seeing a camel, one says the horse has a swollen back." Zhong, at sixteen, immediately said his wife was unfaithful because she gave birth at six months. This is truly an example of seeing little and finding much to be strange. Those who give birth after a long pregnancy tend to live long, while those who give birth after a short pregnancy tend to be prominent. The old sage's grand statement can bring blessings to infants, truly the heart of a Buddha, and the protection of a loving mother. I wish for those who do not know how to read, who have no ink in their hearts but many doubts in their bellies, to read this text three times, to avoid singing the song of the Southeast Peacock. The Buddha, upon hearing this, would surely join his palms and recite a verse saying, "Good, good, indeed, indeed."

    378

    珠江花舫
    　　
    　　邹子《乐生笔记》载江山船云：“江山近水人家，各置一巨舫，画板明窗，巨丽宏敞。父荡桨，母操舵，兄弟执缆，女任烹调。其女子率幼习丝竹歌舞，破瓜时，便使应客。临风咳唾，若即若离，或一二姝，或三四姝，皆靓妆，将以诱过客，弋重资也。富商大贾，往往倾囊登岸，恶矣！”而不知广东珠江花舫，其恶更有胜于此者。一老幕府沈翁，宜兴人，自幼入粤，生平不履勾栏门，亦不娶，由壮而老，仍童子身。每见子弟喜北里游者，则笑之以鼻。醉后尤自夸诩，以为如来世尊，忍欲罗蜜，自家已得三昧，盖尤胜焉，无不及也。岁积修羊已数万金，囊橐累累，而御仆又严酷，锱珠必较。一日，将回里买良田，筑幽室，为归隐计，不复为人家压线作嫁衣矣。素闻花舫名，恐堕其术，乃再四检择，须无一女眷者，始登其舟。

    Explanation

    378

    Jiang Zhu Flower Boats

    Zou Zi's Le Sheng Notes records the Jiang Zhu boats: "The people living near the rivers in Jiang Zhu each own a large boat, with painted boards and bright windows, grand and spacious. The father rows the oars, the mother steers, the brothers hold the ropes, and the daughters cook. The girls are trained in music and dance from a young age. When they reach puberty, they are made to entertain guests. They flirt with the wind, being close enough to be noticed but not close enough to be approached, either one or two, or three or four, all dressed up to attract wealthy customers. Rich merchants often spend lavishly on these services, which is truly despicable!"

    But it is not known that the Jiang Zhu flower boats in Guangdong are even more despicable than this. An old official, Shen from Yixing, entered Guangdong from a young age, never visited brothels, and never married. Throughout his life, he remained celibate. Whenever he saw young men who enjoyed visiting brothels, he would sneeze in disgust. After drinking, he would boast even more, claiming to be like the Buddha, having mastered the art of resisting temptation, and that he had achieved the highest level of self-control, surpassing everyone else. He had accumulated a significant amount of wealth and was strict and meticulous, even with small matters. One day, he planned to return home, buy good land, build a secluded house, and retire, no longer serving as a matchmaker for others. He had heard of the reputation of the flower boats and feared being ensnared by them. Therefore, he carefully selected a boat with no women aboard before boarding it.

    379

    一日检就，先运箱箧，安置图书，而后辞别旧识，始解缆行。一舟为己坐，一舟载仆从，不甚华丽，亦无陈设，饮食亦不甚丰洁，遂帖然意肯。行三四里，忽睹一好女子，淡妆素服，蓬头鸦髻，而意态动人；启舱后小窗，就水浣手，玉腕钏碰板锵然。沈怒，呼仆不应；呼舟子，问女何处来？舟子大恐，伏地面无人色。女急走出舱，裣衽拜曰：“公勿怒，容妾缓禀陈，近情则留之，无理则逐之，惟公所使，未晚也。”曰：“试言之。”女流涕曰：“妾宜兴人，姓刘，乳名小玉。幼随父宦于粤，误适恶少年，奁资供赌博，资罄而夫死，大归，而父母又卒于官，百计营葬。孓然一女子，孀且孤，恐遇匪，陷娼家，思归乡里，剃发皈三宝。欲自买一舟，既无资斧，又无婢妪，迢迢千里难独行。欲附他人舟，又恐遭不测。素闻长者圭璧其身，乃奇男子，必能怜苦况，赐玉成。

    Explanation

    379

    Once everything was packed up, the boxes and chests were moved, and the books were arranged, and then the traveler bade farewell to old acquaintances before untying the ropes and setting sail. One boat was for his own use, and another carried his servants. The boats were not very luxurious, nor were they adorned, and the food was not very abundant or clean, but he accepted the situation contentedly. After traveling a few miles, he suddenly saw a beautiful woman, simply dressed and with a disheveled bun, whose demeanor was captivating. She opened a small window at the back of the cabin and washed her hands by the water, her jade bracelet clinking against the boat's side. The traveler became angry and called for his servant, who did not respond. He then called out to the boatman, asking where the woman came from. The boatman was greatly frightened, kneeling on the ground and looking pale. The woman quickly stepped out of the cabin, bowed respectfully, and said, "Please do not be angry, allow me to slowly explain. If my request is reasonable, please keep me; if it is unreasonable, please let me go. It is entirely up to you, and it is not too late." The traveler said, "Please speak." The woman shed tears and said, "I am from Yixing, my surname is Liu, and my given name is Xiaoyu. As a child, I followed my father to Guangdong. I mistakenly married a bad young man, whose gambling consumed my dowry, and when it was gone, he died. I returned to my parents' home, but my parents also died while serving in office. I spent every effort to arrange their funeral. Now, I am a lonely young woman, widowed and orphaned, fearing that I might encounter bandits and be forced into a brothel. I wish to return to my hometown and become a nun. I want to buy my own boat, but I have neither money nor servants. Thousands of miles away, it is hard to travel alone. I want to hitch a ride on someone else's boat, but I fear unexpected dangers. I have heard that the gentleman is virtuous and noble, a remarkable man, and must be able to sympathize with my plight and grant me favor."

    380

    且妾邻姆姆，又与舟人熟，故附宝舟行。倘赐援手，贱妾生死衔环，日于佛前讽诵，为长者祝千秋。若竟不许，妾当效湘累毕命，盖舍此机会，永无归期耳！”言已悲啼，词旨酸楚。沈愕然久之，曰：“附舟行，何不可，但不许入中舱耳。”女应之，即起入后舱，嘤嘤诵佛号。舟子亦拜谢。问诸仆，仆叱舟子，沈遂信。久之，每进一餐一饭，赞甘美；舟子曰：“此小玉手段也。”每盥一巾一袜，赞匀洁；仆人曰：“此小玉湔祓也。”翌晨，抵小村落，思早馔，呼仆，尚酣寝，忽舟子掀廉进面饼，味鲜美，问何来，曰：“小玉亲上岸为公购来也。”一日，清晨拥被坐，忽闻骨冬一声，舟子大哗曰：“小玉为主人购早馔，上跳板失足落水矣！”沈披衣即起，开舱视之，果有数饼漂水面，众果挈女子自水中出，衣裙尽湿，瑟缩寒颤，扶上船头，将之后舱。

    Explanation

    380

    The humble servant, a neighbor lady, has also become acquainted with the boatman, so she wishes to join the treasure boat. If you grant her assistance, she vows to serve you with unwavering loyalty, reciting Buddha's name daily in front of the Buddha statue, wishing for your long life. If you finally refuse, she will follow the example of Xiang Lü, ending her own life, for missing this opportunity means she will never return. She spoke these words with tears, her words and meaning bitter and painful. The listener was startled for a long time and said, "Joining the boat is not a problem, but you are not allowed to enter the main cabin." The woman agreed, immediately stood up, and entered the rear cabin, softly reciting Buddha's name. The boatman also bowed and thanked her. The listener asked the servants, who scolded the boatman, and the listener then believed. After a while, every meal she took, she praised its sweetness and deliciousness; the boatman said, "This is the skill of Xiao Yu." Every time she washed a towel or a sock, she praised their even and clean state; the servant said, "This is the purification of Xiao Yu." The next morning, they arrived at a small village, and the listener thought of breakfast, calling the servant, who was still sound asleep. Suddenly, the boatman opened the curtain and brought in steamed buns, which tasted fresh and delicious. The listener asked where they came from, and the boatman replied, "Xiao Yu personally went ashore to buy them for you." One day, in the early morning, the listener sat wrapped in a blanket, suddenly hearing a loud splash. The boatman shouted loudly, "Xiao Yu fell into the water while climbing the gangplank to buy breakfast for the master!" The listener put on clothes and immediately stood up, opening the cabin to look, and indeed saw several buns floating on the water. The crowd lifted the woman out of the water, her clothes completely wet, shivering from the cold. They helped her onto the bow and took her to the rear cabin.

    381

    闻舵工太息曰：“小玉仅此一套衣，又无可更换，不几冻煞耶？”沈立命扶入中舱，女不应，众如不闻；沈又言，众曰：“公曾云不许入中舱，小人敢忘却耶？”沈曰：“渠为我至此，忍坐视耶？”呼入，卧小玉于己衾中，钻火代烘湿衣。小玉起着衣，含羞诣后舱，操作如故。沈由此心德小玉。一夜人静后，似鼠子入箱啮衣履，呼仆捕鼠，不应。将自起，见小玉睡眼饧涩，自舱后出，衣绿绸短袄，秉烛问鼠在那厢，公指示之，即为摆扑而后去。一夜，江风大作，桅震震响若欲断，案上灯檠忽灭。呼举烛，不应。又见小玉徐徐笼灯入，披淡黄袄，就灯火，纤指如笋，凌波如芽，略凝睇，又去。一夜，暴雨，水溜自篷隙入，衾枕却当漏处，移就干处，如故。呼仆不应，又见小玉挈衣襦，秉烛姗姗来，登榻跨沈身上过，代塞漏处，溜应手停，汗津津如珠，喘吁吁若断，沈由是心更德之。

    Explanation

    381

    The helmsman sighed and said, "Little Jade has only this one set of clothes, and there is nothing to replace it. Isn't she going to freeze to death?" Mr. Shen ordered to carry her into the middle cabin, but the woman did not respond, and the others acted as if they did not hear. Mr. Shen spoke again, and the others said, "You once said not to let her into the middle cabin. How could we have forgotten?" Mr. Shen replied, "She came here for me. How can I sit by and watch?" He called her in, laid Little Jade in his own quilt, and made a fire to dry her wet clothes. Little Jade got up, dressed herself, and went to the rear cabin with a blush, continuing her work as before. Mr. Shen thus felt grateful to Little Jade. One night, after everyone had gone to sleep, it seemed like a mouse had entered the chest and chewed on clothes and shoes. Mr. Shen called the servant to catch the mouse, but no one responded. He was about to get up himself when he saw Little Jade with sleepy, dull eyes, coming from the rear cabin, wearing a green silk short jacket, and holding a candle to ask where the mouse was. Mr. Shen pointed it out, and she immediately set up a trap and then left. Another night, the river wind suddenly became strong, the mast shook and made a sound as if it would break, and the lamp on the table suddenly went out. Mr. Shen called for someone to light a candle, but no one responded. He saw Little Jade slowly carrying a lit lamp in, wearing a light yellow jacket, and approaching the lamp to light a fire. Her slender fingers were like bamboo shoots, and her steps were like young shoots in the water. She glanced briefly and then left. Another night, heavy rain fell, and water dripped through the gaps in the canopy. The quilt and pillow were under the leak, so he moved them to a dry place, as before. He called the servant again, but no one responded. He saw Little Jade carrying a robe, coming gracefully, climbing onto the bed, and crossing over Mr. Shen's body to block the leak. The dripping stopped immediately, and she was sweating profusely, like beads of water, and breathing heavily as if she would faint. Mr. Shen thus felt even more grateful to her.

    382

    欲挑与语，小玉遽携烛而去。明日，沈忽病痢，仆人懒惰，恒早眠，自伤委顿，叹且泣。小玉闻之，即诣舱代调药饵，司盥濯，极殷勤，数日病瘳。夜静，小玉犹忍冻侍床头，不归寝。沈怜之，挽其臂曰：“卿衣裳太薄，又为老朽侍疾，良不忍。何不权就仆脚畔眠？”小玉不答，促归寝，亦不应。沈曰：“吾犬马如长，卿齿稚，论年岁，当可为吾螟蛉女，眠何碍耶？”小玉点首，遂就脚后眠。沈觉奇暖，胜于汤婆子；又有一种异香钻入鼻，心大动，不能自持，逡巡起，与共枕，摩挲抚慰，口吃吃求欢，小玉曰：“不可，妾既孀，又视翁如父，翁又面许作螟蛉，若此之为，玷辱奚甚。”沈固哀之，小玉曰：“既蒙相爱，何必在此？”曰：“卿如能令我销魂，当没世不忘。

    Explanation

    382

    Desiring to tease her into conversation, Xiao Yu hurriedly took the candle and left. The next day, Shen suddenly fell ill with diarrhea. His servant was lazy and always went to bed early. Shen lamented his own weakness, sighing and weeping. Hearing this, Xiao Yu immediately went to the cabin to prepare medicine for him, managing his washing and bathing with great diligence. After several days, his illness subsided. In the quiet of the night, Xiao Yu still endured the cold to stay by his bedside, refusing to return to sleep. Shen pitied her and took her arm, saying, "Your clothes are too thin, and you are caring for an old man like me. It is truly unbearable. Why don’t you sleep at my feet instead?" Xiao Yu did not respond, urging him to go to bed, but he did not comply. Shen said, "I am like a dog or horse to you, and you are still young. Considering our age difference, you could be my adopted daughter. What harm would there be in sleeping together?" Xiao Yu nodded and lay down behind his feet. Shen felt an unusual warmth, warmer than a hot water bottle, and a strange fragrance entered his nose, stirring his heart deeply. Unable to control himself, he hesitantly got up and lay down with her, caressing and comforting her, stammering in his desire. Xiao Yu said, "No, I am a widow, and I see you as my father. You have already promised to adopt me. If we were to do this, it would bring great shame." Shen firmly pleaded with her, and Xiao Yu said, "Since you love me, why not here?" She replied, "If you can make me utterly captivated, I will never forget you in this life."

    383

    且仆孤身独行，惨与卿同，成就此好，吾之所有，即卿之所有。不然，卿即归宜兴，能枵腹活耶？”小玉不语，沈遂与之定情，备极缱绻，沈喜曰：“今而后，方知枕席之乐，胜于封万里侯也。”小玉曰：“妾之清白，为君轻薄尽矣。”由是相处如夫妇，一切锁钥，悉付小玉。忽闻两仆逃，已而一短童亦逃，小玉颇骇诧，而沈独不甚追究，计自解缆时至今，已八阅月，尚未至。小玉独催促，而沈若不甚介意。久之，视银箧尽空，问小玉，曰：“翁忘却耶？仆盗资若干，僮盗资若干，日食柴米，翁病药饵又若干，舟子支付船值又若干。”观衣箧亦空，问小玉，曰：“翁不知耶？银尽，不借此付质库，将若何耶？蚨去能飞回耶？”沈恋小玉美，昏昏亦不甚盘诘。一日，舟子喜曰：“到矣。”沈欲登岸，小玉止之曰：“翁将何往？常言家无一椽，能露宿耶？故里闻翁挟重资回，必猬集求告贷。

    Explanation

    383

    Since I am traveling alone, my sorrow is the same as yours, and to complete this good deed, what I have is yours as well. Otherwise, if you return to Yixing, how could you survive with an empty stomach?" Xiao Yu remained silent, and Shen then decided to commit to her, showing the utmost affection and intimacy. Shen happily said, "From now on, I understand that the joy of being in bed together is greater than being ennobled as a marquis over ten thousand miles." Xiao Yu replied, "My purity, for your sake, has been completely compromised." Thus, they lived together like husband and wife, and all the keys were entrusted to Xiao Yu. Suddenly, two servants fled, and later a short boy also escaped. Xiao Yu was greatly surprised, but Shen paid little attention. Calculating from the time we untied the rope until now, it has been eight months, and we have not yet arrived. Xiao Yu urged him, but Shen paid little attention. After some time, seeing the silver box empty, he asked Xiao Yu, "Have you forgotten? The servants stole so much, the boy stole so much, the daily expenses for food and firewood, your medicine for illness, and the boatman's payment for the ship." Seeing the clothes box empty, he asked Xiao Yu, "Don't you know? With the silver gone, how can we borrow from the pawnshop? Can the money fly back?" Shen was infatuated with Xiao Yu's beauty and was too dazed to investigate. One day, the boatman happily said, "We have arrived." Shen wanted to go ashore, but Xiao Yu stopped him, saying, "Where are you going? Always saying there is no place to live, how could you sleep in the open? In our hometown, they heard that you returned with a large sum of money, so they will surely gather to ask for loans."

    384

    谓翁之资斧已罄，谁信之耶？然则再求人，谁应之耶？岸上有妾阿姨家，颇雅洁，不如就彼处养疴。妾承翁错爱，既不能树贞节坊，亦不愿别抱琵琶，且作临邛栖止。家虽壁立，妾尚能拈针，量不致有庚癸呼也。”沈思邓山已颓，阮囊依旧，不得已，从之。入其家，果如女言，居以静室，起居安善。女皇皇时出入，时来伴翁，寝时就他处宿。心疑不敢问，亦不敢出。突二三旧友来访，皆粤之名幕府，惊询：“何亦遄返？”笑曰：“返何处耶？何时返耶？”盖沈舟居一载，日对丽人，舟子扬帆行数十里，又溯流退数十里，无论浙邦，且终未出粤界也。顷所居即小玉家，舟子即小玉兄弟辈，盖名妓也。至是始恍然，乃相与干笑，遂仍居广为冯妇焉。又一显宦履任，登画舫耗去五千，而归与夫人言，夫人嗤之。曰：“卿勿笑，他日赴任所见若辈，恐亦能令卿卿销魂也。

    Explanation

    384

    Who would believe that the old man's financial resources are exhausted? If so, who would respond to a request for help? There is my aunt's house on the shore, quite elegant and clean, so it would be better to rest and recover there. I have received your mistaken affection; I cannot build a monument to chastity, nor do I wish to play the lute for another. Let us make do with a temporary residence in Linqiong. Even though our home is as bare as a wall, I can still sew, and it is unlikely that we will face financial distress. I deeply thought that my fortunes had collapsed, but the bag of money remained the same. Unwillingly, I agreed. Upon entering her home, it was indeed as she had described, a quiet room where we lived comfortably. The woman would often come and go, sometimes staying to keep me company, and would sleep elsewhere when it was time to rest. I doubted but dared not ask, nor did I dare to leave. Suddenly, a few old friends came to visit, all prominent officials from Guangdong, and they asked in surprise, "Why have you returned so soon?" I smiled and replied, "Where would I return to? When would I return?" It turns out that I had lived on the boat for a year, daily in the company of a beautiful woman, the boatman sailed dozens of miles, then sailed back upstream dozens of miles, regardless of Zhejiang, and ultimately never left the borders of Guangdong. The place where I had been staying was actually the home of Xiao Yu, the boatman was actually Xiao Yu's brothers, who were actually famous courtesans. By this point, I finally realized, and we all laughed awkwardly, and so I continued to live in Guangdong as a 'Phung Fu.' Another prominent official took office, boarded a painted boat and spent five thousand, and upon returning, told his wife, who laughed at him. He said, "Do not laugh, when you take office in the future, you will see people like these, they may also bewitch you."

    385
