    ”夫人大笑曰：“吾身无淫具，渠能为我作面首人耶？”曰：“不然。吾辈爱优伶，何曾非两雄相爱；恐两雌相爱，亦同此扑朔迷离也。”夫人更大笑，以为妄，且隐隐有醋意。后果买舟，遣女仆迎夫人。舟子女慧丽可人，能眉语，能目听，举止言动，无一不令夫人生欢喜心。乃拜为螟蛉女，朝赏暮宴，金玉珠翠，锦绣玩好，不计其数。船故缓缓行，日仅十余里，比到任，而夫人已妙手空空矣，且船值已累积，短百余金。宦闻之，急遣仆持金赎取夫人归署。冉冉出莲舆，登后堂，官遽跃出，抚掌笑问曰：“何如？”懊侬氏曰：赵简子适楚江也，且惑操楫女；鸱夷子游五湖也，尚挈采莲人。天光云影中，一叶荡漾，得此数辈，供巾栉之周旋，便觉米家书画，赵家琴鹤，一切有情。然瓶供之兰，嗅之可也，若醉服其水，则腹痛而死；鹤顶之珠，玩之可也，若误服为丹，则肠断而亡。噫！黄帝造舟，防溺也；而仍溺之者，盖自溺也。

    Explanation

    385

    The lady laughed loudly and said, "I have no sexual organs. Can he make someone to be my concubine for me?" He replied, "No. We love actors and actresses, which is not unlike two males loving each other. I fear that two females loving each other can also be just as confusing and obscure." The lady laughed even more, thinking it was nonsense, and there was a hint of jealousy. Later, he indeed bought a boat and sent a female servant to fetch the lady. The boat's maiden was intelligent and beautiful, able to communicate through her eyebrows and eyes, and her every gesture and word made the lady feel joyful. He then adopted her as his adopted daughter, rewarding her with gold, jade, pearls, and fine clothes, and they feasted and celebrated daily. The boat moved slowly, covering only about ten miles a day, and by the time they reached their destination, the lady had already become empty-handed, and the cost of the boat had accumulated to over a hundred gold pieces. The official heard this and quickly sent a servant with gold to redeem the lady back to the official residence. As she slowly emerged from her carriage, she ascended to the back hall, and the official suddenly jumped out, clapping his hands and laughing, asking, "How was it?" A certain person said: Zhao Jianzi went to the Yangtze River, and was tempted by a woman who steered the boat; Fuyi Zi traveled the Five Lakes, still carrying a woman who picked lotus flowers. In the light and shadows of the sky and clouds, a small boat floated gently, and with these few people, she served as a personal attendant, which was also a delightful thing. However, lilies in a vase can be smelled, but if one drinks the water, it can cause abdominal pain and death; a pearl on a crane's head can be admired, but if mistaken for a pill, it can cause intestinal rupture and death. Alas! Huangdi created boats to prevent drowning, but still, those who drown themselves are those who do it to themselves.

    386

    卷四
    　　
    　　金竹寺
    　　
    　　余髫龄即闻扬州地下有金竹寺，不得其源。前岁，晤屯田司马杨慧生姻丈，偶话及，云：明季某甲子，有皖人萧灵威，少年任侠，追踪鲁仲连郭解之为人，屡屡睚眦兴怨，后遭仇家，几毙毒手；逋之他县，匿迹韬声者二载。偶步月，闻茅屋中有哭声，探询之，有里豪魏姓名虎者，强娶孀母女，愤不从，欲自戕者屡矣。其孀母朝夕逻守，然虎已诹吉，行将来攫取，故对泣耳。萧闻之，归旅店，袖刃出，访虎居确，跃登其垣，垣尽登屋，脱履步鸳瓦，行无声。视灯光多处，睨虎坐绣阁中，拥艳姬，陪狎客，昵饮甚豪。闻狎客笑曰：“东邻女假惺惺，尚啼哭。

    386

    Final Translation

    Volume 4
    　　
    Jin Zhu Temple
    　　
    When I was still a child, I heard that there was a Jin Zhu Temple beneath Yangzhou, but I could not trace its origin. Last year, I met my father-in-law, General Yang Huisheng, who was in charge of land management. We happened to talk about it, and he said: In the late Ming dynasty, during a certain year, there was a man from Anhui named Xiao Lingwei. As a young man, he was brave and chivalrous, emulating the virtues of Lu Zhonglian and Guo Jie. He often bore grudges over small matters and later encountered an enemy who nearly killed him with a deadly hand. He fled to another county, hiding his tracks and suppressing his voice for two years. One night, while strolling under the moon, he heard crying coming from a thatched cottage. Upon inquiry, he learned that a powerful local figure named Wei Heng (also known as Wang Hu) had forcibly married a widow and her daughter. The daughter, refusing out of anger, had attempted suicide many times. Her mother guarded her day and night, but Wei Heng had chosen a good day and was planning to seize her, so they were weeping together. Xiao Lingwei, upon hearing this, returned to his inn, secretly carrying a knife. He investigated Wei Heng's exact residence, leaped onto the wall, climbed onto the roof after reaching the wall, took off his shoes and walked on the roof tiles, moving silently. He saw a place with many lights, where Wei Heng was sitting in a decorated hall, surrounded by beautiful women and close friends, drinking closely and very generously. He heard one of the close friends laugh and say, "The girl from the neighboring house is pretending to be sincere, but she is still crying."

    387

    明日销金帐中，一尝异味，恐麾之不肯去矣！”虎曰：“倘倔强，当投诸冰窖，寒冻杀！”客曰：“虎郎惜玉，情甚旖旎，岂故作险语，破鬼胆耶？”曰：“嘻！”萧审之确，即跃之地，挥刃奔虎，遽斩之。姬惊晕若毙，客张皇，萧又斩之。仆僮闻声来，又斩二，而逃其一。视案有酒，立饮三大白，指溅血书壁曰：“杀人者萧灵威也！”书已，仍跃登屋，荒窜四顾，不知所之。忽昏黑中，有白衣人执莲花灯前引，随之行如飞。比明，视所抵已五百里。欲寻白衣人，不可得；唯灯弃荒草中，孤焰摇摇若将灭者。趋视之，非灯，竟朱提一锭，量之约四十两，俯拾，即以为资斧。

    Explanation

    387

    Tomorrow, in the golden tent, to try a strange taste, afraid that they will not leave." Tiger said, "If they are stubborn, they should be thrown into the ice cellar, freezing to death!" Guest said, "Tiger, you cherish jade, your feelings are very charming. Are you deliberately making dangerous statements, scaring the ghosts?" Said, "Haha!" Xiao, who is firm, immediately jumps up, wields the blade and runs towards the tiger, quickly beheading it. The lady is shocked and faints as if she is dead. Xiao then beheads him. The servants and attendants hear the sound and come, Xiao beheads two more, and one escapes. Seeing the table with wine, he immediately drinks three big cups. Pointing at the splattered blood on the wall, he writes, 'The killer is Xiao Lingwei!' After writing, he still jumps onto the roof, wildly looking around, not knowing where to go. Suddenly, in the darkness, a white-clad person holding a lotus lamp leads the way, and they walk like flying. By morning, they see that they have traveled five hundred miles. Wanting to find the white-clad person, they cannot; only the lamp is abandoned in the wild grass, the lonely flame swaying as if it is about to extinguish. Rushing to look, it is not a lamp, but a piece of gold, weighing about forty taels, picked up, it becomes the means of travel.

    388

    日处旅馆，渐闻风声促，恐为捕者踪迹得，急易装更姓名过江。闻浙省天竺山菩萨最灵显，虔往焚香求忏悔。至则士女如云，比丘如蚁，然皆装模作样，无一真善知识。焚香毕，即小住山寺，发愿改悔，默求庇佑。学优婆讽诵，至虔至诚。偶游山岭破寂，视石洞中，有老瞿昙趺坐，目若瞑。见萧来，遽喝曰：“富豪强娶，何预汝事？”萧蓦听，如冰沃顶，恐泄其事，心意欲击毙矍昙以灭口。僧大喝曰：“咄！白衣持灯接引汝，尚为仇耶？”萧骇且感，遽伏地，曰：“弟子知罪，大菩萨法力宏深，定能始终解我厄。

    Explanation

    388

    Living in an inn day by day, the protagonist gradually heard rumors of danger and feared that their tracks might be discovered by the pursuers. In a hurry, they changed their clothes and assumed a new name, crossing the river to escape. Hearing that the bodhisattvas at Tianzhu Mountain in Zhejiang Province were the most spiritually powerful, they devoutly went there to burn incense and seek repentance. Upon arrival, they found the temple crowded with women and men like clouds, and monks like ants, but all were putting on an act, none of them genuine spiritual guides. After burning incense, they stayed briefly at the mountain temple, making a vow to repent and silently seeking divine protection. They studied the Upanishads and recited them with utmost sincerity and devotion. One day, wandering through the mountain ridge to break the silence, they saw an old Shakyamuni sitting in the stone cave, his eyes seemingly closed. When they saw the protagonist, the old man suddenly shouted, "The wealthy man forcibly married her, what business is that of yours?" The protagonist was suddenly struck by this, as if a cold shock had hit their head, fearing that the matter might be revealed. They had the intention to kill the old man to silence him. The monk shouted sharply, "Fool! A white-clad figure holding a lamp to guide you, are you still an enemy?" The protagonist was both terrified and moved, immediately prostrating themselves, saying, "Disciple recognizes their fault. The great bodhisattva has profound spiritual power and will surely always resolve my difficulties."

    389

    ”曰：“此处非容身地，盍为我寄书扬州金竹寺与铁方丈，彼处潜身三日，难即已。”因袖出一函，封其固，曰：“速去，勿回头！”萧崩角数百，持函即行。十日过江，抵扬州，遍询无金竹寺，心甚忧虑。不敢居城中，潜寓乡村。夜夕步月东关浮桥上，忽一僧打包来，僧雏携灯前引，灯上大书曰：“金竹禅院。”略凝睇，神灯飘忽已东去，急趋而尾四五里，始追及，已在山谷中。僧问曰：“男子何所见闻而逐我？”萧喘息道天竺遇老瞿昙事，并示以函。僧曰：“我当谁，原是白衣豁棘尊者。居士既远作寄书邮，盍随我归去来。”须臾达一大丛林，月色昏黄中，视钟楼经阁，瑰伟嵯峨。司夜行者，已行梆唱佛歌，声凄心肺。

    Explanation

    389

    "Here is not a place to take refuge," he said. "Why not send a letter to the Jinzhusha Temple in Yangzhou and to Venerable Tie, the abbot? Stay hidden there for three days, and the danger will pass." He then took a sealed letter from his sleeve and said, "Go quickly, do not look back!" Xiao fled with hundreds of others, carrying the letter with him. After ten days, they crossed the river and arrived in Yangzhou. After searching everywhere, they found no Jinzhusha Temple, and Xiao was deeply worried. Not daring to stay in the city, he secretly took shelter in the countryside. One night, he walked under the moonlight on the floating bridge in the eastern part of the city. Suddenly, a monk appeared, carrying a package, and a young novice monk led the way with a lamp. The lamp had a large inscription that read: "Jinzhusha Chan Monastery." Xiao glanced at it, but the divine lamp had already drifted eastward. He hurried after it for several miles, finally catching up with the monk in a valley. The monk asked, "What did you see and hear that made you follow me?" Xiao, out of breath, recounted his encounter with the Elder Votaka in India and showed him the letter. The monk replied, "Who am I? I am originally the White Robe Venerable Hujie. Since you have come all this way to deliver the letter, why not follow me back?" Soon they reached a vast forest. In the dim moonlight, Xiao saw the magnificent and towering bell tower and scripture hall. The night watchmen had already beaten the drum and sung Buddhist hymns, their voices heart-wrenching and soul-stirring.

    390

    打包僧入白方丈，萧拱候丛竹旁，风篁烟筱，文秀。僧出，白方丈已禅定，留书案头，止客就寮房宿，明当晤叙耳。引萧之一斗室，雅洁无此。旋出夜膳，亦极精良。明日，并不闻传唤。往来缁侣，古貌古心，老稚妍媸，其类各别，然较之天竺皈依之大众，则似觉不同。住三日，是夜忽闻钟鱼梵呗声，若开大道场。潜披衣趿履，拟往瞻视。及至正殿，则其声顿寂，唯见满堂无佛像，满地铺毹氍，灯烛辉煌，男女裸体横陈，绸缪交媾，妍与妍偶，媸与媸偶，老与老偶，稚与稚偶。大骇。

    Explanation

    390

    The packing monk entered to report to the abbot. Xiao respectfully waited beside the cluster of bamboo, where the wind rustled through the bamboo and the mist enveloped the grove, creating a scene of elegance and beauty. The monk reported that the abbot was in deep meditation and left a letter on the desk, instructing the guest to stay in the guest room for the night and meet and talk the next day. Xiao was led to a small room, which was elegant and clean, without any flaws. Shortly after, the evening meal was served, and it was extremely refined and good.

    The next day, Xiao did not hear any calls or announcements. The monks and nuns who came and went had ancient appearances and ancient hearts, with a mix of old, young, beautiful, and ugly individuals, each category distinct. However, compared to the large number of people who had converted to Buddhism in India, they seemed different. Xiao stayed for three days. On that night, he suddenly heard the sound of bells, fish (a symbol of the Dharma), and chanting, as if a large religious gathering was being held. Secretly putting on clothes and shoes, Xiao intended to go and see what was happening. When he reached the main hall, the sound suddenly became silent. He only saw the hall full of no Buddha statues, the floor covered with carpets, and the lights and candles shining brightly. Men and women were lying naked, engaged in intimate and sexual activity. Beautiful with beautiful, ugly with ugly, old with old, and young with young. Xiao was greatly shocked.

    391

    略转瞬，则又妍媸老稚互更，互为之偶，或鸾颠，或凤倒，或背成峰，或侧成岭，其态既浓，其声更昵。萧阅之，始骇继怒，不禁大呼曰：“如此昏昏，成何世界！”忽闻背后一人大喝曰：“咄！六合之中，六合之外，六合所成，男欢女爱。俗子无知，大声惊怪。”萧视其人，紫衣科头，面如满月，打包僧侍侧，呼曰：“此铁方丈也，萧居士其稽首。”萧心虽怒，而体不克自主，玉山遽颓，和南伏地。方丈挽起，携入所居，略略问瞿昙踪迹，谓萧曰：“顷之所见，乃佛家之幻景耳。智者见之，大彻大悟；愚者见之，可兴可起。无足惊诧。”萧不敢言。旋谕打包僧曰：“盍携去重瞻水晶域，当发菩提心。

    Explanation

    391

    In a moment, the scene changed rapidly, with beauty and ugliness, youth and old age alternating and intermingling. Sometimes, things were upside down, like a phoenix and a phoenix being turned upside down. Sometimes, backs formed peaks and sides formed ridges. The appearance was intense, and the sound was even more intimate. Xiao saw this and was initially shocked, then angry, and could not help but loudly exclaim, "Such confusion, what kind of world is this!" Suddenly, a man behind him shouted, "Hmph! Within the six directions, beyond the six directions, everything created by the six directions, men and women love each other. Ordinary people are ignorant and make loud and strange reactions." Xiao looked at the man, who was wearing purple clothes and had a bald head, with a face round and full like a full moon. A traveling monk stood by his side. He said, "This is the Iron Abbot. Xiao, bow respectfully." Although Xiao's heart was angry, his body could not be controlled, and he suddenly collapsed, prostrating himself on the ground. The abbot lifted him up and led him into his dwelling, briefly asking about the whereabouts of Buddha. He said to Xiao, "What you just saw was a Buddhist illusion. Wise people see it and achieve great enlightenment; foolish people see it and can be inspired to act. There is no need to be surprised." Xiao dared not speak. Then, the abbot instructed the traveling monk, "Why don't you take him back to look again at the crystal realm? He should cultivate the bodhi mind."

    392

    ”萧辞出，见殿上灯火全无，人物亦杳，唯三世佛像，龛灯明灭而已。忽听鸡唱，传语送客。打包僧手采竹叶一丛与之，曰：“以此聊为居士壮行色。”萧领而藏于袖。送出门外，则迥非旧途，踯躅奔驰，明始辨路径，则已在甘泉山下。回顾袖中，竹叶已坠去其半，视所存者，金竹叶也。再入城询之，瞬已三年，而寺中仅三日耳。遂出竹叶货之，小负贩，大获，设骨董肆，家渐裕。一日，晤一女丐，携女行于途，见萧伏叩，曰：“恩公尚在耶？”萧审视，为当日难中人，急携回密室详询。云：“魏殁后，其子告官，捕得凶手如公状，斩之，其首落地而尸不见。

    Explanation

    392

    Xiao left, and upon seeing no lights on the hall and no people around, only the statues of the Three Buddhas with their lamps flickering, he suddenly heard the rooster crow and was told to send off the guest. The packing monk picked a bunch of bamboo leaves and gave them to him, saying, "Take these to strengthen your journey." Xiao accepted them and tucked them into his sleeve. When they sent him out the door, it was no longer the old path. He stumbled and ran, only recognizing the path at dawn, and then he was already at the foot of Ganquan Mountain. Looking back in his sleeve, the bamboo leaves had fallen half away. Seeing what remained, it was golden bamboo leaves. Entering the city again to inquire, it was already three years, but in the temple, it had only been three days. He then sold the bamboo leaves, starting as a small peddler and achieving great success, setting up an antique shop, and his family gradually became prosperous. One day, he met a beggar woman carrying a girl on the road. Seeing Xiao, she knelt and asked, "Is your kindness still here?" Xiao examined her and recognized her as someone from his past difficulties. He quickly took her back to a secret room to ask in detail. She said, "After Wei died, his son reported to the authorities, and the killer was captured and executed according to your description, but his head fell and the body disappeared."

    393

    妾义而盗首葬，甫破土，其首忽成荷花灯。官闻其事，亦不深诘。妾恐株累，挈女逃，丐于此者已三年。”萧更为述遇僧事，互感诧。遂以其女为室，事外母如母。旋投行伍，得功勋，仍以所易贾姓登剡章，官崇明守备。夫妇事佛甚虔，萧每怒，夫人低呼曰：“金竹寺，”辄敛怒为笑，见人温婉如处女。后外母死，盛补葬奠，送之殡宫，策马呵道归。忽于人丛中睹魏家仆，急携夫人不知所之。懊侬氏曰：路见不平，拔刀相救，佛菩萨赏其义侠，故远导幻境以生之，且诡托秘戏以悟之。盖镜中之镜，瞳中之瞳，岂真地下有逋逃之数，世外有曲躬之树哉？至满堂须陀洹，齐化鸳鸯偶，是耶，非耶？幸勿当作火宅梵嫂一例看也。

    Explanation

    393

    The concubine, out of righteousness, buried the head. Just as the earth was being broken, the head suddenly turned into a lotus lantern. The official heard the matter but did not probe deeply. Fearing entanglement, the concubine took her daughter and fled, and she has been begging here for three years. Xiao recounted the encounter with the monk, and they were mutually astonished. He then took her daughter as his wife and treated her stepmother like a mother. Soon after, he joined the military and achieved merit, and still changed his surname to Gao and submitted his name to the official records, being appointed as the commander of Chongming. The couple practiced Buddhism very devoutly. Whenever Xiao became angry, his wife would quietly call out, "Jinzhushan Temple," and he would immediately suppress his anger and smile, appearing gentle and virtuous like a maiden. After his stepmother died, he made a grand burial and memorial service, sent her to the funeral hall, rode away on horseback, and returned home with a retinue. Suddenly, in the crowd, he saw a servant of the Wei family, and he quickly took his wife and disappeared without a trace. The author of the "Ao Nong" (Disappointed Farmer) said: To see injustice on the road and draw one's sword to help, Buddha and Bodhisattvas rewarded his righteousness and chivalry, therefore leading him far into a dreamlike realm to give him life, and using a secret play to enlighten him. It is like a mirror in a mirror, an eye in an eye. Is there truly a number of fugitives underground, or a tree of bowing outside the world? Until the hall was filled with Arhats, all transformed into pairs of mandarin ducks. Is it true, or is it not? Please do not treat it as another example of the "House of Fire" or "Buddhist Sister."

    394

    石郎蓑笠墓
    　　
    　　崇明濒海处，烟波浩渺，仙灵往来。有大石郎者，幼孤，年十六，无恒产，为人家牧牛。青笠戴月，绿蓑织烟，时以短笛自娱，意亦闲适。顾嬉且勇，与群牧斗，无能胜者。然遇介属随潮以至，则扫而投之，不忍戕其生。所主之田公翁某，颇赏其仁厚。日卓午，与群牧倘徉于绿树间，或捉迷藏，或赌樗蒲；郎独抽尺八，坐避潮墩，吹无腔之韵，偷关山杨柳，指村舍桃花，自以为乐。少顷，潮怒涌，天地皆青，大蚌随一线来，泳游不去，宛为笛韵所羁。潮顿落，即胶滞沙际，屡翕辟，露光芒。群牧知中有夜光珠，议劈而取之。郎思害神物不利，诡云“海物通灵，刀斧莫斫，盍渥以沸汤则自开。”众善其言，竞起拾薪汲水，寻田家假灶突。

    Explanation

    394

    Stone Lang's Straw Hat and Raincoat Tomb
    　　
    In the coastal area of Chongming, the sea is vast and misty, and immortals and spirits come and go. There was a man named Shi Lang, who was orphaned at a young age, sixteen years old, with no fixed property, and he herded cattle for others. He wore a blue hat under the moon and a green raincoat through the mist, often playing a short flute for his own amusement, and his mind was also at ease. He was playful and brave, and when he fought with the other herders, no one could defeat him. However, when shells and marine creatures came with the tide, he would sweep them up and throw them away, unable to harm their lives. The elderly man who owned the land where he worked greatly appreciated his kindness. At noon, he wandered among the green trees with the other herders, sometimes playing hide-and-seek, sometimes playing a game of dice; Shi Lang alone drew out a shakuhachi, sat on a tidal mound, and blew a tune without a fixed melody, stealing the tune of the Yangtze River willows and pointing to the village peach blossoms, finding his own joy. Soon, the tide surged fiercely, the sky and earth turned green, large clams followed a line, swimming and not leaving, as if they were bound by the flute's melody. The tide suddenly receded, and the clams immediately stuck to the sand, repeatedly opening and closing, showing their luster. The herders knew there was a pearl of night inside, and they discussed cutting them open to retrieve it. Shi Lang thought it would be harmful to harm a sacred creature, and he deceitfully said, "Sea creatures are sentient; axes and knives cannot cut them. Why not use boiling water to make them open?" The others approved of his words, and they rushed to gather firewood and draw water, seeking a farmer's chimney to borrow.

    395

    郎待其去远，潜起推蚌行就海，祝曰：“苍茫贝阕，不少汗漫之游，幸勿再登陆，为若辈觊觎。”蚌得水，圉圉焉，洋洋焉，忽挟巨浪如山，俯仰作稽首状；海风忽狂，飒然遂逝。迨群牧以汤至，郎正假寐，物已子虚，叱起问何往，故佯惊作懊丧语曰：“神物化去矣，奈何？”众恍然，知为所赚，唾其面，亦不较。明年夏，时有青色牛，伤海边稻田，貌颇似郎之所牧。问之，不服，白眼争。遂告其所主，诮让之。郎嘿不辩，惟思所牧从未轻纵逸，何得蹊人田？会复有来告者，郎忿极，暮往其处，隐身以伺。翌日黎明，果一牛自海岸出，毛色极纯，与己牧却仿佛，惟顶生一角稍异。既而游戏沟塍，大肆蹂躏。郎暴起，将擒之，以塞人口。牛惊觉，反身遁入海。

    Explanation

    395

    The young man waited until the clam was far away, then secretly rose and pushed the clam towards the sea, praying and saying, "In the vast and mysterious realm of the sea, I do not wish to restrict your freedom to wander, but please do not return to land again, to be coveted by others." Once in the water, the clam moved slowly and calmly, then freely and joyfully, suddenly caught in a huge wave like a mountain, bowing and making a respectful bow; the sea wind suddenly became fierce, and the clam swiftly vanished. When the group of herders arrived with hot water, the young man was just dozing lightly, and the object had already vanished like a shadow. He shouted at them, asking where it had gone, and pretended to be surprised and spoke in a regretful tone, saying, "The divine object has transformed and gone, what can I do?" They suddenly understood, realizing they had been deceived, and spat in his face, but he did not hold it against them. The following summer, there was a blue-colored cow that damaged the rice fields by the sea, its appearance similar to the cow the young man tended. When questioned, the cow gave a white eye and argued. The owner was then informed and rebuked the young man. The young man silently did not argue, only thinking that the cow he tended had never been allowed to roam freely, how could it have trespassed on someone else's field? There were more reports coming in, and the young man became angry to the extreme, going to the place at dusk and hiding to wait. At dawn the next day, a cow indeed emerged from the coastline, its fur color extremely pure, similar to the cow he tended, only with a single horn on its head, slightly different. It then played in the ditches and ridges, ravaging and trampling. The young man suddenly sprang up, intending to capture it to silence the mouths of others. The cow was startled and became aware, turned around and fled into the sea.

    396

    郎忘其为洪涛巨浪，尾而逐，视两旁之水皆壁立，牛所过成一白平坦道。里许，抵一处，峨峨府第，金碧辉煌。牛跃入，郎亦入。听管弦哀怨，内正开筵，执事者皇皇奔走，无暇他窥。庑下有隙地，乃掩身，穷异观。视座上一客类王者，佩陆离之剑，颔下飘白须。一客类大夫，古衣冠，沉静缄默，似抱烦忧。一客类儒士，然星冠羽衣，英致洒然，亦极尊贵。一美人类天女，明翠羽，貌虽艳，而凛凛若冰霜。主位殷殷劝爵者，则一少年郎，被服都丽。阶下歌吴聚，舞楚舞，轻能在掌，高可遏云。客询何节奏，对以水殿抛球，新续第二曲也。白须人亦扣剑作歌曰：“鞭平王兮，吾非不臣；云黯黯兮，奇冤莫伸。吴市吹，萧欲裂，潮怒不可折，心死不可说。

    Explanation

    396

    The young man forgot that he was among the great waves and surges, following behind the cow. Looking at the water on both sides, it stood like walls. The cow passed through, leaving a white, flat path behind. After a short distance, he arrived at a place, a grand mansion with golden and jade-like splendor. The cow leaped into the mansion, and the young man followed. He heard sad and mournful music played by strings and wind instruments. Inside, a banquet was being held, and the attendants hurried about, too busy to look elsewhere. There was a gap under the eaves, where he hid himself to observe the strange sights. He saw a guest on the main seat who resembled a king, wearing a sword with a multicolored hilt and a flowing white beard. Another guest resembled a high-ranking official, dressed in ancient attire, silent and pensive, as if burdened by deep concerns. A third guest resembled a scholar, wearing a star-crowned robe and a feathered garment, elegant and dignified, also extremely noble. A beautiful woman, like a celestial being, with bright and green feathers, though her appearance was radiant, she exuded an icy and severe aura. The host, a young man dressed in splendid attire, was earnestly urging others to drink. Below the steps, songs of Wu and dances of Chu were performed, light and graceful, reaching high into the sky. A guest asked about the rhythm, and the response was that it was a new continuation of the second part of the Water Palace Ballad. The white-bearded man also struck his sword and sang: "Whip the King of Chu! I am not unfaithful! Clouds darken! A strange injustice cannot be redressed! In the market of Wu, the sound of the sheng (a wind instrument) is so loud it seems to tear the air! The tide is furious and cannot be broken, the heart is dead and cannot be spoken of!"

    397

    ”古衣冠曰：“相国尚有余怒哉？”亦攒眉蹙额而作歌曰：“天之险，不可升，地险山川与丘陵；人心之险更叵测，朝为变兮夕为更。珠宫在水兮何其晶莹，坦荡荡兮日月自明。龙伯召我兮，佩菊与之兰襟。吊汨罗之万丈兮，臣心同清。”儒士笑曰：“大夫变新声，却骚韵，然亦哀而艳矣。某请和以巴里歌”。歌曰：“当年臣本寄书邮，谁幸三生结并头。怜煞秀才康了辈，水天漂泊几沙鸥。”群起酌以巨觥曰：“洞庭艳福，诚可羡也，然尚有袍泽之情乎？”言已，视美人无一言。咸询：“贞姑，可否按律吕，赐新声？”对曰：“请以珊管瑶笺，书二十八字，命乐工歌之，何如？”众曰：“善。”须臾书就，阶下人歌之曰：“蓬莱清浅几成尘，门外野风愁煞人。三十六湖凉月里，珠光长照女儿身。

    Explanation

    397

    The anciently dressed one said, "Does the Prime Minister still harbor resentment?" Frowning and furrowing his brow, he composed a song: "The peril of heaven, which cannot be ascended; the danger of the land, with its mountains, rivers, and hills. The peril of human hearts is even more unpredictable, changing in the morning, altering in the evening. The pearl palace in the water, how brilliantly clear; wide and open, the sun and moon shine clearly. Dragon Lord summons me, and I wear chrysanthemum and orchid on my chest. Mourning the deep waters of Miluo River, my heart is as clear as it. The scholar laughed and said, "The official has changed to a new tune, distancing from the traditional Li Sao style, yet it is also sad and beautiful. I will respond with a Bali song." He sang, "Once I was just a messenger, who was fortunate to have three lives tied together. Pity the scholars like Kang, wandering like gulls in the sky and water." All raised large cups and said, "The beauty and good fortune of Dongting Lake are truly enviable, but is there still camaraderie among comrades?" After speaking, they looked at the beautiful woman, who remained silent. All asked, "Madam Zhen, can you follow the musical scales and grant us a new tune?" She replied, "Please use a jade flute and a precious scroll, write twenty-eight characters, and have the musicians sing it, how about that?" Everyone said, "Good." In a moment, the writing was done, and the musicians sang it: "Penglai is so shallow it has almost turned to dust, the wild wind outside the door makes people sad. In the cool moonlight over the thirty-six lakes, the pearl-like light always shines on the daughter's body."

    398

    ”郎聆之，情不自禁，矢口大呼，曰：“美哉！凛凛之风，何其婉而厉也！”咸愕眙诧：“俗子何得犯宫禁？”少年郎呼缚付鳖丞惩治，武士出索，反接其手，将行，呼曰：“缚太急！”既而大笑曰：“不图石大郎死于此处，大奇！”少年瞠视良久曰：“君海牧牛之石大郎耶？”曰：“然。”曰：“然则我恩人也，何得作阶下囚！”叱解其缚，亲自降阶，执礼甚恭，曰：“仆非君，早为儿童戕矣。此龙宫也，某王世子也。前化蚌出游，听君雅韵，险遭不测者，即某是也。盛德隆情，久未能报。顷能不速，颇惬素心。”即引之登堂，遍与诸客揖，曰：“此伍相也，此屈大夫也，此洞庭柳真君也，此露筋真妃也。”郎茫然不辨云何。

    Explanation

    398

    The young man listened, unable to control his emotions, and shouted spontaneously, saying, "How beautiful! The cold and awe-inspiring wind, how gentle yet severe it is!" All were surprised and stared in amazement, saying, "How dare a commoner violate the palace rules?" The young nobleman called for the tortoise officer to punish him, and a warrior came out to bind him, tying his hands behind his back. As they were about to take him away, he shouted, "The binding is too tight!" Later, he laughed and said, "Who would have thought that Big Brother Shi would die here, very strange!" The young nobleman stared at him for a long time and said, "Are you Big Brother Shi, who herds cattle by the sea?" The young man replied, "Yes." The young nobleman then said, "Then you are my benefactor, why should I be a prisoner?" He scolded the warriors to release him, personally descended the steps, and greeted him with great respect, saying, "If it weren't for you, I would have been killed by children long ago. This is the Dragon Palace, I am the Crown Prince of a certain king. Before, I transformed into a clam and went out, and heard your elegant voice, nearly met with misfortune. Your great virtue and deep feelings, I have not been able to repay for a long time. Recently, I did not come quickly, but it is very much in line with my simple intentions." He then led him up to the hall, bowed to all the guests, and said, "This is Wu Zixu, this is Qu Yuan, this is Liu Hong, the True Lord of Dongting, and this is the True Consort of the Dewy Veins." The young man was completely unable to understand what they were.

    399

    伍相云：“是子本天上水星之精，好与农丈人斗，得牵牛救解，旋以劾堕劫，归元之期非遥，世子尚宜调护。”客皆告别，郎亦兴辞，世子阻之曰：“君宜少安。”送客回，重与入座，问君何能来，以实告，曰：“能来不能去矣。适君所逐者，分水犀也，子非犀，则出门一步即死。”郎窘急，几坠泪，曰：“容请命于家君，或有法。”遽去。俄顷，持一大珠如龙眼者：“此辟水珠也。家君感子救某故，持赠。有此，入水可无濡首之虞。幸勿示人，恐有焚身之祸。子行矣，珍重此身，报德正未艾也。”殷勤送之出门外，果无投足地。试挥珠对水，奔腾浩瀚中，忽露一通衢，且平直，犹如犀之在前，瞬登彼岸。群牧咸惊。然大郎终不自慎，恒握珠走狂澜，既欲炫田以吓愚氓，不禁驾冯夷而驱海若。一日，抱一古铎出，上刻篆词曰：“大禹驱山之铃。”又一古玉，上刊奇字云：“秦皇塞海之宝。

    Explanation

    399

    伍相说：“这位公子原本是天上的水星之精，喜欢与农夫争斗，后来被牵牛星所救，不久又被指控堕落劫难，回归本源的日子不远了，世子还是应该好好照顾他。”客人们都告辞离去，郎君也起身告辞，世子拦住他说：“你还是稍安勿躁。”送走客人后，世子重新坐下，问郎君是如何来的，郎君如实回答：“我能来，但不能离开。刚才你追赶的，是分水犀，你不是犀牛，一旦出门一步就会死。”郎君窘迫急切，几乎要落泪，说：“让我请示家父，或许有办法。”说完便匆忙离去。不久，他拿着一颗像龙眼般的大珠子回来：“这是辟水珠。家父感念你救了我，特意赠送给你。有了这珠子，入水时不用担心头会湿。希望你不要给别人看，否则可能会有焚身之祸。你走吧，好好照顾自己，报答恩德还远未结束。”世子殷勤地将他送出门外，果然外面没有立足之地。郎君试着挥动珠子对准水，只见奔腾浩瀚的水中，忽然出现一条平坦直通的路径，就像有犀牛在前方引路，瞬间便到达了彼岸。群牧都感到惊讶。然而，大郎始终不够谨慎，总是握着珠子在狂澜中行走，既想炫耀珠子吓唬愚氓，又忍不住驱使水神和海神。一天，他抱着一只古钟出来，钟上刻着篆字：“大禹驱山之铃。”又有一块古玉，上面刻着奇字：“秦皇塞海之宝。”

    400

    ”他如鉴魑魅之铜，斫蛟鼍之剑，珊瑚树真有高六七尺者。人谓售之碧眼贾，富可跨石崇。郎哑然笑曰：“吾不能为龙宫之贼。”乘观者不意，悉举而投之，人皆恶其痴。尝有巨鼋出海翘首，目闪，即随之下。少刻醉饱出。人询何往，曰：“世子悬弧之辰，遣介士招饮耳。”众谋攫其宝。偶吹笛，倦而眠，六七辈蓦集搜腰缠。大郎惊醒，惧失信于世子，因珠入口，奋与众斗。众虽辟易，而珠亦滑入喉际，吞吐两难，遂鲠而死。乡人怜之，殓以桐棺，海畔。是夕风雷震震，翌已成一巨坟。明年海涨，复于坟下拥一沙岗，凡斥卤之地多坍塌，大郎坟独无恙。而潮亦至此止。死后且时著灵异，乡民即坟为祠。百里外浏河，素多海患，居民避波臣者纷然。时梦一神人，卤薄赫赫，冠冕峨峨，自云：“我石大郎也，悯此处没于海，急迁棺，或可免厄。

    Explanation

    400

    A Mirror Reflecting Demons, a Sword Cutting Through Dragons and Alligators, and Coral Trees Truly Six or Seven Feet Tall

    There are mirrors that reveal demons and monsters, swords that can cut through dragons and alligators, and coral trees that truly reach six or seven feet in height. People say that if one sells such treasures to a foreign merchant with green eyes, one can become as wealthy as Shi Chong. The young man, however, laughed silently and said, "I cannot be a thief from the dragon palace." Taking advantage of the audience's lack of expectation, he threw everything away, and everyone disliked his foolishness. Once, a giant turtle emerged from the sea, raising its head, and its eyes flashed. He followed it down. After a short while, he returned, drunk and full. When people asked where he had gone, he replied, "The crown prince is celebrating his first birthday, and he sent an envoy to invite me to a feast." The crowd planned to seize his treasure. One day, he happened to be playing the flute, grew tired, and fell asleep. Suddenly, six or seven people gathered and searched his belt for money. The young man, now awake in a panic, feared he would break his promise to the crown prince. He swallowed a pearl and fought fiercely with the crowd. Although the crowd was repelled, the pearl also slid into his throat, making it difficult to swallow or spit it out. He choked to death. The locals pitied him and buried him in a simple coffin near the sea. That night, there was a storm with thunder and lightning, and by the next day, a large mound had formed. The following year, the sea rose again, creating a sandbar around the grave. In areas prone to erosion, many graves were destroyed, but the young man's grave remained untouched. The tide also stopped at this point. After his death, he was often associated with miraculous occurrences, and the locals built a shrine at his grave. Far away, in the Lui River, which is often affected by sea disasters, residents fled in fear of the sea gods. One night, he dreamed of a divine figure with a grand retinue and majestic attire, who said, "I am Shi Da Lang. I pity this place being submerged by the sea. Hurry and move the coffin, and perhaps you can avoid the disaster."

    401

    ”同日而梦者百人，咸惊异，询崇明，果有大郎坟。请于土人，不可；欲盗其骨，更不可。祷者宿神座下，夜闻天乐大作，大郎车骑而至，呼其人而告之曰：“我有一蓑一笠，尚悬所主田翁家，迎而葬之，亦可免冲突。”翌起，往恳某翁，视东壁后有小舍，数堆破败物，拨灰尘寻之，果得风雨具，为大郎旧制。遂鼓乐迎归葬海口，如古名臣之衣冠墓，且筑如崇明之庙。工甫竣，水至，即遽退。两处春秋赛会，士女如蚁，香火如云，均称福荫。每至月白风清，居民犹仿佛大郎笛声与海潮相呜咽也。懊侬氏曰：犀牛逃遁，即耸身蹈海擒之，是何等气焰！海藏宝物，取而炫人，旋复弃之，是何等廉介！辟水珠至死不肯轻掷，是何等信实！豪哉，石大郎！何待捍潮御患，兼及浏河，始为之神耶？噫！冠裳中且不可多得，况牧竖乎？诗云：“牧人乃梦。”吾知其不为鱼而为龙。

    Explanation

    401

    A hundred people dreamed the same dream on the same day, all were surprised and astonished. They asked about Chongming, and indeed, there was a tomb of Dalang. They asked the locals, but they refused; they wanted to steal his bones, but it was even more unacceptable. Those who prayed stayed overnight at the shrine. At night, they heard heavenly music playing, and Dalang arrived in a carriage and chariot. He called out to them and said, "I have a cloak and a hat, still hanging in the house of the old farmer I served. Bring them and bury them, and we can avoid conflict." The next day, they went to plead with a certain old man. They saw a small shed behind the east wall, with several piles of broken and ruined objects. They sifted through the dust to find them, and indeed found the raincoat and hat, made by Dalang in the past. They then welcomed them with music and buried them at Haikou, like the tombs of ancient famous ministers, and built a temple like the one in Chongming. Just as the work was completed, the water came and immediately receded. During the spring and autumn festivals, men and women gathered like ants, and the incense smoke rose like clouds. Both places were said to be blessed. Every time the moon was bright and the wind was clear, the residents still faintly heard Dalang's flute sound mingling with the sea tide. Aonian said, "When the rhinoceros escaped, he stood up and jumped into the sea to catch it, what kind of spirit is that! He took treasures from the sea to show off, but then quickly discarded them, what kind of integrity is that! He would not lightly throw away the water-repelling pearl, even to the point of death, what kind of honesty is that! How heroic, Dalang! Why wait until he defended against tides and disasters, and also helped the Liuhe River, to be deified? Ah! Such heroes are rare even among the nobility, let alone among shepherds. The poem says: 'The herder had a dream.' I know that he did not become a fish but became a dragon."

    402

    马姓
    　　
    　　金陵有林茂才，寇至陷城中，羁某馆，胁授伪书记。夜阑，听刁斗四起，悲从中来，不知此身作何究竟，又不知眷属有无存没，爰默诵《金经》不辍。久之，觉遍体清凉。一夕正诵经，闻窗外一叟呼曰：“林生林生，此时尚不随我三更耶？”三更者，贼语逋逃者名色也。生出户观之，一白发叟，布衣冠，古须眉，素昧平生，不敢多语。叟凝视生，曰：“尔病痴耶？”言已，径前揽生袖急走，飘飘若御风行。时城，鸣钲击柝，贼令森严；而叟所至，巡逻者若无闻见。至狮子山，挈生猛跳城下，若履平地。生私揣江口，若何飞渡。突一满江红大船停泊下关口；登之，叟急解缆挂帆，风瑟瑟，直指江北，盖叟即弄船人也。生入舱，见一男一女，已先在。男，生之同窗友也；女，生之邻家娃，素艳之而未能销魂者也。略寒温，生即瞑目坐，女频视生，秋波盈盈，宛似心许，若碍友不敢言耳。

    Explanation

    402

    A Man of the Ma Clan

    In Jinling, there was a scholar named Lin. When the enemy arrived and captured the city, he was detained in a certain guesthouse and coerced into becoming a false secretary. In the late night, he heard the watchfires lit all around, and sadness welled up within him. He did not know what his fate would be, nor whether his family was alive or dead. He began to silently recite the Jinjing (Diamond Sutra) without ceasing. After a long while, he felt a cool sensation spread throughout his body. One night, as he was reciting the scripture, he heard an old man calling from outside the window: "Lin, Lin, why are you not following me at three in the morning?" Three in the morning was the name for those who escaped the enemy. The scholar went out to look. An old man with white hair, wearing plain clothes and a simple cap, with ancient-looking eyebrows and beard, stood there. They had never met before, and the scholar dared not speak much. The old man looked at the scholar intently and said, "Are you foolish?" After saying this, he stepped forward, grabbed the scholar's sleeve, and hurriedly walked, floating as if riding the wind. The city gates were closed, and drums and wooden clappers were beaten, with the enemy's orders being strict. Yet wherever the old man went, the patrol guards seemed not to notice. They reached Lion Mountain, where the old man grabbed the scholar and leaped down from the city wall as if walking on flat ground. The scholar wondered how they could fly across the river mouth. Suddenly, a large red boat was docked at the lower gate. They boarded it, and the old man quickly untied the ropes and raised the sail. The wind was howling, and the boat headed directly toward the northern bank. It turned out that the old man was the boatman. The scholar entered the cabin and saw a man and a woman already there. The man was his schoolmate, and the woman was his neighbor's daughter, whom he had always admired but had never been captivated by. They exchanged polite greetings, and the scholar sat with his eyes closed. The woman frequently looked at him, her eyes filled with longing, as if they had agreed in their hearts. But she was hesitant to speak because of her friend.

    403

    时夜色昏黑，山月坠江，闻友与邻女，时有调笑声，渐有亵声，闻之心大动，急敛神掩耳，危坐默诵《金经》。闻叟在后艄，鼓楫高歌，歌曰：“天风浪浪兮，江水粼粼；月山剑树兮，雪窖火炕。懦懦蠢蠢兮，虫虫情情；何者因何者果兮，絮絮萍萍。夜何其，夜向晨。人鬼有关兮，祸福无门。”生听之，益悚。远村鸡唱，曙色摇波，叟曰：“到矣。”林生出，此真州界也。“尔由此至扬州东乡，得生路，好自为之。”袖出一函与之，曰：“尔行十余里，可开看。”生崩角在地，泣求姓名，叟笑而不答。起视帆影如驶，顷刻若逾金焦，出海门外矣。急到仙女庙，果遇旧雨，挈赴淮安，团眷属焉。视其书，曰：“余本马姓，生平喜于苦海救人。尔能端正，不负吾意。两个小痴虫，故态复萌，可恶，已将他带赴东海去也。”生后入袁端敏公临淮大营，以军功保二千石，时以叟书示人，一夕，忽随风飘去，如叶，如蝶，如纸鸢，顷刻不见。

    Explanation

    403

    At that time, the night was dark, the mountain moon sinking into the river. He heard the playful laughter of his friends and the neighbor’s daughter, which gradually turned into inappropriate sounds. His heart was deeply stirred, and he quickly focused, covering his ears and sitting upright, silently reciting the Jing. He heard an elderly man in the rear of the boat, rowing and singing loudly. The song went: “The wind howls fiercely, the river water shimmers; the moonlit mountains and sword-like trees, the cold snow and hot hearth. Feeble and foolish, the insects stir with emotions; what causes what effect, like drifting chaff. What time is it? Night turns to dawn. The realm of humans and spirits is intertwined, fortune and misfortune are without doors.” The protagonist listened, feeling even more fearful. From afar, the roosters crowed, and the dawn’s light rippled on the water. The elderly man said, “We have arrived.” Emerging from the boat, they were in the territory of Zhenzhou. “From here, you must take the road to the eastern suburbs of Yangzhou, and make your own way.” The elderly man handed him a letter, saying, “After traveling ten or so miles, you may open it.” The protagonist fell to the ground, weeping and begging for the elderly man’s name, but the man smiled and did not answer. Looking up, he saw the boat’s sails moving swiftly, and in an instant, they seemed to pass Jinshan and Jiaoshan, leaving the mouth of the Yangtze River behind. Hurrying to the Temple of the Fairy Maiden, he indeed met an old acquaintance, who took him to Huai’an, where he reunited with his family. Reading the letter, it said: “I am surnamed Ma, and I have always enjoyed helping those in their suffering. You have maintained your integrity, living up to my expectations. The two young women, who have once again succumbed to their foolishness, are detestable, and I have already taken them to the East Sea.” Later, the protagonist entered General Yuan Duanmin’s camp in Huai’an, and through military merit, he was promoted to the rank of Erqianshi. He often showed the elderly man’s letter to others, but one night, it suddenly flew away with the wind, like a leaf, a butterfly, or a kite, vanishing in an instant.

    404

    离垢园
    　　
    　　离垢园者，东浙贾氏园也。贾名云章，字天孙，少颖敏。游庠后，累不第，遂治园辅，植花树以自娱。久之，痴于洁，屋宇几榻无纤尘，即藩溷亦时时湔滁。以“离垢”名园，以“襄云”命馆。联云：“米襄阳爱洁成癖，倪云林嫉俗如仇。”凡鼎彝书画，花鸟虫鱼，无不珍惜；破产觅之，亦所不顾。至戚属邻里，以急难告者，则闭门不纳。生子一，名浑，字许桥，弃儒学贾。每进几谏，不听，乃泣祷于神曰：“父有洁癖，百折不回；见嫉于世，恐生祸灾。伏乞神灵默佑，感甚。”贾知之，大怒。始詈继挞，乔梓寡恩。一日晨起，督僮拂几，帚过，碎定陶磁尊。怒鞭之，僮负痛，钻几下，几翻而砚山又碎。贾愤燥，索刃，僮遂夺刃自戕。其父告于官，许桥上下夤缘，私献秦凤爵，且厚殓僮，养其父，始罢讼。一夕与妻蓝氏酌，婢献羹，碗炙手，坠地碎。

    Explanation

    404

    Lí Gòu Garden
    　　
    　　Lí Gòu Garden was the garden of the Eastern Zhejiang merchant family. The merchant's name was Yunzhang, and his courtesy name was Tiansun. He was young and exceptionally intelligent. After attending school, he failed the exams repeatedly, so he turned to managing the garden, planting flowers and trees for his own enjoyment. Over time, he became obsessed with cleanliness, keeping his house, furniture, and even the fence and latrine free of any dust. He named the garden "Lí Gòu" and the pavilion "Xiāng Yún." The couplet read: "Mi Xiangyang had a passion for cleanliness, while Ni Yunlin hated the world like an enemy." He cherished all bronze vessels, paintings, calligraphy, and depictions of flowers, birds, insects, and fish, going to great lengths to acquire them, even if it meant going bankrupt. When relatives or neighbors came to him in urgent need, he would refuse to open the door. He had one son, named Hún, with the courtesy name Xuqiao, who abandoned his studies to learn the trade. He often tried to gently advise his father, but his father would not listen. One day, he prayed to the gods, saying, "Father has a passion for cleanliness, unyielding despite repeated setbacks; hated by the world, I fear this will bring disaster. I humbly ask for the gods' silent protection, deeply grateful." The father found out and was furious. He began to curse and beat his son, showing little mercy. One morning, he ordered the servant to wipe the table, and the broom accidentally broke a Dingtao porcelain vase. He angrily whipped the servant, who, in pain, crawled under the table, causing the table to tip over and the inkstone to break. The father, now furious, grabbed a knife, and the servant seized the knife and killed himself. The father reported the incident to the authorities, and Xuqiao used his connections to bribe Qin Fengju, buried the servant lavishly, and supported his father, finally ending the lawsuit. One night, he drank with his wife, Lady Lan, and a maid served soup. The bowl was so hot that it fell to the ground and broke.

    405

    贾怒曰：“此供秦制也，尔断吾命根耶？”呼杖，婢惧而投诸井。许桥厚殓之，且召僧讽经超荐。无何，有同学友来访，询及收藏，贾出宋眉子砚以炫之。友呵气试润。贾恶其秽，刺刺不休。友稍讽之，怒以砚击友，几碎其颅。又以炙炭煮汉玉佩，火起，蔓延邻舍，风号焰烈，顷刻数家。许桥背父，婉谢厚偿，始无恙。妻蓝婉劝曰：“古人以洁为束身之范，君以洁为缠身之魔，若不改悟，恐随身皆荆棘场也，尚得为离垢园哉？”贾怒批其颊，妻痛哭。贾恒独眠，每欲敦伦，必看河魁，蓄温水；事毕，澡身更衣薰香，作种种恶态。是夕哀啼至夜，有美妇人自外来，曰：“姊姊冤杀哉，个男儿毫无一点情，侬见之，几欲坠唾。”蓝漫应之。又曰：“我与若游花园，倒好耍子。”袖出一圈示蓝，曰：“此中大有佳处。”视之，果有楼台花木，金碧陆离。遂对镜匀粉黛，整衣衫。

    Explanation

    405

    贾怒曰：“此供秦制也，尔断吾命根耶？”呼杖，婢惧而投诸井。许桥厚殓之，且召僧讽经超荐。无何，有同学友来访，询及收藏，贾出宋眉子砚以炫之。友呵气试润。贾恶其秽，刺刺不休。友稍讽之，怒以砚击友，几碎其颅。又以炙炭煮汉玉佩，火起，蔓延邻舍，风号焰烈，顷刻数家。许桥背父，婉谢厚偿，始无恙。妻蓝婉劝曰：“古人以洁为束身之范，君以洁为缠身之魔，若不改悟，恐随身皆荆棘场也，尚得为离垢园哉？”贾怒批其颊，妻痛哭。贾恒独眠，每欲敦伦，必看河魁，蓄温水；事毕，澡身更衣薰香，作种种恶态。是夕哀啼至夜，有美妇人自外来，曰：“姊姊冤杀哉，个男儿毫无一点情，侬见之，几欲坠唾。”蓝漫应之。又曰：“我与若游花园，倒好耍子。”袖出一圈示蓝，曰：“此中大有佳处。”视之，果有楼台花木，金碧陆离。遂对镜匀粉黛，整衣衫。

    406

    妇以罗带系梁上，对妻再拜，回视案上，灯光如豆，户外鼻息甚酣，万种凄凉，逡巡自缢。婢觉，急告许桥，已不能救。抚尸号痛，死而后苏。贾知之，犹以衣袖擦宣炉，聊滴清泪而已。讣岳，蓝叟告宰，县役到门，汹汹如虎，呼号啖嚼；邻人来观，乱摘花果。贾目睹蹂躏，痛极失声。叟疑渠尚有遗之悲，又以许桥重贿，始忍而罢讼。然家道亦从此中落矣。烂铜碎玉，昔以重价购来，顷以廉资售出。炊烟渐断，仅剩荒园。贾鳏而子未婚，时作楚囚之泣。忽有客携短童来谒，仙风道骨，翩翩在门。迓入与谈，自言张姓无字，人呼为张老相公，渊博古今，尘谈霏屑。贾恨相见晚。许桥思留以慰父，以敝裘付质库，置酒篝灯，且留下榻。自贾之好洁也，君子恶之，小人诳之；富易为贫，朋侪绝迹。顷得张，不禁狂喜。久之，张唾落于壁，必怒于心；遗屙于庭，必怒于色。一日勉与坐，张之童又弄毙架上鹦鹉，怒遂不可忍。

    Explanation

    406

    The woman tied a silk sash to the beam, bowed twice to her wife, and hesitated before committing suicide. She looked back at the table, where the lamp burned dimly like a bean, and heard heavy snoring outside the door. Overwhelmed by a myriad of sorrows, she finally hanged herself. The maid noticed and hurriedly informed Xu Qiao, but it was too late to save her. Holding her body, she wailed in grief, dying and then reviving. Jia, upon learning of her death, still wiped a bronze incense burner with his sleeve, shedding only a few clear tears. He informed his father-in-law, who then told the local official. The county officials arrived at the door, behaving like tigers, shouting and chewing; neighbors came to watch, picking flowers and fruits indiscriminately. Jia, witnessing the desecration, lost his voice from extreme pain. The father-in-law suspected that the deceased still had unresolved grief and, bribed by Xu Qiao, finally relented and ended the lawsuit. However, the family's fortunes declined from this point on. Once valuable copper and jade, now sold at low prices, the smoke from cooking fires gradually disappeared, leaving only a desolate garden. Jia was a widower, and his son was unmarried, often weeping like a Chu captive. Suddenly, a guest arrived with a young boy, exuding an ethereal and scholarly demeanor. Jia welcomed him in, and they talked; the guest introduced himself as Zhang, surname only, and was called "Zhang Laoshengong." Scholarly and knowledgeable about both ancient and modern times, they engaged in trivial conversation. Jia regretted meeting him too late. Xu Qiao thought to keep him to comfort his father, selling his old fur coat to the pawnshop, setting up a feast with lanterns, and inviting him to stay. Since Jia's obsession with cleanliness, gentlemen disliked him, and small people deceived him; from rich to poor, friends disappeared. Upon meeting Zhang, Jia could not help but be ecstatic. After a while, Zhang spat on the wall, which angered Jia; he defecated in the courtyard, which made Jia visibly angry. One day, Jia forced himself to sit with Zhang, but Zhang's child played and killed a parrot on the perch, making Jia's anger unbearable.

    407

    张曰：“莫怒莫怒，吾携君游一胜境以赎罪，何如？”不觉随之假山后，遇一石洞，偻而入，蛇行数十步，洞渐宽，路渐平，达于园，豁然开朗。苍松夹道，流水涓涓，白玉为梁，幽芳作帧。两壁磨崖上镌科蚪奇字，似秦汉以上书。逾岭一重，又得古洞，薜荔五色，彩丝下垂。入其室，则插架牙笺，间以丝竹；炉香犹篆，帘波有纹；几上横琴，古锦为囊。贾欲解视之，张曰：“不可，此吾师所弹也。指误弦，必有天神下降，风雨疾雷。

    Explanation

    407

    Zhang said, "Do not be angry, do not be angry. I will take you to a beautiful place to atone for your sin. How about that?" Without noticing, he followed Zhang behind a false mountain and encountered a stone cave. They bent and entered, slithering like snakes for dozens of steps. The cave gradually widened, and the path became more level, eventually reaching a garden where everything suddenly became clear and bright. Pine trees lined the path, with gentle flowing water nearby. White jade beams supported the structure, and fragrant flowers framed the scene. On the walls carved into the rock, strange characters were inscribed, resembling writings from before the Qin and Han dynasties. Crossing another mountain range, they found another ancient cave, with five-colored climbing plants and colorful threads hanging down. Entering the room, they saw sheets of fine paper inserted into bookshelves, interspersed with string and bamboo instruments. The incense still formed spirals, and the curtains had wavy patterns. A lute lay on the table, its case made of ancient brocade. Jia wanted to look at it, but Zhang said, "No, this is the lute played by my teacher. If the fingers make a mistake on the strings, there will be divine intervention with thunder and lightning."

    408

    君既到此，不可不作东道主。”向内一呼，美人四集，琼筵甫设，醴酒已温。品味既佳，器具尤古。张以巨觥劝客曰：“较君家园庭何如？”吃吃而对曰：“远甚。”须臾美人歌舞，荡魄柔魂。张亦抽古剑示贾，曰：“此鹿庐七星剑也，君能舞乎？”曰：“不能。”张逡巡敛袖，左盘右旋，寒光闪烁。美人更鼓冰丝，顿挫应节。忽闻剥啄声甚厉，一美人自门隙窥之，曰：“师长回矣。”众艳惊散。张变色曰：“君速随我后户出。”缭曲环，绝非来路。

    Explanation

    408

    Since you have arrived here, you cannot fail to be the host. He called out inside, and four beautiful women gathered. A luxurious feast was just set up, and sweet wine was already warmed. The taste was excellent, and the utensils were especially ancient. Zhang invited the guest with a large cup, saying, "How does your own garden compare to mine?" The guest replied with a laugh, "Much farther." Soon, the beautiful women began to sing and dance, moving the spirit and softening the soul. Zhang also drew an ancient sword to show the guest, saying, "This is the Lu庐 Seven-Star Sword. Can you dance with it?" The guest replied, "I cannot." Zhang hesitated, gathered his sleeves, and moved left and right, the cold light flickering. The women beat the drums and played the strings, the music responding to the rhythm. Suddenly, a loud knocking sound was heard. One of the women peered through a crack in the door and said, "The master has returned." The beautiful women scattered in fear. Zhang changed color and said, "Quickly, follow me out through the back door." They took a winding and complex path, which was not the way they came.

    409

    视楼阁最多处曰“琅环秘府，”藏金石处曰“证古斋”，藏经史处曰“辨理窟，”藏诗词处曰“游艺轩，”藏书画处曰“怡情馆。”又一楼，峨峨三层，上矗霄汉，第一层曰“与天为徒；”第二层曰“与古为徒”，第三层曰“与今为徒。”有小村落，畎亩参差，一曰“情田，”二曰“心田，”三曰“福田。”开富贵花，种吉祥草，沃功德水，培干净土。行逾数十高岭，峰峦迭翠，径坦且平，曰“心头方寸地，”“欢喜园”，“水晶域”，“光明藏”。金碧楼台，奇景眩目。突转一径，景物全非，凝神端详，已在自家园中，盖未尝出门一步耳。张曰：“今日之游，乐乎？”曰：“空中幻景耶？”曰：“何者为真，何者为幻？以为真即真，以为幻即幻。真幻皆根于心，而不限以地也。”由是贾奉张如神明，家中所有秘玩，皆质之于张，张均鄙薄，一无许可。贾以为太过。

    Explanation

    409

    In the place with the most buildings, it is called "Lan Huan Mi Fu" (Lingering Ring Secret Treasury), where precious stones and metals are stored, it is called "Zheng Gu Zhai" (Verifying Ancient Study), where scriptures and histories are stored, it is called "Bian Li Ku" (Discerning Principles Cave), where poems and verses are stored, it is called "You Yi Xuan" (Enjoying Arts Hall), and where paintings and calligraphy are stored, it is called "Yi Qing Guan" (Cultivating Emotions Pavilion). Another building, towering three stories high, reaching up to the heavens, the first floor is called "Yu Tian Wei Tu" (Companion of Heaven), the second floor is called "Yu Gu Wei Tu" (Companion of the Ancients), and the third floor is called "Yu Jin Wei Tu" (Companion of the Present). There is a small village with fields arranged in an irregular pattern, the first one is called "Qing Tuan" (Emotion Field), the second one is called "Xin Tuan" (Heart Field), and the third one is called "Fu Tuan" (Merit Field). Planting flowers of wealth and fortune, growing grass of good omen, watering with water of merit, and nurturing with clean soil. Walking past dozens of high hills, the peaks and ridges are green, and the path is smooth and level, it is called "Xin Tou Fang Cun Di" (Heart's Square Inch Place), "Huan Xi Yuan" (Joyful Garden), "Shui Jing Yu" (Crystal Realm), and "Guang Ming Cang" (Light Treasury). Golden and green towers and pavilions, with strange and dazzling sights. Suddenly turning a path, the scenery is completely different. Focusing intently, one finds oneself already in their own garden, it is as if one has never left home at all. Zhang asks, "Is today's journey enjoyable?" "Is it an illusion in the air?" "What is real, and what is an illusion? If one believes it to be real, it is real; if one believes it to be an illusion, it is an illusion. Reality and illusion both stem from the heart, not limited by place." From this, Jia regards Zhang as a deity. All the secret treasures in the house are entrusted to Zhang. Zhang considers them all trivial and gives no approval. Jia thinks this is excessive.

    410

    曰：“君如不信，目前架上古铜象高六尺者，君以为何物？”曰：“满身铜翠，班剥陆离，形制既工，雕镂亦古，的真秦铜也。”曰：“秦则真秦，君知秦人造此何用乎？”曰：“不知。”曰：“女子溺器耳。尚高高供养，不几污秽杀人！”曰：“有何凭证？”曰：“象脊有铜盖嵌下，象四足皆有小洞，以火炙之，必有骚臭气。”已而验之，果然，张云：“阿房宫五步一楼，十步一阁，门首皆设此物，足下即通暗沟，宫人欲溺，即揭盖解裤，骑而溺之。否则重楼宵汉，嫔嫱如云，何有此许多溺器？唯得此，始便耳。”贾由是稍悟，视收藏皆若粪土。一日，谓张曰：“洞中风景绝佳，迥异尘世。然楼阁已稍寓目，而钟鼎琳琅，必多而且富，未知能许俗子一饱馋眸乎？”曰：“不难，嘱小饮，当与君再往一游。”甫三爵，贾沉沉思卧，张忽拉之急走，曰：“前由正门进，后户出，今适旁门开，机不可失，盍急趋可进。”果达一处，日色惨淡，道路亦歧。

    Explanation

    410

    If you don’t believe me, look at the ancient bronze elephant statue on the shelf, which is six feet tall. What do you think it is? It is covered with a greenish patina, has a mottled and colorful appearance, is well-crafted, and has ancient carvings. It is indeed a true Qin dynasty bronze statue. If it is a true Qin statue, do you know what the Qin people used it for? I don’t know. It is a urinal for women. Keeping it high up is disrespectful and could lead to contamination or harm! What proof do you have? The elephant’s back has a copper lid, and its four feet have small holes. If you heat the holes, there will be a foul odor. After testing it, it was indeed so. Zhang said, “The A-Fang Palace has buildings every five and ten steps. At the entrances, there are these statues, and below them are underground drains. Palace ladies would urinate by lifting the lid, removing their skirts, and riding the statue. Otherwise, in such a grand palace with its lofty buildings and numerous concubines, why would there be so many urinals? Only with such a device could they be convenient.” From this, Jia began to understand and saw his collection as worthless. One day, he said to Zhang, “The scenery inside the cave is extremely beautiful and different from the mundane world. Although I have seen some of the buildings, I wonder if there are many and rich treasures like bronze vessels inside, and if I can see them.” Zhang said, “It’s not difficult. Let’s have a little drink, and I will take you there again.” After a few cups of wine, Jia became drowsy. Zhang suddenly pulled him and said, “We entered through the main door before, and exited through the back door. Now that the side door is open, we must take this opportunity. Let’s hurry and enter.” They reached a place where the sunlight was dim, and the paths were branching.

    411

    张忽遇一故人，话良久，告贾曰：“君且略散步，仆随故人行。”行逾远，径逾窄，偶一失足，坠土窟中。鬼物钻集，曰：“贾某来耶？”挈之游地狱。泥犁十八层，宛然吴道子所画者，丝毫不爽。见妻蓝，项拖罗带，舌吐唇外二寸许，婢仆被磨折死者亦踯躅至，齐声索命。贾正危急，急闻王者召，鬼卒引入伏案下，略讯三两句，王拍案大骂曰：“狗彘奴！外风雅而内刻薄，假清洁而真污秽，所谓狂伪鄙俚痴荡惑者，此也！”即命两旁牛头，押赴畜生道中去。鬼卒驱出，至一小园内，颇清雅，遍地芭蕉，茅屋如斗。内有红装女子，捧心娇啼。贾略凝神，鬼卒自后一推，惊醒，则身变作小猪，与老猪众小猪同卧粪汁中。闻人语呼云：“猪产豕雏矣！其数六。”贾心中了了，即以头触壁，狂呼不已，闻耳畔低唤曰：“醒醒！白日，大人即梦魇耶？”张目四顾，身犹在座，客与僮已杳，唯其子与病奴守于侧。

    Explanation

    411

    Zhang suddenly met an old acquaintance, and they spoke for a long time. The acquaintance told Zhang, "Sir, please take a brief walk; I will follow my old friend." As they walked further, the path became increasingly narrow. Zhang accidentally stumbled and fell into a hole in the ground. Ghostly creatures gathered around and asked, "Is this the man named Jia?" They took him on a tour of hell. The eighteen layers of hell were just as depicted by Wu Daozi, not a single thing was wrong. Zhang saw his wife, who was blue, with a silk scarf dragging from her neck and her tongue protruding two inches beyond her lips. Servants who were tortured and died also came stumbling, and they all cried out for vengeance. Zhang was in dire straits when he suddenly heard the king summon him. Ghost soldiers led him to crouch under a desk and briefly questioned him for a few sentences. The king slammed his hand on the table and shouted, "Dog and pig slave! Seemingly refined on the outside but cruel on the inside, pretending to be clean but actually being filthy, a mad, false, vulgar, ignorant, foolish, and confused person, this is you!" The king immediately ordered the ghost soldiers to send Zhang to the realm of beasts. The ghost soldiers led him out to a small garden, which was quite elegant, covered with banana plants and huts like small boxes. Inside, there was a red-clothed woman holding her heart and crying softly. Zhang focused slightly, and the ghost soldier pushed him from behind, causing him to wake up. He found himself transformed into a pig, lying in the feces with a group of other pigs. He heard someone call out, "The pig has given birth to piglets! There are six of them." Zhang realized everything clearly and immediately hit his head against the wall, crying out wildly without stopping. He heard a whisper in his ear, "Wake up! It's daytime, are you having a nightmare, sir?" He opened his eyes and looked around, still sitting in his seat. The guests and servants had vanished, only his son and the sick servant were guarding beside him.

    412

    架上鹦鹉如故，曰：“茶来，主人醒也。”乃泣告子，使人侦之，邻家栅中，果生六豕，一颠痫遽毙。所谓芭蕉者，庭中白菜一畦也。曰：“地狱已在眼前，奈何不悟？”遂以家政委于子，己则长斋绣佛，日诵《金经》，哀号忏悔。许桥娶恽氏，美而贤，能内助。生子二，皆聪颖。阅四载，家渐复。旋得窖金，力行善事，建种种功德，二十年勿替。两孙举于乡，泥金到门，贾犹捻珠讽诵。孙之同年辈，争市花草玩好以媚之，皆却而不受。是时，座客满堂，冠裳云集，贾忽扶杖而出，唏嘘曰：“吾一回头，尔侪始有今日，吾不忘张公德也。拟于离垢园中，肖张公像，子孙奉祀，尔等愿否？”众应之。乃鸠工营造，不日告成。偶偕孙辈扶乩，张忽临坛，与贾叙旧好，请示名氏，乩旋转而大书曰：“张邋遢。”懊侬氏曰：仙人之邋遢，正仙人之清净；仙人之游戏，正仙人之慈悲。不肖儿回头猛省，正不肖儿能寻快活，能讨便宜处。

    Explanation

    412

    The parrot on the shelf remained as before, saying, "Bring tea, the master is awake." The master then wept and told his son, who went to investigate. In the neighbor's pen, six pigs had indeed been born, but one of them died suddenly from epilepsy. What is called the banana plant is actually a patch of Chinese cabbage in the courtyard. He said, "Hell is already before our eyes, why don't we realize it?" Thus, he entrusted the management of the household to his son and himself began to observe long-term vegetarianism and devote himself to Buddhist practice, reciting the Golden Sutra daily and mourning and repenting. Xuqiao married Yunshe, who was both beautiful and virtuous, and could assist in the household. They had two sons, both of whom were intelligent. After four years, the family gradually recovered. Soon, they found buried gold, which they used to do good deeds, building various meritorious acts without ceasing for twenty years. The two grandsons passed the local examination, and gold-leafed scrolls arrived at the door, but the master continued to pray and recite scriptures. The contemporaries of the grandsons competed to buy flowers and playthings to flatter them, but they all refused to accept them. At that time, the hall was filled with guests, all dressed in formal attire. Suddenly, the master, supporting himself with a cane, stepped out and sighed, saying, "If I had turned back just once, you would have had this day, and I would not forget the virtue of Master Zhang. I plan to build a likeness of Master Zhang in Ligou Garden, and your descendants will pay homage. Would you all agree?" Everyone responded in agreement. They then hired workers to begin construction, and it was completed in no time. Once, accompanied by his grandsons, he used a divining board, and Master Zhang suddenly appeared on the platform, recounting their old friendship. When asked for his name, the board spun and wrote, "Zhang Lata." Aghast, the master said, "The immortals' carelessness is actually their purity; the immortals' playfulness is actually their compassion. The unworthy sons who turn back in sudden realization are actually the unworthy sons who can find joy and gain advantage."

    413

    陶庄
    　　
    　　天长龙兴集之北，有感荡湖，烟波浩渺，水禽咯啁，颇称佳景。湖中央有土丘一坯，广可十亩。东为贝冈，蜿蜒起伏，田水四达，涓涓由东之西入湖，必经土丘过。登丘一望，视横冶诸山，宛在几席。而后面又有数十小培，可为靠厢。城阁台榭，遥为四屏；双桥彩虹，如张旗鼓，是真吉壤。惜向无人知，仅为耕农散牧之所。忽有客自江西来，毛姓峤名，方壶其字，自云“堪舆家”，馆于庐陇陶庄，宾主投洽。为人寡言笑，爱趺坐，间言休咎，无不奇中。然每午餐后，必芒鞋竹笠，放浪于山巅水涯，茕茕来去，至夕方归。又自弄小艇游湖上，必穷极烟水深处而后返。

    Explanation

    413

    North of Tianchang Longxing Collection, there lies a lake named Gandang Lake, with its waters vast and misty, and the chirping of waterfowl adding to its scenic beauty. In the center of the lake stands a small earthen mound, covering about ten acres. To the east is Bei Mountain, winding and undulating, with irrigation channels flowing in all directions, a trickle moving from east to west into the lake, passing through the mound. Climbing the mound to look out, one sees the mountains of Hengye as if on a table. Behind there are dozens of small mounds, which can serve as a backrest. City pavilions and terraces, distant as four screens; the double bridge like a rainbow, as if raising flags and drums—this is truly auspicious land. Unfortunately, it was previously unknown, only a place for farming and grazing. Suddenly, a guest came from Jiangxi, last name Mao, given name Jiao, courtesy name Fanghu, who claimed to be a geomancer, staying at the Lu Long Taozhuang. The host and guest got along well. He was a person who rarely spoke or laughed, loved to sit cross-legged, occasionally speaking of good and bad omens, never failing to hit the mark. However, every day after lunch, he would wear straw shoes and a bamboo hat, wandering freely on mountain peaks and water edges, coming and going alone, returning only at dusk. He also played with a small boat on the lake, exploring the deepest parts of the misty waters before returning.

    414

    忽微疾，陶翁亲为侍汤药。瞬西风起，陶翁立制新衣赠之，不受，翁俟其寝熟，潜易其敝者。毛无奈，始衣之。婢仆侍役，礼或稍疏，翁必加杖责。毛感极，忽谓翁曰：“旅人飘泊承盛德，思有以报，未知翁意所在，欲贵乎？欲富乎？乞示我。”翁曰：“能富即贵耳。”曰：“湖中有佳处，可为阳宅，子孙徙而居之，可富数百甲子。”翌日，偕翁放船去，为指示，即是丘也。翁归，即以重资购成，四周水田亦在其内。呼工庀材，倩毛诹吉点向。毛蹙额曰：“翁知鄙人毛遂之意乎？”曰：“不知。”曰：“仆自知命宫，往后有三十六年磨蝎运，恶曜所照，数不可逃。若家居，尤颠沛。

    Explanation

    414

    A sudden illness struck, and Old Master Tao personally tended to him with soup and medicine. As the west wind began to blow, Old Master Tao immediately made new clothes and offered them, but he refused to accept them. The old man waited until he was asleep and secretly replaced his old clothes. Feeling helpless, he eventually wore the new clothes. If the etiquette of the female servants and attendants was slightly lacking, the old man would certainly add a stick punishment. Feeling deeply grateful, he suddenly asked the old man, "A traveler, wandering and far from home, has received your great kindness. I wish to repay you, but I do not know what you desire. Do you want wealth or power? Please tell me." The old man replied, "Wealth will bring you power." He said, "There is a good place in the lake that can be used as a residence. If descendants move and live there, they can enjoy wealth for hundreds of generations." The next day, he went with the old man to release the boat and pointed to a specific location. The old man returned and immediately purchased the land with heavy funds, including the surrounding paddy fields. He called for workers to prepare materials and asked Mao to choose a good direction. Mao frowned and said, "Do you know the intentions of humble Mao Sui?" The old man replied, "I do not know." Mao said, "I know my fate. In the future, I will have 36 years of difficult fate, under the influence of ill-omened stars, and my destiny cannot be escaped. If I live at home, it will be especially difficult."

    415

    顷为翁卜筑瀛第，成则翁富，富则地灵怒，鄙人必丧明，终日黑摸索，谁为给衣食？翁长者，能谕子孙不失信乎？”曰：“是何言？仆即瞑目，当立遗命，违者堕泥犁”。毛喜，即为营造。上梁日，毛犹与翁闲话，曰：“若为竹径，若为荷亭，便不负诗情画意也。”言未已，忽睹日边有黑子如弹，声铮铮自西来，倏忽大如鹰隼，遽扑眉宇，毛大呼倒地，扶起视之，二目已盲。由是坐卧行动，常在一室，饮食供养，精于平时。翁长子次子，均于是秋文武同中式。翁益信毛。时于柴门倚仗，看湖天水月，倏然出尘。忽睹前岸有火光，若青磷之乱舞。然火有焰，而光有芒。问毛，曰：“试往掘之，必有得。

    Explanation

    415

    Recently, I chose a site for the old man to build a grand residence, the "Yingdi." If it is completed, the old man will become wealthy. However, if he becomes too wealthy, the land's spirits will become angry, and I, the humble servant, will surely lose my sight and be left to fumble in darkness, wondering who will provide me with food and clothing? The old man, a wise elder, can instruct his descendants to be trustworthy, can he not?" He replied, "What is this saying? I will close my eyes and leave a will, and those who break it will fall into a hellish place." Maoyi was pleased and immediately began construction. On the day of the beam-raising ceremony, Maoyi still chatted casually with the old man, saying, "If there is a path lined with bamboo and a pavilion with lotus flowers, it will not disappoint the poetic and picturesque atmosphere." Before he finished speaking, he suddenly saw a black dot like a bullet near the sun, with a metallic sound coming from the west. Suddenly, it grew as large as an eagle or falcon and flew into his face. Maoyi cried out and fell to the ground. When lifted up, he found his eyes were already blind. Thereafter, sitting, standing, moving, he always remained within one room, and food and care were more meticulous than usual. The old man's eldest and second sons both passed the civil and military exams in the same year. The old man became even more trusting of Maoyi. Sometimes leaning on a staff at the thatched gate, watching the lake, sky, and moon, he suddenly transcended the mundane. Suddenly, he saw a firelight on the shore, like green phosphorus dancing wildly. But the fire had flames, and the light had rays. He asked Maoyi, "Try digging it up, there will definitely be something found."

    416

    ”如言携畚往，果得窖朱提十二瓮，遂大富。长次两君，欲赴京兆试，毛颇阻挠，不听，公车北上，竟中进士归。翁犹如故，而其子竟不能不心焉疑之。长君旋以太守，次君亦以都司，出仕五六载。翁病笃，呼两子弃官回籍，宦橐甚丰，陈骡纲于庭。翁唏嘘曰：“尔辈知从何始有今日耶？”曰：“大人德荫也。”曰：“非也，此毛君之功。我死后，尔辈积德累功，以报毛君，更事之如父，较重于寻常父执，则地利可坚。倘礼衰即以不孝论。”两人泣受教，翁更托孤于毛，宾主嗟叹。翁卒，二子守制于家，颇事声色，作威福。毛谏之，不听，即亦不再言。而礼竟从此缺。毛枯坐团瓢中，闻厅事酣歌快舞声，詈挞奴仆声，颇不耐。忽又闻两三小童唱曰：“瞎子瞎零丁，吃了多少死苍蝇。

    Explanation

    416

    As he said, he carried a basket and went, and indeed found a hidden place containing twelve jars of valuable metal, thus becoming very wealthy. The elder and younger brothers, wishing to take the imperial examination in the capital region, were greatly obstructed by Mr. Mao, who did not listen. They went north by imperial carriage, eventually passing the examination and returning. The old man remained as before, but his sons could not help but doubt him in their hearts. The elder brother soon became a governor, and the younger brother also became a military governor, serving in office for five or six years. When the old man fell gravely ill, he called his two sons to resign from their posts and return to their hometown, where their official wealth was very abundant, and they displayed a convoy of mules in the courtyard. The old man sighed deeply and said, "Do you know where you began to achieve today?" They replied, "It is the blessing of our father's virtue." He said, "No, this is the merit of Mr. Mao. After my death, you must accumulate virtue and merit to repay Mr. Mao, treating him as a father, more important than ordinary fatherly friends, so that local benefits can be maintained. If rites decline, it will be considered filial impiety." The two brothers wept and accepted his instruction, and the old man further entrusted his children to Mr. Mao, with both guest and host sighing and lamenting. When the old man died, the two sons observed mourning rites at home, indulging in music and beauty and exercising authority and power. Mr. Mao advised them, but they did not listen, and he then stopped talking. The rites were lost from then on. Mr. Mao sat idly in his humble dwelling, hearing the sounds of drunken singing and dancing, scolding and beating slaves and servants, which he could not bear. Suddenly, he heard a couple of young boys sing, "Blind and lonely, how many dead flies have you eaten?"

    417

    瞎子瞎鹿渎，吃了多少钻蛆肉。”心更厌恶。一日，有斗鸡误落藩溷淹毙，次子谕即弃去，长君立命以陶器就火燔熟，为毛午餐。餐已，雏婢来问曰：“先生食鸡汁甘乎？”曰：“味犹是也。”曰：“得味外味乎？”毛知有异，婉询婢，婢缕述，知大郎之恶作剧。嘱勿语，收匕鬯去。由是毛心顿寒而辞色不露。唯命环第四周多种桑，询何所取，曰：“寻常青鸟，只知此处为横冶入湖正脉，沙岸回环，辅山道向，不知此名‘龟趺穴。’植以树则绿荫参天，如龟盖之生绿毛，贵不可言。”两人信而从之。

    Explanation

    417

    A blind man named Xialu Du ate so much drilled maggot meat. His heart grew even more disgusted. One day, a fighting chicken mistakenly fell into the fence toilet and drowned. The second son ordered it to be thrown away, but the eldest son commanded it to be roasted in a pottery vessel over fire, serving it as feathered lunch. After the meal, a young maid came and asked, "Master, did the chicken broth taste sweet?" He replied, "The taste is still the same." She then asked, "Did you get the taste beyond taste?" Knowing something was strange, he gently inquired of the maid, who told him in detail. He realized the eldest son was playing a prank. He ordered her not to speak and took away the ladle and incense. Thereby, the feathered one's heart suddenly turned cold, though his words and expressions did not show it. He only ordered to plant mulberry trees around the fourth circle and asked what the purpose was. The response was, "Ordinary green birds only know that this place is the main vein where the transverse lake meets the river, with sandy shores winding around, aiding the mountain road. They do not know that this place is called 'Turtle Foot Cave.' If planted with trees, the green shade would reach the sky, like the green hair growing on the turtle's shell, priceless to say." The two believed and followed the instruction.

    418

    植甫年余，地忽震动，举宅惶恐，毛遽以手抚匡床，呼曰：“误矣！”询之，曰：“吾妄言，言非公子所深信。盍掘中堂地二尺，可得一断碣。”如言掘，果得。文曰：“形则龟，体则瓢，葬者汉将军，破者江西毛。”字隶体，苔花绣涩，短石如砖。述于毛，曰：“公子无恐，有仆在，禳之尚未晚也。”遂扶之，步第之左右，口嚼土花，辨味而定穴者四，曰：“盍急穿深井。”又于第后植杖画地如人字，曰：“盍急浚小沼，如是，则老元绪当长相守，富贵可万年。”工甫竣，毛之双瞳忽开朗，遂揖别曰：“二十余年坐承豢养，心甚不安。幸天佑盲瞳复明，从此天涯海角，或有晤时。”欲挽之，已飘然徒步行。毛去，茕茕一身，资斧断绝。行至来安山中，有小庙，距郭近，神佛抛露，众丐居之。

    Explanation

    418

    After a year or so of planting, the ground suddenly shook, causing the entire household to panic. Mao quickly placed his hand on the bed and called out, "It's a mistake!" When asked, he said, "I spoke rashly, and what I said is not something the young master deeply believes. Let's dig two feet into the middle of the hall, and we can find a broken stone tablet." Following his words, they dug, and indeed found it. The inscription read, "The shape is like a turtle, the body is like a gourd, the buried one is a Han general, the breaker is Mao from Jiangxi." The characters were in the clerical script, with moss-covered and dull stone as short as a brick. He told Mao, "Young master, do not be afraid, there is a servant here, it's not too late to ward off the calamity." He then supported him, walking around the house, chewing on soil flowers, tasting and determining four spots, and said, "Let's quickly dig a deep well." He also planted a staff behind the house, drawing the ground in the shape of a person, and said, "Let's quickly dredge a small pond. If we do so, Old Yuan Xu will keep watch forever, and wealth and nobility can last for ten thousand years." Just as the work was completed, Mao's double pupils suddenly became clear. He then bowed and said, "For over twenty years, I have been under your care, and I feel very uneasy. Fortunately, my blind eyes have been restored by divine protection. From now on, no matter where we are in the world, there may be a chance to meet again." They tried to stop him, but he had already walked away alone. Mao left, alone and destitute, with no means of support. He walked to La'an Mountain, where there was a small temple close to the city. The gods and Buddhas were exposed, and a group of beggars lived there.

    419

    毛谓丐曰：“尔等能供养我，我能为尔等造命。”众丐争应曰：“诺。”即洁后殿一笏地以居毛。各出乞于村市，归以洁者奉毛，甚殷勤。年余，丐中有黄耳小犬病癫毙，毛命醵钱市小棺，并小衣冠殓犬。各披麻执杖，号泣如生。毛于庙后点一穴葬犬。讵葬后，众丐心顿明，渐知愧耻，忽泣曰：“蹴嗟来，何其难堪乎？”遂改习织蒲，或小负贩。渐得利。不二年，丐俱化为小康，各于近村营家室。不忘毛德，争供养之。毛曰：“尔曹曩以庙败，渎神已甚，盍再醵资略修葺，吾能使庙兴。”众曰：“诺。”毛为之开巨牖二，接南山秀；开土窖一，泄北阴煞。置签筒，集签诗，而神亦灵显。车马纷纷来报赛，卖茶卖香烛者，环居成村落。

    Explanation

    419

    Mao said to the beggars, "If you can support me, I can change your fate." The beggars eagerly agreed, saying, "Yes." They immediately cleaned a small area in the back hall for Mao to live in. Each of them went out to beg in the village market and returned with what they had cleaned to offer to Mao, showing great respect and diligence. After a year, one of the beggars' small yellow-eared dog died from madness. Mao ordered them to collect money to buy a small coffin and small clothes and caps to wrap the dog. They all wore coarse cloth and held sticks, crying as if the dog were still alive. Mao dug a grave in the back of the temple to bury the dog. Who would have thought that after the burial, the beggars suddenly understood and gradually became aware of shame. They suddenly cried and said, "How quickly it came, how difficult it was!" They then changed to weaving rush or small peddling. Gradually, they gained profit. Within two years, all the beggars transformed into a comfortable life and established homes in nearby villages. They did not forget Mao's kindness and competed to support him. Mao said, "You all in the past due to the temple's ruin, greatly offended the gods. Why not again collect funds to repair the temple slightly? I can make the temple prosperous." The crowd said, "Yes." Mao opened two large windows to connect with the beauty of the south mountain and dug one earth cellar to dispel the north's evil qi. He placed a sign tube and collected sign poems, and the deity also became more powerful and visible. Carriages and horses came in a hurry to report the success of the ritual, and those selling tea and incense candles surrounded and formed a village.

    420

    又来高僧主席，缁衣白足，不下百人；画栋雕梁，晨钟暮鼓，成大精蓝。是日，正集众善信，开道场，毛亦合掌念佛。忽有香客，云自龙兴集来，凝视诧曰：“公其陶庄之毛先生耶？”曰：“然。”客遂缕述陶庄事。陶自毛去后，不两年，被盗，遭祝融，罹冤狱，家业顿倾。两君削职，已物故。庄乃废为丘墟。子孙式微，不知何往。毛泣曰：“吾以一念愤，不几负吾死友乎？”众益审毛之术神，富家大室，争来邀致，而毛已杳矣。至今陶庄一坏土，尚无居人，四井亦湮没。耕者掘地，常得古砖，上有古钱文凸出，并造砖工人名。细玩之，果墓砖。噫！术人之神，正术人之可畏也。陶君忘父遗命，凭天理亦不克昌，岂待术人之穿凿而后败欤？

    Explanation

    420

    Another senior monk presided, wearing black robes and barefoot, with no fewer than a hundred people present; the hall was adorned with painted beams and carved rafters, and the sounds of morning bells and evening drums filled the air, making it a grand monastery. On this day, many devout believers gathered for a Dharma assembly, and Mr. Mao joined hands and recited the Buddha's name. Suddenly, a pilgrim arrived, saying he came from Longxing, and gazed intently, exclaiming, "Are you Mr. Mao from Taozhuang?" Mr. Mao replied, "Yes." The pilgrim then detailed the events of Taozhuang. Since Mr. Mao left, Taozhuang suffered from theft, fire, and unjust imprisonment, leading to the sudden collapse of the family's wealth. The two gentlemen were stripped of their positions and had already passed away. The village was abandoned and became a wasteland, and the descendants declined, with no one knowing where they went. Mr. Mao wept, saying, "I acted out of a moment of anger, did I not betray my deceased friend?" The crowd increasingly recognized Mr. Mao's magical skills, and wealthy families and large households competed to invite him, but Mr. Mao had already disappeared. Until today, Taozhuang remains a ruined land, still uninhabited, and the four wells have also been buried. Farmers digging the ground often find ancient bricks with protruding ancient coin inscriptions and the names of the brickmakers. Carefully examining them, they are indeed tomb bricks. Ah! The supernatural abilities of magicians are truly awe-inspiring. Mr. Tao forgot his father's last wishes, and even with natural justice, he could not prosper. Did he need the interference of magicians to fail?

    421

    十丈莲
    　　
    　　余乱后售书卖画，时客于古淮阴市上，每聆里巷妇孺啧啧道吴贞女事。顷又幕游嵫水，晤胡君少瑜上舍，详述贞女之生死崖略，辄口凿凿，泪涔涔焉。吴贞女，清河人，其父讳慎裕公，儒士也。贫为当道佣书。母夫人素敦坤范，夜梦口吞莲花，而生贞女。父殁，女方髫龄，终日寡言笑，操刀尺，习女工，幼许字同里徐氏子。念父殁，而夫家亦贫，且构家难，以至女年二十有八，尚未行亲还礼。徐氏子旋以瘵殁，撇堂上白头。母素审贞女贤，秘不以讣。

    Explanation

    421

    Ten-Zhang Lotus

    After the chaos, I sold books and paintings, staying as a guest in the ancient market of Huaiyin. I often heard women and children in the neighborhood discussing the story of Wu Zhen's girl with admiration. Recently, while touring as a guest in the Zisi River area, I met Mr. Hu Shao Yu, a scholar, who detailed the life and death of the virtuous girl with certainty, tears streaming down his face. Wu Zhen's girl was from Qinghe. Her father, surnamed Shen Yu Gong, was a scholar who was poor and worked as a copyist for officials. Her mother, a lady, always adhered to the norms of a virtuous woman and dreamed of swallowing a lotus flower at night, after which she gave birth to the virtuous girl. After the father's death, the girl was still a child, always silent and rarely smiling. She held scissors and practiced needlework, having been betrothed to a man from the same village. Thinking of her father's death and her husband's family's poverty, and facing family difficulties, she had not yet performed the formal marriage ceremony at the age of twenty-eight. The man from the Xue family died soon after due to illness, leaving her elderly parents behind. The mother, knowing the girl's virtue, kept the news of his death secret.

    422

    女晨起，忽哭告母曰：“徐家郎其殒谢已乎？”问语何不祥，曰：“梦有儒衣冠拜于门外者，非耶？”母已审噩耗，不禁失声，泣曰：“诚如儿言，奈何奈何？”女自起，更衣，走哭于徐氏之门。母不忍钳禁，听其去。姑见之，益凄惋惶惑，不知云何。女先拜其姑，始哭其婿，周旋中折，哀毁莫生。行路者过门闻哭声，莫不步为止，而涕为坠也。姑俟其哭竟，忍泪告之曰：“吾无福，负此贤妇，天也命也，夫复何言！然儿之哀衷已申，其即遄回瀛第乎！”女大声曰：“儿无状，未克侍亡者，供含殓，罪戾滋深。

    Explanation

    422

    The woman rose in the morning and suddenly wept, telling her mother, "Has Xu's son already died?" Her mother asked what was wrong, and she replied, "I dreamt of a scholar in official attire bowing outside the door, is that not so?" The mother, having already learned of the bad news, could not help but lose her voice and cried, "Truly as you say, what shall we do?" The woman got up, changed her clothes, and ran to cry at Xu's door. Her mother could not bear to restrain her and let her go. The mother-in-law saw her and felt even more sorrowful and bewildered, not knowing what to do. The woman first bowed to her mother-in-law, then cried for her husband, turning around in the middle of her grief, her sorrow and distress were overwhelming. Passersby who heard the crying stopped to listen, and their tears fell. The mother-in-law waited until she finished crying and, holding back her tears, told her, "I have no good fortune, I have betrayed this virtuous woman, it is the will of heaven and fate, what more can be said! But your sorrow has been expressed, will you return quickly to your home?" The woman cried loudly, "I am disgraceful, I failed to serve the deceased, the guilt and sin are even deeper."

    423

    今既登其堂，奠其主，妾身分明矣，更何归焉？”姑曰：“吾亦贫也，未亡人行将为殍，再添新妇，能吸风为活耶？”曰：“儿不敢以口腹累母，十指尚可谋生活。诚以亡者遽凋落，失温清，抱罪九京下。儿既延残喘，敢不代厥职补幽恨乎？”姑又曰：“儿诚贤矣，其如母夫人者，亦孀孤无依何？”曰：“是惟赖吾姑之慈悲耳。”于是体贞女乌私，使往来朝夕，兼定省焉。女刺绣雕绘，宛转如生。人得其佩囊等物，则珍袭之以为宝。有怜而予以倍值者，女必却之，曰：“吾不幸为女人身，又失所天，是前生因，尚敢取非分财，贻罪戾乎？”人益义之。求手制者，门外踵为接。然由此劳悴，目几失明。

    Explanation

    423

    Now that I have entered her hall and placed her spirit tablet, my identity as a widow is clear, and where should I go next?" The grandmother said, "I am also poor; a widow like me is on the verge of becoming a skeleton. Adding another wife would make it impossible for us to survive by just breathing air." The narrator replied, "I dare not burden my mother with the needs of my mouth and belly. My ten fingers can still earn a living. Truly, my husband died suddenly, and I have lost the duty of providing warmth in winter and coolness in summer, which is a sin in the underworld. Since I am still alive, how could I not take over his duties and make up for the hidden sorrows?" The grandmother said again, "You are indeed virtuous, but what about my mother-in-law, who is also a widow and without support?" The narrator answered, "This depends solely on my grandmother's compassion." Thus, the chaste woman was treated with respect and fairness, and she came and went every day, fulfilling her duties. The woman was skilled in embroidery and painting, and her work was lifelike. People treasured her embroidered pouches and other items as precious possessions. Some, moved by her plight, offered her double the price, but she always refused, saying, "Unfortunate as I am to be born a woman and to have lost my husband, this is due to my past life's karma. How could I dare to take wealth that is not rightfully mine and bring about sin or punishment?" People admired her even more. Those who sought her handmade items lined up outside her door. However, due to the hard work, her eyes were nearly blind.

    424

    一夜梦天女，冉冉降中庭，以丹授之曰：“吾天孙也，怜子贞孝且有疾，携奉神丹，子其吞之。”女视丹，圆如珠，灿如火，再拜裣衽而后服。神女去，女自觉心遽安而疴若失。翌再买丝挑线，功倍往时。逾数年，姑又病，女衣不解带者数月。殆弥留时，笑谓女曰：“吾不幸有殇子，何幸而有贞妇耶？吾年来得汝调养，胜于若在时也。今已矣，行将告尔舅尔夫于地下矣。吾儿幸依阿母活，毋以我为念。”言已，目遽瞑。女毁瘠号痛，泪坠成冰。其母恐女殉，预往防闲之。女泣邀四邻坐堂上，奉母坐屋隅，稽首曰：“吾夫死，吾姑又死矣。族无接续之绪，家无期功之亲，留此数椽，下及什物，何所用乎？拟乞诸长者，代为全售，为吾姑殓葬费用。

    Explanation

    424

    One night, she dreamed of a celestial maiden who slowly descended into the courtyard and gave her a pill, saying, "I am a granddaughter of heaven. I pity you for your loyalty and filial piety, and for your illness. I have brought a divine pill. Swallow it." The woman looked at the pill, which was round like a pearl and shining like fire. She bowed twice, lowered her skirt, and then swallowed it. The celestial maiden left, and the woman suddenly felt at ease, as if her illness had vanished. The next day, she resumed her work, buying silk and picking up her sewing, and her productivity doubled compared to before. Several years later, her mother-in-law fell ill again, and the woman stayed by her side for several months without removing her belt. When her mother-in-law was near death, she smiled and said to the woman, "Unfortunate that I have a deceased son, but how blessed to have a loyal wife! These years, with your care, I feel better than I would have if he were alive. Now it is over. I will soon inform your father-in-law and husband in the afterlife. My child, hopefully live with my mother-in-law, do not worry about me." After speaking, her eyes closed suddenly. The woman mourned deeply, lost weight, and her tears fell like ice.
