    因二尹之妹与首郡太尊之子结亲，送至会垣完姻，置办赠嫁物耳。局主分外趋承，设席宴之，官并邀金铺主同坐，曰：“是我好友。”铺主唯唯听命，方自以为荣。局主乃出绉缎洋呢各物，先奉封翁阅之，封翁皆摇首。局主曰：“此皆上等货也，可以入贡，岂不堪服用耶？”官曰：“既不合父意，可与我妹观之。”饬舆夫扛抬货物，一仆押去。良久未回，又饬一仆往催。舆夫先回曰：“舟中人嘱我禀官，曰绸缎经姑娘目，俱合意，不知应用何号平色银两，请官自去检点。”官谓局主曰：“烦侍父暂坐，我去兑银即回。”乃乘舆去。

    Explanation

    473

    A marriage was arranged between the daughter of a deputy magistrate (二尹) and the son of the chief magistrate of the prefecture (首郡太尊). The bride was sent to the groom's residence to finalize the marriage, and gifts for the bride were prepared. The host (局主) went above and beyond in his hospitality, setting up a banquet for the occasion. The official invited the owner of a gold shop (金铺主) to sit with them, saying, "He is a good friend." The gold shop owner nodded in agreement and felt honored by the invitation. The host then presented various items, including silk and wool fabrics, first showing them to the bride's father (封翁), who shook his head in disapproval. The host argued, "These are all high-quality goods, suitable for offering as tribute—how could they not be fit for use?" The official replied, "Since the father does not approve, we can show them to my sister-in-law instead." The host ordered servants to carry the goods, and one servant was sent to accompany them. After a long time, another servant was sent to check on the progress. The servant returned first, saying, "The person in the boat instructed me to inform you that the bride has approved the silk and wool fabrics, but they do not know what type of silver currency should be used for payment, so they request that you come and check." The official told the host, "Please entertain the father for a moment while I go to exchange the money and return quickly." He then left in a carriage.

    474

    至舟，多给舆夫钱文，曰：“尔等往来劳苦，先吃饭去。”舆夫走而舟开行矣。丐坐局中，俟至更深不来，局主与金铺主皆惶急，不得不追问封翁。丐亦情虚，语言闪烁。群拥之鸣官，大令究其实情，亦无可如何，不过缉而已。释丐出，众褫其衣服，唯靴帽不合时宜，众皆不服。此丐尚戴五品冠，着朝靴，赤体叫化，见者大笑。蒋中丞抚浙时，察访官民之不法者，重惩之。温郡某太守，簋不饬，闻风而惧。突有外来三人操北音者，寓府侧，不言所事。凡太守升堂，必往观之，暇则与馆人辩论太守之是非曲直。馆人怪之，密报府县。

    Explanation

    474

    When they reached the boat, the beggar gave the sedan bearers more money, saying, "You all have been working hard, go eat first." The sedan bearers left, and the boat started moving. The beggar sat in the bureau, waiting until deep night without coming. The manager of the bureau and the owner of the gold shop were both anxious, having no choice but to question the old man. The beggar was also dishonest, speaking evasively. They all gathered to report to the authorities. The magistrate investigated the matter thoroughly, but could do nothing, only ordering the beggar to be detained. After releasing the beggar, the crowd stripped him of his clothes, leaving only his boots and hat, which were out of fashion, and everyone was dissatisfied. This beggar still wore a fifth-rank cap and official boots, begging naked, and those who saw him laughed. When Jiang Zhongcheng was governor of Zhejiang, he investigated and severely punished illegal officials and civilians. A certain magistrate of Wen Prefecture, whose behavior was improper, was frightened by the news. Suddenly, three foreigners with northern accents arrived, staying near the government office, and did not speak of their business. Whenever the magistrate ascended the hall, they would go to observe, and in their spare time, they would debate the magistrate's right and wrong with the officials. The officials found this strange and secretly reported it to the prefecture and county.

    475

    太守俟三人出，遽搜其行李，得中丞访牌一道，凡太守私事俱在内，宛然紫印。又首县致永嘉令一函，尚末缄封，有云：“蒋厅尊奉大宪命，探事贵治，诸祈照察”云云。太守益惧，密与永嘉令商酌，拟重赂之。三人归，见箱箧俱乱，唤馆人诘之，馆人曰：“阁下去后，太尊来拜，必欲面晤，在房中坐候半日方去。动阁下之行李者，其太尊乎？”三人默然，既而曰：“机事泄矣，盍去诸？”遂买舟行。馆人飞报太守，转令永嘉令往拜之。

    Explanation

    475

    The governor waited for the three men to leave, then hurriedly searched their belongings. He found an official document from the Censor-in-Chief, which contained all the governor's private matters, sealed with a purple seal. He also discovered a letter from the county magistrate to the prefect of Yongjia, which was still unsealed and mentioned that "Mr. Jiang, the deputy, was sent by the high official to investigate matters in your jurisdiction, please investigate and examine." The governor was greatly alarmed. He secretly consulted with the prefect of Yongjia and planned to offer a substantial bribe. The three men returned and found their belongings in disarray. They called the innkeeper to inquire about it, and the innkeeper said, "After you left, the governor came to pay his respects, insisting on meeting face-to-face, and sat in your room for half a day before leaving. Could it be that the governor himself disturbed your belongings?" The three men were silent for a moment, then said, "Our secret has been exposed. Why not leave?" They immediately bought a boat and set off. The innkeeper rushed to report to the governor, who then dispatched the prefect of Yongjia to greet them.

    476

    至舟中仅有二人，令问蒋司马何在，二人曰：“我主驰回省垣去矣，留函奉呈。”令收阅。一系首县原函，一系司马自致，云：“公事匆促，不及谋面，深致抱歉之意。”令白太守，修书馈柑桔四桶，中藏白物，因其仆追赠之。未几，太守至省，晤蒋司马，讯无赴温事，知前物已入骗子手，而不敢言。有乘舟携仆访亲者，舟抵岸，谓其仆曰：“我先赴亲戚家，汝将行李收拾，迁至头舱，我着人协同起去。”仆如命，坐于舟首俟之。有一人来，手携竹篮，内存大米数升，至河浜淘洗者，笑谓其仆曰：“此处泊舟，须要小心，昨日我目睹一舟，亦如是停泊，一贼如是登舟。

    Explanation

    476

    When the boat reached the shore, there were only two people on board. The prefect asked about Jiang Sima's whereabouts. The two people replied, "Our master rushed back to the provincial capital, leaving a letter for presentation." The prefect was ordered to read the letter. One was the original letter from the county magistrate, and the other was from Jiang Sima himself, who wrote, "Due to urgent official business, we were unable to meet, and we deeply apologize." The prefect was then ordered to inform the prefect, who wrote a letter and sent four buckets of tangerines, which contained something white inside. The servant was given a gift in pursuit of the matter. Soon after, the prefect arrived at the provincial capital and met with Jiang Sima. Upon inquiry, there was no mention of going to Wen. It was known that the previous item had been taken by a thief, but it was not mentioned. There was a person traveling by boat with a servant to visit relatives. When the boat reached the shore, the person said to his servant, "I will first go to my relatives' house. Pack the luggage and move it to the front cabin. I will have someone help move it." The servant followed orders and sat at the front of the boat waiting. A person came, carrying a bamboo basket containing several measures of rice, and went to the riverbank to wash it. He jokingly said to his servant, "This boat should be docked carefully here. Yesterday, I saw another boat docked similarly, and a thief boarded it in the same way."

    477

    ”其人即跃上，曰：“舟中亦有如是一卷行李，贼如是窃取。”其人即背负之，曰：“如是登岸竟去。”仆方笑曰：“此人何其呆也，自弃其米，为人负重妆点，何为耶？”舟子曰：“此人一去不顾，恐是骗子。”仆悟急追，不知所之矣。有儒生赴岁试者，舟抵岸，行李迁起，尚未议定脚价，姑坐行李上守之。忽有衣冠而来者，对之长揖，曰：“兄台何自来耶？”生忙回揖，细认，彼此皆不识。来人曰：“误矣。”揖谢不安而别。生退坐，仰后倒地，方知行李已为人抽去。芗曰：龙有嗜，可豢之；物先腐，虫生之。

    Explanation

    477

    The man immediately jumped onto the boat and said, "There is also such a roll of baggage on the boat; the thief took it in this way." The man then carried it on his back and said, "In this way, he went ashore and left." The servant laughed and said, "How foolish this person is, to abandon his rice and be burdened by someone else and decorated, why is this?" The boatman said, "This person left without looking back, probably a fraud." The servant realized and chased urgently, but no longer knew where he went.

    There was a scholar going to take the annual examination. The boat reached the shore, and the baggage was moved. The price for transportation had not been agreed upon, so he temporarily sat on the baggage to guard it. Suddenly, a well-dressed person came and performed a formal bow, saying, "How did you come here, sir?" The scholar hurriedly returned the bow, looked carefully, but neither recognized the other. The person said, "Mistaken," and bowed and left with discomfort. The scholar sat down, fell backward, and only then realized that the baggage had been taken away.

    Xiang said: "A dragon has a taste, it can be tamed; if an object is already rotten, worms will grow."

    478

    诸公皆有隙可乘，故入其玄中。然东坡云：“回视人间世，了无一事真。”诚哉是言也。无论中百货杂伪，试问医者充市，畴为张长沙刘河间耶？星者盈街，畴为李虚中袁天罡耶？当今入幕之宾，名为习申韩者，不知申韩是一是二，是何代人，何曾读不害非子之书耶？或者谓九流杂技，固不足道；甚至科甲遍宇内，何者为班马屈宋之词华，何者为周程张朱之理学，叩之茫然者居多，不过以八股时文，骗功名耳。嗟乎！纷纷一大世界，人骗己，己骗人，有时己亦骗己，何怪乎骗子手耶？方幼樗曰：一片婆心，唤醒世间多少自欺欺人之辈，笔亦古秀而健。

    Explanation

    478

    All prominent figures have weaknesses that can be exploited, hence one can gain insight into their inner thoughts. Yet Su Shi remarked, "Looking back at the human world, there is nothing truly real." Indeed, this is a profound statement. Consider the marketplace, where a hundred goods are mixed with falsehoods; ask who among the doctors filling the market are Zhang Changsha and Liu Hejian? Among the astrologers who crowd the streets, who are Li Xuzhong and Yuan Tiangang? Today's courtiers, who claim to study Shang Yang and Han Fei, do not know whether Shang and Han are one or two, of what era they are, or whether they have ever read the books of not harming or not being wrong. Some argue that the nine schools of mixed skills are not worth mentioning; even the imperial examinations, which spread throughout the world, who can distinguish the eloquence of Ban Gu, Sima Qian, Qu Yuan, and Song Yu, and who can discern the rationalist philosophy of Zhou Dunyi, Cheng Hao, Zhang Zai, and Zhu Xi? Most are at a loss when questioned, and they merely use the standardized essays of the time to cheat for fame and rank. Alas! This vast and chaotic world is filled with people deceiving others, others deceiving people, and sometimes one even deceives oneself. How can one be surprised by the prevalence of cheaters? I, Fang Youchu, say: with a heart full of compassion, I have awakened the many who deceive themselves and others, and my writing is also ancient, elegant, and robust.

    479

    某广文
    　　
    　　某学广文，耄而贪，诸生皆恶之。适有少年科甲之学使来，最恶白髯，见之则曰：“汝已老大，好让后生矣”必罢之。故斑白者皆闻声而惧。皆广文须发浩然，遍求乌药，又不肯解囊，勒派诸生代觅。有生谓之曰：“门生之戚，宦于东粤，有好乌药，名透骨丹，初染色红，三复之，则黑如明漆，泽润有光，真无价之宝也。门生感师恩，仅分得少许，敬以奉赠。”广文大悦，谢而受之，如法试验，一染而红，再染而绛，三染而紫赤色；愈洗则愈鲜明，俨如道院中所塑之祝融像，见者大笑。寻其门人，不知所往。竟不敢赴试。致仕归去，生始告人，其药以龟溺熬紫草为之，即染鬃缨之法，岂能改色乎？此广文者，俾终生为红胡子矣。

    Explanation

    479

    A Certain Scholar of the Guangwen School

    A certain scholar of the Guangwen school was old and greedy, and all the students hated him. Just then, a young scholar who had passed the imperial examinations arrived, and he most hated white beards. When he saw the old scholar, he said, "You are already old, it's time to give way to the younger generation," and he must dismiss him. Therefore, all the gray-haired ones heard and were afraid. All the Guangwen scholars had abundant beards and hair, and they searched everywhere for black medicine, but he was unwilling to open his purse and forced the students to find it on his behalf. A student told him, "A relative of my student, who serves in the eastern part of Guangdong, has good black medicine, called Tougudan. At first it turns red, after three repetitions, it becomes black like polished lacquer, lustrous and shiny, truly a priceless treasure. My student is grateful for the teacher's kindness and only obtained a small amount, respectfully offers it as a gift." The Guangwen scholar was very pleased, thanked him and accepted it, tried it according to the method. The first dye turned red, the second turned dark red, the third turned purple and red; the more it was washed, the more vivid it became, looking like the statue of Zhurong in a Taoist temple, those who saw it laughed. Searching for his students, he did not know where they went. He finally dared not take the exam. He retired and returned home, and the student then told others, the medicine was made by boiling purple grass with turtle urine, it was the method of dyeing horsehair, how could it change color? This Guangwen scholar will be a red-bearded man for life.

    480

    三官救劫
    　　
    　　吾邑陈氏，有仆，年逾六旬，职司田庄。夏日随主收租回，至城外，仆渴欲饮水，就岸滩伏河面吸之。其主在后，水中照见仆身影，背插长旗，朱书“饬雷霆诛击恶犯一名某”，即其仆姓名。其主骇甚，问仆有所见否，仆亦睹之，起跪主前曰：“老奴自幼无欺心事，近亦持经修善，更无为非，其为前世事耶？天命不可逃也，请主速回，将奴所司之簿籍，检点收藏。二子不可信托，命其以柩来收殓奴尸，奴死此树下矣，不敢入城惊众也。”其主恋恋不忍，然疾风暴云旋起，不得不奔。及家，雨至，闻轰击之声甚厉，立唤仆之妻子，告以故，皆号泣，欲往护之，其主不允。未几晴霁，老仆欣然归，妻子见之，方讶其主之言谬妄。

    480

    Three Officials Save from Calamity

    In my hometown, there was a family surnamed Chen who had a servant over sixty years old, responsible for managing the estate. In the summer, the servant followed the master to collect rent and returned to the outskirts of the city. The servant, thirsty, wanted to drink water and went to the riverbank, bending over the water surface to drink. The master, who was behind, saw the servant's shadow in the water, with a long flag on his back, red writing saying, "Ordering thunder and lightning to punish a single evil offender named...," which was the servant's own name. The master was very frightened and asked the servant if he had seen anything. The servant also saw it and got up, knelt before the master, and said, "An old servant who has never done anything deceitful since childhood, recently has also held scriptures and practiced goodness, and has not done anything wrong. Is this a matter of past lives? Heaven's decree cannot be avoided. Please the master return quickly, to check and store the records and ledgers that I am in charge of. The two sons cannot be trusted. Order them to come with a coffin to collect my body. I will die under this tree, and dare not enter the city and alarm the people." The master was reluctant and unwilling, but a sudden storm of wind and clouds arose, and he could not help but run. When he reached home, it started raining, and he heard a very loud sound of thunder. He immediately called the servant's wife and children and told them the reason. They all cried and wanted to go to protect him, but the master did not allow it. Soon after, the sky cleared up, and the old servant happily returned. The wife and children saw him and were surprised that the master's words were mistaken.

    481

    仆曰：“主去后，雨即来，我跪树下，瞑目受死。心无杂念，惟口诵素习之《三官经》。闻雷声下击，及树而回。我张目视之，见一大道士，可与树倍，身坐树巅，以袍幅垂护我。正探首仰观间，谅必雷神见我，霹雳骤下，道士挥之以肱，雷始收声，道士亦不知所往。我幸免于难，将告主人，虔诚祀之，以蒙其庥耳。”故吾邑崇奉三官菩萨者，至今甚盛。或曰：扶灾危而救苦难，神之功德大矣。芗曰：然非也，亦视其人何如耳。以人道论，天君犹皇帝，三官犹宪司，雷神犹缇骑耳。帝使戮人，宪司历陈其人生平之善，德可掩眚，帝必赦之。使其人凶恶，宪司执奏，以为能奉事臣躬，请曲宥之，则谴谪立加，何能回天听乎？然则福善祸淫，彼苍之定理也，谄渎何为哉！

    Explanation

    481

    The speaker said: "After the master left, the rain came immediately. I knelt under the tree, closed my eyes to accept death. My heart had no other thoughts, only reciting the San Guan Jing that I had long been familiar with. I heard the sound of thunder striking, and it hit the tree before returning. I opened my eyes to see, and I saw a great Taoist, twice the size of the tree, sitting at the top of the tree, his robe hanging down to protect me. Just as I was looking up in wonder, I guessed the Thunder God saw me, and lightning struck suddenly. The Taoist waved his arm at it, and the lightning ceased. The Taoist also disappeared. I luckily escaped the disaster and planned to inform the master, worship him with sincerity, and receive his protection." Thus, in our county, the worship of the Three Officials remains very popular to this day. Someone said, "To help those in disaster and save those in suffering, the virtue of the god is great indeed." The speaker replied, "But no, it also depends on the person." From the perspective of human conduct, the Heavenly Lord is like an emperor, the Three Officials are like the Censorate, and the Thunder God is like a palace guard. If the emperor orders someone to be killed, the Censorate lists the person's good deeds, and virtue can cover up faults, so the emperor must pardon them. If the person is evil, the Censorate reports, saying they can serve the emperor, and requests leniency, then immediate punishment is added, and how can one change the will of Heaven? Therefore, rewarding virtue and punishing immorality is the fixed law of Heaven. Why flatter and defile?"

    482

    雷神
    　　
    　　《笔谈》载宋治平中，雷震天王寺柱，倒书十一字。《耳谈》载姑苏韩姓，堂中暴雷，绕击砌石，上绘梅花一枝，纹理精研。是雷神善书画，见于记载久矣。吾邻查氏宅，暑雨中，暴雷绕垣奋击，后视垣面一砖，去粉琢磨，朱书令字，径四寸余，秀健如赵文敏笔法。查氏抽换其砖，以治邪谑，砖至即愈。吾邑有安国寺，夏月雷绕大殿，其左边柱作旋螺纹。伽蓝神后墙上，绘一鸟迹，四趾带爪，阔大盈尺，作鹰拿状，入砖三分。僧以石灰补之，数日复现。

    Explanation

    482

    Thunder Deity

    Dream Pool Essays records that during the Zhiping era of the Song dynasty, a thunderstorm struck a column of the Tianwang Temple, leaving eleven characters written upside down. Tales Heard records that in Suzhou, a sudden thunderstorm struck the paving stones in a hall, leaving a single branch of plum blossoms painted on them. It is evident that the Thunder Deity is skilled in calligraphy and painting, as documented for a long time. In my neighborhood, there is a house belonging to the Zha family. During a summer rain, a sudden thunderstorm struck the walls, leaving a red character "令" written on a brick, with a diameter of over four inches, in a style similar to Zhao Mengfu's calligraphy. The Zha family replaced the brick to treat a supernatural force, and the condition improved. In my county, there is an An Guo Temple. During the summer, a thunderstorm struck the left column of the main hall, leaving a spiral pattern. On the wall behind the guardian deity, there was a footprint of a bird, with four toes and claws, about a foot wide, resembling an eagle's talons, and penetrating the brick by three parts. The monks filled it in with lime, but it reappeared after several days.

    483

    吾戚金氏楼居，炎暑之际，以幼孩卧床上，已酣，其父母皆下楼去。大雨如注，霹雳一声，穿楼而过，在室之人，莫不惊悸丧神。其父母忆及幼孩在床，谅必惊毙矣。登楼视之，前后窗俱辟，帐亦高卷，不见幼孩。觅之，以席卷横置于床下，取出观之，儿睡犹未醒也。席面朱书一字，非篆非符，无识之者。又郑孝廉，暑月赴乡，遇雨，避于房檐下，雷声甚厉。忽见云中坠一火球，后有数十神将，体为云护，惟足着尖靴，相随电光疾下，大震一声，半里外田间击死一人，背有古篆，不知所云。然则雷书恒有之，不足异。所可异者，自古有方履，无尖靴，神亦从时，其为今之神欤？

    Explanation

    483

    My relative, the Gold family, lives in a tower. During the hot summer, a young child was soundly sleeping on the bed. The parents left the tower. A heavy rainstorm with thunder struck, and a bolt of lightning passed through the tower. Everyone in the room was terrified and lost their composure. The parents thought that the child must have been killed by the lightning. They went back upstairs to check, finding all windows open and the curtains high, but no sign of the child. They searched and found the child under the bed, wrapped in a mat, still asleep. There was a red character written on the mat, neither seal script nor magic symbol, and no one could recognize it.

    Another story involves a scholar named Zheng, who encountered rain during the summer and sought shelter under a roof. The thunder was very loud. Suddenly, a fireball fell from the clouds, followed by dozens of divine generals, their bodies shrouded in clouds, but their feet wearing pointed boots, descending rapidly with lightning. A loud explosion killed someone in a field half a mile away, with ancient characters on their back, whose meaning was unknown.

    It is said that lightning inscriptions are common and not unusual. What is unusual is that ancient times had flat shoes, not pointed boots. Perhaps these are gods of the present era?

    484

    汤文正
    　　
    　　苏郡社会，甲于全省。暮春之际，举国若狂。其会首绅耆，咸集神庙，公议敛资，置办彩衣，务极鲜艳；搬演古事，则翻新出奇，争奢斗富。即如寿星之袍，以珍珠满缀寿字；百蛮进宝，以径尺翡翠盘，盛金叶火齐珠；钟进士送妹，以二尺余之白玉瓶，内插珊瑚，枝上站云拥美人，随小鬼执绣盖，此之谓抬阁。一座之价，使人不能估测，不仅以金玉镯结栏干而已。如是者数十座。又有所谓肩阁、兜干、彩龙、马道等类，不计其数。以是故，远近来观者千万人。凡临街之楼，招集妇女，皆赁栏俯瞩，粉白黛绿，迷眩一时。故少壮之徒，争睹目力，百十成群，随会来往，评定美人。今年以某处某人为状元，必复之四五日，众议佥同，则探其父母夫家，皆有垂涎之意。

    Explanation

    484

    Translation:

    Duke Tang Wenzheng

    The society of Suzhou Province is the best in the entire province. At the beginning of summer, the whole country goes wild. The leaders of the community, the elderly and respected, all gather at the temple, publicly discuss and collect funds, prepare brightly colored costumes, striving for the utmost brilliance; when performing ancient stories, they innovate and create something novel, competing in luxury and wealth. For example, the robe of the God of Longevity is adorned with pearls to form the character for longevity; the offering of treasures by the hundred barbarians is presented on a jade plate a foot in diameter, holding golden leaves and fire-red pearls; the story of the scholar-official sending his sister is depicted in a white jade vase over two feet tall, containing coral branches with clouds surrounding a beautiful woman, accompanied by little demons holding embroidered parasols. This is called a tai ge. The cost of one such float is beyond estimation, not just adorned with gold and jade bracelets on the railings. There are dozens of such floats. There are also various types of floats and decorations such as jian ge, dou gan, cai long, and ma dao, too numerous to count. Therefore, thousands of people come from far and near to watch. All the buildings facing the street hire women to sit by the railing and watch, the white and green makeup of the women dazzles the eyes. Young and strong men compete to see with their own eyes, forming groups of tens or hundreds, following the parade, evaluating and judging the beauty. This year, someone from a certain place is declared the champion of beauty, this decision is repeated for several days, and the public opinion is unanimous. People investigate the backgrounds of the parents and spouses, all with covetous intentions.

    485

    时有赵五官者，年十七八，已订婚孙姓，在钱局作伙。是日随众纵观，知众所定之元，系及笄女，访之即孙姓，赵五官之未婚妻也。五官意乱神迷，唯欲速娶为快。第岁俸十余千钱，不足奉母，何以为婚。与母谋之，母告以父在日，有钱会，应得一百余千，或可敷用。但须摇点，未能操必得之权。五官忆及大五圣堂，其神甚灵，有求必应。至摇会之日，以香烛往告，云：“如得会，完娶之后，夫妻偕来叩谢。”祝毕赴会，举骰摇之，得三十六点，如愿而归。遣媒定期，纳采迎娶。花烛之下，妇果艳丽倾城，五官不禁狂喜。惟新妇似憎夫貌丑，然亦无词。三朝后，具牲醴同酬五圣而回，妇已卸妆矣。至晚，忽又盛饰端坐，五官入房，妇正色拒之，曰：“我非汝家妇矣。

    Explanation

    485

    At that time, there was a man named Zhao Wu Guan, who was seventeen or eighteen years old and had already been engaged to a woman from the Sun family. He worked in a government mint. On that day, he followed the crowd to watch, and he discovered that the person chosen by the crowd was a woman of marriageable age, who turned out to be his fiancée. Zhao Wu Guan was distracted and confused, but he only wanted to marry her quickly. However, his annual salary of more than ten thousand coins was not enough to support his mother, so how could he afford to get married? He discussed this with his mother, who told him that when his father was alive, there was a financial meeting where he should have received more than a hundred thousand coins, which might have been sufficient. But he had to participate in a lottery, and he could not guarantee a win. Zhao Wu Guan recalled the Great Temple of the Five Saints, whose gods were very powerful and granted all requests. On the day of the lottery, he went to the temple with incense and candles and said, "If I win, after completing the marriage, we will both come to thank you." After praying, he attended the lottery, threw the dice, and got thirty-six points, returning home as wished. He sent a matchmaker to set a date, presented gifts, and married her. On their wedding night, his wife was indeed beautiful and captivating, and Zhao Wu Guan couldn't help but be ecstatic. However, his new wife seemed to dislike his appearance, though she had no words to express it. Three days later, they prepared sacrifices and offered them together to the five saints before returning home. By then, his wife had already taken off her makeup. That night, she suddenly dressed up and sat properly. When Zhao Wu Guan entered the room, she sternly refused him, saying, "I am not your wife anymore."

    486

    五圣将迎我为夫人，法驾将临，汝其速退，勿干神怒也。”五官疑其有疯疾，即出延医，医至而妇已僵，异香满室，遥闻音乐之声。五官大恸。殡殓后，以神夺民妇，讼于邑宰，官斥其诬妄，不准。奔告于府，太守视其人，神色悲忿，姑纳其词。五官赴虎邱问卜。有某瞎子，名重一时，为之卦成，骇曰：“异哉！汝欲与神讼耶？讼果胜，今夜子时听审，勿远离也。”五官欣然归寓。是时，汤文正公抚江南，正直之声，震于遐迩，神亦畏之。故暗随五官来，知太守已收呈状，夤夜入藩库，缘库内有三金宝，系守藏之物，五圣盗之，穿库楼而出，神光烛天，巡守者皆误为火起。鸣钲高叫，方伯亲临，役吏俱集。开库检点，唯金定三不知所之矣。

    Explanation

    486

    Five deities are about to take me as their wife, with a ceremonial carriage approaching. You must leave quickly to avoid angering the gods. The officials suspected she was possessed by a spirit and sought medical help. By the time the doctor arrived, the woman had become stiff, and a strange fragrance filled the room, with distant music being heard. The officials were deeply grieved. After her burial, they accused the deity of taking a civilian woman and sued the county magistrate, who dismissed their claims as false. They then appealed to the governor, who saw the officials with a sorrowful and angry expression and tentatively accepted their complaint. The officials went to a place of divination, where a famous blind fortune-teller, known in his time, performed a divination. He was shocked and said, "Strange! Do you wish to sue the gods? If you win the lawsuit, the trial will be held tonight at midnight. Do not leave." The officials happily returned to their lodgings. At that time, a prominent official, known for his integrity and wisdom, was governing the region south of the Yangtze River. His reputation for fairness spread far and wide, and even the gods feared him. Therefore, the deities secretly followed the officials and learned that the governor had accepted their complaint. They broke into the provincial treasury at midnight, where there were three valuable treasures, stored for safekeeping. The five deities stole the treasures, passing through the treasury building, and their divine light illuminated the sky. The guards mistook the light for a fire and sounded the gong, calling for attention. The provincial governor arrived personally, and all the officials gathered. They opened the treasury and conducted an inventory, but the three valuable treasures were missing.

    487

    五圣以宝入抚院内宅，示梦于夫人，曰：“予为吴江之五圣神，被顽民妄控于府，求为庇佑，愿以金宝为。”乃掷之于妆台。夫人闻震声而觉，则金宝三枚俨在。爰命婢女，请文正入，语以是事。言未毕，而门外传点，请公升坐。文正出，则两司百官咸聚，方伯以失宝事告。文正笑曰：“盗易传，勿张惶也。”问首府曰：“昨有人控神夺民妇者乎？以其词来。”太守曰：“有之。”饬从者取到。立传赵五官，文正面鞫之，得实。归宝于库，具狱，牒正一真人府，请殛之，真人复文，曰：“神虽不正，妇亦淫邪，憎其夫而悦神貌，致启奸图，孽由自作。然阴阳道隔，虽和同强，申革圣神之号，遣发幽都，长为饿鬼，以正其罪，可也。”文正命地方官，扑五圣像，居民争毁之，今改为总官堂矣。

    Explanation

    487

    The Five Saints brought the treasure into the governor's inner residence and showed a dream to the lady, saying, "I am the Five Saints of Wujiang. I was falsely accused by the ignorant people at the prefecture and sought your protection, willing to give gold and treasure as a gift." Then they threw it onto the dressing table. The lady heard the loud sound and woke up, finding three pieces of gold and treasure still there. She then called a maid and asked Mr. Wenzheng to come in, telling him about this matter. Before he finished speaking, the drum outside was beaten, asking the public to ascend to their seats. Mr. Wenzheng came out, and the two provincial officials and hundreds of officials gathered. The governor informed him of the loss of the treasure. Mr. Wenzheng smiled and said, "The thief is easy to catch, do not be alarmed." He asked the chief official, "Did someone yesterday accuse the gods of taking a civilian woman? Bring the accusation here." The prefect said, "Yes." He ordered the attendants to bring it. They immediately summoned Zhao the Five-Official. Mr. Wenzheng personally interrogated him and found the truth. The treasure was returned to the treasury, and the case was prepared, sent to the True One's office, and requested punishment. The True One replied, "The god may be unjust, but the woman is also lewd and wicked. Hating her husband and admiring the god's appearance, she incited evil plans. The disaster was self-inflicted. However, the boundary between the celestial and earthly realms is still there, so even if they were united, it would be forced. Revoked the title of the saintly god, sent him to the underworld, and made him a hungry ghost to punish his crime." Mr. Wenzheng ordered the local officials to destroy the Five Saints' statues. The residents competed to destroy them, and now it has been changed into a General's Hall.

    488

    卜者梁翁
    　　
    　　海昌张端林，父为云南尉，卒于任。端林迎父榇归，道由湖广，米价甚廉，以宦囊所有，籴八百石，舟运入豫江。值大风，望船多处收泊。至一村落，面面皆河，客舟环泊中，有一大家，高其闳，厚其墙垣，门前停舟更密。端林登岸散步，偶入酒肆，沽饮独酌，闻人议论，卜者梁翁，知人过去未来事，言休咎，其应如响。端林就客问之，始知即大宅内之人，遇异人传大六壬。著名问卜，无须开口，即知所事。因此起家巨万。

    Explanation

    488

    The Fortune-Teller Liang Weng

    Zhang Duanlin of Haichang, whose father was the magistrate of Yunnan, died in office. Zhang Duanlin returned his father's coffin home, traveling through Huguang where rice prices were very low. With all the wealth he had accumulated as an official, he bought eight hundred shi of rice and transported it by boat to Yujiang. Encountering a strong wind, he looked for a place with many boats to anchor. He arrived at a village surrounded by rivers, with boats of travelers anchored around. There was a large house there, with a high and grand main gate and thick walls, and even more boats parked in front of the door. Zhang Duanlin went ashore and took a walk, accidentally entering a tavern where he bought and drank alone. He heard people talking about a fortune-teller named Liang Weng, who could predict past and future events, foretell good or bad omens, and whose predictions were as accurate as an echo. Zhang Duanlin approached the guests and asked about him, and learned that Liang Weng was the person inside the grand house, who had met a mysterious person and learned the Great Sixty-Nine. Liang Weng became famous for divination, needing no words to know the matter at hand. As a result, Zhang Duanlin became extremely wealthy.

    489

    近以年老，每日只卖十二课，须黎明至其家，与挂号者清钱百文，课金一两，得到簿内，则得占；迟则挂号不及，即不得与人争趋之。有不远千里而来候教者，故门前舟常满也。端林亦起意问卜。次日，赍银钱入其家。门房内设柜，掌柜者系其亲戚，收仪登号。及端林去，十二数已满，强之增添，则曰：“非翁自主，不敢有加也。”乃持簿邀十二客入内，端林随入观之。登堂入室，室中陈设精雅。有老翁年近八旬，带四品冠，据案上坐。前列牙筹一个，两旁设四小几，各具笔砚；其徒四人，伺应书单，前后坐椅环列。客入，翁起让坐。客各就位，掌柜人开簿唱号，曰：“第一号某客，请抽筹。”客抽送翁前，翁观筹掐指，谓其徒曰：“某客得某时，以某事问课，主何凶吉。

    Explanation

    489

    In recent years, due to his advanced age, he limits himself to selling only twelve readings each day. He must arrive at his home at dawn, where he meets with those who have registered, paying them a hundred coins and a one-tael fee for the reading. If their names are recorded in the register, they can receive a reading; if they arrive late, they cannot register, and thus cannot compete for a spot. There are those who come from far away to seek a reading, so the place is always busy. Duan Lin also decided to consult a fortune-teller. The next day, he brought money to the fortune-teller's house. Inside the gatehouse, there is a cabinet, and the manager is a relative, who collects the fees and registers the clients. By the time Duan Lin left, the number of clients for the day had already reached the limit, and attempts were made to add more. The manager replied, "Without the master's permission, we dare not increase the number of clients." He then held the register and invited the twelve clients inside, with Duan Lin following to observe. They entered the main hall, and the room was decorated with refined elegance. There was an elderly man near eighty years old, wearing a fourth-rank cap, sitting on the table. In front was a toothpick, and on either side were four small tables, each equipped with writing tools. The old man had four apprentices, who attended to the writing of the results, sitting around the table. When the guests entered, the old man rose to invite them to sit. The guests took their seats, and the manager opened the register and called out the numbers, saying, "First number, Guest A, please draw the toothpick." The guest drew the toothpick and handed it to the old man, who examined it and said, "Guest A will receive a reading for a certain time, asking about a certain matter, and the result will be favorable or unfavorable."

    490

    ”徒举笔照录，所断皆合来意，无一爽者。十二课次第毕，客亦陆续退出。端林目注神凝，忘其进退。翁忽谓曰：“远客不及入号，老朽合送一课，以尽地主之谊。足下姓张，从滇南来耶？”端林曰：“然，何以知之？”翁曰：“足下坐下离宫，正时属午，度值张宿。我固知之。今日乙卯，三传申酉戌为日之财官，值贵神，太常、玄武、白虎、凶神同官爻，为有官之尊属，舟中合有父棺。戌为地狱，生前曾为司狱之官。太常为米麦，附酉金而兼连茹，当带有稻米。两金重四，其八百石乎？寅申一冲，箕星动矣。明日寅时，转西北风大顺。

    Explanation

    490

    The guest merely picked up the pen and transcribed, and all the judgments aligned with the visitor's intentions, not a single one being wrong. The twelve lessons were completed in order, and the guests also left one by one. End Lin focused intently, forgetting to move forward or backward. The old man suddenly said, "The distant guest did not have time to enter and register; I, an old man, should offer one lesson to fulfill the host's duty. Are you surnamed Zhang, coming from the south of Yunnan?" End Lin replied, "Yes, how do you know this?" The old man said, "You are sitting under the palace, and the time is exactly at noon, estimated to be in the constellation Zhang. I naturally know this. Today is the day of Yi Mao, and the three transmissions of Shen, You, and Xu are the wealth and official positions of the day, corresponding to the noble deity, Taichang, Xuanwu, Baihu, and evil spirits together with the official hexagram, indicating the respected relatives of officials, there should be a father's coffin in the boat. Xu is the hell, before death, he was an official in charge of prisons. Taichang is rice and wheat, attached to You gold and also connected with continuous consumption, should carry rice. Two gold coins weigh four, is it eight hundred stones? Yin and Shen are in conflict, the Jixing star is moving. Tomorrow at the Yin hour, the northwest wind will be very favorable."

    491

    末传为地足，与卯作合，应十二月之卯日到籍，其米不但不得价，且颗粒全无。缘财入玄武耗散之手，尽化为三传之鬼矣。足下其慎之。”端林得课，回舟，果于五鼓得顺风，扬帆而进，于腊月二十六日己卯，抵邑之王家桥。改岁之际，无暇安葬。泊舟处，有姊夫陆某，米客也，借其闲地权厝父棺。因思米运入家，宗族强借可虞，不如近就姊家，以寄于栈。端林奉母回城度岁。岁朝往贺姊家，叩关而入，其姊对之号泣，曰：“尔姊夫亏客货千余金，上年尔所寄之米，被客强起去，姊夫无以对尔，今不知遁于何处。”端林忆及梁翁之课，慨然曰：“数已前定，果无可逃。姊勿惶急，忝在至亲，米价不定计论矣。我为寻访姊夫归家可也。”

    Explanation

    491

    The final transmission is the position of the earth's foot, harmonizing with the character "Mao," indicating that on the twelfth month's Mao day, the rice will arrive at the registry, but it will not only fail to fetch a price, but will be entirely lost. The money entered the hands of the "Black Warrior," who dissipated it, and everything was transformed into a ghost associated with the "Three Transmissions." You should be cautious about this." End Lin received the lesson, returned to the boat, and indeed, at the fifth watch of the night, the wind was favorable, and they set sail. On the twenty-sixth day of the twelfth month, the Jia Mao day, they arrived at Wangjia Bridge in the city. At the time of the New Year, there was no time for a proper burial. At the place where the boat was moored, there was his sister-in-law, Lu, a rice merchant, who lent them an empty space to temporarily place the father's coffin. Considering bringing the rice home, End Lin thought that the family might demand it, and it would be better to stay close to his sister's house and store the rice in a warehouse. End Lin returned to the city with his mother to spend the New Year. On New Year's Day, he went to visit his sister's house, knocked on the door, and entered. His sister greeted him with tears, saying, "Your brother-in-law lost over a thousand gold worth of goods last year, and the rice you sent last year was forcibly taken by someone. Your brother-in-law could not account for it, and now he is missing, and we do not know where he has gone." End Lin recalled the lesson from Master Liang and said resolutely, "Fate has already determined this, and there is no escape. Do not be alarmed, dear sister. As a close relative, the price of rice is uncertain, and I will search for your brother-in-law and bring him home."

    492

    小王子
    　　
    　　江左徐君，知医道，开设药肆为业。门临官河，栽柳成阴，有美荫。一丐者小艇，恒泊于下，有二妇操异音，日与其夫荡桨行乞。盛暑之际，忽闻二妇大放悲声，徐往观之，丐已欲毙，气存一息。发恻隐心，谓其妇曰：“尔夫当是痧症，我取药来，或可救也。”遂与蟾酥丸，用童便灌之，大醒，越日大愈。丐登门叩首，谢活命恩。自此放舟一去，不复返矣。历十年余，徐积得百数金，亲友合本，渡海赴关东贩豆。遇狂风，飘入大洋，舟不能为力，砍桅去帆，任其播扬。不知几日夜，亦不知几千百里，遇岛触机而舟碎，财物俱沉，所幸人无伤损，聚哭于沙屿而已。此岛内系大镇市，朝鲜所辖。

    492

    Little Prince

    A man named Xu from the Jiangnan region, skilled in medicine, ran a pharmacy as his profession. His door faced an official river, and he planted willows that formed a beautiful shade. A beggar with a small boat would always anchor there, accompanied by two women with distinct voices, who rowed and begged daily with their husband. During the hot summer, suddenly, the two women were heard wailing loudly. Xu went to see them slowly, and found the beggar nearly dead, with only a faint breath left. Feeling compassion, he told the woman, "Your husband probably has a heatstroke. I will fetch medicine, and perhaps he can be saved." He then gave him toad venom pills, administered them with young boy's urine, and the beggar woke up greatly. The next day, he was much better. The beggar came to the door and knelt down, expressing gratitude for saving his life. From then on, he set sail and went away, never returning again. For more than ten years, Xu accumulated several hundred gold coins. With the help of relatives and friends, he crossed the sea to go to Guandong to trade beans. He encountered a violent wind, drifted into the open ocean, and the boat could not withstand it. He cut down the mast and took down the sails, allowing it to drift. He did not know how many days and nights passed, nor how far he traveled. He hit an island and struck something that caused the boat to break, and all his belongings sank. Fortunately, no one was harmed, and they gathered and wept on a sandbank. This island had a large town under the jurisdiction of Korea.

    493

    有望者，见舟破人存，咸来问询，备录名单，飞报所司。值小王子巡边，闻报见单，亲临检点。至徐君，审视其行业，曰：“先生门前垂柳尚存乎？”徐不知所以，谨以实对。王子乃命司官，以诸客就养于馆舍，厚给其饩廪，曰：“遇便送还天朝可也。”命备法驾，肃徐登辇，王自乘骑前导。白旄黄钺，禁尉传呼，待人跪道。徐怦怦然，如梦如痴，听之而已。越两日，及大城，百官郊迎。添卤簿，易銮舆，称跸而入。城中三市六街，人烟辐凑。至宫门，王子挽徐手，升殿交拜，分宾主西东对坐。传命后宫，宣妃主夫人见客。乃有彩娥贵嫔，翼二夫人出，法衣象服，佩玉鸣铛，向徐盈盈再拜。

    Explanation

    493

    There were people looking, who saw a broken boat with people alive, and all came to inquire. They prepared and recorded a list, reporting quickly to the relevant authorities. When the young prince was on a border inspection, he heard the report and saw the list, personally coming to inspect. When it came to Mr. Xu, he carefully examined his profession, asking, "Is the willow tree in front of your house still there?" Mr. Xu did not know why, and answered truthfully and respectfully. The prince then ordered the officials to have the guests accommodated in the guesthouse, providing them generously with food and supplies, saying, "When convenient, send them back to the Heavenly Court." He ordered the preparation of the ceremonial carriage, having Mr. Xu ascend the carriage respectfully, and the prince himself rode ahead to lead the way. With white flags and yellow axes, the guards shouted, and people knelt on the path. Mr. Xu felt startled, as if in a dream, and listened to it and nothing more. After two days, when they reached the great city, the officials greeted him at the outskirts of the city. They added the ceremonial entourage and changed the imperial carriage, entering with the proclamation of the emperor's presence. The city had three markets and six streets, with people gathered like spokes of a wheel. When they reached the palace gate, the prince took Mr. Xu's hand and they ascended the hall to bow to each other, sitting facing each other with the host on the west and the guest on the east. They sent a message to the harem and summoned the concubines and ladies to see the guest. Then there were colorful maidens and noble concubines, flanking two ladies, wearing ceremonial robes and ornaments, with jade pendants jingling, and they bowed twice gracefully to Mr. Xu.

    494

    徐答拜不迭，长跪于地，曰：“小人何德何能，敢受我王殊礼？请明告我，以志异数。”王子亦跽告曰：“先生忘十年前，柳荫下，小艇中之病丐乎？即寡人也。我国法，凡贵者，得异症，必远丐三年，以神忏悔。寡人二十余岁时，忽得眩疾，发则眩晕即死，过日而苏。前王命太史占之，须乞丐中华沿海之区，必有奇遇，疾以痊也。”乃指二妃，曰：“寡人带渠行乞，得遇先生，以仙丹救治寡人，自此即愈。逮今十余年，不复发矣。昔年王父崩，寡人即位，国服甫满，尚未朝贡请封，故国人犹以小王子称寡人耳。今天幸先生来此，俾寡人有以报德。先生欲贵乎？欲富乎？欲贵则特设宾师之位，以奉先生；欲富则珍宝货财，惟先生命。

    Explanation

    494

    He slowly responded with a bow, kneeling on the ground, and said, "What virtue or ability does a lowly person like me have to dare receive the special courtesy of my king? Please clearly inform me so that I may remember the extraordinary treatment." The prince also knelt and said, "Have you forgotten ten years ago, under the willow shade, in the small boat, the sick beggar? That was I, the ruler. According to our customs, anyone of high status who contracts a strange illness must wander as a beggar for three years as a form of spiritual repentance. When I was over twenty years old, I suddenly contracted a dizziness illness. When it would flare up, I would become dizzy and die, but would recover after a day. The previous king ordered the historian to divinate, and it was said that I must beg in the coastal areas of China, and I would surely have a miraculous encounter, and the illness would be cured." He then pointed to his two consorts and said, "I wandered with them as beggars and met you, who cured me with the divine elixir. From then on, I was healed. It has been over ten years since then, and the illness has not recurred. Last year, my father the king passed away, and I ascended the throne. The period of mourning attire has just been completed, and we have not yet paid tribute or requested a title, so the people still call me the 'Prince.' Now, by the grace of heaven, you have come here, allowing me to repay your kindness. Do you desire nobility? Or wealth? If you desire nobility, I will specially set up a position for a guest teacher to honor you; if you desire wealth, then treasures and riches are at your command."

    495

    ”徐曰：“小人远方医士，无奇才异能，敢妄玷宾师，以速官谤？无已，本为运豆而来，请假为资本，送之关东，则感戴无既矣。”王子笑曰：“关青豆不及小邦者，不意中华贵至此。异日，请先生观我仓贮，何如？”乃开大醵毕，馆徐于天使行宫，命大臣之善华言者陪侍，娱之以本国杂乐。王子日往朝之。居数日，徐思家，欲归辞。留数四，不得已，王子命开太仓，请往观豆，则一颗之大，约重四五分，果胜于关青数倍。徐欲之，王子举仓以赠，载海舶数十艘，奉徐与诸客，以王大船送之归国。徐货豆得十余万金，改药肆为质库矣。或曰：惜哉徐君，不取珍宝，而于豆，芗曰：倬哉徐君，不贪珍宝，而仅仅以豆。不失我中华体统，其市侩之豪杰哉？

    Explanation

    495

    Xu said, "I am a humble physician from a distant land, lacking extraordinary talent or ability. How dare I rashly soil the position of guest and teacher, thereby inviting official criticism? If there is no other way, I originally came here to transport beans. Please allow me to use these beans as capital and send them to Guandong, and my gratitude will be boundless." The prince smiled and said, "Who would have thought that the beans from our small country are inferior to those of China? In the future, please let me show you my granaries. What do you think?" After holding a grand feast, the prince accommodated Xu in the embassy's residence, ordered a minister who was skilled in Chinese to accompany him, and entertained him with the country's mixed music. The prince visited Xu daily. After several days, Xu began to miss home and wanted to return. The prince tried to persuade him to stay several times, but Xu could not refuse. The prince then ordered the opening of the grand granary and invited Xu to see the beans. Each bean was about the size of a walnut, weighing around four to five grams, and was indeed several times larger than the beans from the border region. Xu was impressed, and the prince gave the entire granary as a gift. Dozens of sea ships were loaded, and Xu and the guests were accompanied back to their country on the king's large ship. Xu sold the beans and earned over ten thousand gold coins, changing his pharmacy into a pawnshop. Some said, "Alas for Xu Jun, he did not take precious treasures, but only focused on beans." Another said, "Magnificent is Xu Jun, who did not covet precious treasures, but only valued the beans. He did not lose the dignity of our Chinese nation; he is a hero among merchants."

    496

    卷二
    　　
    　　科场五则
    　　
    　　吴生，浙之李人，儒而兼贾，不预科场久矣。是年梦其父祖，催令入闱。生自知此道荒疏，无可侥幸之处，置之勿论。嗣又梦父祖厉色督责之，曰：“汝若不去，场中缺一孝廉矣。是为违天。违天不祥，必有后祸。”生及述其无文，何遇。父笑曰：“易耳。今科头题为‘乡人皆好之’一节，本家兰陔先生有此文，汝入闱时，访而录之可也。”生始欣然温故入闱，访问兰陔先生所在。夫吴兰陔者，时文中之名手也，其门下从学之徒数百人，发科甲入词林者甚众。唯先生落笔高古，屡困场屋，时年已五旬外矣，功名之念甚切。

    Explanation

    496

    Translation:

    Volume Two

    Five Principles of the Imperial Examination

    Wu Sheng was a man from Rui Li in Zhejiang, who was both a scholar and a merchant, and had not participated in the imperial examinations for a long time. That year, he dreamed that his father and ancestors urged him to enter the examination hall. Realizing that he had neglected his studies and saw no hope for success, he dismissed the idea. Soon after, he dreamed again that his father and ancestors scolded him sternly, saying, "If you do not go, there will be a vacancy for a xiao lian in the examination hall. This is a violation of heaven. Violating heaven is inauspicious, and there will surely be future misfortune." Wu Sheng then explained that he had no talent for writing and asked what he should do. His father smiled and said, "It is easy. The first question this year is on the section 'All the villagers love him.' Our family’s Mr. Lan Gai has written this passage. When you enter the examination hall, find and copy it." Wu Sheng was delighted and prepared his old knowledge before entering the hall. He inquired about the location of Mr. Lan Gai. Mr. Wu Lan Gai was a renowned writer of his time, with hundreds of disciples under his tutelage, many of whom passed the examinations and entered the literary world. However, Mr. Lan Gai’s writing was highly refined and ancient, and he had repeatedly failed the examinations. By that time, he was over fifty years old, and his desire for fame and success was intense.

    497

    生访得之，致其景仰之意，曰：“闻先生窗下有‘乡人皆好之’一节题文，为士林传诵。小子与先生居隔百里，未由亲炙。今日尚闲，务求赐教。”兰陔见其殷勤难却，录出与观。生曰：“容小子携回号舍，细细揣摩可乎？”兰陔颔之，生欢喜捧去。未几，兰陔亦往生号内答之，见其在卷上挥毫疾书，讶曰：“尚未出题，何得有文？”生笑曰：“小子读先生文，不忍释手，恭缮试卷，以志钦佩，即文不对题，不过被黜而已，亦所甘心。”兰陔曰：“我累足下矣，奈何奈何？”遂别去。逢相识者告之，一时传作新闻。是夜试题出，果对。

    Explanation

    497

    The student sought out Lan Gai and expressed his admiration, saying, "I have heard that your essay on 'All the people love you' is widely acclaimed among scholars. I live a hundred miles away from you and have not had the opportunity to learn directly from you. I am free today and earnestly request your instruction." Seeing his earnestness, Lan Gai could not refuse and handed over the essay for him to read. The student said, "May I take it back to my examination hall and study it carefully?" Lan Gai nodded, and the student happily took it with him. Soon after, Lan Gai also went to the student's examination hall to return the favor, seeing the student writing quickly and fluently on his scroll. Lan Gai was surprised and asked, "The exam questions haven't been announced yet, how can you have an essay ready?" The student smiled and said, "I have been reading your writings and cannot put them down. I have respectfully copied the essay, as a token of my admiration. Even if the essay is off-topic, I will only be disqualified, which I am willing to accept." Lan Gai said, "I have burdened you, what can I do?" and then left. When he met acquaintances, he told them, and it became a news story for a time. That night, when the exam questions were announced, it turned out to be correct.

    498

    兰陔不胜悔恨，曰：“得意之作，既被人录去，谅天意终身不得售矣！”遂信笔一挥，交卷而去。二三场为门人苦劝讫事，是科竟中。兰陔以旧作入见座主，曰：“门生薄有微名，闱中之作，聊以塞责，不堪为多士寓目，请以此文易之。”座主曰：“可。虽然，此文若在场中，未必中式。盖阅卷时，走马看花，气机流走者，易于动目。此文非反复数过，不知其佳处，试官有此闲情乎？故无益也。”兰陔悟，遂有《读墨一隅》之选。先是吴生归，不作第二人想，整顿衣冠，预备筵宴，思作新孝廉之乐，若登天然。瞬过重阳，闱榜发而好音竟绝，觅得题名录观之，兰陔高捷矣。

    Explanation

    498

    Lan Gai was overwhelmed with regret and said, “My work of which I was so proud has been taken by someone else; surely it is heaven’s will that I shall never succeed!” So, he wrote carelessly, handed in his paper, and left. On a few occasions, he was urged by his disciples, and in the end, he passed the exam. Lan Gai presented his old work to the examiner and said, “Your humble disciple has a slight reputation; the work I submitted in the examination hall was merely to fulfill the duty and cannot be seen by many scholars. Please allow me to replace it with this article.” The examiner replied, “Yes. Although, if this article had been in the examination hall, it might not have passed. When reading the papers, examiners skim through them quickly, and those with flowing qi and movement are easy to catch the eye. This article requires several readings to appreciate its merits, and do the examiners have the leisure to do so? Therefore, it would be of no benefit.” Lan Gai understood, and thus compiled the collection Read Ink from One Corner. Before this, the student returned home, not expecting to come in second place. He tidied up his clothes, prepared a banquet, and thought of the joy of being a new xiao-lian, as if ascending to heaven. Time passed quickly, like the ninth day of the ninth month, and when the exam results were announced, the good news was absent. He searched for the list of names and looked at it, and to his surprise, Lan Gai had passed with high marks.

    499

    怨恨之极，怒其父祖，曰：“何为诓骗子孙耶？”欲毁木主。夜复梦父祖来，怒责之，曰：“不肖子，何知此中自有天命？汝若不抄袭兰陔之文，彼必自录，又不得中式矣。”生曰：“彼之中与不中，与我何干耶？”父曰：“闱中饭食，皆出帑项，即为天禄，非生时注籍，岂易得哉！汝命中尚有一次，不完，总不得安静也。”生悟，次科仍入闱，其友曰：“前此得极妙文章，尚不入彀，今何为耶？”生曰：“公等皆抡元夺魁手，我自来领钦赐食，以了公案耳。”北闱大学士某公典试，题为“回也，闻一以知十”二句，所取文内，有用《易经》“天一地二”及“七日来复”、“八月有凶”等语，不慊士心。

    Explanation

    499

    In extreme resentment, he was angry with his ancestors and said, "Why are you deceiving my descendants?" He wanted to destroy the wooden tablet used for ancestral worship. That night, he dreamt again that his ancestors came and angrily scolded him, saying, "Unworthy son, how could you know there is a heavenly destiny in this? If you had not copied Lan Gai's text, he would have had to write it himself, and he still would not have passed the examination." The son replied, "Does his passing or failing have anything to do with me?" His father said, "The food provided in the examination hall is all funded by the state treasury, which is considered a divine provision. It is not easily obtained unless one is registered at birth. Your life still has one more chance; if you don't complete it, you won't be able to rest in peace." The son understood and entered the examination hall again in the next cycle. His friend said, "Before, even with an excellent article, you still didn't pass. Why are you doing this now?" The son replied, "You all are capable of winning the top honors. I came here just to receive the imperial food and settle this matter." During the northern examination presided over by a high-ranking official, the question was "Hui, who heard one thing and knew ten." The selected essays used phrases from the Yijing (Book of Changes) such as "Heaven is one, Earth is two," "Seven days and it returns," and "August is inauspicious," which did not satisfy the scholars' hearts.

    500

    好事者撰新戏云：玉帝巡守，忽见怨气上冲阻驾，问于太白星官，奏曰：“此时人间乡试，士子有不才而遇，才而不遇者，不安义命，故有此怨毒之气，致干圣驾。”帝曰：“乡试取士，皆有定额，本属善法。若二教中仙佛，漫无定数，致有弄法欺人、兴妖作怪之辈，朕甚虑之。亦将仿照人间，举行乡试，可乎？”太白曰：“善哉善哉，不可缓矣。”爰命文昌历举文理优长之神仙，以充试官，如儒童菩萨、文殊菩萨及地下修文郎辈，皆命往洞天福地，纷纷去矣。唯玉京尚无典试者。帝问太白星官，太白曰：“此处应位尊爵显者为之。

    Explanation

    500

    Those who like to do good things wrote a new play: The Jade Emperor was on his inspection tour when he suddenly saw a grievance rising up and obstructing his journey. He asked the White Star Official, who reported, "At this time, the imperial examinations are being held in the human world. There are scholars who are talented but not recognized, and those who are unrecognized despite their talent. They are not at peace with their fate, hence this grievance rises up and obstructs the sacred carriage." The Jade Emperor said, "The provincial examinations select scholars, all with fixed quotas, which is a good method. However, in the Buddhist and Taoist religions, there are deities without a fixed number, leading to those who manipulate laws to deceive people and those who cause mischief and chaos. I am very concerned about this. Shall we also imitate the human world and hold provincial examinations, may we?" The White Star Official said, "Good idea, good idea, it should not be delayed." Therefore, he ordered the Celestial Scholar to list those deities who excel in literature and reason to serve as examiners, such as the Bodhisattva of Scholarly Youth, the Bodhisattva of Wisdom, and the Underworld Scholar Officials, who went away one after another. But the Jade Capital still has no examiner. The Jade Emperor asked the White Star Official, who said, "Those who hold high positions and have noble titles should be appointed here."

    501

    ”乃举齐天大圣孙悟空，帝曰：“尊矣显矣，奈其不通文墨乎？”太白曰：“天下试官，未必尽通。况猴子最灵，奉命之后，自能设法延请高明相助，可无虑矣。”爰召悟空，命之主试，不得推辞。悟空不敢违命，入文昌宫，请友为助。文昌曰：“我宫内天聋地哑二童，俱为人聘去矣，焉有余人？”悟空退，思吕纯阳为大唐进士，必通文理，往商之，吕祖曰：“我已奉命典试琅环福地，何暇相助？无已，或访知命之士，以命取人，亦不为屈。”悟空往访鬼谷先生，行抵北天门，与玄天上帝晤，问知来意，上帝笑曰：“若须知命者，不必远求，我座下龟灵圣母，为当今第一能手。

    Explanation

    501

    Then the emperor chose the Great Sage Equal to Heaven, Sun Wukong, and said, "Exalted and eminent, but is he not proficient in writing?" The White Tiger Star replied, "Officials presiding over examinations throughout the world may not all be proficient. Moreover, monkeys are the most intelligent. After receiving the order, he will be able to devise methods to invite knowledgeable and capable people to assist, so there is no need to worry." The emperor then summoned Sun Wukong and commanded him to preside over the examinations, with no room for refusal. Sun Wukong dared not disobey, so he entered the Palace of Literature to seek assistance. The God of Literature said, "Both of the mute and deaf attendants in my palace have been hired by others. How can there be anyone else?" Sun Wukong withdrew and thought that Lu Chuanyang, a scholar from the Tang Dynasty, must be well-versed in literature and would be suitable to consult. He went to see Lu Chuanyang, who said, "I have already been ordered to preside over examinations in the Land of Bliss and Learning. How can I have time to assist? If not otherwise, perhaps you could seek out wise and fated individuals, and order them to select candidates. It will not be a hardship." Sun Wukong then went to visit Master Guiguzi, arriving at the Northern Celestial Gate, where he met the Supreme God of the North. He asked about his purpose, and the god smiled and said, "If you need wise and fated individuals, there is no need to seek far. Under my command is the Divine Mother of Tortoise Wisdom, who is the foremost expert of the present day."

    502

    ”悟空悦，乃召圣母见之，曰：“蠢然一物，请入闱中，未免不雅。”圣母曰：“我之法身能大能小，能现能隐，请缩为金钱龟，藏于大圣袖中，则人皆不觉。及阅文时，我知其命应中式者，以我八卦衣，在大圣前显之，大圣取之无误也。”悟空从之，故是科多取八卦者，戏为皇上所闻，罚试官俸，而停用泛词者三科会试。江右召贡生，有三子，皆举业，长为廪膳生，次为增广生，其三应童子试，十余年不售，长为老童生矣，其父厌恶之，谪在厨房司灶，故“烧火三相公”之句，噪于戚里。值开科年，长次二子高列优等，将届入闱。

    Explanation

    502

    Wukong was pleased and summoned the Mother to see him. He said, "A foolish thing like this, please enter the examination hall, but it would inevitably be unseemly." The Mother replied, "My divine form can be large or small, visible or hidden. Please shrink into a gold turtle and hide in the Great Sage's sleeve, so no one will notice. When reading the essays, I will know those destined to pass the exam, and I will use my Eight Trigrams robe to reveal them. The Great Sage will take them without error." Wukong agreed, so in that exam, many candidates wrote about the Eight Trigrams, which amused the Emperor. The Emperor penalized the examiners' salaries and suspended the use of vague terms for three consecutive exams.

    In Jiangxi, a scholar had three sons, all engaged in the imperial examinations. The eldest was a student receiving a government stipend, the second was a student who had been promoted, and the third was taking the preliminary exam. For over ten years, the third son failed without success, and the eldest became a long-time unsuccessful candidate. His father grew to dislike him and demoted him to work in the kitchen as a stove-keeper, hence the nickname "the Third Master who cooks firewood" spread among the extended family. In the year of the imperial examination, the eldest and second sons ranked highly in the top tier and were about to enter the examination hall.

    503

    父命三相公同往会垣，供奔走之役，三相公欣然应命。入见其妻，呜咽悲泣，三相公叩其故，妻曰：“二伯人也，汝亦人也。何二伯若座上客，汝为灶下养耶？已属无耻；今乐为送考之下走，为汝妻者，何颜立于妯娌之间耶？”三相公曰：“我岂乐为，父命难违耳。”妻曰：“我何敢教汝违父命。汝若有志，亦得进场，我与有荣施矣。”三相公曰：“童生焉得与大试，奈我命何？”妻曰：“汝此去必谒丈人，是为方伯管库之吏，捐纳省监，系属专司。我有金珠在，汝以质与丈人，捐一监生，亦可观光闱屋矣。”三相公欢然从之。偕父兄赴会垣，谒妻父。纳监讫，归谓父曰：“丈人强与儿监，欲儿就试也。

    Explanation

    503

    Father ordered the three brothers to go together to the meeting hall to serve by running errands, and the three brothers gladly complied. Upon entering to see his wife, she was sobbing and weeping, and the narrator asked her the reason. His wife said, "Your second elder brother is a person, and you are also a person. Why is your second elder brother treated like a guest at the table while you are treated like someone who cooks for them? This already belongs to being shameless; now you enjoy being a servant accompanying on an exam, and as your wife, what face can you show among your sisters-in-law?" The narrator replied, "I do not enjoy doing it, but I cannot disobey my father's command." His wife said, "How dare I teach you to disobey your father's command. If you have the will, you can also enter the examination hall, and I will also have honor and benefit." The narrator said, "How can a candidate for the imperial examination join the major examination? What can I do about my fate?" His wife said, "You will surely visit your father-in-law this time, who is the provincial supervisor in charge of the treasury. You can donate and purchase a position in the provincial supervisory office, which is a specialized responsibility. I have gold and jewels, and you should pledge them to your father-in-law to purchase a supervisory position, which will also allow you to attend the examination hall." The narrator happily agreed. Accompanied by his father and elder brother, they went to the meeting hall to visit his father-in-law. After donating for the supervisory position, he returned home and told his father, "My father-in-law insisted on giving me a supervisory position, wanting me to take the examination."

    504

    ”父曰：“遗才不取，或贴出墙东，看汝何颜见丈人耶？”及录遗，公然附取，得随两兄入闱。因三相公食量甚宏，其父为之备绍酒金蹄烧鸭薰鹅之类，满足一挑。三相公领卷入号舍，见其左右邻，皆武林寒士，三相公慷慨食之，邻士皆悦。是夜题来，“譬如为山”四句。至次日之下午，三相公仍大烹以延邻士，皆议论名人作法，三相公默然。邻士叩其故，三相公实告以：“初次观光，遇大题敷衍难成，奈何？”邻士笑曰：“若欲完篇，何难之有？十三经中不乏山字话头，莫管议论，填砌成文，则洋洋数千言，尚引用不完也。”三相公亦实告以腹内空虚之故。

    Explanation

    504

    The father said, "To leave talent unselected, or perhaps display it on the east wall, how can you face your father-in-law with such shame?" When the son was finally selected, he openly attached himself to the selection and was allowed to enter the examination hall with his two brothers. Because the third son had a very large appetite, his father prepared a lavish meal of Shaoxing wine, golden trotters, roast duck, and smoked goose, which filled a whole basket. The third son took his exam papers into the examination hall and saw that his neighbors on both sides were all poor scholars from Wulin. He generously ate with them, and all of them were pleased. That night, he composed a poem titled "Pretending to be a Mountain" with four lines. By the afternoon of the next day, the third son still prepared a grand feast to entertain his neighboring scholars, who all discussed the methods of famous scholars. The third son remained silent. The neighboring scholars asked him why, and he honestly told them, "On my first occasion of seeing the world, I encountered a major question that was hard to answer satisfactorily, what should I do?" The neighboring scholars laughed and said, "If you want to complete your essay, what difficulty is there? The Thirteen Classics are not short of phrases about mountains. Don't worry about the discussion; just fill in the text with thousands of words, and you will still not exhaust the references." The third son also honestly told them that he was empty inside, lacking knowledge or preparation.

    505

    邻士争为写书，且教以连用之法。三相公悟，挥洒自如，千言立就。试毕，其长次二兄皆录文呈父，父乃举酒阅文，恬吟密咏，推为必售之作。三相公亦技痒，以其稿恭呈父前，其父拍案大呼曰：“浮泛至此，亦可以见人乎？不知愧恧，至汝极矣！”其兄碎其文，喝令速退，勿触父怒也。三相公抱头鼠窜而去。是科主试者，非邓奇即帅怪。此二公者，生性偏僻，好为诡异。十五日例设抡元宴，隔帘相叙。内则正副二主试，带同十八房考官，外则监临中丞，相率提调名官合宴。此夜公请主试宣明题义，应取何等文字，以定元魁。大主试笑曰：“文无定法，唯真山真水者中。

    Explanation

    505

    Neighbors competed to write books for him and taught him advanced writing techniques. The Third Prince of the Three Princes realized and wrote with ease and confidence, completing a thousand words instantly. After the examination, his two elder brothers both copied their texts and presented them to their father. The father raised a cup and read the texts, savoring and reciting them with great pleasure, and recommended them as works guaranteed to be successful. The Third Prince of the Three Princes also felt the urge to demonstrate his skills and respectfully presented his draft to his father. His father banged the table and shouted loudly, saying, "So superficial and shallow, how can you show this to others? Not knowing shame, you have reached the extreme!" His brother tore up the text, ordered him to leave quickly, and warned him not to provoke his father's anger. The Third Prince of the Three Princes fled in a panic, like a rat carrying its head. The chief examiner of that year was either Deng Qi or Shuai Guai. These two men had peculiar personalities and liked to be strange and unusual. On the fifteenth day, the traditional banquet for the top candidates was held, and they conversed through a curtain. Inside, the chief and deputy chief examiners, along with the 18 examiners responsible for grading the papers, were present. Outside, the supervisor of the examination and other officials gathered together for a banquet. That night, the officials asked the chief examiner to clarify the meaning of the questions and what kind of writing should be accepted to determine the top candidate. The chief examiner smiled and said, "There is no fixed method for writing; only those who write about real mountains and real waters will succeed."

    506

    ”此戏言也，中丞不觉失声耍笑，众官和之，哄堂一粲。大主试怒，拂袖而起，曰：“我欲云云，谁敢尔尔！”竟罢宴入内，众官不欢而散。皆私议，特觅此等浮泛之作以玩之。幸有三相公之妙文在，一房官得之，笑不可遏；众官闻声趋视，曰：“有此不通主试，即有此不通举子，可谓千古奇遇。”众曰：“何不荐之？”此房官曰：“无乃过谑。”众曰：“我等公荐如何？”皆首肯。于是十八官相率呈堂，曰：“职等自奉命后，在九千六百余卷中，仅搜求得真山真水者一本，用敢公呈电鉴。

    Explanation

    506

    This is a joke, the Censor-in-Chief couldn't help but burst into laughter, and the other officials joined in, causing the room to fill with mirth. The chief examiner became angry, brushed his sleeves and stood up, saying, "I will say what I want, who dares to act as they please!" He finally ended the banquet and entered the inner chambers, and the officials left in dissatisfaction. They all privately discussed, searching for such superficial and insubstantial writings to mock. Fortunately, there was the excellent writing of the respected scholar, and one official found it, laughing uncontrollably; the other officials heard the laughter and came to look, saying, "With such an incompetent chief examiner, there must be such an incompetent candidate, this can be called a rare encounter in a thousand years." They all asked, "Why not recommend it?" This official replied, "Isn't this a bit too much of a joke?" They all said, "What if we recommend it together?" They all nodded in agreement. Thus, the eighteen officials led one another to present it to the examination hall, saying, "We officials, after receiving our orders, searched through over nine thousand six hundred scrolls and found only one with genuine and meaningful content, and dare to present it publicly for scrutiny."

    507

    ”大主试阅之，明知众官谑己，拍案而起曰：“如此典博之文，不合抡元耶？”举墨笔于填书之处，密密圈之，标定第一名。众官面面相觑，不敢作声。幸副主试闻声而来，持此卷且读且笑，谓大主试曰：“博则博矣，无乃稍涉浮泛乎？”大主试曰：“此元我定，与阁下无干。放榜后，我自挂弹章，请皇上处分可也。”咸知其固执之性，倔强难挽，皆无言而退。三相公居然发解矣。是时召公率其三子，移寓西湖之麓。至龙虎日，高会亲友，开宴以俟捷音，各出文互相赞诵。三相公举碗擎杯，往来应酬。或索其文阅，复为父兄诟谇。或怒曰：“渠既入场，何至不堪如此！”携三相公手，踏月湖堤，以销不平之气。值报捷者飞舆而来，问之，有召姓，三相公喜曰：“我兄中矣。

    Explanation

    507

    The chief examiner read the essay, knowing full well that the other officials were mocking him. He slammed his desk and stood up, saying, "Such a profound and learned essay, is it not worthy of being the top scorer?" He took his pen and heavily marked the paper, designating it as the first place. The other officials looked at each other in silence, unable to speak. Fortunately, the deputy examiner heard the commotion and came over, reading the paper and laughing, and said to the chief examiner, "It is indeed learned, but isn't it a bit too superficial?" The chief examiner replied, "I have already decided this as the top scorer. It has nothing to do with you. After the results are posted, I will file a complaint with the emperor and ask for his punishment." Everyone knew his stubborn nature and found it difficult to persuade him, so they all left without saying anything. The third gentleman was surprisingly admitted to the next stage of the examination. At that time, Mr. Zhao and his three sons moved to live near the foot of West Lake. On the day of the imperial examination results, they gathered with relatives and friends, held a banquet, and exchanged and praised each other's essays. The third gentleman raised his cups and glasses, socializing and entertaining. Some asked to read his essay and criticized it harshly. Others angrily said, "He has already taken part in the examination, how could it be so bad!" They took the third gentleman's hand and walked along the lake embankment under the moonlight to vent their frustration. A messenger arrived with news of success, and it was revealed that the messenger had the surname Zhao. The third gentleman was happy and said, "My brother has passed."

    508

    ”共拥至父前，众称非常之喜，先索报资，不与名条阅视。召曰：“我大儿合中久矣，今发已迟，不足奇也。”众曰：“否。”召曰：“然则我次儿正应中试。”众亦曰：“否。”召曰：“否则误矣。岂有烧火三相公得中举人耶？”众曰：“然。”召曰：“果有之，已属万幸，不过副车，好则榜尾耳。”众曰：“请定赏例，自观名条可知也。”召曰：“副举十金，正榜倍之。”众曰：“元魁如何？”召曰：“魁则五十，元可百金，决无此理。”众使书券讫，攫其百金一纸，而与之报条，公然第一。召骇曰：“文风之变迁，至于此极，今而后不敢论文矣。

    Explanation

    508

    They gathered and brought it to the father's presence, and the crowd exclaimed with great joy, first demanding payment, but did not provide the name list for review. The father called out, "My eldest son has been destined to pass for a long time, but now it has come too late, which is not surprising." The crowd replied, "No." The father then said, "Then my second son should have passed the examination." The crowd also replied, "No." The father said, "Otherwise, it would be a mistake. How could the third son, who tends the fire, pass the imperial examination?" The crowd replied, "Yes." The father said, "If it is indeed so, it is already a great fortune, at most a secondary pass, at best the last on the list." The crowd asked, "Please set the reward example, and you can know by looking at the name list." The father said, "A secondary pass is ten gold, the main list is double." The crowd asked, "What about the top scorer?" The father said, "The top scorer is fifty, the first place is a hundred gold, there is absolutely no way." The crowd made him write the contract, took the hundred gold in one document, and gave him the name list, openly declaring him the first. The father was shocked and said, "The change in literary style has reached this extreme, from now on, I dare not discuss literature anymore."

    509

    ”有朱解元者，眇一目，人呼为朱瞎子，亦曰朱半仙，时文中之能手也，名噪一时。其未发解之前一科，偕友赴试。八月初七日，夜梦见二青衣，相邀入一殿廷，有冕旒王者，降阶相迎曰：“闻先生文名籍甚，今有尔浙闱墨，请先生为之润饰。”朱唯唯。延入后殿，朱衣神以卷送阅，王者命置笔砚于几而退。朱见元作格法高超，惟稍有未圆融处，为之易数字，已尽善矣。王者复来，谓朱曰：“先生且停笔墨。今科解元，文才尚好，不意该县城隍神来奏，此生竟有奸人室女事，阴德有亏，应削其籍。予已追取下科解元，文到请先生正之。”朱阅其文，曰：“此必童子之作。

    Explanation

    509

    A man named Zhu, who was a jieyuan, had lost sight in one eye and was therefore called Zhu the blind. He was also known as Zhu the half-immortal. At the time, he was a master of writing and his name was famous for a while. Before he was selected in the previous examination, he went to take the exam with a friend. On the seventh day of the eighth month, he had a dream in which he saw two servants in green clothes inviting him into a palace hall. There, a king with a crown and streamers descended from the steps to greet him, saying, "I have heard of your great literary reputation. Now I have your essay from the Zhejiang examination. Please have you polish it." Zhu nodded in agreement. He was then led into a rear hall, where a deity in red robes presented him with the essay to read. The king ordered the pen and inkstone placed on the table and then withdrew. Zhu saw that the original work was of high quality, but it was slightly lacking in harmony. He changed a few words, and it was now perfect. The king returned and told Zhu, "Please put down your pen and ink. The current jieyuan has good literary talent, but unexpectedly, the city god of the county reported that this person was involved in a matter of seducing a woman in another's household. There was a deficiency in his virtue, and his name should be erased from the records. I have already retrieved the jieyuan from the next examination. When the essay arrives, please have you correct it." Zhu read the essay and said, "This must be the work of a child."

    510

    质地虽佳，功夫未到，何以冠多士？”王者曰：“且请就文整顿，资格所限，不能易他人也。”朱大加删削，炼作老境笔路，以呈王者，王者许可。命梦神飞传与之。然后次第阅竟，亦大费经营矣。王者大悦，曰：“先生在后科之元也，今以阅卷功，拔补下科元缺，以酬劳瘁。致所黜之元，系山阴某生奸其邻女，幸未破败，然而神目如电，已为所司执秦。先生归去，访其人，劝其改过，将来尚可登科也。至新解元，诚如先生所云，系新进童子，其父兄皆为词林，仁和人，祖宗功德甚厚，子孙科第，未有艾也。

    Explanation

    510

    The quality is good, but the skill has not reached, why should it be the best among many scholars?” The ruler said, “Please revise the text; limited by qualifications, one cannot replace another.” Zhu heavily revised and polished it into a mature writing style, and presented it to the ruler, who approved. He ordered a dream spirit to deliver it. Then, he read through it in order, which also required much effort. The ruler was greatly pleased and said, “The teacher will be the top scholar in the next exam; now, for the merit of grading the papers, you will be promoted to fill the top spot in the next exam to reward your hard work. As for the disqualified top scholar, it was a student from Shan Yin who had seduced a neighbor's daughter; fortunately, it was not yet broken, but the divine eye saw clearly, and he was already seized by the authorities. The teacher, upon returning, should visit him, advise him to repent, and he could still pass the exams in the future. As for the new top scholar, indeed as the teacher said, he is a new student, his father and brothers are all scholars, from Ren He, with great ancestral merit; the descendants' academic achievements will continue indefinitely.

    511

    然此子拔早一科，阴律应减阳寿五年，先生亦为之劝勉，俾绳其祖武，不但寿可免减，禄且日增矣。”遂命青衣仍送朱回寓，而梦觉，已高卧三日。其仆守之，见朱忽醒，曰：“何病耶？头场将毕，自误功名，奈何？”朱曰：“倦耳，无病。”遣仆往接其友，录出两元作。俟友回，出门访见山阴生，及仁和童子，告以神语，以文为证，皆惶悚受教。朱归，视亲友之文，一读破题，即知其中第几名。有佳文，曰：“惜哉不售，其伤阴耶？”初不之信，后皆不爽，遂有半仙之称。吾乡有刘君者，应童试不售，去而习申韩业，公然宪幕。丁卯岁朝，梦迎天榜，伊名列第一。不觉技痒，复理故业，纳监入闱。头场犯规被贴，居然第一，此鬼神揶揄之耶？抑若吴生之命有天禄耶？必居一于此矣。

    Explanation

    511

    The person was promoted prematurely in one category, and according to hidden laws, their lifespan would be reduced by five years. The teacher also encouraged and advised them to follow in the footsteps of their ancestors, not only to avoid a reduction in lifespan but also to see their fortune increase daily. The teacher then ordered a servant to take the person back to their residence, and the person woke up from a dream, having slept for three days. The servant, who had been watching over them, saw the person suddenly wake up and asked, "What illness do you have? The first exam is about to end, and you are jeopardizing your chances of success. What will you do?" The person replied, "I am just tired; I have no illness." They sent the servant to fetch their friend and copied out two top essays. After waiting for the friend to return, the person went out to visit another scholar from Shanyin and a young boy from Renhe, telling them about the divine message and using the copied essays as evidence. All were deeply moved and accepted the advice. After returning home, the person read the essays of their friends and relatives and could immediately determine their ranking. If there was a good essay, the person would say, "Too bad it wasn't successful, did it harm their fortune?" Initially, the person did not believe it, but later found that it was always accurate, and the person eventually gained the reputation of being a semi-divine figure. In the person’s hometown, there was a man named Liu who failed the children’s exam and then studied the strategies of Shang Yang and Han Fei, eventually becoming a prominent official. On the first day of the year in the Dingmao year, Liu dreamed of welcoming the imperial list and saw his name as first place. He couldn’t resist the urge to try again, so he resumed his old studies, entered the examination hall, and took the exam. During the first exam, he made a mistake and was penalized, yet still managed to come in first place. Was this a joke from the gods, or did Liu have a special destiny like Wu’s? It must be one of these two possibilities.

    512

    姚幕府
    　　
    　　台逆之乱，制军已飞章入告，集百官议策守计，连日未决。忽有严旨下，值制军筵宴时也，客皆退避。制军接读之间，目定口呆，神魂失据，颓乎座上矣。仆皆惶急，奔告公子。公子来，先捧谕旨，与幕友姚先生阅之，骇曰：“殆矣，上意切责其怠缓，限十日内平定。为今之计，唯有刻即进兵，以副庙莫。”公子曰：“父病垂危，焉能进剿？”姚公曰：“我与尊公宾主相投，久而无间，今日之事，不得不出身赞襄，姑将尊公抬送大堂，我当代为发令。”公子不得已，从之。乃命文武员弁，俱集辕门听令。鸣炮升堂，连座抬制军于暖阁，垂其帷幕，若避风然。

    Explanation

    512

    The rebellion of the rebels, the military governor had already sent an urgent report, gathered all officials to discuss defensive strategies, but they had not reached a decision for several days. Suddenly, a strict imperial order arrived, just as the military governor was holding a banquet. The guests all retreated. The military governor, upon receiving the order, became fixed in his eyes, his mouth dumb, his soul lost, and he fell back into his seat. The servants, all in a state of extreme anxiety, rushed to inform the young master. The young master came, first holding the imperial decree, and read it with the advisor Mr. Yao. He was shocked and said, "Alas, the emperor's intention is to severely criticize our laziness and delay, and to limit us to settle the matter within ten days." The young master replied, "My father is gravely ill, how can we engage in military action?" Mr. Yao said, "I have been in a close and unbroken relationship with your father, and today's matter, I cannot but step forward to assist. Let us carry your father to the main hall, and I will issue orders on his behalf." The young master, reluctantly, agreed. He then ordered all military and civilian officials to gather at the gate to receive orders. The cannon was sounded, and they ascended to the main hall, carrying the military governor to the warm room, lowering the curtains as if to avoid the wind.

    513

    姚服从者衣冠，以令箭出入传谕。先命水师立备战舰，命水军总戎为先行，五鼓放洋，直取鹿门耳。左军从左，右军从右，张两翼，以助先行。中军擐甲执械，齐集海门，以候本督部征进。命蕃司速运军需。并拣选文职之可以参赞军谋者，随军听用。移请中丞督率臬道府县保守城垣，以备非常。是时，文自制军以下，畏葸不前；武自千把以上，争先欲战。以致内外惶惑。兹闻制军忽然振作，号令井井有条，军弁莫不踊跃从事。令毕，抬制军入内，以安神丸与参汤灌之，渐苏，见妻子环伺，失声悲泣曰：“吾命休矣。”公子以姚先生所为告之，益骇曰：“如此，则尸骨不得存矣。

    Explanation

    513

    Yao, dressed in formal attire and carrying a formal document, issued orders as he moved about. First, he commanded the navy to prepare their warships and appointed the commander of the water army as the vanguard, ordering them to set sail at the fifth watch and proceed directly to capture the strategic point of Luomen. The left flank of the army was positioned on the left, and the right flank on the right, spreading out to support the vanguard. The main force donned armor and took up weapons, gathering at the coastal area of Haimen to await the advance of the supreme commander. He ordered the department responsible for logistics to quickly transport military supplies and selected civilian officials who could assist in military planning to accompany the army. He also requested the high-ranking official, the Governor, to oversee the defense of the city walls and prepare for any extraordinary situations. At that time, civilian officials below the rank of the supreme commander were hesitant and reluctant to act, while military officers above the rank of captain were eager to engage in battle. This led to confusion and anxiety both within and outside the camp. Now, it was heard that the supreme commander suddenly became more energetic, issuing clear and well-organized orders, and the officers and soldiers all enthusiastically carried out their tasks. After the orders were completed, the supreme commander was brought inside, and he was given a calming pill and a soup of ginseng to drink. Gradually, he regained consciousness and saw his wife and children surrounding him, and he cried out in despair, saying, "My life is over!" The young man informed him of what Mr. Yao had done, and he became even more terrified, saying, "In that case, my body will not remain intact."

    514

    ”请姚先生商之，姚曰：“此事无所再议者。不进，则圣怒莫测，难保自家；进则虽死犹荣，况生还可必乎矣！”制军思之，跃然而起，曰：“先生之言，胜于良药，吾无病矣。唯有求先生偕渡重洋，始终其事。”姚曰：“诺。兵贵神速，请即启行。”挽制军手而出。文武官弁，群以升舆。至海门，前军已发，两翼犹留。讯其故，缘艨艟不足。姚传制军令于海口，曰：“有能以商舟济我师者，予五品官。”有舟子来试投之，立给水师守备。于是群舟争集，三军毕登，扬帆冲浪而前。海神呵护，一昼夜直抵鹿门，攻其无备。前军已入，两翼从之。制军统全师进围台城，出逆意外。

    Explanation

    514

    Mr. Yao was asked to consult on the matter, and he replied, "This matter cannot be reconsidered. If we do not advance, the emperor's anger is unpredictable, and we cannot ensure our own safety; if we advance, even if it leads to death, it brings honor, let alone the possibility of survival!" The general pondered his words and suddenly stood up, saying, "Mr. Yao's advice is better than any medicine; I am cured. I can only ask Mr. Yao to accompany me across the vast ocean and see this matter through to the end." Mr. Yao agreed, "Agreed. Speed is of the essence in war; let us depart immediately." He took the general's hand and led him out. The civil and military officials all lifted him into his carriage. When they reached the sea gate, the vanguard had already set out, but the wings were still holding back. They inquired about the reason, and it was because there were insufficient warships. Mr. Yao conveyed the general's order at the sea gate, saying, "Anyone who provides a merchant ship to transport our army will be granted a fifth-rank military officer." A boatman came to test the order, and he was immediately given the rank of water army defender. As a result, many ships gathered, and the entire army boarded, setting sail bravely. Under the protection of the sea god, they sailed directly to Luomen in one night, attacking when the enemy was unprepared. The vanguard had already entered, and the wings followed. The general led the entire army to besiege Taicheng, an action that was unexpected by the enemy.

    515

    贼党掳掠未回，皆散还村落间，守御单弱。姚与制军巡督，见城以竹木为之，伏两翼于后道，夜使前军纵火声喊，缺后道以逸之。贼出遇伏，一鼓成擒，招降余党，分派内地，不旬日而全台俱平。飞骑报捷，帝大悦，召制军入觐，曰：“朕视尔畏葸犹昔，此举何其奋勇，必有能参赞之者。”制军不能隐，奏知姚幕友之事。特旨召见，欲予一官，姚顿首曰：“草莽之臣，敢因圣训，而自居功？且赞襄助顺，即所以报效朝廷，与有官等耳，敢辞。”帝嘉其刚直，赐四品卿衔，命永镇闽督幕府。芗曰：才如姚君，方不愧为入幕之宾。不然，庸庸者流，奚啻书吏之头目，臧获之首领耶，乌足道哉！

    Explanation

    515

    The bandits, after plundering, had not yet returned and had dispersed back to their villages, leaving the defenses weak and sparse. Yao and the military commander went on patrol and inspection, and they saw that the city was made of bamboo and wood. They set up ambushes on both sides of the rear path. At night, they ordered the front troops to set fires and make loud noises, while leaving the rear path open to lure the bandits out. When the bandits emerged and encountered the ambush, they were captured quickly and decisively. The remaining bandits were then recruited to surrender and were distributed within the interior regions. Within a short period, the entire island was pacified. News of the victory was swiftly relayed by horseback, and the emperor was very pleased. He summoned the military commander to court and said, "I still see you as timid as before. How brave you have been in this endeavor! There must be someone capable assisting you." The commander could not hide the truth and reported Yao's involvement. The emperor issued a special edict to summon Yao, intending to grant him an official position.
