Yao knelt and said, "A humble subject like me, how dare I claim personal credit due to the emperor's instructions? Assisting in this way is a form of service to the court, equivalent to holding an official position, and I dare not decline." The emperor praised Yao for his integrity and straightforwardness, granting him the rank of a fourth-grade official and ordering him to permanently serve in the military commander's office in Fujian. The commentator said, "A person like Yao would truly be a worthy advisor. Otherwise, a mere clerk or servant, hardly worth mentioning, would be no better than the head of household servants."

    516

    周封翁
    　　
    　　蜀之周老人，八旬外，樵于山而得宝藏，娶卖浆者女，生二子，此见于记载久矣。更有异者，其长子目有真光，察人无错。生子煌，少年入词林，故其父皆以封翁尊之。有楚人某进士，煌同年也。人本傥，貌亦魁梧，与煌友善。因归班候选，急于自效，谋捐分发。素稔周氏多财，与煌借银两千。煌曰：“我祖年届百有二十，我将告假上寿，兄其偕回我籍，与父商之，谅无不慨允也。”归告乃翁，翁出见客，款接殷勤。入室谓煌曰：“如数与银，还否听之，但此后务宜疏远，以防后患。

    Explanation

    516

    Final Translation

    Zhou the Old Gentleman

    In Shu, there was an elderly man named Zhou, who was well beyond eighty years old. While chopping wood in the mountains, he unexpectedly discovered treasures. He married the daughter of a water seller and had two sons, a story that has been recorded for a long time. What is even more extraordinary is that his eldest son had true light in his eyes and was never wrong in his observations of people. His son, Huang, entered the literary circle as a young man, and thus his father always respected him as a "Old Gentleman." There was a certain scholar from Chu, who was a contemporary of Huang. This scholar was bold in character and tall and strong in appearance, and he was friendly with Huang. Because he was returning to the capital for a selection of officials, he was eager to prove himself and planned to donate to secure a government position. He was familiar with the Zhou family's wealth and asked Huang for a loan of two thousand taels of silver. Huang said, "My grandfather is now one hundred and twenty years old. I will take leave to celebrate his longevity. Please accompany me back to my hometown, and we will discuss this with my father. I am sure he will generously agree." Upon returning home, Huang informed his father, who warmly received the guest. After entering the room, the old man said to Huang, "Give the silver as requested. Whether it will be repaid or not is up to him, but from now on, you must keep a distance, to guard against future troubles."

    517

    ”某得银援例，分发江南，旋补巨邑。专丁备礼，赍银付还，致书感谢，并达思慕之忱。翁使记室答之，以司总列名，厚给使者盘费，嘱告乃主，以翁父子，俱未能作书，因病故也。不久某以赃败身戮。是时，甫定寄顿者罪例，富室被诬，往往有之。是以究其家人，词连周氏。煌时在馆，即以与某虽系同年，并无音讯往来为辩。上司检查无据，宥释不问。又程老实者，质库中之立柜伙也。其库主以游荡败，欲举十万金之资本，愿以八折速售。程每对人嗟叹，曰：“焉得八万金，则先发加二财也。

    Explanation

    517

    A certain individual obtained silver through a petition and was assigned to the southern region, where he was soon promoted to a major city. He sent a letter of gratitude, expressing feelings of longing and admiration. The old man instructed the office of records to reply, listing the names of the department heads, generously providing the messenger with travel expenses, and informing the head of the household that the old man and his son were unable to write due to illness and unfortunately passed away. Soon after, the individual was executed for corruption. At that time, the penal code for those who stash goods had just been established, and wealthy families were often falsely accused. Therefore, their family members were investigated, and the words implicated the Zhou family. When Huang was in the hall, he defended himself by stating that although he was the same year of passing the imperial examination as the individual, they had no communication or correspondence. The superior officials found no evidence and released him without further inquiry. There was a trustworthy person named Zhang, a clerk in a pawnshop. The owner of the pawnshop failed due to idleness and wanted to sell his ten thousand gold pieces of capital at an eighty percent discount. Zhang often lamented to others, saying, "How can I get eight thousand gold pieces to double my capital?"

    518

    ”或绐之曰：“周氏以数百万举债，汝往贷八万金，有何难哉？”程信以为然，乃假伙伴衣冠，往周宅，请见封翁。翁见之，陈其来意，翁笑曰：“八万金似非容易，姑缓图之。”程自觉鲁莽，告退。翁送之出，值阴沉欲雨，客去翁回。而雨渐大。翁思山路难行，程已年高，恐其失跌，使家人追之，去逾时而客始返，翁密询家人，云：“客出门遇雨，在树林下，去其衣履，包裹而行。小人追回，复在大门内，重着衣冠方入。是以迟迟。”翁乃问程曰：“足下衣冠自有之乎，抑假于人乎？请以实告。”程忸怩曰：“借诸同伙，数人凑合者。”翁曰：“善，今天雨泥泞，勿损人之物，姑止一宿。

    Explanation

    518

    Someone deceived him, saying, "The Zhou family has borrowed hundreds of millions in debt; if you go to borrow eight million coins, what difficulty is there?" Cheng Xin believed this and borrowed his companion's clothes and hat, then went to the Zhou residence to request an audience with the respected elder. The elder saw him, explained his purpose, and smiled, saying, "Eight million coins does not seem easy to obtain; let us take it slowly." Cheng Xin felt foolish and hastily took his leave. The elder saw him off, and at that moment, the sky was overcast and threatening rain. As the guest left, the elder returned. The rain began to fall heavily. The elder thought the mountain path would be difficult to travel, and Cheng Xin was already old, fearing he might slip and fall, so he sent a servant to chase after him. After a long time, the guest finally returned. The elder secretly asked the servant, who reported, "The guest encountered rain upon leaving, took shelter under a tree, removed his clothes and shoes, wrapped himself up, and walked. I chased him back, and he re-entered the main gate, putting his clothes and hat back on before entering. That is why he was delayed." The elder then asked Cheng Xin, "Did you have your own clothes and hat, or did you borrow them from someone? Please be honest." Cheng Xin, feeling embarrassed, replied, "I borrowed from my companions, who pooled their resources together." The elder said, "Very well. Today, the rain has made the ground muddy; do not damage others' property. For now, stay overnight."

    519

    明日我与足下偕往城中，检点质库，再商行止可耳。”程唯唯。次日，翁果与程车马仆从而去，质库中人见之，深讶其事。翁历内外，检阅一过，谓程曰：“足下之言不谬。”尽与八万金接续，且平分余资，使之司总。程感激图报，克俭克勤，是以岁入之利，较他处为优。或问于翁曰：“某进士品格貌相，人人所愿交者，而翁必欲绝之，后果败，几被其累。程掌柜贸然而来，人人所讥诮者，而翁毅然从之，至今深得其力，是何道也？”翁曰：“道在目前，人自不察耳。某身为进士，入仕有途，何必欲速，其贪可知矣。贪以败官，古之训也。程掌柜假人衣冠，尚知保惜爱护，矧巨万财物乎？故知其受托不苟也。”

    Explanation

    519

    Tomorrow, I will accompany you to the city to inspect the pawnshop and then discuss our next steps," said the old man. Cheng nodded in agreement. The next day, the old man indeed went to the city with Cheng, accompanied by carriages, horses, and servants. The people in the pawnshop were deeply surprised by this. The old man inspected both inside and outside the pawnshop, and after a thorough review, he said to Cheng, "Your words were not wrong." He fully compensated with eight million coins and equally divided the remaining funds, appointing Cheng as the manager. Cheng was grateful and sought to repay the old man's kindness, being thrifty and diligent, which is why the annual profit was superior to that of other places. Someone asked the old man, "A certain scholar-official, with a good character and appearance, is someone everyone wishes to associate with, yet you insisted on cutting ties with him, and he later failed, almost dragging you down. Mr. Cheng came unexpectedly, someone everyone ridiculed, yet you decisively accepted him, and have greatly benefited from him. What is the reason for this?" The old man replied, "The reason is right in front of you, but people fail to notice it. He was a scholar-official, and there were established paths to officialdom. Why the rush? His greed can be seen. Greed leads to the downfall of officials, a principle of ancient times. Mr. Cheng wore the trappings of a gentleman, yet he still knew how to cherish and protect, how much more so with vast wealth? Thus, it is known that he took his responsibilities seriously."

    520

    陬邑官亲
    　　
    　　西域之变，大将军福公，奉命进剿。统领巴图鲁，及吉林健旅，声势赫奕，所过州县，以办差不善，登白简者不一。传檄至陬邑，缺既清苦，官亦疲惫，闻前途才干之员，每有失误，心切惶恐，日惟涕泣而已。其官亲某，向司征比，默默无闻。今见其戚官将败矣，不忍坐视，乃谓之曰：“库中有二三百金乎？”令曰：“有之，不敷所用。”曰：“既有之，尚可为也。尽以与我，不问出入，或者无碍前程乎！”大令无可如何之际，姑以听之。时值盛暑炎炎，几至流金烁石，官亲乃与工房相度馆舍，极其宽敞，染古色纸以表糊之，字画皆用旧物。其椅桌以油核桃仁薰作乌木色。

    Explanation

    520

    The Turmoil in the Western Regions and the Official Relative of Zouyi

    The Western Regions were in turmoil, and General Fu, a high-ranking military officer, was ordered to campaign and suppress the unrest. He led the brave warriors (Batulü) and the elite troops from Jilin, with a magnificent and imposing presence. As they passed through various states and counties, many officials were criticized for poor performance and recorded in official documents. A proclamation was sent to Zouyi, a county that was both impoverished and exhausted. Hearing about the mistakes of capable officials ahead, the officials in Zouyi were deeply worried and often cried.

    One of these officials had a relative who was previously in charge of tax collection. This relative was silent and unknown. Now, seeing that his relative's official career was about to fail, he could not stand by and do nothing, so he told him, "Is there two or three hundred gold pieces in the treasury?" The official replied, "Yes, but it is not enough." The relative then said, "If there is some, it can still be done. Give it all to me, without asking about the details, and perhaps it will not harm the future prospects!" The magistrate, having no choice, reluctantly agreed.

    At that time, the intense heat of summer was almost melting metal and burning stones. The official relative then worked with the construction department to measure and design the guesthouse, making it very spacious. They used ancient-colored paper to cover the walls and hang old paintings and calligraphy. The chairs and tables were treated with walnut oil to give them a black wood color.

    521

    以人家用旧之藤竹席片蒸洗一色，按其大小形象，制为引枕靠垫，蓝缎为边，以玫瑰杂杨花代扎实之。其帘幕用深绿色虾须竹为之。其天棚，自村口直接至上房，长有里许。因惜买多席，仅敷一层，恐透暑气，加以盐包杂松毛盖之。其陈设，则山中购小松柏，栽数百盆，夹道而列。所有茉莉花夜来香之属，列在其后，只觉芳馥，而不见形影。公馆后，及左右墙外，三面环列水桶，以竹截作喷筒，伏民夫数百人，各持一筒吸水，向上细细喷之，俾屋上棚间，不干不湿，润泽而已。西瓜为汁，以绢沥之，稍加冰糖薄荷水相和，其凉沁腹。茶用兰芽雪瑞，本系北产，气香味厚，色亦清冽，用沙瓯烹熟，坐于水筒铺，以取温和。

    Explanation

    521

    The old rattan and bamboo mats commonly used in households were steamed and washed into a uniform color, then cut according to their size and shape to make into pillows and cushions. Blue silk was used as the border, and rose petals and willow catkins were used as stuffing. The curtains were made from deep green shrimp whisker bamboo. The ceiling, from the village entrance directly to the main house, was about one li long. Regretting the purchase of too many mats, only one layer was laid down, fearing it might let in summer heat, so salt bags and pine needles were added on top. For decoration, small pines and cypresses were bought from the mountains, dozens of pots were planted, lined up along the path. All jasmine and night-blooming cereus were placed behind, only the fragrance was felt, but not seen. Behind the official residence, and outside the left and right walls, water buckets were arranged in three directions, bamboo was cut to make sprinklers, hundreds of laborers held one each, sucking water and spraying it upward, to keep the roof and ceiling neither dry nor wet, just moist. Watermelon juice was made, strained through a silk cloth, a bit of rock sugar and mint water added, its coolness penetrated the stomach. Tea was made from orchid buds and snow, originally from the north, fragrant and rich in aroma, the color was also clear and cold, brewed in a sand pot, sit on a water bucket mat to get a mild temperature.

    522

    陈甫毕，大将军至矣，令出远迓。将军由皎日之下而来，舆入村口，已觉阴凉；至公馆内，两旁松柏阴森，更觉沉静古穆；并无结彩悬灯，耀目增光之物，心地一爽。坐其铺垫，皆软滑清香。不觉大乐，曰：“天仙界，水晶宫，不是过矣。不意僻陋小邑，竟有是耶！可见人以才能为贵也。”及进西瓜汤，饮兰雪茶，莫名其妙。唤从官入，曰：“此系尖站，并非住宿之所，然我自出都以来，日夜不得偃息，讵肯舍此清凉地，而就火焰山耶？汝等弹压兵役，前往宿站，只须留数人伺应，我五鼓启程来也。

    Explanation

    522

    After the initial arrangements were completed, the great general arrived, and an order was given to go out and welcome him. The general came from under the bright sun, entering the village entrance by carriage, and already felt cool and shady; upon reaching the public hall, the pines and cypresses on both sides were dense and gloomy, making the atmosphere even more solemn and ancient; there were no decorations or lanterns to dazzle the eyes and add brightness, and the heart felt refreshed. Sitting on the cushions, all were soft, smooth, and fragrant. Unable to help but greatly enjoy, he said, "This is no exaggeration of the realm of heavenly immortals or the crystal palace. Unexpectingly, in this remote and humble small town, there is such a place! It shows that people value talent." Upon tasting the watermelon soup and drinking the lansnow tea, he was puzzled by their unusual flavors. He called in his attendants and said, "This is a rest stop, not a place to stay. However, since leaving the capital, I have not had a moment's rest day or night. How could I abandon this cool place to go to the Flame Mountain? You all should control the soldiers, go to the lodging station, and only need to leave a few people to serve. I will start my journey at the fifth drumbeat (early morning)."

    523

    ”传令入，曰：“我食不甘味，睡不贴席者久矣，贤大令造此福地，我今夜宿此，已分外挠扰，勿多备酒席，勿多赏从人银钱，有强索，即告我处置。汝以长才屈于下邑，上司之过也。我当保奏。”令叩首谢出。及进酒席，则雪雁冰参，卤鸭糟鸡之类，只觉香鲜配口，无一毫肥腻气味，亦大醉饱。乐甚，以二千金与令，以赏其费。及起马，令来道谢叩送，将军赞叹不绝而去。令后此果膺保荐，仕至宪司，皆一官亲力也。或曰：才能之士，湮没无闻，惜乎！芗曰：此其小焉者也。古来救君主于垂危，转覆败于俄顷，而史不列其名姓者，如赵之厮养卒，金之两书生；功高不赏，是以名没不彰。其怨恸于九泉耶，抑不屑不洁，如遗逸辈之特隐其名耶？东坡云：“但愿我儿愚且鲁，无灾无害到公卿。”由此观之，是卒与书生，固不屑为公卿也。

    Explanation

    523

    The general summoned the magistrate to enter and said, "For a long time, my food has not tasted good, and my sleep has not been comfortable. The esteemed magistrate has created this blessed place, and I will stay here tonight, which is already causing unnecessary trouble. Do not prepare too much food, and do not give too much money to the attendants. If anyone insists on more, immediately inform me to handle it. You are using your talents in a low-ranking office, which is the fault of your superior. I will recommend you." The magistrate knelt and expressed gratitude before leaving. When the banquet was served, it included delicacies such as snow goose and ice ginseng, braised duck, and pickled chicken. Everything was fragrant and fresh, without any trace of greasy smell, and he was very satisfied and drunk. He was very pleased and gave two thousand taels of gold to the magistrate as a reward for his expenses. When the horse was ready to leave, the magistrate came to thank and bow farewell, and the general praised continuously as he left. The magistrate later indeed received a recommendation and rose to the position of Inspector General, all due to his personal merit. Some said, "Talented people, buried and unheard of, how pitiful!" The author said, "This is a minor issue." From ancient times, saving the emperor in dire straits and turning defeat into victory in a moment, but history does not record their names, such as the attendant of Zhao and the two scholars of Jin; high achievement is not rewarded, therefore their names are not prominent. Do they grieve in the netherworld, or do they disdain to be unclean, like the recluses who hide their names? Su Shi said, "I only wish my son to be simple and unambitious, without disaster or harm to become a high official." From this, it can be seen that these attendants and scholars certainly do not desire to be high officials.

    524

    同胞三鼎甲
    　　
    　　明季之乱，盗贼纵横，畿内几无完邑。有某翁者，乡居而巨富，好行善事，平日造桥修路，给药施棺，无不踊跃从事。及饥馑之年，助赈外，独赡其乡邻者非一次。故遐迩称善人，莫不感激，虽流贼之暴，戕官劫库，亦不忍犯翁家。时有草寇之中伪大将军，掳掠妇女千余人，肆其淫乐。忽奉伪王调取赴敌，伪将不能携带妇女，贼伙商令寄存翁家，因其闲房宽大，而有养赡也。翁不敢辞，受而舍之，给予饮食。贼去后，夜闻妇女悲恸声，翁不忍，欲纵之。入问妇女，有所归否，皆曰：“愿死于道路，不愿生为贼妻也。”翁各给银米，夜使远矣。

    Explanation

    524

    During the late Ming dynasty, there was great turmoil, with bandits and rebels roaming freely, and the regions surrounding the capital city were almost entirely devastated. There was an old man who lived in the countryside and was extremely wealthy. He was known for his charitable deeds, often building bridges and repairing roads, providing medicine and coffins to those in need, and always enthusiastically engaging in such acts. During years of famine, he not only provided assistance but also supported his neighbors more than once. As a result, people far and wide called him a good man, and he was deeply appreciated. Even though the bandits and rebels were known for their violent actions, killing officials and robbing treasuries, they did not dare to attack the old man's home. At one point, a bandit leader who claimed the title of false general seized over a thousand women and indulged in lustful pleasures. Suddenly, he received an order from a false king to go fight the enemy. The bandit leader could not take the women with him, so he agreed to leave them with the old man's house, as it had spacious rooms and the old man was known for his generosity. The old man, though hesitant, accepted them and provided them with food. After the bandits left, one night he heard the women crying, and he could not bear it, wanting to release them. He went in to ask the women if they had a place to go, and they all said, "We would rather die on the road than live as the wives of bandits." The old man gave each of them silver and rice, and at night, he sent them away safely.

    525

    遣仆广收牲畜骨殖，散布各屋内，纵火以焚，嘱邻里勿泄。未几贼归，翁垂涕而告以失火故，贼见瓦砾场上，焦骼残骸无算，信之不疑。旋清兵入，戡定大难，圣主即真，人民复业。翁家三子，皆读书入泮。其大郎娶王氏女，旧族也，得时文真传，能决科第。于归后，索夫窗课阅之，曰：“郎君笔下，超超元箸，惜法脉未清，词华杂凑，师之过也。妾为郎君一点窜间，即入彀矣。”大郎心悦诚服，录示法家，佥曰：“数日不见，学业骤长至此，其入生花之梦耶？”于是二郎三郎，闻嫂氏能，各以文呈教。王夫人曰：“两叔皆天才，文虽不及乃兄元气混沦，然偏师先济，皆科甲中人耳。”亦尽心指导。

    Explanation

    525

    The old man sent his servant to widely collect the bones and remains of livestock, distributed them in each room, set fire to burn, and instructed the neighbors not to reveal it. Not long after, the bandits returned, and the old man shed tears and told them about the fire. The bandits saw the ruins covered with countless charred bones and remains, and they believed him without doubt. Soon after, Qing troops entered and quelled the great turmoil. The true sovereign ascended the throne, and the people resumed their livelihoods. The old man's three sons all studied and entered the school. The eldest son married a woman from the Wang family, an old and respected clan. She mastered the true art of contemporary literature and could decide on the ranking in the imperial examinations. After the wedding, she asked to see her husband's window lessons. She said, "Your writing is exceptionally excellent, but unfortunately, the logical structure is not clear, and the language is a mix of various styles. This is the fault of the teacher. I will make a few corrections, and it will be perfect." The eldest son was sincerely convinced and showed the corrected work to the scholars. They all said, "After a few days, his studies have improved so much. Has he entered the realm of divine inspiration?" Thus, the second and third sons, hearing of their sister-in-law's ability, each presented their writings for her to teach. The Lady Wang said, "Both uncles are geniuses. Their writing, though not as comprehensive as their eldest brother's, is still impressive. They will succeed in the imperial examinations." She also gave them careful guidance.

    526

    三郎甚颖悟，数月间，卓然入妙。旋中式，连捷南宫，探花及第。欲于中门悬匾额，王夫人阻之，曰：“是不能越分，应俟两兄。”命悬于门右，众皆窃笑之，然不敢不从。二郎奋志功名，逾年，亦发榜眼及第，王夫人命悬额于门左。是时大郎犹诸生也，对其妻泣曰：“两弟皆飞腾而去，我恐没世无闻矣。”王夫人笑曰：“偏师先济，妾预言之，郎君勿灰厥志，龙头属老成，自古云然。俟元气充沛，自当超两弟而上之。”是科仅得榜尾。又越三年，果状元及第。泥金报至，戚里无不佩服夫人者。翁大悦，谓之曰：“是皆儿妇之力也。

    Explanation

    526

    The third son was exceptionally sharp and insightful, and within a few months, he had achieved remarkable mastery. Soon after, he passed the imperial examination and successively advanced through the ranks, ultimately becoming the third-place laureate. He wished to hang a plaque at the main gate, but Lady Wang objected, saying, "This cannot exceed one's bounds; one should wait for the older brothers." She ordered that the plaque be hung on the right side of the door, and everyone secretly laughed at it, though they dared not disobey. The second son, determined to achieve fame and success, surpassed himself the following year and also became the second-place laureate. Lady Wang then ordered that his plaque be hung on the left side of the door. At that time, the eldest son was still a student, and he wept to his wife, saying, "My two younger brothers have both risen to great heights, while I fear I will remain unknown forever." Lady Wang smiled and said, "The vanguard army crosses first; I had predicted this. Do not lose heart, for leadership belongs to the experienced, as has always been the case. When your vital energy is abundant, you will surely surpass your two younger brothers." That year, he only managed to secure the last place. However, three years later, he indeed became the first-place laureate. When the gold-embossed imperial edict arrived, everyone in the neighborhood admired Lady Wang. The father was greatly pleased and told her, "This is all due to the efforts of your daughter-in-law."

    527

    今尚有女未婚，我欲得一传胪婿，则尽善尽美矣。”王夫人曰：“文笔之发与不发，妇能决之。两叔鼎甲，亦偶然耳，何能预定传胪耶？虽然，姑请试之。”议开文会，凡邑中之生童未婚者，皆延之，不论贫富。三复而得一生，甚寒苦，翁赘之于家，使王夫人督课之，果得传胪及第。翁自为门联，曰：“一婿传胪今世有，三儿鼎甲古来无。”芗曰：幼时闻吾父吾兄言之凿凿，久而忘其姓氏，姑缺俟补。书曰：“作善降祥。”天之定理。第以年少而决科如神，远于男子，似言者过矣。殊不知翁之所活妇女千余，其精华萃于王氏一身，英灵敏妙，如握宝鉴，如持玉尺，自然大胜凡庸。且冥冥中示果报之功，为善人劝，其何疑耶？

    Explanation

    527

    The father still had an unmarried daughter, and he wished to find a husband who had achieved the rank of zhuānlú (a high-ranking official), then everything would be perfect. Mrs. Wang said, "Whether one's literary talent flourishes or not is decided by the wife. The two uncles achieved the top ranks by chance; how could one predict the zhuānlú rank? Nevertheless, let us give it a try." They decided to hold a literary gathering and invited all unmarried young men in the county, regardless of their wealth. After three rounds of selection, one young man was chosen, who was very poor and suffering. The father-in-law took him into his family and had Mrs. Wang oversee his education. He indeed achieved the rank of zhuānlú. The father-in-law wrote a couplet for the gate, saying, "Having a son-in-law who achieved zhuānlú is rare in this world, but having three sons who achieved the top ranks is unprecedented in ancient times." The author says: When I was young, I heard my father and brother speak of this with certainty, but I have long forgotten his name, so I will leave a gap here to be filled later. The Book of Changes says, "Doing good brings blessings." This is the natural order of things. But it is said that he passed the exams with divine speed because of his youth, far beyond the capabilities of ordinary men, which seems to be an exaggeration. However, it is not known that the father-in-law had supported over a thousand women, and the essence of these women was concentrated in Mrs. Wang, making her exceptionally intelligent and skilled, like holding a precious mirror, like holding a jade ruler, naturally surpassing ordinary people. Moreover, this shows the unseen workings of cause and effect, encouraging good people, what doubt is there?

    528

    义猫
    　　
    　　武林金氏，望族也，代有闻人。有某翁者，救死恤生，利人爱物，至诚恻怛，人皆仰之。然厄于命，年逾强仕，家中落拓。夏日纳凉院中，有饥猫倾侧将毙，翁睹之恻然，自起伺之。从此猫不他往，恋恋依翁侧。翁每饭必食以腥，即外出，必嘱家人尽心爱养，由是猫渐肥健，能捕鼠，而粮无耗矣。是年秋涝，粒米无收。翁家乏食，借贷无门，典质已尽，搔首踟蹰，牛衣对泣而已。猫更无从得食，嗷嗷于侧，小女子责之曰：“人尚无食，汝欲食耶？主人困穷至此，心烦意乱，汝不念平日养育恩勤，何以报德，而反嗷嗷取憎耶？”猫呦然似诺，一跃登屋去。人皆异之，翁亦破涕为笑。未几，猫衔一物掷翁怀中，视之，妇女旧抹额也，上缀东珠二十余，光明圆正，大如芡实，值千金。

    Explanation

    528

    义猫

    The Wulin Jin clan, a prominent family, has had distinguished members for generations. There was an old man who rescued the dying and comforted the living, benefiting people and loving creatures, with utmost sincerity and compassion. People all admired him. However, he was misfortunate due to fate, and by the age of forty, his family was in decline. On summer days, he spent time cooling off in the courtyard. One day, he saw a starving cat lying on its side, about to die. The old man felt pity and got up to care for it. From then on, the cat did not go anywhere else and stayed by the old man's side. The old man always fed it with meat whenever he ate, and even when he went out, he would remind his family to take good care of it. As a result, the cat gradually became fat and healthy, able to catch mice, and the family's grain supply was no longer depleted. That year, there was a flood in autumn, and no grain was harvested. The old man's family had no food, and they could not borrow money. All their collateral was exhausted, and they were scratching their heads and hesitating, crying together in poverty. The cat had no way to get food either and whined beside the old man. A young woman scolded the cat, saying, "People still have no food, do you want to eat? The master is in such dire straits, his mind is in turmoil, and you do not remember the master's kindness and care, how can you repay his kindness, and instead whine to make him angry?" The cat nodded in agreement and jumped onto the roof and left. Everyone found this strange, and the old man burst into laughter. Soon after, the cat brought an object and threw it into the old man's lap. He looked at it and saw it was an old woman's headband, adorned with over twenty oriental pearls, which were bright, round, and as large as lotus seeds, worth a thousand gold pieces.

    529

    翁惊讶失色，一喜一惧，曰：“猫虽通灵，但窃取之物，不但污我品行，且恐失物之家，冤及婢仆，性命攸关，奈何？”其妻女曰：“翁言虽是，但井上之李，岂无主者，廉士尚且取之，所谓饥不择食也。况此物自至，必天神怜翁，假手以济，岂尽狸奴力耶？无已，姑先质资度岁，暗访物主，明告其故而归以质券，似亦无伤。”翁不得已，姑从之。次年遍访，无失物家。或曰：“此巨家殉葬物，年久墓崩，家贫棺坏，则猫取之矣。”或曰：“有心计妇，家有荡子，藏此物于复壁承尘中，为子女谋。

    Explanation

    529

    The old man was shocked and pale, feeling both joy and fear. He said, "Though the cat is clever, the stolen item not only tarnishes my character but also risks implicating the servants of the owner, who might suffer grave consequences. What should I do?" His wife and daughter replied, "Your words are true, but consider the plums on the well—surely they have an owner. Even a person of high moral standing might take them out of necessity, as one does not choose food when hungry. Moreover, this item came to us by chance, surely a sign of divine assistance, not just the cat's doing. If there is no other choice, we will temporarily pawn it to get through the year, discreetly search for the owner, and return it with a pawn ticket as proof of our good faith. It seems harmless." Reluctantly, the old man agreed. The following year, they searched for the owner without success. Some said, "This item might have been part of a burial offering from a wealthy family. After many years, the tomb collapsed, and the family's poverty led to the coffin's decay, allowing the cat to take it." Others suggested, "Perhaps a clever woman, whose husband is a wastrel, hid this item in a secret compartment in the wall for her children's future."

    530

    未及交代，猝病而亡，猫故取之，无碍。”皆是也，要之以神天赏善之说为正。翁闻人议论近理，乃赎而货之，缘是起家。子孙发甲，世承祖训，爱畜猫，食必以腥。有仕至宪司者，署中猫且数十头，出入随从，专有饲猫之职，至今不衰。芗闻而叹曰：人生世上，财可忽乎哉？不但饮食起居，以之自奉：即庭帏行孝，棣萼情联，莫不藉此。甚且至爵可得而鬻也，刑可得而赎焉。以之救济，仁名顿起；以之施与，传为美谈。信乎？金圣叹曰：“名以银成，无别术也。”彼猫乌知之，亦以此取义，且永锡尔类，岂不异哉！

    Explanation

    530

    Before being able to explain, he suddenly fell ill and died. The cat took over by default, without any hindrance. This is all true, ultimately, the belief in divine rewards for goodness is considered correct. The old man heard people discussing this matter in a reasonable manner, so he redeemed and sold it, thereby starting a family. His descendants flourished, continuing the family tradition of loving and keeping cats, feeding them exclusively with meat. There was someone who rose to the position of a constitutional official, and in his office, there were dozens of cats, accompanying him in and out, with a specific role dedicated to feeding the cats, and this tradition has not declined until now. The author, Xiang, heard this and sighed, saying: In life, can wealth be neglected? Not only for daily meals and living, but also for self-sufficiency; even filial piety within the family and familial bonds, none of these can be achieved without wealth. Moreover, titles can be bought, and punishments can be redeemed. Using wealth for charity, the reputation of benevolence suddenly rises; using it for giving, it becomes a beautiful story. Is it not true? Jin Shengtan said: "Fame is made by silver, there is no other method." How could the cat know this? It also takes meaning from this, and bestows forever upon its kind, is it not strange?

    531

    李老
    　　
    　　恒山李老。农家者流，有地数顷，称小康。中年生一子，名曰壹。稍长附学读书，督课极严。壹时年十二，游嬉误学，畏父师训责，窃资逃去。李老夫妇情急，悬金以购，搜索无所不至，迄无影响。其母哀痛迫切，几致轻身。李老犹以年齿正强，可望生育慰之。然妇已思子成疾，屡劝置妾延嗣，李老不忍。光阴迅速，瞬逾十年，年将古稀，仍无所出。宗族之贫苦者，咸思争继，哓哓不休，益厌苦之。自度精力尚强，且值旱涝不匀之岁，闻韩魏间售子女者值甚廉。李老携百金往，投人牙，以清钱五十贯，择得端庄小女子，大称心怀。

    Explanation

    531

    Li Lao of Hengshan. A farmer by profession, he owned several acres of land and was considered moderately well-off. In his middle years, he had a son named Yi. As the boy grew older, he was enrolled in school to study, with his father supervising his studies very strictly. When Yi was twelve, he played and was idle, thereby neglecting his studies, and feared the reprimands of his father and teacher. He secretly took money and ran away. Li Lao and his wife, desperate, offered a reward in gold, searched everywhere without exception, but up to now, there was no trace. His mother fell ill due to missing her son and was on the verge of taking her own life. Li Lao, considering his own strength and energy still strong, hoped to have more children to comfort himself. However, his wife had fallen ill due to missing their son and often urged him to take a concubine to continue the family line, but Li Lao was unable to bear it. Time passed quickly, and in an instant, ten years had gone by. He was approaching seventy and still had no offspring. The poor and needy relatives all thought of contesting for succession, arguing incessantly, which made him even more tired of it. Considering his own strength and energy still strong, and in a year of uneven drought and flood, he heard that the selling of children in the regions of Han and Wei was very cheap. Li Lao carried a hundred gold pieces to go, went to a broker, with fifty strings of cash, and chose a modest and proper young girl, greatly pleasing his heart.

    532

    女叩以姓名籍贯，翁实告之，讶曰：“妾乃与翁同姓同乡，异哉！”李老曰：“同姓或有之，乡则路隔五百里，难言同也。”女曰：“幼闻吾父言悉矣。云系李姓，名壹，恒山人，因逃学出，为人义子。亲父母在乎否乎，念诵涕泣。妾与母时慰劝之。”李老亦讶曰：“据汝言，确是吾子也，汝当为我孙女。幸言之早，速赴尔家验之！虽相隔十余年，声音笑貌，应不改也。”遂携女至村，呼女父出，果李老子也。哭述所由，云：“逃出后，惘惘南奔，资用告罄，乞食此村。有老人同姓，蓄为义子，为我娶妻，连生四丁二女。义父母相继殁，逢此歉岁，故卖女度日耳。”李老大悦，命子货其家具，携其子孙男女八人归。其妻孤苦零丁，抱病而卧，闻夫归，忽然子孙满堂，不觉跃然而起。
    　　
    　　丁养虚

    Explanation

    532

    The girl inquired about the old man's name and hometown, and he indeed told her. Surprised, she said, "I am from the same surname and hometown as you, how strange!" The old man replied, "It is possible to share the same surname, but our hometowns are separated by five hundred miles, so it is difficult to say we are the same." The girl said, "I have heard my father say it all. He said he was surnamed Li, named Yi, from Hengshan. He ran away from school and was adopted as a son. Do his parents still live? He would recite their names and cry. I and my mother often comforted him." The old man was also surprised and said, "According to what you said, he is indeed my son, and you should be my granddaughter. Fortunately, you mentioned it early, go quickly to your home to verify it! Even though we have been separated for over ten years, his voice and appearance should not have changed." So he took the girl to the village and called out the girl's father, who turned out to be the old man's son. He cried and recounted the reason, saying, "After running away, I wandered aimlessly to the south, and my resources ran out. I begged for food in this village. An old man of the same surname adopted me as his son, married me to a wife, and we consecutively gave birth to four sons and two daughters. The adoptive parents passed away one after another, and we encountered a famine year, so we sold the daughter to make ends meet." The old man was very happy, ordered his son to sell their furniture, and took his eight children and grandchildren back home. His wife, alone and destitute, was lying sickly in bed. When she heard her husband had returned, she suddenly found her children and grandchildren all around her, and could not help but leap up.

    　　Ding Yangxu

    533

    吾师丁养虚先生，奇伟人也。学围棋于施湘霞，传瑶琴于郭去非，皆国手也。先生入其堂奥，且精于奇门禽遁之学。能以拳石筑小山，为桥梁亭榭；栽径寸松柏，郁郁茸茸，有天然之致。山巅悬瀑布一道，穿桥曲折泻落，承以磁盆，水流循环，昼夜不绝。有欲窃其机巧者，拔起观之，不得，仍置盆内，水止不流。经先生拨弄，依然洋溢。殆按八门生死法耳。好事者愿重价购之，不肯售。问其故，曰：“入他人手，不过旬日，水法不灵矣。人必以我为欺，我不愿贻人口实也。”一日谓家人曰：“盗将入我室，宜慎防之。”至晚，以椅凳十余，纵横排列院中，遥望之，似有烟雾弥漫。先生嘱闭门，许在窗隙潜窥。夜深人静，见一壮者持械越墙下，潜入凳内，钻爬逾跃，费尽伎俩，现诸身段，家人不禁大笑。资似侧耳，欲遁，至天明不能出。先生去其一椅，突于缺外逃，为众擒缚。先生曰：“休矣，彼一夜辛劳，未得一物，而供我玩戏，勿复苦之。”乃释缚。

    Explanation

    533

    My teacher, Mr. Ding Yangxu, was a remarkable and extraordinary person. He studied Go with Shi Xiangxia and taught the guqin to Gu Qufei, both being national masters in their respective fields. Mr. Ding mastered the inner workings and profound aspects of these arts and was also skilled in the study of divination techniques, including the Eight Trigrams and other esoteric practices. He could build small mountains with fist-sized stones, creating bridges and pavilions within them; he planted pine and cypress trees as small as a few inches, which grew lush and dense, giving a natural charm to the landscape. At the summit of the mountain, a waterfall hung, flowing through the bridge in a winding manner, supported by a porcelain basin, with the water flowing in a continuous cycle, day and night.

    Those who wished to steal its secrets tried to lift it to examine it but failed. When they put it back in the basin, the water stopped flowing. After Mr. Ding manipulated it, the water flowed again abundantly. It was likely due to the principles of the Eight Trigrams and the laws of life and death. Those who appreciated it offered a high price to buy it, but he refused to sell. When asked why, he said, "If it falls into the hands of others, it will not function properly after just ten days. People will think I am deceiving them, and I do not wish to provide them with grounds for accusation."

    One day, he told his family, "Thieves will enter my house; be careful and guard against them." In the evening, he arranged more than ten chairs and benches in the courtyard, placed them randomly, and when viewed from a distance, it seemed as if there was a mist弥漫. Mr. Ding instructed them to close the door and allowed them to secretly peek through the window cracks. In the stillness of the night, they saw a strong man with weapons climbing over the wall, sneaking into the chair arrangement. He crawled and jumped around, trying various tricks, and his movements made the family unable to hold back their laughter. The man seemed to be listening intently, trying to escape, but he could not get out until dawn. Mr. Ding removed one chair, and the man suddenly escaped through the gap, only to be caught and bound by the others. Mr. Ding said, "Enough, he spent the whole night in labor, but did not get anything, and provided us with entertainment. Do not make him suffer further." He then released him.

    This translation aims to be faithful to the original text while maintaining natural and readable English. It preserves the cultural and historical context, handles idiomatic expressions appropriately, and ensures consistent terminology for proper nouns and technical terms.

    534

    先生呼食啖之，问其故，曰：“吾侪三人，小人技最精，故先入，见宅门内，房屋壮丽。跃入，四面皆墙，或逾之，或穴之，愈进墙愈多，但闻人声，难觅出路。正惶恐间，忽见门开，冲突被擒，小人知罪矣。”问彼二人何不入，对曰：“闻宅内嗤嗤不绝，知有备，先遁矣。”先生笑曰：“归语尔曹，勿再至我家，恐墙多，一夜爬不尽矣。”盗不解其故，唯唯谢去。时二子皆冠，尚未婚娶，计非千金不能毕事。无已，乃藉新春设酒肆，用法择吉。

    Explanation

    534

    The master called for food and ate it, asking the thief why he had entered. The thief replied, "We three, I am the most skilled among us, so I entered first. I saw inside the house, the rooms were magnificent. I jumped in, and all around were walls; some climbed over them, some dug through them. The more we went in, the more walls there were. We only heard people's voices and could not find a way out. Just when we were frightened, suddenly we saw a door open, and we were caught in a struggle. We know we are guilty." The master asked the other two why they did not enter, and they replied, "We heard inside the house, the sound of laughter never stopped. We knew there was a trap, so we fled first."

    The master laughed and said, "Go back and tell your companions, don't come to my house again. Fear the walls are too many, you can't climb them all in one night." The thief did not understand the reason and nodded and left.

    At that time, both sons were adults, but they had not yet married. They calculated that without a thousand gold pieces, they couldn't complete the wedding. Having no other choice, they then used the New Year to set up a wine shop, using methods to choose a good day.

    535

    凡奇门家验正时到，必天地人三才皆应，斯于谬误。先生择某月日寅时，应天微雪，地色白，先有一人青衣红带持壶沽饮，后有文武二官星过门，即刻开张大吉。至日五鼓，起呼家人，备祀神物。先生率二子悬灯开炉，果微风飘雪，有皂隶着色服，持锡壶，叩关入，曰：“冷甚，幸汝店早开，藉得御寒，甚善。”先生询其夜深应差故，役曰：“都督阅边，舟抵马头，从本官往迎耳。”问知总制系文殿撰出身，所随中军参将，系武状元，始悟即二星官也。役去未几，仪卫八驺，呼拥而过。先生即陈祭礼，鸣爆竹，开市。

    Explanation

    535

    When the expert in the art of Qi Men verifies the auspicious time, it must correspond to the three talents of heaven, earth, and humanity, thus avoiding error. The master chose a certain month, day, and hour (寅时), and it was a time when the sky had a light snowfall, the ground was white, and first there came a person in a blue robe with a red sash, holding a flask to buy drinks. Then, two officials, one civil and one military, passed by the gate, and immediately the shop opened with great auspiciousness. On the appointed day, at the fifth watch of the night, the master called the family and prepared offerings for the gods. The master led his two sons to hang the lanterns and open the furnace. Indeed, there was a light breeze with snowflakes, and a low-ranking official in colorful clothing, holding a tin flask, knocked on the door and entered, saying, "It is very cold; thank you for opening your store early, which helps me stay warm, which is very good." The master asked about the reason for being on duty so late at night, and the official replied, "The governor-general is inspecting the border, and the boat has arrived at the ferry landing. I am following the official to greet him." Upon learning that the governor-general was a graduate of the literary examination and the middle-ranking general was a graduate of the martial examination, the master realized they were the two stars. Not long after the official left, eight attendants in ceremonial dress passed by with shouts and a crowd. The master then set up the offerings, set off firecrackers, and opened the market.

    536

    从此沽饮者，虽倍于前，千金究难骤至也。因自开烧锅，价廉而味美，贩客厚获赢余，远近争趋之。每晨停其门者，肩挑百十。期月得利千余金，势不能止。风闻入邑宰耳，当是时，烧锅之禁甚严，将搜索酿具而罪之。幸族人为吏得信，急为之备。其蒸甑所制铜壶，式甚高大，重百余斤，或谋埋之，或谋毁之，先生曰：“否否，势不及，必致败露。”率家人共举其壶于厅事，众目共睹之地。妻孥方疑讶间，宰已入搜。先生进内宅。

    Explanation

    536

    From now on, those who bought and drank, though doubled compared to before, still found it difficult to quickly accumulate a thousand gold pieces. Therefore, he opened his own distillery, offering cheap and delicious drinks. Merchants made substantial profits, and people from far and near rushed to buy. Every morning, dozens of people with shoulder poles would stop at his door. In a month, he made over a thousand gold pieces, and the momentum could not be stopped. The news reached the ears of the county magistrate. At that time, the ban on distilleries was very strict, and they were going to search for brewing equipment and punish him. Fortunately, a relative who was an official got the news and quickly prepared. The copper pot used in the steaming vessel was very large and heavy, weighing over a hundred pounds. Some suggested burying it, others suggested destroying it. The master said, "No, no, it's not possible to do that; it will definitely be exposed." He led his family to lift the pot into the hall, where everyone could see it. His wife and children were just wondering when the magistrate entered to search. The master went inside the inner house.

    537

    叱役穷搜，甚至掘地发墙，毫无踪影。宰出坐厅事，侍从吏役百余，壶明明在案间，无一睹者。宰去，集家人从容碎，熔化灭迹。或问何以官不能见，先生曰：“搜远不搜近，偶然耳。”予知先生不肯泄机，必用六戊藏形法也。遂为子毕姻，改酒房为钱局，俾二子掌之。先生以琴棋自娱，不与俗事矣。其妻父朱氏，为邑名医，子四人，或继业，或设肆，因此起家。无赖之徒，觊其有肆，欺诬讹诈，四子苦累。教子读书应试，凡入庠者，可支门户，盼望綦切。

    Explanation

    537

    The servants were ordered to search thoroughly, even digging up the ground and dismantling walls, but found no trace of it. The magistrate came out and sat in the main hall, with over a hundred officials and servants in attendance. The pot was clearly on the table, yet not a single person saw it. After the magistrate left, the family gathered and quietly broke the pot, melting it down to destroy the evidence. Someone asked why the official could not see it, and the master replied, "They searched far but not near, it was just an accident." I knew the master would not reveal the secret, so he must have used the Six Wu method to hide it. He then arranged a marriage for his son, changing the wine room into a money office, and had his two sons take charge of it. The master entertained himself with music and chess, no longer engaging in mundane affairs. His father-in-law, Zhu, was a famous doctor in the county, with four sons who either continued the family business or set up shops, thereby rising in status. Unscrupulous people, coveting their businesses, cheated, slandered, and extorted them, causing great hardship. The master taught his sons to study and take exams, and those who entered the school could support the family, with great hope for their future.

    538

    时朱翁考终，将葬，舅以葬期谋于先生，曰：“姊夫明晰阴阳，能为人福，使我子侄一人入泮，举家感甚。”先生敬诺：“择冬月某日未时，应天微雨，二狗衔花戏墓侧，一男子戴铁帽，一孝妇索取石灰，此正时也。举棺封壤，孙必游庠。”诸舅皆哗，曰：“冬月唯有梅花，品甚贵重，狗乌能得？铁帽唯军中有之，太平之世，谁敢戴此？恐未必如所算。”先生正容曰：“我亦不能预知，不过依书为断。我尽心为岳父推选，恐未至大谬。

    Explanation

    538

    When Master Zhu passed away, preparations for his burial were underway. The uncles consulted the scholar about the timing of the funeral, saying, "My brother-in-law has a clear understanding of yin and yang, and can bring good fortune. If he can ensure that one of my nephews or nieces enters the school, the entire family would be very grateful." The scholar respectfully agreed, "I will choose a specific day in the winter month during the hour of the horse. On that day, there should be a light rain as if sanctioned by heaven, two dogs carrying flowers playing by the tomb, a man wearing an iron hat, and a virtuous woman requesting lime. This will be the auspicious time. When the coffin is placed and the earth is sealed, my nephew will surely enter the ranks of scholars." The uncles all exclaimed, "In the winter month, only plum blossoms are in bloom, which are highly valued. How could dogs possibly carry flowers? Iron hats are only found in the military, and in a peaceful era, who would dare to wear such a hat? I doubt these conditions can be met." The scholar responded gravely, "I cannot predict the future, but I will rely on the book to make my decision. I have done my best to select a good time for my father-in-law, and perhaps it won't be too far off the mark."

    539

    ”至日，果小雨，舅已奇其言，舆榇入茔，停待正时安穴，见两小犬，争芦花一枝来墓间；有农夫买得一锅，戴于首上，以代雨具；孝妇亦至。诸舅大悦，推棺闭冢。次年，孙入泮。芗曰：先生以琴棋名噪一时，当道富室，重聘争迎，不应，高士也。其术数之学，老益韬晦，有求之者，曰：“我亦犹夫人耳，从宪书指与一黄道日而已。”予从学琴，见案头奇门，谨请指教。先生卜曰：“孺子尚可，”随指示奇壬布局法，不以术告也。叩之，曰：“此在自己研究，非师可授也。况以此炫人，恐有杀身之祸，何苦乃尔？”予不敢渎。今先生逝矣，惜秘学失传，谅下愚无德以承受之耳。

    Explanation

    539

    On that day, it indeed rained lightly, as the uncles had found his prediction remarkable. They carried a coffin into the tomb, waiting for the proper time to settle the burial place. They saw two small dogs fighting over a branch of reed grass at the tomb; a farmer bought a pot and wore it on his head as a substitute for rain gear. The filial daughter-in-law also arrived. The uncles were greatly pleased, pushed the coffin into the tomb, and closed it. The following year, the grandson entered the academy. Xiang said, "The master became famous for his skill in playing the qin and chess. Powerful officials and wealthy families offered high salaries to invite him, but he did not accept, showing his high moral character. His knowledge of divination and numerology became even more concealed with age. Those who asked him said, 'I am just like anyone else, and I follow the calendar to choose a favorable day.'" I studied the qin from him and saw the Qi Men on his desk, respectfully asking for guidance. The master divined and said, "The young man is still capable," then showed the method of Qi Ren layout but did not reveal the technique. When I asked further, he said, "This must be studied on your own, not taught by a teacher. Moreover, using this to show off may lead to death, why would you do such a thing?" I dared not be disrespectful. Now that the master has passed away, it is a pity that the secret knowledge has been lost, knowing that I lack the virtue to inherit it.

    540

    阮封翁
    　　
    　　翁，江南仪征人，性恻隐，好义，壮为鹾伙，岁八百余金，往往恤亲友，一挥数十金，贫不能赡妻子，泊如也。一日至广陵，转运鹾务，市不戒于火，被焚千余家，赤贫者无力赁屋，男妇老幼皆露处。忽澍雨滂沱，立泥淖中，相向而哭。翁触目伤心，计极穷人不过百十，费无多，思有以援之。急趋至商家，谋所安置之法。商人以翁人微言轻，志大妄举，相与讪笑之，无肯为筹划者。翁忿且愧曰：“诸君既不顾乡谊，我虽非郡人，亦当独任。”咸笑曰：“汝大才有力，愿好为之，弗累我等，此功德无量也。”翁去，向居停作秦庭之哭，求预假薪俸数十金，呼匠为席棚百十间，俾贫民避风雨。欢呼感激，祈天为善士降祥。是年翁应得劳金，俱预支费讫，徒手归，几不能卒岁。幸其子为诸生，肄业书院，聪明才知，为当道所器重，均有赠遗，藉以苦度。

    Explanation

    540

    The Benevolent Old Man Ruan

    The old man, from Yizheng in Jiangnan, was compassionate and devoted to righteousness. In his youth, he worked as a salt merchant and earned over eight hundred taels of silver annually. He often provided relief to relatives and friends, spending dozens of taels at a time, though he was poor and unable to support his wife and children, remaining content with poverty. One day, while in Guangling, he was overseeing the transportation of salt when a fire broke out in the city without warning, burning down over a thousand households. The destitute had no money to rent houses, and men, women, the elderly, and children were all exposed to the elements. Suddenly, a heavy rain poured down, and they stood in mud and water, crying facing each other. The old man felt heartbroken at the sight, calculating that the extremely poor were no more than a hundred or so, and the cost would not be much. He thought of ways to help them and hurriedly went to the merchants to plan a way to settle them. The merchants, considering him of low status and little influence, with grand ambitions and reckless actions, laughed at him mockingly and no one was willing to help plan. Angered and ashamed, the old man said, "Since you all disregard our hometown ties, even though I am not from this prefecture, I will undertake it alone." They all laughed and said, "You have great talent and strength, wish you good luck, do not burden us, this will bring infinite merit." The old man went to his host and wept like the people of Qin, asking for an advance on his salary of several dozen taels. He called carpenters to build a hundred or so thatched shelters to allow the poor to shelter from wind and rain. The community cheered and thanked him, praying for blessings to be bestowed upon the virtuous man. That year, the old man's wages, which he should have earned, had already been spent in advance, and he returned with empty hands, barely able to make it through the year. Fortunately, his son was a student at a school, studying at a traditional Chinese educational institution. Intelligent and knowledgeable, he was highly regarded by the local authorities and received gifts and donations, which helped the family manage to endure the hardships.

    541

    未几乡会俱捷，入词林，大试皆前列，简在帝心，典试学差，旋畀以封疆重任，巡抚浙江。翁受三品封而殁。服阕后，节制两粤，入赞黄扉，晋封太傅。天道福善，有以也夫。或曰：吾子持福善祸淫之说，每遇封翁，无不啧啧称叹，似矣。然如颜子渊德行，首列四科，宜其身膺显爵，子孙昌盛。何以身前屡空，且夭殁，又无贤裔，是天道不可问久矣！芗曰：否否，圣贤虽不可以常理论，然颜子独善其身，不屑兼善天下故也。尝读《韩诗外传》，孔子谓回曰：“家贫居卑，胡不仕乎？”对曰：“回有郭外之田五十亩，足以给粥；郭内之田四十亩，足以为丝麻；鼓琴足以身娱；学于夫子者，足以自乐。回不愿仕也。”由是观之，品则高矣，而无益于苍生，天故报以配享圣殿，俎豆千秋，又世袭博士以奉之，正所以酬其德也。吾屡验天道，总以泽及于人者，因之速得美报。所述诸封翁，德未必如颜子，而膏泽下于民，更觉难能而可贵也。

    Explanation

    541

    Not long after, he succeeded in both the provincial and metropolitan examinations, entered the literary world, and ranked among the top in the grand examinations. He was favored by the emperor, appointed as an examiner or educational official, and soon entrusted with the heavy responsibility of a frontier governor, serving as the governor of Zhejiang. The old man received a third-rank title before he passed away. After mourning his death, he was given command over Guangdong and Guangxi, entered the inner court to advise the emperor, and was promoted to the position of Grand Tutor. The way of heaven rewards virtue, and indeed it does. Someone said, "You uphold the view that virtue is rewarded and vice is punished, and whenever you encounter a man of virtue, you always comment with admiration, as if it were true." However, take the case of Yan Hui, whose moral conduct was ranked first among the four categories, and he should have held a high rank and his descendants should have flourished. Why did he live in poverty, die young, and have no virtuous descendants? This shows that the way of heaven has been unanswerable for a long time! I say, "No, no, even sages cannot be judged by ordinary standards, but Yan Hui excelled in his own virtue and did not seek to benefit the world." I once read the Han Shijing Wai Zhuan, where Confucius said to Yan Hui, "Your family is poor and your status is low. Why don't you seek an official position?" Yan Hui replied, "I have fifty acres of land outside the city walls, which is enough to provide me with food; forty acres of land inside the city walls, which is enough for silk and hemp; playing the zither is enough to amuse myself; studying with you, Confucius, is enough to bring me joy. I do not wish to seek an official position." From this, we see that his moral character was high, but he did not benefit the common people. Therefore, the heavens rewarded him with honor in the temple of sages, eternal sacrifices, and hereditary doctors to serve him, which is the way to repay his virtue. I have often tested the way of heaven, and in general, those who benefit others quickly receive good rewards. The men of virtue I have described may not have been as virtuous as Yan Hui, but their benefits to the people make them even more admirable and valuable.

    542

    赵甲
    　　
    　　晋人李某，在京师佐典商，岁入俸钱三百缗。有同乡赵甲者，与相识，无事业，谋欲设杂货肆而无资。商于李，慨然以百金付之，曰：“姑以此试，得意后，与吾合业可也。”一言为约，并无文券，无人知。未几，李以疾卒，典主呼其子来，扶榇而返。适赵甲置货他出，归后，知李已卒，为位哭奠。由是兴旺，不十年，业隆数万。李子家中落，衣食不充，亲党咸劝外出营谋，卜之大吉。适有人入都者，相伴而去。至旧典主处，与父执求引。都有人知赵与李故交，今赵业大兴，往求之，谅可录用。挽友往说。赵闻李子来，欣然款接，曰：“我因尔父，得有今日。我觅子久矣，奈无音耗；今日来投，若有神使。”纳为主帐，而不议劳金。

    Explanation

    542

    Zhao Jia
    　　
    　　A man from Jin named Li, assisted in the business of a merchant in the capital, earning an annual salary of three hundred strings of cash. There was a fellow townsman named Zhao Jia, who knew Li, had no business, and planned to set up a miscellaneous goods shop but lacked funds. Zhao Jia discussed this with Li, who generously gave him a hundred gold pieces, saying, "Try this temporarily, and if you succeed, we can combine our businesses." They agreed on a verbal contract without any written documents, and no one else knew about it. Soon after, Li died of illness, and the head of the household called his son to come and carry the coffin back. At the time, Zhao Jia had placed goods elsewhere and returned home. After learning that Li had died, he set up a place for mourning and offered incense. His business then prospered, and within ten years, it became very wealthy with tens of thousands of assets. Li's son's family declined, and they were struggling with insufficient clothing and food. Relatives and friends all advised him to seek new opportunities, and divination indicated it was very auspicious. Just then, there was someone going to the capital, and he accompanied him. They arrived at the old head of the household's place and sought an introduction from his elders. Everyone knew that Zhao and Li were old friends, and now Zhao's business was thriving. They went to seek Zhao out, believing he could employ them. Zhao invited a friend to go and speak on their behalf. When Zhao heard that Li's son had come, he warmly received him, saying, "I owe your father for my success today. I have been looking for you for a long time, but there was no news; now that you have come, it feels as if a divine messenger sent you." He accepted him into the main account without discussing wages.

    543

    李子安于初学，亦不计较，尽心竭力，随同营运。赵见其辛勤克苦，出入无私，顾而乐之，曰：“子已弱冠，能自成家，应议婚娶，为嗣续计。”李子曰：“侄依伯父为生，尚无进益，何敢娶妇自取累乎？”赵曰：“姑缓亦是。但尔既为主帐，应将我所有总计之，现存若干。”李子唯唯。数日查毕，现资并货物，合计六万余。赵曰：“与剖之，我与子应得其半。”李子骇曰：“伯父何出戏言？侄在此数年，伯父周给衣食，感无既矣。矧在小郎之列，本不应得劳金，纵伯父怜而恤之，不过年例数十贯钱而已，何若是之多耶？伯父子孙振振，非无人承受者，侄何敢越分？敬辞。”赵笑曰：“子无却也，我自有法。”乃设盛宴，邀其荐主及乡里长者咸集，李子亦在座。

    Explanation

    543

    Li Zi, while in his early studies, did not consider himself above manual labor but instead worked diligently alongside others in business. Zhao saw his hard work and selflessness, and was pleased with him, saying, "You have reached adulthood; you should be able to start your own family and consider marriage for the continuation of your lineage." Li Zi replied, "I rely on my uncle for my livelihood and have not yet made any progress; how dare I marry and burden myself further?" Zhao said, "Let's wait a bit. But since you are now in charge of the accounts, you should tally the total assets I have, and see how much remains." Li Zi nodded in agreement. After several days of checking, the current assets and goods totaled over sixty thousand. Zhao said, "Let's divide it equally; you and I should each receive half." Li Zi was startled and said, "Uncle, how can you be joking? I have been here for several years, and you have provided me with food and clothing; I am deeply grateful. As a younger relative, I should not even receive wages for my labor. Even if you pity and care for me, it would only be an annual allowance of dozens of strings of cash. How could it be so much? Your descendants are numerous, and there are many capable ones to carry on the family; how dare I overstep my bounds? I respectfully decline." Zhao smiled and said, "Do not refuse; I have my own method." He then arranged a grand feast, inviting his main benefactor and respected elders from the community, and Li Zi was also present.

    544

    三杯后，赵谓众曰：“某昔落魄京师，人皆明白。李故友虽与我素交，并无瓜葛。一言之下，慨然助以百金，不立契券，是诚信我也。我由此起家，而李兄已逝。当时有与我合群之说，既有此语，获利理应均分。我初晤李侄时，本欲表白，恐少不更事，入手挥霍去也。今见其勤俭，能自经营，我何敢负李兄于地下乎？”乃出李子所开单目，曰：“请诸公作主匀分。”众顾李子曰：“赵伯世所难得，君有福哉！窭人顿成巨室，吾等借酒以贺。”李子曰：“诸公且止，听我一言。赵伯所云，并无凭证，是欲为义士耳。侄虽年幼，亦不敢取非义财。即亡父果存百千钱，以远年债，一本一利，取二百贯足矣。

    Explanation

    544

    After three cups of wine, Zhao addressed the crowd: "In my past days of hardship in the capital, everyone understood my situation. My old friend Li, though we had been friends for a long time, had no close ties with me. At a word, he generously lent me a hundred gold coins without a written contract, showing his honesty and trustworthiness. I rose to wealth because of this, but my friend Li has since passed away. At that time, there was talk of pooling our resources. Since we made this agreement, the profits should be divided equally. When I first met Li's nephew, I intended to explain my situation, but I was concerned that he might be young and inexperienced, and squander the money. Now seeing that he is diligent and thrifty, able to manage his own affairs, how dare I betray Li in the afterlife?" Zhao then produced a list prepared by Li's nephew and said, "Please, gentlemen, decide on the fair distribution." The others turned to Li's nephew and said, "Zhao, a man of such integrity is rare in the world, and you are fortunate indeed! A poor man has become wealthy, and we will celebrate with wine." Li's nephew replied, "Gentlemen, please listen to me. What Zhao says lacks proof, and he wishes to act as a righteous person. Although I am young, I dare not accept unjustly gained wealth. If my father had indeed left behind a hundred thousand coins, after accounting for old debts with principal and interest, two hundred strings of cash would be enough."

    545

    多即非义，何敢自污？”赵笑而入，命群仆以三万数百金出，曰：“今日交清，卸吾重肩，唯子所欲。”李子取其百金而出，追之，遁矣。赵乃邀众作证，呈报城坊，求访恩主。有司异其事，行文山右，唤李子至，质明判给。李子曰：“吾侪小人，实无功德，不劳而得多金，暴富不祥，故不敢纳也。”官曰：“无已，今某庙久圯，汝其葺之，非功德乎？”二人叩谢去，争出布施，庙貌焕然一新，官易庙额，号曰“双义，”而颁赵甲以额曰：“重义轻财。”芗曰：晚近之世，至亲分家不均，甚至争讼，从未闻让财而逃，官访恩主者。不意市井小民，竟超出乎世家之上。忆成案载：雍正六年，豫民崔氏有拾秦太遗银一百七十两，访归原主，毫不受谢。制军田文镜入奏，奉旨予七品顶戴，赏银百两，以旌其善。赵甲之事在后，当事者宜援此例官之，以昭盛世之典，而示天下以法，惜乎未及此也。

    Explanation

    545

    "Excess is not righteousness; how dare I defile myself?" Zhao smiled and entered, ordering his servants to bring out 30,000 to 400 taels of silver, saying, "Today we settle the debt and relieve my heavy burden; do as you wish." Li took 100 taels and left, pursued but escaped. Zhao then invited witnesses to verify the matter and reported it to the local authorities, seeking to find the benefactor. The officials were amazed by the incident, issued a document to Shan You, summoned Li to court, and judged the case at dawn. Li said, "We are mere commoners, truly without merit. To gain wealth without effort is an ill omen, so I dare not accept it." The official said, "If there is no other way, this temple has been dilapidated for a long time. You should rebuild it; isn't that a merit?" The two knelt and thanked him before leaving, competing to donate money. The temple was restored to its former glory, and the official changed the temple's name to "Double Virtue" and bestowed the title "Values Righteousness Over Wealth" upon Zhao Jia. Xiang said, "In recent times, even among close relatives, dividing property unevenly can lead to disputes, and I have never heard of someone giving up money and fleeing, let alone seeking recognition for a benefactor. Unexpectedly, common people have surpassed the gentry in moral virtue. I recall a case from the sixth year of Yongzheng: a man from Henan named Cui found 170 taels of silver left by Qin Tai, returned it to its rightful owner, and did not accept any reward. General Tian Wenjing submitted a report, and the emperor granted him a seventh-rank hat, awarded him 100 taels of silver, and commended his virtue. Zhao Jia's case followed, and those in charge should follow this example and reward him, to demonstrate the virtues of a prosperous era and set an example for the world. Unfortunately, it did not reach this point."

    546

    转女为男二则
    　　
    　　吾乡史苕楣明经，壮年博学，功名未遂，改业钱谷，为袁简斋明府所识拔，因入其幕。终任后，在八闽为历任方伯所倚重，修脯丰厚，慷慨好施，济贫拔蹇，极一时之雄豪，坐上食客常满。及其老也，望孙念切。子妇怀孕，未卜男女。时有精歧黄之道，不屑以医名者，苕楣延诊。其人曰：“脉主得女，然吾翁之善，可回天意，请竭吾术，使转为男，以报知己。第阳茎须移一肢改造，得男必缺一肢，翁愿之否？”苕楣诧曰：“先生之学，素所敬佩，不意竟能化女为男。无已，请移其足指，无碍观瞻，更妙。”其人曰：“不能。上可移下，下不能上。再三筹度，唯两手小指无用，可以挪借。”苕楣欣然诺谢。

    Explanation

    546

    Final Translation

    Two Cases of Turning a Girl into a Male

    My hometown's scholar, Shi Tao Mei, was well-versed and ambitious in his youth, but his fame and success remained unfulfilled. He changed his profession to finance and grain administration and was recognized and promoted by Yuan Jianzhi, a prominent official, and thus entered his entourage. After completing his term, he was highly trusted by successive provincial governors in Fujian, receiving generous remuneration. He was generous and charitable, helping the poor and lifting those in distress, and was renowned as a hero of his time. His house was always full of guests. As he grew old, he deeply desired a grandson. His wife was pregnant, but the gender was unknown. At that time, there was a skilled practitioner of traditional Chinese medicine who did not seek fame as a doctor. Shi Tao Mei invited him for a diagnosis. The doctor said, "The pulse indicates a girl, but with my master's skill, we can change fate. Please allow me to use all my techniques to turn it into a boy, to repay my friend. However, the penis must be moved and transformed by one limb, and the boy will be missing one limb. Will Master agree?" Shi Tao Mei was surprised and said, "Your skills are always admired, and I never expected you could turn a girl into a boy. If there is no other way, please move one of his toes, as it will not affect appearance and would be even better." The doctor replied, "That is not possible. Upper limbs can be moved to lower limbs, but not vice versa. After careful consideration, only the little fingers of both hands are useless and can be moved." Shi Tao Mei gladly agreed and thanked him.

    547

    遂设炉炼药，佩服兼行。及期，果产男孩，手仅八指。见客腆，宛如闺阁中人。及长，羞涩更甚。有欲验其指者，则啼而匿，为同人所噱。昔苕楣与吾师父王公交善，方其归也，王谓之曰：“阁下与当世名公友，而仅诸生；资至巨万，而仍寒士。依然故我，徒劳无功，窃为君不取也。”苕楣大笑曰：“吾之出也，有一肩行李，笔墨而外无他物。不数年，出入公卿间，推贤让能，有为予荐拔置身青云者，有赖予仰事俯蓄裕衣食者，各若干人，畴不企史善人之号，亦可以自豪矣。足下欲予坐拥万镒，纳资为郎，方谓吐寒士气耶？此守钱虏所为，卑卑者何足道！”其胸襟之阔达如是，宜其遇转女为男之奇士，此其中盖有天道也。

    Explanation

    547

    He then set up a furnace to refine medicine, practicing both taking and wearing it. By the time the period arrived, he indeed gave birth to a boy, who had only eight fingers on his hand. In front of guests, he was shy, as if he were a lady from the inner quarters. When he grew up, he was even more shy. Those who wanted to examine his fingers would cry and hide, and were laughed at by his peers. Formerly, Tao Mei was on good terms with my master Wang, and when he was returning, Wang said to him, "You have contemporary famous officials as friends, but are still just a scholar; your wealth reaches millions, but you are still a poor scholar. Still the same as before, in vain and without achievement, I secretly think it is not worth it for you." Tao Mei laughed loudly and said, "When I left, I had only a shoulder bag, with nothing but writing materials. In a few years, I moved among high officials, promoting the worthy and yielding to the capable. There were those who recommended and elevated me to high positions, and those who relied on me for sustenance and livelihood, various numbers of people, who do not aspire to the title of a good person in history, can also take pride in themselves. You want me to sit and possess millions of taels, to pay money to become an official, only then can you call it shedding the air of a poor scholar? This is what a money-hoarding bandit would do, such a petty person is not worth mentioning!" His broad and open-mindedness was like this, and it is appropriate that he encountered a strange man who transformed a woman into a man, for there must be a heavenly principle in this.

    548

    姑苏有老翁，富而无嗣，仅生一女，及笄，病笃，医皆束手。翁不惜重资，聘名医叶天士诊之，笑曰：“是非病也，肯以若女为我女，且从我游，百日后，还阁下以壮健者，非复娇弱之态矣。如迟疑不决，是翁自杀之。死非正命，良可哀也。”翁诧曰：“诚如是，愿以千金送阁下。”天士携归，另洁密室，选婢之美而艳者，使伴女宿，嘱曰：“此汝姑也，终身依倚在是，顺姑无违；稍有拂逆，致增其病，唯汝是问。

    Explanation

    548

    In Suzhou, there was an old man who was wealthy but had no heir, having only one daughter. When she reached the age of fifteen, she fell gravely ill, and all doctors were at a loss to treat her. The old man, not sparing heavy expenses, hired the famous physician Ye Tianshi to diagnose her. Ye smiled and said, "This is not an illness. If you are willing to let your daughter be my daughter and follow me around, after a hundred days, I will return to you a healthy and strong one, no longer in a delicate and weak state. If you hesitate and are indecisive, it is like the old man committing suicide. Dying is not a natural death, and it is truly pitiful." The old man was surprised and said, "If it is truly like this, I am willing to give you a thousand gold pieces." Ye Tianshi took her back, prepared a clean and private room, chose a beautiful and charming servant, and made her accompany the girl to sleep. He instructed and said, "This is your aunt. Rely on her for life. Obey your aunt without disobedience. If there is any slight disobedience, it will worsen her illness, and only you will be held accountable."

    549

    ”于是日给药饵，恒往观之，见女体渐壮，容渐舒，与婢情好日密，形影相随，知事已遂，遽入其室，迫喝婢曰：“汝与姑所作何事，我窥觇洞彻，必尽言之。如敢隐讳，将以刑求，勿自苦也！”婢视女而泣，女忸怩曰：“婢之伴我，翁之严命，如违应责，顺何罪耶？”婢因曰：“是主陷奴也。以郎君伪称义女，而使奴同衾共枕，违既不敢，从又获咎，置身何地？”天士大笑曰：“已顺从姑夫耶？方为汝喜，岂汝责耶？”速女改装，去发而辫之，以药展其弓足，衣冠履舄，居然美男子。
    延其父至，告曰：“阁下以子为女，伪疾诳我，误使义女伴之，今为其所乱，将如之何？”翁愕然不解所谓。乃使小夫妇出拜，翁顾而大乐，愿以婢为儿妇，与天士结为姻娅，往来无间。芗曰：变女为男之法，见于《医经》。史以盛德而遇良医，理所应得，无足怪者。唯叶所治之女，其《医经》所载之五不男耶？名曰天、捷、妒、变、半。

    549

    Thereupon, he provided her with daily medicine and nourishment, constantly going to observe her. He saw that her body gradually grew stronger, and her appearance became more relaxed. Her relationship with the maid grew closer day by day, and they were inseparable. Realizing that the matter had been accomplished, he quickly entered her room and forcefully called out to the maid, "What did you and my wife do? I have seen it all clearly. You must tell me everything. If you dare to hide anything, I will use torture to extract the truth. Do not make yourself suffer!"

    The maid looked at the girl and wept, while the girl felt embarrassed and said, "The maid's duty to accompany me was the strict command of the old man. If I disobeyed, I would be punished; but if I obeyed, what crime did I commit?"

    The maid then said, "It is the master who has entrapped the slave. He pretended to be my foster daughter and deceived me with a false illness, making me share the same bed and pillow with him. If I disobeyed, I would be punished; but if I obeyed, I would also be blamed. Where am I to stand in this situation?"

    Tian Shi laughed loudly and said, "Have you already submitted to my husband-in-law? I am only happy for you; how could it be your fault?" He quickly had the girl change her clothes, cut her hair and braided it, and used medicine to stretch her bound feet. He dressed her in formal attire and shoes, and she looked like a beautiful man.

    He invited her father to come and said, "You pretended your son was your daughter and deceived me with a false illness, mistakenly making me take the foster daughter as a companion. Now she has been corrupted by him. What should be done about it?" The old man was startled and did not understand what was being said. Then, he had the young couple come out to pay respects. The old man looked at them and was very pleased, wishing to marry the maid to his son and make a marital alliance with Tian Shi, with no barriers in their interactions.

    Xiang said: The method of changing a woman into a man is described in the Medical Classic. The historian, with great virtue, encountered a good doctor, which is reasonable and not surprising. But is the girl treated by Ye one of the five types of men described in the Medical Classic? They are named as Heaven, Swift, Jealous, Change, and Half.

    550

    任冲不盛，宗筋不成曰天；值男即女，值女即男曰捷；男根不满，似有似无曰妒；半月能男，半月能女曰变；虽有男根，不能交媾曰半。此五等人，状貌血气，本具男形，唯任冲二脉不足，似男而不成其男，为父母者误认作女。年至十六，气足神旺，阳事兴矣，郁不得发，是以病笃。幸遇名医，充以妙药，诱以所欲，自然阳茎突出，不复女矣。吾意五不男中，唯天阉外，皆可以药救也。故见于《医药经》。奈世鲜精其技者。叶天士，医药名家，固其宜也。而史公所遇之人，其学亦不亚于叶，名逸不传，惜哉！由是推之，天下之抱绝技而隐没者，不知凡几，岂仅学医之道为然哉！

    Explanation

    550

    In cases where the Ren and Chong meridians are weak and the primary sinews are undeveloped, it is called "Tian" (天); if the sex changes from male to female or vice versa, it is called "Jie" (捷); if the male organ is underdeveloped and appears intermittently, it is called "Du" (妒); if the sex alternates every half month, it is called "Bian" (变); and if there is a male organ but it cannot be used for sexual intercourse, it is called "Ban" (半). These five conditions involve individuals who, in terms of physical appearance and constitution, are essentially male, but due to insufficient Ren and Chong meridians, they do not fully develop as males and are mistakenly identified as females by their parents. By the age of sixteen, when the body's energy and spirit are strong, sexual desire or function has developed, but if it is suppressed and cannot be expressed, it leads to serious illness. Fortunately, they may encounter a famous doctor who provides effective medicine and uses the patient's desires to encourage treatment, resulting in the natural erection of the penis and the individual no longer identifying as female. My opinion is that among the five types of male infertility, only true impotence (天阉) cannot be treated with medicine. Therefore, this information is recorded in the Medical Classics. Unfortunately, few people today are skilled in this medical knowledge. Ye Tianshi, a renowned medical expert, is well-suited for this task. The person encountered by the historian, whose skills are comparable to Ye Tianshi's, is not widely known, which is unfortunate. By this reasoning, there are many hidden talents in the world, not just in the field of medicine.

    551

    卷三
    　　
    　　妓笃故谊
    　　
    　　黄沙陈星堂，近从安南归帆，客囊充溢，盖居奇所获，已累数千金矣。陈于归装后，朋辈为其洗尘，设花酌矣。座客有劝其挟妓者，陈叙前因曰：“仆尝于此三出三入，非此无由落魂，然亦非此无从有今日。”众请其说，陈曰：“话本太长，言之缕缕，恐阻诸君酒兴，不如且理觞政，俟酒阑灯烬时细言之，为诸君醒酒。”其后获闻详细者，皆叹妓丽春之于陈，实生死而肉骨也。先是陈固千金子，然以渔花柳，沃产悉售于人，遂致孓然一身，几等于丐。丽春者，前所识之妓也，陈尝助百数十金为其削籍。嗣别数载，杳不相闻，忽见之于新桥市，时陈已落魄如鬼矣。乍睹丽春，讶为面善，而苦无由忆。丽春忽停步询之，陈蓦然心动，始忆为春。春问其近况何如，胡为至此，陈曰：“风流落魄，羞见故人。”春曰：“此非深谈地。

    Explanation

    551

    Chapter 3

    　　
    　　The Bond of Old Friendship with a Courtesan
    　　
    　　Chen Xingtang from Huangsha recently returned from Annan by boat, his travel bag full of valuables, having accumulated thousands of gold pieces. After his return, his friends entertained him, arranging a luxurious banquet. One of the guests suggested he bring a courtesan, and Chen recounted his past experiences: "I have come and gone here three times. Without this, I would have no reason to lose myself, but also, without this, I would not have reached this point." The others asked for more details, and Chen said, "The story is too long, to tell it in detail might dampen your spirits. It might be better to manage the drinking rules first, wait until the wine is finished and the lamps are extinguished to tell it in detail, to help you sober up." Later, those who heard the full story all marveled at the profound impact that the courtesan Li Chun had on Chen, bringing him back from the brink of despair. Originally, Chen was a wealthy man, but by indulging in the pursuit of women, he had sold all his assets and ended up destitute, almost equal to a beggar. Li Chun was a courtesan he had known before, and Chen had once helped her with a hundred or so gold pieces to clear her name. After parting ways for several years, they lost contact, until Chen suddenly saw her in a market in Xinqiao. By then, Chen was as destitute as a ghost. Upon seeing Li Chun, he found her face familiar but could not recall why. Li Chun suddenly stopped and asked him, and Chen felt a stirring in his heart, remembering her. She asked about his recent circumstances and why he was in such a state, and Chen replied, "I have fallen from grace, ashamed to see old acquaintances." Li Chun said, "This is not a place for deep conversation."

    552

    ”乃导至贤思里一室中，陈设颇丽，婢媪参前。盖春前已从良，夫殂丧，颇有所积，然孤影无依。一遇陈，不觉前情在念。嗣审其流落之故，叹曰：“君痴于情者。然日夕置身爱河中以为事，安得不罹灭顶乎？今日知悔乎？且君向所厚者，曾有能为君地者乎？”陈曰：“此辈身不由己，况亦不足责报。我于前事，视之如水流花谢耳。但今春梦已觉，倘获立足境，当视此为畏途矣。”春曰：“君能改辙，亦何忧无立足地？”乃露自荐意，拟重寻啮臂之盟。陈闻之，如从九幽十八狱再睹人世，然不敢直应，乃嗫嚅曰：“此固三生幸事，但恐为卿辱耳。”春乃叙前事，言良人已死，历两载，不敢妄意从人；君固旧好也，况曾受露覆，今岂容坐视漂溺乎？于是与陈同处，誓以后事。迨审其持重，乃以数百金，使陈从友行贾于安南，兹果发迹。

    Explanation

    552

    Then, he led her to a room in the village of Xiansi, where the furnishings were quite beautiful, and female servants attended her. It was said that Spring had reformed before, having been a virtuous woman, but her husband had died, and she had accumulated some savings. Yet, she was now alone and without support. Upon meeting Chen, she unconsciously recalled her past feelings. Later, after investigating the cause of her wandering, she sighed and said, "You are foolish in love. However, you spend day and night immersed in the river of love, how can you avoid being overwhelmed? Do you regret it today? And those you once considered close, were any of them able to stand by you?" Chen replied, "These people are not in control of their own lives, and it is not enough to demand recompense from them. Regarding past events, I see them as water flowing and flowers falling. But now that I have awakened from my spring dream, if I find a place to stand, I will regard this as a dangerous path." Spring said, "If you can change your course, why worry about not finding a place to stand?" Then, she revealed her intention of self-recommendation, planning to renew their oath of biting the arm. Chen heard this and felt as if he had emerged from the nine depths and eighteen hells back into the world of the living, but he dared not respond directly. He hesitated and said, "This is indeed a fortunate event in three lives, but I fear it will bring disgrace to you." Spring then recounted her past, saying that her dear husband had died two years ago, and she dared not recklessly follow others. "You are my old friend, and I have received your protection before. How could I sit idly by and watch you drift and drown?" Thus, she lived with Chen, vowing to help him in the future. After observing his seriousness, she gave him several hundred gold coins, sending him to trade with friends in Annan, where he indeed achieved success.

    553

    破镜重圆
    　　
    　　何明达，粤西桂林人，前岁商于岭南，挟资数千，频年亏折，所存者千金以下耳。何因旅况无聊，偶随朋友作曲巷游。过眼烟花，何本不甚着意；不料于柳絮沾泥之际，卒复莲花并蒂之缘。先是何随友辈在新堪地买醉，花天酒地，骚兴悠然。座客陈某，谓此地过于征逐，虽金迷粉醉，究不合雅人幽会，不若暗里平康之得静中乐趣也。众谓其迂腐，嗤之曰：“君素不履莺花庭院，只合于罗刹鬼窟，独拥母夜叉作人髓饮局耳。”陈曰：“君等滥矢风流，实花月场中饕餮。但士各有见，君等不见静中之趣，犹余不见闹中之乐也。自今往，请扬镳分道。

    Explanation

    553

    A Broken Mirror Reunited

    He Mingda, a native of Guilin in the western region of Guangxi, had been a merchant in the southern region of Lingnan the previous year, carrying capital in the thousands, but had suffered frequent losses over the years, leaving him with less than a thousand gold. Feeling bored from his travels, he occasionally accompanied friends on a visit to the back alleys. Though passing beauties did not particularly interest him, unexpectedly, at a time when willow catkins were settling in the mud, he found himself reunited with a twin-lotus bond. Previously, he had accompanied friends in a new place to get drunk, living a luxurious and indulgent lifestyle with a leisurely mood for poetry. A guest named Chen remarked that the place was too much about chasing after things, even if one was intoxicated by gold and beauty, it ultimately did not suit the refined meeting of scholars, and it was better to find the quiet pleasure in the secluded brothel. The others called him old-fashioned and mocked him by saying, "You have never stepped into the courtyard of singing and dancing, you are only fit for the lair of demons, alone with a fierce woman as a partner for a feast of human flesh." Chen replied, "You all are falsely claiming to be elegant, you are truly gluttons in the world of flowers and moon. But each scholar has his own perspective, you do not see the joy in the midst of the chaos, just as I do not see the quiet pleasure. From now on, please part ways."

    554

    ”陈与何颇相莫逆，嗣遂招之作游侣，偶于仙城巢云别馆，即私娼院也。陈所契者，名翠莲，颇风韵，略知染翰。镇日于碧桐窗下，作茂猗书法，见之者颇许为香国翘楚。是日陈何踵院，何见翠莲颇具林下风姿，隐相倾羡。倏见有淡妆素服丽人，从帘外过，莲瓣纤小，逼近吴寸趾；其举止哀艳，又若江采苹，楼东独步焉。陈指以问翠莲，莲曰：“此求售者，以其选择良苛，尚未经有主顾。”陈请荐诸何，倩翠先容。翠曰：“事必无成。”陈曰：“女元龙太过，高自位置，岂欲得王孙贵价耶？”翠曰：“非也。伊有隐衷，难以尽说。”何曰：“请道其略。”翠谓：“伊遭家难，鬻身至此，然不屑作柳絮随风。

    Explanation

    554

    Chen and He were very close friends, and Chen subsequently invited He to be his traveling companion. They happened to visit the Cloud Nest Pavilion in the fairyland city, which was actually a private brothel. Chen had a friend named Cui Lian, who was graceful and knowledgeable in calligraphy. She spent her days practicing the elegant style of Wang Xizhi under the green tamarind window, and those who saw her often praised her as the most beautiful among the beautiful. On this day, Chen and He visited the brothel, and He noticed that Cui Lian had a graceful and refined demeanor, which he secretly admired. Suddenly, they saw a beautifully dressed woman passing by the curtain, with delicate and small feet, resembling the ideal of small feet. Her movements were sorrowful and graceful, much like Jiang Caiping, walking alone on the eastern side of the building. Chen pointed her out and asked Cui Lian about her. Cui Lian replied, "She is a woman seeking to sell herself, but she is very selective and has not yet found a suitable buyer." Chen then asked Cui Lian to introduce her to He, and he requested that Cui Lian make the introduction. Cui Lian said, "The matter will surely not succeed." Chen responded, "The woman is too proud and holds herself in high regard. Is she seeking a high price from a nobleman?" Cui Lian said, "No, she has hidden feelings that are difficult to fully explain." He asked, "Please tell me the general idea." Cui Lian explained, "She suffered a family misfortune and sold herself to come here, but she does not want to be like willow catkins floating in the wind, easily influenced by the wind."

    555

    院主怜其幽淑，听其备价自赎。苟有作泥中援拾者，伊宁委身事之。若作道旁花相视，伊不愿也。”陈曰：“伊肯与人接谈否？”翠曰：“与予颇相倾慕，谓予能知其苦也。”何曰：“伊果系遭难坠溷，复能卓然自立，此有志节女也。如可赎，愿倾囊相助。”莲走告之，携入见何等，告诉苦衷。据言“本姓谭氏，某宅之育女也。夫隶西粤，姓王名基，客游至此，于前岁娶妾，以事被逮于官，客囊耗去，需百金始能出狱。妾念夫妻大义，鬻身以成之。迨夫脱狱，妾遂坠火坑矣。又念身本清白，以为夫故，不惜自鬻；但不能作章台柳，攀折由人。蒙院主深怜，许我自赎。

    Explanation

    555

    The head of the institution, moved by her virtue and gentleness, allowed her to redeem herself by paying the price. If there were anyone who would rescue her from the mud, she would entrust herself to their care. But if she were to be treated as a flower by the roadside, she would not wish for that. Chen asked, "Would she be willing to converse with others?" Cui replied, "She has a strong admiration for me, believing that I understand her suffering." He said, "If she indeed suffered misfortune and fell into prostitution, yet could still stand out and be self-reliant, this is a woman of strong principles. If she can be redeemed, I am willing to help with all my resources." Lotus went to inform her, bringing her to meet He, and she told her story. She said, "I am originally from the Tan family, a woman raised in a certain household. My husband, Wang Ji, was a guest from the Western Yue region. He came here last year, married me, and was arrested for some matter, leaving his travel funds depleted. He needs a hundred gold pieces to be released from prison. I thought of the great bond of marriage and sold myself to help him. When my husband was released from prison, I fell into the fire pit. But I also thought that I was originally pure, and for the sake of my husband, I did not hesitate to sell myself; but I could not be treated as a willow on the Chang'an Road, to be picked and plucked by anyone. I received deep compassion from the head of the institution and was allowed to redeem myself."

    556

    苟能脱妾于苦，当作婢以图报耳。”何闻之，不胜慨曰：“尊夫与吾同籍，不忍见其家属流落，如可赎，即措资相助。但不知尊夫已西旋否？”妇曰：“半月前闻伊尚逗留司院街。”何曰：“待吾访确，使复团圆，何如？”妇闻，崩角在地，曰：“皇天后土，实闻此言；粤海冤禽，复逢比翼。非君之力不至此。”迨何果于司院街，访得妇原夫，为道其故，助资使往赎妇。夫妻重会，不啻破镜重圆。此癸酉三月事也。吁！何本客途失意，而竟于狎游中作此义举，诚可谓高谊薄云矣。该妇缘夫自鬻，已分生离；然能脱却陷井，则虽琵琶另抱，犹胜作倚门侣也。不谓偶遇黄衫，遂使红颜复归故主，妇人之大幸，亦事之罕见欤！

    Explanation

    556

    If you can free me from my suffering, I will serve as a maid to repay your kindness." Hearing this, He was deeply moved and said, "Your husband and I are from the same region; I cannot bear to see his family in hardship. If it is possible to redeem her, I will provide the funds to help. But I do not know if your husband has already returned home." The woman replied, "Half a month ago, I heard that he was still lingering on Siyuan Street." He said, "I will go and confirm this. Let us reunite him, what do you say?" The woman, upon hearing this, fell to the ground in despair and said, "May the heavens and earth bear witness to these words; the unfortunate birds of the South Sea have been reunited.
