Without your help, I would not have reached this point." Indeed, He went to Siyuan Street and found the woman's former husband. He explained the situation and provided the funds to redeem her. The couple reunited, and it was as if a broken mirror had been mended. This event took place in the third month of the year Guiyou. Alas! He, who had been out of luck on his journey, ended up performing such a noble act in a place of leisure. Truly, his noble friendship is as thin as clouds. This woman, because her husband had sold himself, was destined to be separated from him; yet, if she could escape from a trap, even if she took up another lover, it would still be better than being a woman who waits at the door. Who would have thought that a chance encounter with a man of high status would bring the beautiful woman back to her original master? This is a great fortune for women and a rare event indeed.

    557

    珠妓殉情
    　　
    　　区容阶，楚产也。厥祖作贾穗城，遂家焉。父某生三子，容生越七日而父丧，母抚三子成立。长子弱冠而夭，次子行贾，往来楚粤间；惟容习举子业，思以诗书起家。讵所与游者，多非益友，酒食游戏相征逐者，趾错于途；纵有二三老成持重者，施以针砭，而一暴十寒，终归无益。容遂与此狎邪游辈，相依为命。眷珠江花舫九娘，每一留醉，累旦达霄。凡得九娘一言，则挥金如土，不少靳也。

    Explanation

    557

    A Pearlservant Sacrifices Her Life for Love

    Qu Rongjie was born in Chu. His ancestor engaged in trade in Guangzhou (Canton) and settled there. His father had three sons, and Rongjie was born seven days after his father's death. His mother raised the three sons to independence. The eldest son died young at the age of twenty, the second son traveled as a merchant, moving between Chu and Yue (modern-day Hubei and Guangdong); only Rongjie studied for the imperial examinations, hoping to establish his family through poetry and books. However, those with whom he associated were mostly not good friends, engaging in drinking, feasting, and playing, competing with each other, and filling the roads; even if there were a few mature and responsible individuals who gave him advice, their efforts were inconsistent and ultimately of no benefit. Rongjie thus became closely associated with these immoral companions, relying on them for survival. He was attached to a courtesan named Jiu Niang on a pleasure boat on the Pearl River. Every time he stayed drunk with her, he would spend the entire day and night. Whenever Jiu Niang spoke, he would spend money like water, without any hesitation.

    558

    月圆之夜，偕友开宴珠海，作团圆会，花满，酒满，月满，无殊极乐世界也。岂期乐极生悲，容于酒阑灯，未免有情，芙蓉帐里，竟犯不治之症。甫晓，以肩舆归。亭午，神魂遂返极乐世界。母妻儿女，痛哭殊常。检其所佩绣囊，有并命鸳鸯金钱，知系花柳场中谛盟所得者。将访诸其友，欲识容所与谛好者，系属何人。殊九娘一闻容殒命消息，悲不欲生，追思情况，神思如醉。其母思所以慰之，拉诸姊妹设筵作“解愁会”。九娘以酒沥江中曰：“一滴何曾到九泉，念及死者，何忍下咽！”语毕，清泪双垂，诸姐妹叹其情深，皆为坠泪。

    Explanation

    558

    On the night of the full moon, he gathered with friends for a banquet in Zhuhai, holding a reunion gathering. The flowers were abundant, the wine flowed freely, and the moon was full, making the scene no different from the Pure Land. Who could have foreseen that extreme joy would lead to sorrow? When the wine had run out and the lamps had been extinguished, it was inevitable to have feelings, and in the luxurious bed, he unexpectedly contracted an incurable disease. Just as dawn was breaking, he returned in a sedan chair. By midday, his soul returned to the Pure Land. His mother, wife, and children cried with extraordinary grief. They examined the embroidered bag he was wearing and found a coin with a design of mandarin ducks, knowing it was obtained from an oath in the world of courtesans. They planned to seek out his friends to identify those with whom he had sworn an oath, and to whom he was closely bound. Especially when Nine Miss heard the news of Rong's death, she was so grief-stricken that she could not bear to live. Recalling the circumstances, her thoughts were as if drunk. Her mother thought of ways to comfort her and gathered her sisters to hold a banquet called "the Relief of Sorrow Gathering." Nine Miss poured wine into the river and said, "Not a single drop reached the netherworld. Thinking of the deceased, how could I swallow it?" After speaking, tears streamed down her face. Her sisters sighed at the depth of her feelings, and all wept.

    559

    宴撤后，九娘五内无主，不止鱼玄机“易求无价宝，难得有情郎”之叹也。其母虑其有变，刻意防闲。九娘寸心已定，遂欢容笑黛如平时。至十七夜，欲觅洋药自裁，而防范过严，杳不得行。遂检奁间，偶得鸦片灰数钱，以茶和饮之。比母知而救之，已无及矣。九娘遗言，愿得附葬容墓，俾得泉下相依，免令孤魂无主。其母以九娘系属亲生，勉从其志，告诸容友。至双鸳之冢，其成否未遽定也。呜呼！九娘能以身殉情人，亦可一洗青楼薄幸之丑。

    Explanation

    559

    After the banquet, Jiu Niang felt disoriented, not just sighing over the Tang Dynasty poet Yu Xuanji's lament: "It's easy to find an invaluable treasure, but hard to find a true lover." Her mother, fearing a change in her behavior, took strict precautions. Jiu Niang had already made up her mind, so she maintained a cheerful face as usual. On the seventeenth night, she tried to find opium to commit suicide, but the precautions were too strict, and she failed. She then searched through her belongings and found a few pennies' worth of opium ash, which she mixed with tea and drank. By the time her mother found out and tried to save her, it was too late. Jiu Niang left a message, wishing to be buried alongside Rong's grave, so they could rely on each other in the afterlife and avoid the fate of a wandering soul without a master. Her mother, considering her a daughter, reluctantly followed her wishes and told Rong's friends. They reached the double swan tomb, but whether it would be completed or not was uncertain. Alas! Jiu Niang could sacrifice herself for her lover, and this could also cleanse the shame of the courtesan's unfaithfulness.

    560

    然为士者以有用之身，而漫置诸无用之地，识者尤谓不可，矧殉之以命哉！昔人咏云：“二十四友金谷宴，三千里外石城游。人间无此繁华乐，无此繁华无此愁。”此言石崇宴饮，动费数万金；炀帝出游，随行数万众。乃一则亡家，一则亡国，其乐将安在哉？是知贪快活者，必招烦恼；极闹热者，必变凄凉。故君子以安享和平为福，画阁灯红，不如山头月白；华筵扇乱，不如林下风清。眼界放开，自有天然真景。彼沾沾于歌舞场中，恣意钗裙，适情弦管，自谓得无上趣矣。沈石田尚书一语道破曰：“脂粉两般迷眼药，笙歌一路败家声。”

    Explanation

    560

    Yet those who are scholars with useful bodies, are casually placed in useless positions, and those who understand especially believe it is not acceptable, how much more so if one sacrifices one's life for it! An ancient poem says: "A gathering of twenty-four friends at Jin Valley, a journey of three thousand miles to Shicheng. There is no such pleasure in this world, nor is there such pleasure without such sorrow." This refers to Shi Chong's extravagant feasts, which often cost tens of thousands of gold pieces; Emperor Yang of Sui's travels, accompanied by tens of thousands of people. One led to the downfall of his family, and the other to the collapse of his state. Where will their joy be? It is known that those who seek pleasure will inevitably invite trouble; those who reach the peak of excitement will inevitably turn to desolation. Therefore, the virtuous person finds peace and harmony as a blessing, the red lights of painted pavilions are not as good as the white moonlight on the mountain; a lavish feast with chaotic fans is not as good as the clear breeze under the forest. If one broadens their horizons, they will naturally see true scenes. Those who indulge in the dance halls, freely enjoying themselves with women and music, think they have found the ultimate pleasure. Shen Shitian, the Minister, said in one sentence: "Lipstick and powder are two types of eye-catching cosmetics, and music and songs lead to the downfall of a family."

    561

    四川某贾轶事
    　　
    　　蜀商某者，受主人托，代为营运。红白花蜡，贩负来申，计资八千金。讵料某自抵申后，被玉餐珠，花天酒地，俨然贵介弟，来作狎邪游也。昵某姬，有白头约，具千金脱其籍。将谋金屋，倾想吴门，遂偕乡人郑某，买棹姑苏，营宅虎阜，盖已尽其资矣。川中得耗，乃东间关而至，冀收桑榆。乃燕去鸿来，中途相左，茫茫前路，唯有涕泗滂沱而已。某则摒挡既毕，复来申迎姬，将图偕老。至则有酒食征逐者流，以情告某。某乃匿迹姬家，料量衣饰，急拟赴苏潜避。日者，舣船江岸，某与姬肩舆二乘，鱼贯而出。

    Explanation

    561

    四川某贾轶事
    　　
    　　A merchant from Sichuan, entrusted by his master to manage business affairs, brought red and white wax to Shanghai, investing eight thousand taels of silver. Unexpectedly, upon arriving in Shanghai, he indulged in luxurious food and drink, living a life of debauchery and luxury, appearing like a noble young man engaging in licentious pursuits. He befriended a certain concubine, had a promise of marriage until old age, and spent a thousand taels to redeem her from servitude. Planning to marry her, he longed for Suzhou, and thus accompanied his fellow villager Zheng to buy a boat to Suzhou, where he built a house on Tiger Hill, having spent all his capital. Upon receiving news from Sichuan, he traveled eastward through difficult terrain, hoping to salvage the situation in his old age. However, just as swallows left and geese arrived, they met in the middle of the journey, and the future ahead was uncertain, with nothing but tears streaming down. The merchant then completed all preparations and returned to Shanghai to meet the concubine, planning to live together until old age. Upon arrival, there were those who sought him for feasts and revelry, and they told him about the situation. He then hid his identity at the concubine's home, calculated the value of her clothes and ornaments, and quickly planned to flee to Suzhou in secret. One day, he docked the boat at the riverbank, and he and the concubine rode in two sedan chairs, emerging one after another in an orderly manner.

    562

    某之车不下帘钩，防人识面也。当路出宝善街，适丹桂园演剧将散之际，车马拥挤，久不得前，而某之帘衣已坠，真面已呈。某方肆漫骂，而乃东正侧足其间，趾而望之，伊人宛在。于是挽以下车。某曰：“事已至此，请从我登舟榷算耳。”将抵吴门，某以阿芙蓉膏毕命。姬念前因后果，一旦成空，不觉泪下沾衣，于是夕亦仰药舟中，合欢地下云。乃东将其衣饰变卖，以为收敛之费，而八千金则飞去无迹矣。方乃东之来也，时值炎暑，人皆纱罗，而彼犹大布为裳，则平日之俭朴可想。乃以己之节啬，而供人之挥霍，守钱奴其亦何以为情哉？

    Explanation

    562

    The narrator's carriage did not lower its curtain hooks to prevent people from recognizing his face. On the road, passing through the Baoshan Street, just as the play at the Dan桂 Garden was about to end, the carriages and horses were crowded, and he could not move forward for a long time. By then, the narrator's curtain had fallen, and his true face was revealed. The narrator cursed wildly, and just as he stood in the midst of the crowd, looking at closely, that person was still there. He then pulled him down from the carriage. The narrator said, "The matter has reached this point. Please come with me to the boat to settle the accounts." About to reach Wumen, the narrator ended his life with opium paste. The lady, reflecting on the cause and effect, found everything suddenly empty, and she could not help but shed tears, wetting her clothes. That night, she also took poison in the boat, hoping to reunite in the afterlife. Na Dong sold his clothes and ornaments to cover the expenses for the burial, while the eight thousand gold coins vanished without a trace. When Na Dong came, it was during the hot summer, and everyone wore light clothing, but he still wore coarse cloth, indicating his daily frugality. Thus, he used his own frugality to supply others' extravagance; what kind of affection is that, a miser?

    563

    记瘦腰生眷粤妓莲真事
    　　
    　　瘦腰生，鉴湖旧家子也，随父宦粤，眷一姬名莲真，弱不胜衣，使舞掌上，当不减汉宫飞燕也。而双翘之瘦小，仅若红菱一角，更足令人魂销。姬有母，恃姬为钱树子。而生又簪缨门第，不知稼穑之艰，举凡火齐木难，为姬取给，半年而资用不支。惧见责于亲庭，来沪上游，作避地计。繁华如梦，回首都非。虽不能摒绝路柳墙花，而曾经沧海，除却巫山，月下花前，常有抚今追昔之感。姬亦念生无虚日。适珠海有花丛之禁，风月无边，瞬作烟霞过眼。姬言于母曰：“郁郁处此，何以为生？曷迁地为良耶？”实则意不忘生也。航海来沪，僦居老旗昌之遇祥楼。日候楼头，而生杳无音耗。一日夜漏二下，生有友邀饮其家，赌酒斗歌，声传户外。

    Explanation

    563

    The Tale of Lotus True, the Beloved Concubine of Thin-Waisted Man

    Thin-Waisted Man was a young man from an old and distinguished family in Jianshui, who followed his father to serve in Guangdong. He had a concubine named Lotus True, who was so frail that she could hardly support the weight of her clothes. She would dance on the palm of his hand, her grace and elegance no less than that of Zhao Feiyan, the legendary beauty of the Han dynasty. Her slender legs, thin and small, were like a single piece of a red water chestnut, captivating the hearts of all who saw her.

    Lotus True had a mother who relied on her daughter as a source of wealth. Thin-Waisted Man, coming from a family of high social status, was unaware of the hardships of farming and labor. He lavished her with precious gems and jewels, spending lavishly to support her. After half a year, his finances were depleted. Fearing reprimand from his family, he came to Shanghai to seek refuge. The prosperity he once enjoyed seemed like a fleeting dream upon his return. Although he could not entirely avoid the temptations of the world, the memories of his past experiences made his current life seem less significant. The moonlit nights and flower-filled gardens often filled him with a sense of nostalgia for the past. Lotus True also thought of him constantly. Coincidentally, there was a prohibition on certain activities in Zhuhai, where the beauty of nature was boundless, but it was like a fleeting glimpse of mist and clouds. She told her mother, "Being unhappy here, how can I survive? Why not move to a better place?" In truth, she still thought of Thin-Waisted Man. She sailed to Shanghai and rented a residence in the Yuxiang Building of the old company Laqi Chang. She waited at the top of the building every day, but there was no news of him. One night, when the clock struck two, Thin-Waisted Man was invited by a friend to a gathering at his home, where they gambled, drank, and sang, the sounds echoing outside.

    564

    姬审听之，曰：“意中人固在斯也，何其声之相似耶！”搴帘一睇，遽倒生怀，掩泣不能成语，良久始曰：“前情俱在，君竟水流花谢，置身月地花天耶？曾一念及莲真，尚在风尘沦落否？”慰藉良久，破泣为欢。洗盏更酌，留宿姬家。如是半月余，生不言归，姬亦未以缠头相索。会生有族叔赴京陛见，欲挈生偕行。生语姬，姬曰：“宴安鸩毒，不可怀也。妾洞悉君今非昔比，恐君以妾贫富易心，故半月以来，伴君岑寂，以表妾心。

    Explanation

    564

    The courtesan listened carefully and said, "Indeed, my beloved is here, how similar the sound is!" She lifted the curtain and took a quick glance, then immediately collapsed into his arms, covering her face in tears and unable to speak coherently. After a long while, she began to say, "All the past feelings are still here, but you have finally become like a flowing stream and withering flowers, placed yourself in a world of moonlight and flowers, have you ever thought of whether I, still in the dust and decay, am still suffering?" She comforted him for a long time, then stopped crying and became happy. They washed the cups and poured more wine, and he stayed overnight at the courtesan's house. Like this for more than half a month, he did not mention returning home, and the courtesan also did not ask for payment in the form of a red scarf. It so happened that his uncle was going to the capital to meet the emperor and wanted to take him along. He told the courtesan, who said, "Luxury and ease are like poison, one must not indulge in them. I fully understand that you are no longer the same as before, and I fear that you might change your heart based on my wealth or poverty. Therefore, for the past half a month, I have accompanied you in silence, to express my feelings."

    565

    勾栏中岂妙手空空儿久居之所，妾不加索，其如姊妹行白眼何？君行矣，勿以妾为念。如富贵逼人，未必无相见期也。”嗣后悲欢离合，南北西东。生北上时，已徐娘半老。别生数年，门前车马，愈形冷落不堪，渐至不能自存。扁舟返粤，改名歧凤，杜门谢客，以待生归。今秋，生援例得半通黄绶，两翅乌纱归里，而姬于两月前已逝。白头未遂，红粉已埋。生曰：“黄伯舆为情而死，我宁从卿于地下矣！所难堪者，薛涛坟上，已落桃花；关盼楼头，空归燕子耳。”奠墓毕，哭失声而仆，为从人灌救方苏。生殆深于情者，而红颜薄命，振古如兹。为志端倪，俾天下有情人咸为青衫之湿也。

    Explanation

    565

    In the entertainment district, could it be a place where a master thief like "Miao Shou Kongkong" would stay for long? If I don't search, what will my sisters say? Go ahead, don't worry about me. If wealth and status come to me, we might meet again. After that, there were ups and downs, separations and reunions, travels to the north, south, east, and west. When he went north, she was already past her prime. After several years apart, the traffic in front of her door became even more desolate. She returned to Guangdong by boat, changed her name to Qi Feng, closed her doors, and refused visitors, waiting for him to return. This autumn, he obtained a half-official position and returned home with a black cap, but she had died two months earlier. Their white-haired dreams were unfulfilled, and the red powder (her beauty) was already buried. He said, "If Huang Boyu died for love, I would rather join you in the afterlife! What is hard to bear is that on Xue Tao's tomb, the peach blossoms have fallen; at Guan Pan's tower, only the swallows return empty-handed." After offering incense at her grave, he cried out loud and fell unconscious. He was revived by his attendants. He seemed deeply moved by love, and the fate of beautiful women has been thus from ancient times. To record this beginning, let all people in love shed tears of sorrow.

    566

    崇川侠妓
    　　
    　　余谱香，湘乡名士也，年少多情，翩翩有致。唯家不中资，故每橐笔出游，藉衣食于奔走焉。时有某太守者，方以厘务于役崇川，慕余名，聘于幕中，而与之偕。余抵崇川后，公余之暇，偕二三友人，联袂香街，闲情风月。眷一所欢，曰春林，本广陵人也，貌仅中人，而饶有情韵。余与之遇，搴帷觌面，欢若三生；自春徂秋，几及半载有余。凡除酒局应分之外，缠头所需，则不妄费余一文，且款接之间，实有逾于琴瑟者。

    Explanation

    566

    In the city of Chongchuan, there lived a courtesan named Chunlin. I, Pu Xiang, am a renowned scholar from Xiangxiang, known for my youthful passion and graceful demeanor. Although my family is not wealthy, I often travel with a pen in hand, earning my living through my wanderings. At that time, a certain governor was stationed in Chongchuan, managing affairs and serving the region. He admired my reputation and invited me to join his entourage. After arriving in Chongchuan, during my leisure time after official duties, I would often accompany a few friends to the fragrant streets, enjoying the romantic atmosphere. I fell in love with a courtesan named Chunlin, who was originally from Guangling. Though her appearance was merely average, she possessed a charming and poetic grace. When I first met her, we were introduced face-to-face, and our connection felt as if we had known each other for three lifetimes. From spring to autumn, we spent nearly half a year together. Beyond the expenses for social gatherings, I did not spend a single coin on her, and our interactions were even more harmonious than the playing of a zither and a lute.

    567

    余因赠以联云：“秋日春风，毕竟在杨柳楼台，批杷门巷；山中林下，好记取美人低唱，高士狂吟。”会某太守以他事交卸，余亦辞去。客况萧条，几无以归计。日夕踌躇，至废寝食。春林已窥知其情，谓余曰：“是处岂子所得久居者耶？当速整行装，别图安砚之所，庶免关山失路，至贻笑于友朋也。”乃潜搜所积，仅得十金，计不敷，复私卸臂上条脱，为质十余金，授余曰：“以此赠别，藉表寸心。”余感愧交并，至于泥首。遂即日束装起程，致声珍重，为订后日之永好，洒涕而别。嗟乎！寒士值天涯沦落之时，虽戚亲亦鲜有过问者，而况其为青楼卖笑之人哉！吁！若春林者，诚可谓妓中之侠也。

    Explanation

    567

    I presented him with a couplet: "Autumn days and spring breezes, ultimately found in the willow-lined towers and the lanes of loquat trees; in the mountains and beneath the forest, remember the soft singing of a beauty and the unrestrained poetry of a noble scholar." Just as a certain local governor was about to hand over his duties, I also decided to leave. As a guest, I was in a desolate condition, unable to plan a way back home. Day and night, I hesitated, to the point of neglecting to eat and sleep. Spring Lin, sensing my feelings, said to me, "This is not a place where you can stay for long. You should quickly pack your things and find another place to settle, so as not to end up lost in the mountains and hills, to the point of being laughed at by friends." I secretly gathered what I had saved, only managing to get ten taels of silver, which was not enough. I then privately removed the bracelet from my arm, using it as collateral to get another ten taels of silver. She handed it to me, saying, "Take this as a parting gift, to express my heartfelt gratitude." I was filled with gratitude and shame, bowing my head in thanks. That day, I packed my things and set off, bidding farewell with heartfelt words, promising to meet again in the future, and we parted with tears. Alas! A poor scholar in a state of despair, even close relatives rarely ask about him, let alone those who are courtesans in brothels! Ah! If Spring Lin can be called a knight among courtesans, truly she is a remarkable woman.

    568

    冶游遇故妻
    　　
    　　宝钗再合，漳浦奇闻；破镜重圆，洛昌幸事。顾聚无不散，眼前即是短长亭；会甚于离，身后尚余花酒梦。此则遗恨巾帼，气尽须眉者矣。某生，宦裔也，籍本芜湖，家侨穗石。少孤，母氏溺爱，操举业罔就，乃复荒坟鬼唱，久伤椿树之长凋；宝人遥，更痛萱花之暗萎。盖岁庚午而母氏见背，生嗣是益不检。书画鼎彝，皆充市鬻。尤可慨者，家无宿储，妻常寄外氏为活。生素渔花柳，遂致久疏琴瑟，至此无以为家；则一身之外，更无复撄念者。是岁秋后，竟不知所往。其妻惯依母家，有传某死耗，说其改适者，而女不顾也。于是楼空而春风自惜，不绝啼鸦；镜破而明月难圆，暗辞野鹄。思芟红豆，永绝情缘；悲检青衫，都余旧泪，其志亦可哀矣。

    Explanation

    568

    Encountering an Old Wife on a Joyride

    The reunion of Baochai, a strange tale from Zhangpu; the broken mirror reunited, a lucky event in Luo. Gatherings must inevitably disperse, and the present is but a fleeting moment. Meeting is more important than parting, but even after death, the dream of flowers and wine remains. This is a case of a woman leaving regret, and a man exhausting his spirit. A certain man, a descendant of officials, originally from Wuhu, now living in Suishui. Orphaned at a young age, his mother doted on him, but he failed to achieve success in his studies. His paintings, calligraphy, and ritual objects were all sold in the market. Especially pitiful is that he had no savings, and his wife often relied on her family for support. He was always indulging in the pleasures of life, leading to a long separation from his wife. After that autumn, he disappeared without a trace. His wife was accustomed to relying on her mother's family, and when rumors spread that he had died, some suggested she remarry, but she refused. The house became empty, and the spring wind seemed to pity it, with crows crying incessantly; the mirror broke, and the bright moon could not be reunited, as the wild geese silently departed. She thought of pruning the red beans, to forever sever the ties of love; she mournfully examined her green robe, still stained with old tears. Her resolve is also pitiable.

    569

    讵意其兄，浮梁作贾，女以与嫂咀唔，不能安于其室，遂为浮言所惑，适媒家以为再嫁地。其嫂视若已死，不复与闻。迨岁辛未，兄浮梁归，问妹消息，嫂言：“姑少寡难守，我不能作主，遂任其意。在媒家，适一贾人，后闻媒媪说，该贾已携之返籍矣。姑以我不能体其意，于未嫁时已成枘凿。比改嫁，绝不通问，故不及详知其家世也。”兄闻，默然良久，出访之，杳无影响，如是者已历二年。讵料嫁者不成为嫁，死者亦不成为死。癸酉年三月，某生客帆归省，行李焕然。卸装后，抵岳家视妻，则妻不获面；妻兄作贾，妻嫂亦已下世，只余小舅小姨辈，问及其妻，小舅言已经改嫁，叩其究竟，亦不能详，生遂辞出。未几妻兄自贾所归，弟妹告以姊丈固未死。

    Explanation

    569

    Who could have thought that the brother, who was engaged in trade in Fuliang, would find his sister often in conversation with her sister-in-law, unable to be at peace in her home, and thus swayed by rumors to seek remarriage through a matchmaker. The sister-in-law treated her as if she were dead and no longer involved herself in the matter. Until the year Xinwei, the brother returned from Fuliang and inquired about his sister's news. The sister-in-law said, "The young widow found it difficult to remain unmarried, and I could not make the decision, so I let her do as she wished. At the matchmaker's house, she married a merchant. Later, I heard from the matchmaker that the merchant had taken her back to his hometown. I felt I did not understand her intentions, and even before the remarriage, there was already a mismatch. When she remarried, she never communicated with me again, so I did not get to know her family background in detail." The brother remained silent for a long time, then went out to inquire about her, but found no trace or news. Such was the case for two years. Who could have thought that the remarried one was not truly remarried, and the dead one was not truly dead? In the third month of the year Guiyou, a certain guest returned home to visit, and his luggage was fresh and new. After unloading his luggage, he went to his father-in-law's house to see his wife, but he did not see his wife. His wife's brother was a merchant, and his wife's sister-in-law had also passed away, leaving only the younger brother-in-law and younger sister-in-law. They asked about his wife, and the younger brother-in-law said she had remarried. When asked for details, he could not provide them, and the guest then left. Not long after, the wife's brother returned from his business trip, and the younger brother and sister informed him that his elder brother-in-law was not dead.

    570

    妻兄闻，急访见某生，告以前后根因，且谓因己不在家，致妹如此，向生告罪。生亦无语。妻兄去后，或劝生讼之，生曰：“我不能自主，以致家变若此，于彼何尤哉！”闻者服其有度。先是生无计谋生，自拼绝望。后闻有戚作宦江西，颇居显要，生欲往而艰川资。嗣有相识作客南雄，生恳附舟同往。至雄，所识略赠资斧，生遂入江西，获见其戚南昌知府某公。因生无甚长才，难授以重任，姑使之管帐。迨任满，出署居馆，生以颇有所获，暂辞戚，旋省墓。至是以客游初返，孤寂如鹜，不觉顿触旧好，游于谷埠花船。心厌波涛撼梦，改游蟠龙南某别院。生甫入幽房，即魂飞天外，盖所见丽者，即结缡人焉。生颇有镇静意，致言俟无人，细谈衷曲。妻会意，遂不语。

    Explanation

    570

    The brother-in-law heard and hurried to visit the young man, informing him of the cause and effect, and said that because he was not at home, his sister had ended up in this state, and apologized to the young man. The young man remained silent. After the brother-in-law left, some advised the young man to sue him, but the young man said, "I could not control myself, leading to such a family upheaval; what blame is there for him!" Those who heard it admired his composure. Initially, the young man had no way to make a living and resigned himself to despair. Later, he heard that a relative was serving as an official in Jiangxi and held a prominent position. The young man wanted to go but found it difficult to afford the travel expenses. Subsequently, a friend was traveling in Nanxiong, and the young man earnestly asked to join him on the boat. Upon arriving in Nanxiong, his friend gave him some money, and the young man then went to Jiangxi and met his relative, the governor of Nanchang, Mr. So-and-so. Because the young man did not have much talent, it was difficult to give him important responsibilities, so he was temporarily assigned to manage accounts. After his term ended, he left the office and stayed in a guesthouse. The young man, having gained quite a bit, temporarily bid farewell to his relative and soon went to visit his ancestors' graves. Upon returning from his travels, he felt lonely like a duck and unexpectedly felt a sudden longing for the past, visiting a flower boat in the harbor. He was tired of the waves disturbing his dreams and changed his destination to a secluded villa in the south of Panyu. As soon as he entered the secluded room, his spirit soared into the sky, for the beautiful woman he saw was his wife. The young man had a calm demeanor and said he would wait until no one was around to discuss their inner feelings in detail. His wife understood and remained silent.

    571

    迄乎酒阑灯尽，话及当年事，相对如梦寐焉。盖生妻为贾人赚娶，转货于娼家，后遂转徒至此。生叹曰：“此我之罪，与卿无干，顾卿何以自处！”妻曰：“覆水难收，君即不见责，然势难再合。”乃相与坐叹。至晓，生暂别，归思所以策其后者。临别时，妻曰：“君于午后须再至，作此生终见之缘。”生以抱恨成猝病，不能如约，迨相隔七八日再往，则已玉暗香消。询其死由，鸨母含涕，谓“乃仰洋药以死者。”且言“伊无欠负人家债目，又非有所格而不能遂，实未审其致命之故也。”某惊惶无语，唯问其葬所，鸨言草草殡殓；俟问土匠，始知之。生归，叹人世情缘，甚于梦幻泡影，拟挥慧剑，断烦恼丝，现未审果否。噫！三生难问，一切达观。但愿有酒浇愁，任歌得意，若以无稽诮我，宛在斯人。

    Explanation

    571

    Until the wine was finished and the lamps extinguished, when talking about past events, they looked at each other as if in a dream. It was said that the man's wife had been sold by a merchant to a brothel and then moved to this place. The man sighed, "This is my fault, not yours. How should you deal with yourself?" She replied, "Once water is spilled, it cannot be collected again. Even if you do not blame me, the situation makes reconciliation impossible." They sat together and sighed. Until dawn, the man temporarily parted, returning to think about how to deal with the situation later. When parting, the wife said, "You must come back after noon, as this will be our final meeting." The man, burdened by his sorrow, fell ill suddenly and could not keep the appointment. After being apart for several days, when he went back, she was already gone, her beauty and fragrance faded. When he asked about the cause of her death, the madam wept and said, "She died from taking opium." She owed no debts to anyone, nor was there anything preventing her from continuing her life; the true cause of her death remains unknown. He was shocked and speechless, only asking about her burial place. After asking the grave diggers, he finally learned. When he returned, he lamented that human relationships are more fleeting than dreams, illusions, or bubbles. He intended to use a wisdom sword to cut through his troubles, accepting everything with equanimity. He just wished to drown his sorrows in wine and sing to his heart's content. If someone were to criticize him for this, they would be like this person.

    572

    阿韩传
    　　
    　　阿韩者，鸳湖荡桨女也。风鬟云鬓，绰约多姿。禾中裙屐少年，殷商大贾，爱坐其船。每当春夏之交，放棹于烟雨楼前，杨柳风和，藕花香送，人面与波光相掩映。而性尤能解颐，秋波一转，娇态动情。所得卖笑钱，钗簪装饰而外，积有余资。有某生者，才能倚马，隔宿无粮，与韩缱绻最深，而韩亦不以寒素薄之。解香奁以佐膏火资，无虚夕也。迨生桂籍登名，而韩则欣然自诩，以巾帼识英豪矣，遂愿抱衾与稠也。成婚之夕，女貌郎才，人称双绝云。呜呼！寒士值寥落之秋，即戚族亦鲜有顾问；而韩能于风尘中，独具慧眼，女侠也，亦天缘也。不然，当其名噪一时，意中人岂少哉，何必眷恋此牢落青衫之人也哉！

    Explanation

    572

    A Han's Biography

    A Han was a woman who rowed in the Yuan Lake, her hair styled like wind and clouds, graceful and full of charm. Young scholars and wealthy merchants loved to sit in her boat. During the transition between spring and summer, she would row near the Yanyu Tower, where the breeze from the willows was gentle, the fragrance of lotus flowers wafted through the air, and her face reflected in the shimmering water. She was particularly skilled at charming her companions with her playful glances and gentle demeanor. The money she earned from her entertainment, after spending on jewelry and decorations, left her with a surplus. There was a scholar who could write on horseback, yet he often lacked food for the next day, and he had the deepest affection for A Han, who did not look down on him because of his poverty. She would open her treasure box to help with the expenses of lamp oil, and there was no night without her support. When the scholar passed the imperial examinations and was inscribed in the imperial register, A Han was delighted and proud, saying that she recognized a hero among women. She then wished to share her bed with him. On their wedding night, their beauty and talent were praised as a double rarity. Alas! Poor scholars in their desolate times, even their relatives rarely offered them advice; yet A Han, in her humble surroundings, had a unique insight, she was a heroine, and it was also a fateful connection. Otherwise, when her name was famous for a time, how many would have been her admirers, why would she have been so devoted to this scholar in a lowly position?

    573

    九月桃花记
    　　
    　　春申浦之滨，有桃花一株，九月盛放。时人以为灾异，或谓天时恒燠所致。天河生曰：“皆非也，桃花根浮而寿短，自古以为红颜薄命之喻。上海城北，为桃花最盛之场，阴气所钟，故有九月复开之异。”一日偶与友人语及，而未尝播诸众人也。是夕就枕，闻门外舆马倥偬，使者二人进谒曰：“主人敬遣相迓。”生茫然，问主人为谁，使者曰：“桃花仙馆夫人也。”生曰：“素昧生平，何事见招？”迁延不欲往。使者致意殷勤，敦迫就道，生不得已，许之。约行十数里，见郭门隐约，云霞变灭，望如锦城。香气沁人，心神怅惘。

    Explanation

    573

    September Peach Blossom Record

    On the banks of the Chūshēn River, there stood a peach tree that bloomed in September. People at the time considered this an omen of disaster, or attributed it to the consistently warm weather. Tianhe Sheng said, "Neither is correct. The peach tree has shallow roots and a short lifespan, a metaphor since ancient times for beautiful women having short lives. The north of Shanghai is the place where peach blossoms flourish most, and the concentration of yin energy here causes the unusual phenomenon of the tree blooming again in September." One day, he happened to mention this with a friend, but had not shared it with anyone else. That evening, as he lay down to sleep, he heard the sound of carriages and horses outside, and two messengers entered to pay their respects, saying, "The lady of the Peach Blossom Fairy Hall respectfully sends her greetings and invites you." Tianhe Sheng was perplexed and asked who the owner was. The messengers replied, "The lady of the Peach Blossom Fairy Hall." Tianhe Sheng said, "I have never met her before; why am I being invited?" He hesitated and did not want to go. The messengers, however, were very polite and urged him to set out. Tianhe Sheng, having no choice, agreed. After traveling about ten miles, he saw the city gates in the distance, with clouds and mists changing colors, appearing like a splendid city. The fragrance permeated the air, and his heart and spirit felt a mix of sadness and confusion.

    574

    生问何郡邑，使者曰：“君日处青山中，而不知耶？此亦一青城也。为东皇渡海行宫，群芳之吏，萃于是焉。”生既入城，见绿萼红苞，奇葩异卉，纤丰约，五色迷离。每过一门，各有题额曰：“芍药宫，”“芙渠苑”与夫“梨园”、“柳衙”之属。树木掩映，楼阁周回。转折久之，忽见宫阙巍然，朱门碧瓦，鳞次栉比，非复人间，则所称桃花仙馆者也。使者引入，嘱生暂歇廊庑间，而自复命于夫人。时重门内外，韶艳女郎，不下百余，华妆靓饰，窥客而嬉。少顷，使者出，传命请见，生肃容而入。门闼洞启，珠帘乍钩。堂以上，嫔从如云，环纷沓。夫人方升坐，神光霞艳，丽若天人。

    Explanation

    574

    The protagonist asked which county and city this was. The messenger replied, "You live day by day in the green mountains, yet you do not know? This is also Qingcheng. It serves as the residence of the Eastern Emperor crossing the sea, and the officials of all flowers gather here." After entering the city, the protagonist saw green calyxes and red buds, exotic and unusual flowers, with delicate and full forms, their colors mingling in a bewildering array. Each time passing through a gate, there were inscriptions on the gate, saying "Peony Palace," "Hibiscus Garden," and others such as "Pear Orchard" and "Willow Hall." Trees and buildings were interwoven, and pavilions and towers were arranged around. After turning and winding for a long time, suddenly a grand palace appeared, with red doors and green tiles arranged in neat rows, no longer of this world—it was the so-called Peach Blossom Fairy Hall. The messenger led him in and instructed him to rest temporarily in the corridor, while he himself went to report to the lady. At that time, inside and outside the heavy doors, there were no fewer than a hundred graceful and beautiful young ladies, dressed in splendid attire, peeking at the guests and playing. After a short while, the messenger came out and conveyed the lady's command to see him. The protagonist entered with a serious and respectful demeanor. The door was wide open, and the pearl curtain was suddenly hooked. Above the hall, attendants were like clouds, and the sounds of their jewelry were incessant. The lady ascended her seat, her divine light and rosy beauty resembling that of a celestial being.

    575

    生及阶而揖，不敢仰视。夫人曰：“奉劳玉趾，愿达一言。今人谐媚成风，事无定论。譬如秋斋供菊，冬岭寻梅，自命风流，清寒常态。所异者，文人墨客，咏及秋容，往往轻薄春光，以桃李为凡姿俗艳。不思天生万物，菀枯递嬗，各擅胜于一时。桃李不借力于东风，而春色必叨荣于桃李，藉以增辉万物，黼黻河山也。设使气转鸿钧，东君命驾，而乾坤冷落，风日萧条，何以挽回寒瘦之观，敷畅阳和之气哉？况乎夭桃之姿，更不同于杏李；王母瑶池之树，元都仙观之珍。春官则门第争夸，风化则室家兴咏。曼卿抛去，五百岁而始花；方朔偷来，三千年而一实。

    Explanation

    575

    He ascended the steps and bowed, not daring to look up. The lady said, "I trouble your noble feet, wishing to convey a single word. Nowadays, flattery and sycophancy have become the prevailing trend, and there is no fixed conclusion or judgment. It is like the practice of placing chrysanthemums in the study during autumn and searching for plum blossoms in winter, which are considered refined and elegant, yet they are natural and unpretentious. What is different is that literati and scholars, when writing about the autumn scenery, often belittle the spring light, considering peaches and plums as ordinary and vulgar. They do not think that heaven creates all things, which undergo cycles of growth and decay, each excelling in its own time. Peaches and plums do not rely on the east wind to bloom, yet the spring scenery must honor them, using them to enhance the beauty of all things and adorn the mountains and rivers. Suppose the cosmic order changes, and the sun god arrives, but the universe becomes cold and desolate, and the weather becomes bleak and dreary. How can we restore the view of a cold and thin landscape and spread the warm and harmonious energy of spring? Moreover, the graceful and delicate appearance of peach blossoms is even more different from apricots and plums; the trees in the Jade Pool of the Queen Mother of the West and the precious trees in the Daoist temples of the capital. In spring, families compete to boast about their lineage, and the moral customs inspire families to sing praises. Zhang Hua threw away his peach blossom tree after 500 years, and Han Wen stole a peach from the Queen Mother of the West's garden after 3,000 years.

    576

    而先生以为根浮寿短，何所见之疏也。“吾不揣冒昧，请于东皇，特于重阳之后，小春之前，命彼花神，择人烟辐辏之区，点缀一株，为世俗一新耳目。俾知华之质，亦足以寄傲风霜；艳丽之姿，何不可争芳松竹。置夔龙于叔世，岂无逆鲠之忠；嫁钟郝于蓬门，讵少摩笄之节。桃花之不与菊梅比烈者，不为也，非不能也。时不同也。若谓化工嘘拂，亦其福命使然。彼寂寞空山，凄凉篱下者，时运之不逮，又何忮乎他人哉？悠悠之言，初无足责。先生灵根未泯，慧果犹存，慎勿以褊见迂词，令彼名花抱恨也。”生闻言，惶愧无地，如梦初醒，一灯荧然，瓶花犹弄影也。

    Explanation

    576

    The Master believes that the peach flower is rooted shallowly and has a short life, how shallow is his understanding! "I dare to speak without considering my boldness, I will request the Flower Goddess to, specifically after the Double Ninth Festival and before the early spring, to adorn a single plant in a densely populated area, to offer the world a new sight. Let it be known that the essence of the peach flower is also sufficient to take pride in enduring the wind and frost; its beautiful appearance, why cannot it vie for beauty with pines and bamboos? Placing夔 and龙 in a dark age, there is still unwavering loyalty; marrying钟 and郝 to a humble home, there is no lack of integrity. The peach flower does not compete with chrysanthemums and plums in intensity, not because it is incapable, but because the times are different. If it is said that the work of nature is determined by fate, then those in lonely mountains and cold fences, whose timing or fortune has failed, why should they envy others? Vague and idle words are not worth blaming. The Master's spiritual root is not yet extinguished, and wisdom and fruitfulness remain, be cautious not to let narrow-minded views and outdated words make the famous flower suffer in regret." The student heard these words, feeling profound shame and regret, as if waking from a dream, a single lamp flickered, and the flower in the vase still played with its shadow.

    577

    记珠江韵事
    　　
    　　羊城每当士子云集之时，珠江风景，分外清娱。其往游谷埠，呼妓侑酒者，纷如也。有某生狎一妓，名阿云，甚为眷爱，形影不离。阿云明眸善睐，肤如凝脂，殆江淹赋所云“气柔色靡”者也。颇能识字，解诵风词，每一掉文，几如匡说解颐，不数郑家婢泥中之对也。某生曾赠以词曰：“帘前记执纤纤手，堂中细酌盈盈酒。语软情温，惆怅巫山一段云。背人特地留侬住，惊风又拂衣衫去。无闷无愁，万唤千呼不转头。”又云：“惊春正滞珠江棹，悲秋始返雪山道。此日相适，疑是飞琼下碧空。绣裙半掩名花饰，云鬟低亚胭脂赤。相对多情，只少些儿画不成。”某生拟娶之为侧室，以千金为之脱乐籍。惟须待鹿鸣宴罢，然后仍商之堂上也。然二词盛传于勾栏中。

    Explanation

    577

    Translation:

    A Tale of the Pearl River's Charm

    When scholars gather in large numbers in Guangzhou, the scenery of the Pearl River becomes especially pleasant. Those who go to visit the area known as Gu Bu and summon courtesans to entertain them with wine do so in great numbers. There was a scholar who became intimately involved with a courtesan named Ah Yun. He held her in great affection, and they were inseparable. Ah Yun had bright eyes that glanced gracefully, skin as smooth as congealed fat, and was like the "gentle demeanor and delicate appearance" described in Jiang Yan's writings. She was quite able to read and recite poetic works, and whenever she engaged in literary banter, she provided amusement and enlightenment, far surpassing the overly complex responses of the maid from the Zheng family. The scholar once composed a poem for her:

    "Before the curtain, I held her delicate hands,
    In the hall, I sipped the wine she poured.
    Her words were soft, her feelings warm,
    I longed for the clouds of the Wushan Mountains.
    She lingered behind me, refusing to let me go,
    But the wind startled her, and she fled, leaving my clothes behind.
    Without worry or sorrow,
    No matter how many times I called, she would not turn around."

    He also wrote:

    "Startled by the spring, I linger on the Pearl River,
    In autumn, I return to the snowy path.
    On this day, we are well-suited,
    It seems as if the celestial maiden has descended from the blue sky.
    Her embroidered skirt half-conceals the adornment of a famous flower,
    Her cloud-like hair falls low, its rouge red.
    We gaze at each other with deep affection,
    Only a few words remain unspoken, as if they cannot be painted."

    The scholar planned to marry her as a concubine and spent a thousand gold pieces to release her from her status as a courtesan. However, he would have to wait until after the Lu Ming Banquet, and then still seek approval from the elders of his family. Nevertheless, the two poems became widely circulated in the theaters.

    578

    得新忘旧
    　　
    　　楚客述武昌胡健中获报事，殊可发人猛省。胡贸易于闽，近年复商于粤，赎名娼作小室。所谓娼者，名宝琴，谷埠楼船翘楚也。胡邂逅遇之，刻不能忘，捐重金削其籍，置诸朱楼绣阁间，几以为得神仙福矣。胡中表郑少岩，年少逸才，皎若临风玉树。虽随胡作贾，而雅无市廛气。胡以通家懿亲，不复嫌疑，胡郑至，宝琴每不避。或觞酒品风月，仍命宝琴侑尊，以符四美。顾宝虽曾隶乐籍，而丰怀洒脱，无脂粉习气，且流丽中寓以端庄。无因之笑，有令人不敢倾吐于前。郑尝至健中所，健中不在，宝出应之。

    Explanation

    578

    A Story of Forgetting the Old for the New

    A guest from Chu recounted an incident involving Hu Jianzhong of Wuchang, which is very much capable of awakening one to a sudden realization. Hu engaged in trade in Fujian and, in recent years, resumed business in Guangdong, where he redeemed a famous courtesan named Baoqin and made her his concubine. Baoqin was the best among the courtesans of the river boats in the grain port. Hu met her by chance and could not forget her immediately. He spent a large sum of money to remove her from the register and placed her in a red-painted building with embroidered walls, almost thinking he had attained the happiness of a fairy. Hu's cousin, Zheng Shaoyan, was young and talented, bright and elegant like a jade tree in the wind. Although he followed Hu in trade, he was still free from the airs of a market. Hu, considering them as close relatives, did not hesitate. When Hu and Zheng came, Baoqin never avoided them. Sometimes they would drink wine and enjoy the scenery, still having Baoqin serve as a companion to match the four beauties of literature, wine, music, and companionship. But Baoqin, although she had once been a member of the music register, had a generous and free-spirited nature, was free from the airs of a courtesan, and was graceful yet dignified. She had a smile that was not for show, and there was something about her that made people hesitant to speak openly in her presence. Zheng once came to Jianzhong's place, but Jianzhong was not there. Baoqin came out to greet him.

    579

    郑见其花眸，似有泪意，讶之，宝不能自隐，曰：“君知表兄有新宠否？”郑曰：“不知也。”宝谓：“此非今日事，去秋向侬索金钗去，此即其时也。既有此宠后，神意即淡然，偶有酬对，亦只貌合而神离也。”郑曰：“嫂何知之确？我虽与之共处，然此节则俨在梦中，非惟未见，且不及闻也。”因遂别去。迨胡竟以新问旧，而弛宝之宠，宝虽不怨遇人不淑，而良宵风月，幽恨时形。胡视之俨如赘，不惟不贵之，且欲去之也。郑知此事，不复往其家。去冬，宝沾疾，胡医治之，不三日遽殁，且殡葬皆从简。郑见其随地移情，心弗善也，而亦未能测宝致死之由。

    Explanation

    579

    Zheng saw her flower-like eyes, which seemed to have tears. He was surprised, and Bao could not hide himself. He asked, "Do you know if my cousin has a new favorite?" Zheng said, "I don't know." Bao said, "This is not a recent event; last autumn, he asked me for a gold hairpin. After gaining this favor, his spirit became indifferent. Occasionally, there is a response, but it is only superficial." Zheng said, "How do you know this for sure? I live with him, but this matter is like a dream to me. Not only have I not seen it, but I haven't even heard about it." So he left. When Hu finally asked about the old, Bao's favor was relaxed. Although Bao does not blame himself for bad luck, the quiet nights and moonlight often reveal hidden sorrows. Hu regards her as a burden, not only not valuing her, but wanting to get rid of her. Zheng knew this and no longer went to his house. Last winter, Bao fell ill, and Hu treated him, but he died within three days. And the funeral was conducted simply. Zheng saw that he shifted his feelings with the situation and did not approve. And could not determine the cause of Bao's death.

    580

    本年五月，胡之新置爱妾，名爱云；病热，每怔忡中时，如有所见，胡叱为谬。一日，从昏迷中叹气曰：“我病何得以寒凉之剂，速我生命。”胡叱曰：“尔真昏谬，尔病大热，非此品无得奏功。”爱云曰：“我不谬，尔亦非谬，但其新孔嘉，遂令其旧者可厌耳。”胡闻言，遂如冷水浇背。盖宝琴之死，胡实有不可告人之处，遂疑爱云此日乱语，系宝琴冤魂所凭也。爱自尔日后，寝疾不安，言皆涉宝琴，因延巫者解禳。铙鼓喧嚣时，爱即批颊自挝，狼藉二十余日遂死。胡自爱死，寸衷忧惧不惶。

    Explanation

    580

    In the fifth month of this year, Hu acquired a new concubine named Ai Yun. She was suffering from a fever and, during her delirium, seemed to see something, which Hu scolded as being mistaken. One day, while in a coma, she sighed and said, "Why should my illness be treated with cold medicines that hasten my life?" Hu scolded her, saying, "You are truly delirious and mistaken. Your illness is very hot, and without this medicine, there can be no effect." Ai Yun replied, "I am not mistaken, and you are not mistaken either, but because the new one is so charming, it makes the old one seem unappealing." Upon hearing this, Hu felt as if cold water had been poured over his back. The death of Baoqin, Hu had something he could not reveal, so he suspected that Ai Yun's words that day were the result of Baoqin's vengeful spirit. After that day, Ai Yun's illness worsened, and all her words involved Baoqin. So she invited a witch to perform exorcism rituals. When the drums and gongs were noisy, Ai Yun would slap herself. She was disheveled for over twenty days and then died. After Ai Yun's death, Hu was filled with deep anxiety and fear.

    581

    去月偶病，招中表郑至，嘱曰：“汝可看先人面上，为我善办后事。”郑惊曰：“君偶病，何遽至此？”胡摇首曰：“非汝所知也。”郑亦隐其事，遍为安慰。初四日延医投方后，胡忽检视曰：“似此缓药，何日可愈！”乃从秘箧中出方一纸，则极凉之剂也，速命合二剂，递次煎饮毕，蒙被而卧。郑至榻问之，则已垂绝，言语不明。幸生意籍中，夙昔曾交托明白。郑问其家人服何药，何遽至此。及呈药方，郑曰：“此乃旧方，谁命服此？”家人曰：“此主人所命者也。”郑知其不久人世，急为经理后事。翌日遂殁。健中至死所谈并无不经事，而郑则深明其致死之由，录之以戒轻于爱憎者。

    Explanation

    581

    Last month, I happened to fall ill. I summoned my cousin Zheng and instructed him, "Please take good care of my affairs after I am gone, for the sake of our ancestors." Zheng was surprised and said, "You have just fallen ill, why are you talking about this so soon?" Hu shook his head and said, "It is not something you can understand." Zheng also kept the matter secret and comforted everyone thoroughly. On the fourth day, after consulting a doctor and receiving a prescription, Hu suddenly inspected and said, "Such a mild medicine, how long will it take to heal?" He then took out a prescription from a secret drawer and said, "It is an extremely cooling remedy." He quickly ordered to prepare two doses, which were prepared and consumed one after another, and he lay down under the blanket. Zheng went to the bed and asked him, but he was already on the verge of death, and his speech was unclear. Fortunately, I have left instructions in my will, and I have previously entrusted everything clearly. Zheng asked the family what medicine they were taking and why they had taken it so suddenly. When the prescription was presented, Zheng said, "This is an old prescription; who ordered this to be taken?" The family replied, "This was ordered by the master." Zheng knew that he would not live much longer and hurriedly managed the after affairs. The next day, he passed away. Jianzhong talked about nothing improper until his death, but Zheng deeply understood the cause of his death. He recorded this to warn those who act lightly on love and hate.

    582

    女仙降乩词
    　　
    　　前在西湖太和坛扶乩，有女仙降坛，书《醉太平》词一阙，云：“风来露凉，云归月茫，银河界破秋光，坠飞星过墙。蕉荫半窗，藤荫半廊，回头悄问檀郎，是情长梦长。”请仙姓氏，则乩已寂然矣。绎其词旨，轻茜清圆，宛似弹丸脱手。吾杭频乡女史，以词名。成庙宣庙时，为赵秋大令入室弟子，所著《频香词》若干卷，神似香消酒消一编，为词中别开生面。此词风致颇为近之，岂频香精灵不昧，尚来往于六桥三竺间乎？顾以姓氏请，则终不应，何也？吾闻频香所适非天，大有淑真断肠之戚，常画饮酒读骚乔影，自谱北曲一套题之，慷慨淋漓，铜琶铁板，每以豪纵为解嘲；而词中则呢呢私语，韵致凄凉，竟判然如出两手，岂尚不忘绮语之结习耶？抑遭兵火，另有才女，抑郁以死，而不能自秘其情耶？姑具所见，以俟知者云。

    Explanation

    582

    A Poem by a Descending Female Deity

    Previously, at the Taihe Altar by West Lake, during a séance, a female deity descended and wrote a poem in the form of Zui Taiping (Drunk in Peace). It reads:

    "The wind brings coolness, the dew is refreshing, the clouds return and the moon is hazy. The Milky Way divides the autumn light, and a falling star passes the wall. Banana shadows half-cover the window, and vine shadows half-shade the corridor. Turning back, she softly asks her beloved, 'Is our love long, or is our dream long?'"

    When asked for the deity's name, the séance ended abruptly. Analyzing the meaning of the poem, it is light, clear, and round, as if a ball had been thrown with a flick of the wrist. Our Hangzhou lady, known for her poetry, was a disciple of Zhao Qiuling during the reigns of the Cheng and Xuan emperors. She wrote several volumes of Pinxiang Ci (Frequent Fragrance Poems), which are reminiscent of the fading fragrance and wine of a past era, offering a fresh face to the world of poetry. This poem is quite similar in style, leading one to wonder if the spirit of Pinxiang still lingers in the scenic areas of Six Bridges and Three Zhus? Yet, when asked for her name, the deity did not respond. Why is this? I have heard that Pinxiang's fate was not in the heavens, and she experienced great sorrow akin to the heartbreak of Shuzhen. She often painted herself drinking wine and reading poetry, composing a set of northern-style music and inscribing it, expressing herself with bold and unrestrained force, like the copper plectrum and iron tablet of Su Shi. Yet, in her poetry, she spoke in soft, intimate whispers, with a melancholic and elegant tone, so different from her bold style as if they came from two different hands. Could it be that she still clung to the habit of elegant and ornate language? Or was she another talented woman who died in the chaos of war, unable to keep her feelings hidden? I present these thoughts for those who know.

    583

    船女奇缘
    　　
    　　网船女子阿巧者，故小家女也。始生时，父母不欲举之，为网船某妪乞归抚养，以为童媳云尔。乃阿巧至十一二龄时，姿容即明媚可喜，某媪颇钟爱之，不令作船上生活，凡拖篙打桨诸务，皆不问也。阿巧性尤慧，能得媪之欢心。又数年，媪稍裕，另置一艇，使阿巧守之，而日与其子驾旧船出谋食。盖以其子亦将弱冠，故令远之也。网船之泊于鸳湖也，有定所。女之船适泊在东门。东门为人烟繁盛之地。有西泠某生者，风神楚楚，有灵和张绪之风。少尝学书，中更丧乱，遂弃儒而贾，设肆于禾地之东郭。每当清晨，市未集时，则踞楼槛而读。水鸟惊飞，渔人皆起，盖日以为常矣。女之船适在某生楼下，闻生晨读则亦起，临波掠鬓，照水更衣。生亦习见之，未为奇也。一日，方盛暑，生散步河干柳荫之下，则见女方依篷刺绣，着白苎形，不加妆饰；而冰肌无汗，艳绝天然，始大惊异。停足凝眸，女亦仰目注视，素颊微赤，梨涡浅晕。

    Explanation

    583

    Ship Girl's Strange Affair

    A girl named Aqiao from a small family was originally given to an old woman of a net boat to raise as a child bride. At the age of eleven or twelve, Aqiao's appearance was bright and lovely, and the old woman loved her very much. She did not make Aqiao work on the boat and did not involve her in any boat-related tasks. Aqiao was especially intelligent and could please the old woman. After several years, the old woman became more prosperous and bought another boat for Aqiao to manage, while her son would go out with her to earn a living. This was because her son was about to reach adulthood, so the old woman sent them away.

    The net boats had a fixed place to dock at Yu Lake, and Aqiao's boat happened to be docked at the East Gate, which was a bustling area. There was a young man from the West Lake with a graceful and charming demeanor, reminiscent of the elegance of Zhang Xu. He had once studied calligraphy but experienced turmoil during his middle years and then abandoned his scholarly pursuits to become a merchant. He set up a shop in the eastern suburb of Huzhou. Every morning when the market was not yet open, he would sit by the window and read. The birds would be startled to fly, and the fishermen would be awakened, which had become a daily routine.

    Aqiao's boat happened to be docked below the young man's shop. When she heard him reading in the morning, she would also get up, comb her hair by the water, and change her clothes. The young man had become accustomed to seeing her and did not find it strange. One day, when it was very hot, the young man walked along the riverbank under the shade of willows and saw Aqiao sitting under the canopy, embroidering. She was wearing a white cotton dress and was not adorned. Her skin was cool and sweatless, and her beauty was naturally stunning. He was greatly surprised and stopped to stare at her. Aqiao also looked up and gazed at him. Her cheeks were slightly flushed, and her dimples were faintly visible.

    584

    生揖而前，似欲有白，女冁然曰：“君非楼头晨读者耶？”生曰：“然，久托毗邻，未经拜访，今得一面，亦三生之幸也。”女怒之以目，曰：“此岂君咬文嚼字之地耶？往来人杂，宁无多露之畏耶？”生遂登其舟，自隐于芦箔之内，长跪而请，愿自附于婚姻。女笑曰：“君真妄想者，侬为船妪童媳，宁不知之哉？”生哀之再四，但求一促臂，得亲香泽，死且不憾。女不忍固拂其意，以手引之使起。生乘势跃起，狎抱之，遂共为野鸳鸯矣。

    Explanation

    584

    The man bowed and moved forward, seeming to want to say something. The woman smiled and said, "Are you not the man who reads aloud in the morning from the tower?" The man replied, "Yes, I have long lived nearby, but never visited. To meet you now is a great fortune in three lifetimes." The woman glared at him and said, "Is this not a place where you can indulge in wordplay? With so many people coming and going, are you not afraid of being exposed?" The man then climbed onto her boat and hid himself behind the reed curtain, kneeling down and begging, hoping to become part of her family through marriage. The woman laughed and said, "You truly have delusions; do you not know that I am a boatwoman's young daughter-in-law?" The man pleaded with her repeatedly, only seeking to touch her arm and feel her fragrance, even if he died, he would not regret it. The woman, unable to firmly reject his intentions, pulled him up. The man took the opportunity to leap up and embrace her intimately, and they became an informal couple, like wild swans.

    585

    过数月，女忽招生至舟中，泣而告曰：“君害侬矣。谁知竟种蘖根哉！今将奈何？不告亦死，告亦死耳。不告则死迟，告则死速耳。今将奈何？”生亦相持泣，既而曰：“无已，其亟以情告母乎？倘母肯舍卿一人，则其子娶妇之事，我当任之。”女以为不可，曰：“此唯有走为上着耳。老姆之前，侬何能自达于前耶？”生因赁宅于岳庙之村中，迎女而居焉。媪觅女不得，生因使人微告之故，而赂以重金。媪叹曰：“吾因疑此儿太艳，乃尤物，非舟人子所能堪也。一接而孕，岂非天乎？”受其金，而仍令以母女往返焉。

    Explanation

    585

    After several months, the woman suddenly gave birth to a child on the boat and cried out, saying, "You have harmed me. Who would have thought that the root of the problem would sprout! What shall I do now? Whether I tell or not, I will die either way. If I don't tell, I will die later; if I tell, I will die sooner. What shall I do now?" The man also wept, holding her hand, and then said, "There is no other way, perhaps you should quickly tell the truth to your mother. If your mother is willing to let you go, then the matter of my son's marriage, I will take care of it." The woman thought this was not possible and said, "This is only the best course of action. Before the old lady, how can I speak for myself?" The man then rented a house in the village near the temple of his wife's ancestors and brought the woman to live there. The old lady could not find the woman, so the man had someone discreetly inform her of the reason and offered her a large sum of money. The old lady sighed and said, "I suspected that this child was too beautiful, a rare treasure, which the son of a boatman could not bear. To become pregnant after a single encounter, is it not fate?" She accepted the money and still allowed the mother and daughter to come and go.

    586

    情死
    　　
    　　语曰：“牡丹花下死，做鬼也风流。”此王伯伦为情而死也。然苟非其人，则等一死如鸿毛矣。前岁阅香港《新报》，载有梁某与某妓，恋奸情密，为妓所卖。迨妓因厌其盘桓不去，赴政务司衙门，控其凶恶，差提质讯，忿极自戕一案，亦为情死。不想无独有偶，睽隔数千里，竟有遥遥相对者。如法租界兴圣里，有吴人名阿桂者，失其姓，向在宝兴戏馆门前，摆水果摊度日也。姘识同乡某妇，迄今九载，鳏夫寡妇，已同结发，皆图唱随偕老计。

    Explanation

    586

    It is said, "To die under the peony flowers is to die even as a ghost, still elegant." This is the case of Wang Boren, who died for love. However, if it is not the right person, then such a death is as insignificant as a feather. Last year, I read in the Hong Kong Newspaper about a case involving Liang and a certain courtesan, who were engaged in a secret and intimate relationship, but she betrayed him. Eventually, the courtesan, tired of his persistent presence, went to the Government Office, accused him of being cruel, and the officials questioned him. In a fit of rage, he committed suicide. Unbeknownst to many, this was not an isolated incident. Despite being separated by thousands of miles, there were similar cases. For example, in the Xingsheng Lane of the French Concession, there was a man named Ah Gui from Wu, whose surname is unknown. He used to sell fruit in front of the Baoying Theater to make a living. He had an affair with a woman from his hometown and, after nine years, they had become like a married couple, both widowed, and were planning to live together until old age.

    587

    岂料妇女水性杨花，近日竟别有情人，从前恩爱，一笔勾销。妇之视夫，早若眼中之钉矣。初尚稍知避嫌，继则毫无顾忌。前日阿桂因事出外，野鸳鸯正当交颈之际，为阿桂于奸所双双捉获。妇本无媒，遂议异离。及至阿桂检点家中所有，早被某妇运之一空。虽属小本经营，而数年之积蓄，一旦弃之他人，未免愤郁难舒，遂自闭户服紫霞膏毕命。呜呼哀哉，作不醒之黄梁耳。其死也，不动声色，先自剃头洗浴，衣服穿整，皆莫之能防，可谓视死如归，可惜死非其所，亦愚矣哉！噫！
    　　
    　　记邗江张素琴校书毕命事

    Explanation

    587

    Who would have thought that women are as fickle as water and as capricious as willow catkins, and that recently she has found another lover, completely forgetting the past affection. The woman regards her husband as a thorn in her eye. Initially, she still knew how to avoid suspicion, but later she had no reservations. The other day, when Arui went out on business, the wild mandarin ducks were in the midst of their romantic encounter when they were caught in pairs by Arui at the place of their infidelity. The woman had no matchmaker, so they discussed separation. When Arui checked all the possessions at home, they had already been taken away by the woman. Although it was a small-scale business, the accumulated savings over several years were suddenly abandoned to someone else, which inevitably caused anger and frustration hard to express, leading him to shut himself in and die by taking purple mist ointment. Alas, he lived like a dream that never awakens. His death was calm and composed; he first shaved his head and bathed himself, dressed himself neatly, and no one could prevent it. He regarded death as returning home, but unfortunately, he died in the wrong place, also foolish indeed! Ah!

    588

    珠娘身价，邗江最重。薛涛院内，关盼楼头，每喜风月清淡，一洗烟花习气。现虽江流日下，大不如前，然自爱者，犹金玉其身，不为阿堵所动。非若寻常流俗，见金夫不有躬也。张素琴者，本良家女，结缡半载，琴瑟颇谐。无奈家室贫寒，牛衣对泣，计无所出。适有鸨母，蛊惑其夫，遂致枭鸱入室。平康误落，非其志也。忽遘狂且，逼以势利。琴盖心焉许者钱生，至是宿愿难偿，竟誓以死。一日，钱偕友腊屐过院，张筵小叙，琴艳妾劝酒，坐钱膝上，曰：“妾生不辰，流光荏苒，轻尘弱草，于今十七年矣。又不幸中道睽违，事难如意。今已矣，花残月缺矣！君若惠顾前好，葬妾蜀冈之上。并有六龄弱弟，惠而教之，死瞑目矣！”钱与友诧异不已，慰藉间而琴力不支，气绝死，盖已仰药而未以告人也。钱出数百金，为琴葬玉并恤其弟。噫！九原可作，同怜薄命红颜；百折不回，为认荒丘黄土。芙蓉诔召，豆蔻香消。红粉内竟有斯人，青楼中又增佳话矣。

    Explanation

    588

    Juwang's value was the highest in Yangzhou. In Xue Tao's courtyard in Chengdu and Guan Pan's pavilion in Yangzhou, she always enjoyed a refined and elegant lifestyle, free from the habits of the courtesan's profession. Although Yangzhou's prosperity had declined significantly, and it was far from its former glory, those who valued themselves still maintained their dignity and worth, not swayed by wealth. Unlike ordinary people who were easily attracted to power and wealth, Zhang Suqin, originally a virtuous woman from a good family, had been married for half a year and had a harmonious marriage. However, her family's poverty and the hardships they faced left her with no way out. Her husband was seduced by a madam, leading to an unwelcome influence in their home. She did not choose to become a courtesan but was forced into a difficult situation by a violent person. Zhang Suqin had secretly agreed to marry Qian Sheng, but her long-held wish could no longer be fulfilled, and she vowed to die rather than live in disgrace. One day, Qian Sheng and his friend visited the courtesan's house, and Zhang Suqin, dressed in her finest attire, served them wine, sitting on Qian Sheng's knee and said, "I was born at a bad time, and the years have passed swiftly. I am like a weak grass, now seventeen years old. Unluckily, my marriage was disrupted, and things did not go as I wished. Now it is over, the flowers are withered, and the moon is broken! If you honor our past affection, bury me on Shugang Hill. Also, take care of my six-year-old younger brother, and I will die content!" Qian Sheng and his friend were greatly surprised and tried to comfort her, but Zhang Suqin, unable to bear the strain, took poison and died without telling anyone. Qian Sheng then spent several hundred gold pieces to bury Zhang Suqin on Shugang Hill and provide for her young brother. Ah! In the afterlife, we can pity the fate of beautiful but unfortunate women; her perseverance despite many setbacks led her to recognize the barren hill and yellow earth. The summoning of the lotus lament, the fading of the fragrance of young girls. Among the courtesans, there is such a person, and in the brothels, another fine story is added.

    589

    记钱姬假途脱籍事
    　　
    　　天下情人，终成眷属，世间好事，每致多磨。故情之所萃，意果独钟，不惜委曲以将，婉转以赴。正不止如马盼盼借大腹贾为彼岸慈航，安得谓青楼自古无情地哉？然得于此，必失于彼。苟非其人，万勿醉生梦死于裙带中而不一悟，为他人作嫁衣裳，亦殊不值得耳。钱丽君者，吴门校书也。莲性虽胎，不遭泥染。杨枝无力，偶宕风前。虽处章台，固非随浪逐波之女子也。与陈生善，有啮臂盟。陈有怀依玉，无屋营金，不敢以妙手空空儿蹈红绡故智，而事竟不集。

    Explanation

    589

    The Story of Qian Lijun's Escape from Her Register
    　　
    All lovers will eventually become a couple, but good things in the world often come with many difficulties. Therefore, when affection is concentrated, the heart truly favors, and one is willing to take detours and make gentle efforts to achieve it. It is not just like Ma Panpan, who borrowed a wealthy merchant as a means to escape her fate; how can one say that brothels have always been heartless places? However, what one gains, one must lose elsewhere. If not for the right person, one should never indulge in a life of pleasure and forgetfulness in the company of women without a moment of awakening, nor work hard for the benefit of others without personal gain, as it is truly not worth it. Qian Lijun, a courtesan from Wu Gate, possessed a pure nature, though born in the mire, she remained untainted by corruption. Her willow branch is weak, occasionally swaying in the wind. Living in Chang'an, she is not a woman who follows the crowd. She had a close friendship with Chen Sheng, and they made a blood pact. Chen had a fondness for jade but no place to live, and he dared not use his clever tricks to achieve his goals, and thus the matter did not come to fruition.

    590

    万不得已，无可奈何，任其光阴荏苒，送旧迎新而已。有某者，蜀中大贾也。旅于吴，慕姬名，备蜀锦十端，踵门请见。丽姗姗来迟，贾恨相见晚，逾于所欢。由此雨夜雪霄，花晨月旦，某非姬不乐，姬去某难欢。人情冷暖，世态炎凉，固不必身入侯门，而前此之陈生，固已早同陌路矣。一日，谓某曰：“昔日以勾栏为香粉地狱，君能使火坑内现出莲花否耶？情好如君，若终作露水夫妻，路墙花柳，岂不徒呼负负！出桎梏而登衽席，君有意乎？”某曰：“惟卿所命。

    Explanation

    590

    In a situation of absolute necessity and complete helplessness, one must let time pass, transitioning from one year to the next. There was a wealthy merchant from Sichuan who traveled to Wu and admired the name of Ji. He prepared ten pieces of Shu brocade and knocked on the door to request an audience. Ji arrived beautifully late, and the merchant regretted meeting her too late, finding her more joyful than previous happiness. From then on, whether it was rainy nights and snowy skies, or flowered mornings and full moons, the merchant was not happy without Ji, and it was hard to be happy when Ji left. The warmth and coldness of human feelings, and the warmth and coldness of the world, did not require one to enter a noble household, but even previous friends like Chen had already become strangers. One day, Ji said to the merchant, "In the past, the brothel was seen as a hell of perfume and powder. Can you make a lotus flower appear in the fire pit? With a friend like you, if we end up as temporary lovers, street wall flowers and willows, wouldn't we just cry out in regret! To escape from shackles and step onto the bed, do you have the intention?" The merchant replied, "As you command."

    591

    文姬归汉，千金之义，岂让曹瞒。所不可知者，恐卿心匪石耳。”丽曰：“山移谷变，誓死靡他；石烂海枯，钟情不易。君肯一为援手，妾且生死惟命。”于是某商于鸨，以八百金脱其籍。将有成议，丽曰：“青楼龟鸨，千古无情，一出此门，则寸缕尺丝，均非我有。妾将以蛮头秃袖入乎？”某曰：“微子言，我已筹之烂熟。”于是吴绫蜀锦，为作嫁衣；缕金箱子，折叠其中，舁以畀女。择期雇轿，迎娶以归，僦居于吴。琼华仙子，下嫁蓬莱。不阅月，而衫痕眉黛，云影风声，销归乌有。

    Explanation

    591

    Cao Zhi's return to Han was an act of great moral significance, one that could not be compared to Cao Cao's actions. What is uncertain is whether your heart is as unchanging as stone. She replied, "Even if mountains move and valleys change, I swear never to change; even if stones rot and seas dry up, my loyalty remains unchanged. Will you lend me a hand? I will follow your commands, whether it leads to life or death." He then negotiated with the brothel owner and paid eight hundred gold to free her. When they were about to finalize the arrangement, she said, "Brothels and their owners have been heartless for a thousand years. Once I leave this place, every thread and strand will no longer belong to me. Will I have to enter poverty?" He replied, "Without your words, I have already thought this through carefully." He then prepared luxurious fabrics from Wu and Shu, made a bridal gown, and placed it in a golden-threaded box. They chose a date, hired a carriage, and married her, taking her to live in Wu. It was as if a fairy from the Jade Flower descended to marry in Penglai. Within a month, the stains on her clothes and traces of makeup, the cloud shadows and wind sounds, all faded away into nothingness.

    592

    然衣裳在笥，犹不疑其有他也。急启葳蕤，而已空诸所有。盖以为云神女，已同奔月嫦娥；侦骑四处，杳无影响矣。或曰：此桓伊假途之故智也。丽以陈生为心上人，而陈实格于资，不能举手。适逢其偶，某之贸贸然来，于是与陈欲即反离，求亲姑疏，假手于某，以为从陈地步。初本疑信参半。有识陈者，见陈于数日前，曾过姬室，故知其从陈而去，略无疑义云。我闻如是，人云亦云。情波变幻，欲海迷离，月地花天，过于蜃楼海市。走马王孙，坠鞭公子，尚无错于用情，而为彼美之播弄也。

    Explanation

    592

    Despite the clothes being in the box, there was still no doubt that something was amiss. Hastily opening the lush and flourishing garments, they found the box empty of everything. It was thought to be a cloud goddess, already like Chang'e who flew to the moon; scouts were everywhere, yet there was no trace or effect. Some said: This is the old strategy of Huanyi taking a detour. Li considered Chen Sheng as her heart's desire, but Chen was constrained by his circumstances and could not act. Happening to meet the right person, I came carelessly, and thus with Chen, I wanted to come and then leave, seeking closeness but remaining distant, using me as a tool to follow Chen's path. Initially, doubt and belief were equal. Those who know Chen saw him a few days ago, having passed by her room, so they knew he had left with her, with slight doubt. I heard as such, and others say the same. Emotional waves change, the sea of desire is bewildering, moonlit ground and flower-filled sky, exceeding the mirages of the sea. Young nobles riding horses, young nobles dropping their whips, still not wrong in using emotions, but being manipulated by the beauty.

    593

    记紫蓉女录事逸事奇逢
    　　
    　　秀才望榜，心怦怦然，恒情也；而不谓属望殷殷者之出于青楼，则已奇。登贤书，纳宠姬，育鳞儿，乐事也；而不谓纷至沓来者之集于弥月间，则尤奇。若吴门陈生之事所纪焉。生，名士也。少孤力学，丸熊画荻，皆母氏所陶成。弱冠，补博士弟子员，每试辄列优等，师友各以伟器目之，而生亦毅然不作第二人想。顾负才自许，拓落不羁。二三朋侪，时作狎邪之游。白征歌，红牙按拍，酒绿灯红之地，时复相与流连，母亦不之禁也。春间以就试故，僦居省门。

    Explanation

    593

    Record of the Strange Encounter with the Clerk Zicong

    Scholar hopefuls looking at the examination results, their hearts thumping with excitement, is a common sentiment; it is indeed strange that those with great expectations would come from a brothel. Being listed among the successful candidates, taking a concubine, and giving birth to a promising son, these are joyful events; it is even more extraordinary that so many things would come together in the first month. Such is the case with the story of Chen from Wu. Chen is a famous scholar. Orphaned at a young age, he studied hard, and his mother educated him through traditional methods. At the age of 20, he was admitted as a student of a doctoral candidate, always ranking among the top in every exam. His teachers and friends regarded him as a person of great potential, and he was determined not to be second best. Despite his talent, he was unrestrained and free-spirited. With a few close friends, he often engaged in debauchery. Singing for wealthy patrons, playing high-quality musical instruments, and lingering in places of entertainment, his mother also did not prohibit it. In spring, because of the exams, he rented a place near the provincial capital.

    594

    悦李绣蓉女校书，因与定情。豆寇梢头，不知春风几度。绣蓉貌故端好，楚楚自怜；其妹紫蓉，姿态尤艳，双翘瘦不盈指凌波欲渡，风疑仙，鸨母尤爱怜之。会当破瓜之年，每欲为之选婿。生于酒酣耳热之际，许以金屋贮之。紫蓉私幸得人，遂杜门谢客。筵席无生，不复侑酒；非生至，亦不出户。四月间，遂作眠香会矣。昵枕低帷，情好益笃；流连忘返，学业渐荒。无何，紫以孕告，促登室。生故非素封，而又夙秉慈训，未敢遽以告。进退维谷，渐至积愁成疾。梦魂相唤，举止失常。

    Explanation

    594

    The protagonist was delighted by Li Xiurong, a female scholar, and they established a romantic relationship. At the age of youth, the passage of time had been many times over. Li Xiurong was graceful and somewhat self-pitying, and her sister Zirong was particularly beautiful in her demeanor. Zirong had very slim feet, as if she were about to cross water, and the wind seemed to doubt if she was a fairy. The madam of the brothel especially loved and pitied her. When Zirong reached the age of sixteen, the madam often wanted to find a suitable husband for her. The protagonist, in the heat of wine and passion, promised to keep her in a golden house. Zirong secretly hoped to find a suitable partner and thus stayed indoors, declining visitors. At banquets, she no longer served wine, and she only left the house if the protagonist was present. In April, a gathering for napping was held. In the cozy, intimate setting, their affection became even stronger. They lingered so much that they forgot to leave, and the protagonist's studies gradually fell by the wayside. Soon after, Zirong informed the protagonist of her pregnancy and urged him to marry her. The protagonist, however, was not from a wealthy family and had always upheld kind teachings from a young age, so he dared not immediately tell her. Caught between a rock and a hard place, he gradually fell ill from accumulated worries. Dreams and spirits called out to each other, and his behavior became unusual.

    595

    母知之而无如何，惟诫以勤揣摩，励精进，苟秋风得意，则虽为之脱籍，不汝靳也。无如生痼疾已深，狂游如昔。试期将届，置若罔闻。母训之而如故，紫规之而亦如故。而时已七月矣，紫知其不可以言虚勖也。使之下帷于己院，为攻苦地。青灯佐读，红袖添香，复为之考勤惰，课工拙。操业少怠，即白眼相加，将以青楼作绛帐焉。鸨欲夺其志，矢不渝。强嬲之，则与龃龉，之死靡他之志，百折而不回。比生入闱，则又长斋绣佛，朝夕焚香顶祝，属望之切，盖较诸生及生母尤甚焉。揭晓之夕；四出侦探，傍徨中夜，杳无好音。

    Explanation

    595

    The mother knew this but had no way to change it. She only warned him to study diligently and strive for progress. If he succeeded in the autumn (referring to the imperial examination season), she would even free him from his registered status as a performer without hesitation. However, his chronic illness was deeply rooted, and he continued to indulge in reckless behavior as before. As the exam date approached, he ignored everything. Despite her warnings, he remained unchanged, and even Zi's advice had no effect. By then, it was already July, and Zi knew that empty encouragement would not work. She had him study in his own courtyard, turning it into a place of hard study. She provided a green lamp to aid his reading and a beautiful woman to add fragrance (symbolizing support and comfort). She continued to check on his diligence and assess his skill and clumsiness. If he showed the slightest laziness, she would give him a cold stare, intending to turn the brothel into a place of learning. The madam tried to take away his resolve, but he vowed never to change. She persistently tried to persuade him, but he resisted stubbornly, with a determination to remain faithful until death, and he would not be swayed even by a hundred setbacks. When he entered the examination hall, he fasted and prayed to the Buddha day and night, with intense hopes that were even greater than those of the other students and his mother. On the night the results were announced, she searched everywhere, wandering through the night, but found no good news.

    596

    则手合鸩药一刀圭，持谓生曰：“若不得志，则足为妾道地者，赖有此耳。”生亦为之泫然。漏四下，忽喧声达户外，盖报捷者知生匿姬院，而踪迹之也。生闻捷之下，惊喜欲狂，归家摒挡。旬余，遂议践前约，鸨复故索千金值以靳之。生无如之何，姬以好事多磨，濒于死者再。鸨知其不可夺也，请以二百金归生。生母以爱怜少子故，复如数出藏镪畀之，始得迎登室。往返筹议，盖至是已小阳月矣。归生未逾月，举一子，戚里之赴汤饼会者，咸以阿侯绝类其母。咸请见之，辄惊为天人。而各艳生之遭际，争相传述，因笔而为之记，以见天下有情人终成眷属云尔。

    Explanation

    596

    Then, she prepared a small amount of poison and held it, saying to the man, "If you fail to achieve your ambitions, then I will be able to justify my actions, relying on this." The man was also moved to tears. At the fourth strike of the water clock, suddenly a commotion reached the door, as those who reported the good news knew the man was hiding in the courtesan's quarters and were tracking him down. Upon hearing of the success, the man was filled with joy and nearly went mad, returning home to make preparations. After more than ten days, they finally discussed fulfilling their previous agreement, but the madam again demanded the agreed-upon sum of a thousand gold pieces, being stingy with it. The man had no way to deal with it, and the courtesan, having faced many difficulties, nearly died twice. Knowing that she could not be forced, the madam requested that the man pay two hundred gold pieces to take her back. The man's mother, out of love and pity for her young son, took out the full amount of hidden money and gave it to the man, allowing him to finally welcome her into his room. They discussed and planned back and forth, and it was already the tenth month of the lunar calendar. Within a month of the man's return, the courtesan gave birth to a son. Relatives attended a gathering for noodles, and all thought the child resembled his mother greatly. They competed to recount the story, and because of this, they made a record to show that lovers in the world ultimately become husband and wife.

    597

    记珠江才妓事
    　　
    　　客有谈珠江才妓者，询其名，则汪蟾辉也。汪本南海良家女，秉性温和，吐词隽雅；幼时母授以书，辄能记诵。稍长，爱作小诗，颇有风致。及笄，误嫁娼家，深以为恨，然亦无可如何。惟时时背人饮泣而已。姑亦怜其俊慧，俗客造访，概勿与通；遇文人词客，始令接见。所居小楼半间，窗明几净，法帖奇书，杂陈左右。笙笛之类，不屑置也。客至，焚香煮茗，相对清谈，不杂淫亵语。逢二三知己，或飞觞月下，或分韵花前，兴亦不浅。与番禺徐生菊仙，性情最洽，几于无日不至。常持葵扇乞诗，生戏题二绝云：不须蝉噪画来工，已得常持素手中。好向小亭花影里，扑来萤火一星红。欲锡嘉名定合欢，暑消三伏胜裁纨。只愁约赴黄昏后，故障娇容不许看。既而生父闻之，严加防范；欲寻旧好，不得其便。

    Explanation

    597

    A Tale of a Talented Courtesan of the Pearl River

    There was a guest who spoke of a talented courtesan of the Pearl River. When asked for her name, he replied that it was Wang Chanhui. Wang was originally from a good family in Nanhai, known for her gentle nature and elegant speech. From a young age, her mother taught her books, and she could memorize them effortlessly. As she grew older, she developed a love for writing short poems, which were quite charming. At the age of fifteen, she was mistakenly married to a brothel, deeply regretting the situation, though she could do nothing about it. She would often cry secretly. Her mother-in-law also pitied her intelligence and grace, and common visitors were generally not allowed to see her. Only literati and poets were permitted to meet her. She lived in a small, clean, and well-lit room, with calligraphy and rare books displayed around her. She did not care for musical instruments like flutes and pipes. When guests came, she would burn incense, brew tea, and engage in refined conversation, avoiding any indecent or vulgar remarks. With a few close friends, she would enjoy poetry and wine under the moon or among flowers, finding great pleasure in these activities. She had a particularly close relationship with Xu Sheng Juxian from Panyu, visiting almost every day. He would often bring a fan and ask her to write poetry on it. Playfully, he wrote two poems for her:

    No need for cicadas to draw intricate designs,
    Already, I hold it in my hand, white and pure.
    Best to seek it in the shadow of a small pavilion,
    Where a single red firefly comes to light.

    Desiring a good name to bring joy,
    The cooling effect of the fan surpasses even the finest silk.
    Only I worry about meeting after dusk,
    For her delicate beauty is not meant to be seen in the dark.

    Later, Xu Sheng's father heard about their relationship and strictly prevented further contact. They could no longer easily resume their previous relationship.

    598

    汪犹未知也，以书招之，不至，因缄诗以寄云：情书昨已倩鳞鸿，满拟西窗话旧衷。不意近来踪迹阔，仍将离恨寄丝桐。记否当年月下时，双携素手步迟迟；总然未定三生约，合向春风忆旧知。半缕情丝表热肠，更裁诗句问平康。倘无别院娇姿恋，妾拟邀君共一觞。君如许妾卜佳期，宜惜流光若马驰。春去苦留留不住，及今犹有好花枝。生读之，感念昔游，亦寄诗以谢云：初度相逢卿忆否？嫩凉天气近中秋。凭栏共玩西楼月，残夜疏帘半下钩。醉月评花兴一般，每逢佳日共追欢。怜余抱恙秋风下，药检奇方手自丸。舟从邻郡乍归时，即寄鱼笺报我知。无限离情浑不诉，先持葵扇乞新诗。裁将佳句诉情浓，更剪香云密寄侬。良会渐稀无别故，只缘仆性近疏慵。及秋，生赴试羊城，竟寓其家，缠绵甚挚。闻生拟以金赠其姑，迎置别馆，想姻缘簿必能为其如意珠也。
    　　
    　　蚌精

    Explanation

    598

    Wang was still unaware of this. He invited him with a letter, but he did not come, so he sealed a poem to send along with it, saying:

    The love letter was sent yesterday by the messenger of fish and geese, fully intending to talk about old feelings under the west window. Unintentionally, his recent movements have been broad, still entrusting the sorrow of separation to the lute. Do you remember the time under the moonlight? Holding hands and walking slowly. Even though the three-life pact is not yet settled, it is fitting to recall old friends in the spring breeze. A strand of emotional thread expresses warm feelings, further crafting verses to inquire about peace and prosperity. If there is no affection for the beauty in another courtyard, I would like to invite you to share a cup of wine. If you agree, I will choose a good time, it is advisable to cherish the fleeting time like a galloping horse. Spring goes away, and it is hard to keep it, still there are beautiful flowers today. After reading it, he felt nostalgic for past travels and also sent a poem to thank him, saying:

    Do you remember our first meeting? The cool weather is approaching the Mid-Autumn Festival. To lean on the railing and enjoy the moon at the west tower, in the late night, the sparse curtain is half lowered. Drunk on the moon and appreciating flowers,兴致相同. Every time on a good day, we enjoy ourselves together. Pity me for being ill under the autumn wind, to check the strange medicine and make pills by hand. When returning by boat from a neighboring county, immediately send a letter to inform me. Infinite feelings of separation are not expressed, first hold a fan and ask for new poetry. To craft beautiful verses to express deep feelings, to further cut and send fragrant clouds to you. Good meetings are becoming rare without any special reason, only because I am naturally lazy. In autumn, the person went to take the examination in Guangzhou, ended up staying in her family, very intimate and sincere. Hearing that the person intended to give gold to her mother-in-law, to welcome and place in a separate house, thinking that the marriage register will surely make her a perfect match.

    Baijing

    599

    何元者，姓氏莫详，亦不知其何许人。乔寓佛镇，以扮蚌精得名，人皆呼之为蚌精。元美丰姿，清眸传盼，神彩秀发，虽古之宋朝弥瑕，不是过也。前镇中有好事者，庆演秋景会，以多金延之作蚌精。于时皓月呈辉，红灯射彩，夜影迷离之下，愈觉妩美异常，体态绰约，观者果已忘其为童男，更疑其为仙女，飘然霞举，离碧落而至红尘也。于是，元之名噪于一时。及陈村有盛会，出数十金延之往，所演春色，亦推第一。其为人亦如玉树临风，琼林映月。镇中闺阁诸女子，有不愿作纨绔妻，而愿为蚌精奉箕帚者无数；特以月下老人，未为撮合，故坦腹东床之选，未能及之。今月，元竟不费一钱，而得美妇，盖其妇固再醮者也。先是妇为某甲妻，姿容妖冶，态度风流，而裙底莲钩，又复峭如菱角；轻躯鹤立，艳影鸿翩，见之者无不色授魂飞。不意红颜命薄，归甲未一载，而甲以消渴病遽殒。其姑劝其勿嫁，盖冀暮年有所依也。妇亦知姑意，聊应之。

    Explanation

    599

    He was a man named He Yuan, whose surname and origin were unknown. He lived in a town called Fo Zhen and gained fame by portraying the role of a "Baijing" (a mythical creature). People commonly referred to him as "Baijing." Yuan possessed a beautiful appearance, with clear eyes that conveyed his charm, and a radiant and elegant demeanor. His beauty surpassed even that of the legendary beauty from the Song Dynasty, Mi Xia. During a celebration of autumn scenery in the town, he was hired with a large sum of money to perform as the "Baijing." Under the bright moonlight and colorful lanterns, he appeared even more enchantingly beautiful. His graceful posture made the audience forget that he was a young boy, and they began to suspect that he was a fairy, floating down from the heavens to the mortal world. As a result, Yuan's fame spread widely at that time. When there was a grand event in Chen Village, they offered him a large sum of money to perform there, and the performance he gave during the spring festival was considered the best. His demeanor was like a jade tree standing in the wind and a crystal forest reflecting the moon. Many women in the town would rather be his wife than marry a wealthy but uninteresting man. However, the matchmaker (Yue Xia Laoren) had not yet brought them together, so he had not yet found a suitable match. This month, he unexpectedly found a beautiful wife without spending any money. It turned out that his wife was a widow who had been married before. She was once the wife of someone named "Mou Jia," known for her beautiful appearance and charming demeanor. Her ankles were slender and sharp like a water chestnut. Her figure was as graceful as a crane, and her shadow as elegant as a swan. Those who saw her were captivated by her beauty. Unfortunately, her beauty was short-lived; she had been married to "Mou Jia" for less than a year when he died of diabetes. Her mother-in-law advised her not to remarry, hoping that her daughter-in-law would provide support in her old age. The widow understood her mother-in-law's intentions and agreed.

    600

    然＊＊＊＊之中，不忘涂泽，志在择对从人。一日天赋奇缘，元适经其门外，妇适偕某妪倚门小立。见元来，注目视之，不转一睫。元见之，亦为神夺，不觉行步踟蹰，徘徊不进。斯时觌面相逢，只隔银河一水耳。某媪见妇神情若失，戏之曰：“娘子亦识此人为蚌精阿元乎？元固以艳名著遐迩者也。渠虽男子，乃如弁而钗者，其一种温柔无异于女子也。娘子若得嫁渠，诚为三生有幸矣！”妇红潮上颊，沉吟不语，某媪遂为之作撮合山。

    Explanation

    600

    In the midst of [the situation], she did not forget to apply cosmetics, her intention being to choose a good match and follow someone. One day, fate brought them together, and Yuan happened to pass by her door. The woman was standing by the door with a certain old lady.
