When she saw Yuan, she looked at him intently, not blinking an eyelid. Yuan saw her and was also captivated by her, unconsciously hesitating and lingering. At that moment, they met face-to-face, separated only by a river like the Milky Way. The old lady noticed that the woman's expression was as if she had lost something and teased her by saying, "Do you recognize this person as the pearl spirit Yuan? He is indeed famous for his beauty far and wide. Although he is a man, he is like a man with a hat and a woman with hairpins, and his gentleness is no different from that of a woman. If you can marry him, it would truly be a great fortune for three lifetimes!" The woman blushed, fell silent in thought, and the old lady then set about bringing them together.

    601

    于上已后三日，竟系红丝，归于元焉。花烛之夕，极尽燕婉，虽鸳鸯之戏水，鸾凤之回风，无以喻其乐也。元之诸友，皆集资贺其得佳妇。元设宴款之，于是飞斝传觞。正尔欢洽，忽有叩门索债者，正妇之姑也。盖妇之嫁元，乘姑扫墓而窃奔者也。其姑见元，百般凌诟，元诸友为之排解，元曰：“是非余钻穴逾墙所得，乃妇自从我，与我何尤？即于官亦无惧也。”媪为语塞。元诸友出数金，纳其袖使去，于是元遂拥妇安居焉。今僦屋于佛山潘巷。一对璧人，一时佳话，记之以博一轩渠。

    Explanation

    601

    On the third day after the Upper Yuan Festival, the red threads were finally tied, and the woman was united with Yuan. On the wedding night, they enjoyed the utmost in marital bliss, their joy surpassing even that of the playful鸳鸯 in the water or the graceful鸾凤 in the wind. Yuan's friends pooled their resources to congratulate him on his fortunate marriage. Yuan hosted a banquet to entertain them, and as they reveled in the festivities, suddenly there was a knock at the door from a creditor, who turned out to be the bride's mother-in-law. It was revealed that the bride had eloped with Yuan while her mother-in-law was away on a tomb-sweeping visit. When the mother-in-law saw Yuan, she berated him in every way, but Yuan's friends intervened to mediate. Yuan calmly said, "This union was not the result of any clandestine or improper behavior on my part; it was the bride who chose to come to me. What fault could I have in that? Even if we were to face the authorities, I would not fear." The mother-in-law was left speechless. Yuan's friends then gave her a few coins, slipped them into her sleeve, and sent her away. Thus, Yuan and his wife settled down to live together. They now rent a house in the Pan Lane of Foshan. This beautiful couple became the subject of a lovely tale, one that was recorded to share with a small circle of friends.

    602

    品花剩语
    　　
    　　打狗桥至老北门口，路几半里，列屋而居者，江北流娼，动千百计。至夜，门外皆缀一灯，从打狗桥一望无际，如繁星然。然牛鬼蛇神，皆作夜叉形状。夕阳西坠，排列门前，粗脂笨粉中，求一略似人形者，渺不可得。盖彼处俗名“花烟间”，真所谓香粉地狱也。鞭丝帽影，固不屑勾留；舞扇歌衫，尤不甘俯就。人云因不敢于粗头乱服中，以求佳丽，如九方皋之相马，赏识于牝牡骊黄外也。秋思方深，散步豫园。茶罢出城，道经是地。于花妖木贼中，忽有翩若惊鸿，矫若游龙者；虽裙布钗荆，而妩媚天然；如孟德曜，不失大家风范。始叹何地无材，固不能拘于俗见也。惜其门户丛杂，屋宇龌龊，难于插足。还望于日斜风定后，斜倚门前；仆于散步时庶几遇之，勿如人面桃花，则幸甚矣！因叹浆家饼肆，狗屠钓客，怀才未遇者，不知几何；论才者慎勿眼光如豆，拘于俗见也哉！

    Explanation

    602

    Remnants of Flower Appreciation

    From Daga Bridge to the Old North Gate, the road is nearly half a mile long, lined with houses where prostitutes from the north bank of the river, numbering in the thousands, reside. By night, each house has a light outside, and from Daga Bridge, they stretch out endlessly, like stars. Yet, the people are like demons and monsters, all with nightmarish appearances. As the sun sets, they line up before the doors, their makeup and attire crude and unappealing, making it impossible to find one who even vaguely resembles a human. This place is commonly known as "Flower Smoke Interspace," truly a hell of perfumes and powders. The wealthy, with their whip and hat shadows, disdain to linger; the entertainments of dancing fans and singing robes, they are unwilling to stoop to. It is said that one dares not seek beauty among the unkempt and disheveled, like Ji Fanggao recognizing a good horse by looking beyond its outward appearance, appreciating the essence beyond the surface. As autumn thoughts deepen, I take a stroll in Yuyuan Garden. After tea, I leave the city, passing through this area. Among the women and men who are not well-dressed, suddenly there is one who moves like a startled swan, graceful and agile like a dragon; though dressed in simple skirts and wearing plain hairpins, she is naturally charming; like Meng Deyao, she retains the grace of a noble family. I begin to marvel that there is no place without talent, and one cannot be bound by conventional views. Unfortunately, the doors and windows are cluttered, and the houses are squalid, making it difficult to enter. I hope to lean against the door after the sun sets and the wind calms; perhaps I will meet her while I am walking, without it being like the fleeting beauty of a peach blossom, which would be fortunate indeed! Therefore, I reflect on the various tradespeople, including those who make drinks, sell baked goods, butcher dogs, and fish, who are talented but not yet recognized, how many are there? Those who judge talent should be careful not to have a narrow view, bound by common opinions.

    603

    李芸小记
    　　
    　　白门，妓薮也。板桥旧迹，芜草皆香。有校书李芸，年齿稍长，风韵超其侪。偶僦屋莫愁湖畔，编竹为篱，泊然雅素；抚琴洗研，晏如也。初未知名，吴门秦子钟吾，过江访艳，赠之以诗，书于冷金笺上，粘于围屏，以碧纱笼之，于是名倾白下。唯性极高傲，若不当意，虽贵客大贾，不纳也。当时江左章台，竞尚华丽。芸虽负盛名，独莳花种竹，非文酒之宴不预。有妹曰绿媛，姿容慧丽，较芸尤艳。善为酒纠，并善诙谐；词意之间，翩翩有致。兼工箫笛，发声清越，足以怡情，士林称为双绝。芸后归黄吏部公子，宠之专房。喜画梅，埽枝圈花，顷刻数幅；老干纷披，间作娟态。绿媛年二十，误适匪人。河东狮子，日吼数声，未一年，抑郁而死，广陵散亦遂绝矣。岂顿老琵琶，小文琴韵，只应天上有乎？录之以补余澹翁《板桥杂记》之缺。
    　　
    　　卷四
    　　
    　　吴门张少卿校书花烛词并序

    Explanation

    603

    Little Records of Li Yun

    White Gate, a haven for courtesans. The old traces of the Board Bridge, even the weeds are fragrant. There was a scholar named Li Yun, whose age was somewhat advanced, and her grace surpassed her peers. She occasionally rented a house by Lake Mochou, weaving bamboo into a fence, living in plain and elegant simplicity; she played the zither and washed the inkstone, remaining calm and composed. Initially unknown, Qin Zizhongwu from Wu Gate crossed the river to seek beauty, and presented her with a poem, written on cold gold paper, pasted on a screen, and covered with green gauze, thus her fame spread throughout White Gate. However, she had an extremely high and proud nature; if she was not pleased, even wealthy guests and merchants were not admitted. At that time in Jiangnan, the courtesans competed for luxury. Although Li Yun was renowned, she alone planted flowers and bamboo, attending only literary and wine gatherings. She had a sister named Lvyuan, whose beauty and intelligence were even more striking than Li Yun's. She was good at managing the wine and also skilled in witty conversation; her words and expressions were graceful and meaningful. She was also proficient in playing the flute and dizi, producing clear and resonant sounds, sufficient to delight the heart, and the literati called them a double rarity. Li Yun later married the son of the Minister of Personnel, and he favored her exclusively. She enjoyed painting plum blossoms, sketching branches and circling flowers, completing several paintings in a moment; the old branches were sprawling, occasionally displaying delicate grace. Lvyuan, at twenty, was mistakenly married to an unworthy man. The 'Lion of Hedong' roared several times a day, and within a year, she died of depression, and the Guangling San (a famous piece of music) was also lost. How could the old sound of the pipa and the delicate music of the qin only exist in heaven? This is recorded to fill the gap in Yu Danong's Miscellaneous Notes on the Board Bridge.

    Fourth Volume

    Flower and Candle Poem by Zhang Shaoqing, the Scholar of Wu Gate, with Preface

    604

    吴门名校书张少卿者，系出毗陵间，小家之碧玉。身依慧水，托雅集于黄。听雨僧庐，梦云禅榻。深情款曲，绮语缠绵。早已游侣名，痴人逐队矣。无何，迹附鸾灯，歌传雀舫。虎邱春暖，荡鸳梦而低昂；鹤市宵深，听弦而掩抑。嗣以金吾禁切，玉女藏踪。深潜于桃李园中，久匿于芰荷丛里。琵琶小拨，檀板轻敲。卷花之鲸浪虽狂，荫叶之莺身自稳。迨乎风姨威息，月姊恩多，结小社于红楼，试新联于翠帖。（自注：有巨公赠以联曰：“少之时不亦乐乎；卿以下何足算也。”）门前车马，巷里笙歌，时盖居干将坊巷也。灯红酒绿之场，非卿不乐；月地花天之下，与子为欢。断三生杜牧之场，名流倾倒；作一夕秦宫之梦，我辈勾留。乃有张小尹观察者，筮仕滇池，羁身沪渎。近复以奉催军糈，解橐吴阊。问柳章台，品花吴市。缘深一面，遽尔销魂；梦结连宵，翕然意满。词托微波之达，好倩蹇修；鉴忘祸水之临，敢称副室。

    Explanation

    604

    A Famous Scholar of Wu Gate named Zhang Shaoqing, descended from the vicinity of Piling, was a modest and virtuous woman. She relied on the wisdom of the water and entrusted refined gatherings to Huang, listening to rain in a monk's hut and dreaming in a cloud-filled meditation room. Her deep affection and tender, elaborate language had already made her famous among travelers, though she might be seen as naive or gullible. Soon, she followed the trail of the phoenix lamp and her singing was passed on the bird boat. In the warm spring of Tiger Hill, the dreams of love rose and fell; in the deep night of Crane Market, listening to the strings, she became subdued. Subsequently, the guards strictly prohibited, and the jade maiden hid her trace. Deeply hidden in the peach and plum garden, long concealed in the lotus and water chestnut grove, she gently plucked the pipa and lightly tapped the檀板. Though the wild waves of the whale-like flower rolled, the swallow's body under the shade of leaves remained steady. When the wind spirit's wrath subsided and the moon goddess's grace abounded, she formed a small society in the red building and tried new couplets on green paper. (Self-note: A great official once gave her a couplet saying, "In youth, is it not also joyful? Below the rank of '卿,' what is worth counting?") Carriages and horses at the door, music and songs in the alley, she lived in the Fangxiang Lane of Ganjiang. A place of red lights and green wine, not enjoyable without her; under the moon and flowers, she was happy with you. Breaking the three lifetimes of Du Mu's field, the elite were captivated; making a night's dream of the Qin palace, we lingered. There was Zhang Xiaoyin, an observer, who divined his career in Dianchi and was tied up in Shanghai. Recently, he was tasked with delivering military supplies and arrived in Wuzhen. Visiting the willow trees on Zhangtai and appreciating flowers in Wu Market, a deep connection from one encounter, suddenly soul-stirring; a dream spanning several nights, filled with intent. Words entrusted to the subtle waves, good to ask the messenger; forgetting the impending disaster, daring to call oneself a secondary wife.

    605

    爰访委禽之礼，用图比翼之欢。遂于月之十六日，迎归于曹家巷寓庐。红交拜，金盏同倾，礼也。蓝桥重度，看蝉鬓之斜欹；玉札谁缄，顾鸾弦之再奏。聊疏短引，兼附新诗，藉佐奇谈，以供一噱云尔。词云：卸却黄冠画翠眉，靓妆顾影弄娇姿。无端一夕东君力，博得雍容象服宜。姐妹常时共倚楼，尊前老大各生愁。红颜毕竟多浓福，夫婿居然在上头。七宝香车锦作堆，彩旗绣织两行开。旁人休与争迟疾，八座巍然命妇来。

    Explanation

    605

    Thus, a visit was made to inquire about the betrothal ritual, in order to seek the joy of being as close as wings. The wedding took place on the sixteenth day of the month, and she was welcomed back to the residence in Cao Family Lane. Red robes were exchanged, and golden cups were shared, this is the ritual. Re-crossing the Blue Bridge, watching the slightly tilted hair. Who has sealed the jade tablet, looking back at the lute strings being played again. Briefly writing a short introduction, and adding a new poem, to assist in strange tales, and to provide a laugh. The words say:

    Unbuckle the yellow crown and paint the green eyebrows, dressing up and admiring one's reflection, playing with a charming pose. Suddenly, one night, the power of the eastern king, gaining the grace of a noble lady. Sisters often shared the same balcony, but now, as time passed, each bore their own sorrows. Red beauty, after all, has many blessings, the husband, indeed, is in the lead. The seven treasures carriage is piled with silk, colorful flags and embroidered ribbons line the way. Others may rush to compete, but the eight seats of the noble lady await.

    606

    黄纸新缄御笔封，入门便荷宠光隆。而今休说梁红玉，合向先生拜下风。旧日栖迟王谢堂，新巢京兆有雕梁。不须更说乌衣巷，飞入高枝变凤凰。记得欢场手共携，女床鸾凤换巢栖。琵琶还向江心弄，笑尔浔阳估客妻。大弦急迫小弦舒，合卺青庐事不虚。愿得多情似关盼，深楼常伴老尚书。浓艳凝妆倚晚霞，一枝移植到仙家。苏台春色知何许，多少王孙不看花。此梦芜庵主稿也。叙事周详，言情婉约，而弦外传音，更觉有余不尽，是真得风人之旨者矣。

    Explanation

    606

    A New Imperial Edict Sealed with the Emperor's Own Hand
    Upon entering, one is immediately honored with great favor. Now, do not mention Liang Hongyu; one should bow to the teacher. Once dwelling in the halls of Wang and Xie, now in Jingzhao, there are carved beams. There is no need to mention the Black-clad Lane; soaring to a high branch and transforming into a phoenix. Remember the hand in hand in the place of pleasure; changing nests like the phoenix and the crane. Playing the pipa by the river; laughing at the wife of a merchant from Xunyang. The large strings are urgent, the small strings are relaxed; the marriage in the green tent is not in vain. Wishing for a passionate love like Guan Pan; always accompanying the old Shangshu in the deep building. Dressed in rich colors, leaning against the evening glow; transplanted to the immortal's home. The spring scenery of Su's platform is known where? How many princes do not see the flowers? This dream is the work of the master of Wù An. The narrative is detailed, and the emotions are expressed in a gentle manner. The music beyond the strings, even more so, is endless. This truly captures the essence of the Feng people.

    607

    虎阜名姝与榕城生逸事
    　　
    　　榕城瞿生某，美丰姿，少聪颖。读书过目辄成诵；能琴，尤工绘事。以贫故，弃书学贾，往来吴越间有年矣。吴下故繁华之区，花柳之盛，甲于天下。珠帘十里，箫鼓三更，入其中者，鲜不目迷心醉。故凡巨商大贾，至止是邦，虽较利尽锱铢，而缠头锦则一掷千金而不吝也。瞿生素谨愿，居吴下日久，而未尝作狎邪游。同辈辄非笑之，谓其有道学气。生谢曰：“仆非不好此也。仆素情重，而若辈多无情者。仆恐为情所累，故不愿耳。”一日，有巨绅某招生饮，乘生醉，挟之往青楼中。有妓名张若涛者，字薛仙，丰貌闲雅，吐属温柔；弹琴赋词，敲棋度曲，无一不臻精妙。书法尤工，簪花小格，秀骨天成，为闺阁中之仅见也，以是名噪一时。

    Explanation

    607

    The Famous Beauty of Huafu and the Idle Affairs of a Scholar from Fuzhou

    A certain scholar named Qu from Fuzhou, with a handsome appearance and a keen intellect, could read and memorize anything he read. He was skilled in playing the qin and particularly adept at painting. Because of his poverty, he abandoned his studies to learn trade and had been traveling between Wu and Yue for many years. Wu is a former prosperous area, where the prosperity of flowers and willows ranks first in the world. Jade curtains stretch for miles, and flutes and drums sound until the third watch of the night. Those who enter are rarely not captivated by the sights and sounds. Therefore, all wealthy merchants, when they arrive in this region, though they calculate profits down to the smallest detail, they spend thousands of gold coins on entertainment without hesitation. Qu, who is naturally cautious and virtuous, has lived in Wu for a long time but has never engaged in lewd or improper activities. His peers often mock him, saying he has a scholarly air. Qu replied, "I am not uninterested in this. I value emotions deeply, while many of you lack such feelings. I fear being burdened by emotions, so I do not wish to engage in such activities." One day, a wealthy scholar invited him to drink. Taking advantage of his drunkenness, he took him to a brothel. There was a courtesan named Zhang Ruotao, with the style name Xue Xian, who had a graceful appearance and gentle speech. She played the qin and composed poetry, played chess and sang, and nothing she did was not exquisite. Her calligraphy was especially skilled, with flowers beautifully arranged in small patterns, and a natural elegance. She was rare to see among women, and thus her name became famous for a time.

    608

    王孙贵戚，慕名求见者踵相接，而若涛意殊落落，少所许可。是时生乘醉而来，举目四顾，于两行红粉中，有乞取紫云之意。某绅见生情景，笑曰：“某呆甫入温柔乡，便真个销魂耶？昔日之假惺惺胡为者？”因命置酒，为生与若涛合欢。迨漏三下，某等皆扶醉归，生独留焉。若涛初见生，颇不满意，乃伪醉而假寐。生傍徨室中，见其布置精雅，图书满架，壁间悬古琴一张，不觉触所素好，思一奏技，又恐惊其清梦。屏息枯坐，夜已将阑矣。少顷，若涛始醒。生笑曰：“美哉睡乎？”若涛不答，从容对镜理鬓讫，即以炉焚香，向壁间抱琴下，敛容抚之，极目送手挥之妙。弹未半，忽为变徵之音，凄凄切切，如泣如诉。生听之，不觉凄然欲泪，所弹盖胡笳十八拍也。

    Explanation

    608

    The descendants of kings and nobles, those who came to see him by reputation were so numerous that they followed one another like the links of a chain, yet Iflow's demeanor was particularly aloof, and he rarely gave his approval. At that time, the guest came while drunk, looked around, and among the rows of beautiful women, he had the intention of seeking out the Purple Cloud. A certain gentleman saw the situation and laughed, saying, "Just entering the land of pleasure, and already losing one's soul? What was the pretense of the past?" He then ordered to set up wine and made a match between the guest and Iflow. Until the third drum beat, the others all returned while supporting each other due to drunkenness, but the guest remained alone. Upon first seeing the guest, Iflow was quite dissatisfied, so she pretended to be drunk and feigned sleep. The guest wandered in the room, seeing her arrangement was elegant, with books filling the shelves, and a ancient zither hanging on the wall. Unconsciously, he was reminded of his own fondness for music, thinking of playing a piece, but fearing he might disturb her peaceful sleep. He held his breath and sat silently, and the night was nearly over. After a short while, Iflow began to wake up. The guest smiled and said, "How beautiful the sleep was!" Iflow did not respond, calmly adjusting her hair in front of the mirror, then burning incense in a brazier, went to the wall to pick up the zither, with a serious expression, played it, her eyes fixed and hands moving gracefully. Halfway through, suddenly changing to a mournful tone, sorrowful and plaintive, like weeping and lamenting. The guest listened to it, unconsciously feeling sorrowful and about to cry, the piece she played was the "Eighteen Beats of the Huqin."

    609

    若涛见生如此，罢弹问生曰：“君亦能此乎？何所感之深耶？”生曰：“卿以此自寓冷落之感，仆亦同此情者；入耳惊心，能不悲从中来耶？”若涛闻言，默然久之，谓生曰：“试更为君弹一曲，可乎？”于是重理旧弦，别翻新调；生倾听之际，愈加感叹，曰：“伯牙钟期，千载难遇；卿弹此高山流水之操，而以知音许我，我何敢当。然如卿者，亦未始非青楼中之伯牙也。”若涛自是始有喜色，与生剪烛，窗前娓娓谈家事。东方既白，亦无暇作巫山之梦矣。生归寓后，魂梦颠倒，颇不自持。次日，若涛遣人来，赠生瑶琴一张，玉笛一枝，玉二事，诗扇一握。扇为若涛亲笔所画，诗亦近作也。生得之狂喜，思为琼瑶之报。

    Explanation

    609

    If Tao saw the student like this, she stopped playing and asked him, "Can you also do this? What is the deep feeling you have?" The student replied, "You express your feelings of loneliness through your music, and I share the same emotions; hearing it moves me deeply." If Tao remained silent for a long time, then said to the student, "Let me play another piece for you, shall I?" She then retuned her strings and played a new tune. As the student listened, he felt even more moved, saying, "Yu Boya and Zhong Ziqi, such a friendship is hard to find even after a thousand years; you play this piece 'High Mountains and Flowing Water' and consider me your kindred spirit, how dare I accept such a high compliment. But in your case, you are not unlike Yu Boya in the context of a brothel."

    From then on, If Tao began to show a smile and, along with the student, trimmed the wick of the candle, talking about family matters gently and continuously by the window. When the east turned white, there was no time left for a night of passion. After returning to his lodging, the student's thoughts and dreams were in turmoil, and he could hardly control himself. The next day, If Tao sent someone to give the student a precious stringed instrument, a jade flute, two jade pendants, and a fan with poetry written on it. The fan was personally painted by If Tao, and the poems were recent works as well. The student received them with great joy, thinking of a precious and beautiful return gift.

    610

    适有人携周《汉宫春晓图》求售，生以白金双百易之；复购得汉玉连环一件，自绘梅花帐沿一幅，翡翠管紫颖一床，亲携之往，谓若涛曰：“明翠羽，卿固有之，仆亦不敢以俗物溷卿清赏。此区区者，虽不足贵，然亦非寻常绣闼中，所能解识者。风雅如卿，当留作红闺雅伴也。”若涛欣然曰：“妾以弱质，溷落红尘，君独不视为章台柳，而宠异之若此，妾当悬佩终生，不啻如太真之金钗钿合矣。特未知君子之心，固何如耳！”自是往来益密。

    Explanation

    610

    Just then, someone brought the Han Palace Spring Dawn by Zhou to sell. The author exchanged two hundred taels of silver for it, then purchased a Han dynasty jade double-ring, painted a plum blossom curtain border himself, and prepared a bed with emerald-green and purple ink holders. He personally carried these items over to Ruotao and said to her, "You already have a bird with bright feathers. I也不敢 with common things to disturb your refined enjoyment. This little thing, though not very valuable, but also not something that can be understood in ordinary chambers. As elegant as you are, it should be kept as a refined companion in the inner chamber." Ruotao replied happily, "I, with my weak nature, have fallen into the red dust. You do not see me as a willow on Changtai, treating me so specially. I will treasure it all my life, no less than the golden hairpin and inlaid comb of Tai Zhen. But I don't know what the gentleman's heart truly is." From then on, their visits became more frequent.

    611

    一日，若涛告生曰：“明晨花朝，妾等姐妹为盒子会。画船箫鼓，于虎丘山塘间，作竟日清游。但须各奏一技，琴棋书画，唯其所能。君盍同往一游，为绘画以志盛会，何如？”生许之。翌日，生与若涛偕往。于是众美毕集，或拈毫觅句，或对局弹棋，或抚冰弦，或摹晋帖。须臾酒炙杂陈，云竞奏。生于其间，左顾右盼，目眩神移，恍置身蕊珠宫，亲按宾云小队矣。酒酣，伸纸作图，点染工致，并以八分书题其图曰：“闹红一舸。”诸美人喜，竞以巨觞为寿。

    Explanation

    611

    One day, Iflowing told the artist, "Tomorrow is the Huazhao Festival, and my sisters and I will hold a box banquet. We will travel all day in a decorated boat, accompanied by flutes and drums, along the Tiger Hill canal, enjoying a serene outing. Each of us will perform a skill—whether it be playing the qin, chess, calligraphy, or painting, whatever we can do. Why don't you join us for a tour and paint a picture to commemorate this grand event? What do you think?" The artist agreed. The next day, the artist went together with Iflowing. Thus, all the beauties gathered, some picking up brushes to search for verses, others playing chess, some playing the qin, and others copying Jin dynasty calligraphy. Soon, various dishes and drinks were served, and they competed to perform. The artist, amidst the scene, looked around, his eyes dazzled and spirit moved, as if standing in the Palace of Rui Zhu, personally leading a small group of guests. When intoxicated, the artist stretched out paper to draw a picture, meticulously painting it, and inscribed it in eight-part script: "A Boat in the Midst of Red Flowers." The beauties were pleased and competed to offer large cups of wine as a toast.

    612

    若涛曰：“如此雅集，有图不可无诗。”因援笔赋诗二绝，诗曰：春波潋滟绿湔裙，夹岸花枝点鬓云。难得花朝天气好，酒船归去趁斜曛。点拍飞觥事事宜，群花貌出影迷离。一奁合受薰香供，知否凝眸吮笔时。题毕，生大加叹赏，命酒复酌。少焉红日衔山，乃命榜人理归棹。兰桡桂楫，缓缓游来，生与诸美均凭舱延眺，兴致幽然，迨至家已月上矣。生家本寒素，迩来以居积，稍有盈余；然碌碌依人，自叹殊非长策。若涛知之甚谂，颇以为忧，而未形于外也。一夕，忽谓生曰：“妾甚思食茯苓糕，君明日可向市中购来，然须亲交妾手，勿令他人知也。”生诺之，心窃怀疑，不解其意。次日姑买之携往，甫至门，若涛已于楼头窥见，急迎至楼下。生甫出舆，若涛急以手捧茯苓，伛偻登楼，若携重物者。

    Explanation

    612

    If Tao said, "Such a refined gathering, with illustrations, cannot do without poetry." Then he took up his brush and wrote two poems, which read: The spring waves shimmer like green skirts, and the flowers along the riverbank resemble clouds of hair. It is rare to have good weather during the flower festival, so we return in a boat under the slanting evening sun. The lively and harmonious atmosphere, with the blurred appearance of the flowers, fills the box with fragrant offerings. Do you know the moment of deep concentration while writing? After finishing, the protagonist greatly praised the poems and ordered more wine to drink. Soon, the red sun set behind the mountains, and he ordered the boatman to prepare the return journey. The boat moved slowly, and the protagonist and the beautiful women all leaned against the cabin and looked out, enjoying a serene and pleasant mood. When they arrived home, the moon had already risen. The protagonist's family was originally poor, but recently, through trade, they had a little surplus; however, they were dependent on others and lamented that this was not a good long-term plan. Tao knew this very well and was quite concerned, but he did not show it. One evening, he suddenly said to the protagonist, "I really want to eat Poria cocos cake. You can buy it from the market tomorrow, but you must personally hand it to me, and do not let others know." The protagonist agreed, but secretly doubted and did not understand the meaning. The next day, he bought the cake and took it to the door. Tao had already seen him from the top of the building and quickly welcomed him downstairs. As soon as the protagonist got out of the carriage, Tao quickly held the Poria cocos cake in his hand and bent over to climb the stairs, as if carrying a heavy object.

    613

    入室，即纳之柜中，迄无他语。次晨，生欲去，若涛乃启椟取糕出，仍交生曰：“君可将去矣，妾不须此。”生愈不解，然持携之，似觉甚重。贸然归来，开而视之，则其中金光灿烂，与雪粉颜色相掩映，盖皆永昌赤金叶也，稀之约有五镒云。生惊疑愈甚，即往询若涛，曰：“卿岂以云鬓助妆之品，尚须添置数色耶？果尔，仆当为卿购之，奚用此阿堵物为者？”若涛曰：“君远客异乡，阮囊之羞涩，可想而知。聊以此为君客中买酒之资，想不以为辱金而挥之不顾也。”生固辞不受，若涛乃曰：“妾日来亦无所需此者，君为妾暂存之，可乎？”生始诺之。若涛虽生长娼家，然志气甚傲，每思脱籍从良。顾见来往青楼者，非龌龊之金夫，即浮逸之浪子；但解黄金买笑，未能白首相依；以是郁郁不自得，遂成心疾，时发时止。

    Explanation

    613

    Upon entering the room, he immediately placed it in the cabinet, and there was no further conversation. The next morning, the scholar wanted to leave, and Ruo Tao then opened the box and took out the cake, handing it back to the scholar and saying, "You can take it with you; I no longer need this." The scholar was even more puzzled, but he held the cake, feeling it was unusually heavy. He returned home carelessly, opened it to look inside, and found that it was filled with dazzling golden leaves, shining brightly against the white powder. They were all pieces of Yon-chang red gold, approximately five catties in total. The scholar was even more surprised and suspicious, so he went to ask Ruo Tao, saying, "Do you still need more items to adorn your hair? If so, I will buy them for you; why would you need such a thing?" Ruo Tao replied, "You are a distant guest in a foreign land, and the meager contents of your purse are easily imagined. I just want to give you some money for buying wine while you are away, and I don’t think you would consider it a disgrace to spend it." The scholar firmly refused to accept it, and Ruo Tao then said, "I don’t need this anymore. Can you keep it for me temporarily?" The scholar finally agreed. Although Ruo Tao grew up in a brothel, she had a very proud spirit and often thought of leaving her profession and leading a respectable life. However, she saw the people who frequented the brothels; they were either sordid gold diggers or frivolous playboys. They only understood buying laughter with gold but could not promise a lifelong partnership. Therefore, she was often unhappy and dissatisfied, and she developed a heart condition that flared up and subsided.

    614

    自识生后，见生举止大方，于温柔乡中，颇能用情体贴，即拟为终生之托，而未言也。一日，旧疾复发，生往视疾，询其症之由来，若涛俱以告；词气之间，微露生死相依之意。生感其情，因谓之曰：“卿之心事，仆固知之。但仆上有老母，须禀命而行；且糟糠在室，一旦河东狮吼，卿能堪乎？”若涛曰：“抱衾与，实命不犹；小星之分，妾固甘之。君宜急作书，禀命慈母，妾实不能久居此火坑中也。”言已，泪簌簌下，生亦相向悲泣。后月余，生母书来，责生以大义，言瞿氏从无纳妓女者，切勿败坏家风；且命生返家，勿久恋此邦云云。生得书，持以示若涛，曰：“非仆不欲，其如老母见责何？白头之约，期以来生，卿善自为计可耳。

    Explanation

    614

    Since I recognized [him/her] after birth, I saw [his/her] demeanor was dignified and composed, and in the intimate and affectionate environment, [he/she] showed a lot of affection and consideration. I considered [him/her] as a lifetime commitment, but I did not say anything about it. One day, [his/her] old illness flared up again, and I went to visit [him/her] to inquire about the cause of the illness. [If Tao] told me everything; in the tone and manner of speaking, there was a subtle hint of an inseparable bond even in death. I was moved by [his/her] feelings and said to [him/her], "Your thoughts, I certainly understand. But I have an elderly mother, and I need to seek her permission before acting; moreover, I have a wife who has been through hard times with me, and if I were to leave, she would surely be overbearing. Can you bear that?" [If Tao] said, "To share a quilt, truly a shared fate; to be a minor star in the sky, I willingly accept it. You should write a letter quickly to seek permission from your mother. I cannot stay in this fiery pit for long." After saying this, [s]he shed tears, and I also cried in front of [her/him]. After more than a month, my mother's letter came, rebuking me with great principles, saying that the [Q]u family has never accepted a courtesan, and I must not ruin the family's reputation; and she ordered me to return home, not to linger in this country for long. I received the letter and showed it to [If Tao], saying, "It's not that I don't want to, but how can I face my mother's rebuke? Our promise for a future life, I hope you take good care of yourself."

    615

    ”若涛见书，痴绝良久；及闻生言，不觉失声哭曰：“命也如斯，夫复何言！自今以往，妾无意于人世矣！”遂绝粒不食，生慰藉再三，始强进糜粥。然自此心疾愈甚，面庞清瘦，言语支离，病已入膏盲矣。生在前，尚稍稍作笑语，否则日以眼泪洗面也。会生母又有书来，促生速归；生不得已，束装南旋。若涛知生归，送生至垂虹桥畔，问生再来期，生答以来年春初。若涛泣曰：“妾病已深，旦暮将作泉下入。君明年来，倘念旧情，能至邓尉陈墓间，酹妾一杯酒，九泉有知，当笑倚梅花，来拜君贶也。”生亦掩泣。移时，挂帆去。明年，生来吴，再往访之，则若涛化去久矣。闻其属纩时，尚连呼生字者三，何情之所钟，竟一致于此哉！生于是为之营奠营斋，并至墓下，浇以百花酿酒，曰：“洒不到刘伶坟下土，今日之奠，卿果有知耶？天知耶？”遂痛哭而去，终生不复作青楼梦云。

    Explanation

    615

    Upon reading the letter, Ruo Tao was utterly bewildered for a long time; upon hearing Sheng's words, she involuntarily burst into tears and said, "Fate is like this, what more can be said! From now on, I have no intention of living in this world!" She then refused to eat, and Sheng comforted her repeatedly, forcing her to finally eat a thin porridge. However, from then on, her mental illness worsened, her face became thin and gaunt, her speech became fragmented, and her illness had reached the bone and marrow. When Sheng was present, she would still occasionally make light-hearted remarks, but otherwise, she would wash her face with tears every day. Sheng's mother then wrote to him again, urging him to return home quickly; Sheng had no choice but to pack and return south. Ruo Tao knew that Sheng was returning, so she accompanied him to the side of the Chuihong Bridge, asking about the time of his next visit, to which Sheng replied that it would be early next spring. Ruo Tao wept and said, "My illness is now deep, and I will soon be descending into the underworld. Next year, if you remember our old feelings, could you come to the tomb at Dengwei and pour a cup of wine for me? If there is any consciousness in the underworld, I will laugh and lean on the plum blossoms to come and pay homage to your kindness." Sheng also wept silently. After a while, he hoisted the sail and left. The following year, Sheng came to Wu again and visited her, but Ruo Tao had passed away for a long time. It was heard that at the moment of her death, she still called out Sheng's name three times. How intense was the affection they shared, that it reached such a point! Sheng then arranged for her funeral and memorial services, and went to the tomb, pouring a wine made from a hundred flowers, saying, "The wine does not reach the soil of Liu Ling's grave, but today's offering, does it truly know, Heaven?" He then wept bitterly and left, never again dreaming of the life of a courtesan.

    616

    西冷太瘦生偶记
    　　
    　　余自庚申仲春，遭粤寇后，饥来驱我，始作客游，其间问渡申江，舫行岭表，萍踪转徙，历有年所。追溯往来赠答，与骚人韵士，兴酣握管，分韵拈题，殊数数也。唯粤东为众香国，名花如林。花天酒地之中，檀板金尊之会，丰姿绰约者，容或有之；吐词温雅者，曾不一睹焉；又遑问藏钩射覆，读曲填词，此中人能乎否乎？癸亥秋，宁园太尊奉檄端州，延余入幕。以落落庸材，作翩翩书记；端溪校艺，试院衡文；鄙陋荒芜，深以为愧。暇时偕二三同事，访美河干。

    Explanation

    616

    A Casual Record by the Thin Scholar of Xiling

    In the second month of the Geng Shen year, after encountering the Cantonese rebels, hunger drove me to begin traveling as a guest. During this time, I asked to cross the Huangpu River in Shanghai and sailed through the southern provinces. Like floating duckweed, I moved about aimlessly, living a wandering life for many years. Reflecting on the exchanges of gifts and letters with poets and scholars, we often became so engrossed in our enthusiasm that we would seize our writing brushes, dividing rhymes and selecting themes, doing so frequently. Only in the southeastern region, known as the Land of Fragrant Flowers, were there gardens filled with famous blooms. Amidst the luxurious and hedonistic lifestyle of "a world of flowers and wine," in the refined gatherings of music and fine wine, there might be those with graceful and elegant appearances, but I never saw anyone with warm and elegant speech. Moreover, how could one even inquire about the ability of these people to engage in the games of wit and riddles, or to compose poetry and lyrics? In the autumn of the Gui Hai year, the respected official Ning Yuan received an edict to serve in Duanzhou and invited me to join his entourage. With my mediocre and unremarkable talent, I became a graceful and talented secretary; I examined literary works in Duanzhou and judged them in the examination hall. I felt deeply ashamed of my vulgar and barren work. In my spare time, I would accompany a few colleagues to visit the banks of a beautiful river.

    617

    连掎裳，闲寻风月；而蛮衣窄袖，抹脂涂红，温柔乡无异罗刹国也。洎全浙肃清，补行秋试，余亦理装，将赋归与。同人为余祖饯，半月来殆无虚夕。月明映水，灯彩摇波，鬓影衣香，不酒而醉。乃主宾交错，男女杂坐。有云娘者，意态娴雅，貌亦娟秀，与之接谈，语尤绝俗；见扇头诗，喃喃作诵，问卿能此乎？掩口微笑；由此目成心许，意拳拳也。启行之前数日，向余索送别诗，爰曲绘离情，即席率成三绝，曰：岭海饥驱秋复春，青衫憔悴老风尘。生平毕竟畴知己，第一珠帘半卷人。

    Explanation

    617

    Connecting skirts, leisurely seeking the pleasures of love; with barbarian attire and narrow sleeves, applying makeup and rouge, the place of pleasure is no different from a land of demons. When the entire Zhejiang region was pacified, a supplementary examination was held, and I also prepared to return home. My friends held a farewell banquet for me, and for nearly half a month, there was no idle night. The bright moon reflected on the water, and the colorful lights rippled on the waves. The shadows of hair and the fragrance of clothes, intoxicating without the need for wine. The host and guests mingled, and men and women sat together. There was a woman named Yun娘, who had a graceful demeanor and a beautiful appearance. Talking with her, her speech was especially refined; seeing the poem on the fan, she murmured it aloud. I asked if she could write poetry, and she smiled coyly. From this, we agreed upon something with a look, and our hearts were in harmony. A few days before I set out, she asked me for a farewell poem. I composed a poem that vividly portrayed the emotions of parting, quickly writing three short poems on the spot, saying:

    • Driven by hunger across the mountains and seas from autumn to spring,
    • Wearing a blue robe, worn out and aged by the world,
    • In my lifetime, who truly understands me,
    • The first person who partially raises the pearl curtain.
    618

    好花过眼尽云烟，惆怅今宵又别筵。蜡炬未残更向尽，笔花和泪记良缘。话别分离声暗吞，一腔愁绪一灯昏。生憎鹦鹉偷传语，漏泄春光不敢言。（姬有婢，每于鸨母前播弄唇舌。）云娘朗吟数四，意亦良会。尔时苍茫夜色，几报更筹，云娘意余归署，乃凭肩小语曰：“蒙君知爱，虽一面缘，尚尔前定；岂往还数日，而三生石上，独无前缘在耶？请君留此，妾将以和诗为媒。”裁笺试砚，信笔吟成，诗曰：狼籍烟花十七春，不堪回首堕红尘。郎真爱我还知我，青眼从今有几人。

    Explanation

    618

    Beautiful flowers seen by the eye turn to clouds and mist, melancholy tonight at another farewell banquet. The wax candle has not yet burned out but is nearing its end, I write flowers with tears to remember a good match. The sounds of parting and separation are swallowed in silence, a heart full of sorrow and a dim lamp. I hate the parrot for secretly passing on words, leaking spring light without daring to speak. (The concubine has a maid who often whispers behind the madam's back.) Yun娘 recites these lines several times, her thoughts also deeply moved. At that time, the night was dim and hazy, the night watchman's bell ringing several times, Yun娘 seemed to suggest that I return to my office, and she whispered softly, "Being favored by your kindness, though our meeting is brief, it is still predestined; how could a few days of coming and going, on the Three Lives Stone, there be no predestined connection? Please stay here, I will use my response poem as a matchmaker." She took out a piece of paper and tried the inkstone, and wrote the poem with ease: The chaotic and fleeting nature of seventeen years of spring and flowers, unable to bear looking back at falling into the mundane world. You truly love me and understand me, how many people have the favor of you from now on?

    619

    剧怜故土尽烽烟，幸我良朋列绮筵。妾是解人勤慰藉，三生石上一宵缘。隔溪桃李总无因，（河西为潘氏桃李园）脉脉相思泪欲吞。侬不逢辰郎不遇，一般萧瑟坐黄昏。诗尚清真，意亦凄惋，殊恨相见晚，而相暌太速耳。余归后，来游沪上，七载于兹。时往过访，冀复一遇。彼牛鬼变相，不辨之无者，无论矣。乃有声名藉甚，举止渊雅，亦不识斯文为何物，则又何也？是殆为豪华之所颠倒，日受金银尘俗气，遂使聪明固闭，无由开其奥窍耶！余不敏，请质斯人之日游其中者。

    Explanation

    619

    Deeply lamenting the homeland engulfed in war, blessed am I to have good friends who arrange splendid feasts. I am one who understands and diligently consoles, with a deep, perhaps predestined connection that lasted only a brief night. Across the stream, peach and plum trees stand, always without cause, and I silently long, my tears almost swallowed. I am unlucky, and the man did not meet his fortune; both of us sit in a desolate and bleak evening. The poetry values purity and sincerity, its meaning is sad and poignant, deeply regretting that we met too late, and parted too quickly. After returning, I came to visit Shanghai, and it has been seven years now. Occasionally visiting, hoping to meet again. Those who cannot distinguish the real from the fake, let them go. There are also those who are famous and dignified, yet do not know what literature is, and why is that? It is likely that they are distorted by luxury, daily exposed to the mundane and materialistic, thus causing their intelligence to become closed off, unable to open the door to their inner wisdom. I am not very intelligent, but I ask these people about their daily indulgences.

    620

    情种轻生
    　　
    　　汉镇友人言娼女屈大姑一节，虽其事极鄙秽不足道，而情之所钟，至于如此，亦可矜矣。先是新火路上边地方，有私娼一户，名为住家妓，止一二人，客来无摆酒听曲之事；唯月上花梢，则紧闭洞房，高会巫山之梦。妓如是，客亦可知。殆温柔乡中之黑暗狱也。大姑者，屈姓，年十八，貌不过中材，而有媚态。父业屠，以年迈不堪操持，而又无子，即将大姑堕入此中，藉其夜合之资以为食。是亦老屠多杀之报也。大姑于去年秋后与孝感县之令吏有交。令吏每解税来省，必从大姑小住数日。嗣后往来既稠，情好愈密，然而大姑迫于其父，欲嫁而不能。此吏本无多金，又惧妻妒而不敢。二人辗转计之，总难为比翼之鹣矣，因易同心之结，而为同穴之期。七月望后，日向晚，相与涕泣私语，忽又沐浴更衣。

    Explanation

    620

    Sentimental Suicide

    A friend from the Han Dynasty recounted the story of a prostitute named Qu Daigu. Though the tale is extremely vulgar and unworthy of mention, the depth of her emotional attachment, which led to such an extreme outcome, is truly pitiable. Previously, in a place near the New Fire Road, there was a household of private prostitutes, known as "home-based courtesans," consisting of only one or two women. Guests did not come for feasts or music; instead, when the moon rose high, they would lock themselves in the inner chamber and indulge in the dream of the Wu Mountains. The courtesans were like this, and the guests could be imagined. It was a dark prison in the realm of pleasure.

    Qu Daigu, aged eighteen, was not exceptionally beautiful but had a charming demeanor. Her father was a butcher, but due to his old age and lack of a son, he could no longer manage the business and sold Qu Daigu into this life, relying on her earnings from clients to support himself. This was also a punishment for the old butcher's many killings.

    Qu Daigu had a relationship with a low-ranking official from Xiaogan County last autumn. The official would come to the city to collect taxes and always stayed with Qu Daigu for several days. Over time, their relationship became closer, and their affection grew stronger. However, Qu Daigu was constrained by her father and could not marry. The official, lacking much wealth and fearing his wife's jealousy, dared not act. After much deliberation, they found it impossible to be inseparable like the feng and yang birds, so they decided to bind themselves together with a deep emotional bond and plan to be buried together. On the night of the seventh day of the seventh month, as the sun set, they wept and spoke privately, and suddenly they bathed and changed their clothes.

    621

    其鸨乍疑之，而又为暑天常事，要终惊其耳目焉。夜分置酒对酌，盈盈相视，大有悲惨之色。迨闭户而穴隙窥之，二人者始则饮泣，继则无声，竟以洋烟倾入茶瓯中，搅匀之，将永除此生烦恼矣。鸨乃大声急呼，破扉而入，急先摔其毒饮，细诘根由，知为情种之深也。再三慰劝，并亟语其父，为之格外防闲。而友人书中之所述如此，亦不尽悉其余。于是情魔子闻而叹曰：“夫情者，尽人所具。臣死忠，子死孝，皆情也。自夫人为利所溺，而情薄矣。大姑之为妓，妓中之下乘，是甘于利而为此也。令吏身不择术，而充县吏，又贪于利而为此也。一旦因色欲所感，几致忘身以殉，是以弃利如敝屣，而结情如胶漆。以视见利忘情者，相去几何？特是事甚鄙秽，转致其情为不足道，要不得谓非情种也。”情魔子既矜其情，而又惜其情之误用，著此说以辨明之。俾用情之正者，勿让此二人之轻利也。

    Explanation

    621

    Her mother-in-law initially suspected her, but it was a common occurrence in summer, and ultimately, it surprised her senses. At midnight, they set up wine and drank together, looking at each other with tears in their eyes, and there was a great deal of sadness in their expressions. When the door was closed, they peered through a crack; at first, they wept silently, then they became silent, and finally, they poured opium into the teacup, stirred it well, and intended to permanently rid themselves of this life's troubles. Her mother-in-law then shouted loudly, bursting in through the door, quickly smashing the poisonous drink, carefully questioning the reasons, and knowing that it was due to deep feelings. She repeatedly comforted and advised them, and urgently told her father to take extra precautions. The friend's account in the letter described this, but did not fully detail the rest. The demon of love heard and sighed, saying: "Love is something everyone possesses. A minister dies for loyalty, a son dies for filial piety, all are forms of love. Since the woman was drowned in greed, love became thin. The older sister became a prostitute, the lowest form of a prostitute, this was done out of greed. The county official did not choose his methods and became a county official, and did this out of greed. One day, influenced by lust, almost to the point of sacrificing oneself, therefore, they discarded greed like a worn-out shoe, and bound love like glue and lacquer. Compared to those who forget love for the sake of profit, how different are they? But this matter is very dirty and shameful, it makes their love seem unworthy of mention, but it cannot be called anything other than a seed of love." The demon of love then praised their love and lamented the misuse of their love, wrote this explanation to clarify it, to encourage those who use love correctly, not to let these two people be swayed by profit.

    622

    接录和张少卿花烛词
    　　
    　　读梦芜庵主赋滇南张小伊观察娶吴门张少卿校书花烛词，感成七言绝句八首，聊以记事。六郎自昔擅佳名，佳话而今倍有情。一续鸾胶传盛事，从教四海艳卿卿。象服辉煌稳称身，花冠掩映面罗新。灿然珠履盈门客，一笑新人是故人。阿母今春新嫁娘，归来料简女儿装。红毹蟒绣双双拜，如此东床喜欲狂。（少卿之母，白发垂矣，今春挟厚资别择嫁，亦异闻也。）明珠不费丽姝来，天假奇缘却自媒。妾骤贵时郎骤富，缠头金翠满妆台。檀板金尊旧侣携，下风羞涩判云泥。至今十倍青楼价，不肯从人作小妻。好好原来是一家，钟情底事说闲邪。愿今永比鹣鹣翼，莫踵前人怨落花。曾闻金屋旧藏娇，蓬梗无端断复飘。料得华堂花烛夜，有人珠泪泣鲛绡。郎君官贵锦衣游，万里鸳鸯得自由。太息滇南征戍士，几时解甲问衾。

    Explanation

    622

    Translation:

    Continuing the Record and the Lantern Festival Poem of Zhang Shaoying

    Reading the Lantern Festival Poem of Zhang Xiaoyi, the observer, who married Zhang Shaoying, the scholar from Wu, moved to compose eight seven-character quatrains, merely to record the events. Sixth Brother has always had a good reputation; the beautiful story today is even more touching. One continuation of the phoenix glue to spread the grand event; let the whole world admire you. The ceremonial dress is splendid and fits well; the flower headdress is new and beautiful. The guests are filled with splendid pearl shoes; a smile, and the new bride is like an old friend. The mother-in-law is a new bride this spring; returning and preparing the daughter's clothes. Red silk carpet with dragon embroidery, they bow together; such joy on the east bed. The beautiful lady comes without spending pearls; heavenly coincidence, yet self-proposed. The wife becomes suddenly wealthy, and the husband becomes suddenly rich; golden and green decorations fill the makeup table. Holding the wooden board and golden cup, old friends accompany; ashamed and embarrassed, like clouds and mud. Now ten times the price of a brothel; not willing to be a secondary wife. It turns out they are originally from the same family; devoted to love, what is the point of idle talk? Wishing to always be like the wings of a phoenix; do not follow the previous person's regret over fallen flowers. Once heard about the golden house hiding the beauty; the reed stem breaks and floats aimlessly. Expecting the grand hall during the lantern festival night; someone is crying tears of pearls on the fish skin. The young man is an official and travels in fine silk; a thousand miles of mandarin ducks getting freedom. Sighing at the soldiers in Yunnan; when will they take off their armor and ask for a quilt?

    623

    张少卿题虎阜寺壁四绝
    　　
    　　风逼蓬窗秋杪天，连宵支枕不成眠。阿侬已作征人妇，谢却歌衫舞袖缘。稽首慈悲大士前，桃花命薄愿垂怜。难忘旧日情如海，濡墨留题泉石边。诗写荒园墨未浓，船头津鼓促行踪。孙郎若问真消息，已隔云山一万重。迢迢驿路已凄惶，旧事回思倍断肠。缘结玉萧期再世，好将鸿雪证山塘。
    　　
    　　玉峰樵客游虎阜题壁绝句并序
    　　
    　　玉峰樵客后游虎阜，拂拭新题，殊为惆怅，而芳迹莫可追矣，因和四绝：瑟瑟西风欲暮天，夕阳衰柳恼人眠。何堪更读秋娘句，许结来生未了缘。何事留题古寺前，万千情绪亦堪怜。行云踪迹原无定，欲寄相思何处边。粉香虽淡墨香浓，遥想伊人去后踪。我已忏除情旖旎，为卿翻惹恨千重。兰思蕙怨两凄惶，念及当初欲断肠。诗和涛笺留艳笔，从今深怕过山塘。

    623

    Four Absolute Poems Inscribed on the Wall of Tiger Hill Temple by Zhang Shaoying

    The wind presses against the thatched window on the autumn's end,
    Spent the night awake, propping up on my pillow.
    I am now a wife of a soldier,
    Thanks for the singing clothes and dancing sleeves.
    I bow deeply before the Benevolent Great Master,
    Wishing for compassion for my fragile fate like peach blossoms.
    Unforgettable is the love of old, vast as the sea,
    I moisten the ink and inscribe on the rocks by the spring.
    Writing in the desolate garden, the ink is not yet thick,
    The drum at the ferry urges my departure.
    If Sun Lang asks for true news,
    It is already separated by a thousand layers of clouds and mountains.
    The long journey on the post road is already sorrowful,
    Recalling the past events makes me feel even more heartbroken.
    The bond with the jade flute is to meet again in the next life,
    Good to leave a trace of the snow on the mountain pond.

    Four Absolute Poems Inscribed on the Wall of Tiger Hill Temple by the Woodcutter of Jade Peak

    After the Woodcutter of Jade Peak revisited Tiger Hill,
    Wiping away the new inscription, feeling especially sad,
    And the fragrant trace can no longer be followed,
    He composed four absolute poems in response:
    The瑟瑟 (sè sè) west wind on the dusk sky,
    The setting sun and withered willows disturb sleep.
    How can one bear to read the lines of Autumn Lady,
    To promise to meet in the next life for an unfinished bond.
    Why leave an inscription before the ancient temple,
    The myriad emotions are also pitiable.
    The traces of wandering clouds are originally uncertain,
    Where to send the longing.
    The powder fragrance is faint, but the ink fragrance is strong,
    To imagine the traces of the beloved after they have gone.
    I have already confessed and renounced the charming emotions,
    For you, I have incurred countless layers of hatred.
    The thoughts of orchids and the resentments of lilies are both sorrowful,
    Thinking of the beginning, it almost breaks my heart.
    Poems and notes written with a beautiful pen,
    From now on, I fear passing by the mountain pond.

    624

    和张少卿女史虎阜寺题壁诗原韵
    　　
    　　今春小住金阊，友人道及少卿校书，津津有味，因偕往访之。欢生却扇，韵度绕梁，遂深悦其丰姿，并倾倒其谈吐。况复弦歌之下，又工吟咏。近日吴下各姝，殆无其匹也。仆以徘徊未久，惜又言旋。复读《申报》，知清和之多情，喜云英之早嫁，窃为少卿幸也。今见虎阜题壁诗，并玉峰樵客和作，始知身虽跨凤，却又未能忘情于野鸳鸯；然与种情者，固应如是；而于薄幸者，又当何如耶？仆之用情，虽较异于玉峰樵客，然不忍其独枨触于怀，爰和原韵四章：薄游犹记暮春天，拥髻联吟夜未眠。谁料销魂真个后，行云何处认良缘。想见诗题画壁前，慈云大士定相怜。珍珠字比相思子，红豆丛生佛座边。不道吟情尔许浓，转来萧寺记芳踪。十郎薄幸孙郎密，一样蓬山隔万重。别时无语最凄惶，料得秋娘亦断肠。他日吴门重返棹，满腔愁绪在横塘。

    Explanation

    624

    And in Response to Ms. Shaoqing's Poems Inscribed on the Wall at Hufu Temple, Following the Original Rhyme

    This spring, I stayed briefly in Jinchi, and my friends spoke of Ms. Shaoqing, the librarian, with great interest, so I went to visit her. Joyful interactions and melodious impressions led me to deeply admire her graceful appearance and be captivated by her eloquent speech. Moreover, under the music, she was also skilled in poetry. Among the beauties of Wu recently, none could match her. I lingered not long enough, and then had to leave. Reading the Shen Bao newspaper, I learned that Qinghe is very affectionate and happy that Yunying has married early, and I secretly feel fortunate for Ms. Shaoqing. Now seeing the poems inscribed on the wall at Hufu Temple and the responses by the Woodcutter of Yufeng Mountain, I now know that although she has married into a noble family, she still has feelings for her former lover. But for those who have cultivated feelings, it should be this way. And what about those who are unfaithful? My feelings, though different from those of the Woodcutter of Yufeng Mountain, I cannot bear to let such emotions stir within me alone, so I have composed four poems in response to the original rhyme:

    A brief visit still recalls the evening of spring. Holding hairpins, we composed poetry together through the night. Who would have thought that after such a passionate encounter, where can the floating clouds recognize a good match? I imagine seeing poems inscribed on the wall before the compassionate cloud deity must surely pity us. Pearl-like characters compared to the seeds of longing, red beans growing around the Buddha's seat. It was not expected that the poetic sentiment would be so intense, and now at the temple, I remember her graceful presence. Ten Gentlemen are unfaithful, while Sun Gentleman is close; both are separated by countless mountains. When we parted, our silence was the most heartrending, and I imagine that Autumn Lady must also be heartbroken. One day, returning to Wu Gate on a boat, all my sorrow will be in the cross pond.

    625

    和张少卿题虎阜寺壁绝句并序
    　　
    　　吴门张少卿校书者，风情月貌，倾倒一时；蕙质兰心，流传七字。余初未见其笔墨也。及读十月上旬《申报》，有题虎阜寺壁四绝句，意甚凄惋，语极温存。虽使苏小复生，亦当首肯。并读玉峰樵客和韵，音节悲凉，令人呜咽。可知名士倾城，难于遇合，千载如一辙也。特少卿既捐旧好，用博新欢，今秋为清和观察携之归里。彼时芜香馆主，虽作《花烛词》八首，至拟以红玉之归韩元帅，盼盼之适张尚书，以志倾慕。

    Explanation

    625

    Translation:

    In Harmony with Zhang Shaoqing's Inscription on the Wall of Hu Fu Temple, with Preface

    Zhang Shaoqing, the proofreader of Wu Gate, possesses a graceful demeanor and a beautiful appearance, captivating everyone at the time; with her gentle and refined nature, her seven-character lines of poetry became widely known. I had not seen her writings before. Upon reading the Shen Bao newspaper in early October, I found four quatrains inscribed on the wall of Hu Fu Temple, which were deeply poignant in meaning and extremely warm in tone. Even if Su Xiaoxiao were to be reborn, she would surely agree. I also read the harmonious verses composed by the Mountain Peak Woodcutter, whose melody was sorrowful and moved me to tears. It is clear that the admiration of a talented person and the difficulty of meeting the right match have remained the same over many centuries. But since Zhang Shaoqing has abandoned his old friends, he has sought new pleasures, and this autumn he was taken back to his hometown by Qing He, the observer. At that time, the master of Wu Xiang Pavilion, though he wrote eight wedding poems, went so far as to compare them to the story of Hong Yu marrying a military leader and Pan Pan marrying an official, to express his admiration.

    626

    想为观察者，自应白首相偕，不渝终始矣。兹又闻梦醒樊川，依然薄幸，则一寸眉峰，更不知愁添几许矣。余既爱其才，又惜其遇，因和原韵，以当长门之赋云。娲皇无计补情天，剩得花枝伴月眠。惆怅题诗分彩笔，当初一面竟无缘。拟嫁张星路不前，新怀旧梦总相怜。那堪回首长堤柳，万绪情丝绾两边。虎阜清流蠡水浓，画桡一去渺无踪。不知此后门如海，望断蓬山又几重。画眉无意更凄惶，令我同回九曲肠。一样天涯悲冷落，潇潇暮雨听横塘。

    Explanation

    626

    As an observer, I had expected to share a lifelong partnership, unchanging from beginning to end. Yet now, I hear that in the wake of Du Fu's exile at Fan Chuan, he remains unfaithful. Thus, my own sorrow has deepened beyond measure. I admire his talent, yet pity his misfortune. Therefore, I compose a poem in the same rhyme scheme as his, as a substitute for the Changmen Fu (The Long Gate Ode). Nüwa has no way to mend the realm of emotions, leaving only the flowers to keep me company as I sleep under the moonlight. With regret, I write poetry with colorful brushes, for we never had the chance to meet. I intended to marry him, but the path was blocked; new feelings and old dreams intertwine in mutual affection. How can I bear to look back at the willows along the long embankment, where countless threads of emotion are entangled on both sides? The clear waters of Tiger Mountain flow with the dense Li River, but the painted oars have disappeared without a trace. I wonder what lies beyond this door, which is as vast as the sea, and how many layers of distant mountains I must look through. Drawing my eyebrows without intention, I am even more sorrowful, causing my heart to twist like a nine-bend intestine. Both of us, at the ends of the earth, share the sorrow of loneliness, listening to the evening rain falling on the pond.

    627

    双龄小传
    　　
    　　双龄，邗江村僻女也。年十六七，秀骨丰肌，眉目如画，望之若神仙中人。家固贫，纺织自劬，依母为活。偶有龌龊隶见而悦之，欲娶以为妇，逼胁再四，女乃引刀自刺其臂，血溢襟袖，哭甚悲。有某绅怜其志，诉邑宰而惩是隶焉。时有越人张秋十者，游毗陵太守幕，过维扬。客中无以自遣，偕二三友人出郭翔步。野花蓬勃，好鸟勾，略一瞻顾，有女郎掩映竹篱茅舍。凝睇之，玉蕊琼英，未足方喻，盖女郎非他，即双龄也。生乃叹曰：“玉人如可购，何吝明珠一斛哉！”遽请邻媪，以意示其母，其母曰：“老身衣食，皆赖女十指，嫁之则残朽何以自存？若郎君果欲得而甘心，非五百金不可。”生检点囊资，十不及一。

    Explanation

    627

    Double Age Biography

    Double Age was a beautiful young woman from a remote village in Hanyang. At sixteen or seventeen, she had a slender yet plump figure, her eyes and eyebrows as beautiful as a painting, and her appearance seemed to be that of a goddess. Her family was poor, and she supported herself and her mother through hard work in weaving. A lowly man saw her and fell in love with her, wanting to marry her. He pressured her repeatedly, and in desperation, she cut her own arm with a knife, causing blood to flow onto her clothes, and she cried bitterly. A local gentleman, moved by her resolve, reported the matter to the county magistrate, who punished the man.

    At that time, there was a man from Yue named Zhang Qiushi, who was traveling in the office of the governor of Pizhong and passed through Yangzhou. With nothing to occupy his time, he accompanied a few friends outside the city walls for a leisurely walk. The wildflowers were in full bloom, and the birds sang sweetly. As they glanced around, they saw a young lady hidden behind bamboo fences and thatched cottages. They gazed at her fixedly, and her beauty was so exquisite that it could not be adequately described. It turned out that the young lady was none other than Double Age. Zhang Qiushi sighed and said, "If this jade person could be bought, why would one hesitate to offer a measure of pearls!" He immediately asked an elderly neighbor to convey his intentions to her mother. Her mother replied, "My old age and food depend on her fingers. If she is married away, how will the old woman survive? If the young master truly desires her, it cannot be less than five hundred gold." Zhang Qiushi checked his purse and found that he lacked even a tenth of that amount.

    628

    然思无术，嗒焉若丧。女知之，谓母曰：“睹郎君气宇不凡，必非长于贫贱者。儿若得所，母无忧矣。何求重值耶？”母不忍拂，廉其价而遣之。生遂献囊，载美而归。夫人贤且勤，见双龄妩媚可人，顿生怜爱，即典质钗钏，为女制衣履，令生抚么弦也。女事夫人亦良谨，不苟言笑。米盐琐屑，能代夫人经纪。夫人曰：“向道是画中人，不能操井臼，今若此，吾何虑焉？”田百亩，桑五十株，女缫丝织绢，光洁无匹，贾人踵门争购之，得其值，纳太平之赋有余。生由是名花相对，无志功名，亦不复出，游吴会。尝有一绝句云：琴剑飘零久惜身，温柔乡里作齐人。功名二字休提问，常对名花悟夙因。亦可想其闺房之趣矣。

    Explanation

    628

    However, without a method of thought, she felt desolate. The girl understood this and said to her mother, "I saw that the young man has an extraordinary presence; he must not be from a poor and lowly background. If I find a suitable place, you will have no worries. Why seek a high price?" Her mother, unable to refuse, bargained for a lower price and let her go. The man then presented a bag and returned with her, bringing beauty with him. The lady was virtuous and diligent. Seeing the two young ones charming and pleasing, she immediately felt compassion and affection. She promptly pawned her jewelry to make clothes and shoes for the girl, and instructed the man to play the highest-pitched string. The girl served the lady with great care, speaking and laughing only when necessary. She managed the daily necessities and could handle the lady's affairs. The lady said, "I used to think she was a painted figure, incapable of household chores, but now she is like this, what do I have to worry about?" They had one hundred acres of land and fifty mulberry trees. The girl spun silk and wove it into fine, unmatched cloth. Merchants visited the door to compete for it. With the proceeds, they paid the peace tax and had plenty left over. The man thus lived with a beautiful woman, without ambition for fame and glory, and no longer went out to travel to Wu and Kuaiji. He once wrote a short poem:

    琴剑飘零久惜身，温柔乡里作齐人。
    功名二字休提问，常对名花悟夙因。

    One can also imagine the pleasures of their domestic life.

    629

    泺阳女子题壁诗
    　　
    　　泺阳女子雪侬，与西溪生有白头之约。西溪生固文士，因饥驱，橐笔出游幕府。日久未归，其母悔盟，鬻于豫客。客挟以偕，时寓中和店，临行，题诗于壁云：征鞍欲上泣无声，意里连环绾不成。此去死生悲异路，他年魂梦逐归程。琵琶有怨嗟鸳侣，翡翠无温听雁更。试问天涯久游客，黄金何日赎残生？词旨哀怨，恻恻可怜。惜乎胡笳蔡女，未知其果返汉关；而赵郡美人，亲见其拥归叱利。西溪生闻之，正不知何以为情也！

    Explanation

    629

    A Poem on the Wall by a Girl from Lüyang

    The girl from Lüyang, named Xuenong, had made a lifelong promise with Xixi Sheng. Xixi Sheng, a scholar by nature, was driven by hunger to travel as a writer in a military camp. After many days had passed without his return, his mother regretted the promise and sold him to a guest from Henan. The guest took him with him, and they stayed at the inn called Zhonghe. On the eve of his departure, he inscribed a poem on the wall: "As I am about to mount my saddle, I cry silently; the ties of our hearts remain unbroken. This journey of life and death leads us to different paths, and in the future, my spirit and dreams will follow the path of return. The pípá laments the couple, and the fèicuì lacks warmth as I listen to the sound of geese. Let me ask, traveler far from home, when will gold redeem my remaining life?" The words of the poem are filled with sorrow and pity. Alas, the fate of Huqin Pian, the woman who played the huqin, is unknown; while the beauty from Zhaozhou witnessed her being taken back to Chilí. Upon hearing this, Xixi Sheng could not help but wonder what he should do.

    630

    一度风流千贯钱
    　　
    　　月老姻缘簿上，一曰缘，二曰貌，三曰财。有一于此，须天下之红粉青衫，无不可作有情眷属。然缘尽则止，虽白首曾盟，绿窗已字，成陌路也。试举一事以实之。浙宁望京门，有某氏女者，芙蓉出水，杨柳临风，阿环之苗裔也。芳龄十八，待字闺中，东李西张，肥钱瘦赵。得婿良难，迄无成说。一日宁之西门外天宝社演剧，菊部大佳。观剧者如火如荼，几几乎万人空巷，斗新妆也。女椿萱并茂，弟已成童，日罄室往，门加钥焉。场有纨绔少年某，观之，以为惊鸿游龙，不足喻也。眼溜眉波，色授神与，就女旁木立。

    Explanation

    630

    A Moment of Charm Worth Thousands of Strings of Coins

    On the Book of Marriages kept by the deity Moon Matchmaker, it is written that marriage depends on three things: destiny, appearance, and wealth. If one possesses any of these, a man or woman from any background can become a loving couple. However, when the destined connection ends, even if they have sworn to be together for life and the woman has been married, they can become strangers. Let me recount a story to illustrate this. In the vicinity of the Hopeful Gate in Zhejiang, there was a young lady from a certain family. She was as beautiful as a lotus flower emerging from the water and as graceful as a willow swaying in the wind, a descendant of Lady Aohan. She was eighteen years old, waiting to be married, and had many suitors, but none had been successful. One day, the Tianbao Society performed a play outside the west gate of Ningbo. The actors were excellent. The audience was enthusiastic and numerous, to the point that the streets were almost empty, as people competed in their new and elaborate makeup. Her parents were healthy, and her younger brother had grown up. She visited the theater every day, even going to the point of emptying her room, and the door was locked to prevent her from going out. Among the audience, there was a young, wealthy man who watched her and thought she was more beautiful than a startled swan or a soaring dragon, a sight that could not be described. He gazed at her with admiration, and she seemed to feel his attention. He stood motionless beside her.

    631

    向午，有飨女者，女出柔荑，春葱不啻。少年更魂魄都酥。女淡饼饵，腻流袖上，举手向后，舌舔油污，余食固犹然在玉指中也。少年见手泽口泽，惊喜欲狂。见其持饵向后肩，以为与己，因诵“匪汝之为美，美人之贻”句，接而啖之，女回顾，红潮涨颊，怏怏特甚；于是乘兴而来，兴尽而返，挽母归。母兴高采烈，不能舍去，遣弟送姐，弟犹有童心，到门即去，不忘优孟衣冠也。少年尾女。启钥，弟去，窃喜。知屋内杳无其人，披帏入曰：“蒙卿惠赐，食德饮和，敬从鱼轩，乞卿教我。”女惊魄不定，半向无言。少年拥女求欢，女又愤又羞，乍惊乍喜。

    Explanation

    631

    At noon, a woman was invited to dine, and she displayed her delicate hands, no less tender than spring onions. The young man was completely enchanted by her. She ate pastries, and grease dripped onto her sleeves. She raised her hand behind her and licked the grease from her fingers, with food still clinging to her delicate fingers. The young man, seeing her moist hands and lips, was astonished and nearly overwhelmed. He thought she was offering the food to him, so he recited a line from the Book of Songs: "It's not for your beauty, but for the beauty of the lady who gave it to me." He took the food and ate it. The woman turned her head, and her cheeks flushed with embarrassment or anger. The young man came with enthusiasm but left when his interest waned, pulling his mother back home. The mother was excited and reluctant to leave, so she sent her brother to accompany the sister. The brother, still a child, went only as far as the door and didn't forget to put on his "costume." The young man followed the woman. He opened the door, the brother left, and the young man secretly felt pleased. Realizing the house was empty, he entered and said, "I am grateful for your kindness, and I have enjoyed the meal. Please teach me further." The woman was startled and speechless, unsure how to respond. The young man tried to embrace her, and she felt both angry and embarrassed, alternating between shock and pleasure.

    632

    盖以少年，似曾相识，固宁城之多金季子也。卿须怜我我怜卿，半推半就间，已谐好事。嗣江上峰青，曲终人散，母拔关入，女正与少年酣睡未醒。殆两军对垒，主客皆疲；嗣息鼓偃旌，再图接战。不图为母氏所觉。时伊父尚道途仆仆也，少停，返，母迎问曰：“日之夕矣，亦作知还之鸟乎？倘女室有人，作韩寿之偷香，将何以处？”女父以语无伦次，姑漫应之曰：“男子生而愿为之有室，女子生而愿为之有家。如男子未婚，女子未嫁，字之而已。”即告以情节，唤女起，跪而自投。

    Explanation

    632

    It is because the young man seemed familiar, he is indeed the wealthy youngest son of Ningcheng. You must pity me, and I will pity you. In a half-refusal and half-acceptance, good things have already come to pass. After the music ends and the people disperse, the mother enters by force, and the girl was still sleeping soundly with the young man. It is like two armies facing each other, both exhausted; after resting and lowering the flags, they plan to fight again. Unfortunately, the mother discovered it. At that time, his father was still traveling. After a short while, he returned, and the mother greeted and asked, "It is evening, are you also a bird that knows to return home?" If there is someone in the girl's room, like Han Shou stealing fragrances, what will you do? The girl's father, confused by the incoherent words, replied casually, "Men wish to have a home, and women wish to have a family. If men are unmarried and women are unmarried, they are simply referred to as such." He then told her the situation, woke her up, and she knelt and confessed.

    633

    备审少年邦族，知中馈犹虚，即妻以女，曰：“始乱之，终成之，唯子而已。吾不忍使中郎弱息，作春风别嫁也。虽然，必有以报我。”着少年书券三纸，共钱一千贯，俾作嫁资。一俟清偿，央媒行聘；三星在户，即妆女出阁时也。少年急于践约，今秋，已全行归楚。央媒作撮合山，则以齐大非偶，已别字乡人矣。某随得心疾，每咄咄书空，曰：“风流一度，出资千贯。”语不绝口。嗟乎！十年待字，缘深五百年前；一梦曾酣，缘了三生石上。前则交无一面，凤翥鸾翔；后而缔有百年，鸾离鹄别。主姻缘簿者，何尝不愿有情人成眷属，须天下士无别离也？然而各有前缘，不能强也。是则缘之一字，为不可诬矣。

    Explanation

    633

    A Discerning Young Man of the Clan, Knowing the Inner Chamber Still Empty, Married His Daughter to Him, Saying: "To Begin with Disorder and End with Completion, It Is Only You. I Cannot Bear to Let the Young Son of the Middle Official Be Like a Spring Breeze That Leaves Without a Trace. Although So, There Must Be Something to Repay Me." The Young Man Wrote Three Documents and a Total of One Thousand Strings of Cash, to Serve as a Dowry. Once the Debt Is Repaid, the Matchmaker Will Arrange the Wedding; When the Three Stars Are in the Door, It Is the Time for the Bride to Be Dressed and Leave the House. The Young Man Was Eager to Fulfill the Agreement, and This Autumn, He Had Already Fully Returned to Chu. The Matchmaker Acted as a Go-Between, but Because a Match with a High-Ranking Family Was Not Suitable, He Had Already Been Betrothed to a Local Person. I Subsequently Developed a Heart Ailment, Often Making Strange Gestures and Saying, "One Moment of Passion, Costing a Thousand Strings of Cash." He Would Not Stop Talking. Alas! Ten Years of Waiting, a Karmic Connection Dating Back Five Hundred Years; One Dream Was Deeply Satisfying, the Karmic Connection Was Resolved on the Stone of Three Lives. Previously, They Had Not Even Met, Like Phoenixes and Cranes Flying Together; Later, They Were Bound in a Lifelong Union, but Separated Like Cranes and Geese. The Keeper of the Book of姻缘, Who Would Not Wish for Lovers to Become a Couple, but Requires That All Scholars Have No Separations? However, Each Has Their Own Predestined Fate, Which Cannot Be Forced. Thus, the Concept of Fate Is Not to Be Denied.

    634

    天缘巧合
    　　
    　　严子卿乔居宝华坊，琴瑟情香。妻王氏，结缡两载，罹娩难。严不忍续娶，鳏厥居者六年。季夏，仲兄某自汉阳贾所归，谓之曰：“予漂泊半生，虽略有所弋获，而嗣续之计杳。然弟既丧偶，胡不早为胶续，虚此韶年？且情之所钟，原不在此。即《雉朝飞》曲，操之终生，而谓泉下人知之乎？又况舍宗祧大计，以尽闺房燕婉之私，泉下人有知，将必愀然不安其所。为子计，实宜早作后图。”严思先代，只传弟兄两人，兄久客无子，己苟不立继室，恐所谓百年未尽之计者，及身而见其尽也。乃寻媒氏露以意。媒陈媪，积年老滑也，而貌洵洵然如愚妇。今严命为之采访，骤无合式者。严促之曰：“婆子作媒半生，直尔如许柔缓。”陈媪曰：“君勿躁也。鲁莽而耕者，亦鲁莽而报。

    Explanation

    634

    A Stroke of Fate

    Yan Ziqing moved to the Bao Hua Lane, where he and his wife Wang shared a harmonious and loving relationship. After two years of marriage, Wang suffered a difficult childbirth and passed away. Yan Ziqing could not bring himself to remarry and remained a widower for six years. In late summer, his second eldest brother returned from a business trip in Hanyang and told him, "I have wandered for half a lifetime and, though I have gained something, the plan for descendants is still uncertain. Yet, you have lost your wife; why not marry again and not waste this youthful time? Moreover, the affection I have is not focused on you. Even if I played the 'Cockscomb Bird Flies Early in the Morning' piece throughout my life, would those in the netherworld know about it? How much more so, then, to neglect the continuation of the family lineage and indulge in the private joys of the inner chambers, leaving those in the netherworld feeling uneasy and uncomfortable. For your sake, it would be best to plan for the future early."

    Yan Ziqing thought about his ancestors, who had only two sons, and his brother, who had been away for a long time and had no children. If he did not remarry, the plans for a long life and the continuation of the family lineage would come to an end in his own lifetime. He decided to seek a matchmaker and expressed his intention. The matchmaker, Chen Aunty, was an old and experienced woman who appeared gentle and innocent like a simple woman. Yan Ziqing instructed her to find a suitable match, but she immediately found no suitable candidate. Yan Ziqing urged her, "You have been a matchmaker for half a lifetime; why are you so gentle and slow?" Chen Aunty replied, "Do not be impatient. A reckless farmer will also report recklessly."

    635

    君既责以精选，而又促之以时日，天下容有是理乎？君能为市井子者，娶半截回头人，则老身早有以应命矣。”严笑曰：“此又不必拘也。八珍甫备，而厨者先膏；大厦未成，而匠人先坐。予只求人物中选耳，再醮何害。”媪曰：“君既不厌此，老身当为谋之，然不必另起炉灶矣。”严曰：“何如？”媪言：“南门里司后街，有新寡卓氏女，王姓子托老身执柯。男女业相会面，王子已中肯綮，而彼女嫌其不，且性近佻，遂不就。君若及早谋之，事可图也。”严闻言，姑请媪引导一会。媪暗失笑，盖明知彼女高自位置，贵胄富商，多不入其目，何有于区区贾竖？特逗之以为戏耳。讵意缘之所在，一撮而露合机；引与相会，而女若甚殷殷著意者，嘱媪访其家世，媪诡言以应，且谓“此不过据彼言之，是否有参虚实，仍请察核”。女曰：“家资可不必论，只访其人耳。”媪虽不知女底细，而闻其颇有所蓄，又不意于严骤有垂成也。

    Explanation

    635

    The lord had already been tasked with selecting the best, and was also pressed for time, but could there possibly be such a thing in the world? Could you, as a commoner, marry a woman who has remarried? If so, I would have already been able to fulfill the order. Yan smiled and said, "This need not be bound by such rules. Just as when the eight delicacies are just ready, the cook gets fat first; and when the great building is not yet completed, the craftsmen sit down first. I only want the best person chosen, and what harm is there in remarrying?" The old woman said, "Since you are not averse to this, I shall make arrangements for you, but there is no need to start from scratch." Yan asked, "How so?" The old woman replied, "There is a newly widowed woman from the Zhuo family on South Gate Lane, Street of the Official. A man surnamed Wang entrusted me to act as a matchmaker. They have already met, and the man has hit the mark, but the woman finds him lacking in refinement, and his nature is too frivolous, so she did not agree. If you were to make plans early, the matter could be resolved." Yan, upon hearing this, asked the old woman to guide him to meet her once. The old woman secretly laughed, knowing full well that the woman positions herself highly, and that noble and wealthy merchants are often not in her sights. What does she care about a lowly merchant? She was just teasing her for fun. Who could have thought that缘分 (缘分) was at work, and they were suddenly brought together? When they were brought together to meet, and the woman seemed very interested, she asked the old woman to investigate her family background. The old woman lied to respond and said, "This is just based on what she said; whether it's true or false, please verify." The woman said, "Wealth is not important; just investigate the person." Although the old woman did not know much about the girl, she had heard that she had some savings, and she did not expect that Yan would suddenly succeed.

    636

    乃妒之，言于严曰：“此举若成，君之后福，实属不浅，君当何以报我？”严曰：“薄具钗金五两何如？”媪曰：“富家人出手太俭，质言之，聘礼多寡，尚可不拘，媒礼则非五十两不可。君能不靳此，老身与效奔走，即折胫曳踵所不计耳。”严言：“汝何索之重！”媪曰：“君勿靳此，亦抛砖引玉耳。彼女所拥，诚不可以数计，君亦何所靳，而不与老身乎？”严思其意装乔，不知葫芦中实卖何药，遂一笑置之。至七月初旬，严婚事仍复低昂不就。有友人拟娶再醮妇。七月初五日，往丛桂南相阅，邀严为伴客。严初谢之，友曰：“闻君亦欲行此道，胡不同行？藉资法眼，或者天缘所在，我不就而君就，未可知也。”严笑诺之。比至，玉人缓步出。严讶为神女凌波。甫却扇，严惊不置，盖即前者司后街相阅之女。缘女以探访亲眷，就媒氏宅，为婚姻地也。

    Explanation

    636

    The elderly woman was envious and said to Yan, "If this matter succeeds, your future blessings will be truly significant. How will you reward me?" Yan replied, "What about a modest gift of five taels of silver?" The woman said, "The wealthy are too stingy. Frankly speaking, the amount of the betrothal gift is not strictly fixed, but the matchmaker's fee cannot be less than fifty taels. Can you not hesitate to give this? I will do my best, even if it means breaking my legs and dragging my feet." Yan said, "Why do you ask for so much?" The woman said, "You should not hesitate to give this; it is also a small gesture to elicit a larger response. What she possesses is truly countless. Why should you hesitate to give it to me?" Yan thought her intentions were pretentious and didn't know what medicine was being sold in the gourd, so he dismissed it with a laugh. By early July, Yan's marriage negotiations were still fluctuating without success. A friend planned to marry a woman who had been widowed and remarried. On July 5th, he went to the south of Conggui to inspect and invited Yan as a guest. Yan initially declined, but his friend said, "Hearing that you also want to do this, why don't we go together? To benefit from your discerning eye, perhaps it is meant to be. It might be that I don't succeed while you do." Yan laughed and agreed. When they arrived, the beautiful woman slowly emerged. Yan was surprised, thinking she was a fairy descending from the waves. As soon as she fanned herself, Yan was amazed and couldn't stop staring. It turned out to be the same woman he had seen on the back street before. Because the woman was visiting her relatives and staying at the matchmaker's house, which was the place for marriage arrangements.

    637

    女见严，亦错愕不已。旋入室，使佣妇出问严曰：“前既有约，胡久无音？”友不知其绪也，穷问之，严告以前曾相阅之故，且托佣妇覆女，言前某媪，勒索媒礼，且料其必无成意，故置之。女闻，复出帘下谓严曰：“彼妇诈殊甚。彼前言覆我，谓君无意采菲。讵知彼因诈君不遂，因而诈覆耳。今无意见君，始知前者，流水落花，均非无意，但为撮合山所阻耳。”友聆言深讶前谊，赞成之。十二日完娶，陈媪见严，无意作合，不胜妒恚，使人播扬于严，谓女前夫以疯疾下世。严闻，归而叩女，女曰：“此必妒君者之造言也。妾前夫乃豫章人，娶妾为路头妻，彼以年老返籍，薄有所遗，任我所适。妾以稚齿无依，故为择木计。”因出妆资示严。嗣密访，始悉谗言实始陈氏之口，遂亦一笑置之。观此可知天缘所定者，人事难阻其成，小人实枉作小人耳。

    Explanation

    637

    The woman saw Yan and was greatly startled. She quickly entered the room and sent the maid out to ask Yan, saying, "Since we had an agreement before, why have you been silent for so long?" The friend did not understand the situation and pressed her for an explanation. Yan told him about the previous examination and asked the maid to inform the woman, saying that the previous matchmaker had demanded a large dowry and had assumed there was no intention of proceeding, so he had put it aside. Upon hearing this, the woman came out from behind the curtain and said to Yan, "That woman's deceit is very serious. She said she would inform me, saying you had no intention of accepting me. How could she know that she failed to deceive you, so she deceived me instead? Now that I see you, I realize that before, the flowing water and falling flowers were not without meaning, but it was blocked by the matchmaker." The friend was deeply surprised by the previous friendship and approved of it. On the twelfth day, the marriage was completed. Chen's grandmother saw Yan and had no intention of arranging the marriage, and she was overwhelmed with jealousy and anger. She sent someone to spread rumors to Yan, saying that the woman's former husband had died of madness. Upon hearing this, Yan returned home and asked the woman. She said, "This must be the slander of the envious one. My former husband was from Yuzhang, and he married me as a concubine. He returned to his hometown when he was old and left me with a small inheritance, letting me choose my own path. I, being young and without support, chose a good tree to settle on." She then showed Yan the dowry. Later, she secretly investigated and learned that the slander came from Chen's mouth. She then dismissed it with a laugh. From this, one can see that what is destined by heaven is difficult for human affairs to obstruct, and the envious ones are truly acting foolishly.

    638

    珠江风月
    　　
    　　珠江风月，谈者艳之。独仓山一老，来此作狎邪游，大不满意。其门下士，亦以为一样春色，其丑诋痛斥，可谓不遗余力矣。我友某太史，偶来粤中，登临之暇，偕二三裾屐，买醉谷埠。到眼莺花，绝无当意，唯赏识一雏妓，年近十四五，绮龄玉貌，绰约可怜。酒罢宵阑，赠以四绝句，为写之团扇，此亦珠江一段佳话也。诗云：饮罢莆萄尽醉归，画船红烛映斜晖。巫云入夜浓如许，漫向劳人梦里飞。良宵风月快清谈，十里波光色蔚蓝。座倚雏鬟嘲暂解，返叫人笑宝儿憨。玉笛风声谱落梅，珠江锦绣枉成堆。垂髫人唱黄河远，艳绝旗亭第二回。仿佛潮州看水嬉，三生杜牧本情痴。他年领郡来宜早，莫待成荫子满枝。

    Explanation

    638

    The Beauty of the Pearl River
    　　
    　　The beauty of the Pearl River is often praised by those who speak of it. Yet, an old man from Cangshan came here to indulge in licentious pursuits and was greatly dissatisfied. His disciples also found the scene to be unremarkable, and they viciously criticized and condemned it, sparing no effort. My friend, a certain historian, happened to come to Guangdong, and during his leisure time, he accompanied a few companions to get drunk in the taverns of Gubu. The sights and sounds that caught his eye were utterly unappealing, but he alone admired a young courtesan, who was around fourteen or fifteen years old, with a beautiful and graceful appearance, and a charming yet pitiful demeanor. After the wine had run out and the night grew late, he presented her with four quatrains, which he wrote on a folding fan. This too is a fine story of the Pearl River. The poems read:

    After drinking grapes and becoming thoroughly drunk, I return on a decorated boat with red candles casting a slanting light. The clouds of desire are so dense at night, they seem to drift into the dreams of weary people. In the pleasant evening of a fine night, we engage in clear and lively conversation, and the waves of the ten-mile river are a vivid blue. Seated beside the young courtesan, we offer gentle jests for a moment, and then laugh at the innocent playfulness of the child. The sound of a jade flute plays the melody of the falling plum blossoms, but the splendor of the Pearl River is in vain, like a heap of useless treasures. A child sings of the distant Yellow River, and the beauty of the tavern is unparalleled in its second act. It seems as if we are watching water sports in Chaozhou, and for three lifetimes, Du Mu was always a romantic fool. In the future, one should take charge of a prefecture early, and not wait until one has a full branch of shade.

    639

    记李三三逸事
    　　
    　　醉倚朱栏带异香，娇羞欲语对斜阳。人间第一风流种，不让西施巧样妆。李三三，逸其本姓，浙宦某之孙女也。生长金阊，习于富贵，灵椿秋冷，大树飘零。母不能持家，暇则招女妓来家，开筵取乐。时而出游，寄宿妓院。家之数万，不几年，挥霍殆尽。嗣以三吴守吏，禁止烟花。其母以不堪寂寞，一叶扁舟，来游海上，租寓于大亭客栈，邻近娼寮，个中人时相过从。于是入茶楼，登酒馆，谈笑于稠人广众之中，遨游乎雪月风花之地；掌上明珠，则固朝随夕侍焉。耳濡目染，絮为泥粘。

    Explanation

    639

    Record of the Anecdotes of Li Sansan

    Leaning on a red railing, intoxicated and carrying an unusual fragrance, she is shy and wants to speak while facing the setting sun. She is the most charming and elegant person in the world, not inferior to Xi Shi in her exquisite makeup. Li Sansan, whose original surname is unknown, is the granddaughter of a certain high-ranking official from Zhejiang. She grew up in Jinchi, accustomed to wealth and luxury. The pine tree (symbolizing longevity) is cold in autumn, and the great tree is falling, indicating a decline in family fortunes. Her mother could not manage the household and, when she had free time, would invite courtesans to her home and hold feasts for entertainment. She would occasionally go out and stay at brothels. Her family's wealth of tens of thousands was almost completely spent within a few years. Later, the governor of Sanwu banned prostitution. Her mother, unable to bear the loneliness, came to the sea in a small boat and rented a room at the Dating Inn, near a brothel. People from the brothel would often visit her. She would then enter teahouses, visit taverns, and chat with crowds in public places, enjoying herself in the world of poetry and beauty. She was treated like a precious gem, always accompanied by her mother day and night. She was constantly exposed to and influenced by the environment, becoming deeply immersed in it.

    640

    惟是长安居大不易，乃知囊内钱空，始觉旧游如梦，一家待哺，半策莫筹。时姬已当二八芳年，艳若一只红杏，姿容绰约，宛似唐环；体态轻盈，几疑赵燕；婷娉轶众，袅娜动人。乃有较字名娼，分香妙伎，巧言哄劝，婉女相商。欲从骥尾附蝇，敢向佛头着粪。倘莺花肯入队，许汝管领春风。姬母惑于口巧如簧，遂使掌珠为饵，就华堂作曲院。筵开玳瑁，客来珠履三千；曲唱懊侬，踏破软红十丈。于是黄歇宾朋，谢安子侄，争相问津，艳声大噪。壬午花朝，艳榜以第二名入选，奖曰：“兰心蕙质，大雅不群。”余以牡丹评之。

    Explanation

    640

    Indeed, living in Chang'an is not easy; I now know that my purse is empty, and I begin to feel that past pleasures seem like a dream. The family is waiting to be fed, and no plan or strategy can be devised. At that time, the girl was in her prime, eighteen years old, as beautiful as a red apricot, with a graceful and elegant appearance, much like Tang Huan; her figure was slender and graceful, almost like Zhao Yan; she was graceful and surpassing others, charming and captivating. There were famous courtesans who compared their skills, skilled in the art of perfuming and dancing, flattering and persuading with clever words, gently negotiating with a woman. She desired to attach herself to the tail of a horse to catch flies, daring to put feces on the Buddha's head. If the flowers and birds (metaphor for beauties) were willing to join the group, she would be allowed to enjoy the spring breeze. The girl's mother was bewitched by the smooth and persuasive speech, thus making the precious pearl (metaphor for the daughter) the bait, turning the grand hall into a pleasure garden. A feast spread out with precious dishes, guests arriving in their thousands, all wearing valuable shoes; singing melancholic tunes, trampling through the soft red (metaphor for the world of pleasure) for ten zhang. The guests of Huang Xie and the nephews and cousins of Xie An competed to seek advice or guidance, greatly renowned for beauty. On the Flower Festival of the 2nd lunar month, she was selected as the second on the list of beauties, awarded the title: "Heart of orchid, quality of lily, elegant and unique." I evaluated her as a peony.

    641

    仓山旧主赏识之，首为提倡，名播士林，有《三三词》六十首，择其善者，摘录于后。姬初居祥春里，芳声传颂，遐迩皆闻。继居西合兴里，更名张韫玉，门前车马，依旧喧阗。癸未春，始从某公子仍返吴中，寻芳者多有崔护刘郎之感。余尝数饮其家，故悉其始末较详。其词曰：此邦风月冠江南，万紫千红任客探。行过章台三十里，无人不道李三三。寻春心事十分酣，醉入花间蛱蝶憨。阅遍环肥兼燕瘦，风情都逊李三三。容光四射暗香含，压倒群芳定不惭。愿把金铃营十万，深深重护李三三。记从江北到江南，不数名花信手探。瘦较清癯肥较俗，姿容谁似李三三。

    Explanation

    641

    The old master of Cang Mountain appreciated her and was the first to promote her, and her fame spread through the scholarly world. She had sixty poems titled "San San Ci," and I have selected the best ones to record here. Initially living in Xiangchun Lane, her good reputation was widely praised, known far and wide. Later she moved to Xihexing Lane, changed her name to Zhang Yunyu, and the crowds of vehicles and horses in front of her door remained as busy as before. In the spring of Guiwei, she first returned with a certain young nobleman to Wu, and those who sought her company often felt the same way as Cui Hu and Liu Lang did. I have often dined at her house, so I know the details of her story quite well. Her words say:
